raw_text: ABRAMS, ARTHUR BARROW.—Fellow of Cent. Assoc. of Acnts. (Incor.) England; asst. head storekeeper, P.W.D., Gold Coast, 9th Oct., 1905; head storekeeper, 26th May, 1906; offl. mem. town coun., Sept.-Oct., 1906; asst. ch. clk. and acnt., 12th May, 1907; ch. clk. and acnt., 18th Oct., 1908.

ABRAMSON, ALBERT, O.B.E.—B. 1876; war serv. with E.E.F.; O.B.E., 1918; twice ment. in despe.; mily. gov., Hebron, 1918; chmn., land comm., and dir., land dept., 1920; ch. Br. rep., Transjordan, 1921; dist. gov., southern dist., 1922; dist. commnr., northern dist., 1925.

ACHESON, A.B.—B. 1895; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Keble Coll., Oxford (scholar, 1913); on mil. service, Rifle Brigade and Machine Gun Corps, 1914-19; served in France, Belgium and Germany; ment. in despe., Croix-de-Guerre; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 12th Jan., 1920; astt. prin., 1st Apr., 1920; sec. to comte. on the development of scientific training and research 19th Nov., 1920; sec. to comte. on trade and taxation for Br. W. Africa, Oct. to Dec., 1921; priv. sec. to permanent under-sec., 3rd Oct., 1923; ag. prin., Jan., 1926.
ACLAND, FREDERICK A.—B. 1861; journalist, England, U.S.A., and Canada; apptd. sec. dept. of lab., Canada, 1907; deputy min. of lab., 1908; King's printer, 15th June, 1921.

ACTON, ROGER DAVID.—Cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1896; collr. of land rev., Perak, June, 1900; astt. sec. to res., Perak, Jan., 1903; dep. regist., sup. ct., Penang, May, 1907; ag. sheriff and deputy regist., Penang, Dec., 1907; deputy regist. and astt. offl. assignee, Apr., 1908; ag. 2nd mag., Singapore, Dec., 1910; promoted to Cls. III., Apr., 1911; 2nd mag., Singapore, Apr., 1911; ast. dist. judge, 1912; ag. regist., sup. ct., Penang, Oct., 1912; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, Oct., 1913; ag. audr.-gen., Feb., 1914; ag. senr. dist. offr., Prov. Wellesley, June, 1915; ag. censor, Penang, June, 1915; ag. solr.-gen., Oct., 1915; ag. dist. judge and 1st mag., Penang, Dec., 1916; ag. atty.-gen., June, 1920; dist. judge, Singapore, July, 1921; dist. judge and 1st mag., Penang, May, 1923; ag. puian judge, Jan., 1924; ag. judge, Johore, June, 1924.
ADAMS, HON. ALEXANDER SAMUEL.—B. 1861; ed., pub. schls. and Otago Univ., N.Z.; barrister and solr., 1883; crown solr., 1920; judge, N.Z. sup. ct., 1921.
ADAMS, C. H.—Astt. dist. commnr., E.A.P., 18th Dec., 1908; dist. commnr., Mar., 1917.

ADAMS, ERNEST, O.B.E. (1924), F.C.I.S. (Eng.) F.A.A. (Australia).—B. 1884; ed. Royal Orphanage, Wolverhampton; company sec., Sydney, N.S.W., 1908-1914; office of agt. gen. for N.S.W., London, 1915; liquidator of enemy firms, E.A. Prot., 1915; custodian of enemy property, occupied territory of German E. Africa, 1916 and Tanganyika territory, 1919; compt. of cust., and custodian of enemy property, Tanganyika, 18th Jan., 1923.

ADAMS, ERNEST VICTOR.—B. 1875; ed. at Cranleigh Schl. and City of London Coll.; served articles, Quan. Surv., London, 1891-1894; passed prelim. exam. Surv. Inst., 1896; Fellow of the Registd. Corp. Acctnts. (F.C.R.A.), 1915; tech. astt., accts. br., H.M. Office of Works, London, 1894-1896; acctnt. clk., civ.-engnr.-in-chf's. dept., Admiralty, in charge, corresp. br., ag. on various occasions as ch. surv., 1895-1900; transf. to Simon's Bay Dkdy. Extension, Cape Colony, as senr. acctnt. clk., in charge accts. and corresp., S.C.E.'s Office, 1900-1905; served as agt. in Simon's Bay Dkdy. Vol. Rifles (Boer War), 1901; read. comm., in Cape Gar. Artillery, Sept., 1902, prom. captain, Mar., 1905; ch. acctnt., Engnrs., India, 1906-1910; astt. sec., Engnrs., England, 1910-1912; acctnt. and oshr., Gold Coast Harbs., Accra and Sekondi, 1912-1918; rly. acctnt., in charge rly. and pub. wks. accts., Anglo-French occup., Togoland, 1918-1919; astt. rec.-gen., Gambia, 22nd May, 1919; senr. astt. recr. gen., 1st Jan., 1920; contrlr., food and fuel, July to Sept., 1920; J.P. and commnr., ct. of requests, 14th Sept., 1920; ag. rec.-gen., P.M.G., currency offr., and chmn., navigation and pilotage bd., July to Oct., 1920, and July, 1921 to Jan., 1922; mem., exec. and leg. couns., July to Oct., 1920 and Sept., 1921, to Jan., 1922; astt. acctnt., P.W.D., Gold Coast, 9th Dec., 1922.

ADAMS, THEODORE SAMUEL.—B. 1885; ed. King's Schl., Canterbury, and All Soul's Coll., Oxford, B.A.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1908; ag. supervisor of cust., Taiping, 1910-11; astt. dist. offr., Klang, 1911; dist. offr., Kuala Langat, 1912; dist. offr., Ulun Kelantan, 1914; supdt., lands, Kelantan, 1918; ag. 1st astt. sec., 1919; ag. 1st astt. sec. to govt., 1920; ch. astt. dist. offr., Kinta, 1920; ag. dist. offr., Ulun Selangor, July, 1922; ag. dist. offr., Larut, Sept., 1923; ag. regis. titles, Perak, and chmn. sany. bd., Larut and Selama, Dec., 1923; dist. offr., Larut and regis. titles, Perak, Nov., 1925.

ADAMS, THOMAS, F.S.I.—B. 1871; ed. Corstorphine and Edinburgh; pres., Young Scots' Soc., 1900; sec., Garden City Assoc., 1901-1904; sec. and man., First Garden City Co., 1903-1906; town planning adviser to Marquis of Salisbury, Earl of Lytton, Sir Richard Paget, Bt., and other landowners, 1906-1909; town planning inspr., L.G.B. of England and Wales, 1909-1914; examr. in civic design, Univ. of Liverpool, 1912-1914; rep. of L.G.B. on arterial roads; Confcs. of Greater London, 1913-1914; prep'd. offl. rept. on housing and town planning invstgtns. in U.S., Germany, Sweden, and other countries and on first town planning schemes in Britain, 1908-1918; organised cheap cottage exhibtn., Letchworth, 1905, and Wolverhampton, 1908; housing and town planning adviser to Canadian Govt. since 1914; hon. mem. Instn. of Mun. and Cnty. Engrns. (England); mem. of American Soc. of Mun. Improvements; pres. Town Planning Inst. of Britain, 1913-1914, and mem. of coun., 1914-1919; mem. bd. of govt., American City Planning Inst., 1915-1918; chmn., Town Planning Inst. of Canada, 1919.

ADAMSON, SIR JOHN ERNEST, KT. B.A.C. (1924), C.M.G. (1923).—B. 1867; ed. in London; Litt.D., M.A., London Univ.; principal, Normal Coll., Pretoria, 1902; dir. of educn., Transvaal, 1906; M.L.C., Transvaal; mem. of coun. of Cape University; dir. of educn., Transvaal Prov.; vice-chan., Univ. of S. Africa; master, Rhodes Univ. Coll., Oct., 1925.
ADDIS, WILLIAM.—B. 1906; ed. Rugby and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; astt. dist. commnr., Zanzibar, Feb., 1924.

ADDISON, D'ARCY WENTWORTH, M.V.O., I.S.O.—Ent. Tasmanian pub. serv., 1st Dec., 1887; clk. of exec. coun., 1903; sec. to premier since 1902; ch. electoral offr., 1914; mag., 1918; admtr. of charitable grants, 1914-19; sec. for neglected children's dept., 1914-19; state organiser, visit of H.R.H. Prince of Wales, 1920; under sec. for Tasmania.

ADDISON, JOSEPH BARTLETT, M.B.E. (1918), M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P.—Ed. at St. Mary's Hosp., London; asst. med. offr., Seychelles, 1907; chief med. offr., 1908; ag. priv. sec. and clk. to exec. coun. from May to Nov., 1912; mem., legis. and exec. couns., 1918; prin. civ. med. offr., Hong Kong, Feb., 1924; ag. mem., leg. coun., May, 1925 to Feb., 1926.
ADELAIDE, BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. ARTHUR NUTTER THOMAS, D.D., Cantab.—B. 1869; rector of Guisborough, Yorks., 1901-1906; bishop of Adelaide since 1906.

ADERS, WALTER MANSFIELD.—B. 1881; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Univ. Coll., London; Marburg Univ., Prussia, Ph.D. (science honours) 1902; engaged on research work, Marine Biological Assoc., Plymouth, 1903; asst. to pathologist, Egyptian govt. hosp., Cairo, and curator of pathological museum, 1906; res. offr. of rinderpest serum station, Abassia, Cairo, 1906; priv. asst. and scientific sec. to dir. of quarantine bd., Alexandria, 1907; detailed to El Tor for research work re cause of dysentery amongst pilgrims; research work with Prof. Vigueri Algiers (marine biology); research work, Welcom Laboratory, Khartoum, 1909; London Schl. of Trop. Med., 1910; research work, Zanzibar, since 1911; recd. thanks of S. of S. for foreign affairs and the Order of the Brilliant Star of Zanzibar, 2nd cls., for services rendered, 1912; astt. postal censor, 1914-1916; ch. censor, 1916-1918; lent to mily. authorities for work in Dar-es-Salaam and Kilwa, 1917; economic biologist, 1st June, 1917; at request of gov. of Tanganyika Territory proceeded to Dar-es-Salaam to advise on anti-malarial work, 1921; author of numerous papers and reports on scientific subjects.

AGAR, ARTHUR KIRWAN.—B. 1877; ed. at Brighton Coll., called to the bar, Grays Inn, May, 1919; temp. lieut. R.A.S.C., Oct., 1915; ag.-capt. R.A.S.C., Aug., 1916; staff capt., W.O., Feb.-Dec., 1919; dist. mag., dep. judge, ct. of summary juris., coroner, reg. of sup. ct., escheator gen., provost marshal, reg. gen., reg. of titles and legal adviser to govt. of Montserrat, 1st Oct., 1920; dist. comsnr., Belize, Br. Hond., Nov., 1923; ag.-atty-gen., 5th Dec., 1923 to 13th Apr., 1926; res. mag., Jamaica, 12th Oct., 1925.

AGBEBI, DENYI.—Ast. warder, Lagos prison, 1885; 4th cls., treas., 1886; examg. offr., customs, 3rd Aug., 1893; paymr., Ibadan detchmt., Hausa Force, 15th May, 1897; in conjunction, postmr. and clk. to res., Ibadan, 14th Dec., 1901, to Mar., 1903; audit clk., June, 1903.

AHEARNE, CHRISTOPHER DOMINIC.—B. 1886; ed. Christian Schls., Cork, and Trinity Coll., Dublin, scholar 1907; senr. moderator, B.A., 1909; cadet, S. Stilts., 1910; India, studying Tamil, 1911; ast. supt., Indian immigrants, Selangor, 1912; 1st ast. contrlr. of labr., Penang, 1913; dep. contrlr. of labr., Klang, 1914; dep. reg. sup. ot., Penang, 1916; dist. offr., Balik Pulau, 1916; ast. supt., govt. monopolies, S'pore, 1917; ast. reg. imp., and expts. (war dept.), 1918; offr., cls. iv., Jan., 1919; ast. adviser to govt. of Johore, Batu Pahat, 1919-21; 2nd mag., Singapore, Mar., 1923; dep. contr., lab., Kuala Lumpur, Nov., 1923; ag. dep. contr., lab., Penang, Dec., 1923 and July, 1925; ag. contr., lab., Malaya, in addn., Feb., 1926; ag. emigrn. comsnr. for Malaya, Madras, May, 1925.

AIKINS, SIR JAMES A. M., KNT. BACH. (1914), K.C.—B. 1861; ed. Upper Canada Coll., Univ. of Toronto; graduated in arts; called to the bar, Ontario, 1878; Manitoba 1879; Q.C., 1884; counsel for the C.P.R. Co. at Winnipeg, 1881-1911; pres. Canadian Bar Assoc., 1914-1916; lieut.-gov., Manitoba, 1917; re-apttd. for second term, 1921.

AINSWORTH, THOMAS WILLIAM.—B. 1891; ed. Leeds Gram. Schl. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1913; M.A., 1918; cadet, Hong Kong civ. ser., Nov., 1914; passed cadet, June, 1917; ag. sec., sanitary bd., 1917; seconded for mil. serv., 1917; ag. dep. reg. and appraiser, sup. ct., 1919; ag. 2nd astt. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1919; sec. to the industrial employment of children comsnr. in addn., 1921; J.P., 1922; 2nd pol. mag. and 3rd astt. sec. for Chinese affrs., 1922; astt. col. treas. and astt. assessor of rates, 1923; ch. astt., sec. for Chinese affrs., 1925.

AITKEN, JOHN, B.A., L.L.B. (1906).—B. 1885; ed. Gigglewick Coll. and Christ's Coll., Camb.; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1909; ast. dist. offr., S. Nigeria, 1913; station mag., Nigeria, 1915; crown counsel, Gold Coast, 3rd Nov., 1920; ag. solr.-gen., Dec., 1920 to Apr., 1921, July to Sept. and Oct. to Nov., 1921 and June to July, 1922; ag. puisne judge, Sept. to Oct., 1921, and from July to Sept., 1922; judge, sup. ct., Gambia, 1925.
AKBAR, MAAS THAJOON.—B. 1880; ag. crown coun., Ceylon, Aug., 1907; crown coun., June, 1909; ag. 2nd addnl. dist. judge, Colombo, and as solr.-gen. on several occasions; solr.-gen., Oct., 1924.

AKITOEY, DANIEL.—B. 1866; ed. at Lagos Gram. Schl.; copyist to Queen's Advocate, Lagos, Aug., 1886; was fourteen years reg. of instruments affecting land; dep. reg. of births, deaths, marriages, and aliens, Jan., 1896; ag. reg. at intervals; clk. to comsnr. of lands (now dir. of surveys), Apr., 1901.

ALBUQUERQUE, JOHN PEDROSO D', M.A., (Cantab.), F.I.C., F.C.S. (Lond.).—B. 1865; ed. City of London Schl. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; schl. nat. science tripso, part I., 1st cls., 1887, nat. science tripso, part II., 1st cls. (chem.), 1889; govt. prof. chem. and agrl. science, Barbados, 1890; consulting chem., Impl. dept. of agr. for W. Indies, and chem. in charge, sugar cane experiments, 1900; ag. comsnr., Impl., dept. of agr. for W. Indies on several occasions between 1900-1909; ag., dir., agr., Barbados, 1st Apr., 1920; dir., science and agr., Barbados, 1st Sept., 1925.

ALBURY, G. A.—B. 1865; entd. Bahamas civ. serv., 1898; Out Island comsnr. (1st div.), 1909; sisal inspr., pro tem., Jan., 1919; ag. stip. and circuit mag. for various periods from 1919-1923; ag. stip. and circuit mag. in 1923, 1924 and 1926.
ALBURY, JOSEPH JOHNSON.—B. 1881; Out Island comsnr. (4th div.), Bahamas, 1910; 3rd. div. comsnr., Jan., 1918.
ALBURY, STANLEY VICTOR STRATHMERE.—B. 1873; tidewaiter, Bahamas, 1901; clk., post office, 1907; Out Island comsnr. (3rd div.), 1911; (2nd div.), 1912; supt. of hospital, 1917.

ALBURY, WILSON GRANT.—B. 1877; public school teacher, Bahamas, 1896; resigned, 1906; re-apttd., 1909; head-master, boys' central school, 1914; inspr. of schools, 1915; ag. S. and C. mag., 8th June to 5th July and from 17th July, 1926.

ALCOCK, WILLIAM D. E.—B. 1892; ed. King Edward's schl., Birmingham, and Birmingham Univ.; B.A., 1913 (1st class, two distinctions); cadet, Fiji, Jan., 1915; dist. comsnr., Savusavu, Kadavu, Colo East, Lautoka and Bua, Nov., 1916 to Jan., 1920; qual. in law, Oct., 1917; astt. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1920.

ALEXANDER, CHARLES SHULDHAM, M.A., Cantab.—B. 1877; cadet, F.M.S., 1900; ag. collr., land rev., Matang, Feb., 1901 to Jan., 1902; ag. ass't dist. treas., Kuala Lumpur, Apr., 1902; ag. dist. treas., Telok Anson, June, 1904; ag. dist. treas., Batu Gajah, Sept., 1906 to Nov., 1906; ag. state treas., Perak, Feb., 1908; ag. ass't treas., F.M.S., Apr.-Oct., 1912 and June, 1913; ag. treas., F.M.S., June-Oct., 1914 and Nov., 1921; finan. comsnr., Johore, Oct., 1914; finan. comsnr. and aud. gen., Johore, Jan., 1919; ag. finan. adviser, F.M.S. in add., Jan.-Aug., 1924 and May, 1926; treas., F.M.S., Feb., 1924; finan. adviser and treas., F.M.S., Aug., 1926.

ALEXANDER, EDWARD BRUCE C.M.G. (1925).—B. 1872 ed.; Forest Schl., and Trin. Coll., Oxford (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Dec., 1896; offi. asst. to govt. agt., S. Prov., Aug., 1897; pol. mag., Panadure, Aug., 1899; asst. govt. agt., Matale, Dec., 1900; Kegalle, Oct., 1903; seconded addl. supt. of pol., Cent. Prov., May, 1905; supt. of pol., S. Prov., July, 1906; ag. inspr.-gen. of pol., Mar.-Nov., 1907, and Dec., 1908 to Jan., 1909; supt. of pol., Cent. Prov., Nov., 1907; asst. govt. agt., Puttalam, June, 1910; ag. govt. agt., Sabaragamuwa, Aug., 1911; chmn., mun. coun., and mayor of Colombo, Apr., 1913; on military duty Sept., 1914; govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Apr., 1919; ag. contr., rev. and ag. food contr., Mar., 1920; addtl. contr. of rev., Sept., 1921; ag. prin. collr. of cust., Oct., 1921; govt. agt., W. Prov., Nov., 1921; ag. contr., rev., Nov., 1922 to Feb., 1923; addtl. contr., rev., Feb. to May, 1923; ag. contr., rev., May to Aug., 1923; contr., rev., 21st Aug., 1923; ag. col. sec., 1st Apr. to 17th Oct., 1926, 30th Nov., 1926 to 29th Jan., 1926, and from 18th Oct., 1926; O.A.G., 18th Oct. to 29th Nov., 1925.
ALEXANDER, JOHN EDWARD — Amt. land ranger, E.A.P., Oct., 1912; lands offr., Nyassaland, 21st July, 1922.

ALLAN, KENNETH BRUCE, M.B., B.S. (Durham) Certif., London Schl. of Trop. Med. and Coll. of Med., Durham Univ.—Ed. Denstone Coll., Staffs.; med. offr., W.A.M.S., Gold Coast, May, 1911; temp. lieut., R.A.M.C., 1916-17; transf'd as capt. on gen. list and attd. 1st K.A.R., Nyassaland Field Force, July, 1917 to Jan., 1919; med. offr., W.A.M.S., Gambia, Mar., 1923.

ALLAN, PETER, M.B., Ch.B., D.P.H., Edin. M.D. (1920).—Res. med. offr., Royal Victoria Hosp. for consumption, Edinburgh, 1911-12; clin. asst., med. out-patient dept., Royal Infirmary, Edinburgh, 1912; asst. tuberculosis offr., boroughs of Paddington and Kensington, 1912-13; 1st med. supt., Romley Hill Sanatorium, Birmingham, 1913-1920; capt., R.A.M.O., 1917-18; med. inspr., dept. of pub. health, Union of South Africa, 1920; med. supt., tuberculosis sanatorium, Nelspoort, C.P., 1923; author of "An investigation into the increased mortality from pulmonary tuberculosis, 1914-1920."

ALLARD, JULES.—B. 1869; ed. Nicolet Coll., Quebec; lawyer; elec. to legis. assem., Quebec, 1897, 1900, 1904; app'td. to legis. coun. and min. of pub. wks., Quebec, 1905; min. of agric., 1907; min. of lands and forests, 1909-1917; re-elec. to legis. assem. at bye-election 1910 and g.e., 1912; re-called to legis. coun., 1916; prothonotory of the superior ct., Montreal, 1919.

ALLARDYCE, SIR W. L., K.C.M.G. (1916), C.M.G. (1902), Knt. of Grace of the Order of St. John of Jerusalem (1916).—B.1861; clk.and interp., Provincial Dept., Fiji, 1879; transf'd to Rotuman, 1882; stip. mag., July, 1882; asst. native comsnr. and stip. mag. and inspr. of native taxa, Kadavu, Jan., 1890; mem. exec. coun., Nov., 1893; native lands comsnr. and mem. legis. coun., 1894; native comsnr., 1896; asst. col. sec. and receiv.-gen., Dec., 1898; dep'y gov., 14-20 Feb., 1901; adminstrd. govt., 18th July, 1901, to 9th Sept., 1902; col. sec. and receiv.-gen., 18th July, 1902; gov., Falkland Is., 1904; gov., Bahamas, 28th Dec., 1914; assumed govt. 15th June, 1915; gov., Tasmania, 1920; editor of native newspaper "Na Mata," 1890-99; author of a digest of Native Regulations, 1877-1900; attended first Fed. Conf. on Education, 1907; gov., Newfoundland, 1st Sept., 1922; assumed gov., 1st Dec., 1922.

ALLDER, WM. HOWELL.—B. 1873; 5th clk., C.S.O., Barbados, 1892; 4th do., 1898; 3rd do., 1900; 2nd cls. clk., P.O., 1904; ch. clk., do., 1908; ch. clk., audit off., 1913; acted on several occasions as clk. of the exec. coun. and comtece., as acctnt. in P.O., and as auditor-gen.; sec. to Barbados quarantine comtece., 1903; to West Indian quarantine confere., 1904, and to confere. on trade relations between West Indies and Canada, 1910; sec., Barbados civ. serv. comtece., 1920.

ALLEN, F.T.—Enlisted astrooper in Rhodesian field force, 1900, and served in S. African war; invalided home 1901; obtained comsnr. as 2nd lieut. in 5th Batt. Royal Irish Rifles, Dec., 1901, and returned to S. Africa until end of war; passed Hythe musketry and machine gun course, 1903; military course of instruction, 1906; captain, May, 1908; dist. supt. of police, Uganda Prot., July, 1907; cadet course, Royal Irish Constabulary, 1907; supt. of police, Uganda, 1911.
ALLEN, H.—Tempy. asst. dist. comsnr., K.A.P., Apr., 1919; asst. treas., Uganda, Aug., 1920.

ALLEN, H. T.—B. 1879; app'td., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O. Mar., 1898; conf. clk., Dec., 1907; also clk. to concessions and finance comtece., Sept., 1910 to June, 1916; promoted, under order-in-council of 1910, 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 16th June, 1916; asst. sec. to trade relations comtece., Aug., 1917; sec., Dependencies of Falkland Islands comtece., Jan., 1918; sec., raw materials board, Dec., 1918; asst. priv. sec. to sec. of state (Lord Milner), 1st Jan., 1920; prin., 1st Apr., 1920.

ALLEN, HON. SIR JAMES, G.C.M.G. (1926), K.C.B. (1917), M.A., Camb.—B.1855; entd. New Zealand Parlmt., 1887; lieut.-col. in command Otago Division, New Zealand Garrison Artillery, 1902; min. of defence, 1912; min. of finance, min. of stamp duties and min. of educn., 1913-15; min. of finance and min.of external affairs, 1919-20; high comsnr. in England, 1920-26.
ALLEN, LEONARD GILBERT.—B.1901; entered C.O., 1917; app'td., after exam., asst. clk., 23rd Apr., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920.

ALLEN, LUCIEN ARTHUR.—B. 1888; ed. Merchant Taylors' Schl. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1910; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1912; ag. asst. dist. offr., Batang Padang, Dec., 1913; ag. 2nd asst. dist. offr., Tapah, Feb., 1915; ag. sec. to res., Negri Sembilan, Apr., 1915 to Sept., 1918; passed cadet, June, 1915; asst. dist. offr., Kuantan, Sept., 1918 to Aug., 1919; asst. dist. offr., Lower Perak, June, 1920; Brit. req., Brunei, Mar., 1921; asst. dist. offr., Ipoh, Jan., 1924; ag. ch. asst. dist. offr., Kinta, Mar., 1924; dep. pub. proc., Negri Sembilan and Pahang, Dec., 1924; ag. asst. andr.-gen., F.M.S. Oct., 1925; ag. sec. to res., Selangor, Nov., 1925; ag. offr. assignee and pub. trustee, F.M.S., Sept., 1926.

ALLEN, PERCY TOTHILL.—B. 1878; ed. at Highgate Sch., Christ's Coll., Camb., (honors class. tripes, 1899); cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1902; ag. 2nd asst. prot. of Chinese, Penang, Sept., 1905; ag. asst. dist. offr., Matang, June, 1906; ag. dist. offr. Christmas Is., Nov., 1907 and Nov., 1909; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese and supt. of prisons, Penang, Feb., 1909; ag. 2nd asst. dist. offr., Tapah, Apr., 1909; ag. mag., Seremban, Apr., 1912; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese and munic. comsnr., Penang, Oct., 1913; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Jan. and May, 1917; ag. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Feb., 1917; ag. insp. of prisons, S.S. in add., Jan., 1918; ag. rev. aud., Sel., N. S. and Pahang, April, 1919; ag. prot. of Chinese, Selangor and Negri Sembilan, June, 1919; ag. dist. offr., Ulu Selangor Sect., 1919; ag. state treas., Selangor and asst. treas., F.M.S., Mar., 1920; ag. sec. for Chinese affairs, S.S. and temp. off. mem., leg. coun., S. S., May, 1920; ag. regar., titles, Perak and supk., convict estab., Taiping, Sept., 1922; ag. dist. offr., Larut, in add., May, 1923; ag. chmn., sany. bd., Kuala Lumpur, Dec., 1923 and July, 1924; asst. audr.-gen., F.M.S., Nov., 1924; sec., res., Perak, Mar., 1925; regar., companies and offl. assignee, S.S., 1925; attd., Chinese sect., July, 1926.

ALLEYNE, JAMES DOUGLAS, M.D. (Tuft's Univ., U.S.A.)—Ag. pol. med. offr., dist. A., Barbados and visiting physician, leper asylum, 1917; asst. port health offr., 1919; ag. asst., lunatic asylum, 1919; port health offr., 1921; ex officio mem., quarantine board.

ALLIES, E. J.—B. 1891; apptd. asst. clk., Scottish Educn. Dept., 1910; apptd. after compet. exam. clk. of the 2nd divn. of the civ. serv. to National Insurance Audit Dept., 1913; on mily. serv., 1917-19; assigned to the C.O., Aug., 1919; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922.

ALLISON, WALTER EDWARD, A.M.Inst.T.—Mech. engnr., Br. Honduras rly., Aug., 1913; supt. and traffic man., 1914-18; seconded to German E. Africa (Tanganyika Territory), as asst. traffic man., Jan., 1919.
ALLSOP, HERBERT HENRY.—B. 1885; German East Africa campaign, 1914-16; asst. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1916; 1st grade admstr. offr., 1st Aug., 1922.

ALPERS, HON. OSCAR THOBWALD JOHAN, M.A., LL.B.—B. 1867; ed. Kelakov's Real Skole, Copenhagen, Napier (N.Z.) Pub. Schl. and Canterbury Univ. Coll.; asst. mast., Christchurch Boys High Schl. and asst. prof. of Eng. lit., Canterbury Univ. Coll., 1891; barrister, 1905; judge, New Zealand sup. ct., 1925; author of "Canterbury Rhymes" (1900), "New Zealand in the 19th Century" (1902) and "College Rhymes" (1923).

ALUWIHARE, RICHARD.—B. 1895; on mily. serv., Oct., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Oct., 1920; attd. to Jaffna kach., Oct., 1920; office asst. to dir. of agr., Feb., 1922; ag. pol. mag., Dandaganuwa, Oct., 1923; pol. mag., Pt. Pedro, Nov., 1923; ditto, Panadure, Nov., 1924.
AMERASINGHE, SAMSON FELIX, B.A., Cantab.—B. 1902; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., July, 1926; attd., Kalutara kach., Aug., 1926.

AMERY, LIEUT.-COL. L.C.M.S.—B. 1873; ed. at Harrow and Balliol Coll., Oxford; late Fellow of All Soul's, Oxford; Ouseley Scholar of Imp. Inst. in Turkish; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1902; on editorial staff of The Times, 1899-1909; served during the war in Flanders, Salonika, and the Near East; on the staff of the Impl. War Coun. at Versailles, and as asst. sec. to the War Cabinet and Impl. Cabinet to Jan., 1919; under-sec. of state for the colonies, 14th Jan., 1919; acted as sec. of state during Lord Milner's absence in Egypt, Nov., 1919 to Mar., 1920; First Lord of the Admry., Oct., 1922; S. of S. for the Cols., 7th Nov., 1924; capt. 14th 1st B'h'm Battn., Royal Warwickshire Regt., 1914; lieut.-col. on general staff, 1917; M.P. (U) for S. Birmingham, May, 1911; ditto Sparkbrook div., Dec., 1918; edited "The Times History of the South African War"; author of "Problem of the Army," 1903; "Fundamental Fallacies of Free Trade," 1906; "The Great Question" (with J. M. Robertson, M.P.), 1909; "Union and Strength," 1912.

AMYOT, LT.-COL. JOHN A., C.M.G., M.D., D.A., D.M.S.—Sanitation, second Canadian divn. area, 1914; O.C., san. sec., 1st Canadian divn., France, ch. adviser, san., with Canadian army in France, 1916; twice ment. in despatches; dep. min., dept. of health, Canada, 1919.
ANDERSON, C. E.—Asst. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1st Nov., 1916; A.P.O., cadet, 25th Aug., 1920; confirmed in appt. as A.P.O., 1st Jan., 1921.

ANDERSON, CHARLES OSBORNE.—B. 1868; 2nd. clk. and letter carrier, P.O., Bahamas, 1890; chief clk., 1890; ag. res. just., Inagua, 1896; ag. warehouse keeper and examg. offr., 1906; ag. postmtr., 1907, 1909, 1910; ag. aud. of pub. acct., 1910; postmtr., 1913; mem. H. of A.; M.L.C., 10th Jan., 1924.
ANDERSON, E. L. B.—Asst. dist. commr., E.A.P., Oct., 1919; pvtc. sec. to sq. gov., 1925.

ANDERSON, GEORGE BARTLET.—B. 1880; ed. Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen; clk., acct.-gen'l.'s dept., G.P.O., London, 1896; clk., war office, Jan., 1900; 2nd cls. clk., G.P.O., Johannesburg, Dec., 1902; transf'd. to exec. and legis. councils dept.; 2nd cls. clk., June, 1903; 1st cls. clk., July, 1905; acct.'s clk. and cashier, Transvaal agt.-general's office, London, Oct., 1907; asst. acctnt., Apl., 1908; clk., P.W.D., Nyasaland, June, 1909; chief clk., secretariat, Apl., 1914; ag. asst. chief sec., Aug., 1916; on re-organisation of secretariat apptd. 2nd asst. sec., Aug., 1919; ag. 1st asst. sec., Aug., 1919; ag. ch. sec., June to Nov., 1920; admin. offr., 1st grade, July, 1922; ag. 1st asst. sec. Mar. to Nov., 1922.

ANDERSON, HON. GEORGE JAMES.—B. 1860; ed. Lawrence Gram. Schl.; mem., N.Z. parl. since 1908; chmn. labour bills comtee., five years; chmn., library comtee., eight years; min. of internal affairs, 1920-21; min. of lab., 1921; min. of mines, 1921; min. of marine, 1922.

ANDERSON, MAXWELL HENDRY.—C.B.E. (mill.) (1919); O.B.E. (1918); K.C., Aug., 1919; capt., R.N. (ret.); entered Royal Navy, 1893; ret. at own request, 1912; reed. thanks of Admry. for valuable series of magnetic observations carried out in the Pacific Ocean; instructor, R.N. Coll., Portsmouth, 1908-10; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1909; younger brother of Trinity House, 1910; rejoined Navy on outbreak of war; employed on special services; reed. thanks of Admry. for services rendered to naval prize fund and at courts martial; specially promoted capt., Apl., 1919; govt. mem., central (unemployed) body for London, 1913 to 1919; atty.-gen., Gibraltar, 9th May, 1919; ag. chief just., and col. sec. on several occasions.

ANDERSON, ROBERT MOWBRAY.—Entd. govt. printing dept., St. Vincent, 6th Feb., 1886; chief govt. printer, July, 1894; steward, col. hosp., Aug., 1901; mem. of comtee. to inquire into admin. of poor relief, Aug., 1904; clk. to regir. sup. ct. and regist. of B., M. and D. for dist. I., Nov., 1904; temp. ast. landing waiter for spec. excise duties, Nov., 1904; edited first "Illustrated Handbook of St. Vincent," 1907.
ANDERSON, R. T. H.—Ast.-supt. of police, E.A.F., 1911; supt., pol., Kenya, 1920.

ANDERSON, THOMAS EDWARD.—B. 1877; excise offr., B. Honduras, Dec., 1902; 2nd grade customs and excise offr., July, 1905; ag. 1st grade customs and excise offr., from April, 1904; confirmed March, 1906, rlyw. acctnt., 1st Aug., 1909; on completion of rlyw. construction reverted to substantive appt., 7th Sept., 1910; ag. chief clk., etc., treasy. and customs dept., 20th May to 7th Sept., 1911.
ANDERSON, THOMAS JOHN, M.A., B.Sc., (Hons. Edin.) F.Z.S., F.S.E.—Entomologist, agric. dept., Kenya, May, 1903.

ANDREW, ROBERT CHARLES.—Ed. Liverpool Inst., Harper-Adams Agric. Coll., Newport, and Liverpool Univ.; B.Sc. (agric., London); National Diploma in agric.; mem. (by exam.) of Surv'r.'s Instn.; lecturer under the East Sussex county council, 1905 to 1910; foreign experience, 1911; ast. supt. of agric., N. Nigeria, 24th April, 1912.

ANGLIN, Rt. Hon. FRANCIS ALEXANDER, P.C. (1925).—B. 1886; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Montreal, and Univ. of Ottawa; called to the bar, 1888; K.C. 1902; apptd. sen. puisne judge, exchequer div., high ct. of just., Ontario, 1904; puisne judge, supreme ct. of Canada, 1909; ch. just., sup. ct., Canada, 1924; dep. of gov.-gen.

ANNETTS, HERBERT HENRY.—B. 1888; Ed. Magdalen Coll. sch., Oxford, and Worcester Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1910; ast. master, preparatory schl., Repton, 1910-1911; served in Oxfordshire educm. comtee's office, and inspr. of evening schls., Oxfordshire county council, 1911-1912; apptd. junr. supt., educn. dept., Northern Nigeria, 12th Sept., 1912; senr. supt., 1st Jan., 1913; ag. dir. educn., 4th Apr., 1920; senr. supt., 1st Jan., 1919; dep. dir., 1st Jan., 1920; ag. dir., 1920 and 1922.

ANSON, MAJOR-GENERAL SIR ARCHIBALD EDWARD HARBORD, R.A., C.M.G. (1876), K.C.M.G. (1882).—B. 1826; entered the royal mil. acad., Woolwich, 1841; presented with sword for exemplary conduct; 2nd lieut., R.A., June, 1844; 1st lieut., 1st Apr., 1846; 2nd capt., 9th July, 1852; 1st capt., 1st Sept., 1856; brevet major, 21st July, 1864; lieut.-col., 12th May, 1866; brevet col., 12th May, 1874; col., 23rd Dec., 1875; ret. with the hon. rank of maj.-gen., 26th August, 1879; served at the siege of Sebastopol in 1856; Crimean medal and clasp, 5th class Medjidie, and Turkish medal; inspr.-gen. of pol., Mauritius, Sept., 1858; was employed in 1862 as the responsible mem. of the Br. mission to the court of Radama II. of Madagascar, on the occasion of his coronation; lieut.-gov. of Prince of Wales Is., Feb., 1867; admtd. govt. Str. Stilimta, 1871, 1877, and 1879; conducted the successful military operations for repelling the invasion of the state of Sungeli Ujong, 1875-76 (medal and clasp); ret. 1882; J.P. for Sussex.

ANTHONY, EDWIN AUGUSTUS.—Clk., ch. mag.'s office, Tembuland and Transkei, 2nd May, 1894; clk. and dept. audr. of rev., atty.-gen.'s office, 28th Mar., 1897; examr. of accs., July 1897; ag. ch. clk., pol. branch, 1st Mar. to 30th Apr., 1902; ag. bookkeeper, guardian's fund, master's office, from 16th Jan., 1903; ag. 1st clk., Simon's Town, 14th to 29th Sept., 1904; clk., civ. commnr., Cape, 30th Sept., 1904; ch. paym'ts. office, treasy., 1st July, 1908; regir. of friendly societies and treasy. rep., Cape Town, Sept., 1918; civ. commnr., Cape, Mar., 1921.

ANTHONY, PHILIP ARNOLD, C.M.G. (1918), M.I.C.E.—B. 1873; ed. at Mill Hill Schl.; served in engineering dept., G.W. Rly., 1894-1910; gen. man. and chief engr., F.M.S. Rlys., 1910; services lent to ministry of munitions, May, 1915 to Nov., 1916, resumed duty in F.M.S., Feb., 1917; services lent temporarily to govt. of South Aust., Apr., 1918; resumed duty in F.M.S., Jan., 1919; ret., Dec., 1924; reported on Palestine rly., 1925; ag. ch. engr. (design), C.A., Nov., 1926 to Dec., 1926.

ANTROBUS, MAURICE E., B.A.—B. 1895; ed. Winchester, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; math. exhib., Trinity Coll., Dec., 1913; 2nd lieut., 6th King's Royal Rifle Corps, Aug., 1914; lieut., June, 1915; capt., Jan., 1917; served in Flanders, Salonica and France, 1915-19; 2nd cls. math. tripes, pt. I., 1919; 2nd cls. hist. tripes., pt. II., 1920; B.A., 1920; temp. 2nd cls. clk., C.O., Aug., 1920; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, ast. prin., 7th Feb., 1921; ast. pte. sec. to S. of S., 2nd Nov., 1926.

ANTROBUS, SIR REGINALD L., K.C.M.G. (1911), C.B. (1898).—B. 1863; ed. at Winchester and at New Coll., Oxford; scholar of New Coll., 1872; 1st clas in classical mods., 1874; 2nd clas in final classical school, 1876; B.A., 1876; apptd., after an open compet. exam., to be a clk. in the C.O., May 3rd, 1877; ast. priv. sec. to the Earl of Kimberley, 6th Dec., 1880, to Dec., 1882; priv. sec. to the Earl of Derby, 16th Dec., 1882, to 24th June, 1886; to Col. the Right Hon. F. A. Stanley, M.P. (afterwards Earl of Derby), 24th June, 1886, to 6th Feb., 1886; and to Earl Granville, 6th Feb. to 3rd Aug., 1886; adminstr. the govt. of St. Helena, 5th Nov., 1889, to 8th June, 1890; priv. sec. to Mr. Meade, 1st Feb., 1892, and to Mr. S. Buxton, M.P., 17th Aug., 1892; sen. clk., Jan., 1894; privo. clk., 1896; ast. under-secr. of state Oct., 1898; crown agent for the gold, 1909; retired, 1918.

APPLEGATE, F. W.—B. 1887; ed. Bath Coll. and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1911; ast. dist. commnr., Gold Coast, Apr., 1915; junr. ast. col. sec., 13th May to 30th Sept., 1915; travelling commnr., Nasawm, 1st Oct., 1915 to 31st May, 1916, and from 12th Apr., 1917; ast. dist. commnr., Kwahu, 1919.

ARCHER, SIR GEOFFREY FRANCIS, K.C.M.G. (1920), C.M.G. (1913).—Ast. collr., East Africa Prot., 1st Dec., 1902; attached secretariat, June, 1902 to Mar., 1903; dist. commnr., 1st Apr., 1907; spec. serv., Northern Frontier, East Africa, 1909; offr.-in-charge, May to Dec., 1911; awarded Cuthbert Peak Grant by the R.G.S., 1918, for surveys in E. Africa connecting Major Gwynn's Abyssinian triangulation with the triangulation of E. Africa; transf'd. to Somaliland Prot., June, 1912; admtd. govt., July to Oct., 1912, and from June to Oct., 1913; dep. commnr., 31st Mar., 1913; commnr. and comdr.-in-chief, Somaliland Prot., 15th May, 1914; assumed govt., 22nd May, 1914; present at operations against the Dervishes at Shimbere Berris, Feb., 1915 (African General Service Medal and clasp); member of British Mission on occasion of coronation of the Empress Zauditu of Abyssinia, Feb., 1917 (Star of Ethiopia, 1st class); gov., Somaliland Prot., 4th Nov., 1919; directed operations against the Mullah, 1920, resulting in complete destruction of Dervish power in Somaliland; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Uganda Prot., 6th Sept., 1923; gov., Sudan, 1924.

ARCHER, NORMAN E.—B. 1892; ed. R.N. Colls., Osborne and Dartmouth; midshipman in Mediterranean and Home Fleets, 1909-12; sub-lieut. (six 1st cls. certif.), Sept., 1912; lieut., June, 1913; H.M.S. Warrior in Mediterranean and Grand Fleets, 1912-15; qualified, specialist course, ast torpedo lieut., 1915; served with Russian Navy in Black Sea, &c., 1915-17 (Order of St. Stanislas, 3rd cls.); passed exam. as Russian interpreter, 1918; intell. divn. admly., H.M.S. Lowestoft in Adriatic, minelaying squadron, Grand Fleet, and H.M.S. Malaya, 1917-20; apptd. under reconstruction scheme ast. prin., min. of lab., Sept., 1920; temp. attd. to C.O. as ast. prin., Dec., 1921.
ARCHER, W. J.—Supt., govt. printing office, Cyprus, 27th May, 1902.
ARCHIBALD, CHARLES GEORGE.—B. 1869; entd. Trinidad civ. ser., Jan., 1892; cashier, Trinidad govt. rly., 1906; acctnt., 1919.

ARCHIBALD, EDGAR S., B.A., B.S.A.—B. 1885; recd. pmry. educm. at Yarmouth pub. schl. and Yarmouth acad.; grad. Acadia Univ., 1908; also grad. from the Nova Scotia rhet. schl. in the same year, and from Nova Scotia agric. coll. in 1906; recd. deg. of Bach. of Scienc. Agric. from the Ontario agric. coll., 1908; inst. of agric. and expmtlst. at the Nova Scotia agric. coll., 1908; prof. of agric. and farm supt. of the same coll. in 1910; entd. the serv. of the Fed. Dept. of Agric. in June, 1912, as Dominion animal husbandman at the Cent. Expmtl. Farm, Ottawa; ag. dir. of Expmtl. Farms, 1918-1919; dir., June, 1919.

ARCHIBALD, MUNGO TENNENT, M.C.—B. 1885; ed. Glasgow (M.A.), and Pemb. Coll., Oxford; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1909; offr. astt. to govt. agt., N. Prov., May, 1910; ditto, Batticaloa, May, 1911; pol. mag., Kurunegala, May, 1912; astt. land settm. offr., Jan., 1913; special commr. in connection with riots, June, 1915; astt. land settm. offr., Aug., 1915; on military duty, 1916; astt. land settm. offr., Aug., 1919; astt. govt. agt., Puttalam, Dec., 1922; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, Apr., 1923; astt. stimlnt. offr., July, 1923; ag. stimlnt. offr., June, 1924 to Sept., 1925, and from 1st Oct., 1925.

ARMBRISTER, PERCY W. D.—B. 1862; ag. regisr., prothonotary, and clk. of Crown, Bahamas, 1884; ag. ch. clk., col. sec.'s dept., 1886; clk., pol. ct., Nassau, 1887; J.P., 1890; res. mag. and collr. of rev., Abaco, 1890, Eleuthera, 1894, Harbour Is., 1896, Inagua, 1896; ag. stip. and circuit mag., Bahamas, and judge of ct. of com. pleas, 1896 and 1897; regisr. of cts., prothonotary, and clk. of Crown, Jan., 1897; Out Island commr., 1909; recrv.-gen. and treas., 1916; ag. col. sec. for various periods between 1919 and 1926; ag. dep. gov. and senr. mem. of exec. coun. from 9th to 11th Mar., 1922; ag. dep. gov. from 25th to 28th Feb., 1924 and from 8th to 12th Aug., 1926.

ARMITAGE, CAPT. SIR CECIL HAMILTON, K.B.E. (1926), C.M.G. (1911), D.S.O. (1901).—Astt. inspr., G. Coast constab., 1894; served in Ashanti exped., 1896-6 (star); Northern Territories (Neutral Zone), 1897 (medal and clasp); inspr., 1898; priv. sec. to gov. of G. Coast and clk. of leg. and exec. couns., 1899-1900; Ashanti campaign, 1900 (ag. res. during siege of Coomassie, D.S.O., medal and clasp); trav. commr., 1901; commr. of S. Province of Ashanti, July, 1901; comdt. of Ashanti Mines volunteers with rank of major, Nov., 1905; ag. chief commr. of Ashanti, Oct., 1901, to end of year, Apr. to Oct., 1906, Dec., 1907, June, 1908, and from June to Dec., 1909; chief commr. of Northern Territories, 28th Jan., 1910; gov., Gambia, 20th Nov., 1920; assumed govt., 3rd Jan., 1921.

ARMSTRONG, J. R.—B. 1881; clk., defence dept., Union of S. Africa, Nov., 1913; clk. High Commr.'s office, S. Africa, Jan., 1915; apptd. to Swaziland estab., Jan., 1919; ag. conf. clk. to gov.-gen. on various occasions from 1916-1919; astt. sec. (in S. Africa) to Lord Cave's commr. on S. Rhodesia, 1919; senr. clk., 1925.

ARMSTRONG, ROBERT.—B. 1879; ed. Friends' Schl., Great Ayton, Yorks and Durham Univ.; diploma in agric., Armstrong Coll., Newcastle-upon-Tyne, in Univ. of Durham; lecturer, schl. of agric., Egyptian govt., 1906-1910; lecturer and organiser in agric. for the Hampshire County Council; astt. dir. of agric., Zanzibar, 6th Feb., 1913; ag. dir. of agric., 28th Oct., 1915 to 13th Aug., 1916; ag. chief supply offr., 16th Sept. to 15th Nov., 1918, and 30th Mar., 1919; ag. adminstr.-gen., 10th Nov., 1919 to 22nd Jan., 1920; ag. dir., agr., 30th Mar., 1919 to 13th Jan., 1920, and 30th Nov., 1923 to 10th May, 1924; ag. dir. of agr. and ch. supply offr., 7th Feb. to 15th Nov., 1923; ag. dir., agr. and offl. mem., leg. coun., 23rd Jan. to 17th July, 1926.

ARDNT, CARL EVAN.—B. 1896; on mily. serv., Jan., 1916; cadet, Ceylon, Oct., 1920; attd. to Anuradhapura Kachcheri, Oct., 1920; ag. pol. mag., Puttalam, July, 1921; ag. pol. mag., Negombo, Aug., 1922; extra office astt., Kandy kachcheri, Nov., 1923; off. astt., Puttalam kach., Jan., 1925; dep. rubber contr., Apr., 1926.

ARNETT, EDWARD JOHN, C.M.G. (1924), B.A. (1898).—B. 1876; ed. St. Paul's schl. and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; classical trip; called to the bar, 1912; C.A. office, 1902; rev. offr., N. Nigeria, 1903; 3rd cls. res., 1904; 2nd cls. res., 1909; 1st cls. res., 1912; ag. lieut.-gov., N. Prova., Nigeria, Oct., 1920.

ARNOLD, 1ST BARON (creat. 1924), RT. HON. SYDNEY ARNOLD.—B. 1878; was partly priv. sec. to the pres. of the bd. of educn. and to the finan. sec. to the treasy., 1914; lib. M.P. for Holmfirth and later for Penistone, 1912-21; partly under sec. for the cols., Jan., 1924 to 7th Nov., 1924.

ARTHUR, JAMES STARTIN WILLS.—B. 1881; ed. at Marlborough Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; B.A. (1904); cadet, S. Stiltnze., Jan., 1905; ag. 2nd astt. prot. of Chinese, Penang, Sept., 1907; ag. astt. treas., Singapore, June, 1909; 2nd astt. prot. of Chinese, Penang, July, 1910; attached to col. audit office, Feb.-Mar., 1911; ag. 2nd astt. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, July, 1911; ag. dist. offr., Christmas Is., Aug., 1911; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, May, 1912; ag. astt. P.M.G., Penang, Sept., 1912; ag. astt. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Mar., 1915; offr., cls. IV, May, 1916; seconded as ast. adviser, Kedah, May, 1916; ag. temp. as adviser in addn., Feb.-Mar., 1917; offr., cls. III, Jan., 1919; offr., cls. II, Dec., 1921; ag. collr., land rev., Penang, May, 1922; dist. judge, Penang, Oct., 1922; dep. pres. mun. commrs., Penang, May, 1923; dist. judge, Malacca, Jan., 1924.
ARUMUGUM, JOSEPH NALLIAH.—B. 1896; cadet, Ceylon div. serv., Dec., 1921; attd., Anuradhapura kach., Dec., 1921; itg. pol. mag., W. Prov., May, 1924.

ASHTON, DANIEL ASPINALL.—B. 1869; ed. at Manchester gram. schl. and Owens Coll.; M.B., B.Ch., Victoria Univ.; civ. surg., S. African war, 1901-1902 (medal and three clasps); col. med. offr., S. Nigeria, Jan., 1903; served in Cross River expedit., 1904 (medal and clasp); med. offr. at Abe and Obubura.
ASHTON, HUGH.—B. 1878; served in S. African War; sub-impr., Basutoland Mounted Pol., 1906; impr., 1914; served in European War with native labour contingent; astt. comntr., Basutoland, 1923.

ASHWORTH, HENRY TWEEDALE, M.M.—B. 1835; apptd., after compet. exam., clk., G.P.O., 1902; on mily. serv., Oct., 1914 to May, 1919; served in Gallipoli, Egypt (Canal Defences) and France; passed supplementary clk. exam., Jan., 1921; services loaned to C.O., 1st May, 1922; apptd. cler. offr. and assigned to the C.O., 1st Sept., 1922.

ASPINALL, ALGERNON EDWARD, C.M.G. (1918), C.B.E. (1926).—B. 1871; ed. at Eton and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1894; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1897; sec., West India Comte., since 1908 and of the Impl. Coll. of Trop. Agr. since 1921; hon. treas. of W. Indian Club; mem. of comte. on Col. blue-books and blue-book repts., 1917; mem., W. Indian shipping comte., 1919; hon. sec., Trop. Agr. Coll. comte., 1920; hon. sec., W. Indian Contingent comte., 1915-21; mem., W. Indian currency comte., 1923.

ASSER, LIEUT.-GEN. SIR JOSEPH JOHN, K.C.B. (1924), K.C.M.G. (1918), K.C.V.O. (1917), O.B. (1915), and many foreign decorations.—B. 1887; served with Nile expedit., 1897-9; in command of Kordofan expedit., Soudan, 1910; A.G., Egyptian Army, 1907-16; served in European War, 1914-19; base commtd., 1914-16; lieut.-gen., 1919; gen. offr. commdg. Br. troops, France and Flanders, 1919-20; gov. of Bermuda, 4th May, 1922; assumed govt., 9th Aug., 1922.

ASTON, ARTHUR VINCENT.—B. 1896; ed. King's Schil., Chester, and Queen's Coll., Oxford (open maths. scholar); R.F.A., 1915-18, M.O., 1917; apptd., cadet, M.C.S., 1919; ag. astt. to senr. dist. offr., Prov. Wellesley, June, 1920; ag. dist. offr., Nebong Tebal, Sept., 1920; ag. harbrmr., Malacoa, Mar., 1922; ag. collr., income tax, Malacoa, Apr., 1922; 2nd med. and ag. coroner, Penang, Apr., 1923; ag. dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, Dec., 1924.

ATHILL, SAMUEL LAUCHLAND, JUNR.—B.1886; ent. civ. serv., Leeward Is., Sept., 1901; clk. to atty.-gen., Leeward Is., Aug., 1906; sec. to legal affairs inquiry comntr., Montserrat, 1911; ag. regir., Montserrat circuit, Feb.-Mar., 1915; deputy coroner, dists. "A" and "B," Antigua, 6th Oct., 1917; mag., dist. B., Antigua, 1st July, 1924; city comntr., St. Johns, Antigua, Nov., 1925.

ATHLONE, FIRST EARL OF (created 1917), ALEXANDER CAMBRIDGE, G.C.B. (1911), G.C.V.O. (1904), O.M.G. (1917), D.S.O. (1900).—Served in Matabele campaign, 1896-7; S. African war, 1899-1900; European war, 1914-18; maj., 2nd Life Gds., 1911-19; brevet-col., 1919; G.S.O., 1st grade, 1915; ret. with hon. rank of brig.-gen., 1919; personal A.D.C. to the King from 1919; gov. gen. and commdr.-in-chief, Union of S. Africa, 1923; Grand Cordon of Order of Leopold of Belgium, Croix de Guerre (Belgium); G.O. Leg. Hon. and Croix de Guerre (France); Knt. of Justice of St. John of Jerusalem.

ATKINSON, HON. LLEWELYN, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1867; ed. Church Grammar Schil., Launceston, Tas. and Melbourne Univ.; called to the bar, 1893; el. mem. of H. of R., Commonwealth of Australia for Wilmot (Tas.), Dec., 1906; vice-pres. of exec. coun., C. of A., 1923.
ATTERBURY, H. W.—B. 1903; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 14th Oct., 1921.

ATTERBURY, JOHN LUCAS.—B. 1877; admitted a solicitor, June, 1899; served in S. African war, 10th Regt. Imperial Yeomanry (Royal Bucks Hussars), Jan., 1900 to June, 1901, medal and four clasps; dist. commr., Gold Coast, 19th Dec., 1908; ag. prov. commr., Cent. Prov., May-July, 1911; ag. prov. commr., Western Prov., Nov.-Dec., 1912; ag. prov. commr., Cent. Prov., July, 1915, to May, 1916; ag. prov. commr., Western Prov., Nov., 1917, to May, 1918; ag. commr., Cent. Prov., Mar. to Dec., 1919; ag. prov. commr., Cent. Prov., July-Dec., 1920; dep. prov. commr., 1st Jan., 1920; ag. prov. commr., Jan.-June, 1921; prov. commr., 2nd Aug., 1921; ag. sec. for nativ. affrs., 27th Oct., 1923 to 28th Feb., 1924.

AUCHINLECK, GILBERT GRAHAM.—B.S. (McGill), F.C.S., B. 1884; ed. West Indies and McGill Univ., Canada; 1st cls. hon. in chemistry and botany, McGill Univ.; govt. laboratory, Leeward Is., 1902-04; McGill Univ., 1904-06; science and 2nd mast., St. Kitts-Nevis gram. schl., 1906-09; supt. of agric., Grenada, 1909-14; astt. dir. of agric. and chemist, Mauritius, 1914; ag. dir. of agric. and regir., C.C. societies, Dec., 1916 to May, 1917; nominated M.L.C., Mauritius, Jan.-May, 1917; reported on Is. of Rodrigues to the Mauritius govt., 1919; Mauritius del. to Imp. Entom. Confe. in London, 1920; divnl., agric. offr., Ceylon, 1920; seconded to Seychelles to report on the possibilities of agric. development, 1921-22; has served in defence forces of Antigua, St. Kitts and Mauritius; dep. dir., agric., G. Coast, 1925; author of papers on land stlit. and various trop. agric. subjects.

AUCKLAND, BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. ALFRED WALTER AVERILL, D.D.—Ed. St. John's Coll., Oxford (2nd cls. Th. sch.), 1887; M.A., 1891; Ely Coll., 1888; deacon, 1888; priest, 1889, Lon.; formerly curate of St. George's, Hanover Square, 1888-91; Holy Trinity, Dalston, Middlesex, 1891-94; vicar of St. Michael's, Christchurch, N. Zealand, 1894-1909; chaplain of hosps., Christchurch, 1895-1909; hon. canon of Christchurch Cathedral, 1902-1909; archdeacon of Akaroa, 1903-1909; bishop of Waiapu, 1910-1913; bishop of Auckland, 1913; primate and archbishop of N.Z., 1926.

AUDEN, FRANCIS THOMAS.—B. 1869; ed. Shrewsbury; M.D., Edinburgh Univ., 1903; M.B. and C.M., 1891; formerly res. med. offr., Albany general hosp., Grahamstown, Cape Colony; dist. surg., Rustenburg, Transvaal; civil surg., S. African field force, 1900-1901 (medal and clasp); dist. commr. and govt. med. offr., Caicos Islands, Dec., 1914; govt. med. offr., Turks Is., Oct., 1916; mem. legis. bd., Sept., 1916.
AUDETTE, HON. LOUIS ARTHUR, B.A., LL.B., K.C.—B. 1856; apptd. regir. of exchequer of Canada, 1887; astt. judge, exchequer et., 1912; puisne judge, 1919.

AUSTIN, JAMES.—B. 1867; apptd. to home civ. serv. in cust. dept., 1886; bd. of educ., 1891-1896; clk., educ. offr., Natal, 1st Jan., 1897; 2nd cls. clk., audit dept., 1st Aug., 1900; re-transferred to educ. dept., 1st June, 1901;

AYLESWORTH, Hon. Sir Allen Bristol, K.C.M.G. (1911), K.C., B.A. (1874), M.A. (1875).—B. 1854; edn. at Newburgh high school and Toronto univ.; Prince of Wales prizeman; bencher of law soc. of Upper Canada; called to bar, Q.C. (Ontario); one of H.M.'s commrs. for settlmt. of Alaska bounty, 1903; elected to the H. of C. for North York, 1904; postm.-gen. of Canada, 1905; min. of just., 1906; British ag. internat. fisheries arbit., at the Hague, 1910; resigned portfolio and retired, 1911; mem., privy coun. of Canada; called to the Senate, 1923.

AYTON, A.L.—B. 1878; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. 2nd div. civ. ser. and assigned to science and art dept., Mar., 1899; to C.O. July, 1899; clk. in charge of accpts., Western Pacific high comm., Dec., 1913; ag. sec., W.P.H.C., 29th Mar., 1915; returned to C.O. as staff clk. in accts. dept., 29th Jan., 1920; ag. ast. prin., 16th Aug., 1920; sec. to "Discovery" comttee, 1925.

BABER, EDWARD ALFRED.—B. 1888; copyist, survey dept., B. Honduras, 1905; apprentice, ditto, 1906; passed exam. in theory of land surveying; 2nd cls. clk., Aug., 1910; ag. 1st cls. clk., 1911; 1st cls. clk., col. sec.'s dept., Nov., 1913; clk., roads and rivers bd., 1914; ag. ch. clk. and clk. of coun., 1914-15; ag. inspr. of schls. and clk. to bd. of educ., Nov., 1915; senr. clk., col. sec.'s dept., Feb., 1917; ag. ch. clk., etc., for various periods, 1917-21; ag. ast. col. sec., Nov., 1921; ag. priv. sec. to Sir Eyre Hutson, May-Aug., 1922; ch. clk., Apr., 1921; ast. col. sec., June, 1923.

BACKWELL, H.F.—B. 1884; ed. at Charterhouse (jun. and sen. scholarships), and at King's Coll., Camb., exhib.; ast. mast., Portsmouth gram. schl., 1907-8; ast. res., N. Nigeria, 19th Dec., 1908; ast. dist. offr., 1st Jan., 1914; dist. offr., 2nd cls., 29th Aug., 1917.

BADDELEY, FRANK MORRIS, C.M.G. (1925).—B.A., Magdalene Coll., Cambridge, 21st wrangler, Math. tripos, 1896; cadet F.M.S., 1897; passed final-exam. in cantonese, Dec., 1900; called to the bar, Inner Temple, Jan., 1913; ag. ast. dist. offr., Kuala Kubu, May, 1901; ditto, Serendah, Nov., 1901; recd. thanks of govt. in connection with Ulu Selangor riots, Feb., 1902; revenue auditor, Pahang, Jan., 1904; ag. dist. offr., Jelebu Jan., 1905; ag. 2nd ast. prot. of chinese, Singapore, Aug., 1906; ag. dist. offr., Xmas Is., Oct., 1906; ag. offl. assignee, Singapore, Apr., 1907; apptd. a currency commr., May, 1907; dist. offr., Kuantan, June, 1908; supt., govt. monopolies dept., S. Stltmts., Sept., 1909; P.M.G., S. Stltmts., Feb., 1916; food controller, Oct., 1920; under-sec., S. Stltmts., July, 1921; ag. col. sec., S. Stltmts., Apr.-Sept., 1922, May-Oct., 1923 and from Sept.-Nov., 1924; ch. sec., Nigeria, Nov., 1924; O.A.G., Nigeria, May-Nov., 1925; govts. dep., 15th Feb. to 8th Mar., 15th to 20th Apr., 1st to 23rd Dec., 1926 and from 22nd Mar. to 8th May, 1926.

BAGSHAWE, ARTHUR WILLIAM GARRARD, C.M.G. (1915), B.A., M.B., B.C., D.P.H., Camb., M.R.C.S., Eng., L.R.C.P., Lond.; ed. at Marlborough Coll. and Caius Coll., Camb., served in resident hosp. appts., 1896-1899; med. offr., Uganda Prot., Oct., 1900; served as med. offr. in Lango expeditn., 1901; served with Anglo-German bmdry. comm., 1902-4; employed in sleeping sickness extended investigations in Uganda, Apr., 1906, to May, 1907; director of sleeping sickness bureau, 1908.

BAILEY, EDWARD SHEPPARD.—B. 1857; 2nd clk. col. treas. Barbados, 1880; ch. clk., 1892; sec. to emigrn. comm., 1893; ag. col. treas., 1894 and on other occasion; clk. to plantation commrs., July, 1903; man. of agric. bank, 1907.
BAILEY, JOHN F.—Dir. of botanic gardens, Adelaide, S. Australia, 1917; formerly dir. of Brisbane gardens.
BAILWARD, A.N.—Asst. dist. commr., Kenya, Jan., 1920; junr. sec., native affrs. dept., 1926.

BAIN, NORMAN KEIR, B.A. Cantab.—Cadet, S. Stltmts., Jan., 1907; ag. dist. offr., Malacca, Sept., 1907; ag. ast. to res., Malacca, July, 1910; offr. of cls. V., 31st Mar., 1911; ag. dist. offr., Kuala Langat, 29th Mar., 1914; ag. ast. to res. and harbmr., Malacca, Mar., 1918; ag. agent, food controller in addition, 1919; ag. dist. offr., Port Dickson, Negri-Sembilan, Jan., 1921; ag. 2nd mag., Kinta, July, 1921; mag., Ipooh, Aug., 1921; ch. ast. dist. offr., Kinta, Aug., 1922; ag. dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, Apr., 1923; pol. mag., Penang, Sept., 1924; ag. dist. judge, Penang and Singapore, Sept., 1924; offg. 2nd mag., Penang, Sept., 1924; offg. regar., sup. ct., Kuala Lumpur, Apr., 1925; offg. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Nov., 1925.

BAINES, DENNIS LYNCH, O.B.E. (1919).—B. 1881; ed. Wellington and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; ast. collr., Uganda prot., May, 1904; transfd. to German E. Africa, 1st July, 1916; senr. commr., 1st Apr., 1920.

BAKER, ALAN CUSTANCE.—B.A., Oxon.; cadet, S. Stltmts., Dec., 1908; attchd. to land office, Malacca, Jan., 1909; ag. dist. offr., Alor Gajah, 1912; ag. dep. regar., sup. ct., Singapore, Apr. and Sept., 1912; ag. dist. offr., B. Pulau, 1913; joined offr. cadet batt., July, 1916; demob. June, 1919; officiating ast. sup't., monopolies, Penang, June, 1919; officiating ast. offi. assignee and dep. regar., sup. ct., Penang, Aug., 1919; ag. 2nd mag., Aug., 1919; capt., Penang volunteers, Nov., 1919; supernmy. offr., cls. III., Oct., 1920; ag. ast. offi. assignee, Penang, Nov., 1920; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, Jan., 1921; collr., land rev. and ast. treas., Malacca, Mar., 1923.
BAKER, CHARLES MATTHEW, M.B.E.—B. 1878; ed. Felsted Schol., Essex; supt. of educn., Tanganyika Territory, 1921.

BAKER, ERNEST LAIDLAW.—B. 1880; ed. Australia; clk., col. sec.'s office, Fiji, 1901; shorthand writer, legis. coun., 1906; sec., Albert Park Bd. (conjoint apptnt.), 1908; 1st clk., col. sec.'s office, and clk. to legis. coun., 1909; clk., exec. coun. (conjoint apptnt.), 1910; ag. 2nd ast. col. sec., 1913; dist. commr., Colo East, 1914; ditto, R.A., 1917; ditto, Savu Savu, 1918; ag. ast. col. sec., 1920; dist. commr., Lautoka, 1920; ditto, Macuata, 1923.

BAKER, FRANCIS HORACE.—B. 1887; ed. Stratford-on-Avon Schl.; admitted solr., sup. ct., England, 1910; practised in England until 1913 and in Hong Kong, Jan., 1914; on mily. serv., 1914-19; commn., R. Artillery, 1915; staff capt., 1919; mily. mag., Nablus dist., Apr., 1919; mily. mag., Jerusalem dist., May, 1919; pres., Jerusalem dist. ct., Aug., 1919; also pres., Beersheba dist. ct.

BAKER, HERBERT ARTHUR, D.Sc., D.I.C., F.G.S., F.R.G.S.—B. 1886; ed. Univ. Coll. and London Day Training Coll., Univ. of London, 1905-08; B.Sc. hons.; B. of E. teacher's certif., 1908; M.Sc., London, 1918; D.Sc., London, 1920; dipl. of membership, Imp. Coll. of Sci. and Technology (D.I.C.), 1920; R.F.C., France, 1915-16; warrant offr., R.N., 1916-19; govt. geologist, Falkland Is., 1920-23; headmaster, Grenada boys secondary schl., 1924.
BALDWIN, JOSEPH MASON, M.A., D.Sc.—B. 1878; ag. govt. astronomer, Victoria, 1915; govt. astronomer, 1st July, 1919.
BALDWIN, THOMAS HAYWARD, B.A.—B. 1896; ed., Stamford Schol. and Downing Coll., Cambridge; supt., educn. dept., N. Prov., Nigeria, 19th Oct., 1921.

BALDWIN, WILLIAM FREDERICK.—B. 1882; 2nd div. elk., War Office, July, 1901; army audit off., Cape Town, 1904; army cashier, army acct., dept., O.F.S. and Cape CoL, 1908; army cashier, Pretoria (attached to army pay dept.), for S. Africa, 1911; asst. auditor, col. audit dept., S. Nigeria, 2nd Aug., 1911; auditor, Seychelles, 12th Nov., 1912; ag. auditor, Nyassaland, Sept. 1914; attached for one month to audit off., Mauritius, 1915; resumed post of auditor, Seychelles, Mar., 1915; transfd. to Brit. E. Africa for military service in connection with acct., Apr. 1916.
BALFE, R. S.—B. 1892; cler.-asst., Basutoland, 1912; sub-inspr., Basutoland mounted pol., 1915; war serv., 1915-19.

BALFOUR, DOUGLAS HASTINGS.—B. 1887; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., 16th Jan., 1911; attached to Jaffna Kachcheri, Feb., 1911; ag. pol. mag., Point Pedro, May, 1911; extra office asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Nov. 1911; attached to Trincomalee Kachcheri, May, 1912; ag. office asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., July, 1912; pol. mag., Matara, July, 1913; asst. comsrr. of excise, June, 1917; dist. judge, Kegalla, July, 1923; ag. asst. govt. agt., Mannar, Apr., 1926; dist. judge, Negombo, June, 1925.

BALL, ARTHUR DYER.—B. 1888; barrister-at-law; ed., Mill Hill Schol. and Exeter Coll., Oxford (exhib.); B.A. 1911; cadet, F.M.S., 1911; ag. sec., san. bd., Kuala Lumpur, 1912; passed final exam. in Cantonese, Mar., 1914; ag. asst. contrlr. of labour r. Perak, Apr., 1914; passed final exam. in law, June, 1914; 1st cl. mag., Perak, 1914; transfd. Hong Kong, Mar., 1915; ag. asst. dist. offr., 1915; ag. dist. offr., Taipoo, 1917; passed final exam. in Hakka, 1917; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1917; add. to imp. and exp. off., and to col. secs', off., 1918; ag. asst. col. sec., elk. of couns., and elk. to gen. mil. serv. tribunal, 1918 to 1919; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1920; 2nd pol. mag., 1920; asst. crown solr., 1920; asst. atty. gen., 1920; 2nd assist. col. sec. in addition, 1921; ag. asst. col. sec. and elk. of couns., 1922; priv. sec. to O.A.G. in addition, 1922; asst. atty.-gen., 1923 and 1925; ag. pulse judge, 1924; off. recnr., 1925; draftsman, atty. gen's. dept., Ceylon, 1927; editor, "Ordnaces of Hong Kong, 1844-1923"; joint edr., "Regulations of Hong Kong, 1844-1925."

BALLANTINE, CAPT. JAMES ALEXANDER.—B. 1878; 2nd lieut., Lance. Fusiliers, 1901; served in S. African War Queen's and King's meda. with clasp); seconded to W. African Regt., 1905-09; asst. adjutant, 1907-09; asst. dist. comsrr., Gold Coast, 30th Mar., 1910; dist. comsrr., 13th Mar., 1914; seconded for serv., Field Force, Togoland, 7th Aug., 1914; staff offr. to O.C., Br. Forces, Togoland, 21st Nov., 1915 to 21st Aug., 1916; Br. and Victory meda. and "1914-15" Star; dep. prov. comsrr., Ashanti, 2nd Aug., 1921; ag. ch. comsrr., Ashanti, 23rd Nov., 1923 to 30th Mar., 1924; prov. comsrr., Ashanti, 3rd Jan., 1924; ag. ch. comsrr., Ashanti, 7th Dec., 1924 to 18th Mar., 1925.
BALMER, A. B.—Asst. treas., Kenya, Mar., 1918.
BALMER, F. E.—Dep. chief acotnt., G.P.O., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1919; ch. acotnt., G.P.O., Kenya, 1st Oct., 1919.

BAMFORD, LIEUT.-COLONEL H.W.M., O.B.E. (1918); M.C. (1916).—B. 1882; Cape Mounted Riflemen, 1900-1906; S. African War, 1906-1908; Transvaal Mounted Rifles, Zulu Rebellion, 1906; Natal Police, 1907-1912; dep. sheriff, N. Zululand, 1913-1914; adjt., Natal Light Horse, S.A. Rebellion and G.S.W., Africa, 1914-1915; adjt. 2nd S. African Infantry, Senussi Campaign, W.F.F., Egypt (wounded Hazalin Jan. 23rd, 1916); to France with S. African bde., 9th Scottish Div. (wounded Delville Wood, July 15th, 1916); bde.-major, 90th infy. bde., France, 1917; comdg. 2nd S. African infy., 1917-1918 (very severely wounded Beaurevoir, Oct. 8th, 1918); service in France includes Somme, Arras, Messines, Ypres, defense of Hazebrouk, Le Cateau; three times mentioned in despatches; O.B.E., M.C., Croix de Guerre avec palme, 1914-15 Star, G.S. War Medal, Victory Medal; comsrr. of police and inspr. of prisons, Malta, 29th July, 1919; commdt., constab., N. Territories, G. Coast, 28th July, 1922; inspr.-gen., pol. G. Coast, 27th Aug., 1924.

BANCROFT, OSWALD LAWRENCE.—B. 1888; ed., Harrison Coll., Barbados, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1909; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1910; admitted bar, Barbados, 1910; barrister-at-law and solr., sup. ct., Br. Columbia, 1911-24; pol. mag., dist. A, Barbados, 1925; dep. regis., high ct., Tanganyika Territory, 1926.

BANNERMAN, CHARLES EDWARD WOOLHOUSE, O.B.E. (1924).—B. 1884; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, Nov., 1913; admitted to Gold Coast bar as solr. and advco., Jan., 1914; practised before sup. ct. G. Coast up to 31st July, 1919; pol. mag., sup. ct. 1st Aug., 1919; senr. pol. mag. since 1920; ag. prizewinner judge, sup. ct., 30th Mar. to 12th May, and from 26th May to 8th June, 1925.

BARBADOS, BISHOP OF (founded 1824), RIGHT REV. ALFRED PARKHAM BERKELEY.—Foundation scholar, Codrington Coll., Durham; B.A., 1863; M.A., 1908; D.D., 1917; consecrated Lord Bishop of Barbados, 1917; vicar of All Saint's, Antigua, 1886-88; vicar of the Holy Innocent's, Barbados, 1890-1900; rector of St. Philip, 1901-06; dean and rector of St. Michael's Cathedral, 1906-17.
BARGERY, REV. GEORGE PEROT.—B. 1876; jnr. supt., educn. dept., N. Nigeria, 26th Aug., 1913.
BARKER, R. D.—Tempy. asst. dist. comsrr., E. Africa Prot., Apr., 1919.

BARLEY, JACK CHARLES.—B. 1887; ed., Tonbridge Schol. and St. John's Coll., Oxford, B.A., 2nd cls. hona.; cadet, W. Pacific civ. ser., 15th Dec., 1911; attached to res. comsrr.'s off., Apr., 1912; ag. dist. offr., Gizo, 1st Nov., 1912; ag. treasur. and collr. of customs, 16th Apr., 1913; passed cadet, 14th Apr., 1914; deputy comsrr., 29th July, 1914; dist. offr., Gizo, 1915; ag. res. comsrr., 19th Apr., 1917; dist. comsrr., Fiji, Feb., 1922.

BARNARD, BASIL HENRY FRANCIS.—B. 1874; ed. St. Mark's Schol., Windsor, and Keble Coll., Oxford; audit elk., Perak State Rlys., Apr., 1896; damaration offr., Perak, Aug., 1896; asst. forest offr., Perak, Jan., 1898; forest offr., Selangor, Jan., 1900; dep. conserv., forests, F.M.S., Jan., 1908; ag. conserv., forests, F.M.S., in 1911, 1914, 1920 and 1924.

BARNARD, SIR FRANK STILLMAN, K.C.M.G. (1918).—B. 1856; entd. ser. of B. Columbia Express Co. in 1879; subsequently became gen. man. and pres. of the company; promoter, and dir. for many years of the B. Columbia Electric Rly.; identified with many other important interests; mem. of H. C., Canada, 1879-1896; alderman of Victoria, B.C., 1886 and 1887; mayor, 1896; ret. from active business life in 1906; lieut.-govr. of B. Columbia, 1914-1919.
BARNES, A. C.—Computer, survey dept., E.A.P., Oct., 1914; asst. analyst, Apl., 1916; 2nd chemist, agrl. dept., Nigeria, Apr., 1923.

BARNETT, ERNEST ARTHUR.—B. 1884; olk. and pay and qrtmr., 1900; sub-inspr., Fiji constab., 1906; attached to R.I.C. for training, 1908; inspr., Fiji constab., 1911; higher grade, 1914; asst. adjutant to local forces, 1914; capt., Fiji Def. Forces, 1914; ag. inspr.-gen. of constab. and sheriff, June to Dec., 1919; J.F. for the col., 1916; press censor, 1917; ag. inspr.-gen. of constab. and sheriff, 1917 and Sept., 1920; chief inspr., 1918; ag. inspr.-gen. of prisons, 1918; dist. comsnr., 1921; ag. dist. comsnr. and prov. comsnr., Ba, May, 1921-June, 1922; dep. inspr.-gen., constab., 1st Jan., 1922; prov. mem., leg. coun., Nov., 1925.

BARNHILL, WILLIAM WILSON, B.A. Hons. (Cantab.)—B. 1903; ed. Campbell Coll., Belfast, and Clare Coll., Cambridge; asst. dist. comsnr., Sierra Leone, 28th Apr., 1926; ag. asst. col. sec., 8th May, 1926.
BARRETT, UNWIN SANKEY, B.A., LL.B. (Cape).—B. 1874; ed. Bedford Sch., olk., master of sup. ot's. office, Sept., 1902; olk., insmenvoy, July, 1905; master, sup. ct., Pretoria, 1912.

BARRETT-LENNARD, SIR FIENNES OECIL ARTHUR, KT. BACHE (1926)—Served in Anglo-Boer War, but invalided through wounds reed. in action, 1899-1900; judge, sup. ct., Gold Coast, and ex officio of sup. ct., Nigeria, Sept., 1913; served with local mils. forces and as judge, ct. of appeal for eastern Africa and the high ct. of Uganda, Feb., 1916 to Nov., 1916; judge, sup. cts., S. Stltims. and F.M.S., Mar., 1920; judge, ct. of appeal, Johore, June, 1920; judge, ct. of appeal, Kedah, Dec., 1921; ch. just. and keeper of records, Jamaica, Nov., 1925.

BARRON, JACOB MAURICE.—B. 1891; ed. High Sch., Dublin, 1899-1910; sen. modr., nat. sci., 1914, T.C.D.; cadet, F.M.S., 1914; labr. dept., 1915-17; passed in Tamil, 1917; dist. offr., Nibong Tebal, 1917; labr. dept., 1917; offr., als. V., Dec., 1917; supervisor of cust., Port Swettenham, 1917-20; labr. dept., Apr., 1921; supervmy. offr., als. IV., Nov., 1921; asst. dist. offr., Ipoh, Oct., 1922; dep. contr., rubber expts., Jan., 1924; town planning admstr. and valuer-gen., Kuala Lumpur, Jan., 1926.

BARRON, WILLIAM DOUGLAS.—B. 1887; ed. Aberdeen Univ., M.A., 1909, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; cadet, F.M.S., 1911; ag. asst. dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Selangor, July, 1912; ag. dist. offr., Kuala Langat, Dec., 1912; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kroh, Perak, Apr., 1913; passed cadet, Deo., 1913; asst. dist. offr., Kuala Kubu, Selangor, Dec., 1916; asst. dist. offr., Rembau, Negri Sembilan, Aug., 1920; dist. offr., Tampin, Nov., 1920; dist. offr., Temerloh, Pahang, Jan., 1921; dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, Nov., 1922; offg. asst. adviser, Batu Pahat, Jan., 1926.

BARRY, HON. JEREMIAH HAYES, K.C.—B. 1858; ed. at public schls. of Saint Mary's and Fredericton; mem. of Senate of Univ. of New Brunswick; admitted to the bar, 1882; K.C., 1898; judge, sup. ct., New Brunswick, 1909; King's Bench division, 1913; ch. just., King's Bench divn., 1924.

BARRY, RICHARD JOHN.—B. 1876; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; clk., C.S.O., Cape, 1895; C.S. Law, 1899; clk., mag. dept., 1901; asst. mag., various places; relief staff, 1904; oh. clk., master's office, Cape (insolv. brch.), 1906; Natal, 1912; Transvaal, 1912; master, sup. ct., Natal, 1916; ditto, Cape, 1923.

BARTH, SIR JACOB WILLIAM, KT. BACHE (1922), C.B.E.(1919).—Ed. Wadhams Coll., Oxon; B.A., 1895; M.A., 1898; barrister-at-law, Mid. Term., 1900; regiar. and prin. regiar. of documents, E. Africa Prot., 8th Aug., 1902; Crown advoc., 1st Oct., 1902; apptd. to serve on land comm., 31st Oct., 1904; judge, High Court, E. Africa Prot., 1st April, 1905 atty.-gen., E.A.P., Feb., 1914; ag. chf. sec., Aug., 1918; ch. just., Kenya, Aug., 1920.

BARTLETT, C. A.—Olk., senr. police mag.'s court, Bridgetown, Barbados, Jan., 1881; ag. in rev. offr., May, 1883 to Nov., 1884; chief olk., pol. mag.'s courts, dist. "A," June, 1897; ag. pol. mag. on numerous occasions; pol. mag. and revr. of wrecks, dist. "F," Nov., 1911; mem. of police comm., Jan., 1913; pol. mag., coroner and revr. of wrecks, dist. "B," Apl., 1913; mem. of cane fires comm., Apl., 1914.

BARTLETT, FRANK.—B. 1872; ed. Rugby and Trin. Coll., Oxford (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon div. ser., Dec., 1895; asst. collr. of cuat., Trincomalee, June, 1897; pol. mag. and asst. collr. of cuat., Jaffna, Sept., 1898; dist. judge, Tangalla, Mar., 1899; off. asst. to govt. agt., Uva Prov., Jan., 1900; dist. judge, Badulla, July, 1900; pol. mag., Kandy, May, 1902; dist. judge, Negombo, Oct., 1903; asst. govt. agt., Nuwara Eliya, Feb., 1905; dist. judge, Kegalle, Mar., 1910; asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Apl., 1910; govt. agt., N. C. Prov., June, 1911; govt. agt., Prov. of Uva May, 1912; ditto, May, 1913; ditto, S. Prov., May, 1921; ditto, N. C. Prov., Dec., 1924; attimt. offr., Sept., 1925; ag. govt. agt., W. Prov., 18th Oct. to 30th Nov., 1926 and from 17th Mar., 1926.

BARTLETT, HUMPHREY EDWARD GIBSON.—B. 1880; ed. Winchester and Trin. Coll., Oxford, 2nd cls. class. mods., 2nd cls. final honour, schl. of jurisprudence; B.A., 1903; M.A., 1906; solr., 2nd cls. final honours., 1906; asst. dist. comsnr., G. Coast, 1909; dist. comsnr., 1913; ag. prov. comsnr., Dec., 1915 to June, 1916; and from 23rd June, 1919; ag. comsnr., Western Prov., 1st Jan. to 25th Feb., 1920; dep. prov. comsnr., 1921; ag. comsnr., C. Prov., June to Dec., 1921, and from Sept., 1922 to Jan., 1923; ag. comsnr., W. Prov., Feb., 1923; prov. comsnr., 6th May, 1923.

BARTLEY, THOMAS DOUGLAS MURRAY.—B.A. Trin. Coll., Dublin; barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn, 1925; war serv., Aug., 1914—Jan., 1920; adminstr. offr. (cadet) Tanganyika Territory, May 1920; asst. sec., May, 1922; ag. senr. asst. sec., Sep. to Nov., 1923; ag. asst. admstr. gen. and asst. regiar. of companies, June, 1924.

BARTLEY, WM., M.B.E. (1919).—B.A., Dublin; cadet, S. Stltims., Dec., 1908; ag. dist. offr., Labuan, Nov., 1909; ag. asst. to atty.-gen., S. Stltims., Apl., 1910; passed in Malay, Jan., 1910; passed in law, July, 1910; offr. of cls. V., 1st Jan., 1912; ag. asst. dist. judge and 4th mag., Singapore, 2nd Jan., 1912; ag. inspr. of prisons, 30th Jan., 1912; passed in Javanese, 21st Feb., 1912; dist. offr., Dindings, 26th May, 1912; ag. astt. supt., Netherlands Indian immigrants, F.M.S. and S. Sttlmts., 11th June, 1912; ag. astt. controller of labour, F.M.S., 10th Dec., 1912; ag. astt. to atty.-gen., Singapore, 21st Oct., 1914 to 19th Mar., 1915; astt. regis. of imports and exports, S. Settlmts.; sec., food control comttee., June, 1917; mem., shipping comttee., Apr., 1918; sec., food contrl's. comttee., Dec., 1918; agent, food controller, Singapore, June, 1919; services reoog. by sec. of state, Aug., 1919; sec., profiteering coman., 1920; ag. collr.-gen., income tax, Sept., 1920; supermny. offr., cls. III, Nov., 1920; sec. to advising comttee. on allowances, in addition, Dec., 1920; seconded for serv. under Kelantan Govt. as astt. adviser, Apr., 1922; ag. Br. adviser Kelantan in addn., 1922 and 1923; ag. 1st astt. sec. (B), S.S., and clk. of couns., Jan. 1924; ag. under sec., S.S. Mar., 1924; ag. dist. offr., Klang, Apr., 1926; sec. to res., Selangor, Aug., 1926.
BARTON, A. E. V.—Imp. cust.,serv., 1912; cust. astt., Kenya, Sept., 1919; collr. of cust., Apr., 1923.

BARTON, C. J. J. T.—B. 1891; ed. Denstone and Downing Coll., Cambridge; history and law tripeses; B.A., 1913; M.A., 1920; astt. dist. comanr., E.A.F., Aug., 1914; ag. dist. comanr., 1917, 1919, 1921; astt. for native affrs., 1923; ag. senr. astt. sec., 1924.

BARTON, CECIL MOLYNEUX, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1883; ed. Shrewsbury and Trinity Coll., Dublin (hons., classics, law); barrister-at-law, King's Inns, Dublin, 1906; astt. dist. comanr., E.A.P., 1913; res. mag., 1914-21; ag. judge, sup. ct., Apr., 1921 to Oct., 1922; legal adviser, Gambia, Dec., 1922; comanr. to enquire into double inoculation of cattle, 1918; chmn. of comanr. to enquire into inoculation of cattle for rinderpest by vety. dept., 1919; ag. judge, sup. ct., July to Oct., 1923 and July to Oct., 1925; mem., exec. and leg. couns.
BARTON, GERALD JOHN LLOYD, M.C., B.A.—Plant breeder, agril. dept., Kenya, Jan., 1921.

BARTON, MAJOR P. F., V.D.—Assoc., London Assn. of Acctnts.; astt. treas., G. Coast, Mar., 1907; attd. col. sec.'s office, Oct.-Nov., 1907 and Sept.-Nov., 1910; senr. astt. treas., Nov., 1908; ag. ch. astt. treas., Apr.-July, 1914 and June, 1916; ch. astt. treas., Apr., 1917; ag. treas. and currency offr., July-Oct., 1919 and Dec., 1920 to Aug., 1921; dep. treas., Jan., 1920; commanded G. Coast Volunteers, 1914 and during War; ment. in desps., Aug., 1917; col. treas. and currency offr., Sierra Leone, 6th Aug., 1922.

BARWELL, HON. SIR HENRY NEWMAN, K.C.M.G. (1922), LL.B. (Adelaide Univ.), M.P., Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1877; ed. St. Peter's Coll., Adelaide; mem., H. of A., S. Australia, Stanley dist., 1915-25; atty.-gen., 1917 and 1918-20; premier and atty.-gen., 1920-24; fedl. senator, 1925.

BASELEY, REGINALD BRUCE, A.M.I. Mech. E. (1914), M.I. Mar. E. (1911).—B. 1878; ed. Victoria Coll., Jersey; col. engnr., Falkland Is., 1912; ag. comanr., currency on various occasions; ag. local audr., Apr.-Dec., 1920.

BASSETT, JOHN HAROLD.—B. 1896; ed. Wallacey and Glasgow Univ.; war serv., 1914-19; priv. sec. to dep. contr. (organization) Min. of Lab., 1919-20; cadet, F.M.S., 1921; attd. to dist. office, Jelebu, July, 1921; ag. 2nd astt. dist. offr., Klang, Oct., 1922; ag. astt. ditto, May, 1923.

BASSETT, RALPH HENRY.—B. 1896; on mily. serv., Sept., 1914; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1920; attd. to Ratnapura Kach., Jan., 1921; ag. pol. mag., Jaffna, May, 1923; astt. sttlm't. offr., July, 1925.
BATEMAN, G. S., L.D.S., R.S.G., Eng.—Serv. with Forces in E. Africa, 1915 and 1916; dental surg., Uganda, May, 1912.

BATEMAN, WALTER SLADE, O.B.E. (1920).—Serv'd in convict and prisons branch, Cape Colony, 1894-1901; sec., bd. of management, Somerset hosp., Cape Town, 1897-1901; clk., prisons dept., Transvaal, Jan., 1901; chief clk., July, 1901; ag. inspr. of prisons, June, 1903; astt. dir. of prisons, July, 1905; ag. dir., Nov., 1906; mem. of tender bd. at various times during 1902-1905; ag. gov. of central prison and local gaol, Pretoria, June, 1907; gov. of Johannesburg gaol and Diepkloof prison, Dec., 1907; ag. astt. dir. of prisons, Union of S. Africa, Oct., 1911; dir. and under-sec. for justice, 1918.

BATES, ISAAC GRAINGER.—B. 1886; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn, 1909; equity jur., 1909-13; ch. mag. and legal adviser, Br. Solomon Is. Prot., with jurisdiction as a judl. comanr. for the Western Pacific, Nov., 1913; ag. res. comanr. in addn. during 1919; seconded for serv. as ch. pol. mag., Fiji, May, 1920 to Dec., 1921; senr. mag., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1922; ag. judge, high ct., in addition, Aug., 1924.

BATHO, CHARLES PHILIP ARTHUR, M.B.E. (1920).—B. 1883; ed. Elizabeth Coll., Guernsey, and Victoria Coll., Jersey; control and audit office, Cape Colony, 1900; sec., tender bd., 1901; transf'd Transvaal prisons dept., July, 1902; prin. clk., July, 1910; prin. clk., Union prisons dept., Nov., 1912; ch. clk., Nov., 1917; astt. dir., prisons, Apr., 1926; is also comanr. of oaths for Union.

BATHO, G. F. W.—B. 1872; Cape govt. rly., 1892; clk., audit dept., 1893; astt. sec., tender bd., Cape, 1893; sec., 1895; examr. of acct., 1893; acctnt., 1903; ch. acctnt., 1909; prov. audr., Cape, 1910.

BATHURST, HENRY CHARLES.—B. 1887; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Camb. Univ.; maths. schol., 1906-09; 22nd wrangler, 1909; cadet, F.M.S., 1910; passed in law and Tamil, 1912; ag. 2nd astt. supt. of imigrts., Penang, Apr., 1912; ag. astt. supt. of imigrts., Klang, Aug., 1912; ag. astt. contr. of lab., Klang, Dec., 1912; astt. contr. of lab., Klang, Nov., 1913; ag. emigrn. agt., Madras, Apr., 1914 and Mar., 1916; temp. employed in C.O., July, 1915; ag. sec., Madras govt. lab. bureau in addn., Apr., 1919 to Mar., 1920; passed in Telugu, 1920; 2nd mag., Kuala Lumpur, Sept., 1921; astt. offl. assignee and dep. registr., sup. ct., Penang, Oct., 1921; 2nd mag. and coroner, Penang, June, 1922; offg. chief astt. dist. offr., Kinta, Mar., 1923 and Dec., 1924; ag. chmn., sany. bd., Kinta, Mar., 1924; offg. ch. astt. dist. offr., Kinta, Dec., 1924; ag. dep. contr., lab., Penang, May, 1926.

BATHURST, WALTER DUNDAS.—B. 1859; ed. Lancing Coll.; agt. of Congo Free State, 1883-6; apptd. to col. audit branch exchequer and audit dep., Jan., 1889; local audr., Gibraltar, under C. and A. G., Jan., 1891; ch. examr., K. and A. dept., col. audit branch, Dec., 1906.

BATTERBEE, HARRY F., C.M.G. (1918); C.V.O. (1919).—B. 1880; ed. at Grammar Sch., Faversham, and at Oxford; classical scholar, Herford Coll., 1899-1904; 1st cls. clas. moda., 1901; 1st cls. math. moda., 1901; 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1904; apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. clk., exchequer and audit dept., Jan., 1905; 2nd cls. clk., C.O., May, 1905; ag. 1st cls. clk., 4th June, 1916; 1st cls. clk., 6th July, 1917; sec. to W. African lands comttee., 1912; astt. priv. sec. to Mr. Lewis (now Lord) Harcourt, 9th Dec., 1912; priv. sec. to Lord Lilington, 12th Oct., 1914; priv. sec. to Mr. (now Sir Arthur) Steel-Maitland, 31st May, 1915; priv. sec. to Mr. Walter Long, 11th Dec., 1916 to 13th Jan., 1919; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; asst. sec., 1st Mar., 1923; polit. sec. to Adml. commdg. sp. serv. squadron during Empire cruise, 1923-24; asst. sec., 12th Dec., 1924.

BATTERSHILL, WILLIAM DENIS.—B. 1896; on milly serv., Aug., 1914-19; cadet Ceylon, Apr., 1920; attd. to Colombo customs, May, 1920; attd. to Badulla kachcheri, Jan., 1921; office asst., Badulla kachcheri, Mar., 1921; pol. mag., Panadura, Oct., 1922; dep. fiscal, Colombo, June, 1923; addnl. asst. col. sec., Jan., 1925; 4th asst. col. sec., Mar., 1925.

BATTISCOMBE, CHRISTOPHER FRANCIS.—B. 1890; ed. King's Schl., Canterbury, and Haileybury Coll.; schol., Lincoln Coll., Oxford; B.A.; (hons.) 1914; asst. dist. comsrr., E.A.P., 1914; served with R. Berks. Regt. and Grenadier Guards during European War; appt. in col. serv. cancelled on acct. of wounds, 1919; re-ent. col. serv. as headmnr., Machakos indua schl. and inspr. of schl., Ukamba prov., E. Africa, 1921; tutor to Prince Seyyid Abdulla, son of the sultan of Zanzibar 1922; sec. to sultan, 1923; admvte. offr., Zanzibar, 1923.

BAXTER, JOHN BABINGTON MACAULEY, K.C., D.C.L.—Elected to New Brunswick Legislature, 1911; atty.-gen., 1914; leader of provincial opposition, 1920; min. of cust. in Dom. govt., 1921; prime min., New Brunswick, 1925.

BAYLES, HERBERT LAURENCE.—B. 1886; ed. Dulwich Coll.; clk., C.A.'s office, 1906; clk., cust. serv., Nyasaland, 1906; P.W.D., 1910; Nyasaland Field Force, 1914; asst. finan. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1916-17; asst. treas., Zanzibar, 1921; ag. treas., in 1921, 1922, 1923, 1924 and 1926; dep. treas., Kenya, Mar., 1924.

BAYLEY, BENJAMIN HAMILTON.—B. 1874; ed. at Queen's Coll., B. Guiana; draughtsman to titles to land comsrrs., B. Guiana, Oct., 1890; gradual promotion to prin. clk., govt. sec's office, Jan., 1920; dep. col. treas., 12th Mar., 1924; 2nd asst. col. sec., 1st Jan., 1926; ag. asst. col. sec. on several occasions.

BAYLEY, GEORGE D., C.B.E. (1926), J.P.—B. 1870; endl. govt. land dept., Br. Guiana as clk., 1887; survey branch, 1891; J.P., 1891; prin. clk., col. secretariat, 1904; seconded as comsrr. in charge of census, 1911; asst. col. sec. and clk. to Ct. of Policy and Combined Ct., 1911; comsrr. of lands and mines, 1917; has acted in various capacities; offr. mem., Ct. of Pol. and Combined Ct., 1921; mem. ex. coun., 1923; chairman, local govt. bd., 1920; has served as mem. of numerous comsrs. and comtees. and as chmn. of following—financing unindentured immigrn., financing sea defences, expend. on col. estates, financing drainage and irrig.

BAYLEY, SYDNEY HOWARD.—Entd. P.W.D., B. Guiana, June, 1888; transf'd. to pris. dept., 1889; sec. to inquiry into coolie riots at Skeldon, 1895; seconded to audit office, 1895; comvy. of taxation, B. Guiana, Feb., 1896; J.P., Mar., 1903; supt. of gov't. indust. schl., Onderneeming, Aug., 1905; mem., bd. of agric., B. Guiana, 1908; comsrr. of oaths, 1910; census comttee, 1921; mem., bd. of educn., 1921; managing dir., transport dept., B. Guiana, 1922.

BAYNES, EDWARD WILLIAM, O.B.E. (1920).—B. 1880; clk., G.P.O., Antigua, Jan., 1899; clk. to comsrr., Virgin Islds., Jan., 1901; ag. priv. sec. and clk. to admstr., Dominica, Dec., 1901, to May, 1908; ag. elk., gen. legis. coun., Leeward Is., 1908 session; priv. sec. and elk. to adminstr. Dominica, 10th Aug., 1909; transf'd. to Impl. civ. ser. and apptd. 2nd cl. elk., National Health Insurance Comsn. (England), 13th June, 1912; 1st cls. elk., 13th May, 1913; priv. sec. to sec., ministry of shipping, Jan., 1917; sec., National Health Insurance Joint Comttee., Oct., 1919; priv. sec. to parly. sec., ministry of shipping, Apr., 1920 to Mar., 1921; suptg. elk., Min. of Health, in Oct., 1920; sec., "Discovery" comttee., C.O., July, 1923; col. sec., Leeward Is., 17th Oct., 1925; admstr. govt. of Antigua on several occasions.

BAYNES, T. E. P.—B. 1884; clerical asst., col. sec's off., Trinidad, 1903; 6th clk., C.S.O., 1903; 5th elk., C.S.O., 1906; 1st elk., post office, Antigua, 1907; ag. 2nd elk. C.S.O., 1907; ag. 3rd elk., C.S.O., 1908, 1909, 1910 and 1911; 3rd elk., C.S.O., 1911; ag. 2nd elk., C.S.O., 1911-12; ag. clk. to admstr., Dominica, 1912; 2nd elk., C.S.O., elk. of exec. and legis. couns., Antigua, and supt. of govt. printing office, 1912; elk. to admstr., and elk. to exec. and legis. couns., Dominica, 1919; offr. in ch., N. dist., 1924; title changed to warden, N. dist., 1st Apr., 1926; temp. mem., leg. coun., Dominica, Feb., 1926; O.A.G., Montserrat, 1st to 8th Mar., 1926; O.A.G., Virgin Is., Aug. to Nov., 1926.

BAZZELL, C.—B. 1885; ed. Christ's Hosp. and Keble Coll., Oxford (scholar 1904-08); B.A. 1908 (2nd hon. mods., 2nd lit. hum.); astt. mast., Raffles Inst., Singapore, 1914-17 and 1922; ag. headmast., Malay Coll., Kuala Kangsar, 1923.

BEAL, Rear-AdmL ALISTER FRANCOIS, C.B. (1926), C.M.G. (1919), R.N.—B. 1876, ed. H.M.S. "Britannia," Dartmouth; comdr., H.M.S. "Princess Royal," 1912-15; capt., 1915; served on Admty. war staff, 1915-18; in command H.M.S. "Weymouth," 1918-19 and 1920-21; dep. dir., operations divn., Admty. naval staff, 1921-23; commodore commdg. N.Z. squadron and naval adviser to N.Z. Govt., 1923-26; offr., Legion of Honour, 1919; Italian Silver Med. for milly. valour, 1918.

BEAL, PERCY GORDON.—B. 1876; ed. Royal Naval Sch.; dist. traf. supt., F.M.S. Rlys., Apr., 1912; astt. traf. man., Jan., 1913; dep. traf. man., Kuala Lumpur, July, 1914; ag. traf. man. on various occasions, 1918-25.

BEAL, CAPT. WILLIAM PHILIP BARBERT, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1884; vety. offr., Gold Coast, 15th May, 1909; seconded for service, B.E.F. as capt., R.A.V.C., 9th Aug., 1915; O.C., 26th Mobile Vety. Section, 21st May, 1918; promoted ag. maj. whilst holding appt. D.A.D.V.S., 5th Dec., 1918; ment. in desps., 1st July, 1919; demob., 20th Mar., 1919; returned to Gold Coast, 1919; priv. vety. offr., 1st Jan., 1920.
BEAMISH, F. C.—Ast. conservator of forests, E. Africa Prot., Feb., 1919.
