BEATTIE, Lieut.-Col. ALEXANDER ELDER, C.B.E. (1920), M.C. (1915), ret. pay (ree. of offrs.)—Entd. Army (Argyll and Sutherland Highrs.), 1906; transf'd. on promotion to the Queen's (now The Queen's Royal) Regt., 1908; served in Aden and India; seconded for serv. with W.A.F.F., 1911; astt. adjutant, Southern Nigeria Regt., 1912; ag. regt. adjutant, S.N. Regt., 1913; adjutant, 4th Bn. Nigeria Regt., 1914; capt., 1914; Cameroons Expedy. Force, 1914; staff offr. to various field columns, 1914-15; ment. in desps., 1914; M.C., 1915; severely wounded, 1915; astt. staff offr., W.A.F.F., Col. Office, 1915; staff offr. (gen. staff offr.), W.A.F.F., 1916; temp. major, 1916; offg. staff offr., K.A.R., 1915; promoted major by brevet, 1918; tempy. lient. col., 1918; C.B.E., 1920; accompanied inspr.-gen., W.A.F.F., on tour of inspection, Nigeria, Gold Coast, Sierra Leone and Gambia, 1920; C.O. rep. on various inter-depl. comtees., 1918-21; ret., with rank of lieut.-col., Sept., 1922; recd. thanks of S. of S. for services under the C.O.; astt. sec., Gibraltar, Sept., 1922; res. inspr. of schls., Oct., 1922; clk. of exec. coun., Oct., 1922; sec., bd. of educn. (ex officio), 1922; ag. D.P.W. on two occasions; col. sec., Falkland Is., 8th Oct., 1926; ag. gov. from May, 1926; ch. astt. col. sec., Cyprus, 1927.
BEATTIE, JAMES.—B. 1891; European War, 1914-18; Somaliland Camel Corps, 1918-20; M.C., 1920; dist. pol. offr., Somaliland, 1920; dist. commnr., 1922.

BEATTY, DAVID.—Cadet. S. Sttlmts., Nov., 1898; passed final exam. in Chinese, June, 1901; ag. 3rd mag., Sing., Nov., 1901; ag. dist. offr., Christmas Is., July, 1903; 3rd mag., Penang, Oct., 1903; but cont. to act as dist. offr.; ag. 2nd mag., Sing., May, 1904; ag. 2nd astt. prot. of Chinese, Sing., June, 1904; ag. sheriff and dep. regist., Penang, Dec., 1905; ag. astt. prot. of Chinese, Penang, May, 1907; ag. offl. assignee, S. Sttlmts., Apr., 1909; ag. dist. offr., Jasin, Feb., 1911; dep. regist. and offl. assignee, Penang, Apr., 1911; ag. regist., sup. ct. dist. judge, mag. and astt. prot., Chinese, Malacca, Apr., 1911; ag. prot., Chinese, Selangor, Oct., 1913; sec., Chinese affairs, F.M.S., Apr., 1914; prot., Chinese, Tavoy and Mergui (Burma), Mar., 1916; collr., war tax, Singapore, Apr., 1917; ag. prot., Chinese S. Sttlmts., astt. col. sec., and clk. of couns., Mar., 1918; offl. prot., Chinese, S. Sttlmts., June, 1918; ag. prin. astt. col. sec., May, 1920 to Jan., 1921; sec., Chinese affrs., S. Sttlmts., Nov., 1921.

BEATTY, SR KENNETH JAMES, KT. BACH. (1926).—B. 1878; ed. Univ. High Schl. and Melbourne Univ.; qualified Victorian bar, 1900; Transvaal law certif. (including Roman Dutch law), 1904; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1908; served in S. African war as lieut., V.M.I., wounded in action (Queen's medal and 4 clasps); apptd. pub. prosecutor, Transvaal, Nov., 1902; addtnl. astt. res. mag., Mar., 1903; astt. res. mag., Dec., 1905; attached Natal Carbineers, Natal rebellion, 1906; recd. thanks of Natal govt., medal and clasp; pol. mag., master of sup. ct. and regen.-gen., Sierra Leone, Sept., 1908; has acted as ch. just., circuit judge and atty.-gen., Sierra Leone; on milly. serv., 1915-18; puisne judge, Gold Coast, 1921; ch. just., Bermuda, 1924.

BEAUCHAMP, 7th EARL (creat. 1815), VISCT. ELMLEY (1815), BARON BEAUCHAMP (1806, U.K.); K.G.; K.C.M.G. (1899); WILLIAM LYGON; B. 1872; succeeded his father, 1891.—Ed. Eton and Chr. Ch., Oxford; D.L. Worcestershire; Mayor of Worcester, 1895-6; mem. London schl. bd., 1897-9; gov. N.S.W., 1899 to 1900; capt. of hon. corps of Gentlemen-at-Arms, 1906; first commr. of works, 1911-14; lord-pres. of the coun., 1910-15.

BEAUCHAMP, ARTHUR KENNETH.—B. 1888; ed. Worcester Cath., King's Schl. and Christ Church, Oxford, B.A., 3rd cls., math. hon., 1910; headmr., gram. schl. and sub-inspr. of schls., Dominica, Sept., 1912; ag. inspr. of schls., Lee-ward Is., July-Oct., 1916; inspr. of schls., St. Lucia, Jan., 1921; supt., educn., S. Provs., Nigeria, 1926.

BEAUMONT, SR W. H., KNT. BACH. (1910).—Ensign 75th (Stirlingshire) Regt., Aug., 1870; lieut. Oct., 1871; served on the "Langalibalele Expedition" in 1873; ret., Aug., 1875; priv. sec. to Colonel Milles (administering the govt. of Natal), May, 1873; to Sir Benjamin Pine (lieut.-gov.), and clk. to the exec. coun., July, 1873; ag. R.M., Umlazi Div., Aug., 1874; clk., col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1875; ag. gov.'s elk. and clk. to the exec. coun., Oct., 1875; R.M., Newcastle division, Feb., 1878; col. comm. of Dist. No. 1, Natal, at the commencement of the Zulu War, Jan., 1879, during which time he raised levies called the "Newcastle Scouts"; R.M., Inanda, May, 1887; R.M., City div., Pietermaritzburg, 1896; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., for various periods, 1st Feb., 1896, to Oct., 1902; judge of special treason ct., Oct., 1901; puisne judge, sup. ct., 1st Nov., 1902; admstr., 6th June to 1st Sept., 1907; ret., 1910.

BECKETT, HAROLD.—B. 1891; ed. at Monmouth Grammar Schl. and Oxford; exhibitioner at Wadham Coll., 1910-1914; also Symons exhibitioner, 1912-1914; 1st cls. Class. Moda., 1912; 3rd cls. Lit. Hum., 1914; B.A., 1914; apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 30th Nov., 1914; astt. priv. sec. to Lord Milner, 14th Jan. to 16th Mar., 1919; ag. 1st cls. clk., 17th July, 1919; priv. sec. to perm. under sec. (Sir G. Fiddes), 1st Jan., 1920; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; sent to Mauritius as spec. commnr. to enquire into and rept. to S. of S. on questions of currency and exchange, Apr., 1926; returned to C.O., Oct., 1925.

BECKETT, OSBORNE.—B. 1889; ed. S. Andrew's Coll., Dublin and Trin. Coll., Dublin (scholar, B.A.); cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1912; on milly. serv., Dec., 1914-July, 1919; astt. sec. to res., Perak, July, 1919; dist. offr., Port Dickson, July, 1921; dist. offr., Ulu Langat, June, 1924.
BERCHING, HERBERT.—Seconded from Basutoland service; apptd. ag. sub-inspr., Bechuanaland Prot. pol., Dec., 1920; ag. astt. res. mag., 1921; ag. res. mag., 1923; passed 3rd grade Seswana examn., 1926.

BELCHER, CHARLES FREDERIC, O.B.E. (1928), M.B.E. (1919), M.A., LL.B.—B. 1876; ed. at Geelong Gram. Schl. and Trin. Coll., Melbourne (final scholarship in classics and comp. philology, Shakespeare scholarship); admitted to practice, Victoria, Oct., 1902; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, June, 1909 (cert. of honour); conveyancer, Uganda Prot., July, 1914; custodia of enemy property, Uganda, Apr., 1915; mag., Uganda, June, 1916; astt. judge, Zanzibar, June, 1920; judge of H.B.M. ct. of appeal for E. Africa, Aug., 1920; atty.-gen., Nyassaland, Sept., 1920; mem., exec. and legis. couns.; ag. judge, high ct., Mar., 1921 to Jan., 1922; judge, high ct., 14th May, 1924.

BELL, HON. LIBUT.-COL. EDWARD, O.B.E. (1924), M.B.E. (1918), V.D.—Served in R.I.C., Dec., 1885 to Aug., 1896; attached 2nd Batt., Durham Light Infantry, 1896; Hythe certif. of musketry and instr.'s certif., Maxim machine gun; drill instr. to local force, St. Kitts-Nevis, 12th Aug., 1896; ag. inspr., L. Ialda, police and adjut. St. Kitts-Nevis defence force, 1898; inspr., L. Ialda, police, Sept., 1898; and capt. and adjut. defence force; ag. inspr.-gen., L. Ialda, police, and ag. inspecting offr. of defence forces of colony, Apr. to Nov., 1903; ag. inspr.-gen., L. Ialda, police, 1st Apr., 1905, subsequently confirmed in commd. of force as chief inspr.; comdt. local forces, with local rank of lieut.-col.; extra A.D.C. to gov. Sir Bickham Recott, 1912; mem. exec. coun., Leeward Is., 1915; police medal, 1915; recd. thanks of H.M.'s Govt., for services rendered during riot and disturbances in Antigua in March, 1918; is a J.P.

BELL, Rt. Hon. Sir Francis Henry Dillon, P.C. (1926), G.C.M.G. (1923), K.C.M.G. (1915), K.O.—B. 1861; B.A., Camb.; barrister, Middle Temple, 1874; entd. New Zealand parl., 1893; leader of legis. coun., 1912-26; min. of internal affairs, 1912 and 1920; min. of immigr. in National ministry, 1915; also leader of leg. coun.; atty.-gen., 1918-26; min. of educm., min. of public health and min. of immigr., 1919-20; commr. of state forests, 1919-22; min. of marine, 1921-22; min. of just., Jan. to June, 1923-26; min. of external affrs., 1923; prime min., 14th-30th May, 1926; mem. of exec. coun. without portfolio, 1926.

BELL, GEORGE FREDERICK.—Cape civ. ser., 8th Feb., 1902; registr. of native lab., Transvaal, 1910; pub. prov., native affrs. ct., Johannesburg, 1913; S. African Expedy. Force, Dee., 1915; polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 10th Aug., 1916.

BELL, MAJOR GRAHAM AIRDIE.—Clt., P.O.D., Ottawa, 1890-92; rly. dept., 1892; finan. compt., 1913; asst. to min., 1917; dep. min., 1919; mily. serv. with 43rd Regt., Ottawa, 1891-1915; sp. serv. in England and France, 1915; vice-pres. and vice-pres. of finance, Canadian nat. rly., 1922.

BELL, MAJOR G. H., M.C., Officer Legion d'Honneur.—B. 1886; entered army, 1906; India, 1907-9; Nigeria, W.A.F.F., 1911; asst. dir. of surveys, Nigeria, 1912-14; served in France, 1914-16; Palestine and Syria, 1917-18; dep. ch. polit. offr., Egyptian Expedy. Force, 1919; G.S.O., Intelligence, E.E.F., 1920; dir. topographical branch, Gold Coast Survey, 1920.

BELL, SIR HENRY HESKETH JOUDOY, G.C.M.G. (1925), K.C.M.G. (1908), C.M.G. (1903).—B. 1866; ed. in Paris; 3rd clk. gov.-in-chief's office, Barbados, May, 1882; transferred to treas. dept., Grenada, 1883; supervisor of customs, G. Coast, Jan., 1890; sen. ass't treas., G. Coast, Aug., 1891; ag. dist. commr. and sheriff, Accra, 1892; ag. col. treas., 1893; rec-gen., Bahamas, Dec., 1894; represented Harbour Ialda, in House of Assem., 1895-6; ag. col. sec., July to Oct., 1897, Apr. to Nov., 1898; adminstr. of Dominica, Aug., 1899; ag. gov., Leeward Is., Aug., 1904, and from June, 1905; commr. and commr.-in-chief, Uganda Prot., 31st Jan., 1906; gov., ditto, 18th Oct., 1907; gov., N. Nigeria, 30th Oct., 1909; gov. Leeward Is., 1912; gov., Mauritius, 7th Jan., 1916; assumed gov't., 18th May, 1916; author of "Geography of the Gold Coast," "Obah," etc.
BELL, LESLIE LIVINGSTONE.—2nd clk., govt. sec.'s dept., Papua, 20th Jan., 1906; chief inspr., dept. of native affairs and control, 20th Feb., 1909.

BELL, NORRIS GARRETT, M.I.C.E.—B. 1860; ed. Edinburgh Collegiate Schl. and Edin. Univ.; in Queensland rly. serv. for 28 yrs., and sometime ch. engnr.; engnr.-in-ch., Commonwealth rlys., Apr., 1914; rly. commr., C. of A., 1922.

BELLAMY, WILLIAM EDWARD.—Ag. asst. commr. of taxation, B. Guiana, 12th Sept., 1893; dist. commr. and crown land offr., Pomeroon River, 26th July, 1895; 3rd grade commr., I.R.B., treasury, 24th Mar., 1896; 2nd grade ditto, 22nd May, 1901; asst. inspr. of dists., L.G.B., 5th Sept., 1906; ag. sec., L.G.B., and inspr. of dists., 14th Dec., 1907 to 12th June, 1909, 1st Oct., 1910 to 23rd Nov., 1911, 24th Oct., 1912 to 14th Feb., 1914, and from 15th Mar. to 12th Oct., 1914.

BELLANTI, J. J., LL.D., Malta Univ., 1910.—B. 1886; ed. St. Ignatius' Coll., Malta; matric. (3rd place in hons. list), Malta Univ., 1902; olk., civil serv., Malte, 1907; mem. of coun. of govt., 1911; asst. crown advocate, Goso, 1914; assessor of taxes, Malta treasury., Apr., 1923.

BELLETH, BENJAMIN, I.S.O. (1926).—B. 1875; ag. office asst., Kalutara Kach., Ceylon, July, 1918; apptd. to cls. V. of civ. serv., July, 1919; extra office asst. to the col. sec., Aug., 1920; off. asst. to col. sec., Apr., 1926.
BEMINSTERS, FREDERICK ARBON.—Tech. instr., N. Nigeria, 17th June, 1914; instr. in arts and crafts, 1st Jan., 1920.

BENHAM, JOHN ELLIOTT.—B. 1886; capt. 3rd E. Lancs. Regt.; war service, German and Portuguese E. Africa, 18th Mar., 1917 to 1st Dec., 1918; asst. dist. commr., Sierra Leone, 29th Apr., 1914; dist. commr., 21st Aug., 1922; asst.-col. sec., 1st Jan., 1925; J.P., 1926; ag. senr. asst. col. sec. and olk., exec. coun. on several occasions.

BENJACAR, LEO.—B. 1863; ednt. Malta civ. ser., 1881; clk., 1st cls., 1905; asst. registr., superior cts., 1907; registr., ditto, 1910; comptroller of charitable institns., 1915; offl. mem. exec. coun., and of coun. of govt., 11th Feb., 1916.

BENJAMIN, LOUIS EDMUND.—B. 1865; ed., pub. schl., Graham's Town, S. African Coll., Cape Town and St. Peter's Coll., Cambridge; sometime Maynard and Univ. scholar, Univ. of Cape of Good Hope, and law scholar, St. Peter's, Cambridge; B.A., Capo, LL.B., Cambridge; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1889; K.O., Cape, 1907, and Union of S. Africa, 1910; judge, sup. ct., Union of S. Africa, 1920.

BENNETT, CHARLES KAYE.—B. 1867; Tempy. outdoor offr., S. Leone, July, 1887; tide waiter, 1888; bd. of trade clk., 1889; recd. thanks of S. of S. with gratuity, 1889; warehouseman, 1890; ch. clk. of customs, Niger Coast Protectorate, 1891; inauguration of P.O., 1891; framing of cust. laws and regns., 1892; supervisor of cust., S. Nigeria, 1900; auditor, Abeokuta, Lagos, 1903; requested to re-organise cust. dept. at Abeokuta, 1904; financial adviser (comptroller), 1907.

BENNETT, HON. SIR JOHN ROBERT, K.B.E. (1916).—M.H.A., St. John's West, Newf'dld., 1904; M.E.C., 1913; col. sec., 1913; mem., defence comtee., 1915; deputy chief censor, telegraphs and cables, 1914; press censor, 1915; min. of militia, 1917.

BENNETT, SYDNEY CHRISTMAS, A.M.I.C.E., A.M.Inst. Mun. and Cty. Engrs.—Ed. Sherborne Schl.; asst. engnr., P.W.D., E.A.P., 1914-15; pioneer, E. A. Pioneers, 1915-16; 2nd lieut., E.A. Rly. Corps., 1916-17; lt., 1917-18; exec. engnr., Kenya, 1921; ment. in depe., "1914-15" Star, Br. War and Victory meda.

BENNETTS, ALBERT THOMAS.—B. 1890; ed. Camborne Schl. of Mines, 1908-11; medallist of the Royal Cornwall Polytechnic Soce., 1910; 1st cls. hons. and silver medallist, City and Guilds of London Inst., 1911; 1st cls. diploma Camborne Sch. of Mines, 1911; mines dept., F.M.S., 1912-25.

BENSON, SIR J. HAWTREY, KT. BACH. (1912), A.B., M.D., F.R.C.P. Irel.—Ed. Trin. Coll., Dublin; 1st hons. maths., graduated, 1864; consulting physician to Royal City of Dublin Hosp., and to the Monkstown Hosp.; Fellow of Acad. of Med., Ireland; late censor and examr., Royal Coll. of Phys., Ireland, and late external examr. in med., Dub. Univ.; is now med. adver. for Ireland to C.O.; author of numerous papers and articles on technical med. subjects; ret., 1926.

BENTINCK, MAJOR WALTER GUY, BARON, C.M.G. (1912), D.S.O. (1902).—Ed. at Marlborough Coll., Jena Univ., Germany, and Royal Mil. Coll., Sandhurst; joined Rife Brig., 1885; capt., 1894; major, 1902; ret., 1905; intelligence dept., W.O., 1899; served throughout S. African war, 1899-1902; (ment. in despatches, Queen's medal with six clasps, King's medal with two clasps); dist. comnrs., Vereeniging, 1900; res. mag., Wakkers-troom dist., Transvaal, 1901-1907; Transvaal mem. of Natal-Transvaal boundary comnrs., 1902; sec. in office of high comnrs. for S. Africa, 1907; ag. Imperial sec., Aug., 1907, to Mar., 1908; asst. Imperial sec., Apr., 1908; chmn. Swaziland water comnrs., 1909.

BENTWICH, NORMAN DE MATTOX, O.B.E., M.C.—B. 1883; ed. St. Paul's Schil. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; 1st cls., class. tripes, pts. I and II; Whewell schol.; called to bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1908; inspr., native cts., Egypt, 1912; lecturer, Khedivial schil. of law, 1913-15; major, R.A.S.C., 1917; senr. judi. offr., O.R.T.A. (S.), 1918-20; legal sec., govt. of Palestine, 1920-22; atty.-gen., 1922.
BRNZIES, DOUGLAS, M.A., B.Sc.—B. 1899; ed. Heriot's Schil., Edinburgh; Edin. Univ., 1919-25; M.A., B.Sc.; France, June-Dec., 1918; demob., Jan., 1919; asst. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.

BERKELEY, HENRY SEGRAVE—B. 1879; served in S. African War with Kitchener's Horse (medal and five clasps), 1900-1901; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1904; political offr., N. Nigeria, 29th April, 1906; stat. mag., Nigeria, Jan., 1914; pol. mag., 21st July, 1921; ag. puisne judge, 9th Apr. to 10th May, 1923.

BERKELEY, CAPT. HUBERT, I.S.O. (1922).—B. 1864; midshipman, R.N.R., 1881; to Perak, 1884; pol., 1886; supt. of Penghulus, 1889; ag. collr. and mag., Upper Perak, 1891; asst. mag., Gopeng, 1894; ag. dist. mag., Kuala Kangsar, 1896; dist. mag., Upper Perak, 1899; dist. offr., Klang, 1903; ditto, U. Perak, Sept., 1904; also warden of mines and asst. comnrs. of police for Upper Perak; capt., Worcestershire regt., 10th Deo., 1914; served through European War (3 medals); resumed duty, Perak, Deo., 1918; I.S.O., 1922.

BERKELEY, SIR MAURICE JULIAN, KT. BACH. (1927).—Called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn; entd. col. serv., 1883; ag. pol. mag., Tobago, 1882; ditto, St. Vincent, 1884; ditto, Barbados, 1885-1892; pol. mag. Dist. "D," Barbados, 1892; ditto, Bridgetown, 1899; ag. judge, ct. of appeal, 1900 and 1901; S.J.P., Pol. of Spain, Trinidad, 1902; ag. judge, sup. ct., 1906, 1907 and 1908; ag. mem. of educn. board, 1907 and 1908; puisne judge, B. Guiana, 1906; ag. chief just., July to Sept., 1910, Apr. to Sept., 1912, May to July, 1913, May to Nov., 1914, Oct., 1919 to Feb., 1920, Oct., 1920, Feb. to Oct., 1922, and Apr. to Dec., 1925; ag. atty.-gen., Oct., 1912 to Feb., 1913.

BERNE, CAPT. JAMES LEO, O.B.E. (1912)—B. 1886; ed. Dulwich Coll.; 2nd Lieut., Royal Irish Batt., 1904; capt., 1910; served with Sierra Leone Batt. W.A.F.F., 1907-1912; served with Franco-Liberian bndry. comnrs., 1908 (recd. thanks of French govt.); served with Anglo-French bndry. comnrs., 1911; capt., reserve of officers; asst. dist. comnrs., Somaliland Prot., 13th Sept., 1912; dist. comnrs., 28th March, 1914; served with Admiralty war staff, intell. div., Apr. to Aug., 1916; accompanied mission to Abyssinia on the occasion of coronation of Empress Zauditu, 1917; 3rd cls. Star of Ethiopia, 1917; sec. asst. sec. to govt. of Tanganyika Territory, 1st Apr., 1921; ag. asst. ch. sec., Sept., 1922 to May, 1923, Dec., 1923 to March, 1924.

BERRY, LESLIE HAMILTON.—B. 1884; ed. Whitgift Gram. Schil. and Finsbury Tech. Coll., Assoc. of Inst. of Chemistry; B.Sc. (London); passed lower stand. in Mende; asst. dist. comnrs., 27th Nov., 1922; dist. comnrs., 3rd Oct., 1917.

BERTEAU, F. C., I.S.O. (1914).—Ed. Victoria Coll., Jersey, 1866-72; ent. Newfoundland civ. ser. as olk. H.M. Customa, 1878; olk. of cust., Labrador, 1881; olk. in col. sec.'s off., 1889; ch. olk., 1890; J.P. for col., 1893; mission to Ottawa to inquire into Canadian fin. system, with view of applying same to Newfild., May, 1898; audr.-gen. Newfild., July, 1898; ag. cashier, Newfoundland savings bank, 1911.

BERTRAM, SIR ANTON, KT. BACH. (1916); K.C. (1913)—Ed. at City of London sch., and Camb.; scholar of Caius Coll., 1887; Fellow, 1891; 1st cls. class. trip., part I., 1890; ditto, part II., 1891; Powis medal for Latin verse; Chancellor's medal for classics, 1891; R.A., 1890; M.A., 1892; president, Union Soc., 1891; Tancred law studentship, Lincoln's Inn, 1890; called, 1893; atty.-gen., Bahamas, 1902; elected to H. of Assem., June, 1903; ag. col. sec., May to Sept., 1903; admstd. govt., Sept. to Oct., 1905; ag. ch. justice, Sep., Nov., 1906; chrmn. of comnrs. on the Out Is., 1906; puisne judge, Cyprus, 1906; atty.-gen., Ceylon, 19th May, 1911; mem. of exec. and legis. couns.; ag. ch. justice, Apr., 1918; ch. justice, July, 1918; services lent to govt. of Palestine, Dec., 1920 to May, 1921; ret., 1925; joint author, "Handbook to Workmen's Compensation Acts, 1897 and 1900."

BEST, HON. SIR ROBERT WALLACE, K.C.M.G. (1908).—B. 1856; vice-pres. of the exec. coun., Australian Commonwealth; leader of the Deakin govt. in the Senate, Feb., 1907; min. for trade and customs, C. of A., 1909; now retd.

BEST, SIR THOMAS ALEXANDER VANS, K.B.E. (1928), C.M.G. (1916), C.B.E. (1918).—B. 1870; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1894; Taylorn Univ. scholar; gold medalist of French Ministry of pub. instruction; asst., B. Cent. Africa admin., 1896; judicial offr., 1896; ag. ch. judicial offr., Nov., 1899, to Mar., 1900; ag. vice-consul, Chinde, Aug., 1902, to Apr., 1903; ag. dep. comnrs., Apr. to Dec., 1903, and June, 1905, to Jan., 1906; dist. res., 1st cls., Apr., 1906; col. sec., Falkland Is., Oct., 1909; ag. gov., Deo., 1909, to Dec., 1910; attended Impl. Educm. Conf., 1911; col. sec., Leeward Is., Jan., 1913; ag. gov., May to Sept., 1914, Apr. to Aug., 1915, and from Jan., 1916, to Mar., 1919; reed. thanks of H.M.'s Govt. for services during disturbances in Antigua in Mar., 1918; col. sec., Trinidad, Ang., 1919; ag. gov., June-Aug., 1920, June, 1921 to Jan., 1922, June to Oct., 1923 and from Aug., 1924; lieut.-gov., Malta, 1925.
BETANCOURT, NELSON.—B. 1887; sworn survr.; 2nd asst. inspr. of mines, Trinidad, 1st April, 1909; 1st inspr., mines, 17th Apr., 1921.

BETHEL, CHARLES PREVIVAL.—B. 1891; ag. 4th clk., post office, Bahamas, Apl. and Nov., 1909; cadet, Out islands, Feb., 1910; 3rd clk., C.S.O., and clk. to pub. bd. of wks., New Providence, Sept., 1912; 2nd clk., C.S.O., Feb., 1914; ag. chief clk., C.S.O., 12th May to 1st Aug., 1919; chief clk., C.S.O., 1st Aug., 1919; enlisted for active service, 1915, 2nd lieut., Mar., 1919; served in France; twice wounded; discharged from Army, Apr., 1919; resumed duties, May, 1919; ag. clk. in charge, col. sec.'s office, 20th Aug. to 2nd Sept., 1923; ag. col. sec. and temp. mem., exec. coun., 22nd Oct. to 12th Nov., 1923, 4th May to 26th Oct., 1924 and from 13th Sept., 1925; ag. A.D.C. to admstr., 26th Sept. to 8th Nov., 1925.

BETHELL, WILLIS H.—B. 1875; pub. schl. teacher, Bahamas, 1897; customs offr., Mar., 1901; ag. chief clk., audit dept., June, 1913; chief clk., audit dept., Apl., 1913; ag. auditor of pub. acct., for various periods between 1916 and 1923; audr., Dec., 1925; audr., pub. acct., 1st Dec., 1925.

BEVEN, ALLAN.—B. 1867; ed. Royal Coll., Colombo, Barrister-at-Law, Mid. Temple; cadet, local div., Ceylon civ. ser., Sept., 1892; asst. collr. of cust., Galle, Jan., 1893; pol. mag., Panadure and addl. mag., Kalutara, Mar., 1895; pol. mag., Chilaw, Nov., 1896; offr. asst. to govt. agt., E. Prov., Sept., 1896, to Oct., 1899; dist. judge, Batticaloa, Mar., 1899; Kalutara, Mar., 1900; Kegalla, Oct., 1900; commr. of requests and pol. mag., Kurunegala, Mar., 1905; dist. judge, Ratnapura, Oct., 1907; Negombo, April, 1909; Tangalla, July, 1909; Ratnapura, Oct., 1912; Kalutara, Oct., 1914; ag. addnl. dist. judge, Colombo, June, 1921; dist. judge, Kurunegala, Sept., 1922.

BEVEN, HARRY EDWARD.—B. 1874; cadet, local div., Ceylon civ. ser., May, 1896; offr. asst. to govt. agt., Kurunegala, June, 1896; Anuradhapura, May, 1900; Galle, Mar., 1901; pol. mag., Balapitiya, Sept., 1901; Matara, Dec., 1901; offr. asst. to govt. agt., E. Prov., Aug., 1905; pol. mag., Galle, April, 1907; ag. dist. judge, Galle, Oct., 1910; pol. mag., Galle, Jan., 1911; ag. dist. judge, Negombo, Mar., 1913; Kegalle, Aug., 1916; dep. commr. of stamps, July, 1919; ag. regisr.-gen., May, 1926.

BEVIR, ANTHONY, M.A.—B. 1895; scholar of Eton, 1909, and of Hertford Coll., Oxford, 1914; M.A., 1923; served with 7th King's Liverpool Regt., July, 1915 to Apr., 1919; twice ment. in despatches; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, asst. prin., C.O. 9th Feb., 1920.
BEWES, GEORGE PRICE HOPE.—B. 1892; ed., Clifton Coll. and Royal Naval Coll., Osborne; asst. dist. commr., Gold Coast, 25th Aug., 1920.

BEWSHUR, FREDERICK WILLIAM, D.S.O., O.B.E., M.C., F.Z.S., F.R.G.S.—B. 1886; ed. St. Paul's and Merton Coll., Oxford; served in Gt. War, 1914-18 (brig. major); comdt., Palestinian Gendarmerie, 1921; offr. commdg. Transjordan Frontier Force, 1926.

BEYERS, FREDERICK WILLIAM, B.A., LL.B., K.C., M.L.A.—Ed. S. African Coll., Schol. and at S. African Coll., Cape Town; called to the bar in 1908; mem., Transvaal parlt., for Turffontein, 1907-10; attended Col. Confoe, with General Botha, London, Mar.-May, 1907; atty.-gen., Transvaal, 1911-14 and for Cape Prov., 1914-15; resigned as atty.-gen., Aug., 1915; elec. to Union parl., as mem. for Edenburg, O.F.S., since 1918; min. of mines and industries in Hertzog cabinet, 30th June, 1924.

BEYERS, G. J.—Ck., P.W. dept., Cape, Aug., 1891; bookkeeper, treas., Feb., 1892; asst. acctnt., July, 1897; stamping commr. and asst. acctnt., Nov., 1903; asst. assessor of income tax, Kimberley, Oct., 1908; recvr. of rev., Bloemfontein, Jan., 1911; offr. i/c. Orange Free State repatriation debts, Dec., 1912; recvr. of inland rev., Bloemfontein, Nov., 1916.

BHARUCHA, NAVOJI MANEKJI.—B. 1890; B.A., Cantab. cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Jan., 1914; office asst. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Jan., 1914; attached to Kurunegala Kach., June, 1914; extra office asst. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., in addition to his own duties, Aug., 1914; ag. pol. mag., Kurunegala, Apr., 1916; office asst. to govt. agt., E. Prov., Jan., 1917; pol. mag., Kurunegala, Nov., 1917; Balapitiya, Nov., 1918; dist. judge, Tangalla, Mar., 1922; ag. dist. judge, Chilaw, May, 1922; asst. stimt. offr., May, 1925.
BICKLE, W. H.—B. 1885; apptd. after compet. exam. clk. of the 2nd div., and assigned to C.O., 17th Mar., 1906; cler. offr. higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922.

BICKMORE, CHARLES WILLIAM.—B. 1888; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., 17th Nov., 1911; attached to Colombo Kachcheri, Dec., 1911; col. sec.'s office, Aug., 1912; Galle Kachcheri, Nov., 1912; ag. office asst. to govt. agt., N.C. Prov., Feb., 1913; offr. asst. to govt. agt., W. Prov., Dec., 1913; pol. mag., Jaffna and Kayts, June, 1914; office asst. to govt. agt., Cent. Prov., Nov., 1914; pol. mag., Panadure, Aug., 1915; seconded for serv. as ag. financial asst. to chmn., mun. coun., Colombo, Mar., 1916; pol. mag., Kurunegala, Jan., 1917; ag. asst. govt. agt., Mullativu, Nov., 1917; on mil. duty, 1918; ag. dist. judge, Kegalla, June, 1919; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, Nov., 1919; ditto Matare, May, 1921; asst. col. treas., May, 1922; ag. col. treas., 30th Sept. to 17th Oct., 1926.

BIDEN, ARTHUR GERALD.—B. 1878; ed. at Radley Coll. and Trin. Coll., Oxon.; B.A. (honour jurisprudence), 1902; clk., col. audit branch of E. and A. dept., Feb., 1903; asst. audr., G. Coast, Apr., 1904; local audr., Gambia, Sept., 1905; lieut., Gambia volr. artillery, July, 1907; asst. audr., E.A.P., 1st June, 1908; seconded to audit dept., Somaliland Prov., Nov., 1908; local auditor, Mauritius, 24th May, 1912; food contr., 1917; audr., Br. Guiana, 27th Feb., 1920; audr., Trinidad, 17th Mar., 1923; 2nd commr., income tax, Trinidad, Jan., 1924; audr., Nigeria, 1926.
BIENEMAN, G. A. J., B.A.—Ed. Dean Close, Cheltenham and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; junr. supt., educn. dept., N. Nigeria, 9th July, 1913; head of training coll., 1st Oct., 1921.

BIGELOW, HENRY VERDER, K.C., B.A., LL.B., M.A.—B. 1874; ed. pub. schls., Truro, N.S., and Dalhousie Univ., B.A., 1894; LL.B., 1896; M.A., 1897; admitted to bar of N.S. 1896, practised law at Truro, N.S., and Regina, Sask.; K.C., Sask., 1913; judge, King's Bench, Sask., Feb., 1918.

BIGG, WILFRED JOSEPH.—B. 1897; boy clk., G.P.O., 22nd Nov., 1912; apptd. after compet. exam. clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv. and assigned to C.O., 1st Dec., 1914; on mil. ser. from 31st Aug., 1914, to 8th May, 1919; cler. offr., higher grade, 1922; asst. prin., 1st Aug., 1925.
BILKE, ROBERT HAROLD.—B. 1882; ed. privately; L. & N.W. Rly., 1897-1914; dep. acct., F.M.S.R., May, 1914; ch. acct., Oct., 1915; mem., rly. bd., Oct., 1923.

BIRCH, SIR ERNEST WOODFORD, K.C.M.G. (1911); C.M.G. (1900).—B. 1857; ed. Harrow; cadet, S. Sttlmts., Jan., 1876; employed in the C.O., Downing-street, to July, 1878; sec. to the pol. comm., 1879; passed final exam. in Malay, Aug., 1880; J.P. and a mag., Malacca; took charge Malacca land office, Feb., 1881; ag. collr. of land rev., Singapore, 1881, 2nd ast. col. sec., 1882; on special mission in H.M.S. "Essoir," to report on the Cocos-Keeling Is., 1885; mag. and collr. of land rev., Malacca, 1888; ag. Br. res., Selangor, May, 1892, to Jan., 1893; sec. to govt., Perak, July, 1893; ag. res., Perak, Sept., 1895, to July, 1896; Br. res., Negri-Sembilan, June, 1897; govnr. and commander-in-chief of the col. of Labuan and of B.N. Borneo, Dec., 1900; Br. res., Perak, 10th Feb., 1904; retired, 1911.

BIRCH, HUGH MASSEY, M.I.C.E.—Ed. privately and King's Coll., Ldn.; trooper, M.M. Pol., 1897; exec. engrn., P.W.D., E.A.P., 1907; local capt., E.A. Forces, 1916-18; exec. engrn., P.W.D., Kenya, 1918; B.S.A. Coy.'s, Br. War and Victory meda.
BIRCH, W. C.—Ast. analyst, E.A.P., July, 1914; senr. chemical offr., Apr., 1923.

BIRD, EDGAR CLARENCE FREDERICK.—B. 1886; ed. Xaverian Coll.; ast. chief of customs, Zanzibar Prot., 29th Oct., 1914; ag. chief of customs, 24th Jan. to 16th Aug., 1915, 16th May, 1916, to 23rd Feb., 1917, and 19th Dec., 1917; marshal, H.B.M. Court for Zanzibar (in Prize), 25th Jan. to 15th Aug., 1915, and from 19th Dec., 1917; immigr. offr., 23rd Sept., 1915; ast. comptr., cust., Tanganyika Territory, 15th Oct., 1920; ag. comptr., cust., 14th Feb. to 8th Dec., 1921, 26th Sept., 1922 to 17th Jan., 1923 and from 18th Oct., 1923.

BIRD, REGINALD.—B. 1893; ed. Merton Coll., Oxford; B.A. 1919; 12th and 6th Batt., York & Lancaster Regt., 1914-19; cadet, S.S., Oct., 1919; ag. dist. offr., Alor Gajah, June, 1920; ast. dist. judge, Singapore, Aug., 1922; ast. sec. to res., Selangor, July, 1924; dist. offr., Kuala Pilah, July, 1925; ag. sec. to res., Negri Sembilan, Apr., 1926; ag. state treas., ditto, in addn., July, 1926.
BISCOE, CECIL JULIAN TYNDALE, M.C., B.A., F.R.G.S.—B. 1895; ed. Trin. Coll., Glenalmond, Trent Coll., Derbyshire, and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; supt., educn., Tanganyika Territory, 1924.

BISCOE, VINCENT FREDERICK.—B. 1886; ed. at Cheltenham Coll. and Clare Coll., Camb.; B.A., June, 1908; 2nd lieut. London Rifle Brig. (Territorial Force), June, 1909; called to the bar, Inner Temple, Nov., 1910; ast. res., N. Nigeria, 2nd Aug., 1911; 2nd cl. dist. offr., 5th Dec., 1919; ag. station mag., 29th Dec., 1924.

BLACK, CAPT. EDWARD HAMILTON, M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), D.P.H. (Oambs.), D.T.M. and H. (Eng.), Capt., R.A.M.C.—B. 1881; ed. Edinburgh Univ., 1906; med. offr., F.M.S., June, 1914; temp. comm., R.A.M.C., June, 1915; B.E.F. (France), 16th divn., Sept., 1916; attd., 7th Corps, B.E.F. (France), 1918; resumed duties health offr., F.M.S., Nov., 1919; ag. senr. med. offr., Perak, Nov., 1920 in addition; health offr., Kinta, regis. of births and deaths, Perak, 1922.

BLACK, HON. FRANCOIS MOLLISON.—B. 1870; ed. Perth Academy; ent. civ. serv., post office savingsbank, England, 1886; proceeded to Canada, 1889; held various business apps.; apptd. to public utilities comm., Alberta, 1917; served on the federal food control bd., 1917; pres., Bd. of Trade, Calgary, Alberta, 1916-17; provincial treas., Manitoba, 8th Aug., 1922; el. for constituency of Rupert's Land, 13th Sept., 1922.

BLACK, JOHN ALAN.—B. 1891; cadet F.M.S., 1914; offr., cls. V., 1917; offr., cls. IV., 1921; ag. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Jan., 1924; ag. prot. of Chinese, Perak, Aug., 1924; ag. prot., Chinese, Negri Sembilan, May, 1925; ag. ast. contr., lab., Seremban, June, 1925; ag. state treas., Negri Sembilan, in addn., Sept., 1925.

BLACK, WILLIAM JOHN.—B. 1872; ed. pub. and high schls., Ont.; grad. in agr., Toronto Univ.; editor, "Farmers' Advocate," Winnipeg, 1902-04; dep. min., agr., Manitoba, 1904-06; pres., Manitoba Agr. Coll., 1905-15; sec., economic and development comm. of Canada, 1915-16; commr. of agr., Canada, 1916-18; chmn., soldier stltnt. bd., 1918-20; dep. min., immigr. and coloni. zation, 1921; man., colonisation dept., Canadian National Rlys., London, 1923; ch. colonisation dept., C.N. rlys., Montreal, 1924.

BLACKALL, HENRY WILLIAM BUTLER, B.A., L.L.B. (1st place).—Ed. Stonehurst and Trinity Coll., Dublin; Victoria prizeman, jurisprudence and internat. law; 1st of 1st cls., hon., mod. history; John Brooke scholar, King's Inn; called to Irish Bar, 1912; crown counsel, E.A.P., Oct., 1919; M.L.C., Apr., 1920; crown counsel, Nigeria, 30th May, 1923; ag. supt.-gen., July to Sept., 1923.

BLACKMORE, HARRY.—Ed at St. Bartholomew's Sch., Wednesbury; Science and Art Schl., Walsall; St. John's Coll., York, and Victoria Univ., Leeds; senior ast., St. Bartholomew's School, Wednesbury, 1900-1903; English and music master and pupil teacher, Centre and Higher Grade Schl., Walsall, 1904-1905; head master, Riccall Schl., York, 1905-1908; normal master, B. Guiana, July, 1908; headmaster, primary schl., G. Coast, 1912; impr. of schls., 1914; seconded to G. Coast Regt., 1917; returned to civ. duty, 1918; ag. dir. educn., Mar., 1924; ch. inspnr., schls., Sierra Leone, 15th May, 1924; ag. dir. educn., 6th Aug., 1924.

BLACKWELL, KENNETH RAY.—B. 1896; cadet, F.M.S., 15th Mar., 1921; Malay exam., 1st dist., 27th June, 1921; mag., 2nd cls., 23rd Aug., 1921; ast. supt. of chandu, Kuala Lumpur, 5th Dec., 1921; passed Malay exam., 2nd std., 10th Jan., 1922; passed cadet, 10th Jan., 1922; ast. supt. of chandu, Ipoh, June, 1922 and May, 1923; ag. senr. ast. supt., chandu monopoly, Perak, Feb., 1923; supervisor, cust., Kubak, Johore, Aug., 1923; ag. ast. dist. offr., Gopeng and Kampar, Oct., 1923; offr., cls. V., Nov., 1923, ast. supt., lands, Kelantan, Apr., 1924; ag. dist. offr., Pasir Putih, June, 1924; ast. dist. offr., Kroh and ast. supervisor, cust., Kroh, Jan., 1926.
BLACKWOOD, A. Mo.A., M.B., Ch. B. (Glasgow).—Lieut., R.A.M.C., 1st May, 1917; capt., 1918; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1st Apr., 1919.

BLACKWOOD, JOHN ALEXANDER.—B. 1874; ed. Dollar Academy, and at Heriot-Watt Coll., Edinburgh; articled to Scottish solr., 1892; Impl. mil. rlys. (now I.S.A.R.), 1901; entd. Crown Agent's office, 1902; ast. regis. of inscribed stock, 1914; head of insurance and checking dept., 1919; ast. sec. to W. African Currency Bd., 1912-1914; dep. head, stores dept., 14th Nov., 1921.

BLAIR, DYSON.—B. 1874; apptd. to survr.-gen.'s dept., Ceylon, 1st Oct., 1891; ast. supr. of surveys, 1st Jan., 1896; offr. in charge of topographical survey party, 1897 to 1905; asst. comsnr. of lands, Fiji, 13th May, 1906; ag. comsnr. of lands, 22nd Jan., 1907; comsnr. of lands and crown survr., Fiji, 20th Oct., 1908; chmn. of mining bd., 1909; M.L.C., M.E.C., 1919; dep. dir. of surveys, Uganda, 3rd Dec., 1920; ag. dir. of surveys, land offr. and comsnr. of mines, Uganda, 13th Aug., 1923; dir., surveys, land offr., and comsnr. of mines, Uganda, 1st Mar., 1924.

BLAIR, HON. JAMES WILLIAM.—B. 1871; called to bar, 1894; M.L.A., Queensland, for Ipswich, 1902-15; atty.-gen. in Morgan and Kidston ministries, 1903-7; also sec. for mines for most of that period; sec., pub. instr., Denham ministry, 1912-15; puisne judge, Apr., 1922; ch. just., Queensland, 1926.

BLAIR, ROBERT WESTRUP, A.R.C.S.C.I., F.I.C.—B. 1885; ed., George Watson's Coll., Edin., R. Coll. So., Ireland; asst. chem., Imp. Inst., 1905-06; asst. chem., Metro. Water Bd., Lond., 1906-11; lst asst. chem., Wellcome Trop. Research Lab., Khartoum, Sudan, 1911-12; asst. chem., Inst. for Med. Research, Kuala Lumpur, F.M.S., 1912; chem. Inst. for Med. Research, Kuala Lumpur, F.M.S., 1913; 2nd lieut., East Lancs. Regt., 1916; tempy. lieut., 1916; sp. brigade R.E., 1916; ment. in des., 1918; ag. capt., R.E., 1918; chem., Inst. for Med. Research, Kuala Lumpur, Nov., 1919; 2nd lieut., M.S.V.R., Dec., 1922.

BLAKELY, DONALD QUENTIN.—B. 1875; clk., atty.-gen.'s off., B. Honduras, Apr., 1898; ag. regir.-gen., May, 1906, to Mar., 1907; 1st cls. olk., regir.-gen.'s office, Apr., 1907; ag. regir.-gen., Oct., 1908, to May, 1909; comsnr. of sup. ct., B. Hond., Mar., 1908; ag. regir.-gen., Oct., 1908, to May, 1909, and Aug., 1912.

BLAKELY, JAMES FRANCIS.—B. 1877; ag. 5th elk., treasy., cust. and P.O., B. Honduras, May, 1892; temp. clk., P.W.D., Jan., 1893; 3rd clk., C.S.O., Feb., 1894; ag. 2nd clk., Apl., 1895 and Mar., 1896; clk. to survr.-gen., Nov., 1896; elk. and acct., P.W.D., Jan., 1899; ag. cust. and excise offr., Mar., 1904; 1st cls. elk., treasy. and cust. and elk. to quarantine bd., Feb., 1896; ag. ch. elk., Jan., 1907; elk. to comsnrs. of currency, Jan., 1907; ch. elk., treasy. and cust., Apr., 1909; supervisor, cust., 2nd grade, S. Nigeria, Dec., 1909; finan. asst., 2nd grade, Jan., 1911; ag. 1st grade, Jan., 1912; ag. prov. treas., June, 1913; asst. treas., Nigeria, 1st Jan., 1919.

BLAND, EDWARD MALBY, C.M.G. (1927).—B. 1878; ed. Trin. Coll. Schl., Port Hope and Royal Mill Coll., Kingston, Canada; employed on Can. Pac. and Can. Northern rly., 1897-1900; served in S. African war in Strathcona's Horse (medal and three clasps); asst. engr., Impl. mil. rlys., S. Africa, 1900-1902; ditto, C.S.A.R., 1902-1908; Baro Kano rly., 1918, and acted as dir. of rlys., N. Nigeria, Feb. to Oct., 1912; asst. engr. in chief, Nigerian rly., 1st Jan., 1913; special mission to B. Guiana to report on proposed rly. development, 1913; engr. in chief, Nigerian Eastern rly. construction, Feb., 1914; dep. gen. man., Nigerian rly., eastern div., 1916; gen. man., Gold Coast rlys., 1917; gen. man., Nigerian rlys., Nov., 1918.

BLAND, SAMUEL PATRICK, M.B.E. (1925) Cert., Royal San. Inst.—B. 1880; B.S.A. pol., S. Rhodesia, 1901-03; supt., P.W.D., Zanzibar, 24th Mar., 1910; asst. engr., 1st Jan., 1911; ag. asst. D.P.W., 16th Apl. to 7th June, 1915, 27th Jan. to 18th July, 1916 and from 7th Feb. to 25th Apr., 1919; asst. D.P.W., 26th Apr., 1919; ag. D.P.W. for various periods 1915-16, 1918-22 and 1925-26.

BLANDFORD, HON. SYDNEY D.—B. 1868; M.H.A., Newfoundland; elected for Bonavista, 1904, 1908 and 1909; M.E.C. and min. of agric. and mines, 1909; M.L.C. and min. of agric. and mines, 1914; sheriff of Newfoundland, 1917.

BLANKENBERG, SIR R. A., K.B.E. (1920), O.B.E. (1918).—Rhodesian service, 1895 to 1902; conf. clk. to lieut.-gov., Transvaal, 1902; ag. priv. sec. to lieut.-gov., Aug., 1902; ch. clk. to agt.-gen. in London, 1st June, 1907; ch. clk. to high comsnr. for Union of South Africa, London, 1910; asst. sec. to high comsnr., 1912; sec. to high comsnr., 1919; ag. high comsnr., 1920; ret., 31st Mar., 1926.

BLAXTER, KENNETH WILLIAM.—B. 1895; ed. Malvern and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge (schol.); mily. serv., France and Italy, 1916-19; asst. prin., miny. of transport, 1920; sec., royal comsnr. on compensation for suffering and damage by enemy action, 1923; asst. prin., O.O., 1924.

BLEASIDELL, ERNEST HAMILTON.—B. 1887; ed. Keble Coll., Oxford; asst. dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, 1912; ag. dist. comsnr., Dec., 1912 to Feb., 1913, June to Dec., 1913, July, 1914 to June, 1915, Nov., 1915 to 1917, and from July, 1917; dist. comsnr., 19th Aug., 1918.

BLINMAN, HARRY, M.V.O. (1920).—B. 1861; entered customs dept., S. Australia, 1878; cadet, audit off., Dec., 1878; elk., chief sec.'s off., 1883; chief elk., 1913; under sec. and elk. of exec. coun., S. Australia, July, 1916.

BLOOD, HILARY RUDOLPH ROBERT, M.A. (Glas.).—B. 1893; on mily. serv., Oct., 1914-1919; tempy. employed at C.O., Mar.-Dec., 1919 and Aug., 1924 to May, 1925; cadet, Ceylon, Jan., 1920; attd. to Colombo Kachcheri, Feb., 1920; Anuradahapura Kachcheri, Mar., 1920; ag. office asst., Anuradahapura Kachcheri, Nov., 1920; attd., Kandy Kach., Apr., 1921; office asst., govt. agt., Cent. Prov., June, 1921; 4th asst. col. sec., Apr., 1922; 3rd asst. col. sec., June, 1925; dist. judge, Badulla, Oct., 1926.

BLOOMBURGH, CAPT. JOSEPH HUGH, O.B.E.—B. 1867; late King's Own Scottish Borderers, 1885-1906; 6th K.A.R., Somaliland, 1906-10; ch., Khartoum prov. civ. pol., 1910-15; coy. commdr. and qmr., Somaliland Camel Corps, 1915-19; asst. commdt., pol. and dir. prisons, Somaliland, 1919; Sudan med. and clasp, 1888, Gemaizah, Br. War med., Indian med. with clasp, Relief of Chitral, 1896, Punjab Frontier, 1897-98, and Tirah 1897-98, A.G.S. med., clasp, Somaliland, 1908, Mily. Serv. med. and Khedive Star.

BLUMER, REV. REGINALD CHARLES, M.A., B.So., Dipl. Ed.—B. 1889; ed. Sydney High Schl. and Sydney Univ.; Caird schol. in chemy.; B.A., 1910, 1st cls. hons., Latin, 2nd cls. hons., maths.; B.So., 1910; M.A., 1912; dipl., ed., 1921; deacon, 1912; priest, 1913; curate, St. John's, Parramatta, 1912-17; curate, Holy Trinity, Dulwich Hill, 1918; asst. maat., Trinity Coll., Kandy, Ceylon, 1921-25; vice-prin., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1926.
BLYTH, EDWARD D'URBAN, I.S.O. (1926).—Sub-inspr., Basutoland mounted police, 1887; asst. comsnr., 1901.

BLYTHE, WILFRED LAWSON.—B. 1896 ed. Birkenhead Inst. and Liverpool Univ.; R.F.A., Apr., 1916; 2nd lieut., Jan., 1917; France, Apr., 1917-Aug., 1919; ment. in des., 1918; demob., Oct., 1919; cadet, F.M.S., Apr., 1921; attd., Chinese prot., Seremban; att'd., Chinese prot., Kuala Lumpur, Dec., 1921; passed final exam. in Cantonese, May, 1924; ast. prot. of Chinese, Selangor, June, 1924; pte. sec. to ch. sec., Feb., 1925; ag. ast. prot., Chinese, Apr., 1926.

BODKIN, GILBERT EDWIN, F.E.S., F.Z.S.—B. 1886; ed. at Repton Sch., Derbyshire, and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1908; Cambridge Univ. diploma of agric., 1910; govt. economic biologist, dept. of science and agric., Br. Guiana, 1911; Carnegie scholar in economic entomology, and as such toured the prin. agr. stations of the United States, 1912-1913; ast. dir. of sci. and agr., and dep. chmn., bd. of agr., Br. Guiana, 1919; editor, jnl. of the bd. of agr., 1918; joint editor, Br. Guiana Handbook, 1921; represented Br. Guiana at the W. Indian Agr. Conf., 1913, the 3rd congress of trop. agr., 1914, and the Imp. Entomological Conf., 1920; author of numerous papers on the insects of Br. Guiana, scientific rept., to govt., etc.

BOK, DR. WILLEMD EDUARD, JUNE.—Priv. sec. to prime min., Transvaal, June, 1907; sec. to prime min. and clk. to exec. coun., Union of S. Africa, May, 1910; law advdr., Feb., 1916; sec. for just., Aug., 1918.

BOLLARD, HON. RICHARD FRANCIS, M.P.—B. 1863; ed. at public schls. and privately; mem. N.Z. parl., since 1911; chmn. of rly. comtee. of H. of R., 1918; junr. govt. whip, 1918; senr. whip, 1919; min. of internal affrs., 1923.

BOLTON, ARTHUR, B.A., DIPL.—B. 1897; ed. Rugby and Brasenose Coll., Oxford (class.exhbr.); B.A. (hons. chem.), 1920; dipl., educn., 1921; lab. ast., H.M. facty., Sandycroft, 1916; research, Queen's Coll., Oxford, 1921; served in France, 1917-18; wounded; lieut., O.T.C., 1923; ast. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1926.
BOLUS, PERCY REGINALD, M.B.(Lond).—Asst. col. surgeon, Falkland Is., 1904.

BONAVIA, WALTER JOSEPH.—B. 1895; settler, Kenya Col., 1913; on mil. serv., 1914-1916; olk., secretariat, Tanganyika Territory, 9th Apr., 1917; ast. polit. offr. (cadet), 6th Oct., 1920; ag. dist. offr., 21st May, 1926.
BOND, ANDREW THOMAS.—B. 1873; ct. of prob., Ireland, 1890-97; served in S. African war with Cape Mounted Rifles; sub-inspr., Basutoland Mounted Pol., 1901; inspr., 1909; ast. comsnr., 1913.
BOND, MAJOR B. W., M.C.—Asst. dist. comsnr., Kenya, May, 1920.
BOND, RALPH NORMAN, B.A. (Cantab).—B. 1900; cadet Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1923; att'd. Puttalam kach., Jan., 1924; ditto Hambantota kach., Oct., 1924; office ast., Badulla kach., Jan., 1926.

BONYUN, E. C. B.—B. 1885; ed. at Harrison Coll., Barbados and privately; copyist, pol. mag.'s courts, Dist. "A," Barbados, Jan., 1903; 2nd clk., registn. off., Barbados, Sept., 1905; ag. dep. registr., from Jan. to Feb., and Sept. to Nov., 1910; ch. clk., regist. off., Nov., 1913; supervisor of customs, Nigeria, July, 1914; ast. recvr., sup. ct., Nigeria, for the purpose of winding up affairs of German and Austrian firms, Nov., 1914; hon. ast. sec. to West Indian Contingent Comtee. (whilst on vacation leave of absence, and with permission of the S. of S.), Sept. to Dec., 1915; ag. sup't. of customs, 1st grade, Feb., 1916; ast. censor, Bonny, Feb., 1916; censor, Bonny, May, 1916; ag. recvr. of enemy estates, Nigeria, June to Dec., 1916, and Jan., 1917; collr., 2nd grade, headqrs., Jan.-Mar., 1917 and Sept., 1917, to June, 1918; hon. sec., comtes., Nigerian comforts for overseas forces, Sept., 1917 to June, 1918; in charge of Port Harcourt cust., June-Nov., 1918; ag. sup't., 1st grade, Degema, July, 1919 to Jan., 1920; ag. collr., Lagos, Feb., May, 1920; ag. senr. collr., Lagos, May-July, 1920; collr., Victoria, July, 1921.

BOOCOCK, JOHN JAMES.—Enlisted in Cape Mounted Rifles, 1897; forester, Transkeian conservancy, Cape, 1903; sup't. of plantations, 1904; ast. dist. forest offr., Umtata, Feb., 1905; Butterworth, Dec., 1908; Kambl, Oct., 1909; Umtata, Apr., 1913; dist. forest offr., Kologha, Oct., 1914; conservator of forests, Natal Conservancy, May, 1920; served with Cape Mounted Rifles during Anglo-Boer war, 1899-1902; served with S. African Heavy Art'y., Apr., 1917 to June, 1919; conservator, Eastern conservancy, 1926.
BOON, A. H.—Medical Offr., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1919.

BOON, HERBERT.—Copyist, treas. dept., St. Kitts, May, 1908 to Mar., 1910; 2nd olk., admstr.'s office, Apr., 1913; ch. clk., post office, St. Kitts, May, 1916; clk. to admstr. and clk. exec. and leg. couns., 1st Deer., 1924; has held various ag. appts.

BOONE, ARTHUR PEARSON.—B. 1881; ed. Cheltenham and Jesus Coll., Cambridge (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1906; seconded ast. supt. of pol., Colombo, Dec., 1906; ast. supt. of pol., Kalutara, Feb., 1908; Colombo, Feb., 1909; off. ast. to govt. agt., Uva Prov., Apr., 1910; ast. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, June, 1911; dist. judge, Ratnapura, Dec., 1913; dist. judge, Kegalla, June, 1914; ast. govt. agt., Colombo, Aug., 1916; on military duty, 1917; ast. govt. agt., Colombo, Dec., 1919; ditto, Hambantota, June, 1920; ditto, Kegalla, Nov., 1921; ag. dist. judge, Galle, Mar. to Oct., 1923; addnl. dist. judge, Galle, Oct., 1923; ag. dist. judge, Galle, Feb., 1924; ag. dist. judge, Matara, Sept., 1924; ditto, Galle, Apr., 1925 to March, 1926 and from Oct., 1926.
BOOTH, C. A.—Ed. privately; hon. major, Militia; hon. captain, Army; served in South African war, 1900-02; ass't res., N. Nigeria, 16th June, 1906; political offr. with Chibuk expdn., 1906-1907.

BOOTH, JOSEPH RICHARD.—B. 1870; served in R.I.C., 1887 to 1896; passed exam. for grade of dist. inspr.; transferred to Jamaica police force, 1896; transferred to Br. Guiana police force, 1901; dist. inspr., 1905; county inspr., 1914; passed Hindi exam. and Hythe musketry course, 1907.
BOOTH, PEGGY, N.D.A., U.D.A.—Senr. supervisor, agrl. dept., Kenya, May, 1922.
BOOTH, R. W.—B. 1879; stock inspr., Basutoland, 1911; sub-inspr., Basutoland Mounted Pol., 1915; inspr., 1925.

BORDEN, Rt. Hon. Sir Robert Laird, P.C. (1912), G.C.M.G. (1914), K.C., LL.D.—B. 1854; ed. Acadia Villa Acad., Horton, N.S.; called to the bar, 1878; head of firm Borden, Ritchie and Chisholm, Halifax; pres. Nova Scotia Barristers' Society, ten years; Q.C., 1900; LL.D., Queen's and St. Francis-Xavier Univs.; elec. to H. of C., Canada, g. e. 1896; re-elec., 1900; defeated g. e. 1904; re-elec., bye-elec., 1906, and g. e. 1908, 1911 and 1917; leader opposition in H. of C. 1901-1911; president of the King's privy council for Canada and prime minister, Oct., 1911; prime minister and S. of S. for external affairs; mem. of the Peace Conference, Versailles, 1919, as representative of the Dominion of Canada; resigned premiership, 10th July, 1920; Canadian rep. to Armaments Conf., Washington, 1921; chan., Queen's Univ., Kingston, Ont., 1924.

BORGH, ANTHONY J., M.D., Malta.—B. 1889; medical offr., Fiji, 1916; D.M.O. and M.O.H., Ra., 1917; ag. R.M.O., med. supt., pub. lunatic asylum and visiting med. offr., Suva gaol, 1919; D.M.O., Nadroga, 1920; D.M.O. and M.O.H., Nadi, 1921; ditto, Levuka, 1923; ditto, Labasa, 1924.

BOSCH-REITZ, CHARLES JAMES.—B. 1870; ed. in England; entered civ. ser., B. Guiana, 1888; 5th cls. alk., secretariat, 1889; gradual promotion to 2nd cls., 1895; 1st cls. alk., immigrtn. dept., 1903; transf'd. to customs, 1907; survr., 1916.

BOSHOFF, HENRI GUILLAUME, J.P. (Natal).—Advoc. of the sup. ct., atty. and notary public; 2nd puisne judge, native high ct., Natal, 18th June, 1899; acted as 1st puisne judge, 18th Nov., 1900, to 30th Sept., 1901; from 1st Dec., 1903, to 18th May, 1904; and from 1st to 30th June, 1904; and as judge prea., from 19th to 31st May, 1904; 1st puisne judge, 1st July, 1906; ag. judge pres., 1906; judge pres., native high court, Natal, May, 1910.

BOSTOCK, HON. HEWITT.—B. 1864; ed., priv. tuition and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; called to bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1888; went to B.C., 1893; pres., Interior Stock Raisers' Assoc. of B.C., 1916; el. to H.C., g.e., 1896; called to the Senate, 1904; liberal leader in Senate, 1914-21; min. of pub. wks. in King admn., 29th Dec., 1921 to 3rd Feb., 1922; speaker of the senate, 7th Feb., 1922.

BOSTOCK, WIFRED CRAMER.—B. 1879; ed. St. Lawrence Coll., Ramsgate; served, S. African War, Imp. Yeomanry; asst. engnr., P.W.D., N. Nigeria, Sept., 1908; transf'd., Baro-Kano rly., Jan., 1909; res. engnr., constrn., Sierra Leone, Oct., 1914 to Dec., 1918; asst. ch. engnr., Nigerian rly., Jan., 1918; gen. man., Sierra Leone rly., Nov., 1919; ch. engnr., Nigerian rly., Jan., 1922; dep. gen. man., Feb., 1924.

BOTH, COLIN GRAHAM.—B. 1883; ent. atty.-gen.'s dept., Cape Colony, 1901; serv. in offices of civ. comsnt., Cape, and mast. of sup. ct.; in charge, archives of Cape Col., 1912; ch. archivist, Union of S. Africa, 1919; accompanied Sir Reginald Blankenburg as prte. sec. to first assembly of League of Nations, Geneva; hon. or corresponding mem. of several hist., lit. and genealogical socs. of Gt. Britain and Holland; contributed articles to various jnl., mags. and newspapers; author of "Place Names in the Cape District," 1917; "A Brief Guide to the Documents in the Cape Archives, 1652-1806," 1918; "The French Refugees at the Cape," 1919; rept. of tour to various archives in Europe, Canada and U.S.A., 1921; "Social Life in the Cape Colony in the 18th Century," 1927; "Place Names in the Cape Province," 1927.

BOTTOMLEY, W. C., C.B. (1926), C.M.G. (1921), O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1878; ed. Owens Coll. and Trin. Coll., Camb., entrance maj. schlr., 1896; schlr., 1898; B.A. and 3rd wrangler, 1900; ent. C.O. after compet. exam. as 2nd cl. olk., 22nd Oct., 1901; sec. to col. surv. comttee., 1905; sec. to inter-deptnl. pensions comttee., 1907; asst. priv. sec. to Lord Crewe, 16th Apr., 1908; priv. sec. to Sir F. Hopwood, 1st Feb., 1909; priv. sec. to Col. Seely, 1st Jan., 1911; priv. sec. to Lord Lucas, 24th Mar., 1911; priv. sec. to Lord Emmott, 26th Oct., 1911; 1st cls. olk., 1913; principal olk., 8th Dec., 1917; asst. sec., 1st Apr., 1920; mem., E. African Currency Bd., 1920.

BOUCHER, CYRIL HASLEWOOD.—Headqrs. offr., armed nat. constab., B.N. Guinea, Apr., 1905; asst. res. mag., central div., Jan., 1906; mag. for native affairs, Jan., 1906; J.P., Jan., 1906; visiting justice, Port Moresby gaol, Mar., 1906.
BOULDERSON, G. H. C.—Asst. dist. comsnt., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1912; 1st grade admstive offr., Jan., 1924.
BOUR, EDOUARD FRANÇOIS.—M.R.C.S. Eng.; L.R.C.P. Lond.; L.S.A. Lond.; govt. med. offr., Plaines Wilhelms dist., Mauritius, 1907; medical officer in charge H.M. troops, Mauritius, 1915-19.

BOURDILLON, MAJOR BERNARD HENRY, C.M.G. (1924).—Ed. Tonbridge and St. John's Coll., Oxford; asst. mag., United Provs., India, Nov., 1908; under-sec. to govt., Mar., 1913; regt., high ct. Allahabad, May, 1915; apptd. to I.A.R.O., Sept., 1917; jt. mag. (seconded), Mar., 1918; judi. astt., Persian Gulf, June, 1918; settlement offr., Baghdad, Mar., 1919; polit. offr., Baquba, Oct., 1920; polit. sec. to high comsnt., Jan., 1921; dep. sec. to high comsnt., Dec., 1921; sec. to high comsnt., Jan., 1922.

BOURNE, SIR HENRY ROLAND MURRAY, K.B.E. (1918), C.M.G. (1916).—B. 1874; ed. at Radley and New Coll., Oxford; lieut., 4th batt., King's Shropshire Light Infantry, 1893-96; 2nd lieut. 1st batt. Royal Scots (1st foot), July, 1896; Aldershot special signalling certif., 1898; mounted offr.'s certif., 1899; served in S. African war, 1899-1902 (ment. in despa., 2 medals, 5 clasps); capt., Nov., 1902; seconded for service under Transvaal govt., 1902; supt. of repatriation, 1902-4; served in col. sec.'s office, 1904; under sec., Feb., 1905; resigned army comm., July, 1906; under sec. for the interior, Union of South Africa, 31st May, 1910; under sec. for defence, 1st July, 1912; sec. for defence, 1st Apr., 1914; ret., 1922.

BOURNE, JOHN DUNBAR MACDOWALL.—B. 1886; examiner, E. and A. dept., 21st Feb., 1908; asst. auditor, N. Nigeria, 21st Aug., 1909; asst. audr., Br. Guiana, 20th Feb., 1920; audr., Somaliland Prot., 22nd July, 1921; audr., B. Guiana, 5th Oct., 1928.

BOVELL, C.—Landing waiter and tide surveyor, customs, Jamaica, 1895; asst. coll. of revenue and clk. to parochial bd. of Port Royal, 1900; 2nd lieut. in Militia, 1900; lieut., 1902; Hythe schl. of musketry, 1902; capt., 1906; dist. supt. of police, Uganda, 1907; R.I.O. course, 1908; supt. of police, Uganda, 1912; ag. asst. comsrr. of police and prisons, Uganda, on several occasions; adjutant to Uganda Vol. Reserve, 20th Mar., 1916.

BOVELL, SIR HENRY ALLEYN, KNT. BACH. (1902).—B. 1864; Gilchrist scholar (London Univ.), Jan., 1873; LL.B. (Lond.), Jan., 1877, with 2ndclass honours in jurisprudence and Roman law; called to the bar, Lino. Inn, Nov., 1876; solr.-gen., and escheator-gen. of Barbados, and J.P., Mar., 1882; M.L.C., July, 1883; mem. of comn. to revise laws of Barbados, Mar., 1886; chancellor of the diocese, 1884; atty.-gen., Oct., 1886; atty.-gen., Br. Guiana, 1896; ag. legal asst., C.O., Dec., 1900 and 1901; ch. just., Br. Guiana, 1902; ret., 1912.

BOWDEN, HON. ERIC KENDALL.—B. 1872; ed. Newington Coll. and Sidney High Schl.; admitted solr., 1894; el. mem., H. of R., C. of A., for Nepean, 1906-10; re-el., Dec., 1919; min. of defence, C. of A., 1923-26.

BOWDEN, WILLIAM DOUGLAS DAVIS.—B. 1875; ed. at private schls. in Scotland and Germany, Edinburgh Univ. and Technische Hoch-Schule, Dresden; M.A. (Edin.); asst. engr., Sierra Leone Rly. construction, 1901-05; asst. dist. comsrr., Nov., 1905; dist. comntr. (2nd cls.), Apr., 1910; dist. comntr. (1st cls.), Jan., 1915; prov. comntr., May, 1920; ag. col. sec., 9th Sept., 1921 to 19th May, 1922; gov.'s dep. on several occasions; passed lower standard exam. in Mende.

BOWER, W. M. L.—Capt., 4th Batt., Liverpool Regt.; asst. supt. of pol., S. Stiltsn., Mar., 1903; asst. supt. of pol., Singapore, Feb., 1906; asst. supt. of pol., Prov. of Wellesley, July, 1907; asst. supt. of pol., Singapore, Oct., 1908; asst. supt. of pol., Penang, July, 1909; ag. supt. of pol., Singapore, Feb., 1911; ag. 2nd supt. of pol., Singapore, July, 1912; supt. of police, Malacca, Apr., 1912; ag. supt. of pol., Penang, Nov., 1919; supt. pol., Malacca, Dec., 1921.

BOWERLEY, WALTER, B.A., St. John's Coll., Camb. (class. tripus, 1899); apptd. clk., col. audit branch of E. and A. Dept., 4th Nov., 1901; asst. auditor, S. Nigeria, 4th Sept., 1902; ag. asst. treas., Oct., 1902; ag. local auditor in 1903, 1904, 1905 and of amalgamated territories, Lagos—S. Nigeria, 1906-7; ag. local auditor, N. Nigeria, Nov., 1907, to Mar., 1908; local auditor, Gambia, 27th Apr., 1908; local auditor, G. Coast, 3rd Nov., 1909; title changed to auditor, 1910.
BOWIE, ROBT.—P.C. certif., 1894; M.A., Edin., 1897; Normal Coll., Cape Town, 1897-1900; inspr. of schls., O.R.C., 1st July, 1901; transf. to Cape Provincial adminstr., 1922.

BOWMAN, HUMPHREY ERNEST, C.B.E. (1918).—B. 1879; ed. Eton and New Coll., Oxford (2nd cls. mod. hist. 1902); B.A., 1902; M.A., 1906; ent. Egyptian miny. of educn., 1903; seconded to Sudan govt. (dept. of educn.), 1911-13; dir., Egyptian students in U.K., 1913-14; serv. with Br. Army, 1914-20; B.E.F., France, 1915-16; staff capt., H.Q., N. Command, 1916-18; India, 1918; Mesopotamia, 1918-20; ret. with rank of major, 1920; seconded from M.E.F. as dir. of educn., Mesopotamia, 1918-20; seconded to govt. of Palestine, 1920-23; dir. of educn., Palestine, 1920; mem., advisory comn. since 1920; chmn., comntr. on crafts and industries, 1922; mem., transliteration comntr., 1923; chmn., comntr. on transliteration of geographical names, 1924.
BOWRING, ARTHUR GEORGE.—Ed. Nottingham High Schl. and Nottingham Univ. Coll.; capt., Sherwood Foresters, 1914-19; col. audit dept., Nov., 1920; asst. audr., Nigeria, Jan., 1921.

BOWRING, SIR CHARLES CALVERT, K.C.M.G. (1925), K.B.E. (1919), C.M.G. (1908).—Ed. at Clifton Coll.; col. audit branch of exchequer and audit dept., 1890; Hong Kong, 12th Dec., 1892; loc. aud., B. C., Africa, 7th Sept., 1896; loc. aud., East Africa Prot. and Uganda rly., 6th June, 1899; treas., East Africa Prot., 1st Oct., 1901; mem. of executive and legislative couns., East Africa Prot., 1907; chief sec. to govt., East Africa Prot., Apr., 1911; ag. gov., Feb. to Oct., 1912, Aug. to Oct., 1913, Apr., 1917 to Jan., 1919, and from Nov., 1919; gov., Nyasaland, 30th Aug., 1923; assumed govt., 27th Mar., 1924.
BOWRING, HON. SIR EDGAR RENNIE, KT. BACH. (1915).—B. 1858; mem. legia. coun., Newfoundland, 1897; high comsrr. for Newfoundland in London, 1919.

BOWRING, WALTER ANDREW.—Ed. at Eton; clk., col. audit branch, 1st Dec., 1894; served in Cyprus, 1896; asst. auditor, R.A.P. and Uganda Rly., 13th Feb., 1899; ag. local auditor, Uganda Prot., 1899; ag. local auditor, Somaliland Prot., 1900, and in 1902; local auditor, Uganda Prot., 31st May, 1902; treas., Cyprus, 11th Nov., 1909; mem. exec. and legis. couns., Cyprus, 1909; chief refugee comntr., Cyprus, 1915 (during war); passed exam. in Turkish, Feb., 1919.

BOYCE, ARTHUR CYRIL.—B. 1867; ed. Wakefield, York, Carlisle; called to bar, 1890 (hons. med.); K.C., 1908; D.C.L. (hon. causa) Bishop's Coll., Lennoxville, 1913; unsuccessful candidate for H. of C., Canada, Algoma, 1900; elected, West Algoma, 1904; re-elected 1908 and 1911; mem., bd. of rly. comntr., Canada, 1917; chancellor, diocese of Algoma; hon. lieut. col., 51st (Soo) Rgt.
BOYD, A. H.—B. 1865; entered C.O. after exam., Apr., 1887; asst. clk., June, 1892; supplementary clk. in registry, Mar., 1900; cler. othr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922.

BOYD, EDMUND B.—B. 1894; ed. Aberdeen Gram. Schl. and Univ. of Aberdeen; M.A. 1916; 1st cls. hons. classics; on military service, 1916; with 60th siege batt., R.G.A., France and Belgium, Nov., 1917, to June, 1919; 2nd lieut., Oct., 1917; lieut., Apr., 1919; temporary clk. in C.O., Sept., 1919; apptd. by civ. ser. commrs. under reconstrn. scheme, 2nd cls. clk., C.O., Jan., 1920; asst. prin., 1st Apr., 1920; priv. sec. to perm. under-sea., May to Sept., 1921; sec. interdeptl. comttee., income tax in cols., 1922; priv. sec. to parly. under-sec. (Mr. Ed. Wood), 4th Apr., 1922; (Mr. Ormaby Gore), 31st Oct., 1922; sec.; Imp. Inst. comttee. of enquiry, 1923; priv. sec. to Lord Arnold, 26th Jan., 1924; ag. prin., 12th Dec., 1924; prin., 2nd Nov., 1925.

BOYD, PETER FERGUSON.—B. 1898; telegraphist, Union of S. Africa, Sept., 1906 to Dec., 1911; postal ast., N. Rhodesia, June, 1912; ag. ch. ast., Nov., 1916 to Apr., 1917; ast. post office, Apr., 1920; ag. sec., Jan., 1925 to Apr., 1926.

BOYD, ROBERT.—B. 1890; ed. Campbell Coll., Belfast, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; schol. (1912), B.A. (1913); cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1913; passed in law July, 1914; ag. ast. contr. of labr., Mar., 1915; passed in Tamil, Sept., 1915; 2nd lieut., 7th Royal Dublin Fus., Oct., 1915; capt. 10th Royal Dublin Fus., Apr., 1917; demob., Apr., 1919; ast. contr. of labr., Nov., 1919; offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1920; ast. dist. offr., Larut, Mar., 1923; regiar., co-op. societies, May, 1923; ast. offr. in charge, co-op. societies, S.S., Nov., 1924.

BOYD, WILLIAM RYDER.—B. 1887; ed. Campbell Coll., Belfast and Dublin Univ.; B.A., Dublin; cadet, F.M.S., 1st Dec., 1911; ag. ast. dist. offr., Lipis, May, 1912; ag. ast. collr. of land rev., Seremban, Oct. and Dec., 1913 and Aug., 1914; ag. dist. offr., Jelebu, Oct., 1913; ag. ast. dist. offr., Tampin, May, 1914; passed cadet June, 1914; ag. ast. dist. offr., Tampin, Feb., 1915, Dec., 1916 and July, 1918; ast. dist. offr., Kuala Kubu, May, 1915; ag. dist. offr., Kuala Pilah, June, 1918; ast. dist. offr., U. Selangor, Jan., 1920; ast. dist. offr., Larut, Mar., 1920; dist. offr., Raub, July, 1920; dist. offr., Bentong, Feb., 1921; ag. collr., land rev., Kuala Lumpur, Apr., 1922; offr., cls. III., Dec., 1923; dist. offr., Krian, Feb., 1924.

BOYDELL, HON. THOMAS.—Served apprenticeship as engnr. at R. & W. Hawthorns & Leslie, St. Peters, nr. Newcastle-on-Tyne; came to S. Africa, 1903; worked as a fitter in the Natal harbour, wks. and also in the S.A. rly. workshops, Durban; stood unsuccessfully as offl. cand. for S. African Labour Party for the Greyville divn. of Durban at the first Union parly. elections, 1910; contested by-elect. for same const., 1912; re-elec., 1915-20, 21 and 24; dep. leader of the S. African Labour Party in parly.; shmn., parly. labour caucus; national organiser of party; min. of posts and tels. and pub. wks. in Hertzog cabinet, 30th June, 1924; min. of lab., 12th Nov., 1925.

BOYLE, SIR ALEXANDER GEORGE, K.C.M.G. (1921), C.M.G. (1908), C.B.E. (1919).—B. 1872; ed. at Charterhouse, Clifton Coll. and R.I.E.C., Cooper's Hill; priv. sec. to senior puisne judge, S. Stilimts., 1893; resig., 1895; ast. treas.; Uganda Prot., 18th Sept., 1895; ag. sec. to H.M. Comsnnr., Uganda Prot., Feb., 1896, to Apr., 1898; ag. treas., Mar., 1899, to Feb., 1900; dep. treas., Ist Apr., 1900; ag. sec. to admin., May, 1901, to Jan., 1902; appt. collr. with seniority of 18th Sept., 1895, 1st Apr., 1902; ast. sec., 1st Apr., 1902; ag. sub-comsnnr., E. Prov., 1st Nov., 1903; sub-comsnnr., 1st Jan., 1905; ag. dep. comsnnr., 15th Apr. to 29th Oct., 1907; ag. ch. sec., Apr., 1909; admstg. govt., Aug., 1909; col. sec., S. Nigeria, 1910; ret., 1920.

BOYSE, V. H.—B. 1883; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of 2nd div., and assigned to C.O., 17th July, 1902; on military serv. from 26th July, 1917 to 5th Feb., 1919; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922; ast. acctnt., June, 1922; sent on sp. mission to Iraq to advise on accounting methods, Nov., 1922 to Feb., 1923; ag. as finan. sec. to high comsnnr., Iraq, Mar.-Dec., 1924.

BRACKENBURY, ARTHUR JOCELYN.—B. 1876; ed. Clifton Coll.; served in S. African War (Queen's med., three clasps); Transvaal civ. ser., 1901-07; priv. sec., gov., Hong Kong, 1907-09; 3rd grade res., Nyassaland, 1909; on war serv., 1915-18 (1914-15 Star, War Med., Victory Med.); admn. offr., 1st grade, 26th Jan., 1920; ag. prov. comsnnr., Jan., 1924; prov. comsnnr., Nov., 1925.

BRACKENBURY, E. A.—Capt., reserve of officers; educ. at Wellington Coll. and Keble Coll., Oxford; served in S. African War, 1900-02, attached to "The Buffs" and A.S.C. (ment. in desps., Queen's medal and 3 clasps, King's medal and 2 clasps); capt., S.A.C., 1902-1906; dist. ast., N. Nigeria constab., 1905-1907; ast. res., N. Nigeria, 1907; 2nd cls. dist. offr., 1914; served as intell. offr. with Anglo-French forces, N. Cameroons, 1914-16 (ment. in desps); res., 27th Dec., 1922.

BRADBURN, CAPT. WILLIAM ERNEST HORATIO.—Served, Imp. Yeomanry, 1901; S. African Constab., 11th Jan., 1902 to 4th Oct., 1907; 8th King's Royal Hussars, 2nd Sept., 1914 to 15th Apr., 1917; 1st King Edward's Horse, King's Oversea Dominions Regt. (adjutant), 16th Apr., 1917 to 30th July, 1919; local commdt., Cyprus mil. pol., 6th Oct., 1919; passed exam. in mod. Greek, Feb., 1923; ag. ch. commdt., pol., July to Oct., 1923 and June to Oct., 1926; ag. A.D.C. to high comsnnr., Apr. to June and Sept. to Oct., 1924; dep. ch. commdt., pol., Aug., 1924; ag. comsnnr., Aug., 1924; hon. A.D.C. to high comsnnr., Oct., 1924.

BRADDELL, SIR THOMAS DE M. L., KNT. BACH. (1914).—Barrister-at-law; puisne judge, S. Stilimts. and jud. comsnnr., F.M.S., July, 1907; ag. atty.-gen., S. Stilimts., Feb.-Mar., 1908; chief judicial comsnnr., F.M.S., 25th Mar., 1913; ret., 1917.

BRADFORD, SIR JOHN ROSE, K.C.M.G. (1911); M.D., D.Sc., F.R.C.P., F.R.S.—B. 1863; ed. Univ. Coll. Sch., Coll. and Hosp. George Henry Lewes student and Grocer research schlr.; late mem. of Senate, Univ. of London; physician to Univ. Coll. Hosp.; Holme lecturer on clinical medicine to Univ. Coll. hosp. med. schl.; sec. of the Royal Society; sen. med. adviser to C.O., 1912; resigned, 1924.

BRADLEY, BENJAMIN GERALD.—B. 1899; R.M.A., Wedwich, Oct., 1917; 2nd lieut., R.E., Oct., 1918; lieut., R.E., Mar., 1920; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1921; attd. lab. office, Penang, Jan., 1922; ag. ast. contr. of lab., July, 1923; passed cadet, June, 1923; attd., land office, Teluk Anson, July, 1923; ag. ast. dist. offr., Sitiawan, Nov., 1923; offr., cls. V.
BRADLEY, CHARLES HUGH GRANVILLE.—B. 1902; ed. Wellington Coll.; clk., col. audit dept., 29th June, 1925; ast. audr., Hongkong, 11th Sept., 1925.

BRADLEY, JOHN THOS., L.R.C.P.E., L.R.C.S.E., L.F.P. and S.G.; M.D. (Brux.)—B. 1870; ed. at Queen's Coll., Belfast, and Lond. Schl. Trop. Med. (1901); undergrad. in arts, Royal Univ., Ireland; prizeman in med., midwifery, and diseases of women and children, Queen's Coll., Belfast, 1897-8; gold medallist in practical midwifery, Clifton st. Mater. Hosp.; 1st prizeman in med. and surg. diseases of children, Children's Hosp., Queen st., Belfast; late ast. med. offr., Glamorgan Co. Asylum, 1901; ast. med. offr., Seychelles, 26th Aug., 1901; J.P. and chmn. of loc. bd. of health, 1st and 2nd divs., South Mahé; med. supt., lunatic asylum, Seychelles, 1st Mar., 1906; ch. med. offr., 1924.

BRADNEY, GEORGE PRESTON.—Ed. at Oratory Schl., Edgbaston, and Trinity Hall, Camb.; B.A., Cantab.; honours in history, 1899; col. audit off., June, 1901; asst. aud., B.C. Africa, 5th Feb., 1902; asst. auditor, East Africa Prot., 26th Sept., 1904; auditor, Fiji, and Western Pacific Protectorates, 1909; dir. of external audit, S. Stltims., and F.M.S., 26th Oct., 1914, assumed duty, 20th Mar., 1915; ag. aud.-gen., S. Stltims., in addition, 14th Jan. to 2nd Feb., 1916; ag. aud.-gen., S. Stltims., in addition, 1st June, 1916 and Dec., 1918; audr.-gen., F.M.S., 12th May, 1919; pres., comm. to enquire into finan. position, Singapore municipality, Sept., 1921; mem., comm. to enquire into organization and expend. of govt. depts., F.M.S., etc., Feb., 1922.

BRADY, Lieut.-Col. JOHN BANKS, D.S.O.—Head mast., Grey Coll. Schl., 1st Dec., 1901; inspr. of schls., O.R.C., 1st Feb., 1904; inspr. of schls., Rhodesia, Aug., 1909; on active serv., 1914-20; commanded 4th and 18th batts. K.R.R.C.; ment. in desps.; croix de guerre with palm leaves; ag. dir. of educn., S. Rhodesia, 1920-21.
BRAILSFORD, H. E.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., Jan., 1917.

BRANCH, EDMUND RALPH.—M.B., Ch.B., Edin.; dist. med. offr., Dominica, 1902; ditto, Dieppe Bay, St. Kitts, 1902; med. suptd., Cunningham hosp., and surgeon of the gaol, St. Kitts, Feb., 1903; med. offr., dist. 2, St. Kitts, Nov., 1916; served with R.A.M.C. during the war.

BRANCH, SIR CHARLES ERNEST ST. J., Kt., B.A. (1924), B.A., B.C.L., Durham Univ.—Called to the bar, Gray's Inn, May, 1892; ag. chief mag., St. Vincent, 1896 to Jan., 1898; asst. to atty.-gen., Leeward Islds., May, 1898; ag. atty.-gen., July to Nov., 1899; mem. legis. coun. and exec. coun., Dominica; mem. general legis. coun., Leeward Is., Dec., 1899; atty.-gen., Leeward Is., 2 Sept., 1902; chancellor of the Diocese of Antigua, 1903-1909; pres., Antigua defence reserve force, 1903; ag. col. sec., Oct., 1902; apptd. to combined offices of col. sec. and atty.-gen., Leeward Islds., Jan., 1903; deleg. to agric. confocs., Jamaica, 1907; Barbados, 1908; Canadian reciprocity confoc., Barbados, 1908; cotton confoc., Liverpool, 1908; comsnr. to prepare a new and revised edition of laws of Leeward Islds., Feb., 1909; admstd. govt. of Antigua on various occasions in the years 1902 to 1909; ag. gov., Leeward Islds., Nov.-Dec., 1907, and Apr. to Oct., 1909; atty.-gen., Jamaica, Oct., 1909; deleg. to Canada on banana tax question, 1915; deleg. to Indent. Emigrn. Confnce. Trinidad, 1916; ag. ch. just., Jamaica, Apl., 1919; rep. of Jamaica at Canada-W. Indies Trade Confce., May-June, 1920; puisne judge, sup. ct., Penang, Apr., 1921; judl. comsnr., F.M.S., Jan., 1922; ch. just., Jamaica, 1923; ch. just., Ceylon, 3rd July, 1926; ret., 1926.

BRANCH, EDWARD WILLIAM RAWLE, B.A. (Durham), M.B., C.M. (Edin.), 1889.—Med. offr., Virgin Is.; J.P. and M.L.C., 1889; ag. comsnr., Virgin Is., 1890; med. offr. and M.L.C., Montserrat, 1890; dist. med. offr., St. Kitts, 1894; lieut., def. force; ag. supt., Cunningham hosp., 1904; med. offr., dist. 4, Antigua, 1905.

BRANCH, SAMUEL EDMUND.—Ed. at the Lodge Schl., Barbados, and Codrington Coll., Barbados; B.A., Univ. of Durham, 1882; M.A., 1900; deacon, 1884; priest, 1886; headmr. of Antigua gram. schl., 1884; exammg. chaplain of the Bishop of Antigua, 1905; chaplain to H.M. prison, Antigua, 1896; archdeacon of Antigua and canon of St. John's Cathedral, 1906; domestic chaplain to the Earl of Stamford, 1909.

BRANDT, FRANCIS ROBERT, M.R.C.V.S.—Ed. at St. Peters, York; govt. vet. surg., C. of G. H., 1897; vet. inspr., bd. of agric., England, 1903; asst. vet. offr., East Africa Prot., 2nd Apr., 1904; chief vet. offr., Nigeria, 1913.
BRASSEY-EDWARDS, H.—Veterinary offr., E.A.P., Feb., 1910; ag. dir. of agric., 1919; senr. vet. offr., Apr., 1920; ag. dep. ch. vet. offr., 1924.

BRAWN, ALAN O.—B. 1878; ed. "Queen Mary's" gram. schl., Walsall, and St. Mark's Coll., Chelsea (1st cl. Queen's scholarship); teachers' certif. (Bd. of Educ., England); matric. Lond. Univ., 1898; asst. mast., diocesan schl., Hong Kong, 1901-1909; jun. asst. mast., Queen's Coll., Hong Kong, 1910; lecturer, Tech. Inst., Hong Kong, 1910-1912; headmaster, Wantsai dist. schl., 1913; headmaster, Yaumati schl., 1914; headmaster, Wantsai schl., 1915; seconded for mil. ser., 1917; 2nd lieut., 1917; 1st lieut., 1918; served in France, 1917 to 1919; demobilised, 1919; dep. regist., Hong Kong Univ., 1919; headmaster, Wantsai schl., 1919, headmaster, Yaumati schl., 1919; asst. mast., cls. II., 1920; headmr., Victoria Br. schl., 1920; asst. mast., cls. I., 1921; headmr., Wantsai schl., 1921; ditto, Indian schl., 1921; asst. mast., Queen's Coll. and Wantsai schl., 1922; headmr., Wantsai schl., 1923; headmr., Yaumati schl., 1924.

BRAYNE, CHARLES VALENTINE.—B. 1877; ed. Monkton Combe schl., and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1901; off. ast. to govt. agt., N.C. Prov., Apr., 1903; asst. collr. of customs, Trincomalee, May, 1905; asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Feb., 1906; dist. judge, Negombo, Jan., 1909; Badulla, Feb., 1909; ast. land settlmnt. offr., Oct., 1909; ag. ast. govt. agt., Colombo dist., June, 1912; ditto, Prov. of Uva, July, 1912; ditto, Colombo dist., Aug., 1912; off. of Cls. II., Jan., 1913; ag. dist. judge, Jaffna, June, 1914; ast. govt. agt., Kalutara, Sept., 1914; ast. govt. agt., Colombo, Jan., 1915; ast. govt. agt., Kalutara, May, 1916; govt. agt., E. Prov., Feb., 1920.

READING, G. R., D.S.O., CAPT.—Subaltern, R. Africa Rifles, 1899; Ogaden punitive expdn., 1900-1901; medal and clasp, "Jubaland"; Somaliland clasps, 1902-1904; Jibdali (wounded, ment. in desp., D.S.O.); Somaliland, 1909; comdt., Jubaland Camel Corps, 1906-1909; comdt., 3rd K.A.R., 1910-12; capt., Worcestershire Regt., 1904; resig., 1913; senr. company comdr., Somaliland Camel Constab., Dec., 1913; capt., reserve of officers, May, 1914.

BREARLEY, L. J.—B. 1882; passed Lond. matric. and Oxford sen. local exams, June, 1899; apptd., after compet. exam, olk. of the 2nd div. and assigned to ld. chamberlain's office, Feb., 1901; transferred to C.O., 18th Feb., 1901; conf. olk., 1st Jan., 1918.

BREDELL, HERMANUS CHRISTIAAN.—Chief clk. to chief comsnr. of police, Transvaal and Swaziland, 1895, to Oct., 1899, and ag. chief comsnr., 1899 to 1900; A.D.C. to Pres. Kruger, and ast. priv. sec., Oct., 1899; priv. sec. to Pres. Kruger, 1901; inspr. of white labour, C.S.A.R., Apl., 1908; ag. inspr., Transvaal town police and S.A.C., 15th June, 1908; inspr. and sec., Transvaal police, 1st Aug., 1908; dep. comsnr. and sec., S. African police, Union of S. Africa, 15th Oct., 1910; J.P., Union of S. Africa, 15th Aug., 1913.

BREEN, MICHAEL JAMES.—B. 1884; ed. at Clongowes Wood Coll. and Univ. Coll., Dublin; B.A., math., science (honours) in Royal Univ. of Ireland, 1906; cadet, Hong Kong, 16th Nov., 1907; passed cadet, 1911; ag. 2nd asst. regt. gen.; 1911; ag. asst. P.M.G., 1911; asst. dist. offr., 1st Jan., 1914; ag. asst. col. sec., 1914 to 1915; on mil. serv., 1916 to 1919; ag. asst. P.M.G., 1920; ag. P.M.G., 1920 to 1921; asst. treas., 1921; P.M.G., 1924.
BRKTT, E. A.—B. 1880; apptd. to emigrants' information office, 2nd July, 1906; overseas settlmt. office, 1919.

BRETT, F. W.—B. 1886; Cape Mounted Rifles, Nov., 1903-05; 3rd battn., Royal Warwickshire Regt., Jan., 1906-08; asst. dist. comsnr., R.A.F., 4th Sept., 1908; priv. sec. to gov., Oct., 1912-15, and sec. to high comsnr., Zanzibar, Jan., 1914-15; seconded to Royal Flying Corps, 1915-16; dist. comsnr., Dec., 1915; dist. polit. offr., German E.Africa, provisional admstrn., Nov., 1916; adminstr. offr., 1st grade, now senr. comsnr., Tanganyika Territory, 1st Apr., 1920.

BRETT, ROBERT GEORGE.—B. 1851; ed. gram. schl. and Toronto Univ.; post-graduate courses, New York, Philadelphia, Vienna; physician; one of the founders of Manitoba Med. Coll.; estab. Banff Sanatorium and Brett Hosp., Banff; mem. legis. assem., N.W. Territories, 1888-1901; pres. exec. coun., 1889-91; liet.-gov., Alberta, 1915; re-apptd. for a second term, 1920.

BRIDGE, BLACKER FRANK, B.A. (Dublin).—B. 1891; ed. St. Columba's Coll. and Dublin Univ.; cadet, F.M.S., 28th Nov., 1914; ag. asst. contr. of lab., 29th Mar., 1916; passed cadet, 20th Dec., 1916; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, 2nd Apr., 1918; asst. contr. of lab., 19th June, 1918; asst. dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, 6th Feb., 1922; dist. offr., Bentong, 13th Apr., 1922; dep. contr., lab., Mar., 1924; ag. dep. contr., lab., Penang, May, 1925; dep. contr., lab., Kuala Lumpur, July, 1925.

BRIDGER, JAMES FREDERICK EDMUND, M.B., B.S. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Lond.).—Camb. certif., 1st cl. hons.; inter. M.B. (Lond.) 2nd cl. hons. in pharmacology; prize in clinical surgery and hons. in practical surgery and materia medica, St. Mary's Hosp., Lond.; late asst. demonstrator in physiology in St. Mary's Med. Schl.; civil surg. attached to 3rd batt. Northd. Fus., Antigua, 1902; med. offr. and supt. in charge of small pox statn., and med. adviser to gen. bd. of health, Barbados, during epidemic of 1902-3; comsnr. apptd. by gov't. of Barbados to visit and report on system of quarantine practised by port sanitary authorities of New York, also to Trinidad to report on epidemic of eruptive fever in Port of Spain; received thanks of gov'r., H. of A. and gen. bd. of health for services rendered to Barbados; port health offr., Barbados, 1906; mem., cent. quarantine authy., June, 1907; ex-officio mem. of quarantine bd., med. asst. and surg.-liet., Barbados volra., 1917; sany. comsnt., Ceylon, July, 1921; prin. civ. med. offr., Dec., 1924; M.L.C., 1924; title changed to dir., med. and sany. services, Oct., 1925.

BRIDGES, LIET.-GEN. SIR TOM, K.C.M.G. (1919), C.B. (1918), D.S.O. (1904), F.R.G.S.—B. 1872; Grenadier Guards; served S. African War, 1899-1901; Somaliland, 1902-4; European War, 1914-15; mil. attache at the Hague, Brussels, Copenhagen and Christiania, 1910-14; liet.-col., 4th Hussars, 1914-16; brev. col., 1916; maj.-gen., temp. liet.-col., 1917; col., Dragoon Gds., 1920; commdr. Br. mly. mission to Smyrna, 1920; gov., S. Australia, 11th Sept., 1922; assumed govt., 4th Dec., 1922.

BRIFFA, ROBERT J.—B. 1866; entered Malta civ. serv., 1884; 1st ols. clk., 1906; asst. collr. of customs, 1910; ag. collr. cust., in 1910, 1911, 1912 and 1913; collr. cust. and supt. of port, 1st Jan., 1914; mem., exec. coun. and coun. of govt., 1916-21; ag. marshal of prize ct. and as detaining offr. during war.

BRINK, BRIG.-GEN. ANDRIES JACOB EESTHEN, D.T.D., D.S.O.—Joined civ. serv., S. African Repub., 1896; served Mpfu (native) campaign, 1898; Zoutpansberg and Anglo-Boer War, 1899-1920; elected veldt cornet, S. African Repub. forces, 1901; capt., permanent force (staff), Union defence forces, 1912; promoted major, and served as dist. staff offr., No. 6 and later as dist. staff offr., No. 7 mil. dist., 1914; brig.-major, 1st mounted brig., German S.W.A. campaign, 1915; temp. liet.-col., Mar., 1915; dist. staff offr., No. 6 mil. dist., with rank of temp. liet.-col., whilst so emp'd., 17th Aug., 1915; brev. liet.-col., 14th Sept., 1915; brig.-major, 2nd mounted brig., E. Africa, Mar., 1916; gen. staff offr. with rank of col. headqrs., 3rd E. African divn., Apr., 1916; resumed duty as dist. staff offr. No. 6. mil. dist., Dec., 1916; transf. to defence headqrs., and assumed duty as asst. adjt.-gen., defence headqrs., Sept., 1917; ag. ch. of gen. staff and adjt.-gen., with temp. rank of col., Nov., 1919; liet.-col., 1st grade staff offr., Nov., 1919; brig.-gen., and ch. of gen. staff, 21st Sept., 1920; combined post of ch. of gen. staff and sec. for defence, 1st Oct., 1922; 1914-15 Star, Br. War Med., Victory Med., D.S.O., (1917), French Croix de Guerre; men. in despatch., for serv. in G.S.W.A. campaign (1918), medal, Anglo-Boer oorlog.

BRINK, COLONEL CAROLUS, D.T.D., D.S.O.—B.1879; served Jameson Raid, S. African Republic, 1896; Mpfu campaign, Zoutpansberg, S. African Republic, 1896; Anglo-Boer War, Heidelberg commando, staff General Spruyt, 1899-1901; German S.W. Africa campaign, as brig.-major, 2nd Mounted Brig., Northern Force, 1914-15; German E. African campaign as G.S.O.I., 2nd E. African divn., 1916-17; Europe, Western Front, 1917-18; qualified, staff course, Bloemfontein, 1914; qualified, senr. staff course, Cambridge, 1919; qtrmr.-gen. Union Defence Forces; ment. in despatch.; Croix de Guerre(French); med., Anglo-Boer War.

BRISTOW, CHARLES FRANCIS.—B. 1882; 2nd Middlesex, R.G.A., 1899-1901; S. African constab., 1902-07; served in S. African war (Queen's medal and clasps); Moulemein Artillery, 1909-10; asst. inspr. of police, E.A.P., Jan., 1911; passed pol. promotion exam., Aug., 1911; passed Kiswahili exam., lower standard; inspr. of police, Mar., 1913; asst. supt. of police, 1st Apr., 1914; during war period 1914, attached to Moulemein Rifles as liet.; on active service, E.A.P., 1915-17, awarded "1914-15" Star, War and Victory medals.; resumed civil duties as asst. supt. of police, 1918; ag. qtrmr., pol. and prison depts., Oct., 1920 to Mar., 1921; immigr. offr., Apr., 1921 to June, 1922; supt. of pol., 1st Jan., 1923; headqrs. supt., Jan., 1926.

BRITTEN, HENRY.—Apptd. probationer, Dec., 1896; 3rd cl. clk., magistrate's office, Ixopo, June, 1896; clk. and Zulu interpreter and sub-distributor of stamps, Ixopo, Apr., 1897; ag. clk. of the court on several occasions; served through the Boer War, 1899-1900, with the Border mounted rifles, including the siege of Ladysmith; 2nd cls. clk., off. of sec. to nat. affairs dept., Pietermaritzburg, 8th May, 1901; transf. to Transvaal civ. ser. as ast. res. mag., 1st May, 1902; A.R.M., Christiansa, 1st Nov., 1904; ag. R.M., Lichtenburg, 9th Apr., 1906; ag. R.M., Wolmaranstad, 9th Oct., 1906; A.R.M., Belfast, 1st Feb., 1907; ag. R.M., Lichtenburg, 1st Aug., 1908; A.R.M., Boksburg, 1st Dec., 1908; R.M., Boksburg, 1st July, 1909; mag., Potchefstroom, Jan., 1918; mag., King William's Town, Jan., 1925.
BRIZZELL, ANNIE ISABEL, M.B.E. (1925).—B. 1877; staff nurse, Basutoland, 1906; matron, 1907; war serv., 1915-19; ment. in desps., 1918.

BROCKMAN, SIR EDWARD, LAWRIE, K.C.M.G. (1913), C.M.G. (1908).—B. 1865; Cadet, Straits Stltltns., 1886; passed in Malay, 1888; ag. supt. of educn., Penang, 1888; 3rd mag. Penang, June, 1890; ag. col. of land rev., Penang, May, 1889; ag. 2nd ast. col. sec., Feb. to Oct., 1892; dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, Mar., 1892; ag. collr. land rev., Penang, July, 1895; 2nd ast. col. sec., June, 1896; also ag. collr. land rev., Singapore, June, 1896; ag. ast. col. sec. and clk. of coun., Aug., 1897; collr. land rev., Malacca, 1898; ag. 1st mag.
