GYDE, CHARLIE JOHN, M.I.C.E.—Indian govt. service, 1897-1902; asst. engnr. P.W.D., Transvaal, June, 1902; inspr. of wks., July, 1904; dist. engnr., Mar., 1907; inspecting engnr., Sept., 1917; ch. engnr., Jan., 1925.
HADDON, ERNEST B., M.A., F.R.A.I.—B. 1882; ed. Christ's Coll., Camb.; nat. science tripos, 1904; dip. anthrop., Camb., 1912; asst. collr., Uganda, 1905; dist. commr., 1912.

HADDON-SMITH, SIR GEO. BASIL, K.C.M.G. (1915); C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1861; lieut. Royal Guernsey militia, 1879; attached 32nd (D.C.L.I.) regt., 1889; attached 107th (Royal Sussex) regt., 1881; asst. inspr., Lagos constab., 1886; adjt., 1887; priv. sec. to ag. gov. and clk. of couns., 1887, 1889, and 1893; ag. inspr.-gen. and sheriff of Lagos, 1888, 1890, 1891, and 1893; ag. dist. commr., 1887, 1888, 1889, and 1891; took part in Jebu expedition, 1892, mentioned in despatches (medal and clasp); political offr. on Sir G. Carter's mission to Yorubaland, 1893; asst. col. sec., Lagos, 1894; ag. col. sec., 1896; priv. sec. to Sir F. Scott in Ashanti expdn., 1896; ch. ass. col. sec., G. Coast, 1896; ag. col. sec., 1896-7; mem. of exec. and legis. coun., G. Coast coll.; political offr. to Col. Sir J. Willcocks in the Ashanti expdn., 1900; took part in relief of Kumasi, spec. ment. in desps. (Ashanti medal and clasp); ag. gov. Gambia, July to Oct., 1901; col. sec., S. Leone, 1901-1911; ag. gov. of S. Leone on many occasions; gov., Bahamas, 1912; gov., Windward Is., 14th Dec., 1914; assumed govt., 30th Dec., 1914; ret., 1924.
HADDRIII, CYRIL F.—B. 1902; apptd., after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 29th Apr., 1920.

HAHN, D. M., M. Inst. C.E.—B. 1867; ed. Queen's Roy. Coll., Trinidad. Realschool, Hamburg, and Technological Inst., Hamburg; in charge of govt. saw mill, Trinidad, Jan., 1887; foreman, P.W.D., Jan., 1888; ag. astt. engrnr., 1891; astt. engrnr., in charge of wks. and bldings, Feb., 1894; astt. engrnr., office and wks., Nov., 1899; engrnr., office and wks., Apr., 1905; ag. div. engrnr., N. Div., 1903 and 1907, in addition to substantive duties; ag. inspr. of mines, 1907 to June, 1908; and dep. inspr. of mines, July, 1908, in addition to substantive duties; mem. bd of industrial training; astt. dir., pub. wks., Apr., 1914; ag. dir., pub. wks., on several occasions.
HALFORDYARD, HON. W. W.—Elected M.H.A., Fogo, Newfoundland, 1913; min. of agric. and mines, 1917; M.E.C., 1917; col. sec., 1918.
HALL, GUTHRIE.—B. 1897; 2nd lieut., Cheshire Regt., 1915; lieut., 1917; served with Waziristan Field Force, 1919; astt. dist. comsrr., Gold Coast, 1921.

HALL, J. D., B.A. (Oxon).—Cadet, S. Sttlmts., Nov., 1910; ag. dep. regisr., sup. ct., Singapore and sec., Mohammedan and Hindu endowments bd., in addition, Apr., 1911; ag. dist. offr., Alor Gajah, Apr., 1912; passed cadet, July, 1913; supernymy, offr., cls. V., continued ag. dist. offr., Alor Gajah, Dec., 1913; astt. adviser, Batu Pahat, Johore, Feb., 1914; supernymy, offr., cls. IV., Dec., 1919; ag. 2nd mag., Dec., 1919; ag. 2nd astt. col. sec., Mar., 1920; 3rd astt. col. sec., Apr., 1920; ag. 2nd astt. col. sec., Apr., 1921; ag. 2nd astt. sec. A, June, 1921; ag. 1st astt. sec. B, Dec., 1921.

HALL, J. HATHORN, D.S.O., M.C. Croix-de-Guerre (Belgium).—B. 1894; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Lincoln Coll., Oxford (1913-14); B.A.; on mily. serv., France and Belgium, Dec., 1915 to July, 1916, Oct., 1916, to Dec., 1918, Germany, Dec., 1918, to Apr., 1919; lieut. Royal Munster Fusiliers, Dec., 1914; capt., Dec., 1915; staff-capt., Mar., 1918; brig. major, Oct., 1918; wounded, 1916; ment. in desp., 1919; Egyptian civ. serv., miny. of finance, Sept., 1919 to Feb., 1921; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, astt. prin., C.O., 17th Feb., 1921; ag. prin., 11th July, 1923.

HALL, PHILIP EDWARD.—B. 1877; ed. at Clifton Coll. and Wadham Coll., Oxford (B.A.); astt. native comsrr., N.E. Rhodesia, 12th Jan., 1901; native comsrr., 1st Sept., 1902; astt. mag., 15th May, 1905; ag. mag., 26th Oct., 1911 to 16th Sept., 1912; mag., 26th Aug., 1915; ag. res. mag., Barotse dist., 5th Aug., 1920 to 20th May, 1921; res. mag., Barotse dist., 14th Mar., 1925; ag. sec., native affrs., 30th Jan. to 11th July, 1926; J.P.

HALL, ROGER EVANS.—B. 1883; ed. Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; 3rd cl. hons. mods.; 2nd cl. hons. final jurisprudence; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1908; astt. dist. comsrr., Gold Coast, 1910; crown counsel, 1914; ag. senr. crown counsel, 1914; senr. crown counsel, 1915; ag. solr.-gen., 1916, 1917 and 1918; circuit judge, Ashanti (temporary appt.), 1918; ag. atty.-gen., 1919; circuit judge, Ashanti and N. Territories, G. Coast, 1st Jan., 1920; ag. puisne judge, 1920; puisne judge, 1921.
HALL, W.—B. 1901; apptd., after compet. exam., astt. clk., and assigned to C.O., Jan., 1920.

HALLIFAX, EDWIN RICHARD, C.M.G. (1922), C.B.E. (1922), O.B.E. (1918).—Ed. Blundell's schl.; Blundell's scholar, Balliol Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1896; cadet, Hong Kong, 1897; passed cadet, 1900; ag. police mag., New Territory, 1899; pol. mag., Hong Kong, 1899; J.P., 1900; astt. supt. of pol. and pol. mag., N.T., 1900; ag. regisr.-gen., 1902; acted as dep. supt. of pol., astt. supt. of fire brigade, capt. supt. of pol., etc., on several occasions, 1902-04; seconded as Transvaal emigrn. agt. at Chinwangtao, 1905; dist. offr., N.T., 1907; in addition, acted as astt. land offr., N.T., 1907 and 1908; ag. 1st pol. mag., 1909 to 1911; ag. dep. supt. of police and astt. supt. of fire brig., 1911; ag. regisr.-gen. and M.L.C., 1911; M.E.C., 1912; regisr.-gen., 1912; title altered to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1913; priv. sec. to gov., in addition, 1914 to 1915; commr., Hong Kong sec., Br. Empire Exhibn., 1924-25.

HALL-JONES, HON. SIR WILLIAM, K.C.M.G. (1910).—B. 1861; ed. at public schls.; arrived in New Zealand, 1875; mem. of house of representatives for Timaru, New Zealand, 1890-1908; min. of pub. wks. and marine, 1896; premier col. treas., min. for educn. and labour, June, 1906; resig. premiership, and assumed portfolios of pub. wks. and rly., in Ward ministry, Aug., 1906; high comsrr. in Lond., 1908-1912; M.L.C. since 1913.

HAM, GORDON LUPTON.—Cadet, F.M.S., 7th Nov., 1908; lent to Kelantan, 13th, Oct., 1913; cls. IV., S. Sttlmts., 1st Jan., 1918; ag. 2nd astt. col. sec., 14th Apr., 1919; clk. of couns., 2nd Sept., 1919; 1st astt. col. sec., S. Sttlmts. and clk. of couns., Jan., 1920; astt. offl. assigne., Dec., 1920; ag. sec. to high comsrr., Feb., 1921; ag. astt. adviser to gov't. of Kelantan, Apr., 1921; ag. 1st astt. sec. B, S. Sttlmts., Apr., 1922.

HANBLIN, R. A.—B. 1881; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil ser. and temporarily employed in the science and art dept. from Mar. 21st to May 26th, 1899; assigned to C.O. July, 1899; conf. clk., 1st Jan., 1918.

HAMILTON, ANDREW TODD.—B. 1879; astt. mast., educn. dept., Hong Kong, 1910; on mily. serv., 14th Dec., 1917 to 11th Feb., 1919; ast. mast., cls. II, 1st Jan., 1920; astt. mast., 1921; cls. I, Mar., 1926.

HAMILTON, ARTHUR WEDEBERG-HARTWIG.—B. 1887; ed. Tonbridge Schl.; pol. probationer, 1908; proceeded to Amoy to study Chinese (Hokkien), Apr., 1909; passed final exam. in Hokkien, Jan., 1911; ag. astt. supt., pol., Singapore, Feb., 1911; passed final exam. in Malay, Jan., 1912; astt. supt., pol., Penang, Mar., 1913; ag. ch. pol. offr., Kelantan, May, 1915; astt. supt., pol., Singapore, May, 1915; astt. supt., pol., Penang, Oct., 1916; astt. supt., pol., Singapore, Apr., 1920 and Apr., 1923; ag. dir. criminal intell., May, 1920; astt. comsrr., pol., Kedah, Apr., 1921; ag. ch. pol. offr., Malacca, Dec., 1922; dep. comsrr., F.M.S. and ch. pol. offr., Pahang, Feb., 1923; attd. rubber inspection dept., Singapore, May, 1923; dep. comsrr., pol., F.M.S., Feb., 1923; dir., crim. investn. dept., F.M.S., May, 1924; ag. dir., polit. intell. bureau, Singapore, Sept., 1924.

HAMILTON, ERIC WILLIAM, B.A.—B. 1888; ed. Blundell's; exhibtr., Sid. Suss. Coll., Camb., 1906; scholar, 1907; B.A., 1909; cadet, Hong Kong civ. ser., 1911; ag. sec., sanitary bd., 1914; J.P., 1914; returned to Canton, 1914; attached to sanitary dept., 1915; passed final exam., 25th June, 1915; ag. astt. dist. offr., South, 1915; ag. 3rd astt. to sec. for Chinese affairs, and dep. regisr. of marriages, 1915; attached to imports and exports office in addition, 1916; ag. astt. P.M.G., 1916; ag. astt. dist. offr., 1917; attached

HANKEY, Lieut.-Col. Sir, M.P.A., G.C.B. (1919); K.C.B. (1916); C.B. (1912).—B. 1877; ed. Rugby; joined R.M.A., 1896; served in H.M.S. "Ramillies," flagship and 2nd flagship, Mediterranean, 1899-1901; naval intell. dept., 1902-6; granted temporary rank of Major whilst serving on a special comttee., 1906; intell. offr., Mediterranean, 1907; ast. sec., comttee. of Imp. defence, 1908; apptd. sec., 1912; sec., war cabinet, from 1916; British sec., Peace Confe., 1919; comdr., Legion of Honour, 1917; commendatore, Crown of Italy, 1918.

HANKINSON, Walter Crossfield, M.C. (1918).—B. 1894; ed. Manchester Gram. Schl. and Jesus Coll., Oxford (exhibitioner); private, Royal Fusiliers, Sept., 1914; 2nd lieut., Yorks and Lancs. Regt., 9th Jan., 1915; lieut., 1st Apl., 1915; capt., 13th May, 1915; served in Egypt and France; demobilised, 28th Feb., 1919; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, 2nd cls. elk., C.O., Feb., 1920; ast. prin., 1st Apr., 1920; ast. priv. sec. to Duke of Devonshire, 1st Mar., 1923; ast. priv. sec. to Mr. J. H. Thomas, 22nd Jan., 1924 and to Mr. Amery, 20th Nov., 1921; ast. priv., 2nd Nov., 1925.

HANN, Hugh.—B. 1862; joined R.A. 1881; served in India, 1882 to 1890; ret. from serv., 1890; joined home prison serv., 1891; promoted olk. and schoolmaster, 1896; supt. of pris., S. Leone, 1891; sheriff and ag. supt. of pol., S. Leone, July to Sept., 1902; J.P. for colony, 1902; formed S. Leone fire brigade and first supt., 1893; 1st ast. supt. of pris., Ceylon, 1906; supt. of pris., Kandy, 1st Nov., 1906, to Sept., 1908; ast. supt., convict establishment, Colombo, Oct., 1908, to Dec., 1909; ag. insp.gen. of prisons, Ceylon, and supt. of convict establishment, Sept. to Dec., 1909; supt., Mutwal and Hultadorp prisons, Jan. to Aug., 1910; supt., Mahara prison, Sept., 1910; ag. inspr-gen. of prisons, Sept. to Dec., 1911; supt., Fremantle prison, W. Aust., 1st Jan., 1912.

HANNAY, Henry Desmond.—Served Anglo-Boer war, 1899-01; sub-inspr., Bechuanaland Prot. police, 1901; ag. mag., N'Gami, 1907-08; inspr., Feb., 1914; ag. res. mag., 1914; Capt., N. Staffs. regt., European war, 1914-20; granted hon. rank of major, 1926; ag. res. mag., 1926.

HANNIGAN, Charles.—B. 1877; ed. Ranelagh Sch., Athlone, Piper's Coll. and Connell's Acad., Belfast; serv. R. Irish constab., Mar., 1896 to Apr., 1902; inspr., pol., F.M.S., May, 1902; ag. ast. comsnr., pol., Sept., 1906; ast. comsnr., pol., Mar., 1907; ag. supt., pol., S. Stlitmts., Aug., 1916; supt., pol., S. Stlitmts., Sept., 1916; ag. inspr-gen., pol., S. Stlitmts., Jan. to Mar., 1919, Apr., 1920 to Apr., 1921 and Dec., 1922 to Mar., 1923; comsnr., pol., F.M.S., Nov., 1924.

HANSON, B. E.—Educ. Clifton Coll.; elk., exchequer and audit dept., 10th Apr., 1899; elk., audit off., Hong Kong, 1st June, 1900; ag. local aud., 31st Mar., 1901, to 1st Aug., 1902; ast. audr., Uganda, 9th Jan., 1904; ag. local aud., May to Nov., 1904, and July to Nov., 1906; local aud., N. Nigeria, 3rd Mar., 1908; local aud., Sierra Leone, 23rd July, 1909; audr., Nigeria, 25th June, 1921.

HARCOURT, Harold Wallace.—B. 1885; examng. elk., audit dept., Fiji, Nov., 1909; book-pr., treasy., May, 1910; chief audr., Tonga, under three years agreement with native govt., June, 1911; audr.-gen. and govt. sec., Sept., 1911; ag. premier, 1st Jan. to 26th Feb., 1914; 2nd ast. audr., Fiji, 14th Oct., 1914; ag. ch. elk., treasy., 15th Oct., 1918; ag. ast. audr., 3rd July, 1920; ag. ch. olk., treas. and sec., comsrs. of currency, 29th Oct., 1920; ag. comsrs., inland rev., 18th Apr., 1921; cust., enemy property and contr., clearing office, Fiji and W. Pacific (conjoint), 11th May, 1921; provisionally M.L.C., 15th Aug., 1921; ag. comsrs. of stamps (conjoint), 18th Jan., 1922; ag. audr. (conjoint), 3rd Apr. to 30th June, 1922; dep. treas. and comsrs. of inland rev. and stamps, 1st Jan., 1923; ag. col. treas., 15th Apr. to 16th Aug., 1924; ag. prov. mem., exec. coun., 22nd Apr., 1924; ditto, leg. coun., 20th July, 1924; ag. col. treas. and ag. chmn., retd. sailors and soldiers advances bd. of control (conjoint); apptd., prov. mem., exec. coun., Feb., 1926.

HARDING, ALFRED J., O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1878; ed. Christ's Coll., Brecon, and St. John's Camb.; foundtn. schlr., 1899; 1st class nat. sci. trip. part I., 1900; B.A., 1900; M.A., 1920; ent. C.O. after compet. exam. as 2nd cl. olk., Oct., 1901; sec. to comtee. to inquire into organisation of Crown agents' office, 1908; asst. priv. sec. to Lord Crewe, 1st Feb., 1909; sec. to Royal comsrs. on Mauritius, 1909; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Lewis Harcourt, Nov., 1910; ag. 1st cls. olk., Sept., 1910 to Jan., 1911, and from 1st Apr., 1912; 1st cls. olk., 12th Oct., 1914; sec., colonial blue-books comtee., 1917; ag. prin. olk., 1st Jan., 1920; asst. sec., 1st Apr., 1920; mem., W. African currency bd., 1926; mem., Palestine currency bd., 1926.

HARDING, EDWARD J., C.B. (1926), C.M.G. (1917), M.A. (1907).—B. 1880; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Hertford Coll., Oxford (scholar, 1899); 1st cls. clas. mods., 1901; 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1903; B.A., 1903; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1912; apptd. after compet. exam., 2nd cls. olk., marine dept., bd. of trade, 30th Nov., 1903; 2nd cls. olk., C.O., 31st May, 1904; sec. to distressed colonial seamen comtee., 1908; asst. priv. sec. to Mr. Lewis Harcourt, 14th Oct., 1912; sec. to Dominions Royal Coman., Dec., 1912; ag. 1st cls. olk., 10th Apr., 1916; 1st cls. olk., 4th June, 1916; on military service from 20th Nov., 1915, to 9th Apr., 1916; sec. to comtee. for sttltm. of ex-service men within the Empire, 1917; ag. prin. olk., 1st Apr., 1919; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; asst. sec., 1st Dec., 1921; asst. under-sec. of state for dominions affairs, 30th July, 1925.

HARDY, THOMAS JAMES, A.C.R.A. (1924).—B. 1900; passed exams. of London Assn. of Accts. and Corporation of Accts., 1923; diploma in book-keeping and accountancy, executorship and trust law and accts., mercantile law, auditing and economics, 1923; ent. C.O., 3rd Mar., 1916; apptd. asst. olk., C.O., after compet. exam., 24th Apr., 1918; cler. olk., 1st Jan., 1920.

HARE, REGINALD CHARLES, C.M.G. (1915).—B. 1863; clk. treasy., W. Australia, Apl., 1881; pol. inspr. S. dista., June, 1886; ag. govt. res., Wyndham, May, 1888; ditto, Rockbourne, June, 1889; sec., W. Australian govt. agency, London, May, 1891; ag. agent-gen., 1909.
HARENESS, EDWARD BURNS, C.M.G. (1922).—Entered treasy., New S. Wales, 1891; sec. to Premier's office, 1901-16; under sec., dept. of ch. sec. and public health, since 1916.
HARLEY, JOHN A.—M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), certif. Lond. S.T.M. (with distinction), July, 1909; apptd. to W.A.M.S., Gambia, 16th Apr., 1910; ag. protectorate med. offr., 2nd Jan. to 11th May, 1911.

HARLAN, H. A. A. F., D.S.O., B.Sc. (Lond.).—B. 1881; capt., 4th (S.R.) Batt., S. Staff. Regt.; prins., govt. training inst. for teachers, Accra, Gold Coast, 17th Apr., 1912; attd., Gold Coast Regt., for serv. in E. Africa, 14th May, 1916 to 31st Dec., 1918; slightly wounded, ment. in desps.; prin., King's Coll., Lagos, Dec., 1919; asst. dir., educn., G. Coast, 14th Oct., 1926.

HARLAN, WM. HENRY.—B. 1882; apptd., after compet. exam., olk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv. and assigned to science and art dept., 10th Apr., 1900; to bd. of agric., 6th June, 1901; transf. to C.O., 27th Jan., 1902.

HARNETT, LIEUT. EDWARD.—B. 1887; ed. Brompton Oratory schl.; capt., Lincolnshire Yeomanry; lieut., Sierra Leone Batt., W.A.F.F., 1918-21; passed lower stand. exam. in Mendi and Mandingo; asst. dist. comsrs., Sierra Leone, 11th Jan., 1922.

HARPER, CHARLES HENRY, C.M.G. (1921), O.B.E. (1919).—Ed. at Blundell's schl., Devon, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1899; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 5th May, 1909; cadet, G. Coast, 24th Jan., 1900; attached to Ashanti field force, 1st June to 1st Sept., 1900; asst. dist. comsrs., 6th Sept., 1901; ag. asst. col. sec., 26th Oct., 1904; asst. col. sec., 8th Feb., 1906; ag. chief asst. col. sec. and olk. of couns., 14th Apr. to 26th June, 1908; cantonment mag., Coomassie, 16th Oct., 1908; prov. coman., 23rd July, 1909; census offr., 1911; ag. chief asst. and olk. of couns., 15th May to 29th June, 1912; ag. col. sec., June to Oct., 1912, and from 3rd May to 27th Aug., 1914; attached to Togoland field force, 29th Aug., 1914; sen. polit. offr., Togoland, 30th Sept. to 23rd Dec., 1914; chief asst. col. sec., 14th Feb., 1915; ag. col. sec., 25th June to 17th Nov., 1915, 27th Nov., 1915, to 27th June, 1916, and from 2nd Dec., 1916, to 23rd Apr., 1917; ch. coman., Ashanti, May, 1920; gov., St. Helena, 26th Dec., 1924; assumed gov't., 2nd Feb., 1925.
HARPER, FRANCIS HENRY, M.B.E. (1920).—B. 1877; transferred from the service of the Royal Niger Co. as supplementary olk. to C.O., 1st Jan., 1900; conf. olk., Nov., 1912; deptl. asst., 1st Apr., 1922.

HARRAGIN, WALTER.—B. 1890; barrister-at-law; olk. of the peace and sub.-registrar, San Fernando and Victoria, Trinidad, 9th Mar., 1914; served with the army as temporary captain; mag., Victoria, Sept., 1919; crown counsel, 1924; ag. solr.-gen., Apr. to Oct., 1924 and from 15th Jan., 1925 to 13th Aug., 1926.

HARRAGIN, WILLIAM THORNTON.—B. 1887; clk., sup. ct., Trinidad, 1910; asst. mast., Queen's Royal Coll., 1911; held comsrs. in Gordon Highlanders, 1915-16; served in France, May to Oct., 1915; asst. mast., Queen Victoria Sch., Fiji, 1916; cadet, Solomon Is., 1917; supervisor of customs, 2nd cls., G. Coast, 1st June, 1918; ag. asst. dist. coman., Jan.-Mar., 1919; asst. dist. coman., 28th Apr., 1919; ag. dist. coman., in 1919 and 1920; asst. col. sec., 1st Jan., 1921.

HARREL, MELVILLE DAVID, O.B.E. (1924).—B. 1872; attd. Royal Irish constab. depot, 1896; certifa., drill and police duties; sub-inspr. Jamaica constab., Jan., 1896; spcl. serv. to Nicaragua, Jan., 1897; 3rd cls. inspr., Nov., 1901; 2nd cls., Feb., 1907; 1st cls., June, 1913; deputy inspr.-gen., Oct., 1913; ag. inspr.-gen. of police and prisons, July to Sep., 1914; inspr.-gen. of police and comdt. local forces, Barbados, Aug., 1916; local rank of lieut.-col., 1919; inspr.-gen. pol., Jamaica, 1925.
HARRIGAN, SAMUEL SIMONS.—Head teacher, Wesleyan schls., Virgin Is., 1878; resig., 1903; clerical asst., govt. offices, Road Town, 1903;
HARRINGTON, WILFRED FRANCIS, M.C.—B. 1866; ass't polit. offr., 5th Dec., 1917; adminis. offr., 1st grade, Tanganyika Territory, 1st Aug., 1923.

HARRIOTT, EDMUND COWLES.—B. 1865; clk. to commnr., and to legis. bd., Turks and Caicos Islds., Nov., 1887; inspected govt. schls., 1890, 1891, and 1902; ag. reglar. sup. ct., and of deeds, 1891 and 1894; ag. asst. commnr. (dist. mag., reglar. sup. ct. and reglar.-gen., man. savings bank, and inspr. of pol. and prisons), G. Turk, May to July, 1896, Aug. to Nov., 1901, and May to Aug., 1906; J.P., 1901; ag. aud., Aug. and Sept., 1901, and June to Aug., 1906; ag. dist. mag., visiting Caicos Is., July, 1902; ag. acctnt. and clk. (collr. of cust., postmr., cashier and reglar. shipping); commnr.'s office, Apl. to June, 1903; confirmed July, 1903; ag. offl. mem., legis. bd., 1906; 1st cls. clk., rev. dept., Jamaica, Jan., 1907; ag. asst. commnr., Salt Cay, May to July, 1909; ag. deputy stamp commnr., Aug. to Nov., 1909; served in cust. dept., occupied territory, E. Africa, 1916-18; collr. of taxes and parochial treas., St. Anne, Jamaica, Jan., 1920.

HARRIS, HON. ROBERT EDWARD, D.C.L., LL.D.—B. 1860; ed. High School, Annapolis Royal; pri. tuition; D.C.L., King's Coll. Univ., Windsor, N.S.; LL.D., Dalhousie Univ., Halifax, N.S.; called to the N.S. bar, 1882; K.C., 1889; practised at Yarmouth, 1882-92; Halifax, 1892-1915; justice, sup. ct., N.S., 1916; ch. just., sup. ct., N.S., 7th Feb., 1918; selected as third arbitr. to determine value of Canadian Northern Rly. stock, on its acquisition by the Dom. Govt.; mem. of the coun. of the N.S. Barrister's Soc., and for some years pres.; chancellor, diocese of N.S., 1906; dir., schl. for blind, Halifax; gov. of King's Coll., Windsor, N.S.

HARRISON, ALBERT SEDDON, M.Sc. Manchester, 1908.—B. 1887; on staff of Manchester Univ., and asst. mast., Fielden demonstrn. schl., 1908-10; senr. mast., Halesowen comml. schl., 1910-12; on staff Manchester Univ. educn. dept., and headmnr., Fielden demonstrn. schl., 1912-14; instr. of schls., Ceylon, Apr., 1914; on active serv., 1918-19; ag. asst. dir. of educn., Ceylon, May, 1919, to Apr., 1920; ag. dir. of educn., Apr. to Oct., 1920; ag. asst. dir. of educn., Oct., 1920; prin., training coll., Jan., 1921.

HARRISON, CUTHBERT WOODVILLE.—B. 1874; ed. at Sherborne Schl., Clifton Coll., Balliol Coll., Oxford, and with Walter Wren, Esq.; 3rd cls. class. moda., 1894; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1897; ag. asst. dist. offr., Klang, May, 1899; also ag. harb. mast., Klang, Feb., 1901; passed cadet, Apr., 1901; asst. dist. offr., Ulu Langat, continued also as ag. dist. offr., Klang, and harb. mast. at Port Swettenham, Oct., 1901; ag. dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Dec., 1903; ditto, Tapin, Mar., 1904; also asst. dist. offr., Matang, Oct., 1904; ag. asst. sec. to res., Mar., 1906; ag. sec. to res., Aug., 1906; ag. asst. sec. to res., Dec., 1906; ag. reglar., sup. ct., Nov., 1907; ag. asst. dist. offr., Larut, Feb., 1908; also ag. dist. offr., Larut, and reglar. of titles, Perak North, Aug., 1908; dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Mar., 1909; ag. asst. dist. offr., Larut and Krian, and reglar. of titles, Perak North in addition, Feb., 1911; ag. sec. to res., Jan., 1912; dist. offr., Larut, Sep., 1914; collr. of land rev., Seremban, and reglar. of titles, Negri Sembilan, May, 1915; temporarily ag. British res., Negri Sembilan, in addition, Aug., 1917; collr. of land rev., Kuala Lumpur, and reglar. of titles, Selangor, 8th Mar., 1918; sec. to res., Selangor, 24th July, 1919; sec. to res., Perak, Dec., 1921; ag. commnr., lands, F.M.S., Apr., 1923; commnr., lands, Aug., 1923; ag. under sec. to govt., F.M.S., Apr., 1924; commnr., lands, Oct., 1924; ag. under sec. to govt., F.M.S., Mar., 1925; commnr., lands, Nov., 1925; editor of "Illustrated Guide to F.M.S."; author of "The Magic of Malaya," and "Railway Guide to F.M.S. and Siam."

HARRISON, CAPT. HORACE DURNFORD.—B. 1884; war serv., with D.L.I., 1914-19; France, 1915-18; twice ment. in despr.; headmast., govt. primary schls., Gold Coast, 5th June, 1920; mast., training coll., 1st Jan., 1921; instr., schls., 1st Apr., 1926.

HARRISON, ERNEST, M.S.A. (Ivwa), B.Sc. (Agric.), N.D.A. (Hons.).—Lecturer in agr., schl. of agr., Groofontein, Cape, 1904; prin., schl. of agr., Cedara, Natal, 1913; dep. dir., agr., Kenya, 1921; ag. dir., agr., Feb. to Nov., 1922 and Mar.-Oct., 1924.

HARRISON-JONES, CHARLES.—B. 1884; ed. Reading and St. John’s Coll., Oxford (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1907; pol. mag., Puttalam, Jan., 1909; asst. govt. agt., Colombo and Negombo dists., July, 1910; ag. ast. gov. agt., Hambantota, Apr., 1912; pol. mag., Matara, May, 1912; ag. ast. gov. agt., Mannar, Feb., 1913; on military duty, Jan., 1915; dist. judge Tangalla, Dec., 1919; ag. ast. govt. agt., Matale, June, 1920; ditto, N’Eliya, Nov., 1924.

HART-DAVIS, CHARLES HENRY.—B. 1874; ed. at Eton and New Coll., Oxford, B.A.; served in 13th I.Y. in South Africa, 1900 (Queen’s medal, 3 clasps); priv. sec. to Sir W. Haynes Smith, Cyprus, 1901; ast. insp. of schools, Cyprus, 1901 to 1906; passed exams. in Turkish and Greek, 1903; insp. of schools, Gold Coast, 1906; dis. comntr., 1906 to 1910; sec. to high comntr. for the Western Pacific, 1910; on mily. serv., 1915-19; ch. ast. sec., Cyprus, Apr., 1919; ag. ch. insp. of schls., Aug. to Oct., 1919; ag. ch. sec., Sept. to Dec., 1919; ag. regiar-gen., Dec. to May, 1920; ag. comntr., July to Oct., 1920; ag. ch. sec., 27th Oct., 1920 to 3rd Feb., 1921, 17th to 23rd Mar., 11th May to 3rd Oct., 1921 and 14th July to 13th Oct., 1923; comntr., 1st July, 1922; mem., leg. coun., Jan., 1924.

HARTER, JEAN DENIS EDGARD.—B. 1879; ed. R. Coll. of Mauritius; ast. mast., R. Coll., 1901; ast. mast., Victoria Schl., Seychelles, Aug., 1902; ast. mast., King’s Coll., 1910; headmast., Victoria Schl., July, 1920; ag. dir. educ. and insp. of schls., Seychelles, Jan., 1922; insp. of schls., Seychelles, Jan., 1924; sec. to bd. of educ.; local sec., Cambridge local exam.; ag. pvt. sec., clk. to gov. and clk. to couns. from 16th Dec., 1924.
HARTNELL, G. F. W.—Chief mechanical engr., Uganda rlyw., June, 1918, and from Apr.-June, 1922; comntr., July, 1922; ag. regiar-gen., July-Sept., 1922.

HARVEY, DAVID HAROLD.—B. 1874; entd. Natal civ. serv., Apr., 1898; transf. to Swaziland adminstr. as clk., Mar., 1903; sub-insp., Swazi-land police, Apr., 1907; dep. ast. comntr., Apr., 1911; ast. comntr., Mlanikaana dist., 1920.

HARVEY, JOHN ALLEN.—B. 1900; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1924; addl. dist. office, Port Dickson, Jan., 1925; addl. land office, Seremban, Apr., 1925; ag. ast. dist. offr., Krian, Aug., 1925; priv. sec. to ch. sec. to govt., F.M.S., May, 1926.
HARVEY, HON. JOHN MUSGRAVE.—B. 1885; judge of the sup. ct., New S. Wales, 1913.
HARVEY, REGINALD JAMES.—B. 1901; ed. Charterhouse and Exeter Coll., Oxford, 1920-23; 4th cls., hons., mod. hist.; St. George Schl., Jerusalem, 1923-25; supt., educ., Tanganyika Territory, 1926.

HASKINS, WILFRED J., A.M.C.E.A.—Ast. supt. of wks., S. Stlitlms., Mar., 1905; dist. engr., F.M.S. rlyw., May, 1912; senr. dist. engr., grade B., F.M.S. rlyw., July, 1919; ag. engr. for ways and wks., Nov., 1920 to Oct., 1921; ag. ch. res. engr., for constrn., Mar., 1923; senr. dist. engr., Johore dist., Nov., 1924; ag. engr., ways and wks., Aug., 1926.

HASZARD, HON. FRANCIS LONGWORTH.—B. 1849; ed. at Prince of Wales’s Coll., Prince Edward Is.; called to the bar, 1872; K.C., 1894; elected to legis., P.E.I., 1904 and 1908; mem. exec. coun., 1906; prem. and atty.-gen., P.E.I., 1906; judge, supreme ct., and master of the rolls, P.E.I., 1911.

HATCHER, C. M.—B. 1873; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. 2nd div. civ. ser., and assigned to W.O., 11th Sept., 1893; transf. to C.O., 18th Mar., 1896; minor staff offr., Feb., 1902; staff clk., first grade, July, 1913; deptl. ast., 26th Feb., 1923.

HAUGHTON, T. H.—B. 1881; served in S. African Constab., Mar., 1901, to Dec., 1907; held rank of lieut. and sub-inspr. (Queen’s medal with five clasps); ast. res., Muri Prov., N. Nigeria, 3rd May, 1908.
HAUSSMANN, G. C. J. L. F.—B. 1868; clk., treas., Cape, 1889; acct., pension funds, Cape, 1896; prin. clk., treas., Pretoria, 1910; ch. clk., pensions, treas., 1917; ch. pensions offr., 1919.

HAVENGA, HON. NICOLAAS CHRISTIAAN.—Atty-at-law; mem., prov. coun. for Fauresmith, O.F.S. and mem., exec. comtes. for O.F.S., 1910-15; mem., Union H. of A. for Fauresmith from 1915; min. of finance, 30th June, 1924.

HAWKEN, H. E. A.—B. 1879; ed. pub. schls., Ottawa Colleg. Inst.; ent. civ. serv. of Canada as jun. clk., dept. marine and fisheries, 1901; 2nd cls. clk., 1908; 1st cls. clk., 1910; ch. clk., 1917; ast. dep. min., 1st Apr., 1920.
HAWKEN, HON. OSWALD JAMES.—B. 1870; ed. Patea and Kakaramea pub. schls., N.Z.; mem., N.Z. parl., since 1919; min. of agr. and comntr. of state forests, 1926.
HAWKINS, BRIAN CHARLES KEITH.—B. 1900; cadet, Hong Kong, Dec., 1924.

HAWKINS, GERALD.—B. 1891; B.A., London Univ.; pte., 1/5th Gloucester Regt., 8th Aug., 1914; 2nd lieut., 1/5th Gloucester Regt., 7th Feb., 1916; B.E.F., 29th Mar., 1915; ment. in desp., 20th Dec., 1917; wounded, and discharged with rank of capt., 6th May, 1920; cadet, F.M.S., 20th Nov., 1920; addl. to land office, Penang, 21st Dec., 1920; addl. collr. of land rev., Penang, 14th Jan., 1921; ag. dist. offr., Balik Pulau, 21st Dec., 1921; offr., cls. V, May, 1919; cls. IV., May, 1923; ag. ast. sec., res., Selangor, Dec., 1925; sec., res., Pahang, Apr., 1926.
HAY, CLIFFORD H., C.M.G., M.V.O.—Sec. to premier of New S. Wales since Mar., 1916.

HAY, MORTIMER CSMR.—B. 1891; ed. The Schl., Bishop Stortford and St. Catharine’s Coll., Cambridge (exhib.); B.A., Cantab.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1913; ag. ast. contrir. of lab., Penang, Mar., 1915; ag. ast. dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, Mar., 1916; passed cadet, July, 1916; offr., cls. V, Dec., 1916; dep. contrir. of lab., Klang, Dec., 1920; ag. ast. adviser, Endau, Johore, May, 1921; ag. ast. adviser, Batu Pahat, Mar., 1922; ag. prot. of Chinese and contrir. of lab., Johore, Oct., 1922; ag. ast. adviser, Batu Pahat, Sept., 1923; dep. contrir. of lab., Kuala Lumpur, Sept., 1923; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1925; ag. ast. adviser, Kemaman, Trengganu, June, 1926.

HAYCRAFT, SIR THOS. WAGSTAFFE, KT BACH.—(1919).—B.A., St. John’s Coll., Oxon; called to the bar, In. Tem., 1885; practised in S.E. circuit and Sussex sessions; examr. of the ct., 1889 to 1899; apptd. arbitrator on bd. of Lond. cham. of arbitn., 1897; pres. dist. ct., Larnaca, Cyprus, 1899; pol. mag., Gibraltar, 1911; puisane judge, Mauritius, 1913; ch. just., Grenada, 1916; ch. just., Palestine, 1921; ret., Mar., 1927; author of “Executive Powers in relation to Crime and Disorder.”

HAYDEN, JAMES J.—B. 1883; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Dublin; clasped in feudal and English law, Dublin Univ. (dual gold medallist); admitted solr., sup. ct. of judicature, Ireland, 1906; commr. for oaths and regier., high ct., Uganda, 21st Jan., 1921; is also addnl. dist. mag., 1st cls. admstr.-gen., rev. authority, regier. of companies, patents, designs, trade-marks, documents and business names, custodian of enemy property and contr. of local clearing house.

HAYDOCK-WILSON, HUGH.—B. 1897; ed. Bedford; on war serv., Artists Rifles (London R.) and Manchester Regt., 1915-19; ent. civ. serv., Br. Guiana as 4th cls. clk., C.S.O., 25th Apr., 1921; 3rd cls. clk., 16th July, 1924; capt., B.G. Militia (Art.), 1922-26.

HAYDON, LEONARD GUSCOTE, D.S.O.—M.B., C.M., D.P.H. (Univ. of Aberdeen); admstrv. M.O., plague suppression, Mandvi, Bombay, 1897; pub. health serv., Victorian Govt., 1899; civ. surg., etc., S. Africa, 1900; pub. health serv., Natal gov't., 1902; asst. M.O.H., Union of S. Africa, 1910; A.D.M.S., central force, S.W. African campaign, 1914; prin. med. offr., S.W. African Prot., 1915; asst. M.O.H., Union of S. Africa, 1917 senr.; asst. health offr., Union of S. Africa, 1919.

HAYES, CAPT. JOHN WILLIAM, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1892; ed. Daniel Stewart's Coll., Edinburgh; R. Vety. Colla., Edinburgh, London and Dublin; war serv., India, 1917-20; ment. in deeps. (Afghan War, 1919); vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1921.

HAYES, ROBERT EDWARD, I.S.O. (1926).—B. 1869; ed. New Zealand Univ.; entd. N.Z. postal dept., 1885; act., tourists and industries and commerce dept., 1903; regier., friendly societies, 1906; also supt., national provident fund, 1910; asst. sec. and acct. to treas., 1922; sec. treas., paymr.-gen. and regier. of inscribed stock, 1925; again supt., national prov. fund, 1926.

HAYES-SADLER, CECIL WILLIAM, M.A. (Oxon)—B. 1892; ed. Eton and Trinity Coll., Oxford; served in France with R. Engrs. (Signals), 1914-18; French Croix de Guerre, 1917; asst. dist. commnr., Kenya Col., 1920; ag. senr. asst. sec., 1926.

HAYNES, ALWYN SIDNEY.—B. 1878; ed. at Haileybury Coll.; cadet, F.M.S. civ. ser., Nov., 1901; attached to dist. office, Batang Padang, Jan., 1902; ag. financial asst., Krian, Oct., 1902, to Apr., 1903; passed final exam. in law, Oct., 1902; mag. for Perak, 1902; passed final exam. in Tamil, Aug., 1903; ag. financial asst., Krian, Sept., 1903; administered estate of late Datoh Panglima Kinta in Perak, 1904-1906; passed final exam. in Malay, Nov., 1905; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Mar., 1906; ag. inapr. of obs., Negri Sembilan, Nov., 1906; 1st cls. mag., Negri Sembilan, 1906; ag. asst. supt. of Indian immigrts., Penang, Nov., 1906; asst. supt., ditto, Nov., 1906; examiner in Tamil, Perak and Penang; ag. supt. of Indian immigrts., F.M.S. and S. Stilimts., Feb. to May, 1910; ag. asst. supt. of immigrts., Klang, Jan., 1911; passed final exam. in Siamese, Aug., 1912; ag. dist. offr., Pekan, Sept., 1912; ag. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Dec., 1914; mem. of board of examiners in Malay for Perak, Apr., 1915; dist. offr., Tampin, 1916; sec. to high commnr., F.M.S., Apr., 1920; sec. for agr., S.S. and F.M.S., and dir., food production ditto, Feb., 1922; chmn., small holdings comtes. (rubber restrictions), Nov., 1923; ag. Br. res., Pahang, Feb., 1924; ag. Br. res., Perak, Nov., 1929; ag. Br. adviser, Kedah, Dec., 1926; ag. Br. res., Pahang, Sept., 1926.

HAZELTON, ERIC.—B. 1895; 2nd lieut., Monmouthshire Brig., R.F.A., June, 1915; joined B.E.F., France, Nov., 1915; lieut., R.H. and R.F.A., June, 1916; served with E.E.F., Suez Canal, Sinai, Palestine, 1916-19; cadet, F.M.S., Johore, Dec., 1919; 2nd asst. adviser to govt. of Johore, Jan., 1921; temp. ag. asst. adviser, Endau, Feb., 1921; ag. 2nd asst. adviser, Batu Pahat, May, 1921; passed cadet, Nov., 1921; ag. 4th asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Aug., 1922; 4th asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Nov., 1922; ag. pte. sec. to ch. sec., Nov., 1923; offi. sec., ree., Negri Sembilan, Nov., 1924.

HAZEN, HON. SIR JOHN DOUGLAS, K.C.M.G. (1918), K.C., LL.D.—B. 1860; ed. at Collegiate Sch., Fredericton, and Univ. of New Brunswick; called to the bar, 1888; K.C., 1894; elec. to H. of C., Canada, for St. John City and County, 1891; defeated, 1896; elec. to New Brunswick legis. for Sunbury, 1899, 1903, 1908; leader of the opposition in the New Brunswick legis. during that period; prem. and atty.-gen. of New Brunswick, 1908-1911; has been pres. of St. John law soc. and barristers soc. of New Brunswick; mem. of the P.C. for Canada, and min. of marine and fisheries in Mr. Borden's cabinet, Oct., 1911; chief just. of New Brunswick, Nov., 1917.

HAZLERRIGG, THOMAS MAYNARD, M.C.—Adm. mitted solr., sup. ct., England, 1906; lieut., R.A.S.C., 1914-1915; capt., R.A.S.C., 1915 to 1920; asst. crown solr., Hong Kong, 1920; ag. asst. land offr. in addn., 1921 to 1922 and from 1922 to 1923; ag. crown solr., Mar. to Nov., 1924.
HEAD, W. G.—B. 1899; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., Oct., 1916; on mil. serv. from July, 1917, to Oct., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; higher cler. offr., 19th May, 1925.

HEAD-EVANS, ELLES JAMES, M.I.M. & C.E., A.M.S.E.—Asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Nyasa land, Jan., 1912 to Aug., 1916; ag. asst. D.P.W., Nyasa land, Jan. to Sept., 1913; served with Nyasa land Vol. Res., 1914-15 (A.G.S. Med.); attd. as civil offr. to Nyasa land Field Force as ch. engrnr., road constrn. in operns. in German E. Africa, Mar., 1916 to Sept., 1917; seconded as asst. engrnr., P.W.D., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1916; exec. engrnr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1920.
HEAPE, WILLIAM LESLIE.—B. 1896; ed. Rugby and Sandhurst; asst. sec., Somaliland, Nov. 1919.

HEARST, SIR WILLIAM HOWARD, K.C.M.G. (1917).—B.1864; ed. pub. schls., Collingwood Coll. Inst., and Toronto Univ.; called to the bar, 1888; K.C., 1908; prominent legal practitioner at Sault Ste. Marie, Ontario; now head of firm Hearst, Rowland and Atkin; has been counsel in all more important civil and criminal cases in Algoma district in recent years; unsuccessful cand. for legis. assembly, Ontario, 1894; elec. at g.e. 1908, 1911, and 1914; min. of lands, forests and mines in Whitney administration, 1911; premier of Ontario, 1914; also min. of agric.; defeated at g.e., Oct., 1919; resig. with his govt., Nov., 1919; mem., internat. joint comm. (Can. section), 23rd Feb., 1920.

HEATH, A. P.—B. 1897; ed. Uppingham; on mil'y serv., Aug., 1915; 2nd lieut., Feb., 1917 to Aug., 1917; temp. clk., Min. of Nat. Serv., Nov., 1917; transf. O.S.O., Mar., 1919; apptd. after compet. exam. cler. offr., O.S.O., Nov., 1921.

HEATH, W. G.—M.B., Ch.B., Edin. Univ.; qualified, 1897; med. offr., Windward dist., Dominica, 1898; med. offr., dist. 2, Montserrat, 1901; ag. med. offr., dist. "D," Dominica, 1903; ditto, dist. "A," 1904; ag. sen. med. offr., Montserrat, 1908, 1912, and since 1916; pres., defence reserve, 1909; ag. mem. legis. coun., 1911; ag. med. offr., Antigua, 1911; mem. legis. coun., May, 1915; med. offr.-in-chg. of Montserrat recruits for the B.W.I. regt., Mar., 1916; surg.-lieut., defence force, 1916; provisionally mem. exec. coun., 1916; ag. comnrr., Montserrat, on several occasions since Feb., 1918.

HEDDON, JOHN DONALD, B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1879; clk., cust., Port Elizabeth, 1903; prin. clk., East London, 1915; survr., Durban, 1916; ch. clk., head office, Pretoria, 1918; del. for Union govt. at Imp. Cust. Conf., 1921; cust. adviser to delegn. to Australian govt. (flour shipments), 1921; mem., delegn. to Kenya to rept. on trade prospects, 1922; rep. of Union govt. at economic conference, Antananarivo, Madagascar, 1923.
HEENAN, PETER.—Elec. to Ontario legis., 1919-26; House of Commons, Canada, 1925; min. of lab., 1926.

HEIDENSTAM, EDGAR HENRY DE.—Ed. Rossall and private tutors; ag. priv. sec. to Sir W. F. Haynes Smith, high comnrr. of Cyprus, 1st Nov., 1901, to 13th July, 1902; clk., chief sec.'s office, 14th July, 1902; inspr. of Cyprus military police, 1st Feb., 1905; a.d.c. and priv. sec. to Sir C. King-Harman, and clk. to exec. coun., 1st Mar., 1906; clk. to legis. coun. and ast. clk., chief sec.'s office, 1st Oct., 1907; ag. chief clk., C.S.O., 13th July to 15th Oct., 1908; ag. ast. sec. to govt., July to Oct., 1915, and from Aug., 1916, to May, 1917; ast. sec., 1st Feb., 1918; ag. priv. sec., Nov., 1918; ast. audr., 1st Sept., 1920; ag. audr., Nov., 1922 to May, 1923, Feb. to May and July to Oct., 1924 and Nov., 1924, to July, 1925.

HEIDENSTAM, OSCAR CHARLES.—B. 1877; ed. at King's Schl., Warwick; vol. clk., col. sec.'s off., Cyprus, 5th Feb., 1897; 3rd div. clk., ditto, 16th Jan., 1899; inspr. of pol., 12th Jan., 1904; sent to Egypt for training with H.M.'s troops, May, June, and July, 1904; in command of Kyrenia div., gov. of prison and dep. coroner, 16th July, 1905; in command of Papho div., gov. of prison and dep. coroner, 4th Aug., 1906; acted as comnrr. for Papho on several occasions; passed in mod. Greek, lower standard, Apr., 1906; ag. priv. sec. and A.D.C. to high comnrr., Sir C. A. King-Harman, and clk. of exec. coun., 4th Jan. to 15th Feb., 1907; temporarily attached to ah. sec.'s off., 16th Feb., 1907; in comm. of pol., Kyrenia div., gov. of pris., and dep. coroner, 24th June, 1907; ast. dist. supt. of pol., Uganda Prot., 12th July, 1907; in commd. of Entebbe pol., and supt. of cent. gaol, 10th Sept., 1907; inspr. of police, Cyprus, Apl., 1910; ch. of pol., Grenada, 22nd July, 1914; inspr.-gen., pol., Barbados, 1926.

HELLINGS, GEOFFREY STUART.—B. 1888; ed. Christ's Hosp. (foundation schol.) and St. John's Coll., Camb., Stewart of Bannoch Univ. schol., Camb., 1909; B.A., 1910 (cl. trip, ii, I); cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1911; seconded for mil. ser., Jan., 1915; 2nd lieut., 8th (Service) Batt. Duke of Cornwall's Light Inf'y., Mar., 1915; 2nd lieut., 2nd Batt. D.C.L.I., May, 1916; lieut., 2nd Batt. D.C.L.I., Sept., 1916; ag. capt., 2nd Batt. D.C.L.I., June, 1918; demob., Sept., 1919; ag. 2nd ast. dist. offr., Batu Gajah, Nov., 1919; ag. ast. prot. of Chinese, Perak, Dec., 1920; ag. ast. prot., Chinese, Selangor, Sept., 1921; contr., lab. and prot. of Chinese, Johore, Sept., 1923; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1923.

HELPS, EDMUND ARTHUR PLUNKETT.—B. 1888; ed. Marlborough and Clare Coll., Cambridge; history tripos., 2nd cls. (1909); B.A. (1909); cadet, F.M.S., Jan., 1912; ag. 2nd ast. ast. of imigrts., Klang, Sept., 1912; ag. ast. contr., lab., Aug., 1913; ag. ast. sec. to res., Selangor, May, 1914; passed cadet, July, 1914; offr., cls. V, Mar., 1915; lent for service under the admn. of German E. Africa, July, 1917; offr., cls. IV, Jan., 1919; ast. dist. offr., Raub, Apr., 1920; ag. ast. dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, May, 1920; ast. dist. offr., Larut, July, 1920; dist. offr., Kuala Selangor, May, 1921; add. to sec., rubber restriction comtee., Kulim, Nov., 1923; ag. ast. adviser, Kedah, Jan., 1924; offr., cls. III, Ag. Br. adviser, Kedah, Nov., 1926; ast. adviser, Kedah, Dec., 1925.

HEMMANT, GEORGE.—B. 1880; ed. Tombridge and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; cadet, F.M.S., 1903; ag. ast. dist. treas., Kuala Lumpur, June-Dec., 1904 and Mar.-June, 1905; passed cadet (Malay and Law), May, 1905; ag. rev. andr., Negri Sembilan, July, 1905; ag. ast. dist. offr., Kuala Pilah, Aug., 1906-Mar., 1908; ag. dist. offr., Jeloh, Mar.-July, 1908 and Jan.-Feb., 1909; ag. ast. dist. offr., Kuala Pilah, July-Sept., 1908; ag. ast. dist. offr., Tampin, Oct., 1908-Jan., 1909 and from Mar., 1909-Oct., 1910; ast. collr., land rev., Seremban, Dec., 1910; ag. dist. offr., Temerloh, Oct., 1911-Jan., 1912; seconded, Kedah, Oct., 1914; ag. legal adviser, Kedah, Dec., 1914-Jan., 1916; seconded, Johore, Jan., 1916; ag. ast. adviser, Muar, Jan., 1916-June, 1918; collr. of land rev., Kuala Lumpur and regis. of titles, Selangor, Nov., 1919-Nov., 1920; sec. to res., Selangor, Dec., 1920-Mar., 1922; mag., Kuala Lumpur, Jan.-Apr., 1923; ag. under-sec., S. Sttlmts., May, 1923-Mar., 1924 and from Apr.-July, 1924; ag. col. sec., S. Sttlmts., Mar. and Aug., 1924; gov.'s dep., Mar., 1924; ag. gen. adviser, Johore, Sept., 1924.

HEMSTED, RUPERT WILLIAM, O.B.E. (1919).—2nd treasy. ast., E.A.P., 19th Sept., 1899; ch. acctnt., Somaliland, 1st Dec., 1901, to 1st Dec., 1903; ast. collr., E.A.P., 1st Dec., 1904; collr., 17th Dec., 1906; offr.-in-charge, Masai reserve, 1912; senr. comnrr. (1st cls.), Jan., 1924.

HENDERSON, FREDERICK LOUIS.—Ed. at Camb. Univ.; B.A., 1897; M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lon.); med. offr., E. Africa and Uganda Prota., 18th Oct., 1904; senr. med. offr., Apr., 1920; dist. surg., May, 1923.

HENDRIE, CAPT. HARRY GEORGE, M.C.—Foreman of Works, P.W.D., Gold Coast, 26th June, 1921; head mast., jun. trade schl., educn. dept., 1st Dec., 1921; on mil. serv., 7th Sept., 1914; 2nd lieut., Sept., 1915; coy. comdr. and ag. capt., Sept., 1916; demob. with rank of capt., Feb., 1920; serv. in Northd. Cyclists' Batt., Northd. Fus. and Machine Gun Corps; M.C. and Bar.

HENDRY, WILLIAM, M.A. (Edin.)—B. 1888; ed. Daniel Stewart's Coll., Edinburgh (dux and gold medallist), and Edinburgh Univ.; Egyptian educn. serv., 1911-15 and 1919-20; served Royal Highlanders and Royal Irish Regt., 1915-19; ment. in dep., 1919; dir. of educn., Zanzibar, 1920.

HENKEL, JOHN SPURGEON.—Joined forest dept., Eastern Conservancy, Cape, 1st July, 1888; ast. to conservator, 1st Mar., 1890; dist. forest offr., King William's Town, 1st June, 1892; Stutterheim, 1st Aug., 1898; selected to proceed to Royal Indian Engineering Coll., Cooper's Hill, for scientific course of forestry; obtained coll., diploma and specially commended by pres. of coll., 6th Aug., 1902; asst. conservator of forests, Eastern Conservancy, 1st Oct., 1905; asst. conservator, Western Conservancy, 1st July, 1907; apptd. principal lecturer in forestry, S. African schl. of forestry, 29th Oct., 1906; resig. lectureship, 28th Feb., 1908; chmn. of bd. of management, S. African schl. of forestry, 18th May, 1907 to 31st Jan., 1909; asst. conservator of forests, Midland Conservancy, 1st Feb., 1909; conservator of forests, Midland Conservancy, 2nd grade, 1st Apr., 1912; Conservator of Forests, Natal Conservancy, Dec., 1914; served as captain with dist. mounted troops during S. African war, 1901-2; Queen's medal; tranf., Rhodesian forest serv., 1st Jan., 1920.
HENLEN, GEORGE WILLIAM.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., Feb., 1920; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920.

HENNESSEY, PATRICK H.—M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.); house surg., S. Sttlmts., Feb., 1907; supernumerary med. offr., Penang, Feb., 1908; med. offr. gen. hosp., Singapore, 1st May, 1908; ag. med. offr., Malacca, 14th Oct., 1908; med. offr., grade II, Kuala Pilah, and med. offr., rly. construction, 1st Apr., 1909; ag. med. offr., grade I, 6th May, 1914; ag. senr. med. offr., Selangor, 2nd to 26th Nov., 1918, in addition, and again 3rd Dec., 1918 to 9th Feb., 1919, and from 9th Apr. to 23rd Dec., 1919; ophthalmologist physician in addition, med. offr., gen. hosp., K. Lumpur, 1st Sept., 1920; med. offr. and opthal. surg., Ipoh, 20th Sept., 1923.

HENNIKER-HEATON, HERBERT.—B.A. (Oxon.); ed. at Eton and New Coll., Oxford, (hons. hist., 1901); cadet, Fiji, 1902; attached to col. sec.'s off., to 1905; European offr., armed constab., June, 1903; in regis.-gen.'s off., Mar., May, 1904; ag. adjut., armed constab., musk. instr., and inspr. of pol., Feb., 1905, to Feb., 1906; stip. mag., Feb., 1906, and ag. stip. mag., Lau, and inspr. of taxes, Feb. to May, 1906; ch. elk., native dept., and ag. asst. native comntr., July, 1906; apptd. asst. native comntr., Mar., 1907; ag. chief pol. mag. and offr. recvr. in bankruptcy, Mar., 1910, to Feb., 1911; mem. of mining bd., Apr., 1910; comntr. of customs, Jan., 1911; mem. bd. of examiners, Feb., 1911; mem. nat. Suva schl. bd., Oct., 1911; ag. chief pol. mag., Jan., 1912; mem. native regulation bd., May, 1912; asst. col. sec., Mauritius, Oct., 1913; ag. col. sec., Mar. to Sept., 1914; ag. prot. of immigrts. and poor law comntr., Apr., 1915, to Jan., 1916; ag. col. sec., Jan. to May and from July, 1916, to May, 1917; in comd., cpy., Mauritius Vol. Force, 1916-17; col. sec., Gambia, 1917; ag. gov., June to Dec., 1918, Oct. to Dec., 1919 and July, 1920 to Jan., 1921; col. sec., Falkland Is., 1921; ag. gov., June, 1923 to May, 1924; col. sec., Bermuda, Oct., 1925.

HENRY, HON. GEORGE STEWART, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1871; ed. pub. schs., Upper Can. Coll., Univ. of Toronto, and Ont. Agric. Coll.; farmer; el. to Ont. legis. at by-el., 1913; re-el., 1914, 1919 and 1923; min. of pub. wks. and highways in Ferguson adminstr., 1923.
HERBERT, CHAS. EDWARD.—M.H.A., South Australia, 1900-5; govt. res. and judge of N. Territory, 1905 to 1910; now deputy chief judicial offr. in Papua.

HERBST, JOHN FREDERICK, C.B.E. (1919).—Cik., Cape census off., May, 1891; Carnarvon, Dec., 1891; Willowmore, May, 1895; atty.-gen'l's off., Cape Town, July, 1896; held several ag. appmts.; asst. mag., Gordonia, Apr., 1897; relv. offr., Apr., 1899; ag. asst. mag., Rietfontein, Nov., 1905; confirmed Jan., 1908; ag. civ. comntr. and mag., Somerset, E., May, 1908; mag., St. Marks, July, 1908; detached for special service in B.B. Prot. in connection with inroads of Hottentots ex German S.W. Africa, under Simon Kooper; received thanks of the Cape govt. and Imp. Germ. govt. upon successful accomplishments of mission; inspecting mag., S.W. Africa Prot. (with rank of Major in Union Defence Forces), Sept., 1915; dep. secy., S.W. Afric. Protectorate, Mar., 1916; secy. for Protectorate, Dec., 1916; acmtg. offr. in addition, Apr., 1920; ag. admstr., S.W. Africa, 2nd to 17th Oct., 1920; sec. for S.W. Africa, civ. admstr., 1921; sec. for native affrs., Union of S. Africa, Apr., 1923.

HERCHENRODER, SIR FURCY ALFRED, KT. BACH. (1914). K.C.—B. 1866; ed. Roy. Coll., Mauritius; called to bar, Mid. Term, June, 1888; ag. crown pros., Mauritius, 1893-8; leg. adviser, pol. mag., crown pros., conservr. of mortgages, and curator of vacant estates, Seychelles, May, 1898; judge, Seychelles, 1900; ch. just., 1903; proc. and advoc. gen., Mauritius, 3rd Apr., 1906; ag. chief just., 3rd Sept., 1912; ch. just., 1st Jan., 1913.

HERDMAN, HON. ALEXANDER LAWRENCE.—B. 1869; ed. at Otago and Oamaru high schls.; barrister-at-law, 1894; entd. New Zealand parl., 1902; atty.-gen., min. of justice, 1912; atty.-gen. in National ministry, 1915; resig. 1918, and apptd. judge of N.Z. sup. ct.

HEREFORD, GEORGE A., MA., (Oxon.), barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn.—B. 1876; Cadet, S. Sttlmts., Nov., 1898; ag. dist. officer, Balik Pulau, Penang, Apr., 1899; passed final exam. in Malay, Oct., 1901; supt. of educn., Penang, Nov., 1902; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, Jan., 1904; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, Mar., 1906; asst. dist. offr., Batu Gajah, Jan., 1907; ag. mag., Kuala Lumpur, July, 1908; ag. asst. dist. offr., Larut, July, 1909; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kinta, Feb., 1910; ag. dep. pub. prosecutor, Kuala Lumpur, Apr., 1910; dist. offr., Raub, continued ag. dep. pub. prosecutor, May, 1910; offr., cls. III., Jan., 1912; passed final exam. for the bar, May, 1913; ag. registr., sup. ft., F.M.S., June, 1913; ag. censor, Penang, June, 1915; ag. senr. dist. offr., Prov. of Wellesley, June, 1915; ag. 2nd mag., Singapore, May, 1917; ag. dist. offr., Butterworth in addition, Nov., 1917; ag. pres., munio. comntr., Penang, May, 1919; offr. collr., land rev., Penang, May, 1919; offr., cls. II., Jan., 1919; legal adviser, Johore, offr., cls. I.B., Sept., 1920; ag. registr. of impta. and expts., Singapore, June, 1921; ag. gen. adviser, Johore, Feb., 1924.
HERMON-HODGE, H. B.—B. 1886; ed. Winchester coll. (scholar) and Magdalen coll., Oxford; B.A., hons. in mods. and hist.; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 26th Dec., 1908.

HEROLD, THOMAS BARRY.—Cape civ. ser., 1882-1901; ch. clk. to master of sup. ct., Transvaal, Oct., 1901; asst. master, June, 1902; comntr. of protocols, Dec., 1904; master of the sup. ct., Oct., 1906; mem. of prisons advisory bd., Feb., 1907; dir. (chmn., 1907-8 and 1908-9), land and agric. bank, Oct., 1907; chmn. and gen.-man., land and agric. bank of South Africa, 1912; J.P., Union of S. Africa, 1912; man. dir., land and agric. bank, Oct., 1924.

HERSHENSOHN, J. M. N. A.—lst cls. asst., govt. schls., Natal, 1899; clk., col. sec.'s dept., 1900; sworn translator, Dutch; senr. clk., 1906; civ. ser. examnr., 1902; Dutch bd. of examrs., 1903; one of the secretaries at S. Africa National Convention, 1908-1909; chief clk., admistr's dept., Natal Prov., 1910; ag. prov. sec., Aug.-Nov., 1913, and from Aug., 1917; prov. sec., 1st Feb., 1918; dep. admistr., Natal Prov., Jan.-Apr., 1925.

HERTZOG, HON. J. B. M., B.A., LL.D., M.L.A.—B. 1866; ed. at Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, and Amsterdam Univ.; judge of O.F.S., 1895 to 1900; comd. Boer forces of S.W. div., 1899 to 1902; one of the signatories of the treaty of Voreening; atty.-gen., O.R.C.; min. of just., Union of S. Africa, 1910-1912; leader of opposition in Union parl., 1913-24; prime min., 30th June, 1924.

HESLIP, MAJOR ISAAC.—B. 1876; 3rd batt., R. Irish Rifles; passed course of instrn., R.I.C., Dublin; Hythe certif.; asst. commr., pol., Sierra Leone, 8th Feb., 1911; commr., pol. and sheriff, 8th July, 1914; dist. commr., 22nd Sept., 1920.

HEWINS, WILLIAM ALBERT SAMUEL, M.P.—B. 1885; ed. at Wolverhampton Grammar schl., and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; M.A.; dir. of London schl. of economics, 1896-1903; teacher of modern economic history, Univ. of London, 1902-3; Tooke professor of economic science and statistics, King's Coll., 1897-1903; mem. of senate of Univ. of London, 1900-1903; late sec. of Tariff comm.; M.P. (U) for Hereford, 1912-1918; parly. under sec. of state for the colonies, 26th Sept., 1917, to Dec., 1918; author of numerous works and articles on economics and political economy.

HEWLETT, JOHN BERTRAM.—B.1890; asst. auditor, G. Coast, 25th Feb., 1914; ag. senr. asst. auditor, 28th Jan. to 17th Mar., 1919; ag. asst. col. sec., 19th Aug. to 14th Sept., 1919; jun. clk., Col. Audit Dept., 1st July, 1920; 2nd cls. clk., 1st Apr., 1921; audr., Gibraltar, 3rd Nov., 1922; audr., Trinidad, 18th Sept., 1926.

HEWLETT-COOPER, C. T.—Ent. "Britania," 1889; lieut., 1898; comdr., 1909; capt. (ret.), 1921; S. African Med., 1900; commanded H.M.S. Aquarius, Egan and Adriatic operations, 1914-17; commanded H.M.S. Andes, escort to convoys, 1918; commanded H.M.S. Marshal, 1919-20; joined col. serv. as dir. of marine, Dar-es-Salam, 2nd Oct., 1920.

HEYWOOD-WADDINGTON, ALFRED.—B. 1898; ed. Oriel Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1920; lieut., 3rd Somerset L.I., 25th July, 1918; cadet, F.M.S., 15th Mar., 1921; ag. asst. contr. of lab., 1st May, 1921; ditto, Seremban, Jan., 1923; passed cadet, Aug., 1923; asst. contr., lab., Seremban, Mar., 1924; asst. contr. lab., Klang, Aug., 1924; fedl. examiner in Tamil, Sept., 1924; asst. contr., lab., Penang, Jan., 1926.

HIAM, ROBERT WOOD, M.I.C.E., M.I.M.E., M.I.S.E.—B. 1874; ed. Claremont Grammar Schl.; pupil, G.W.Rly., 1890-1893; engrg. staff, G.W.Rly., 1893-1910; engrg. staff, F.M.S.R., 1910-19; engrg. for ways and wks., F.M.S.R., 1919.
HICKMAN, HON. ALBERT E.—Elec. M.H.A., Bay de Verde, Newfoundland, 1913; M.E.C., 1917; mem. tonnage comtee., 1917; min. without portfolio, 1918.

HICKSON-MAHONY, R.S.B.M.—Civ. ser., Cape Colony and Union of S. Africa, 3rd Jan., 1903 to 19th Mar., 1917; asst. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 20th Mar., 1917; war ser., Boer War, 1899-1900, Great War, 9th S. African Inf., 1915-1917.

HIGGINS, EVELYN SCOTT.—Ed. at Blundell's Schl., Tiverton, and Chatham House, Ramsgate; served on Uganda rly., 1898-1900; supt. of pol., Somaliland Prot., 22nd May, 1905; asst. supt. of pol., E. Africa Prot., Sept., 1911; supt., pol., Kenya, Oct., 1925.

HIGGINS, HON. HENRY BOURNES.—B. 1851; M.L.A., Victoria, 1894-1900; chmn. of royal comm. on legal procedure; mem. of Austn. Fed. Convention, 1897-98, and of the first Federal parlmt.; judge of High Court, C. of A., since Oct., 1906.

HIGGINS, MAJOR ROBERT HENRY CONSTABLE, M.B.E., M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1889; ed. St. Andrew's Coll. and R. Vety. Coll., Dublin; war serv., France, Mesopotamia and Archangel, 1914-19; ment. in desps.; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territry, Feb., 1923.
HIGHAM, REGINALD.—B. 1879; traffic can. wasser, Gold Coast rly., Feb., 1904; ag. senr. traffic ast., May to Oct., 1910; ch. traffic offr., Aug., 1911.

HIGHEIT, DAVID JOHN, M.I.C.E.—First ast. engr., Ulu Selangor extension, Oct., 1890; ch. ast. engrnr., Jan., 1894; ag. res. engrnr. for rlyw., Mar., 1894; dist. rlyw. engrnr., F.M.S.R., Jan., 1903; ag. divisional engrnr., Selangor and N. Sembilan, June, 1904; divisional engrnr., South, Apr., 1906; ag. gen. man., F.M.S.R., May, 1909.

HIGNETT, CHARLES FRANCIS.—Ed. Woking Coll., Surrey; served with Imp. Army, Apr., 1884 to Aug., 1889; 3rd clk., mag. office, Durban, 1st Aug., 1889; sub-inspr., Zululand pol., 27th Jan., 1891 to 31st Jan., 1896 (three years as adjutant); ast. mag., Umlalazi sub-dist. of Eshowe, 1st Feb., 1896; mag., Umlalazi dist., 30th Dec., 1897; Ngutu dist., 10th Aug., 1899; ag. mag., Eshowe dist., Apr. to Aug., 1903; Dundee dist., Sept., 1908 to Feb., 1909; mag., Alexandra div., 31st Aug., 1909; Umgani div., Pietermaritzburg, 6th June, 1911; Eshowe, Zululand, 6th May, 1912; Verulam, 1st Nov., 1915; senr. mag., Durban, 1st Oct., 1920; judge, native high ct., 1st Oct., 1922; war services, rebellion in Zululand, 1888-9; Anglo-Boer War, 1899-1902; Natal native rebellion, 1906.

HILL, ARTHUR HEYLIGER.—B. 1876; ed. Queen's Coll., Br. Guiana, and New Coll., Eastbourne; clk., treasy., Br. Guiana, 1893; 3rd grade commr. of taxation, 1902; 2nd grade, 1904; sec., excise bd. and supervising commr., treasy., im. rev. branch, 1906; passed exam. in Hindi, 1905; 1st cls. clk., immigr. dept., 1907; seconded as ast. emigr. agt., Calcutta, Mar., 1913; ag. emigr. agt. at Calcutta for Br. Guiana, Trinidad, Fiji and Jamaica, 14th Apr. to 7th Aug., 1914, and from 20th Nov., 1915 to 6th Nov., 1916.
HILL, FRANK MACLEY.—Graduated, Lond., 1891; gov't. vet. offr., Bechuana land Prot., 1919; J.P., 1920.

HILL, JAMES REGINALD.—B. 1877; ed. Queen's Coll., B. Guiana; 8th clk., adminstr. gen.'s off., B. Guiana, May, 1896; 5th cls. clk., police dept., Feb., 1897; sub-inspr. of police, Dec., 1900; dist. inspr. of police, Apr., 1903; ag. adjut. and musketry instr., July to Nov., 1906; ag. county inspr. of police, Nov., 1909 to Apr., 1910, and from June to Aug., 1911; ag. pay and qrtmr., from Sept., 1911; pay and qrtmr., 1st Jan., 1912.
HILLIER, CAPT. F. H. J.—Ast. dist. commr., Kenya, Jan., 1921.
HILLYER, HERBERT KEYS, O.B.E. (1919)—B. 1881; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civil service, and assigned to the C.O., July, 1900; short'd writer and ast. clk.

HIME, CHARLES FREDERICK WILLIAM.—B. 1869; ed. Hilton Coll., Natal; sec. to late ch. just. Sir Henry Connor, 1st Jan., 1888; clk., atty-gen.'s office and ag. registr., sup. ct., 1st Mar., 1889 to 30th June, 1904; sec. to min. of just., 1st July, 1904; mag. for colony of Natal, 1st July, 1908, holding post, ast. mag., Pietermaritzburg; ag. ch. mag., Pietermaritzburg, 1st Apr., 1910; mag., Bergvills, 1st Dec., 1915; addnl. mag., Durban, 1st Dec., 1918; mag., Dundee, 1st May, 1920; senr. mag., Pietermaritzburg, 1st Aug., 1921.

HINCKS, MAURICE THORP.—B. 1888; ed. Stonegate Sch., Leicester, and Blairlodge Sch., Polmont, N.B.; solr., sup. ct., 1908; commr. for oaths, 1913; on mily. serv., 1915 to 1920; junr. administrative offr., Gold Coast, Jan., 1920; pol. mag., Accra, Apr., 1920; ag. ch. regiser., Gold Coast, Oct. to Dec., 1921, July, 1923 to Mar., 1924; June to Nov., 1925 and Dec., 1926 to May, 1927; ag. solr.-gen., Jan. to May, 1922.
HINKSON, T. H.—Ast. dist. commnr., Kenya, Jan., 1921.

HISLOP, CAPT. FRANCIS DANIEL, M.A. (hons. hist.), Edin. Univ.—B. 1891; served Territorial Force, 1909-21; on active serv., 1914-19; served Gallipoli, Egypt, France; seconded to Machine Gun Corps, 1917; instr. in machine gunnery, 1918-19; cadet, S. Sttlmts., Oct., 1919; addl. treas., Singapore, Dec., 1919; ast. supt., govt. monopolies, Singapore, June, 1920; resig. on med. grounds, June, 1921; cadet, Kenya, Dec., 1923.

HISLOP, JAMES, M.V.O. (1920), O.B.E. (1919).—B. 1870; entd. New Zealand civ. serv., 1885; New Zealand govt. rlwys., 1890; ast. priv. sec. to min. for rlwys. and postmaster gen., 1900; priv. sec. to prime min., 1907; under sec. for internal affairs and clk. of writs, 1912.
HITCHENS, ALFRED EDEN.—B. 1890; ast. engr., water wks., Trinidad, 24th Dec., 1900; 1st grade dist. offr., P.W.D., Trinidad, 24th Sept., 1906; divnl. engr., 1st Apr., 1925.

HOBBES, HENRY JAMES, I.S.O. (1926).—B. 1876; temp. supervisor, cust., Gold Coast, 26th Sept., 1904; 2nd cls. ditto, 19th Sept., 1901; 1st cls. ditto, 12th Feb., 1905; inspr., preventive services, 1st Mar., 1907; ag. travelling commnr., 31st Dec., 1907; dist. commnr., 7th Nov., 1908; lieut., Gold Coast Regt., 19th Jan., 1917; dep. prov. commnr., 1st Jan., 1921; prov. commnr., 21st May, 1921.

HOBDAVY, WILFRED ERNEST.—B. 1889; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1912; attached to col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1913; Colombo Kachcheri, April, 1913; Trincomalee Kach., Nov., 1913; ag. off. ast. to the govt. agt., Ratnapura, May, 1914; on mil. duty, Sept., 1914; landing survr., Colombo customs May, 1919; 4th astl. col. sec., Jan., 1922; 3rd astl. col. sec., Feb., 1922; 2nd astl. col. sec., Feb., 1922; ast. govt. agt., Matale, Mar., 1925.

HOBSON, SAMUEL GEORGE.—B. 1872; ed. govt. sch., Victoria; ast. supt., post and tels., Selangor, July, 1902; ast. supt., posts and tels., Selangor, Negri Sembilan and Pahang, Jan., 1905; supt., posts and tels., Perak, Nov., 1912; contr., posts and tels., Perak, Oct., 1926.

HOBSON, WILLIAM EDWARD, M.B.E. (1919).—B. 1885; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. in lower (now 2nd) div. of the civ. serv., and assigned to the office of the commrs. of H.M.'s wrks. and pub. bldgs., 1st Jan., 1885; transf'd. to the C.O., April, 1891; ast. registr., 1899; promoted to higher grade, Jan., 1900; staff-ol., first grade, 1911; supt., printing dept., Dec., 1924.
HOCART, ARTHUR MAURICE.—Archaeological commnr., Ceylon, May, 1919.

HODGE, LEONARD PERCIVAL, M. Inst. C.E., F.S.I. by exam.—Ed. Queen's Coll., Taunton, and King's Coll. Lond.; honors construc., 1886; 2nd cl. supt., P.W., Jamaica, 1891; 1st cl. ditto, and attached to head off., 1892; dir. of roads, G. Coast, 1894, but did not take up apptnt.; col. engrnr. and surv.-gen., Dominica, 1898; draughtsman, P.W.D., B. Guiana, 20th Mar., 1901; ast. engrnr. and draughtsman, Apr., 1906; ag. D.P.W., May, 1924; ast. D.P.W., 90th June, 1924.
HODGE, S.O.V.—Ast. dist. commnr., E.A.P., Apr., 1914; ag. dist. commnr., Kenya, 1914.

HODGES, ARTHUR HARRIS.—B. 1894; entd. Impl. civ. ser., and apptd. to scntt.-gen.'s office, G.P.O., London, 1900; ch. acnt.'s office, G.P.O. O.R.C., 1903; Australia, 1908-9; junr. ast. treas., N. Nigeria, 1910; senr. ast. treas., Nigeria, 1920; dep. treas., Nigeria, 1924.

HODGINS, HON. FRANK EGERTON.—B. 1864; educ. Upper Canada Coll. and Trin. Univ.; B.C.L.; called to the bar, 1879; K.C., 1902; prominent legal practitioner in Toronto for several years; pres., Ontario bar assoc., 1908-9; legal agt. for Dominion govt. at Toronto, 1890-96; counsel for Ontario govt. on license investigation, 1907, and on other occasions; judge of high ct. of Ontario, 1912; also local judge in Admiralty, Exchequer Ct.; now a just. of appeal.
HODGSON, GERALD.—B. 1858; ed. Lancing Coll.; entd. crown agents' office, 13th Apr., 1878; head of correspondence dept., 26th Oct., 1892; supt., stock transfer off., 22nd Nov., 1905.
HODGSON, HARRY COURTENAY.—B. 1887; ed. Rockington Schl. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Oxon); ast. dist. commnr., Sierra Leone, 30th Nov., 1910; dist. commnr., 1st May, 1915.

HODKIN, CAPT. THOMAS WILLIAM, Fellow R. Econ. Soc., Fellow R. Statistical Soc., Fellow Inst. of Commerce, Assoc., Chartered Inst. of Secs., Assoc., Inst. of Bankers.—B. 1886; ed. Manchester Grammar Sch., Manchester Univ. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; dipl. in educn., Oxford; served in France with E. Lancs. Regt., 1914-18; astd. to gen. staff; educn. offr., 66th divn., 1918-19; instr., teclh. of educn. for offrs., Caterick, 1919; instr., schl. of instrn. for offrs., Bedford, 1919-20; instr., W.O. schl. of educn., Shorncliffe, 1920-22; brig. educn. offr., 1st Air Defence Brig., Aldershot, 1922; A.E.C., res. of offrs.; examr. for W.O. in bookkeeping and accountancy; commercial master, Zanzibar, 1922.

HODSON, ARNOLD WIENHOLT, C.M.G. (1922).—Ed. Italy, Mulgrave Castle, and Felsted; in Central Queensland, 1900-1902; joined Australian Commonwealth Horse for service in S. Africa, 1902; in Transvaal, 1902-4; sub.-inspr., Bech. Prot. pol., 1904; specially employed in connection with Damara war (med. and clasps); mem. of Ngamiland mission, 1906; ag. ast. commnr., Mar. to May, 1910, and Mar. to Dec., 1911; dist. commnr. and 1st cls. mag., Somaliland, 1912: H.B.M. consul. for Southern Abyssinia, 1914; ditto, S.W. Abyssinia, 1923; served part time in European War; present at coronation of Empress Zoditow, 1917; 3rd cls. Star of Ethiopia; African Gen. Ser. Med. and Clasp, Jubaland, 1917-18; attchd., Abyssinian army in expedition against Tigre, 1919 (med. and clasps); late capt., Royal Sussex Regt. (Territorials); has passed Cape Univ. mag. law exam.; Somali language, lower standard; Galla language, higher standard; gov., Falkland Is., 22nd Dec., 1926; joint author of "A Practical Galla Grammar."

HODSON, THOMAS ARTHUR.—B. 1882; ed. Cheltenham and Wadham Coll., Oxford (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1905; seconded asst. supt. of pol., Galle, Dec., 1906; asst. supt. of pol., Galle, July, 1908; ditto, Tangalla, May, 1909; ditto, Matara, Dec., 1909; office asst. to govt. agt., S. Prov., Jan., 1910; pol. mag., Kandy, Feb., 1911; asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Nov., 1911; ag. dist. judge, Batticaloa, Feb., 1914; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, May, 1914; asst. settlmt. offr. and spec. offr. under Waste Lands Ordon., June, 1916; on mil. duty, 1917; addnl. asst. col. sec., Apr., 1919; asst. govt. agt., Kalutara, May, 1919; addnl. asst. dir., educn. Mar., 1922; ag. asst. dir., educn., Apr., 1922; ag. govt. agt., N.W. Prov., May, 1923; asst. govt. agt., Matale, Mar., 1924; govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Mar., 1925; ditto, N.W. Prov., Oct., 1926.

HOFMEYR, G. M., B.A., Cape.—B. 1867; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; teacher for number of years; attorney, notary-public and conveyancer, Transvaal; reglar. and treas., Victoria Coll., 1908-1910; under-secr. for educn., Union of S. Africa, 1910; sec. for educn., Jan., 1930.
HOGGEN, SIDNEY JOHN, B.A.—B. 1898; ed. Highgate Coll. and Oriel Coll., Oxford; supt., educn. dept., N. Prov., Nigeria, 24th Aug., 1921.

HOGG, WILLIAM EDWARD, A.R.C.S., A.M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1880; ed. City of London Schol. and Imp. Coll. of Science; Whitworth Schol., 1906; eng. inspr., C.A., 1910; asst. engrnr., 1919; dep. head, engnr., insprn. dept., 1920.

HOHENKIRCH, LUDOVIC SMITH, M.B.E. (1926).—B. 1874; ed. in B. Guiana; ag. 6th cls. olk., govt. land dept., B. Guiana, 1890; ag. survr., 1896; 5th cls. olk., 1897; 3rd grade survr., 1897; 2nd cls. offr., lands and mines dept., 1904; ag. 1st cls. offr., 1901 and 1906; J.P. for the col., 1906; ag. warden, No. 3 mining dist., 1906, 1907 and 1909; 1st cls. offr., 21st Sept., 1906; ag. forestry offr., 1914-15; performed duties of prin. olk. in head offr., 1916; represented Col. at Imp. Forestry Conf., Canada, 1923; seconded for duty with B. Guiana exhibit, Br. Empire Exhibn., 1924; supt., forest surveys, forestry dept., 1926.

HOLDENBROOK, HUBERT GEORGE, B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O., Trinity Coll., Dublin, L.M., Rotunda Hosp.—B. 1887; med. offr., S. Stltmtn., 1912; med. offr., F.M.S., 1913; certif., London Schol. of Trop. Med., 1921; health offr., F.M.S., 1921; health offr., Perak North, Feb., 1922.

HOLDEN, GEORGE.—Asst. supt. of wks., S. Stltmtn., June, 1901; supt. of wks., Prov. Wellesley, Jan., 1908; ag. supt. of wks. and surveys, Singapore, July, 1908; ditto, Malacca, May, 1909; exec. engrnr., Malacca, Dec., 1909; ag. exec. engrnr., Singapore, Jan., 1910; ag. exec. engrnr., Prov. Wellesley, Oct., 1910; ag. exec. engrnr., Singapore, June, 1913; ag. exec. engrnr., Malacca, Mar., 1915; sg. exec. engrnr., Singapore, Oct., 1916; ag. dep. col. engrnr., Mar., 1920; exec. engrnr., Malacca, 29th Dec., 1920.
HOLDING, RICHARD HENRY.—B. 1898; on milly. serv., Apr. 1917 to Mar., 1920; temp. clk., O.S.O., Apr. 1920; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., O.S.O., 6th Oct., 1921.

HOLE, Lieut.-Commr. GEORGE FRANCIS, R.N.—B. 1887; cadet, H.M.S. "Britannia," 1902; midshipman, 1904; sub-lieut., 1907; lieut., 1909; commanded H.M.S. "Peterel," 1914-15; invalided and pensioned, 1916; asst. master attendant, Colombo, Ceylon, Mar., 1916; offr. in charge, mine-sweeping Colombo, 1917-19; lieut.-commr., 1917; priv. sec. and ag. A.D.C. to gov., Apr., 1919; ag. master attendant, Colombo and Calle on several occasions; master attendant, Colombo and Galle, July, 1922; harbmr., Hong Kong, 1924.
HOLLAND, J.—B. 1858; apptd. after exam., 3rd cl. messenger, C.O., 17th Aug., 1902; 2nd cl. ditto, 10th June, 1904; 1st cl. messenger, 30th Jan., 1912.
HOLLAND, J. S.—B. 1881; 3rd cl. messenger, C.O., 29th June, 1909; 2nd cl. messenger, 3rd July, 1915; on mil. serv. from 5th Aug., 1914 to 2nd Feb., 1919.

HOLLIDAY, ARTHUR ROBERT.—B. 1884; atty. and notary, sup. of S. Africa, 1906; asst. dist. commr., Gold Coast Colony, 1st Jan., 1915; seconded for service in Togoland; dist. polit. offr., Misahoe, 12th Oct., 1917; asst. admstr. gen., Zanzibar, 19th Jan., 1925.

HOLLINS, NOEL CLINTON.—B. 1886; ed. Rossall Schol. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; B.A. (Cantab), 2nd cls. hist., tripes; qualified in exam. for gen. consultive service; asst. col. sec., Sierra Leone, 31st Mar., 1910; ag. senr. asst. col. sec., 1911 and 1912; asst. dist. commr., 1st Jan., 1913; dist. commr., 1st Jan., 1915; ag. prov. commr., C. Prov., Nov., 1922.

HOLLIS, SIR ALFRED CLAUD, K.C.M.G. (1927), C.M.G. (1911), C.B.E. (1919).—B. 1874; asst. collr., E.A.P., 12th Mar., 1897; employed on transport duty during Uganda Mutiny, 1898; collr., 15th June, 1900; ag. Brit. vice-consul for German East Africa, Apr., 1900 to Feb., 1901; ag. sec. to admin. and priv. sec. to H.M. commr., Feb., 1901 to Apr., 1903; sec. to the admin., 1st Apr., 1903; Jubaland expedn., 1900-1, and Nandi expdn., 1906-6 (medal and two clasps); sec. for native affairs and M.L.C., 13th June, 1907; on special service to Uganda, Oct., Nov., 1909; ag. chief sec. to govt., Apr. to Dec., 1911, and Feb. to Oct., 1912; Swahili examiner, 1901 to 1912; col. sec., Sierra Leone, 26th Jan., 1913; ag. govt., Oct., 1913 to Feb., 1914, June to Oct., 1915, and Jan. to Mar., 1916; sec. to provisional admin., German East Africa (Tanganyika Territory), 4th Nov., 1916; ag. admstr., Feb. to Nov., 1919; chief sec., Tanganyika Territory, 1919; ag. gov., Feb. to Oct., 1922; Br. res., Zanzibar, Jan., 1924; author of works on the Massai, Nandi and other E.A. tribes.

HOLLOWAY, JOHN EDWARD.—B. 1896; B.A. (hons. hist.), 1910, Univ. C of G.H. (Victoria Coll.); res. mast., Grey Coll. Schol., Bloemfontein, 1911-13; mem., coun. of examrs., O.F.S., 1912-13; D.Sc. (Keon.) Lond. (Schol. of Economics), 1917; Hutchinson research medallist; lect., Grey Univ. Coll., 1917-19; Transvaal Univ. Coll., 1919-21; prof., economics, T.U.C., 1921-26; dir., census and statistics, Union of S. Africa, 1926.

HOLM, ALEXANDER, C.B.E. (1926), M.R.A.S.E. (Gold Medallist), F.H.A.S., S.R.A.C.(Dip.Hons.).—Gen. manager, govt. experimental farm, Petchefstroem, Transvaal, 1903; gen. manager and principal, schl. of agric., Potheofstroom, 1909; under sec. for agric. (eductn.), Union of S. Africa, Oct., 1912; dir. of agric., S. Africa Prot., 1918; mem., exec. leg. and inter-col. rly. couns.; pres., agric. and hortirol. society of Kenya; Br. Empire Exhibn. commr. for Kenya, 1924.

HOLMES, HAROLD Kennard.—Ed. All Saints, Bloxham; admitted solr., sup. ct., Hong Kong, 1900; served with K.R.R.C., Somme, 1916; wounded, Guillemont; with 20th London Regt. at Salonika, Palestine, France and Rhine, 1917-18, 2nd lieut., 1918; asst. land offr., Hong Kong, 1919; J.P., 1919; ag. land offr., 1921 to 1922; ag. crown solr., 1922; crown solr., 1922.

HOLMES, ROBERT BLAKE WORSLEY, O.B.E., M.I.M.E., F.R.G.S.—B. 1878; ed. Aberdeen Gram. Schl. and Aberdeen Univ.; asst. engrn., Lagos rly. (constr.), Nigeria, 1900; asst. loco. supt., Nigeria rly., wks. man., dist. loco. supt., dep. ch. mech. engrn., Nigerian Eastern Rly., 1905-15; formed mech. and shipyard formations, inland waterways and docks br., R.E., Richborough, 1916; major, 14th Sept., 1914; lieut.-col., 1st Mar., 1917; ment. in desps. and awarded O.B.E., 1918; col., 1st Mar., 1918; demob., Apr., 1919; recalled to colours, June, 1919, and proceeded to Damascus as asst. dir., rly. traffic, in ch. of Hejaz rly., Nov., 1919; in ch., evacuation of troops by rail from Syria, Dec., 1919; col., grade T., Mar., 1920; gen. man., Palestine mily. rlys., Mar., 1920; transf. to civ. govt. as gen. man., Palestine rlys., 1st Oct., 1920; col., R. of O., 1st Nov., 1921.

HOLMES, STEPHEN LEWIS, M.C. (1918).—B. 1896; King's scholar of Westminster, 1909; 2nd lieut., Royal Garrison Arty., July, 1915; served in France and Belgium, 1916-19; adjt.; ag. major; twice ment. in desps.; scholar of Christ Church, Oxford, 1919; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, asst. prin., C.O., 7th Feb., 1921; prte. sec. to perm. under sec. (Sir S. Wilson), Feb., 1926.
HOLMES, WILLIAM, B.A., LL.B. (Dublin).—B. 1901; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1923; attd. to Badulla kach., Jan., 1924; ditto, N. Eliya kach., Aug., 1924; ditto, secretariat, June, 1925.

HOLMES, WILLIAM FREDERICK, B.A. (Sydney), Assoc., R. Coll. of Science (London) in Geology, Assoc., R. Schl. of Mines (London) in Mining, Mem., Inst. of Mining and Metallurgy, F.G.S. (1900)—Demonstr., mine surveying, R. Schl. of Mines, 1901; mining engrn., Dutch E. Indies, S. Africa, Siam and Gold Coast; senr. inspr. mines, Gold Coast, 1914; sec. for mines, 1922.

HOLMWOOD, A. D.—Supt., roads and local wks., Transvaal provl. admn.; previous serv. since 1891; engrgn. depts. Tasmanian govt. rlys., Impl. mil. rlys., Transvaal, Central S. African rlys., and pub. wks. dept., Transvaal.

HONEY, DE SYMONS MONTAGU GEORGE, C.M.G. (1919).—B. 1872; served in B.S.A. Co.'s expedtn. to Mashonaland, 1891 to 1893; with B.C. Africa admin., 1893 to 1896; cust. dept., Transvaal, 9th Apr., 1901; native affairs dept., 1902; sec. for Swaziland affairs, and acting offr., 20th Oct., 1904; govt. sec., Swaziland admin., 22nd Mar., 1907; res. comntr., Swaziland, 1st Jan., 1917.
HOOD, FREDERICK JAMES, M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1893; ed. George Heriot's Schl. and R. Vety. Coll., Edinburgh; war serv., France, 1916-19; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1922.

HOODLES, D. W., B.Sc.—B. 1887; ed. Sheffield Univ. and King's Coll., London; B.Sc. hona., Lond., 1910; Drewgold medal, mathematica, 1910; associateship of King's coll.; mast., H.M.S. "Worcester," 1911; asst. mast., Queen Victoria schl., Fiji, 1912; head mast., law schl., 1915; J.P. for the colony, 1916; ag. head mast., Queen Victoria schl., 1918; ag. supt., schl., 1922; prov. mem. leg. coun., Nov., 1924.

HOOKER, RICHARD SYMONDS.—B. 1885; ed. Bedford Gram. Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; M.A. (Cantab.); qualified in exam. for gen. consular service; passed lower standard exam. in Mende; asst. dist. comntr. Sierra Leone, July, 1910; dist. comntr., Jan., 1915; ag. prov. comntr., N. Prov., Nov., 1920 to Aug., 1921 and from Sept., 1922; prov. comntr., 1st Jan., 1926.

HOOPS, ALBERT LAUNCELOT.—B. 1876; ed. at King William's Coll. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; B.A., 1900; M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O., 1901; M.D. (stud. cond.) 1903; D.P.H., 1903; L.M., Rotunda, 1903; certif. honours, London Schl. of Trop. Med., 1904; served in S. African War as medical offr., 28th M.I., 1901-2 (severely wounded, ment. in desps., medal and four clasps); house surgeon, Penang, 1904; surg. capt., Penang vol.; ag. col. surg. res., Penang, June, 1906; seconded to organise medical and prison depts. in Kedah, Mar., 1906; state surg. and supt. of prisons in Kedah to Nov., 1920; ag. adviser to govt. of Kedah, Sept., 1906, to Apr., 1907, and from July to Oct., 1907; supt. of Indian immigts., Kedah, in addition to other duties, Oct., 1910; prot. of lab., Kedah, in addition, Nov., 1917; prin. civil med. offr., S. Stlitma, 23rd Nov., 1920; pres. coun., coll. of med., Spore; pres., S.S. and F.M.S. med. council; mem., leg. coun., S. Stlitma, 1st Jan., 1923; chmn., tuberculosis comttee., June to Aug., 1923; pres., 6th Congress of Far Eastern Assoc. of Trop. Med., Singapore, Sept., 1923.

HOPE, JOHN OWEN WEBLEY, C.M.G. (1918).—3rd treasry. asst., E. Africa Prot., 17th May, 1899; asst. collr., 1st Apr., 1900; collr., 1st Apr., 1903; offr.-in-charge, Northern frontier dist., 1909; ag. prov. comntr., Kenya, 1915; political offr., German East Africa, 1915; senr. comntr., Kikuyu, Jan., 1921.
HOPKINS, J. G.—Asst. dist. comntr., E.A.P., Mar., 1917.

HOPKINSON, CAPT. EMILIUS, C.M.G. (1922), D.S.O. (1900), M.A., M.B., B.Ch. (Oxon.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), F.Z.S.—B. 1869; ed. at Haileybury, Trin. Coll., Oxford, and St. Thomas's hosp., Lond.; late ophthalmic house surg., St. Thomas's hosp.; clinical asst., Royal Ophthalmic hosp., Moorfields; house surg., Radcliffe infirmary, Oxford; served in S. African war, 1900-1901, as surg.-capt., 15th batt., I.Y. (ment. in desps., Queen's Med., 4 clasps, D.S.O.); late surg.-lieut., Oxford I.Y.; med. offr., W.A.F.F., Gambia, 1901-1902; prot. med. offr., Gambia, 1903; travelling comntr., South Bank prov., Gambia, 1912; travelling comntr., North Bank Prov., Gambia, 1921; author of "Vocabulary of the Mandingo Language."

HORN, ARTHUR EDWIN, C.M.G. (1922), M.D., B.Sc.(Lond.); M.R.C.S.(Eng.); L.R.C.P.(Lond.); D.T.M. and H. (Cantab.); certif., Lond. Schl. of Trop. Med. (with distinctions).—Ed. at St. Mary's Hosp. and London Univ.; civil surg., 19th brigade field hosp., S. African field force, 1900-1901; apptd. to W. African medical staff, 1904 and has served in G. Coast, N. and S. Nigeria and Gambia; awarded Craggs research prize for original research in trop. med. (London S.T.M.), 1908; recd. thanks of S. of S. for the Colonies for report on cerebro-spinal fever in Northern Territories of the G. Coast, 1908, and for report on sleeping sickness in the Volta River dist. of the G. Coast, 1910; seconded for special service at the C.O., 1910-12; personal asst. to the P.M.O., S. Nigeria, 1913; senr. med. offr. ad mem., exec. and leg. coun., Gambia, 1914-16; prin. civ. med. offr., German E. Africa, 1917-19; addl. Rockefeller comm., for investigation of yellow fever in W. Africa, 1920; dir., med. and san. services, Malaya, 1921-22; pres. elect for 5th Congress of Far Eastern Assoc. of Trop. Med., 1923; med. sec. to Col. Advisory Med. and San. Comm., C.O., 1923-24; junr. med. adviser to C.O., 1924; mem., exec. comm. of Br. Empire Leprosy Relief Assoc.; fellow R. Soc'y of Med.; R. Soc'y, Trop. Med.; author of "Health of Europeans in W. Africa," "Health of Europeans in E. Africa."

HORNBY, CAPT. HENRY EPTON, O.B.E., F.R.C.V.S., D.V.S.M. — B. 1890; ed. King's Coll. Schol. and R. Vety. Coll., London; lect. in bacteriology to S.E. Agric. Coll. (Univ. of London), 1912-13; vety. offr., N. Rhodesia, 1913-20; war serv., E. Africa, N. Rhodesia Rifles, 1914-17; S.A.V.C., 1917-19; ment. in desps.; studied physiology and pathology, Univ. Coll. Schol. of Trop. Med., London, Ecole de Medicine, Paris, and Vety. Research Lab., Pretoria; vety. pathologist, Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1922.

HORNBY-PORTER, CHARLES.—Apptd. dist. commnr., Lagos, 1897; 2nd asst. col. sec., 1901; senior asst. col. sec., 1903; ag. col. treas., 1902-1903; ag. col. sec., 1904; holds dormant commn. to sit as pol. mag., and sat as such in 1897, 1901, 1902 and 1903; on spec. serv. to Ijebu-Ode, 1901 and 1904, and to Oyo and also to Ife, 1904; pres. of pol. commn., 1901; mem. of rly. commn., 1901; pres. of cust. commn., 1904.

HORNE, EDWARD BUTLER.—Ed. at Bedford Gram. Schol.; asst. collr., E. Africa Prot., 21st Apr., 1904; dist. commnr., 1st Apr., 1909; offr. in-charge, Masai res., May, 1923; senr. commnr. (2nd class), Kenya, Jan., 1924.
HORNE, EDWIN NOEL.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 4th May, 1920.

HORNE, HENRY HASTINGS, O.B.E., Mily. (1918).—Ed. at Clifton Coll.; Brit. vice-consul, Mexico, 1900-01; S. Africa, field ntel. force, S. African medal; asst. collr., E. Africa Prot., 3rd Apr., 1903; dist. commnr., 1st Apr., 1907; slavery commnr., Lamu, 1908-9; polit. offr. to G.E.A. exped. force, 1915-18 (mentd. in despatches); lent to Empire Cotton Comm. for sp. rept., Tanganyika Territory, 1920-21; offr. in charge, Jubaland, 1922-24; ag. senr. commnr., Jubaland, 26th Feb., 1924.

HORSBURGH, HARRY, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1876; ed. Aske's Haberdasher's Company Schol.; C.A., Jan., 1908; asst. dir., min. of munitions, 1915; dep. contr., munitions disposal bd., 1919; dep. head, engnr. contracts, 1920; tech. asst., "Discovery Comm." 1923,
HORSLEY, BERNARD HILL.—B. 1890; European War, 1914-18; major, 8th (service) batt., K.O.Y.L.I.; D.S.O., M.C., desps.; dist. polit. offr., Somaliland, 1919; dist. commnr., Somaliland, 1922.

HORSLEY, RICHARD.—B. 1893; ed. Cowley Gram. Schol., St. Helens; gen. fitter, P.W.D., Gold Coast, 13th Aug., 1919; instr. of metalwk., Accra tech. schl., 10th Apr., 1920; ag. prin., 1st Dec., 1920 to 22nd Mar., 1921; house mast., 6th Sept., 1922; head mast., junr. trade schl., 28th Apr., 1926.

HOWARD, HON. SIR WM. HENRY, KT. BACHE.
