(1904).—B. 1862; ed. at Bishop Feild Coll., St. John's; called to the bar, 1886; bencher of the law society, Newfoundland, 1891; Q.C., 1895; elected to the legislature, 1894; acted as col. sec., 1894-5; delegate from the Newfoundland govt. to the Ottawa conf., Apr., 1896, on the subject of confedn. with Canada; mem. of ex. coun., 1894-97; ag. atty.-gen., 1897; elected to legislature to represent Harbour Grace, 1897; apptd. by ch. just., with minister of just. to act in conjunction with judges as committee to amend rules and procedure, sup. court, 1899; minister of just. and atty.-gen., 1900; ex-officio mem. of ex. coun. and of treasy. board; acted as leader of govt. during sess. of 1901; and also in 1902; apptd. ch. just., July, 1902; administered govt., 1902, 1904, 1909, 1912, 1913, 1914; deputy gov., 1907, 1910; chrmn. of royal comm. on Sealing Industry, 1913; pres., Newfild. centre of St. John Ambulance Assocn.; chrmn. of comm. for selection of non-combatants for service abroad; chrmn. of Newfild. fund for hospital costs for soldiers and sailors; representative on Peace Comm. established under treaty between Great Britain and U.S.A., May, 1916.
HOSE, CHARLES, D.S.C.—Cadet Sarawak service, Mar., 1884; res. 2nd cl., 4th div., Jan., 1891; res. 3rd Div., and mem. of sup. coun., May, 1904.

HOSE, EDWARD SHAW, C.M.G. (1924).—B. 1871; junr. offr., Perak, F.M.S., Jan., 1891; ag. financial asst., Kuala Kangsar, Oct., 1891; asst. dist. mag., ditto, Nov., 1893; ag. dist. mag., Batang Padang, Oct., 1898; asst. sec. to govt., Perak, Nov., 1898; ag. dist. offr., Larut and Krian, May, 1903; offr. of cls. III, S. Settlements, Mar., 1904; ag. dep. pub. pros.; ag. 2nd mag., Singapore, July, 1904; ag. commnr., ct. of requests, Singapore, Aug., 1904; chrmn., sanitary board, Kuala Lumpur, Oct., 1904; ag. commnr., trade and cust., F.M.S., Sept., 1913; ag. and -gen., 1912; ag. controller of labour, May, 1913; ag. dir. of agric., Oct., 1915; offr. of cls. II, F.M.S., Mar., 1916; dist. offr., Lower Perak; ag. dir. food production, F.M.S., 8th Feb., 1919; dir. food production, S. Stltmtns., 17th July, 1919, in addition; contr. of lab., F.M.S. and S. Stltmtns. (offr. cls. Ia.), but continues to act as dir. of food production, Sept., 1919; dep. food contr., Malacca, in addition, Jan., 1920; food contr., Mar., 1920; ag. Br. res., Selangor, Sept., 1920; ag. Br. res., Negri Sembilan, Nov., 1921; Br. res., Negri Sembilan, Feb., 1922; ag. ch. sec., F.M.S., May, 1923; resumed duty as Br. res., Negri Sembilan, Oct., 1923; ag. col. sec., S.S., Apr., 1924; col. sec., July, 1924.
HOSKING, E. B.—Asst. dist. commnr. E.A.P., Dec., 1913; ag. dist. commnr., Kenya, 1926.
HOSKING, HON. SIR JOHN HENRY, KT. BACHE. (1925).—B. 1854; barrister and solicitor; K.C., 1908; judge of sup. ct., New Zealand, 1914-25.

HOUGHTON, H. E.—B. 1892; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser. and assigned to the C.O., Apr., 1912; attached to office of high commr. for S. Africa, Nov., 1920; transfd. to Basutoland serv., Nov., 1923.

HOW, DOUGLAS WALSHAM.—B. 1889; ed. Cheltenham Coll., London Matric.; un. of S. Africa civ. serv. lower law exam.; Basutoland 2nd grade Sesuto exam.; clerical asst., Basutoland, 5th Oct., 1910; sub-inpr., Basutoland mtd. pol., 1st July, 1912; chief clk., master of act. and regiar., 1st July, 1917; served as 2nd lieut. with Royal Welsh Fusiliers in France, 1918; inspr. and staff offr., Basutoland mtd. pol., 1st July, 1919; dep. ast., commnr., 1923.
HOWARD, HON. ERATUS EDWIN.—B. 1868; ed. Iroquois High Schol., Inverness Acad., and McGill Univ.; B.A., 1895; gold medallist in maths. and nat. philos.; B.C.L. (gold medallist)

HOWARD, FREDERICK JAMES, O.B.E.—B. 1883; ed. at Bedford; 2nd divn. clk., W.O., June, 1901; 2nd cls. astt. acctnt., army accts. dept., May, 1905; selected for advancement to higher grade of scale, 1st Nov., 1911; ag. 1st cls. astt. acctnt., Oct., 1914; ag. acctnt., Nov., 1916; 1st cls. astt. acctnt., 16th Dec., 1919; personal supernumy. appt. as acctnt., 1st Jan., 1921; serv. with min. of munitions as dir. from 1915, and with disposal and liquidation commn. treas., as astt. contr. of deptl. finances until 1921; O.B.E., 1st Jan., 1918; ag. prin., old style, 1st Jan., 1920; ag. prin. C.O., 15th Sept., 1921.

HOWARD, JOHN CURTIS.—B. 1887; ed. Uppingham and Clare Coll., Camb. (exhib.); called to the bar, Inner Temple, Jan., 1913; 2nd lieut., 4th Lincoln Regt., Mar., 1915; served in France, Jan., 1917 to July, 1920; legal adviser, G.H.Q., France, May, 1919 to July, 1920; pres., dist. ct., Cyprus, Aug., 1920; passed exam. in mod. Greek, Aug., 1921; ag. king's advoc., Nov., 1922 to Mar., 1923 and Aug., 1923; king's advoc., July, 1924; title altered to atty.-gen., May, 1925; joint compilers, 2nd vol., statute laws of Cyprus, 1923; solr.-gen., Nigeria, 1926.

HOWARD, HON. JOSEPH, O.B.E. (1920).—B. 1862; pres. of chamber of commerce, 1920-21; pres., govt. emigr. comtee., 1917-20; head of Malta delegation to France in connection with the employment of Maltese labr. in France, 1919; officier de l'instruction publique, France, 1920; ei. senator in Malta parl., Oct., 1921; head of ministry, 28th Oct., 1921; has recd. Japanese Order, 3rd cls., of the Sacred Treasure and 3rd cls. of the Order of the Rising Sun and also 2nd cls. of Tunisian Order of the Nichan.

HOWARTH, SAMUEL EDWARD JAMES.—Ed. at Buxton Coll., London; served in S. African War, Queen's and King's medals with five clasps; astt. Colony manager, field force canteens, O.R.C., Feb., 1902, to Mar., 1903; astt. acctnt., P.W.D., E. Africa Prot., 6th May, 1905; ch. acct., P.W.D., 1916.
HOWATSON, CHARLES WILLIAM, M.B., C.M. (Edin.).—B. 1861; dist. med. offr., Trinidad, 15th May, 1905.

HOWELL, ALLEYN GRAHAM.—B. 1871; 5th clk., corrpdg. branch, C.S.O., Barbados, 1890; 4th clk., 1892; 3rd clk., Jan., 1898; ch. clk., commr. of agriculture, 1899; sec. to W. Indian agric. conferences, 1900, 1901, 1902, 1905, and 1907.

HOWELL, E. H.—B. 1879; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser. and assigned to the C.O., Mar., 1898; promoted, with gratuity, Dec., 1907; conf. clk., 1911; staff clk., 1st grade, 8th Sept., 1917; cler. offr. higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922; ag. deptl. astt., 16th Jan., 1923; clk. for legal instruments, 26th Feb., 1923.

HOWELL, FREDERICK ROUSE.—B. 1886; sup. clk., P.O., Barbados, Feb., 1907; jun. clk., P.O., Nov., 1907; clk. to master in chancery, Dec., 1907; 4th clk., C.S.O., May, 1909; 3rd clk., C.S.O., 1919; 3rd grade offr., clerk, exec. coun. and exec. comtee., 1925.

HOWELL, JOHN BRUCE.—B. 1867; 3rd clk., treas., Barbados, July, 1886; 3rd clk., col. sec.'s office, Sept., 1891; 2nd clk., C.S.O., and clk. of exec. coun. and comtee., Jan., 1898; ag. priv. sec. and A.D.C. to the gov. of Barbados in 1902, 1904, 1906, 1907, 1910 and 1911; J.P., 1918; ch. clk., col. secy.'s off., Aug., 1918; ag. col. sec. on various occasions 1922-25; has held various other ag. appts.; man., govt. savings bank, 1925.
HOWELL, WILFRED IRVING.—Cadet, botanical and experiment statn., St. Kitts, July, 1907; foreman, Mar., 1909; agr. instr., Nevis, Apr., 1912.

HOWES, ARTHUR BURNABY.—B. 1879; ed. at Dulwich Coll.; called to the bar, Middle Temple, June, 1906; Western circuit; conveyancer, Uganda Prot., 22nd May, 1916; custodian of enemy property, 20th Mar., 1917; dist. mag., dist. judge, and admstr.-gen., Entebbe, Uganda, 1st Apr., 1907; dist. mag., dist. judge, and offl. rec. in bankruptcy, Kampala, Uganda, 18th July, 1917; ag. judge of high ct., Jan.-June, 1920; ag. atty.-gen., Feb. to Dec., 1921; senr. mag., 1st Jan., 1922; ag. judge of high ct., Apr. to Oct., 1922 and June, 1923; puisne judge, Gold Coast, Dec., 1924.

HOWITT, CAPT. CHARLES ROBERTS.—B. 1894; ed. Carlisle and Queen's Coll., Oxford; R. Fusiliers, 1914-16; Border Regt., 1916-19; Malayan civ. serv., Nov., 1920; attd., land office, Johore, Dec., 1920; 2nd cls. mag., Sept., 1921; ag. 2nd cls. mag., Johore, May, 1922; 1st cls. mag., May, 1922; passed cadet, June, 1922; astt. collr. of land rev., Johore Bahru, Sept., 1922; offr. cls. V, June, 1922; collr., land rev., Johore Bahru, Oct., 1922; 2nd lieut., J.V.R., Aug., 1923; astt. adviser, Kota Tinggi, May, 1924; lieut., J.V.R., Aug., 1924; attd., pol. cts., Singapore, Oct., 1925; offr. 3rd mag., Singapore, Oct., 1925; offr., cls. IV, June, 1926.

HOY, SIR WILLIAM WILSON, K.C.B. (1922), KT. BACH. (1916); C.B. (1918).—B. 1868; entd. serv. of Cape Govt. rlys., 1889; representative of C.G.R. in Johannesburg, 1896; ag. astt. traffic manager, Bulawayo, 1897-8; Kimberley, 1898; astt. traffic manager, Port Elizabeth, 1899; traffic manager, Bloemfontein, Johannesburg, 1900-02; chief traffic manager, C.S.A.R., 1902; represented C.S.A.R. at rly. confee. at Washington; ag. gen. manager in 1905 and 1907; astt. gen. manager, 1909; gen. manager of rlys. and harbours, Union of S. Africa, 31st May, 1910.

HUBBALL, JOHN GORET.—Clt., control and audit office, Cape, 1895; examr., 1895; astt. acctnt., 1903; astt. acctnt., contrlr. and auditor-gen.'s office, Union of S. Africa, 1910; prin. clk., 1912; chief acctnt., treas., Union of S. Africa, 1916.

HUBER, JOSEPH, O.B.E. (1923).—B. 1865; entd. Malta civ. serv., 1892; in charge of govt. savings bank, 1892; acctnt., rec. gen. office, and in charge of savings bank, 1893; dep. rec. gen. and dir. of contracts, 1904; astt. treas., 1913; treas. and dir. of contracts, 16th Aug., 1916; also man. of savings bank since 1898; offl. mem. exec. coun. and of coun. of govt., 16th Aug., 1916.
HUDDEN, EDWARD LAMPHIER.—B. 1902; ed. Radley Coll.; lieut., 4th Battn. Gloucestershire Regt., T.A., 1925; cadet, Uganda Prot., 1925.

HUDSON, HAROLD.—Book-keeper, treas. dept., Uganda Prot., Mar., 1911; cashier, treas. dept., Aug., 1911; astt. treas., Mar., 1914; 1st grade astt. treas., Jan., 1922; ag. deputy treas., June to Dec., 1919, Dec., 1921 to Feb., 1922, Sept., 1922 to Apr., 1923, May, 1923 to Mar., 1924, Feb. to Oct., 1925 and from Mar. to Dec., 1926.

HUDSON, PHILIP JAMES.—B. 1897; on mily. serv., June, 1916; cadet, Ceylon, Mar., 1920; attd. to Colombo Kachcheri, Apr., 1920; pol. mag., Balapitoya, Mar., 1921; asst. settlement offr., 1922; office asst., Jaffna kach., Dec., 1924; asst. stintl. offr., May, 1925.

HUDSON, LIEUT.-COL. WILLIAM, O.B.E., M.C.—B. 1880; ed. privately and King's Coll., London; S. African War, 1900-02; Queen's Med., with 2 clasps; King's Med., 2 clasps; contr.'s staff cent. telegraph, London, 1902; dep., P.M.G., N. Nigeria, 1912; ag. P.M.G., Apr. to Aug., 1912; R.E. (signals), 1914-18; offr. in charge, civ. tels., occupied enemy territory admn., Palestine, 1919; P.M.G., Palestine, July, 1920.

HUGGARD, WALTER CLARENCE, K.C.A., LL.D.—B. 1884; ed. at Dublin Univ.; 1st honour man and senr. mod. in legal and polit. science; called to the bar, King's Inns, Dublin, 1907; station mag., Nigeria, Apr., 1914; ag. pnane judge, July to Sept., 1914; pol. mag., Jan., 1917; solr.-gen., Trinidad, Nov., 1920; ag. pnane judge, June-Oct., 1921; K.C., 1921; ag. atty-gen., Jan.-Oct., 1922; atty.-gen., 1st Nov., 1922; prepared revd.edn., Laws of Trinidad and Tobago, 1923; atty.-gen., Kenya, Feb., 1926.

HUGGINS, HORATIO CRICHTON, M. Soc. E.—B. 1880; ed North London Collegiate Sch. and Polytechnic Sch. of Engrng. (London); served three years articles under the Hon. W. Wrightson, M.I.C.E., C.M.G.; served in pub. wka. dept., Trinidad, 1st June, 1900 to 4th June, 1909; 1st grade asst. engr., P.W.D., Nigeria, 5th June, 1909; exec. engr., 2nd grade, 4th Oct., 1918; author of "Bridle Roads in the West Indies" (Trans. Soc. Engrs., 1907); "Reinforced Concrete in Nigeria" (Trans. Concrete Inst., 1913).

HUGGINS, JOHN, M.C. (1916)—B. 1891; ed. Bridlington Gram. Sch. and Leeds Univ., B.A.; 2nd lieut., Yorks Regt., Nov. 1914; Gallipoli, 1915; Egypt, 1916; France, July 1st, 1916; capt., Sept., 1916; ment. in desp.; cadet, F.M.S., 27th Mar., 1920; govt. rice agt., Klang, 28th May, 1920; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, 7th July, 1920; agt. to food contr., Selangor and Pahang, 7th Oct., 1920; passed exam. in law, Mar., 1921; 4th asst. sec. to govt., 9th Mar., 1921; passed final exam., Malay, June, 1921; dist. offr., Kajang, 24th Aug., 1921; offr., ola. IV., Aug., 1923; recvr., Alasgoff concession, Johore, Feb., 1925; ag. 2nd asst. sec. (A.), S.S., Feb., 1926; pvt. sec. to high commr., May, 1926.
HUGHES, D.B.B., M.B., C.M. (Edin.).—1st prizeman, senior surgery; medallist, anatomy and pathology; ag. surg. in charge, col. hosp., Grenada, 1900; dist. med. offr., St. Vincent, Dec., 1900.

HUGHES, JOHN WHITEHOUSE WARD.—B. 1883; ed. Oakham and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1905; M.A., 1919; cadet, F.M.S., 1906; ag. supt., inlaad rev., Kelantan, Dec., 1910; mag., Kota Bharu, Sept., 1911; ag. dist. offr., Temerloh, Oct., 1914; ag. asst. adviser, Kelantan, Jan., 1919; ag. collr., land rev., Penang, Oct., 1921; ag. dist. offr., Kuantan, Oct., 1922; ag. Br. adviser, Perlis, June, 1923; offr., clia II, Dee., 1923; 1st mag., Kuala Lumpur, Apr., 1926.

HUGHES, LESLIE DONALD CHARLETON, M.C.—B. 1890; B.A., Oxon; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1914; attached to Colombo Kachcheri, Dec., 1914; attached to chief censor's office, Apr., 1915; attached to Badulla Kachcheri, Oct., 1915; ag. office asst. to govt. agt., Prov. of Uva, March, 1916; on military duty, 1917-19; asst. dir. of food prodn., Apr., 1920; pol. mag., Jaffna, Mar., 1921; asst. govt. agt., Batticaloa, Nov., 1921; ag. dist. judge, Badulla, Mar., 1923; asst. stintl. offr., Nov., 1923.

HUGHES, BRIG.-GEN. ROBERT HERBERT WILFRED, C.B. (1921), C.S.I. (1918), C.M.G. (1917), D.S.O. (1916), Officer Legion of Honour (1917)—B. 1872; cadet, H.M.S. "Conway," 1885-87; merchant service, 1887-1898; Bd. of T. master mariner's certif.; sub-lieut., R.N.R., 1896; lieut., 1904; commodr., 1914; capt., 1919; served in S. African war, in S.A. Light Horse, Bethune's M.I., and Royal Navy, from 12th Dec. 1899 to 1st May, 1902; marine offr., S. Nigeria, 27th March, 1903; qualified in surveying at R.G.S. in 1906; elected a younger Brother of Trinity House, 1909; senr. marine offr., 1st Jan., 1909; ag. marine supt. and harbr.-mast., Lagos, 1st July to 10th Dec., 1906; commanded Nigerian govt. yacht "Eve" as armed auxiliary under the R.N. in the Cameroons Expd., Aug., 1914-Jan., 1916; Trinity House pilot in N. Sea, Apr., June, 1916; seconded to Army as Major, R.K., 19th June, 1916; lieut.-col., Sept., 1916; brig.-gen., May, 1917; dir. Inland Water Transport, Mespot, till Apr. 1921; demob., 13th Apr. 1921; dep. dir. of marine, Nigeria, Aug., 1921; dir. of marine, Oct., 1921.

HUGHES, RT. HON. WILLIAM MORRIS, P.C. (1916).—B. 1864; M.L.A., New South Wales, 1894-1901; elec. to 1st H. of R., C. of A., 1901, and member since; min. for external affairs, 1904; representative of O. of A. at Navigation Conf., London, 1907; atty.-gen., C. of A., Apr., 1910-1913, and from Sept., 1914, to Oct., 1915; prime min. and atty.-gen., 27th Oct., 1915; ditto, 14th Nov., 1916; ditto, 17th Feb., 1917; ditto, 10th Jan., 1918; resigned, January, 1923.

HULL, HON. H. C.—B. 1860; entd. Cape civ. ser., 1879; left the civ. ser. and practised at the bar for many years; served in S. African war; treas., Transvaal; min. of finance, Unioa of S. Africa, 1910-1912.
HUMPHRESON, LESLIE HERRERT.—B. 1883; ed. King Edward's Sch., Birmingham and Worcester Coll., Oxford; asst. dist. commr., Sierra Leone, 7th Jan., 1926.

HUMPHREYS, JOHN LISSETTER, C.B.E. (1936).—B. 1881; ed. Bromsgrove Sch. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford (senr. class. scholar, 1900); 1st class mods., 1902; B.A., 1904; cadet, S. Distltn., Oct., 1905; dep. collr. of land rev., and regir. of deeds, Singapore, May, 1906; ag. dist. offr., Aler Gajah, Malacca, Nov., 1906; passed final exam. in Malay, passed cadet, July, 1907; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, Dec., 1909; ag. dist. offr., Dinding, Mar., 1910; seconded for serv. in Johore as collr. and mag., Batu Pahat, Feb., 1911; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, Nov., 1912; ag. dep. regir., supr. ct., Singapore, Mar., 1913; also asst. supt. of Indian immgrts.; seconded for serv. in Johore as asst. advcr., Muar, Feb., 1915; ag. Brit. agt., Trengganu, Nov., 1915; British Adviser, Trengganu, Nov., 1919.

HUMPHRYS, H. L.—Ed. at gram. schl., Antigua; senior Camb. certif., 3rd div. honours; cashier, treas., Antigua, Jan., 1892; 2nd indoor offr., 1896; ag. 1st indoor offr. on several occasions; ag. ch. audit clk., Leeward Is., Dec., 1897, to Jan., 1898; ag. audit clk., St. Kitts-Newf. Dee., 1903, to Mar., 1904; 1st indoor offr. of treas., Antigua, Apr., 1904; ag. ch. audit clk. and federal acct., Leeward Is., May to Oct.,

HUNTER, REGINALD JOHN.—Cik., audit office, Transvaal, 18th May, 1901; prin. cik., 1st June, 1901; ag. asst. auditor-gen., Apr. to Aug., 1903, and Apr. to Oct., 1909; ch. cik., control and audit office, Union of S. Africa, 1st Jan., 1912; dep. asst. auditor, 1st Apr., 1912; asst. cont. and audr.-gen., 16th Aug., 1916; ag. contr. and audr.-gen., 18th Feb. to 9th Aug., 1921 and 27th May to 24th Sept., 1925.
HUNTER, THOMAS GILBERT, R.S.M.—B.A., 1873; inspr. of mines, Ceylon, June, 1900; ag. prin. min. survr. in addn., Mar., 1921 to Jan., 1922.

HUTCHINGS, HUGH H.—Inspr. of govt. schls., Turks and Caicos Is., 1889-1906; J.P., 1901; ch. offr., fire brigade, 1901-19; cik. to comsrr. and inspr. of schls., 1907; asst. comsrr., Grand Turk, 1908-19; ag. andr., 1908-13; audr., 1913-19; ag. comsrr. and ag. judge of sup., ct., five occasions, 1911-19; ag. comsrr. of Cayman Is., 1912-13; comsrr., Cayman Is., June, 1919.

HUTCHINSON, GEORGE STUART MENTETH.—B. 1883; ed. United Services Coll., Westward Ho! and Royal Mily. Coll., Sandhurst; 2nd lieut., unattd. list, 1902; Indian Army, 1903; capt., 1911; major, 1917; Burma Mily. Pol., Southern Shan States, 1910-14; Indian Expedy. Force, France, 1915; ment. in desps., 1915; invalided, 1917; examining offr., Miny. of Pensions, 1917; regional wounds offr. for Scotland, 1919; apptd. under reconstruction scheme, asst. prin., C.O., 1st Apr., 1924.

HUTCHINSON, SIR JOSEPH TURNER, KT. BACH (1896).—B. 1860; M.A., Christ's Coll., Camb.; barrister, Mid. Tem., 1879; Queen's advoc., G. Coast, Apr., 1888; ch. just., Jan., 1889; ditto, Windward Is., 1894; ditto, Cyprus, 1898; ditto, Ceylon, 1906; retired, 1911.

HUTCHISON, DONALD MAXWELL WRIGHT, B.Sc. (Eng.), M.I.C.E., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1882; ed. Wellington Coll. and Faraday House, London; asst. elec. engnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., 1911; ch. inspr., electy. enact., F.M.S., 1915; dep. ch. engnr., electy. bd., P.W.D., Jan., 1925; ag. ch. elec. engnr. and inspr., electy. bd., P.W.D., F.M.S., June, 1926.

HUTSON, SIR ETRE, K.C.M.G. (1922), C.M.G. (1911), B.A.—B. 1864; 2nd cik. to gov. Leeward Is., Oct., 1886; asst. priv. sec. to gov. Barbados, Nov., 1885; priv. sec. to gov. Barbados, Mar., 1887; ditto, at Mauritius, Jan., 1890; at Br. Guiana, July, 1893; Jamaica, 1898; apptd. col. sec. and regiar.-gen., Bermuda, July, 1901; chrmn., schools comsrr., 1902; chrmn., St. George's harbour comsrr., 1903; col. sec. Fiji, July, 1908; chrmn., eduon. comsrr., Apr., 1909; represented Fiji and Western Pacific high comsrr., at wireless tel. conf., Melbourne, Dec., 1909; dep. gov., May and Oct., 1909, Sept., 1910, Mar., Sept., Oct., 1911, and June, 1912; comsrr. of currency, Dec., 1914; ag. gov. of Fiji and high comsrr. for Western Pacific, 13th Aug., 1915, to 25th May, 1916, and from 19th June to 9th Oct., 1918; gov., B. Honduras, 26th Nov., 1918; gov., Fiji and high comsrr., W. Pacifico, 1926.

HUTTON, MAJ.-GEN. SIR EDWARD THOMAS HENRY, K.C.M.G. (1901); C.B. (1894).—B. 1848; served in the Zulu and Boer wars, 1879-81; Egyptian campaign, 1882; Soudan expdt., 1884-5; commdg. mounted infantry, and S. African War, 1900-1; raised and commanded M.I. at Aldershott, 1888-92; gen. offr. commdg. forces in N.S. Wales, 1893-6; pres. of mil. conference in Aust., 1896; gen. commdg. Canadian militia, 1898-1900; first gen. offr. commdg. mil. forces of C. of A., 1901-1905.

HUTTON, FREDERICK AUGUSTUS.—B. 1862; ed. Bedford Public Sch., S. African Coll. and Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; B.A., 1883; LL.B., 1892; called to the bar, supreme ct., Cape Colony, 1892; prosecutor, special treason ct., 1901-3; judge, supreme ct., assigned to E.D. local divn., 1913; mem., special treason ct., 1915-16; mem. of rebellion enquiry comrn., 1916-17; judge, Griqualand West Local Divn., Feb., 1923.

HYAMSON, ALBERT MONTEFIORE, F.R.Hist.S.—B. 1875; ed. Oxford High Schl. and Swansea Gram. Schl.; savings bank dept., G.P.O., London, 1895-98; supplementary estab., secy's dept., G.P.O., 1898 to Jan., 1921; dept. of infrm., F.O., Dec., 1917 to Mar., 1918; in charge of sec. miny. of infrm., Apr., 1918 to Nov., 1918; Br. war mission, Nov., 1918 to Mar., 1919; asst. dir., immigrn. and travel, Palestine, Jan., 1921; contrlr. of lab., Dec., 1921; in charge of dept. of immigrn. and travel, Dec., 1923 to Mar., 1924; contrlr., permits, Apr., 1924; sec., cent. town planning comrn., Sept., 1922; mem., economy comttee, chmn., comttee. on rates of wages for govt. employees; author, "A History of the Jews in England," "Palestine—the Rebirth of an Ancient People" etc.

IDINGTON, HON. JOHN, K.C., LL.B. (Univ. of Toronto).—B. 1840; ed. Coll. Inst., Galt, Ontario, and Univ. of Toronto; called to the bar, 1864; Q.C., 1876; judge, exchequer div., high ct. of just for Ontario, 1904; justice, sup. ct. of Canada, 1905.

IEVERS, C.L., L.R.C.P. (Edin.), L.R.C.S. (Edin.), L.R.F.P.S. (Glas.), D.T.M. (Lpool.).—Appntd. to W.A.M.S., Gold Coast, Feb., 1912; Togoland, 5th to 26th Aug., 1914; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1918; senr. med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Jan., 1921.
IMBERT, A. E.—Labour inspection offr., E. Africa Prot., June, 1919; ch. regisr., natives, Kenya, Jan., 1925.

IM THURN, SIR EVERARD F., K.C.M.G. (1905), K.B.E. (1918), C.B. (1900), C.M.G. (1892), M.A.—B. 1852; ed. at Marlborough and Exeter Coll., Oxon; special mag., Pomeroon Dist., Br. Guiana, 1882 to 1890; govt. agt., N.W. dista., 1890; ag. 1st cl. clk., C.O., Dec., 1899; col. sec., Ceylon, 1901; admstr. govt., Ceylon, 1902; gov., Fiji, and high comrn., W. Pacific, 21st June, 1904; retired, 1910; represented Fiji and Western Pacific at the Coronation of H.M. King George V., 1911; editor of the Guiana scientific journal "Timheiri," 1882-7; author of "Among the Indians of Guiana," 1883, and papers on the anthropology, natural history, geography, and history of Guiana; in 1884 made first ascent of Mount Roraima in the interior of Br. Guiana.

INGHAM, RAYWOOD, B.A. (Oxon).—Entered S. Sttlmts. civ. serv., cls. V. (passed in Chinese), 14th Dec., 1912; attached to monopolies dept., 19th Jan., 1913; deputy collr. of land rev., Malacca, 8th Sept., 1915; pol. mag., Malacca (in addition), 10th Nov., 1916; collr. of war tax, Malacca (in addition), 26th Mar., 1917; collr. of war tax, Singapore (temp.), Mar., 1918; ag. supt. of prisons, Malacca (in addition) Mar.-June, 1919; superm. offr., cls. IV., S. Sttlmts., Dec., 1919; asst. prof. of Chinese, Singapore, Jan., 1921.

INGLEDOW, Cedric Francis.—B. 1894; on mily. serv., Mar., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Aug., 1920; attd. to Kurunegala Kachcheri, Sept., 1920; ag. office asst. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Oct., 1921; ag. pol. mag., Chilaw, Feb., 1922; office asst. to govt. agt., Prof. of Sabaragamuwa, Mar., 1924; ditto to govt. agt., N. Prov., 1925; pol. mag., Matale, Feb., 1926.

INGLES, F. H.—B. 1878; ed. at U.S. Coll., Westward Ho., Newton Coll., Devon, and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; B.A. Cantab., 1902; asst. dist. commr., S. Nigeria, 6th Oct., 1908; dist. commr., 2nd grade, 17th Feb., 1913; dist. offr., 1st grade, 20th Oct., 1919; res., 5th Feb., 1922; ag. sen. res., 31st Oct., 1924.

INGRAMS, WILLIAM HAROLD.—B. 1897; ed. Shrewsbury; served K.S.L.I., 10th Sept., 1914 to 6th May, 1919; wounded, Belgium, 29th May, 1916; employed, directorate of recruiting, W.O., 14th Dec., 1916; Ministry of National Service, 18th Dec., 1917; Ministry of Labour, demob. and resettlement dept., 11th Nov., 1918; elected F.Z.S., 21st May, 1919; asst. dist. commr., Zanzibar, 4th July, 1919; F.R.A.I., 1921; F.R.G.S., 1922; M.R.A.S., 1923; mem., Societe d'Anthropologie de Paris, 1923; certif. acad. of internat. law, Hague, 1924; higher standard, Swahili, 1923; ag. dist. commr., 17th Aug., to 17th Sept., 1923; ag. regisr., high ct., off. assignee and regisr., patents, designs and trade marks, 26th Oct., 1923 to 17th Feb., 1924; 2nd asst. sec., 1st Jan., 1925; prvt. sec. to ag. high commr., 1st Apr. to 26th June, 1925; ditto to Br. rev., 20th Nov., 1925 to 11th Aug., 1926 and from 1st Oct., 1926; ag. prvt. sec. to H.H. the Sultan, 20th Nov., 1925 to 22nd June, 1926; clk., prot. coun., 1st Feb. to 26th June, 1926; edfr., Off. Gaz., 30th Dec., 1924 to 26th June, 1925; ag. senr. asst. sec., 10th Mar. to 9th Aug., 1926 and from 1st Oct., 1926; ag. asst. ch. sec., 10th Aug. to 30th Sept., 1926; clk., exec. and leg. couns., 10th Aug. to 11th Oct., 1926; mem. Pemba educn. comttee. and teaching of Koran comrn., 1924; mem. museum comttee., 1924 and ag. chmn., 10th Aug., 1926; part author, "Zanzibar; an account of its People, Industries and History" (1924), "Schl. History of Zanzibar" (1925), and "Aya Za Kuraani" (1926); author "Dialects of Zanzibar Sultanate" (1924), "Chronology and Genealogies of Zanzibar Rulers" (1926) and various pamphlets and articles.
INMAN, HOWARD.—B. 1887; B. of T. Certif.; inspir. of weights and measures, G. Coast, 13th June, 1912.

INNES, CECIL BENNETT ROSE.—Clk., chief acctnt., rlys., 1890; census, 1891; Britsh Bechuanaland serv.; Gordonia, Apr., 1891; relieving clk., Vryburg, Mar., 1892; clk. to P.M.G., Brit. Bechuanaland, May, 1892; sec.'s office, Apr., 1893; clk., regisr. of deeds, Brit. Bechuanaland, Mar., 1894; deeds office, Vryburg, Sept., 1897; ag. regisr., 20th July, 1900; clk. in charge, 1st Dec., 1901; ag. asst. magt., Laingsburg, June, 1903; sub-comrn., war losses compensation comrn., June, 1903; clk. in charge, deeds office, Kimberley, Nov., 1903; rev. clk. civil comrn., Cape May, 1907; ch. clk., Sept., 1908; recvr. of rev., R. London, Dec., 1910; prtn. clk., recvr. of rev., Cape Town, Nov., 1916; ch. clk., recvr. of rev., Dec., 1923.

INNES, Rt. Hon., Sir James Ross, P.C. (1915), K.C.M.G. (1901), K.C.—B. 1855; called to the bar of sup. ct., Cape Colony, 12th Feb., 1878; mem. for Victoria East house of assem., Cape, 1884; subsequently reprtd. Cape div.; atty-gen., 1st Rhodes' ministry, 17th July, 1890, to May, 1893; selected by Imp. govt. to watch proceedings in connection with trial of reform comttee. prisoners at Pretoria, 1896; atty.-gen. 4th Sprigg ministry, 1900-2; deleg. for Cape Colony to attend conf. in Lond. on final ct. of appeal, 1901; ch. just., sup. ct., Transvaal, 29th Mar., 1902; ordinary judge appeal, sup. ct., Union of S. Africa, 1910; ch. ist. of S. Africa, Oct., 1914; ret., 1st Mar., 1927.

INNES, ROBERT THORNBURN AYTON.—F.R.S.E., R.A.S., F.R. Met. A., D.Sc. (hons.), Univ. eiden, Holland; sec., Royal Observatory, Cape own, 1896; dir., Transvaal meteorological dept., 1903; astronomer, Union of S. Africa, 1912.

INNESS, WILLIAM JAMES DEACON, M.R.C.S. ing.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Ireland).—B. 1877; ed. Exeter Schi. and St. Mary's Hosp.; ond.; house physician, Bethlem Royal Hosp.; ed. offr., N. Nigeria, 1905; senr. sany. offr., Igeria, 1914; ast. dir. of med. serv., Nigeria, 1921; dir., med. and sany. services, Sierra Leone, 1924.
INNISS, KOLNYL ULRIC ALEXANDER, M.B., h. B. Edin.—B. 1882; entd. med. serv., rinidad, 16th Aug., 1904; dist. med. offr., 1st ug., 1912.

INSKIPP, PERCY SIDNEY, O.B.E. (Mily.).—lk., adminstr.'s off., S. Rhodesia, 1891; ag. sec., 1892; sec. to adminstr., 1893; under sec., 1897; joined London staff, B.S.A. Co., 1900; dir., S.A. Co., Apr., 1919; gen. man., B.S.A. Co., Africa, June, 1922; mem. of pioneer corps (occupation of Mashonaland), 1890; also served in the Mashonaland horse, Matabele rebellion, 1896 (medal); joined R.A.S.C., 1914; C., R.A.S.C., Harwich dist., 1914-17; S.S.C., 3rd divn., France, Flanders, Italy, 1917-19; Croce i Guerra, Italy; ment. in despatches.

IRBY, G. N.—B. 1889; ed. Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford; 2nd lieut., Oxford and Bucks. L.I., May, 1915; invalided with rank of capt., Jan., 1918; ast. priv. sec. (apptmts.) to Lord Milner, July, 1920; to Mr. Churchill, Feb., 1921; the Duke of Devonshire, Oct., 1922; Mr. H. Thomas, Jan., 1924; Mr. L. C. M. S. mercy, Nov., 1924.
IRELAND, FREDERICK JOSEPH.—B. 1903; ppdt., after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 30th sept., 1920.

IRVINE, FREDERICK ROBERT, B.Sc. Ag.—B. 1898; ed. Royal Grammar Sch., Newcastle-on-Tyne; war serv., Gordon Highlanders, France and Belgium, 1916-19; 2nd lieut., R.A.F., 1918; Sc., agr., Armstrong Coll., Durham Univ., 1920; teacher of agr. and biology, Achimota, Gold Coast, 1924.

IRVINE, ROBERT, M.C. (1917), CROIX DE GUERRE (1918).—B. 1894; ed. Dunfermline; capt., ens., Fusiliers, 1918; cadet, F.M.S., 15th Oct., 1919; 3rd ast. sec., Fed. sect., May, 1920; 4th ast. sec. to govt., F.M.S., July, 1920; passed final exams., Malay and law, Feb., 1920; priv. sec. ch. sec., F.M.S., Mar., 1921; ast. dist. offr., ampin., Apr., 1922; offr., cl. V., Nov., 1922; 2nd lieut., M.V.I., Mar., 1923; ag. 2nd mag., Kuala Lumpur, May, 1923; dist. offr., Jelebu, Nov., 1924; ast. dist. offr., Lower Perak, Jan., 1925; offr., cl. IV., July, 1925.

IRVINE, Hon. Sir Wm. Hill, K.C.M.G. (1914), L.D., K.C.—B. 1886; called to the bar, Victoria, 1884; premier of Victoria, 1902-4; mem. of R. of A., since 1906; atty.-gen., C. of A., June, 913 to Sept., 1914; chief just., Victoria, 5th Apr., 918; lieut.-gov., Victoria, 11th May, 1918.

IRVING, FRANCOIS ERNEST.—B. 1885; ed. at st. Andrew's Coll. and Marist Bros. Sch., S. Africa; served in Boer War, 1901-2; German W. Africa, with Imp. Light Horse, 1914-15; German E. Africa, 1916; seconded to adminstr. of Isafa Is., as finan. offr., Nov., 1916, to May, 1919; astt., cust. dept., Zanzibar, Oct., 1919; ag. astt. ch. of cust. for various periods, 1920-22; astt. ch. of cust., 17th Dec., 1922; ag. ch. of cust., 31st Mar. 1924 to 18th Mar., 1926.

IRVING, HENRY EDWARD.—Ed. at Charterhouse and Handelslehranstalt, Leipzig; trooper, 9th Lancers, 1899-1902; served in S. African campaign; ch. clk. to central repatriation bd., O.R.C., Mar., 1902; clk. to col. sec., Oct., 1902; clk. to adminstr., O.F.S. Prov., 10th Aug., 1910; ch. clk. to prov. sec. (2nd grade), 16th Oct., 1924.
IRVING, HUGH CRAUFORD.—Ast. dist. comanr., Somaliland Prot., June, 1914.

IRWIN, 1ST BARON (cr. 1925), HON. EDWARD FREDERICK LINDLEY WOOD.—B. 1881; ed. Eton and Christ Church and All Souls, Oxford (M.A. and fellow); major, Yorkshire Dragoons; M.P., Ripon divn., Yorks, 1910-26; under sec. of state for the cols., Apr., 1921; min. of educn., Oct., 1922; viceroy of India, 1926.

IRWIN, HENRY MARK, B.A.—B. 1885; ed. Haileybury and Selwyn Coll.; Cambridge exhibitioner; Haileybury exhibitioner; hons. class. tripos, 1908; astt. res., N. Nigeria, 1908; attached to secretariat, July to Dec., 1911; priv. sec. to ag. gov., C. L. Temple, Esq., C.M.G., June, 1912.
IRWIN, ROBERT EDWARD.—B. 1889; ed. at Christ's Hosp.; col. audit dept., Oct., 1925; ast. audtr., Tanganyika Territory, Nov., 1925.

ISAACS, Rt. Hon. ISAAC ALFRED, P.C. (1921).—B.1866; called to the bar, Victoria, 1880; Q.C., 1899; M.L.A., Victoria, 1892-1901; solr.-gen., 1893; atty.-gen., 1894-9; and again in 1900; mem. of Aust. Fed. Convention, 1897-8; and of first Federal parlmt.; atty.-gen. in Deakin ministry, 1906-6; judge of High Ct. of Australia, Oct., 1906; ag. ch. just., 1924.

ISHERWOOD, ALBERT ARTHUR MAGNALL, O.B.E. (1928), M.A. (Oxon).—B. 1889; ed. St. John's schl., Leatherhead, and University Coll., Oxford; jnr. supt., educn. dept., N. Nigeria, 9th July, 1913; loaned to polit. dept., N. Nigeria, 1915-17; polit. offr., Tanganyika adminstr., 8th Oct., 1917; ag. senr. comanr., 27th Feb., 1923 to 20th Jan., 1924; dep. dir., educn., 18th Sept., 1924; ag. dir., educn., 18th Oct., 1924 to 18th Mar., 1925; Swahili examr.

ISLINGTON, 1ST BARON (cr. 1910), Rt. Hon. Sir JOHN POYNIER DICKSON-POYNIER, 6TH BART., P.C. (1911); G.C.M.G. (1913); K.C.M.G. (1911); D.S.O. (1900); J.P.—B. 1866; ed. at Harrow and Christ Church, Oxford; major and hon. col., Wilts Yeomanry; served in S. Africa, 1900 (Queen's medal, 3 clasps); late lieut., 3rd Royal Scots; mem. L.C.C., 1898-1904; M.P., Chippenham, Wilts, 1892-1910; gov., New Zealand, 1910-1912; chmn. of Indian oiv. ser. oomen., 1912; partly, under sec. of state for the colonies, 1914; partly, under sec. for India, 1915.

IVATT, ALWYN ERNEST.—B. 1886; ed. King's Schl., Ely; Gt. Northern Rly., 1902-1909; traffic inspr., Nigerian Rly., June, 1909; astt. traffic supt., Jan., 1911; dist. traffic supt., Aug., 1919; traffic supt., Jan., 1920; ag. div. supt., Eastern divn., Nov., 1920 to Nov., 1921; astt. traffic man., Apr., 1922; ag. traffic man., Apr.-July, 1922.
IVES, WILLIAM CARLOS, B.C.L.—B. 1873; ed. pub. schl., Pincher Creek, Alta., Coaticook, Queb., and McGill Univ.; puisne judge, sup. ct., Alta., 1914.
IZARD, H.—Ast. dist. comanr., E. Africa Prot., Feb., 1915.

IZAT, NORMAN, M.A., Edin.—B. 1888; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1912; attached to Colombo Kachcheri, Jan., 1913; col. sec.'s office, Apr., 1913; ag. pol. mag., Anuradhapura, June, 1913; ag. office ast., ditto, Nov., 1913; comnr. of requests and pol. mag., Pt. Pedro and Chavakachcheri; special comnr. in connection with the riots, June, 1915; addl. ast. govt. agt., Colombo and Negombo, in addition to his own duties, Sept., 1915; pol. mag., Negombo, Apr., 1916; ditto, Gampola, July, 1917; ditto, Kandy, Mar., 1918; ag. ast. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Aug., 1919; ag. sec., Colombo port comnr., Aug., 1921; dist. judge, N. Eliya, Oct., 1921; ast. govt. agt., Kurunegala, Feb., 1924; ditto, Kandy, Mar., 1925.
JACK, MAJOR F. C., D.S.O., M.C.—Ast. dist. comnr., Kenya, 1920.

JACKLIN, S., M.C.—Accnt., repat. dept., Christiana, Aug., 1902; rev. clk., law dept., Nov., 1902; clk., civ. comnr. off., Pretoria, Nov., 1903; clk., rev. dept., Mar., 1906; prin. clk., Oct., 1906; prin. clk., inland rev. dept., Apr., 1912; inspr., inland rev. dept., Nov., 1916; survr., inland rev. dept., Apr., 1918; ag. dep. commr., Johannesburg, Sept., 1919; ch. clk., estabn., treas., Union of S. Africa, 1st Jan., 1920; sec., pub. ser. comnr., Oct., 1921; ret., 18th Oct., 1926; treas., League of Nations, 18th Oct., 1926.

JACKMAN, EDWARD CLARENCE.—Ed. Harrison Coll., Barbados; ent. Middle Temple, 1889; lecture prizeman, real property, 1890; lecture prizeman, equity, 1891; called to bar, 1892; practised at Barbados bar; elec. to H. of A., Barbados, 1904-1913; mem., exec. comtesse, 1909-1913; pol. mag., Bridgetown, Barbados, 1913; ast. judge, ct. of appeal, Barbados, 1921.

JACKMAN, HENRY THOS.—Crystal Palace engng. schl., 1890-3; pupil and ast. on water and drainage wks. to Messrs. McLandsborough and Preston, civ. engrs., 1893-6; in charge of new sewerage wks., Ripon, 1896-7; ast. dist. engr. on electric tramways, drainage, highway and improvement wks., etc., at Sheffield, 1897-1903; exec. engr. on water and drainage wks., Hong Kong, 1903-1920; ag. sam. survr., 1904-1905; seconded for spec. serv. in China, 1908; exec. engr., 1st grade, 1912; ag. 2nd ast., D.P.W., 1920; ast. D.P.W., Kowloon and New Territories, 1921; ag. D.P.W., mem., exec. and leg. couns., vice-pres., any bd., 192.

JACKS, PHILIP, Barrister-at-Law.—B. 1877; ed. Plymouth Coll.; admtd. solr., sup. ct., England, 1900; ast. land offr., Hong Kong, 1905; J.E., 1906; comnr. for oaths, 1906; ag. land offr., 1907 to 1908; ag. land offr. and offl. recvr. in bankruptcy, 1908; ag. land offr., 1911; and from 1913-1914; land offr., 1916; ag. crown solr., 1919 and 1920; called to the bar (Inner Temple), 1922.

JACKSON, BEDVER BLAINE LOWTHER.—Ed. Eton and Univ. Coll., Oxford; clk. to atty.-gen., O.R.C., July, 1901; clk. to legis. coun., June, 1902; 1st clk. to atty.-gen., July, 1904; joint editor of law-book, O.R.C., 1906.

JACKSON, EDWARD ST. JOHN, O.R.B.K. (1918).—Ed. Stonyhurst and Beaumont Colleges and Brasenose College, Oxford; B.A., 1908; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1910; legal adviser, Gambia, 26th Oct., 1912; atty.-gen., Nyassaland, Oct., 1918; judge of high ct., July, 1920; pres. of lands commnr., 1920; pres., finance comnr., 1923; atty.-gen., Tanganyika Territory, 20th Aug., 1924.

JACKSON, MAJOR FRANCIS WALTER FITTON, D.S.O. (1917).—B. 1881; ed. Dover Coll.; entd. R.A., 1899; served in S. Africa, 1901-2; seconded to W.A.F.F., 30th July, 1904; dist. commr., Northern Territories, G. Coast, 1st Jan., 1907; ast. col. sec., G. Coast, 10th Mar., 1911; provincial commr., G. Coast, 23rd Jan., 1915; services lent to W.O., Mar., 1915, to Apr., 1919; served in France, May, 1915, to June, 1918; ment. in despe. 3 times; O.C. British form. and senr. polit. offr., Togoland, July, 1919; represented govt., in final handing over of Lom and that portion of Togoland ceded to France by the Franco-British declrn. of 10th July, 1919.

JACKSON, SIR FREDERICK JOHN.—K.C.M.G. (1913); C.B. (1899); C.M.G. (1902); ed. at Shrewsbury Schol. and Jesus Coll., Camb.; 1st cls. ast., Uganda Prot., July, 1894; vice-consul, May, 1895; dep. comnr., Apr., 1901; dep. comnr., E. Africa Prot., Apr., 1902; lieut.-gov., 1907; East and Cent. African medal with dep., Uganda, 1897-98, and Luba and African gen. ser. medal, Uganda, 1900; mem. of exec. and leg. couns., E. Africa Prot., 1907; gov., Uganda Prot., 21st Mar., 1911; assumed govt., 3rd Apr., 1911; retired.
JACKSON, GILDART.—B. 1901; ed. Edgeborough Schol., Guildford and Westminster; ast. dist. comnr., Sierra Leone, 12th Nov., 1924.

JACKSON, ROBERT BEST, M.B., D.P.H., T.C.D., L.M. Rotunda Hospital, Dublin, certif. London Schl. Trop. Med., math. sizar, schl. and junr. modr., Trinity Coll., Dublin, senr. astr. and catechetical prizeman.—Chief med. offr., Indian Mining Asan, Jharia and Katras Coalfields, 1908-14; R.A.M.C., 1914-17; med. offr., Gen. Mily. Hosp., Edmonton, 1918; med. offr., F.M.S., 20th Nov., 1920; ag. health offr., Negri Sembilan, 1921.
JACKSON, T.—Curator, botanic station, Antigua, Leeward Is., 1906.

JACKSON, WILFRED EDWARD FRANCIS, C.M.G.—B. 1883; ed. Stonyhurst Coll. and Lincoln Coll. Oxford (classical scholar), B.A. 1905; personal sec. to gov., Trinidad, 1906; priv. sec. to gov. and clk. to exec. coun., Bahamas, Sept., 1906, to Oct., 1907; ast. collr., Uganda, Nov., 1907; ag. dist. comnr., Toro, July, 1911, to Apr., 1912; dist. comnr., Apr., 1912; ag. ast. chief sec., July, Dec., 1912; 1st ast. sec., Dec., 1912; ag. ast. chief sec., Aug., 1913, to Feb., 1914, May to Nov., 1914, and from Apr., 1915; col. sec., Bermuda, 19th Feb., 1916; col. sec., Barbados, 1921; col. sec., Trinidad, 13th Feb., 1926.

JACQUES, FREDERIC VIEI., M.B., Ch.B., Bristol.—B.1891; ed. Bristol Univ., Bristol Royal Infirmary and Lond. Hosp.; sub. lieut., R.N.V.R. and surgeon sub. lieut., R.N.V.R., 1909-17; med. offr., Seremban, 1921; ag. health offr., 1922; ag. health offr., Perak North, Apr., 1924.
JAGGER, HON. J. W.—M.L.A for Cape Town (cent.), min. for rlys. and hrbrs., Union of S. Africa, 1921-24.

JAGO, EDWARD.—B. 1896; ed. Churchers Coll. and London Univ.; 2nd lieut., Hampshire Regt., Aug., 1915; lieut., July, 1917; served in Salonica, Egypt, Sudan, Nov., 1916-Oct., 1919; cadet, F.M.S., Apr., 1921; secretariat, Negri Sembilan, May, 1921; dept. of agr., S.S. and F.M.S., Dec., 1921; 2nd lieut., M.V.I., Nov., 1922; ast. dist. office, Lower Perak, Dec., 1923; offr., cls. V, Apr., 1924; ast. dist. offr., Krian, May, 1924; ast. dist. offr., Klang, Apr., 1926.

JAMAICA, LORD BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. G. C. DE CARTERET, M.A., D.D.—Curate of St. Dunstan's, Canterbury, 1889; Holy Trinity. Tulce Hill, London, 1894; St. Mary, Cheltenham, 1896; vicar of St. Paul, Southwark, 1897; Christ Church, East Greenwich, 1901; rural dean of Greenwich, 1913; ast. bishop of Jamaica, 1913; bishop of Jamaica, 1916.

JAMES, ERNEST TREVOR.—B. 1894; M.A., Trinity Coll., Dublin; on active serv., 1915-18; ment. in desp., 1917; cadet, F.M.S., May, 1921; ag. ast. dist. offr., Klang, Jan., 1922; 2nd lieut., M.V.I., May, 1923; ag. ast. dist. offr., Remban, May, 1923; passed cadet, July, 1923.

JAMES, SIR FREDERICK SWTON, K.B.E. (1922), C.M.G. (1902).—B. 8th Apr., 1870; ed. at charterhouse and abroad; ast. dist. comsnn., Niger Coast Prot., 1896; in charge at Opobo and Akwete, 1896-7; with peaceful mission to Bende in 1897; trav. comsnn., 1897; serv. as polit. offr. in expedit. agst. Ekuris, Cross River, 1898; settled disputes in Qua country after cent. div. expedn., 1899; recd. thanks of S. of S. diventl. comsnn., 1901; intell. and polit. offr. with Aro field force, 1901-2 (deeps. medal); recd. thanks of dir.-gen. of intell. and S. of S. in connection with map of Aro operations; ag. sec. and dep. high comsnn., 1905; with Kwale patrol, Nov., 1905 (clasp); prov. comsnn. and mem. of exec. and legis. couns. of S. Nigeria on amalgamation, 1906; ag. col. sec. from 29th Mar., 1907; dep. gov., Apr. and Sept., 1907; ag. col. sec. and deputy gov. on various occasions from 1908 to 1912; ag. gov., Feb. to Sept., 1912; apptd. adminstr. on amalgamation of N. and S. Nigeria, 1st Jan., 1914; col. sec., Straits Settlements, 8th June, 1916; seconded for special services as food controller, S. Stiltns. and Malay States, 25th Dec., 1918; offr. administering the gov't. of S. Stiltns. and high comsnn. for the Malay States, 25th Aug., 1919; seconded for ap. serv. as food contrlr., S. Stiltns. and F.M.S., Dec., 1918; col. sec. in addition, May, 1919; offr. administering gov't., Aug., 1919; col. sec., Feb., 1920. ag. ch. sec., F.M.S., June-Oct., 1920; col. sec.; 14th May, 1921; offr. admtrg. gov't., 28th Apr., 1922; gov't., Windward Is., 23rd Feb., 1924; assumed gov't., 26th July, 1924.

JAMES, GODFREY WARDEN.—B. 1888; ed. at St. Paul's schl., London (Junior and Senior Foundation Scholar) and Oriel Coll., Oxford (Cornwallis Exhibn., 1908), hon. in class. mods., 1909, and lit. hum., 1911; B.A. 1920; M.A., 1924; called to the bar, Middle Temple, Apr., 1923; ast. dist. comsnn., Sierra Leone, Jan., 1914; has acted as dist. comsnn., Port Lokko sub-dist., Bomtali sub-dist., and Sherbro dist., and as ast. col. sec., for various periods from 1914-19; dist. comsnn., May, 1920; dist. comsnn., headqrs. dist., Mar., 1922.
JAMES, HERBERT BASIL.—Ed. Jesus Coll., Oxford (exhibitioner), 2nd class math. mods., 1908; 3rd class final honour schl. nat. science, 1910; ast. res., Northern Nigeria, 30th Nov., 1910.

JAMES, JOSEPH EDWARD.—B. 1867; ed. at pub. schls. and Mieo Coll., Antigua; ag. educational offr., Antigua, Nov., 1901; olk., educn. dept. and educn. offr., Antigua, Apr., 1904; schoolmaster, St. John's Training Schl., Antigua, Feb., 1913, to June, 1917; sup. instr. of schls., Antigua, 1st Apr., 1914; ag. sub-instr. of schls., St. Kitts-Nevia, 1918.
JAMES, MARSHALL NICOLO.—B. 1875; served under the condominium gov't., New Hebrides until Mar., 1922; gov't. agt., New Hebrides, Mar., 1922.

JAMES, SIR WALTER HARTWELL, KT. BACH. (1907).—Ed. in Perth at state and high schls., admitted as barrister and solicitor, 1888; member for East Perth, 1894; represented W. Aust. at fed. convcn., 1897-8; hon. minister, 1901; K.C., 1902; premier and atty.-gen., 1902; agt.-gen. for W.A., 1904-1906.

JAMISON, ROBERT, M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (Ireland), F.R.C.S. (Eng.), D.T.M. (Lond.).—B. 1880; ast. med. offr., Hlatikulu, Swaziland, 1910-13; med. offr., Swaziland, 1913; served, S.W. Africa campaign, 1914-15; prin. med. offr., 1918.

JANSEN, HON. ERNEST GEORGE, B.A., LL.B., M.L.A.—B. 1881; ed. Ladysmith Govt. Schl. and Durban High Schl.; leader of Nationalist party in Natal; mem., Natal Univ. Coll. Coun.; chmn., Saamker Unie van Natalse Vereeniginge (the first body in S.A. to prescribe exams. in Afrikaans) for 7 years; rep. Vryheid in parl. since 1921; foundation mem. of Suid Afrikaanse Akademie vir Taal, lettere en kuns; elec. speaker, Union H. of A., 25th July, 1924.

JANSEN, FRANCOIS JOHANNES.—B. 1869; ed. Graaff Reinelt Coll.; mag.'s clk., Fraserburg, Feb., 1889; Beaconfield, Jan., 1890; examr. of accts., C.O., Oct., 1892; civ. comsnn.'s clk. and ast. mag., Barkly West, Dec., 1894; Aliwal North, May, 1896; Dordrecht, July, 1896; ch. clk. and paymr., Cape pol., Kimberley, Nov., 1897; ast. plague adminstr., Apr., 1901; ast. guardians fund, Mar., 1902; sub-comsnn., war losses compensation comsnn., Aug., 1902; regitr., sup. ct., Eastern dista., Apr., 1904; civ. comsnn. and res. mag., Murrayburg, Oct., 1907; Victoria West, Dec., 1910; ch. clk., dept. of just., Sept., 1917; civ. comsnn., and mag., Uitenhage, Feb., 1923; East London, Feb., 1924.

JANSZ, HERBERT ERIC.—B. 1890; cadet, local division, Ceylon civ. ser., Sept., 1914; attached to Kandy Kashcheri, Sept., 1914; attached to Batticaloa Kash., Dec., 1914; addtl. pol. mag., Batticaloa, in addition to his own duties, Aug., 1915; ag. office ast. to gov't. agt., Batticaloa, Sept., 1915; office ast. to ast. gov't. agt., Kegalla, Jan., 1917; office ast. to ast. gov't. agt., N. Prov., Jan. 1918; pol. mag., Kalutara, Mar., 1920; dist. judge, Ratnapura, Jan., 1925.

JARDINE, DOUGLAS JAMES, O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1888; ed. at Westminster and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; 2nd cls. classical trip., 1910; B.A., 1910; M.A., 1914; chief sec.'s off., Cyprus, 1910; ast. sec. to gov't., 1912; ag. ch. ast. sec. on several occasions, 1912-16; passed in modern Greek, 1912; sec. to the adminstr., Somaliland, 1916; accompanied mission to Abyssinia on occasion of coronation of the Empress Zauditu (3rd cls. Star of Ethiopia), 1917; in charge H.Q. services, Somaliland Expdy. Force, 1920 (A.G.S. med. and ment. in desp.); senr. ast. sec., Nigerian secretariat, 1921; ag. dep. ch. sec., 18th July to 11th Nov., 1925 and 11th June to 12th Aug., 1926; joint-editor "Handbook of Cyprus," 1913 and 1919.

JARDINE, GORDON MURRAY.—Ed. Marlborough Coll. and Clare Coll., Camb.; ast. dist. comsnn., Uganda, 1912; coy. commdr., 4th Batt. K.A.R., 1917-18; called to the bar (Inner Temple), 1919; dist. mag., Tanganyika Territory, 1919; ret. without taking up appt., 1920; dist. mag., Entebbe, Ugan-la, 1921.

JARRETT, NORMAN ROWSTONE.—B. 1889; ed. Highgate and Exeter Coll., Oxford, 3rd cls. class. mods., 1910, 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1912; B.A., 1912; cadet, F.M.S., 1912; passed final exam. in Tamil, Apr., 1914; ag. ast. contrlr. of lab., Apr., 1914; passed exam. in law, July, 1914; ag. ast. dist. offr., Klang, Apr., 1915; ag. dist. offr., Klang, in addn., Nov., 1917; ag. dist. offr., K. Selangor, Aug., 1919; ast. dist. offr., Klang, Sept., 1920; dist. offr., Kuala Langat, Mar., 1921; ag. dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, Apr., 1925.

JARVIS, Lieut.-Col. Arthurb Leonard Fitz-Gerald, I.S.O. (1906).—B. 1852; entd. Canadian govt. service, 1868; priv. sec. to P.M.G., 1882 to 1885; and to min. of agric., 1885 to 1892; sec. of dept. of agric., 1896; commanded Gov.-General's Foot Guards, 1899 to 1904; ast. deputy min. of agric., 1909.

JARVIS, Edward Blackwell, C.M.G. (1920); Croix Commander of the Order of Leopold II.—B. 1873; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s office, Leeward Is., 1890; clk. of gov.'s office, 1891; ag. 1st clk., 1891; ag. 1st clk. col. sec.'s office, Mar. to June, 1896. On reorganisation of office, apptd. 2nd clk.; ag. ch. clk., and clk. to fed., exec., and legis. couns. and local legis. coun. of Antigua, Apr., 1897, to Jan., 1898, and July to Dec., 1899; P.S. to ag. gov., June to Oct., 1899; ch. clk. col. sec.'s off., and clk. to fed., exec., and legis. couns. and local legis. coun., Antigua, 3rd July, 1901; dir., pub. offr.'s guarantee fund 6th Dec., 1902; ast. col. sec., Leeward Is., Feb., 1903; ag. col. sec., Leeward Is. and Island sec., Antigua, 24th Mar. to 15th Oct., 1905; ag. fed. treasur., Aug. to Oct., 1905; trade and income tax assessor, 27th Dec., 1905; ag. col. sec., Leeward Islda. and Island sec., Antigua, on several occasions in 1907, 1908, 1909, 1911 and 1912; J.P., Antigua, 1908; chmn., St. John's City commsnr., 1st Jan., 1910 to 10th Feb., 1911; pres. of Antigua defence res. corps, 30th Sept., 1911, to 12th May, 1912; deleg. to agric. conf., Trinidad, 1912; ast. chief sec. and registrar-gen. of births, deaths and marriages, Uganda, 15th July, 1912; ag. chief sec., Uganda, 24th July to 29th Dec., 1912, 5th to 22nd June, 1913, 8th July, 1913, to 23rd February, 1914, 23rd May to 19th Nov., 1914, and from 18th Apr. 1916, to 12th Sept., 1918; official censor, 18th Aug., 1914, to 31st Mar., 1915; pres., war council, Uganda, 30th Apr., 1917; ment. in desp.; deputy to the gov. on various occasions from 20th Jan., 1914; chief sec., Uganda, 13th Sept., 1918; ag. Governor, June, 1921 to March, 1922, Oct., 1922, to Feb., 1923 and from Jan. to May, 1925.

JAYEWARDENE, Don Adrian St. Valentine, K.C. Barrister-at-law, Inner Temple.—B. 1877; dist. judge, Colombo, Ceylon, July, 1922; ag. puisne justice, sup. ct., Mar. to Nov., 1923 and from June, 1924; author, "The Roman Dutch Law in Ceylon" "The Law of Registration" and "The Law of Partition."
JEBB, R. R. H., O.B.E. (1918).—B.A. (Oxon); B. 1882; ast. auditor, S. Leone, Jan., 1907; ast. auditor, E.A.P., Nov., 1909; ast. dist. commsr., Somaliland Prot., 1916.

JEFF, Capt. John.—B. 1896; cadet, F.M.S., attd. Chinese prot., Ipoh, Apr., 1920; ag. ast. prot., Chinese, Selangor, Jan., 1923; ast. prot., Chinese, Seremban, Dec., 1923; ast. cust. dept., Jan., 1925; ag. ast. dir., educn., (Chinese), Oct., 1925.

JEFFERY, George, O.B.E. (1924), F.S.A.—Scholarship, R. Coll. of Art and Science, 1872; scholarship in architecture, R. Academy of London (1874); F.R.I.B.A., 1892; architect to Rt. Rev. Bishop in Jerusalem and the East, 1892; hon. corres. mem. of Imperial Institute of Arch-aeology of Russia, 1897; local sec. for Cyprus Society of Antiquaries; inspr. of pub. wks., Cyprus, 1898; curator of ancient monuments, Cyprus, 1903; author of "Summary of Architectural Monuments of Cyprus," and of several papers on the medieval archaeology of Cyprus and Jerusalem.

JEFFRIES, Charles J.—B. 1896; ed. Malvern Coll.; class. demy., Magdalena Coll., Oxford, 1914; Wiltsire Regt., 1915-17; tempy. 2nd clk., C.O., 27th Dec., 1917; apptd., under reconstruc-tion scheme, 2nd cls. clk., June, 1919; sec., E. Africa land settm. selection board, 1919; sec., tropical diseases research fund advisory comtce.; ast. sec., E. Africa currency board; prin., 1st Apr., 1920.

JEFFRIES, Charles William.—B. 1882; Computer, Royal Observatory, Greenwich, 1897; junr. ast., Royal Observatory, Cape of Good Hope, 1902; 1st ast., Royal Observatory, Hong Kong, 1907; chief ast., ditto, 1912; ag. dir. in 1910, 1920, 1924 and 1925.

JEKYLL, Lieut.-Col. Sir Herbert, K.C.M.G. (1901), R.E., C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1846; lieut. R.E., Apr., 1866; employed under War Office and Trinity House in 1868 to 1870, and destruction of sunken ships, from submarine mining, and under G.P.O. in the telegraph dept., from 1870 to 1873, and 1874 to 1876; served in the campaign of 1873-74 on the G. Coast; priv. sec. to the Earl of Carnarvon, Jan., 1877, to Feb., 1878; sec. to the col. defencce comtce., 1878; sec. to the roy. comm. on the defence of Br. possessions and commerce abroad, 1879-82; specially employed to visit and report on the defences of Singapore and Colombo, 1883-4; sec. col. defence comtce., 1885; priv. sec. to Lord Carnarvon, viceroy of Ireland, June, 1885, to Jan., 1886; sec. to roy. comm. for Melbourne centennial exhibn., 1888; priv. sec. to lord-lieut. of Ireland, 1892-5; sec. to the roy. comm. for the Paris exhibition, 1900.

JELF, Arthur Selborne, C.M.G. (1927).—B. 1876; ed. Marlborough and Exeter Coll., Oxford (exhr.); 2nd cls. class. mods., 1879; 3rd cls. lit. hum., 1899; B.A., 1899; M.A., 1913; cadet, F.M.S. civ. serv., 1899; ag. ast. sec. to res., Selangor, Jan., 1901; passed final exam. in Malay, May, 1901; ag. dist. offr., Kuala Langat, Selangor, Dec., 1903; ast. sec. to res., Selangor, Jan., 1905; ag. sec. to res., Negri Sembilan, March, 1905; ag. dist. offr., Pekan, Pahang, March, 1907; ag. ast. sec. to res., Perak, Oct., 1908; ast. general adviser to Johore govt., Jan., 1911; offr. of cls. IV., 1st Jan., 1912; chmn., san. bd., Kinta, Perak, Apr., 1914 to Apr., 1917; 2nd lient., M.S.V.R., 16th Sep., 1914; lient., M.S.V.R., 20th Jan., 1915; seconded for mil. serv. (lient., general list), Apr., 1917; offr. of cls. III., 12th Dec., 1917; tempy. capt., general staff, S. Stiltns command; attchd. general staff, War Office (milit. intell.), Jan., 1918; H.M. Petroleum Executive (sec. to Lord Harcourt's comtce.), June, 1918; offr., cls. II., 1st June, 1919; ag. dist. offr., Larut, Perak, Nov., 1919; ag. under sec. to govt., F.M.S., June, 1920; dir., Malayan polit. intelligence bureau, cls. 1b, 8th Dec., 1921; mem. Br. Malaya opium comtce., Dec., 1923; ag. contr. lab., S.S. and F.M.S., Mar., 1924; pres., estates health comm., Apr., 1924 (reed. thanks of S. of S.); col sec., Jamaica, Oct., 1925; ag. gov., 6th Oct., 1926.

JELLICOE, 1st Viscount, of Scapa (creat. 1918); Sir John Rushworth Jellicoe, O.M. (1916), G.C.B. (1915), G.C.V.O. (1916).—B. 1859; entd. navy, 1872; served in Egyptian war, 1882; China expeditionary force, 1900; European war, 1914-16; battle of Jutland; naval asst. to controller, 1901-3; dir. of naval ordnance, 1906-7; A.D.C. to King Edward VII., 1906-7; 3rd sea lord of the Admiralty and controller of the navy, 1908-10; cmdr.-in-chief of the Atlantic fleet, 1910-11, and of the Grand Fleet, 1914-16; 2nd sea lord of the Admiralty, 1912-14; 1st sea lord, 1916-17; admiral of the fleet, 1919; governor-general of New Zealand, 1920-1924; G.C. Legion of Honour, and Croix de Guerre (France); G.C. Order of Leopold, and Croix de Guerre (Belgium); St. George 3 (Russia); G.C.R.S. with Pavlovna (Japan); G.C. Savoia (Italy).

JOHNSON, GEORGE CUNYNGHAM.—B. 1876; 2nd olc., treasy., St. Kitts, Apr., 1894; 2nd olc. admstr.'s office and olc. of legis. coun., Mar., 1896; clk. to admstr. and olc. of couns., Jan., 1901; treas. and compt., St. Kitts-Nevis, June, 1923; ag. admstr., St. Kitts-Nevis, June, 1923; offl. mem., exec. and legis. couns., St. Kitts-Nevis and offl. mem., Fed. exec. coun.; censor, St. Kitts during the war; is a J.P. for St. Kitts-Nevis; ag. col. sec., Leeward Is. and admstr. govt., Antigua, June, 1925.

JOHNSON, JOHN COLEMAN DE GRAFT.—B. 1884; ed. Wesleyan Coll. Sch., Cape Coast; 5th grade olc., Ashanti, 1907; 4th grade olc., police dept., 1912; asst. inspr. W. and M., 1914; asst. sec. for native affra., 23rd Mar., 1920.
JOHNSON, HON. SIR WILLIAM ELLIOT, K.C.M.G. (1920).—B. 1862; speaker of the H. of R., C. of A., 1913-14 and 1917-28.

JOHNSON, WILLIAM JOSEPH.—B. 1892; serv. in I.E.F., E. Africa, 1914-17; ment. in despa., 1917; transfd. to depot, 111th Mahars, India, 1917; offr. in charge, clearing house, office of field contr. of mily. accts., E.E.F., Cairo, 1918; major, ch. accts. offr., O.E.T. admstr., Palestine, 1919; treas., Palestine civ. admstr., 1st July, 1920; dep. treas. on abolition of post of finan. sec., May, 1922; ag. treas. and mem. exec. and advisory couns., June to Sept., 1923, June to Sept., 1924, Jan., Apr. and May, 1926 and May to Sept., 1926; mem., comte. on pub. security forces, 1923; mem., comte. on establishment of permanent and pensionable cadre for gen. clerical serv., 1925; mem., road bd., 1926.

JOHNSTON, ALEXANDER.—B. 1867; ed. at pub. schls., and St. Francis Xavier Coll., Antigonish, Nova Scotia; entd. local legis. of Nova Scotia, April, 1897, as repres. for Cape Breton Co., Nova Scotia; resig., 1900; elect. to H. of C., 1900; re-elec., 1904; dep. min. marine and fisheries of Canada, June, 1910.
JOHNSTON, H. LINDSAY.—Govt. statist., S. Australia, 1916.

JOHNSTON, JOHN ROOKE.—B. 1898; ed. St. Ninians, Moffat and St. Bees Schls. and Sandhurst; 2nd lieut., The Black Watch, 23rd Dec., 1917; seconded to 2/1st K.A.R., 23rd Dec., 1920; adminis. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 12th Sept., 1923.

JOHNSTONE, ANGUS COLIN DUNCAN.—B. 1889; ed. Trin. Coll., Glenalmond, and in Germany; passed Army entrance exam., R.M.C., Sandhurst, 1908; higher grade staff, G. Cent. Rly., 1909-12; served, Balkan War, with Br. Red Cross Unit attd. Turkish Forces, 1912-13; lieut., 5th (Res.) Batt. West Yorks Regt. (T.), Nov., 1914 to Apr., 1915; asst. dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, 10th Sept., 1913; lieut., W.A.F.F., and attd. Gold Coast Regt., 23rd Apr., 1915; seconded Br. Forces, Togoland, May, 1915; offr., cl. II., polit. serv., 1st Jan., 1920; dist. comsnr., Ashanti Akim, 2nd Sept., 1922.

JOHNSTONE, BANNER CARRUTHERS.—B. 1882; ed. Eton and Trin. Coll., Camb.; B.A. (Oantab.); passed R.G.S. survey course, 1908; asst. supt., surveys, Ceylon, 21st Oct., 1909; asst. collr. (afterwards asst. dist. comsnr.), Zanzibar Prot., 23rd Apr., 1913; ag. 1st ass't sec. and olc. of coun., 16th Nov., 1914 to 11th June, 1915; ch. land attlimt. offr., 1st Jan., 1916; seconded for active serv. in German East Africa, 13th Aug., 1916; on mil. serv., France, 30th Oct., 1916 to 7th Mar., 1919; ag. dist. comsnr., Pemba, 21st July, 1919 to 25th Apr., 1920; dist. comsnr., Pemba, 24th Nov., 1921; ag. senr. comsnr., 23rd Aug., 1924 to 22nd Feb., 1925; 3rd cls. Order of the Brilliant Star of Zanzibar, 1920; ag. senr. comsnr. from 26th Oct., 1926; mem., leg. com.
JOHNSTONE, JOHN WILKIE.—B. 1884; sol., England, 1906; astl. polit. offr., G.E.A. (Tanganyika Territory), Nov., 1918; ag. mag., Apr., 1919 to Feb., 1921; called to bar, Gray's Inn, 1921.

JOHNSTONE, SIR ROBERT STEWART, Kt. B.Ach. (1915).—Ed. at Trin. Coll., Dublin; B.A. (honors and medal in history and political science, 1882; LL.B., 1884; M.A., 1886; Holt schla., Gray's Inn, 1885; called to the Irish bar, 1889; called to the bar at Gray's Inn, Jan., 1889; lieut. 3rd (Militia) Batt. Manchester Regt., 1883; capt., 1887; dist. comsnr., Lagos, 1889; served on Lagos and Porto Novo boundary coman., 1890; stip. and circuit mag. and judge of ct. of com. pleas., Bahamas, Jan., 1894; ag. atty.-gen., Aug. to Nov., 1902; ch. just., Grenada, 1909; ret., 1914.

JOMARON, ADOLPHE CHARLES.—B. 1883; ed. at University Coll. Schl. and Corps Christi Coll., Camb. (class. schol.)—B.A. 1920; 2nd hum. R.F.A., 1914; lieut., 1915; capt., 1917; asst. major, 1918; wounded, 1916; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1920, attd. to asst. adviser's office, Muar, Johore; 2nd astl. adviser, Muar, Johore, Jan., 1922; passed cadet, June, 1922; offr., cl. V., June, 1922; seconded for serv. in Kelantan as dist. offr., Pasir Puteh, July, 1923; ag. rupt. lands, Kelantan, astl. state audr., chmn., conservancy bd., Khota Bharu and coroner, June, 1924.

JONES, CHARLES BENJAMIN.—B. 1882; 6th cls. olc. treasy. dept., S. Nigeria, 1906; 6th cls. olc., 1906; 4th cls. clk. div. comsnr.'s off., Beny, 1906; prov. comsnr.'s off., E. Prov., 1906; 2nd cls. clk., Jan., 1907; head clk., sol.-gen.'s off., E. Prov., Sept., 1909; 1st cls. clk., Jan., 1912.

JONES, CHARLES ERNEST.—B. 1892; B.A., B.Sc., Lond.; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1914; attd. to Kegalla Kachcheri, Dec., 1914; Ratnapura Kach., Apr., 1915; Kegalla Kachcheri, Jan., 1916; censor's off., Apr., 1916; on mily. duty, 1918; returned to censor's off., June, 1919; pol. mag., Matara, July, 1919; sec., retrenchment coman., Jan., 1922; addnl. astl. dir., educ., Dec., 1923; dist. judge, N'Ellya, Apr., 1925; astl. govt. agt., Mannar, Nov., 1925.

JONES, EDGAR ANDERSON AVERY, M.B.E. (1918).—B. 1875; ed. at Cathedral Schl., Hereford and St. John's Coll., Oxford (B.A.); probationer, N.E. Rhodesia, 4th July, 1901; astl. nativ. coman., 16th Aug., 1901; nativ. comsnr., 1st Sept., 1903; ag. comdt., N.E.R. constab., 1st Apr., 1906; comdt., 1st Feb., 1906; astl. mag., 31st Oct., 1906; has acted as mag.on various occasions between May, 1914 and Dec., 1915; mag., 20th Apr., 1916; J.P.

JONES, FREDERICK JAMES, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1874; ed. State schl., Otago Boys High Schl. and Canterbury Univ. Coll., N.Z.; entd. New Zealand govt. rly. serv., 1889; dist. engrnr., 1901; ch. engrnr., Feb., 1924; chmn., rlya. bd., Dec., 1924.

JONES, G. A.—B. 1889; appt., after compet. exam., cl. of the 2nd div. of the civ. serv., and assigned to the C.O., Dec., 1907; astl. sec. to Oversea Prize Disposal Comtes., Mar., 1917; astl. sec. to ditto, Jan., 1918; promoted, under O.in-t. of 1910, 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 14th Mar., 1919; prin., 1st Apr., 1920.

JONES, GWILYM ARTHUR.—Agric. diploma, Univ. Coll., Bangor, N. Wales, with 1st cls. in agric.; awarded Wm. Griffith prize, 1906-8; holder of certif. in forestry; ag. agric. and science master, Antigua Gram. schl., 1909; astt. curator, botanic gardens, Dominica, Aug., 1909; in charge of agric. schl., Dominica, Aug., 1909, to Jan., 1910; astt. sec. to permanent exhibtn. comtee., 1909; ag. curator, botanic gardens, Dominica, 1913; chemist, agric. dept., Dominica, Apr., 1916; and a director of the Victoria museum, 1915.
JONES, HAROLD W. J.—B. 1877; ed. Berks-stead and London; astt. engtn.; P.W.D., F.M.S., Apr., 1901; ex. engtn., grade III, Nov., 1900; ditto, grade II, Jan., 1913; sen. ex. engtn., 1923.
JONES, LLEWELLYN A. F.—Ast. dist. comnrs., E.A.P., 18th Dec., 1908; junr. ast. sec., Feb., 1912; priv. sec. to gov., 1915; dist. comnrs., June, 1916; ag. senr. comnrs., Kenya, 1926.

JONES, MALCOLM LUDLOW, O.B.E. (1919).—Served at Bd. of Trade (Labour Dept.), 1897-1901; chief clk., Emigrants' Information Office, 1901; visited Canada on behalf of E.I.O., 1907; chief clk., Oversea Settlem. Office, 1919.

JONES, NELSON.—B. 1895; ed. Glasgow Univ.; comsmd., Cameron Highlands, 1915; M.C., Apr., 1917; bar, June, 1918; capt., July, 1918; discharged on account of wounds, Apr., 1919; cadet, F.M.S., Oct., 1919; astt. contr. of lab., K. Lumpur, May, 1921; on sp. duty in cust. dept. in connection with toddy shops, July, 1922; 2nd lieut., M.V.I., Nov., 1922; astt. to dist. office, Klang, to act as lst cls. mag., 1923; ag. astt. dist. offr., Ulu Langat, July, 1924; 3rd mag., Singapore, July, 1924; ag. dep. contr., lab., Kuala Lumpur, May, 1925; ag. dep. contr., lab., Penang, July, 1925; ag. dep. contr., lab., Kedah, Jan., 1926.
JONES, ROBERT NOBLE.—B. 1864; barrister and solr., N.Z., 1890; judge, native land ct. and dist. land regtr., 1903; chief judge, native land ct., 1919; under sec. native dept., 1922.

JONES, SAMUEL BENJAMIN, M.B.E. (1924), M.A. (Durh.), B.A. (Lond.), Inter. LL.B. Exam. (Lond.), L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S., (Edin.), L.R.F.P.S. (Glasg.)—B. 1874; ed. Antigua Gram. Sch.; med. offr., and mag., Anguilla, 1918; thanked by exec. coun. of St. Kitts for checking outbreak of small pox in Anguilla, 1921; med. offr., dist. No. 2, St. Kitts, 1924.

JONES, STANLEY WILSON.—B. 1888; B.A. (Manchester), 1st cls. hons. hist. (1908); cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1911; ag. astt. dist. offr., Pekan, June, 1912; passed cadet, Dec., 1913; ag. astt. dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, Nov., 1914; offr., cls. V, Dec., 1914; ag. astt. dist. offr., Kuantan, Mar., 1916; astt. dist. offr., Pekan, Aug., 1917, astt. dist. offr., Kualas Kangar, June, 1918; offr., cls. IV, Jan., 1919; dist. offr., Jelebu; Jan. 1920; dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, May, 1920; ag. dist. offr., Klang, Aug., 1923; ag. dist. offr., Kuantan, Sept., 1923; local examr. in law and Malay, Nov., 1923; offr., cls. III, Dec., 1923; astt. to federal secretariat on sp. duty, Aug., 1924; local examr. in Malay, Salangor, Sept., 1924.

JONES, SIR WM. HENRY HYNDMAN, K.T., B.A.C. (1906).—Barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn; LL.B., Cantab.; b. 1847; ag. sen. pol. mag., Barbados, 1880; comnrs. to inquire into working of pol. force, 1880; ag. judge of ast. ct. of appeal, Jan., 1881; 1st dist., St. Lucia, Mar., 1881; M.L.C., ditto, Oct., 1881; deleg. to W. Indian telegraph confere., May, 1882; ag. ch. just., St. Lucia and Tobago, Jan., 1883; ag. atty.-gen., and mem. of exec. coun., Feb., 1883; ag. ch. just. of St. Lucia and Tobago; mem. of Windwards ct. of appeal, Sept., 1886; mag. and mem. of exec. and legis. couns., Grenada, Mar., 1887; res. mag. for different districts of Jamaica, April, 1888, to Feb., 1893; ag. prsne judge, Jamaica, May, 1893, to June, 1895; ag. atty.-gen., June, 1895; supernumerary res. mag., Jamaica, May, 1896; prsne judge, S. Stiltsns., 3rd Dec., 1896; ag. jud. comnrs., in addition to other duties, 25th Mar., 1903, to 3rd Feb., 1904; jud. comnrs., 16th June, 1904; ch. jud. comnrs., 1st Jan., 1906; ch. just., S. Stiltsns., 22nd Aug., 1906; ret., July, 1914.
JONES, WILLIAM JOHN ANDREW, B.A. (Oxford).—B. 1889; ed. Jesus Coll., Oxford; astt. dist. comnrs., Gold Coast, 26th Mar., 1913; dist. comnrs., 18th Dec., 1918; dep. prov. comnrs., 3rd Jan., 1924.

JONES, W. PATRICK.—Impl. service, 1891 to 1902; clk., treasy., Transvaal, 18th Mar., 1902; priv. sec. to col. treasy., Apr., 1903; principal clk., treasy., 1st July, 1904; principal clk., dept. of finance, U. of S. Africa, 1910; chief clk., treasy., Union of S. Africa, 1st Aug., 1912; chief clk. and acctnt., in rev. dept., Mar., 1917; oh. survr. of rev., 1st Nov., 1921; recr. rev., Johannesburg, Dec., 1923.

JONES-BATEMAN, REGINALD.—B. 1894; on mil. serv., Sept., 1914; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1919; attached to Kurunegala Ksch., Dec., 1919; office astt. to supt. of census and dir. of statistics, Oct., 1921; pol. mag., Jaffna, Oct., 1924; astt. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Nov., 1924.

JORDAN, ARTHUR BENJAMIN.—B. 1890; ed. Nottingham High Schl., and Jesus Coll., Oxford (class. exhibr.).—B.A., Oxon., 1913; cadet, F.M.S., 28th Nov., 1913; passed final exam. in Cantonese, 26th Apr., 1916; ag. astt. contr. of lab., K. Lumpur, 6th June, 1916; 2nd lieut., Malayan Vol. Infy., 6th Deco., 1916; passed F.M.S. govt. exam. in law, and passed cadet, 26th Dec., 1916; offr., cls. V, offtng. as astt. contr. of lab., K. Lumpur, 26th Dec., 1916; ag. prot. of Chinese, Perak, 30th Nov., 1917; astt. contr. of lab., K. Lumpur, 15th Jan., 1918; lieut., Malayan Vol. Infy., 6th Dec., 1918; astt. dist. offr., Raub (Bentong), 28th Feb., 1919; offr., cls. IV, 28th Nov., 1920; astt. prot. of Chinese, Negri Sembilan, 15th Apr., 1921; ag. prot., Chinese, Perak, Dec., 1923; ag. prot., Chinese, Selangor and Pahang, Apr., 1925; offr., cls. III, Nov., 1925.
JORDAN, A. H.—B. 1903; ent. C.O., 17th Mar., 1919; cler. offr., 14th Oct., 1921.

JOY, GEORGE ANDREW.—B. 1896; ed. St. Francis Xavier's Coll., Bruges; ent. Army, 1914; served in Belgium and France, 1915 and 1916; Palestine and Syria, 1917-18; staff. gen. headqrs., Egypt and Palestine, 1919-20; S.W. Arabia, 1921 to 1923; ment. in desps., lieut., R.A.R.O., Feb., 1919; astt. to res. comnrs., New Hebrides, Aug., 1924.
JOYCE, G. F. W. C.—B. 1897; ent. E.I.O., June, 1912; astt. olk., Jan., 1915; on mil. serv., Sept., 1914 to Apr., 1919; cler. offr., O.S.O., 1st Jan., 1920.

JOYCE, BREVIET LIEUT.-COL. PIERCE CHARLES, C.B.E. (1920), D.S.O. (1918).—B. 1878; ed. Beauumont Coll., Old Windsor; joined 1st Batt. The Connaught Rangers, 1900; serv. S. African War, 1900-02 (severely wounded, Queen's Med. with 3 clasps, King's Med. with 2 clasps); astt., Egyptian Army, 1907-16 (Egyptian Med. with clasp, 4th cls. Order of the Medjidieh, 4th cls. Order of the Nile); European War, 1915 (Gallipoli); Arab campaign against the Turks, 1916-18; mil. advr. to Amir Faisal, 1917 (ment.in desps. five times, brevet of lieut.-col., C.B.E., D.S.O., Croix d’Officier, Legion of Honour, Arabian Order of the Nahdah, 2nd cls.; gov., E. and S. Desert Provs., Egypt, 1920; mil. adviser to Iraq Govt., Mar., 1921.

JOYNT, HENRY RAYMOND, B.A.—B. 1888; ed. Bradfield, and Balliol Coll., Oxon. (math. schol.); cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1911; ag. 2nd asst. contr. of lab., Penang, Mar., 1913; ag. asst. contr. of lab., K. Lumpur, Apr., 1914; passed cadet, July, 1914; ag. rev. audr., Sel., N. Sembilan and Phg., June, 1918; dist. offr., K. Langat, Nov., 1919; dep. contr. of lab., Seremban, Mar., 1921; agt. to food contr., N. Sembilan in addn., Apr. to July, 1921; ag. dep. contr., lab., Penang, Nov., 1921; ag. contr., lab., in addition, Apr., 1922 on sp. duty, rubber census office, Kuala Lumpur, June, 1922; ag. 2nd asst. sec., govt. of F.M.S., Nov., 1923; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1923; second asst. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Nov., 1923.

JUST, SIR HARTMANN W., K.C.M.G. (1911), C.B. (1902), C.M.G. (1900), B.A. (1877).—B. 1854; appointed, after compet. exam., clk. in office of sec. of state for the col., 3rd June, 1878; ed. at Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; was an open scholar on the foundation; 1st class in classical mods., 1874; Taylorian exhibitioner for German, 1876; 2nd class in final classical school, 1877; asst. priv. sec. to the late Earl of Derby, 6th Feb., 1883, to Col. F. A. Stanley (now Earl of Derby), 24th June, 1886, and to Mr. Stanhope, 3rd Aug., 1886; priv. sec. to Mr. Osborne Morgan, 6th Feb. to 3rd Aug., 1886; asst. priv. sec. to Sir H. Holland (afterwards Viscount Knutsford), 12th Jan., 1887, and to the Marquess of Ripon, 17th Aug., 1892; asst. sec. to the col. confee., 1887; 1st class clk., Apr., 1895; prin. clk., Mar., 1897; accompanied Rt. Hon. Joseph Chamberlain on his visit to S. Africa, 1902-3; asst. under-sec. of state, Jan., 1907; jt. sec. to Imp. Confce., 1907; sec. to Imp. Confce. on naval and mil. defence, 1909; visited Canada, 1910; also Australia and New Zealand, 1914; permanent sec. to Imp. Confce.; reg. of the Order of St. Michael and St. George; represented C.O. on advisory comte. of comm. intell., B. of T.; retired, 1916.

KANE, RICHARD RUTLEDGE, M.C. (1916), Bar (1917).—B. 1877; ed. Royal Arty. Inst., Belfast and Portora Royal Schl., Enniskillen; served l’echuanaland Field Force, 1896-7 (S. African Gen. Serv. Med. with Bech. clasp); cadet, Fiji, Apr., 1899; clk. to stip. mag., Ba, Apr., 1901; inspr. of stock, Mar., 1905; stip. mag., Fiji, Jan., 1906; ag. stip. mag. and asst. native comsnr., Kaduva, Jan., 1906; ag. stip. mag., Ba, Apr., 1906 and Jan., 1908; ag. sub-inspr., constab., Jan., 1907; asst. stip. mag., Lautoka, Jan., 1909; assigned dist. of Nadi, Jan., 1909; 2nd grade mag., Jan., 1910; mag., Ba, Feb., 1910; ag. stip. mag., Levuka, and comsnr., Lomaiviti, Mar., 1913; dist. comsnr., Ba, Jan., 1914; 1st grade dist. comsnr., July, 1915; proceeded on mil. serv., Aug., 1915; resumed duty, May, 1919; mag., dist. of Rewa, May, 1919; prov. comsnt., Naitsai-siri (conj.), May, 1919; dist. comsnr., Tallevu, May, 1919; nom. mem. leg. council, Fiji, June, 1919; spec. comsnr., N.W. Viti levu, during Indian strike, Feb.-Mar., 1921; ment. by gov. in council for work in connection with strike, Apr., 1921; ag. res. comsnr., Br. Solomon Is., Apr., 1921; dep. comsnr., W. Pacific, Apr., 1921; res. comsnr., Br. Sol. Is., Sept., 1921; judl. comsnr., W. Pacific, 26th July, 1922.

KANNANGARA, EDWARD WILMOT.—B. 1894; B.A., Lond.; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., local divn., Aug., 1919; attached to Kandy Kach., Aug., 1919; attached to Batticaloa Kach., Jan., 1920; ag. addtl. pol. mag., Colombo, Negombo and Avissawella, May, 1921; pol. mag., Avissawella Mar., 1923; ditto, Jaffna, Nov., 1924; ditto, Balapitiya, June, 1926.

KANTAWALA, MOHAN HARGOVINDDAR.—B. 1890; B.A., Cantab.; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1915; attached to Anuradhapura Kach., Feb., 1915; addtl. pol. mag., Anuradhapura, in addition to his own duties, July, 1915; addtl. pol. mag. and addtl. mun. mag., Colombo, July, 1917; pol. mag., Avissawella, Mar., 1918; office asst. to govt. agt., E. Prov., Oct., 1919; pol. mag., Negombo, Jan., 1921; ditto, Panadura, June, 1923; ditto, Pt. Pedro, Nov., 1924.

KAUFMANN, HILARY PILKINGTON.—B. 1892; on mil. serv., Aug., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Apr., 1920; attd. to Jaffna Kachcheri, June, 1920; ag. office asst. to govt. agt. N. Prov., May, 1921; pol. mag., Balapitiya, Nov., 1922; dep. rubber contr., Nov., 1923; pol. mag., Kandy, Feb., 1926.
KAUNTZE, W. H.—Bacteriologist and pathologist, E. Africa Prot., Jan., 1919.
KEAN, HON. ABRAHAM.—M.H.A., Bonavista, Newfdld., 1886-89, Bay de Verde, 1897; mem. exec. coun. (without portfolio), 1897; ag. min. agricul. and mines, 1898.
KEARNEY, G.—Asst. supt., pol., Kenya, Dec., 1923.

KEATINGE, WILLIAM MAYBURY, B.A., LL.D.—B. 1879; ed. Monmouth Gram. Schl. and Trinity Coll., Dublin (Jellett prizeman); B.A., LL.B. (1900); LL.D. (1903); solr., 1904; admstr.-gen. regir. of deeds and sec., Wakf comen., Zanzibar, 30th Mar., 1915; joint custodian of enemy property and liquidator of enemy firms, Nov., 1915; offr. trustee, 1919; contr., local clearing office, Nov., 1920; regir.-gen., pub. trustee and off. recr., Kenya, June, 1924.

KEAY, DAVID DOIG.—Ck., Natal govt. rly., 1901-2; clk., law dept., Transvaal, Mar., 1903; ag. ch. clk., admstr. branch, atty-gen.’s off., Nov., 1903; ch. clk., July, 1904; chief clk., dept. of just., 1st Apr., 1912; under sec. for justice, Nov., 1917.

KEET, JOHAN DIEDERIK MOHR.—Asst. dist. forest offr., working plans, eastern conservancy, Jan., 1908; asst. dist. forest offr., Keiskama Hoek, Jan., 1909; ag. dist. forest offr., Keiskama Hoek, Jan., 1914; ag. dist. forest offr., Knyasa, Nov., 1915; dist. forest offr., Knyasa, Apr., 1917; conservator of forests, Transvaal, Mar., 1923.

KEIR, A., M.A., U.D.A.—B. 1884; ed. Fordyce Acad. and Aberdeen University; M.A. (Aberdeen), 1906; diploma, agr., Cambridge Univ., 1913; educ. dept., S.S. and F.M.S., 1906; inspr., schls., Selangor, 1911; head, Malay training coll., Matang, 1913; inspr., schls., Perak, 1920; repd. British Malaya at Imp. confce. on educn., 1923; ch. inspr., English schls., S.S. and F.M.S., Dec. 1923-Sept., 1924; inspr., schls., Perak, Sept., 1924.

KEITH-ROACH, EDWARD.—B. 1885; ed. privately; 2nd lieut., 7th Lancs. Fusils., Aug., 1914; capt., Nov., 1914; major, July, 1915; seconded, gen. staff, G.H.Q., E.E.F., July, 1915; Bimbashi, Egyptian Army, May, 1916; intell. offr., Sudan govt., May, 1916; inspr., Port Sudan, Aug., 1916; Darfur Exped. and first inspr., Umkedada, Darfur, Oct., 1916 (Sudan Med. and Bar); 2nd cls. mag., 1916; dist. comsnr., Eastern Darfur, Jan., 1917; passed Sudan govt. Arabic exam., 1917; 1st cls. mag., June, 1917; pub. cust., enemy property in Palestine, under occupied enemy territory adminstrn., Dec., 1919; 1st asst. sec., Palestine govt., 11th Oct., 1920; lent to C.O. by Palestine govt. and ag. prin., Feb., 1924 to Feb., 1925; Eq. of the Order of St. John of Jerusalem; asst. dist. commr., N. dist., 1st Oct., 1925; publications, "Darfur," a Province of the Anglo-Egyptian Sudan," joint editor, "Handbook of Palestine, 1922."

KELLAGHER, GEORGE BANNERMAN.—B. 1889; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1919; cadet, S.S., 1913; add. res. councillor's office, Penang, Dec., 1913; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, Feb. to Mar., 1914; add. G.P.O., Singapore, May, 1914; ag. 4th mag. and asst. dist. judge, Nov., 1914; passed cadet, Feb., 1917; supernumy. offr., cl. V., Feb., 1917; on mil. serv., Apr., 1917; 2nd lieut., R.E., Dec., 1917; B.E.F., France, Apr., 1918; wounded Oct., 1918; lieut., R.E., June, 1919; demob., Feb., 1920; supernumy. offr., cl. IV., Nov., 1920; ag. asst. offr. assignee, Singapore, Apr., 1921.
KELLY, F. L.—Asst. conservator of forests, E.A.P., Jan., 1913.

KELLY, SIR HENRY GREENE, KT. BACH. (1906).—Ed. Trin. Coll., Dub.; honorman and prizeman in hist. and political science; B.A. and LL.B.; called to the bar, King's Inn, Dub., Mich. Term, 1884; apptd. a puisne judge in the Niger Territories, 1891; sat on several occasions as chief justice; chief justice, 1899; chief justice of Southern Nigeria, 1900; ret., 10th Feb., 1908.

KELLY, HON. HUGH THOMAS.—B. 1868; ed. St. Michael's Coll., Toronto, and Toronto Univ.; called to the bar, 1886; K.C., 1908; pres., York Co. Law Assoc., 1910 and 1911; chmn., Toronto pub. lib. bd. for 3 years; a gov. of Toronto Univ., 1906-1912; judge of high ct. of Ontario, 1911.

KELLY, PERCY JAMES.—B. 1876; M.B., Ch.B., Glasgow, 1906; house surg., W. Kent general hosp., Maidstone, 1907-8; employed by govt. of Bengal on famine duty, 1907; certif. Lond. schl. of trop. med., 1908; apptd. to W. African med. serv., G. Coast, 1908; med. offr., Hong Kong, 1910; med. offr. to Victoria gaol and visiting med. offr. to Tung Wa hospital; med. offr. in charge of infectious diseases hosp., Kennedy Town; registr., med. coll., Ceylon, 1912; on military duty, 1915-1918; surg. gen., B. Guiana, 1922.

KELSICK, OSCAR RICHARDSON.—Ag. cashier, savings bank, Antigua, 21st Aug., 1906; apptd. as ditto, 16th Jan., 1908; ag. acctnt., savings bank, 16th Jan., 1908; apptd. as ditto, 1st Sept., 1908; ag. acctnt., treas., 1st May to 4th Aug., 1909; junr. audit clk., 12th June, 1909; ag. govt. offr. (acctnt.), treas., Dominica, 16th Apl. to 31st Aug., 1910; ag. chief audit clk. and federal acctnt., Dec., 1914 to Feb., 1915, and May to July, 1915; 3rd customs offr., treas., Antigua, 1st Apl., 1915; clk. to treas.; clk. to postmaster, cashier and acctnt., savings bank, clk. to exec. coun., Virgin Is., 21st July, 1919; asst. treas., collr., cust., postmr., etc., Montserrat, 14th Apr., 1924; mem., leg. coun., Montserrat, 25th July, 1924; J.P., 14th Nov., 1924.

KEMP, HON. SIR ALBERT EDWARD, K.C.M.G. (1917).—B. 1858; ed. Public Schl., Clarenceville, Quebec and Locolle Acad.; pres. of Canadian Manufacturers' Assocnt., 1895 and 1896; pres. of board of trade of the City of Toronto 1890 and 1900; elec. to H. of C., Canada, 1900; re-elec., 1904, 1911 and 1917; mem. of the Privy Council for Canada and min. without portfolio in Sir Robert Borden's cabinet, Oct., 1911; min. of militia and defence, Nov., 1916; min. of overseas military forces, Oct., 1917; called to senate, Nov., 1921.
KEMP, GEN. THE HON. JAN C. G.—M.L.A., Union of S. Africa since 1920; min. of agr., 30th June, 1924.

KEMP, SIR JOSEPH HORSFORD, KT. BACH. (1927), C.B.E. (1918), K.C. (1918).—B. 1874; B.A., Cape Univ.; barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn; cadet, Hong Kong, 1898; land office work, New Territory, 1899; registr., land court, 1900; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1900 to 1904; ag. 1st pol. mag., 1904; asst. sec., sanitary bd., 1904; deputy-registr. and appraiser, sup. cl., 1904; ag. registr., sup. ct., offl. admstr., offl. trustee, and registr. of companies, 1907; ag. offl. recvr. in bank, Apl., 1907, to Apl., 1908; ag. 1st pol. mag., 1908 to 1909; head of san. dept., 1908; registr., sup. ct., offl. admstr., offl. trustee, and registr. of companies, 1909; crown solr., 1911; ag. puisne judge, 1913; ag. atty.-gen., 1914; atty.-gen., 1915.

KEMP, JOHN TURNBULL.—B. 1892; ed. Christ Coll., Brecon, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Oxon.); war service, E. Africa 1917-18; passed progress exam. in Temne; asst. dist. commr., Sierra Leone, 15th Jan., 1915.

KEMPE, SIR JOHN ARROW, K.C.B. (1910), C.B. (1900), B.A.—B. 1846; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Trin. Coll., Camb.; clk., treas., 1867; priv. sec. to chanclr. of excheqr., 1874-80, to Sir R. Lingen, 1880-81, to Lord F. Cavendish, 1881; mem. of comm. on agric. and dairy schls., 1887-88; prin. clk., treas., 1888; dep. chmn., bd. of cust., 1894; mem. of royal comm. on elec. communities with lighthouses and light vessels, 1892-97; asst. comptlr. and auditor, 1904; comptlr. and auditor-gen., 1905; hon. auditor of certain crown colonies and protectorates, ag. on behalf of S. of S. for the cols.; retired, 1911.

KEMPE, JOHN EBESKINE.—B. 1888; ed. Radley Coll. and University Coll., Oxford (2nd cls. hon. soh. mod. hist.); B.A., 1910; cadet, F.M.S., 1911; passed final exam., Malay, May, 1913; ag. asst. dist. offr., K. Kangsar, Oct., 1912; asst. dist. offr., K. Kangsar, Feb., 1915; ag. 2nd mag., K. Lumpur, Mar., 1918; offr., cl. IV., 1st Jan., 1919; dist. offr., Pekan, Feb., 1919; 2nd lieut., M.V.I., Pahang, Apr., 1919; seconded for serv. as ag. commr., lands, Trengganu, Feb., 1923; offr., cl. III., Nov., 1923.
KEMPSFORD, GEORGE HENRY.—B. 1870; apptd., after exam., 3rd cls. messenger, C.O., 22nd Feb., 1904; 2nd cls., 1907; 1st cls., 3rd July, 1915.
