KENDALL, NEVILL.—B. 1871; ed. Eton and Pembroke Coll., Oxford (B.A.); jun. offr., Perak, 16th Aug., 1896; ag. asst. dist. mag., Selama, 7th Apr., 1896; ag. coll. of land rev., L. Perak, 30th Jan., 1897; asst. dist. mag., Ulu Brayas, 1st Jan., 1899; ag. asst. dist. mag., Gopeng, 19th Mar., 1899; coll. of land rev., L. Perak, 17th Aug., 1899; ag. Indian immigrn. agt., 24th Aug., 1902; ag. dist. offr., Jelebu, 8th Sept., 1902; dep. chmn. and sec., sany. bd., Kinta South, 1st Jan., 1905; chmn. and sec. sany. bd., Kinta South, 1st Jan., 1907; ag. ch. asst. dist. offr., Kinta, and regir. of titles, Perak South, 29th Dec., 1910; ag. dist. offr., L. Perak, 15th Nov., 1913; ag. sec. to res., Perak, 3rd Apr., 1914; dist. offr., Batang Padang, 1st Feb., 1915; regir. of titles, Perak, inspr. of prisons, F.M.S., and ag. supl., convict estabnt., Taiping, 13th Sept., 1920; regis. of titles, Perak and ag. supt., convict estab. and inspr., prisons, in addition, Sept., 1920; ag. supt., convict estab., Taiping and inspr., prisons, F.M.S., Dec., 1921; offg. regis. titles, Perak in addition, Jan., 1922; seconded for serv. as adviser, land office, Kedah, May, 1922; superm. offr., cl. I., B. for U.M.S., July, 1921.
KENNAN, MYLES.—B. 1894; clk., Basutoland, 1913; war serv., 1915-18; sub-inspr., Basutoland mounted pol., 1926.
KENNAN, THOMAS BRERETON, M.C.—B. 1891; cler. asst., Basutoland, 1910; sub-inspr., pol., 1913; inspr., 1919; served in European War.

KENNEDY, F.—B. 1889; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., C.O., Apl., 1917; passed London metric., June, 1917; on mil. service from 17th Sept., 1917, to 19th March, 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920.

KENNEDY-SKIPTON, GEORGE STACY.—B. 1898; ed. Sherborne and Trinity Coll., Dublin; 1st class schl., 1919; triple senr. mod., 1920-21; cadet, Hong Kong, Dec., 1921; ast. head, sany. dept. and sec., sany. bd., 1924; ast. sec., Chinese affs., 1926; ast. supt., impts. and expts., 1926.
KENNEDY, W.—Vet. offr., E.A.P., May, 1910; dep. chief vet. offr., Aug., 1914; ch. vet. offr., Kenya, Apr., 1921.
KENT, HON. JAS. M.—K.C., B.A.; M.H.A. Newfoundland, 1904; mem. of exec. coun. and min. of just., 1907; judge, supreme ct., 1916.

KENWORTHY, MAJOR H., R.E. (ret.), O.B.E.—B. 1873; ed. privately; mech. and civ. engnr., specialising in land reclamation and irrig., anti-malarial drainage; serv. in France, Belgium and Italy, 1915-20 (twice ment. in desp., chevalier Crown of Italy); C.R.E., Toronto; tech. adviser, disposal bd.; lent to Italian Genio Marina for anti-malarial reclamation and drainage; supt., P.W.D., Seychelles, 1921.

KENYON-SLANEY, NEVILLE AGLIONBY.—Ed. at Haileybury Coll.; ast. collr., E. Africa Prot., 18th Oct., 1903; attached to secretariat, Mar., 1904; 3rd ast. sec., 1st Apr., 1906; dist. commr., 28th Jan., 1908; ag. prov. commr., 1910, and 1913-1914; ag. senr. ast. secy., 1915; seconded as lieut., 3rd K.A.R., 1915-16; polit. offr., E. African Expedy. Force, 1916-18; ment. in desp.

KERMACK, STUART GRACE, M.A., LL.B.—B. 1888; advoc.; comm. in R.F.A. (T.F.), 1910-19; serv. in Gallipoli and Egypt, 1915-18; dist. judge, Sudan, 1918; gov't. advoc. and regis. of companies and industrial property, Palestine, 1920.

KERR, DAVID IRVINE.—B. 1878; clk. to pol. mag., Grenada, 1899; rev. offr. (various grades) 1902-15; 2nd clk., treas., 1918; ch. rev. offr., 1919; ag. ch. of pol., 1920 and 1924; clk., P.W.D., 1921; ast., of prisons and recorder of meteorological obsns., 1922; seconded for war service (Egypt and E. Africa), 1916-19; J.P., 1919.

KERR-PEARSE, MAJOR B. A. T. (formerly Rifle Brigade).—B. 1871; A.D.C. to gov'. gen. of Australia (Lord Northcote), 1906-07; priv. sec. to gov. of West Australia (Sir G. Strickland), 1909-12; priv. sec. to gov. of Tasmania (Rt. Hon. Sir W. Ellison-Macartney), 1913-16; priv. sec. to gov'. gen. of Australia (Rt. Hon. Sir R. Munro Ferguson), 1915-16; mil. sec., 1916.

KERSHAW, HENRY VALDMR.—Capt., 19th Patt., London Regt., adjutant Nairobi defence force; served as lieut., in 2nd Middlesex Regt. in S. African War, 1899-1902 (Queen's medal and 4 clasps); ast. traffic supt., Jamaica govt. rlyw., Apl., 1906; travelling auditor, Lagos rlyw., Apl., 1908; ast. chief acctnt., Sierra Leone govt. rlyw., Mar., 1909; ast. acctnt., Lagos rlyw., June, 1909 to Dec., 1910; ast. chief acctnt., Uganda rlyw., 23rd Oct., 1912 to 31st Mar., 1915; deputy chief acctnt., Uganda rlyw., 1st Apl., 1915.
KETTLE, JAMES DAVID, B.Sc. (Lond.), F.I.C., F.C.S.—B. 1879; ast. govt. analyst, Trinidad, 1922.

KEYTE, CHARLES ROBERT, Mem. I.R.E., A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1883; offr. in charge, wireless station, Georgetown B. Guiana, 1909; senr. engr., with rank of sub-lieut., naval wireless station, Demerara, May, 1915; engr.-operator in charge, wireless station, Ocean Is., Aug., 1915; ast. conf. clk., res. commr.'s office, Gilbert and Ellice Is., June, 1918 to Feb., 1919; dep. commr. for the W. Pacific, Feb., 1919; offr. in charge at govt. headqrs. during absence of res. commr., May to Nov., 1919; dir., posts and telegraphs, Somaliland, Nov., 1920; ag. D.P.W., Apr. to Oct., 1924; P.M.G., N. Rhodesia, 6th April, 1926.

KIDD, MAJOR GEORGE MONTGOMERY, R.A.—Dublin, M.C.—B. 1889; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1912; ag. ast. dist. offr., Tampin, Sept., 1914; seconded mily. serv. as temp. 2nd lieut., R. Irish Fusiliers, Mar., 1915; temp. capt., ditto, Aug., 1915; ag. maj., R. Inniskilling Fusiliers, Jan., 1915; demob., Apr., 1919; returned to duty, F.M.S., Nov., 1919; sec. to advr., Kedah, Jan., 1920; ast. ast., food contr., S. Kedah, Feb., 1920; ast. ast., food contr., Kedah, Sept., 1920; dep. supt., census, Kedah, Oct., 1920; ag. ast. to advr., land, Kedah, June, 1921; sec., sany. bd., Alor Star, Sept., 1922; sec., rubber restn. commr., Kedah, Oct., 1922; attd., rubber restn. off., Kuala Lumpur, Feb., 1925; chmn., assess. comm., F.M.S., Mar., 1925; ag. commr., lands, Trengganu, Mar., 1926.
KIDD, CAPT. W. R., M.C., B.A. (Oxon.)—Ast. dist. commr., Kenya, Feb., 1920.

KIDSON, NORMAN S. B.—B. 1885; served in Connaught Rangers, 1914-19; 2nd ast. mast., Boys' Gram. Schl., Fiji, Mar., 1920; clk. of peace, Rewa, Sept., 1920; ag. dist. commr., Rewa and ag. prov. commr., Naitasiri, Mar., 1921; ag. dist. commr. and prov. commr., Colo East, Apr., 1921; govt. sec., Br. Solomon Is. Prot., Nov., 1922; on sp. duty as judl. commr. for W. Pacific, Apr., 1924; has acted as offr.-in-charge at headqrs. on several occasions; ag. res. commr., Dec., 1923.

KILBY, WALTER WHEATLEY.—B. 1890; ed. Reading Schl. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; White scholar, 1908-12; 2nd cls. class. mods.; 3rd cls. lit. hum.; ast. dist. commr., Gold Coast, 29th Apl., 1914; ag. dist. commr., Oct., 1914, to June, 1915, and from Nov., 1915, to Dec., 1916.

KILPIN, ST. ERNEST FULLER, K.C.M.G. (1910), C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1854; clk. and shorthand writer, col. sec.'s office, Cape, June, 1876; priv. sec. to Hon. J. Gordon Sprigg, premier and col. sec., Feb., 1878, to June, 1880; clk. ast. of the house of assem., June, 1880; clk., 16th July, 1897; has served as sec. on the following Cape govt. commss.: Dorthesia, 1877; war expenditure, 1881; Liesbeek municipality, 1883; diamond lawn, 1887; liquor laws, 1889; lighthouses, 1890; fisheries, 1892; soah, 1893; defence, 1896; and acted as priv. sec. to premier, the Hon. Sir T. C. Scanlen, Feb. and Mar., 1883; sec. to the Anglo-German comm. on Angra Pequena and W. Coast claims, 1885; author of partly agents' manual, Cape, 1902, 2nd edtn., 1906; of S. African Union and private bill legislation, 1908, and editor of civil service list, 1885-1910; prepared standing ules and orders for the legis. coun., Rhodesia, 898; has frequently been consulted on parly. procedure in South Africa; at the invitation of he O.R.C. govt., assisted in the inauguration f parly. institutions there in Dec., 1907; was examiner for shorthand under civ. ser. commrs., 889-1910; is a J.P. for the Cape Province; chief sec., S. African National Convention, 908-1909; retired, 1910.

KILPIN, RALPH.—B. 1887; ed. Diocesan Coll., Tondebosch, S. Africa; clk. of papers, Cape H. f.A., 1905; clk. of papers, Union H. of A., 1910; nd clk.-asst., 1917; clk.-asst. and acctnt., 1920; dmitted sworn translator (Eng. and Dutch), up. ct., of S. Africa, 1912; assessor apptd. by dmstr., Cape exec. comttee. elections and elec. f senators since 1910; on active serv., E. Africa, 916-17; author of "The Old Cape House."
KIMBELL, ALFRED HENRY.—B. 1875; ed. at Tago Boys' High Sch., N.Z.; entd. N.Z. publ. erv., 1896; held various positions in P.W.D., including land purchase offr.; under-secr. of mines, 1920.

KINDER, F. T.—Ast. engrnr., Singapore lwy., Mar., 1900; ast. supt. of wks., S. Stilts, Mar., 1903; ast. supt. of wks. and surveys, Malacca, Mar., 1908; ast. supt. of wks., 'enang, Apr., 1909; ast. engrnr., Jan., 1910; exec. engrnr., prov. Wellesley, Jan., 1910; exec. engrnr., supernumy., Penang, Jan., 1912; ast. exec. engrnr., Malacca, June, 1913; exec. engrnr., supernumy., Penang, Mar., 1915; ast. exec. engrnr., Singapore, July, 1919; exec. engrnr., grade I., Aug., 1919; ast. exec. engrnr., grade I., Malacca, 8th Jan., 1920, and 3rd Mar., 1920; ast. dep. col. engrnr., Penang, 20th Feb., 1920, and 26th Mar., 921; ast. exec. engrnr., P.W.D., Singapore, 14th eb., 1921.

KINDERSLEY, WILLIAM LORING.—B. 1868; d. Marlborough Sch., and Exeter Coll., Oxford B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Jan., 1893; priv. ec. to lieut.-govr., Sept., 1893; off. ast. to ov't. agt., Uva Prov., Oct., 1895; addl. pol. mag., Matara, Dec., 1895; pol. mag., Kandy, May, 897; dist. judge, Kalutara, Apr., 1903; ast. ov't. agt., Trincomalee, Sept., 1904; Chilaw, Nov., 1905; Puttalam, Apr., 1906; Matare, Mar., 907; gov't. agt., N.C. Prov., May, 1912; ast. ov't. agt., N.W. Prov., Dec., 1912; regis. gen. ept., 1914; gov't. agt., Uva, Sept., 1919; gov't. gt., Cent. Prov., July, 1920.

KING, CLMENT HAMPDEN.—B. 1876; ed. meen's Coll., B. Guiana; ast. 5th cls. elk., police ept., B. Guiana, June, 1893; gradual prom. te rnm. elk.; dist. inspr. of police, 1901; county inspr., Dec., 1908; ast. adjt., July, 1907 to Mar., 908, Sept., 1908 to Feb., 1909, and from Oct., 911 to Apr., 1912; adjt. and staff offr., local prces, Oct., 1914 to Aug., 1919; capt., B. Guiana filitia, Oct., 1914; major, Aug., 1919; ast. eputy inspr.-gen. and supt. of Georgetown fire rig., July to Oct., 1914; ast. deputy inspr.-gen. on various occasions; dep. inspr.-gen. pol. and apt. fire brigade, 20th Aug., 1919; comnrs. of Sierra Leone, 13th June, 1921.

KING, SIR FREDERICK TRUBY, Kt. Bach. (925), C.M.G. (1917), M.B., B.Sc.—Founder of New Zealand Plunket Soc'y, and famous for his work in connection with infant-life protection; ir. of divn. of child-welfare, N.Z. health dept., 21; inspr.-gen., mental hosps., 1925.

KING, GEORGE HOWARD.—Ast. clk., treasy., St. Kitts, 1894; ag. rev. offr., Nevis, 1895; clk., O., St. Kitts, 1896; ag. clk. regiar.'s office, Nevis, 1897; rev. offr., Nevis, Jan., 1898; gov't. offr., St. Kitts, 1899; rev. offr., Nevis, June, 1905; 1st clk., P.O., and sub-inspr. of schls., St. Kitts, 1906; ag. postmr., St. Kitts, 1914; postmr., May, 1915.

KING, HOWARD THOMPSON McKENZIE.—Clerical ast., comsry. dept., B. Guiana, 1st July, 1882; ag. ast. comsry., June, 1884 and Sept., 1885; confirmed as ast. comsry., 5th July, 1887; dist. comsry., 1st Dec., 1890; comsry. of N.W. dist., and stip. mag., 9th July, 1907.

KING, HON. JAMES H., M.D.—B. 1873; ed. McGill Coll.; graduated with honours in medicine and surgery, 1888; practised in Cranbrook, B.C.; took active interest in politics; has large interests in lumber and mining industries in B.C.; mem. legis. assem., 1903-1909; unsuccessful candidate for H. of C., 1911; el. legis. assem., 1916; min. of pub. wks. in Brewer cabinet, 1916; el. to H. of C., bye-el., 14th Mar., 1922; mem. of privy coun. for Canada and min. of pub. wks. in King admin., 3rd Feb., 1922.
KING, JOHN EDMUND.—B. 1900; entered C.O., Sept., 1916; served with R.N.V.R. from 30th Sept. to 19th Dec., 1918; apptd. after compet. exam., ast. clk., O.O., Apl., 1918; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920.

KING, JOHN HAMPDEN, C.B.E. (1923).—Ch. clk., secretariat, Br. Guiana, 1895; ag. inspr. of prisons, 1898, 1899 and 1900; ast. col. sec. and clk. to exec. coun., ct. of policy and combined ct., Jan., 1900; ag. auditor-gen., 1900, 1902, 1903, 1904, 1906 and 1908; ag. col. sec. on various occasions between 1906 and 1926; J.P. for colony, 1894; immigr. agt.-gen., June, 1911; mem. of ct. of policy, combined ct., and exec. coun.; coman. as gov't. dep. with full power, on twenty-six occasions between 1907 and 1922; ag. admstr., Dominica, 4th Apr. to 9th June, 1919; off. rep. of Br. Guiana at W. Indian confee., London, May-June, 1926; served on the following comsns.:—manufacture and storage of bitters and cordials (chmn.), administrator of the funds of the De Safton Trust, supplies for the lunatic asylum (sole comsnr.), reformy. and indust. schl., 1906; on outbreak of war in 1914 apptd. chmn. of foodstuff advisory comttee. and chmn. of sugar control (export) comttee.

KING, NAPLETON WAUTER.—B. 1876; cler. ast., surg.-gen's office, Br. Guiana, Apr., 1893; 6th cls. clk., off. of inspr. of prisons, 1896; 5th ditto, June, 1897; 4th cls. offr. of cust., Jan., 1906; ast. supr., alm house, June, 1912; ast. supr., prisons and ast. to inspr., Sept., 1916; ag. supr., prisons and ast., Bartica, Oct.-Dec., 1917; ag. supr., Onderneeming indust. schl. and immigr. agt., No. 4 dist., Dec., 1918-1919 and from Nov., 1920-Feb., 1921; dep. to D.P.W., June, 1919; ag. man. dir., col. steamer serv., Mar.-Dec., 1921; traffic supt., col. transport dept., Jan., 1922; ast. prisons, inspr. of ditto and mag., Bartica, June, 1922.

KING, PHILIP ARTHUR.—B. 1883; clk. to shipping mast., Barbados, Oct., 1900; ag. ast. harb.mast., Jan. to Mar., 1902; elk., police etc., "A," Aug., 1902; elk. to mast. in chancery, May, 1904; 3rd elk., treasy., Dec., 1907; ag. ast. harb.mast., Aug., 1909; ag. harb. and shipping-mast., Dec., 1909, to Jan., 1910; sec., central quarantine authority for the W. Indies.

KING, SYDNEY NOEL.—B. 1897; ed. Dover Coll. and Bedford Schl.; served in R.F.A., Jan., 1916-Jan., 1920 (coman., Aug., 1916); cadet, S. Stilts, Mar., 1920; ag. ast. to senr. dist. offr., Butterworth, Prov. Wellesley, Dec., 1920.

KING, THOS. HENRY.—B. 1883; ed. at Exeter Gram. Sch.; pol. probationer, Hong Kong, 1904; passed in Cantonese, 1907; attchd. to Punjab pol., 1907; passed in Hindustani, 1907; ag. ast. supt. of pol., 1908; J.P., 1908; ag. supt. of imports and exports, Dec., 1909; ast. emigr. offr. in addition to pol. duties, 1910; passed in Punjabi, 1910; ag. ast.-supt. of police, 1911; mem., bd. of examrs., 1912; ast. supt. of police, 1912; ag. dep. supt. of police and fire brigade, 1913; dep. supt., pol., 1921; ag. capt. supt., pol., 1921 and June-July, 1925; dir., criminal intelligence, 1923.

KING, Rt. Hon. WILLIAM LYON MACKENZIE, P.C. (1922), C.M.G. (1906), M.A., Toronto Univ., LL.B., Toronto Univ., Ph.D., Harvard Univ., B.C. 1874; apptd. dep. min. of labour and editor of "Labour Gazette," 1902; reglar. of boards of conciliation and investigation, 1907; sworn of the Privy Council for Canada and min. of labour, 1909; defeated at g.e., 1911; special investigator, Rockefeller Foundation, 1914; chosen leader of the opposition, H. of C., Aug., 1919; elec. by acclamation for Prince Co., P.E.I., 20th Oct., 1919; re-elec. for N. York, Ont., g.e., Dec., 1921; prime minister, Dominion of Canada, 29th Dec., 1921, also pres. of the coun. and sec. of state for external affairs; represented Canada at Imp. Confex., 1923; prime min., Sept., 1926.

KING-BULL, ERIC JAMES.—B. 1897; ed. King's Schl., Ely and privately; ent. R. Navy, Jan., 1915; served in Grand Fleet, Mediterranean, W. Coast of Africa and Flying Squadron (H.M.S. "Furious"), Jan., 1915-Nov., 1918; joined S. America Squadron, Mar., 1919; staff of commdr. in ch., S. America, May, 1919-Mar., 1920; ret. with rank of paymr. lieut., Feb., 1921; mem., Gray's Inn, May, 1921; cadet, Malay civ. serv., Nov., 1921; passed cadet, Jan., 1923; ag. ast. dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, Apr., 1923; ag. dist. offr., Bentong, Mar., 1924; ast. dis. offr., Gopang and Kampar, Jan., 1926.

KINGDON, DONALD, M.A., LL.B.—Ed. at Eastbourne Coll. and St. John's Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, In. Tom., July, 1906; legal ast. and inspr. of schls., Gambia, Jan., 1907; mem. of exec. and legis. couns.; ag. col. sec., Mar. to July, 1912; atty.-gen., Uganda, Oct., 1912; ag. chief just., Apl., 1917, to July, 1918; atty.-gen., G. Coast, Sept., 1918; mem. of exec. and legis. coun.; ag. col. sec., Apl. to Oct., 1919; compiler of 1920 revd. edn., G. Coast laws; atty. gen., Nigeria, Apr., 1921; compiler, revd. edn., Nigeria laws, 1923.

KING-HARMAN, SIR CHARLES ANTHONY, K.C.M.G. (1900), C.M.G. (1893).—B. 1851; ed. Chelt. Coll.; B.A., of Trin. Coll., Camb., 1872; M.A., 1873; priv. sec. to the gov. of the Bahamas, Nov., 1874, to Nov., 1879; priv. sec. to high commnr., Cyprus, Dec., 1879; ast. commnr., Cyprus, Dec., 1879; ast. to the ch. sec., Cyprus, Mar., 1881; ag. ch. sec. and mem. of exec. and legis. coun., June to Sept., 1882; auditor-gen., Barbados, 1883; elected mem. of house of assem., 1884-93; ag. col. sec. and mem. of exec. coun., 1884, 1885 and 1886; col. sec., Mauritius, 1893; ag. gov., Jan. to Aug., 1894, and Mar. to Sept., 1896; admintr., St. Lucia, 1897; gov. S. Leone, Oct., 1900; high commnr., Cyprus, 15th Apr., 1904; represented Mediterranean Colonies at the Coronation of H.M. King George V., 1911.

KINGSBURY, ALLAN NEAVE, B.Sc. (Eng.), M.B., B.S. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P., D.P.H., D.T.M. & H. (Lond.)—Sometime ast. pathologist, Middlesex Hosp. and bacteriologist, Middlesex Hosp., venereal diseases clinic; pathologist, City of Lond. Maternity Hosp.; lieut., R.F.A.; ag. govt. pathologist, Penang, 1923; pathologist, Inst. for Med. Research, Kuala Lumpur, 1924; on sp. duty in India and Ceylon, 1924; ag. bacteriologist, Inst. for Med. Research and ag. dir., govt. laboratories, F.M.S., 1925; professor of bacteriology, King Ed. VII. Coll. of Medicine, Singapore, 1926; author of many medical and scientific publications.

KINGSTON, THOMAS WALTER HENRY.—B. 1887; ed. Wesley Coll., Dublin, and Trinity Coll., Dublin (math. scholarship, jun. moderatorship, classics); cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1910; ag. ast. prot. of Chinese, Sel. and Negri Sembilan, Sept., 1913; passed cadet, July, 1914; offr., cl.v., July, 1914; ast. contr. of lab., Kuala Lumpur, Feb., 1915; prot. of Chinese lab. at Tavoy, Burma, Mar. to Dec., 1917; ag. prot. of Chinese, Perak, Jan., 1918; offr., clv., Jan., 1919; ag. sec. for Chinese affrs., F.M.S., Sept., 1920 to Mar., 1921; ag. prot. of Chinese, Selangor and Negri Sembilan, in addn., Sept. to Oct., 1920; ag. prot., Chinese, Selangor and Penang, Feb., 1922; supernumy. offr., cl. III., Nov., 1922; ag. prot., Chinese, Kedah, Dec., 1922; ag. ast. adviser, Kedah, May, 1923; prot., Chinese, Kedah, Jan., 1924.

KINSEY, WILLIAM EDWARD.—B. 1876; inspr. of mines, Negri Sembilan, June, 1902; sttlmt. offr., Oct., 1902; ast. conservr. of forests, 2nd grade, Nov., 1905; ast. conservr. of forests, 1st grade, June, 1908; ag. dep. conservr. of forests, grade II, Nov., 1909 to Feb., 1910 and June, 1910; dep. conservr. of forests, Jan., 1911.

KINTORE, EARL OF, SIR ALGERNON HAWKINS THOMOD KEITH-FALCONER, P.C., G.C.M.G. (1889).—B. 1852; lord in waiting, 1885; capt. of yeomen of the guard, 1886; privy councillor, 1886; gov., S. Australia, 1889-96; a lord in waiting, 1896-1905.
KIRBY, A. C.—Ast. dist. commnr., R.A.P., Dec., 1913; 2nd grade res., Nyassaland, Jan., 1926; 1st grade res., Mar., 1925.

KIRBY, AUSTIN HENRY, B.A. (Cantab.), O.B.E. (1926).—B. 1879; lecturer in science, Antigua, under the Imperial dept. of agric., 1903 to 1909; scientific ast. in the Imperial dept. of agric. and in charge of publications, 1909 to 1913; ast. director of agric., Southern Prove., Nigeria, 1913; dir. of agr., Tanganyika Territory, 1921; author of publications on tropical agric., educn. and tropical hygiene.

KIRKHAM, G. H.—B. 1881; S. Africa constab., 1901; served S. African War, 1901-02 (wounded); Transvaal pol., 1908; adjt., 4th S. African Mounted Riflemen, 1914; S. African Rebellion, 1914; capt., 1st S. African Inf. (Overseas Brig.) 1916-18; German W. Africa campaign, 1914-16; ment. in desps., France, 1916-17 (twice wounded); M.C., Apr., 1917; ast. commnr., pol. and prisons, Tanganyika Territory, 1918.

KIRKHAM, V. H.—B. 1881; ed. Portsmouth Grammar Schl., Munic. Coll., Portsmouth and Schl. of Agr., Camb. Univ., B.Sc. (Lond.), 1903; A.I.C., 1905; F.I.C., 1908; dip. agr. (Camb.), 1910; govt. analyst, E. Africa Prot., 1911; dir. chem. research, Kenya, 1919; dir., agr., Zanzibar, 1923 and govt. chem., 1926; off. mem., leg. com., 1926.

KIRKPATRICK, IVONE.—Cadet Sarawak civ. ser., Limbang, Aug., 1892; extra offr., Simanggang, Jan., 1893; res. 2nd cls., Mar., 1898, of Upper Rejang, May, 1899; res. 2nd cls., Sadong, Feb., 1901; Kapit, 1905.
KIRSOFF, GEORGE DOUGAL.—Asst. collr. cust., E.A. Prot., Jan., 1914; collr. cust., Kenya Col., Jan., 1920; ch. of cust., Zanzibar, Mar., 1923.
KIRWAN, ARTHUR CONINGSBY—Jun. clk., col. sec.'s off., Falkland Is., 1st Jan., 1907.
KITCHING, A. E.—Asst. dist. commr., E.A.P., Apr., 1914; admstve. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 19th Nov., 1919; 1st grade admstve. offr., 4th June, 1926.

KITTERMMASTER, H. B., C.M.G. (1926), O.B.K. (1918).—Ed. Shrewsbury and Christ Ch., Oxford; Transvaal educn. dept., 1902-07; asst. dist. commr., E.A.P., 18th Dec., 1908; dist. commr., 1915; offr. in charge, N.F.D., 1916; secy. to admstrm., Somaliland, Aug., 1921; gov. and commdr.-in-chief, Somaliland Prot., 1926.

KNAGGS, HARRY LESSLIE, O.B.E. (1924).—B. 1867; clk., San Fernando Hosp., Trinidad, Apr., 1885; 4th clk. col. sec.'s office, Oct., 1885; 3rd clk., July, 1892; 2nd clk., Nov., 1900; 3rd inspr. of immigrts., Aug., 1897, to Nov., 1900; asst. asst. col. sec., 1896, 1899, 1900, 1906 and 1907; asst. col. sec., 1st to 7th May, 1907 and 11th July to 22nd Aug., 1924; sec. to civ. ser. comm. and to rly. and roads comm., 1894; ch. clk., col. sec.'s off., and supt. of govt. printing, Apr., 1906; asst. asst. col. sec. on various occasions, 1908-18; asst. col. sec., 17th Jan., 1920.

KNAPMAN, GEORGE WILLIAM.—S.A. War; Transvaal civ. serv., 1900; ch. clk., land dept., E.A.P., 1909; office supt., secretariat, E.A.P., 1911; lieut., K.A.R., 1917-19; estab. offr., secretariat, Kenya, 1923; Queen's S.A. med., Br. War and Victory meds.

KNIBBS, STANLEY GEORGE CURTHOYS, F.R.G.S.—B. 1886; ed. Sydney Gram. Schl., New South Wales; dipl. in all subjects, Hawkesbury Agrl. Coll., 1908; land survr., Fiji, 1911; land survr., Victoria and N.S.W., 1912; crown survr., Br. Solomon Is. Prot., 1913; ag. supt., pub. wks. in addn., 1918; ag. dist. offr., Shortlands during 1919; regsr. of land titles in addn., 1919; dep. commr. for W. Pacific, 1919; dep. for natives in lands commr., 1920 and 1921; ag. res. commr. during 1920; commr. of currency (temp.), 1920-24; qualified as civ. engrnr. to shires and municipalities of N.S.W., 1923; commr. of lands and crown survr., Br. Solomon Is. Prot., 1924.

KNIGHT, WALTER AUGUSTUS.—Copyist, G.P.O., Grenada, May, 1903; 2nd clk., G.P.O., June, 1904; clk. to supt. of wks., Jan., 1906; clk. to pol. mag., South Dist., May, 1907; rev. offr. and road survr., Western Dist., Jan., 1909; addtnl. coroner, St. Mark's parish, Jan, 1913; road survr., Northern Dist., Dominica, Dec., 1914.
KNIGHT-BRUCE, G. K.—Asst. dist. commr., E.A.P., Dec., 1913; mag., Uganda, 1925; mag., Zanzibar, 1925.

KNOLLYS, WILFRED ESKINE.—Ed. King's Schl., Bruton, and Worcester Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Hons.), 1905; clk., colonial audit branch, E. and A. dept., 9th Oct., 1906; asst. auditor, E. Africa and Uganda Prots., 8th Nov., 1907; asst. auditor, Uganda Prot., 27th Aug., 1909; senr. asst. auditor, E. Africa Prot., 10th June, 1915; sen. asst. audr.in-charge, German E. Africa prov. admstn., 24th April, 1917; audr., Tanganyika Territory, 1st Oct., 1919.

KNOWLES, CHARLES HENRY, B.Sc. (Lond.)—B. 1878; ed. Burton-on-Trent gram. schl. and Mason Univ. Coll., Birmingham; res. master, agric. schl., St. Vincent, Jan., 1902; supt. of agric., Fiji, 1906; M.L.C., 1912; chmn., agric. experiments comttee., 1916; chmn. food supply comttee., 1917; ag. govt. entomologist (conjoint), 1917 and 1919-21; J.P., 1917; M.E.C. (temp.), June, 1920; prov. supt., agric., G. Coast, May, 1921; dep. dir., agr., Aug., 1924; dir., agr., Dec., 1924.
KNOWLES, JOHN WILLIAM FREDERICK.—B. 1871; joined Trinidad govt. serv., 1898; warden, counties Nariva-Mayaro, 16th June, 1922; warden, St. Andrew and St. David, 1st Dec., 1925.
KNOX, RT. HON. SIR ADRIAN, P.C. (1920), K.C.M.G. (1921), C.M.G. (1918), K.C. (1906), LL.B.—Ed. at Cambridge; chief just., high ct., Commonwealth of Australia, 1919.

KOTZE, HON. SIR JOHN GILBERT, KT. BACH. (1917), K.C., LL.B. (LOND.).—B. 1849; ed. South African Coll. and Lond. Univ.; called to the bar, Inner Temp., 1874; sole judge of the high court of the Transvaal, 1877 to 1881; one of the commrs. under the convention of Pretoria to settle claims for losses and injuries suffered during the Boer War of 1880-81; chief justice of the Transvaal, 1881-1898; chmn. of bd. of examrs. in literature and science, Transvaal, 1890-1898; recd. the Portuguese honour of Knight Grand Cross of the Order of the Conception, 1896; atty.-gen. and member of exec. and legis. coun., S. Rhodesia, 1900; ag. admrstr. S. Rhodesia, 1902; judge of sup. ct., Cape of Good Hope, 1903, and judge pres., Eastern dists. ct., 1904; chmn. of the coun. of Rhodes Univ. Coll.; examr. in law and jurisprudence to the Univ. of C. of G. H., 1903-1908; puisne judge, C. of G.H. Prov. Div., 1st Apr., 1913; judge, president, 14th June, 1920; judge of appeal, appellate divn., June, 1922.

KOTZE, SIR ROBT. NELSON, KT. (BACH.), B.A.—B. 1870; ed. at S. Africa Coll., and R. Schl. of Mines, Clausthal, Germany; gold med., S.A. Coll. and of Chem. Met. and Mining Soc. of S.A.; asst. engnr. and consulting engnr. to Transvaal Gold Fields Ltd., and subsidiary coys., 1896-1907; govt. mining engnr., Transvaal, 1st Jan., 1908; ditto, Union of S. Africa, 1910; vice-chancellor, Univ. of Witwatersrand; ret., 1st Nov., 1926.

KRAUSE, FREDERICK EDWARD TRAUGOTT.—B. 1868; ed. Grey Coll., Bloemfontein, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, Amsterdam, London, Cambridge Univ.; B.A. (Hons.), Cape, 1888; LL.D., Amsterdam, 1893; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1893; LL.D., Cape, 1905; advoc., sup. ct., S. Africa and high ct., Rhodesia; state prosecutor and ag. atty.-gen., Witwatersrand, 1896-1900; sp. commdt. and mil. govt., Witwatersrand, 1900; chmn., mining regs. commn., 1907-9; mem., Johannesburg town coun., 1905-9; M.L.A., Vrededorp, 1907-10; chmn., hosp. inquiry comm., Johannesburg; mem., prop. representation commn.; mem., Witwatersrand cent. schl. bd.; K.C. (Union of S. Africa), 1912; ag. judge, Transvaal prov. divn., 1914; judge, sup. ct., Transvaal prov. divn., 1st Apr., 1923.

KRIGE, HON. CHRISTIAN JOEL, M.L.A.—B. 1868; intern. B.A.; atty.-at-law; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; represented Caledon in parliament since 1904; speaker, H. of A., Union of S. Africa, 1915-24; ah. whip, S.A. party for several years prior to elec. as speaker.

KUPFERBURGER, JOHANN FRIEDRICH WILHELM.—B. 1866; ed. Stellenbosch, C.C., Univ. of the C. of G.H. (B.A.), Edin. (B.Sc.), and Zurich; inspr. of schls., O.F.S., Sept., 1895, to Oct., 1899; re-apptd. O.R.C., July, 1909.
KUSCHKE, HERMANN F.G., B.A.—B. 1891; ed. Johannesburg and Pretoria; head, normal sec., Stoefberg Gedenk Schi., 1916-24; organizing inspr., native educn., O.F.S., 1924.

LABATT, JOHN BAGOT, M.L.C.E.—B. 1861; draftsman and engineering asst., engnr.-in-chief's dept., S. Australia, 1881; asst. engnr., harbours and jetties, 1906; deputy chmn., S. Aust. harbours bd., 1914.

LABORDE, ARTHUR LIONEL CRICHTON.—B. 1876; supernumerary, col. sec.'s off., Barbados, 3rd Jan., 1898; asctd as clk. to gov. in ex. comte. and to the exec. coun., May to Oct., 1896; ch. clk., col. sec.'s off., Lagos, Apr., 1897; ag. ch. registr., July to Aug., 1901; ag. col. postmr., 29th Aug., 1901, to 31st Mar., 1902, confirmed in the appt.; ag. asst. col. sec., Aug., 1905, to Apr., 1906; dist. comanr., S. Nigeria, 1st May, 1906; ag. provl. sec., Cent. Prov., May to June, 1907.

LABORDE, EDWARD DANIEL, C.B.E. (1922), I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1863; 1st clk., audit office, Tobago, Jan., 1882; dep. regsr., ag. confidential clk. to administrator, and clk. of couns., June, 1882; 2nd clk., administrator's office, St. Lucia, Feb., 1883; ch. clk., Sept., 1883; ag. 2nd clk., gov.-in-chief's office, Barbados, 1884; ch. clk. to gov., Windward Is., Grenada, 1885; priv. sec. to Gov. Sendall, 1886; ch. of pol. and excise, St. Vincent, 1889; inspr. of prisons, 1889; supervisor of cust. and port offr., 1897; in charge of relief work after hurricanes of 1898; specially mentioned in despatches and thanked by S. of S. for services; chmn. town bd., 1899-1901; mem. ex. and legis. couns.; ag. col. sec., Apr. to May, 1901; ch. of pol. and inspr. of prisons, St. Lucia, 1902; has served on several comans. of enquiry in different colonies; ag. admstr., St. Vincent, June to Dec., 1904; ag. admstr., and col. sec., St. Lucia, July to Nov., 1905; ag. col. sec., Oct., 1906, to Jan., 1907; ag. admstr. and col. sec., May to Nov. 1907; treas., St. Lucia, 1912; col. treas., Grenada, 1915; ag. col. sec., Grenada, for various periods, 1915-20; admstr., govt., Sept.-Oct., 1915, and Sept. to Oct., 1920.
LACY, ALFRED TRAVERS.—B. 1892; ed. King Edward VI Gram. Schi., Norwich and King's Coll., Cambridge; class. tripes; hons. degree, M.A.; supt., educn., Tanganyika Territory, 1924.

LAERLA, CAPT. ALBERT V., LL.D.—B. 1887; ed. Flores's Coll., Malta; matric., Malta Univ., 1903; grad. in law, 1910; lieut., King's Own Malta Regt. of Militia, 1903; capt., 1911; demob., 1920; dir., elementary schls., Malta, Aug., 1920.

LAFFER, HON. GEORGE RICHARDS.—M.P. for Alexandra, S. Australia; sometime chmn. of comtees.; comanr. of crown lands and imigrn. and min. of repatriation, 8th Apr., 1920; comanr. of crown lands and imigrn. and min. of irrigation and repatriation, 1922-24.
LA FONTAINE, S. H., D.S.O., M.C. (1916).—Asst. dist. comanr., E.A.P., 1910; district comanr., Kenya, Oct., 1918.

LAGDEN, SIR GODFREY YEATMAN, K.C.M.G. (1897), C.M.G. (1894).—B. 1861; clerk, G.P.O., London, 1869-77, after compet. exam.; clk. to sec. to govt., Transvaal, 1878; priv. sec. to admstr., 1878-81; sec. to exec. coun., 1880-81; priv. sec. to Sir W. Bellairs and Sir E. Wood, while administering govt., 1881; sec. to Transvaal sub-coms. for compensation claims, 1881-82; engaged as war correspondent, Egyptian campaign, 1882-3, present at all engagements, including Tel-el-Kebir and capture of Cairo; on special financial duty, G. Coast, 1883; sec. and acctnt., Basutoland, 1884; ast. comanr., 1886; established magistracy in Masupha's country, 1886; acted as res. comanr., May to Nov., 1890; ag. Br. comanr., Swaziland govt. comtee. of adminstr., Apr., 1892; sec. comanr., Basutoland, 1893-1901; comanr. for native affairs, Transvaal, Aug., 1901; mem. of ex. and leg. couns.; chmn. of S. African intercol. native affairs coms., 1903-4; retired, 1907.

LAING, R. H. BEETSON.—B. 1873; served in Montmorency's Scouts, S. Africa, 1900; sup't Transvaal constab., 1901; invalided; staff apptmt., I.Y. headqrs., Oct., 1901; returned to S. Africa, Dec., 1901; resig. comanr. Jan. 1905; medal with 5 clasps; apptd. to N. Nigeria constab., Nov., 1903; ag. staff offr., Aug. to Oct., 1906; ag. cantonment mag., Nov. 1906 to Jan., 1907; transf. to political dept. as asst. res., Feb., 1907; 3rd cls. res., 1st Oct., 1908; res. in charge, Zaria Prov., Nov., 1911 to July, 1912.

LAING, MALCOLM BUCHANAN.—B. 1890; ed. at Forest Schi., Essex; clorial ast., treas., B. Guiana, 1905; ast. commissary, commissaries dept., Oct., 1909; seconded as chief clk., imigrn. dept., Apr., 1913; passed govt. exam. in Hindi, Nov., 1913; 2nd cls. clk., imigrn. dept., July, 1914; mem. bd. of examra. in Hindi, etc., Aug., 1914; 2nd lieut., B. Guiana Artillery (Militia), Mar., 1915; ag. imigrn. agt., Dec., 1915 to Feb., 1916.
LAMACQ, FRANK CONSTANT.—B. 1903; apptd. cler. offr., London telephone service, May, 1921; transf. to C.O., July, 1923.
LAMB, BENJAMIN.—Lieut., R.F.A.; served in S. African war, Jan., 1900, to Aug., 1902; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to gov., Barbados, Oct., 1902, to Aug., 1903.
LAMB, F. M.—Senior staff survr., E.A.P., May, 1907; ast. dist. comanr., Uganda Prot., Feb., 1910; ast. dist. comanr., E.A.P., Jan., 1911; dist. comanr., Jan., 1918.

LAMB, PEBBY HUTCHINSON.—Ed. at Yorkshire Coll., Leeds; engaged in land reclamation and agriculture in Egypt, 1906-1908; studied cotton cultivation in United States on behalf of govt. of Uganda, 1909; ag. head of agric. dept., Uganda, 1909; dir. of agric., Uganda, Nov., 1911; dir. of agric., Northern Provs., Nigeria, since July, 1912; visited West Indies on behalf of govt. of Nigeria, 1913.

LAMBERT, C. E.—B. 1900; on mil. serr., May, 1918 to Nov., 1919; temp. clk., const., Dec., 1919 to Jan., 1920; R.I.C., Feb., 1920 to Feb., 1921; temp. clk., Min. of Lab., Apr., 1921 to 7th July, 1923; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 9th July, 1923.

LAMBERT, SIR HENRY CHARLES MILLER, K.C.M.G. (1919); C.B. (1910).—B. 1868; ed. at Eton Coll., and New Coll., Oxford; 1st cls. in classical mods., 1889; 1st cls. in final classical schls., 1891; apptd. 2nd cls. clk. in the C.O. after open compet. exam., Feb., 1892; mem. of comtee., emigrts. information office, 1894; chmn. of the comtee., 1897-1907; acted as priv. sec. to Mr. Chamberlain for the purposes of the comtee. of the H. of C. on South Africa, 1897; 1st cls. clk., Nov., 1898; visited Canada, 1903; visited Australia, on behalf of E.I.O., in connection with emigration questions, 1906; prin. clk., Jan., 1907; acting officer, C.O.; ast. under-sec. of state, 4th June, 1916; sec. to the Impl. Confoe; senr. crown agt. for the colo., 1st Dec., 1921; ag. under sec. of state for the colo., 30th July, 1924 to 4th Aug., 1925.
LAMBERT, H. E.—Tempy. ast. dist. comntr., R. Africa Prot., Apr., 1919.
LAMBERT, R. W.—Ast. auditor, E.A.F., Mar., 1912; ast. dist. comntr., May, 1914; 1st grade admstive offr., Kenya, 1925; ag. senr. ast. sec., 1925.

LAMBORN, WILLIAM ALFRED STEDDWEIL, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P.—B. 1877; ed. Oxford Preparatory Sch., King's Sch., Canterbury, and Middlesex Hosp.; ast. med. offr., St. Mary's Infirmary, London, 1899 to 1905; ast. med. offr., British Guiana, 1905; ast. med. offr., Oxford County Asylum, 1906; med. offr., Nigeria Bitumen Co., 1908; govt. entomologist, S. Nigeria, 1913; entomologist, Imp. Bureau, 1914; temp. med. offr., Tanganyika, 1917; med. offr., Nyasaland, 1919; ast. med. entomologist, F.M.S., 1920; med. entomologist, Nyasaland, 2nd Dec., 1921.

LAMINGTON, LORD (2nd Baron, creat. 1880), SIR CHARLES WALLACE ALEXANDER NAPIER COCHRANE-BALLIE, G.C.I.E. (1903), G.C.M.G. (1900), K.C.M.G. (1896).—Only son of 1st baron; born, 1860; dep. lieut. Lanarkshire; lieut. Lancashire yeomanry, 1883; M.P. for N. St. Paulos, 1886-90; gov. Queensland, 1896-1901; gov. of Bombay, 1903-7.
LAMPSON, P. E.—Ed. Wesleyan mission schl., Anamaboe, G. Coast; ast. master, govt. schl., Accra, 1893.

LANCLOT, CHARLES, K.C.—B. 1864; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Montreal, and Laval Univ.; private tutor; called to the bar 1885; K.C., 1899; special law offr., Quebec, 1891; dep. atty.-gen., Quebec, 1905; mem. of comntr. for revision of code of civil proceed., 1893-97; comntr. for revision of statutes of Quebec, 1909-10; published "Traité théorique et pratique du droit criminel"; "Manuel du juge de paix"; "Annotated Criminal Code."

LANE, CHARLTON ADELBERT GUSTAVUS.—B. 1890; ed. Winchester and Univ. Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Hons.), 1911; ast. dist. comntr., R.A.P., 1913; ag. dist. comntr., 1918; addl. secretariat, 1918; ag. senr. ast. sec., 1919; senr. ast. sec., 1920; dist. comntr., Zanzibar, 1923; mag., Tanganjika Territory, 1925.

LANG, HON. SIR FREDERIC WILLIAM, KT. BACH. (1916).—B. 1852; mem. of H. of R., New Zealand, 1893-1905 and 1906-22; chmn. of comtecs., 2nd Aug., 1912 to 26th June, 1913; speaker from 26th June, 1913 to 1922; M.I.C., 1924.
LANG, HON. JOHN THOMAS.—M.L.A. of New South Wales for Parramatta; treas. in Storey and Dooley govt.; premier, N.S.W., 1925.
LANGE, ANDRE PHILIPPE, M.R.C.S., Eng.—B. 1863; entd. med. serv., Trinidad, 21st Jan., 1890; dist. med. offr., 15th July, 1915.

LANGHAM-CARTER, W.—Ed. at Bradfield Coll., Berks.; cadet, S. Stittsley, 1890; passed final exam. in Malay, Dec., 1892; ag. dist. offr., Malacca, Jan., 1896; dist. offr., prov. Wellesley, Apr., 1897; continued to act at Malacca; ag. collr. of ld. rev. and regist. of deeds, Singapore, Dec., 1898; impr. prisons, S.S., Jan. to May, 1901; ast. P.M.G., Penang, Dec., 1901; 2nd magis., Sing., Aug., 1902; ag. collr. of land rev. and regist. of deeds, Sing., Sept., 1902; apptd. to cls. III, B, Jan., 1903; 2nd mag., Sing., Jan., 1905, but cont. to act as collr. of land rev.; ag. sen. dis. offr., Prov. Wellesley, Apr., 1907; senr. dist. offr., Prov. Wellesley, April, 1911; British adviser, Kelantan, Dec., 1912; ag. dist. judge and 1st mag., Singapore, May, 1916; offr., cls. I., Oct., 1919; ag. res. councillor, Malacca, 15th Mar., 1922.

ANGLEY, EDWARD RALPH.—B. 1889; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Cairns Coll., Cambridge; B.A. (Cantab); war service, E. Africa 1917-18; passed lower stand. exam. in Temne; ast. dist. comntr., Sierra Leone, 29th Apr., 1914; ag. ast. col. sec. in 1916 and 1918; ag. senr. ast. col. sec., May to Oct., 1919; dist. comntr., 21st Aug., 1922.

LANGSTON, STEPHEN HORATIO.—B. 1877; ed. Westminster Schol. and Keble Coll., Oxon.; B.A.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1900; ag. ast. dist. offr., Kuala Selangor, Jan., 1902; passed cadet, May, 1903; ag. ast. dist. offr., Kuala Kubu, Dec., 1903; ag. ast. dist. offr., Serendah, May, 1904; ag. ast. dist. treas., K. Lumpur, Dec., 1904; ag. dist. treas., Seremban, Mar., 1906; 2nd ast. dist. offr., Tapah, June, 1906; ag. dist. treas., Raub, Aug., 1905, and July, 1906; offr., cls. V, Jan., 1906; ag. ast. dist. offr., Raub, Mar., 1906; ag. ast. collr. of land rev., Seremban, Aug., 1906-Feb., 1908; ag. ast. dist. offr., Ipoh, May, 1909; ag. 2nd ast. dist. offr., K. Kangsar, Jan., 1910; ag. dist. offr., K. Langat, June, 1911; offr., cls. IV, Jan., 1912; dist. offr., coast, Neg. Semb., Apr., 1912-Apr., 1913; dist. offr., Tampin, Apr., 1914; censor, Penang, S.S., June, 1916; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, Oct., 1916; ast. dist. judge, Penang, Oct., 1916; ag. ast. regitr. of impts. and expts., in addn., Aug., 1917; offr., cls. III, Jan., 1918; offr., cls. II, Jan., 1919; dist. offr., Batang Padang, Aug., 1920; mag., K. Lumpur, Sept., 1920 and June, 1921; ag. collr. of land rev., K. Lumpur, Dec., 1920; ag. temp. as 2nd mag., K. Lumpur, in addn., May, 1921; ag. collr., land rev., K. Lumpur, Sept., 1921; on sp. duty as town planning admtr., Apr., 1922; valuer-gen., Jan., 1923; town-planning admtr., Ipoh, Dec., 1924; ag. dist. offr., Lower Perak, Mar., 1926.

LANKTREE, CHARLES JOSEPH DANE, Barrister-at-Law, Kings Inns, Dublin.—B. 1896; on mil. serv., Sept. 1914; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., October, 1921; attd. Colombo Kachcheri, Nov., 1921; ag. office astt. to govt. ast., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Oct., 1922; office astt. to govt. ast., Cent. Prov., Mar., 1924; ditto to dir. of med. and army services, Oct., 1926.

LANSDOWN, CHARLES WILLIAM HENRY, B.A., LL.B.—Ck., C.G.R., Mar., 1889; ck., C.S.O., Capetown, Oct., 1893; atty.-gen.'s off., Nov., 1894; mag.'s ck., Aliwal N., Feb., 1898; pte. sec. to atty.-gen., Aug., 1898; ag. chf. ck. to sol.-gen., Grahamstown, May, 1901; ag. ast. mag., Port Elizabeth, Sept., 1901; ch. ck. and pte. sec. to atty.-gen., Oct., 1901; addnl. legal advwr., July, 1907; prof. ast., atty.-gen.'s off., Capetown, Apr., 1912; law advwr., dep. of justice, Union of S. Africa, Aug., 1916; atty.-gen., Cape Prov., Dec., 1926.

LANSDOWNE, 5TH MARQUIS OF (Gt. Brit., creat. 1784), K.G., P.C., G.C.M.G. (1884), G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E.; Rt. Hon. Henry Charles Keith Fitz Maurice, eldest son of the 4th Marquis.—B. 1846; succeeded his father in 1866; was under-sec. for war from Apr., 1872, to Feb., 1874, and under-sec. India Office, May to Aug., 1880; gov. gen. of Canada, 1883; viceroy of India, Nov., 1888-94; sec. of state for war, 1895-1900; sec. of state for foreign affairs, 1900-1906; chancellor of the Order of St. Michael and St. George, 1917.

LAPOINTE, HON. ERNEST.—B. 1876; ed. Rimouski Coll. and Laval Univ., Quebec (B.A., LL.B.); advoc., K.C., Quebec, 1908; el. by accl. for Kamouraska to H.C., 12th Feb., 1904; re-el. g.e., 1904, 1908, 1911 and 1917; el. at bye-el. for Quebec E., 27th Oct., 1919; re-el. g.e., 1921; min. of marine and fisheries in King admin., 29th Dec., 1921; del. to League of Nations assem., 1922; min. of just. and atty.-gen., 1924.

LASCHELLES, SIR ALFRED GEORGE, KT. BACH.—(1913), B.A.—Univ. Coll., Oxon; 2nd class mod. hist.; b. 1857; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1885; acted on several occasions as pres. of dist. cts. in Cyprus and as puisne judge; pres. of dist. ct. Papho, 1892; ditto, Larnaca, 1893; ag. puisne judge, June, 1896; ag. Queen's advoc., Feb., 1896; pres. dist. ct., Nicosia, 1896; Queen's advoc., 1898; atty.-gen. of Ceylon, 1902; ag. ch. just., Mar., 1906; atty.-gen., Oct., 1906; chief just., May, 1911; retired, 1914.

LASSALLE, CHARLES FRANCIS, M.D., Edin., M.B.C.M. (Hons.), Edin., D.P.H., Oxford.—B. 1871; ed. Univ. of Edinburgh; postgraduate work at London schl. of trop. med., 1902; the Polyclinic, London, 1902; Univ. Coll., London, 1902; Lister inst. of Preventive Medicine, 1912; mem. of B.M.A.; mem. of coun. of med. bd., Trinidad and Tobago; apptd. govt. med. offr., Trinidad, 3rd Nov., 1894; port health offr. and med.inspr. of immigrants, Trinidad, 1st Jan., 1910; asst. med. offr., inspr. of health, 7th Dec., 1920; has acted as asst. M.O.H., res. surg. at colonial hosp., and also as D.M.O. at Cedros, Naparima, St. Joseph, Arima, Chaguanas and Princes Town; author of "Notes of lectures on Midwifery for Midwives," and contributions to med. journals.
LAST, JOSEPH SPURRIER.—B. 1897; on active serv., Egypt and Palestine, 1915-1917; asst. dist. comsnr., Zanzibar, 3rd Dec., 1919.

LATCHFORD, HON. FRANCIS ROBERT, B.A., K.C.—B. 1866; ed. Ottawa Univ.; called to the bar, 1886; K.C., 1888; elec. to Ontario legis., 1899; re-elec., 1902; min. of pub. wks., Ontario, 1899; atty.-gen., 1904-5; judge, chancery divn., high ct. of just., Ontario, 1908; ch. just., 2nd divn.l ct., sup. ct., 1923.

LATREILLE, HERBERT ROWLAND.—B. 1884; cler. asst., col. sec.'s office, Jamaica, 1st July, 1901; asst., June, 1905; 2nd cls. elk., Oct., 1906; 1st cls. elk., Apr., 1917; ag. sen. elk., Aug., 1918 to June, 1919; sen. asst. treas., Tanganyika Territory, June, 1919; ag. dep. treas., Dec., 1921 to Mar., 1922, Dec., 1922 to Sept., 1923 and from Mar. to Oct., 1924.

LAUDER, JOHN ILES.—2nd elk., regir. and prov.-marshal's offr., Dominica, Jan., 1901; ag. asst. mast., Dominica gram. schl., 8th May to 4th Aug., 1905; govt. offr., treasy., Sept., 1905; elk. to comsnr. and elk. of couna., Montserrat, Oct., 1906; educnl. dist. offr., Aug., 1907; deputy coroner, Dist. "I," Mar., 1911; mem. of road bd., Apr., 1911; ag. cur.ator, botanic station, 20th Jan. to 13th Feb., 1912; J.P., Mar., 1912; ag. mag., Dist. "I," 10th to 21st Nov., 1911 and 14th to 26th May, 1912; ag. offr. in charge of prison discipline, 2nd May to 22nd June, 1912; hon. sec. and mem. of pub. lib. comtee., Apr., 1908, to June, 1912; mem. of Montserrat defence reserve, Nov., 1906, to June, 1912; asst. reo-gen., Gambia, 23rd June, 1912; ag. reo-gen., on various occasions, 1912-17; ag. master of govt. vessels from Oct., 1912 to Oct., 1913; provisional mem. exec. and legis. couna., on various occasions, 1912-18; mem. of navigation and pilotage bd., 25th May, 1916; ag. curator of intestate estates, June to Oct., 1914; offr.-in-charge, secretariat, on various occasions, 1916-18; ag. asst. col. sec., Feb. to June, 1918, and from Dec., 1918 to Feb., 1919; food cont., June to Dec., 1918; dep. compt. of customs, G. Coast, 28th May, 1919; ag. compt., June to Dec., 1919, and Jan. to June, 1920; contrlr. of enemy property, July, 1919 to Feb., 1920 and from Feb. to Aug., 1921; comptlr., cust., Sierra Leone, Oct., 1922; ag. col. treas., 4th Feb. to 29th Apr., 1925; ag. col. sec., 29th Sept. to 12th Oct., 1926; gov.'s dep., lat. to 8th Oct., 1926.
LAURENCE, C.G. W.—B. 1902; apptd., after compet. exam., aler. offr., C.O., 4th May, 1920.

LAURENCE, HON. SIR PERCIVAL MATTLAND, K.C.M.G. (1911); KT. BACH. (1908).—Ed. at Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge (B.A. 1876; LL.M. 1879; LL.D. 1884); called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1878; admitted to the bar of the sup. ot., Cape Col., 1880; examr. in lit. at Cape Univ. in 1881 and 1886-1889; raised to the bench of the sup. ot. and assigned to the high est. of Griqualand as second puisne judge, 1882; ag. judge pres., 1887; judge pres., 1888; ret., 1913.

LAVILLE, LOUIS VICTOR JOSEPH.—B. 1888; ed. at Bedford schl. and Corpus Christi Coll., Camb. (schol.), 2nd cls. class. and hist. tripca., B.A., Camb.; cadet, S. Stiltna, Dec., 1912; attd. to col. secy.'s office, Jan., 1913; ag. dep. regir., sup. ct., Singapore, Aug., 1913; ag. asst. supt., Indian immigrn., in addn., Mar. Oct., 1914; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, Nov., 1914; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, Jan., 1915, and Aug., 1916; ag. dep. regir., sup. ct., Penang, May, 1915; ag. dist. offr., Balik Pulau, Jan., 1916; passed final exam. in Malay, 26th Jan., 1916; passed cadet, 26th Jan., 1916; supy. officer, ela. V, 26th Jan., 1916; lent to Govt. of Kelantan, June, 1917; ag. dist. offr., Pasir Putih, Aug., 1917; offtig. dist. offr., Ulu Kelantan, Jan., 1918; supy. offr., ela. IV, Dec., 1919; ag. tempy., 2nd mag., Singapore, June, 1920; offtig. dist. offr., Bukit Mortajam, July, 1920; offg. collr., income tax, Singapore, 3rd Apr., 1922; mag. and asst. regir., sup. ct., Seremban, Dec., 1923; dep. pub. pros., Perak, July, 1925.

LAW, CHARLES EWAN.—B. 1884; ed. High Schl., Croydon and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; Campbell-Foster prizeman, criminal law, Middle Temple, 1903; called to bar, 1906; Burma bar, 1906; staff capt. (tempy. Brit. serv.), Burma divn., Mar., 1917; Volunteer Long Service med. (India), Feb., 1919; maj. (temp. Ind. army res. of offr.), comdg. 15th Mech. Transport Coy., July, 1919; released from serv., rank capt., L.A.R.O., Feb., 1920; crown counsel, Kenya, Jan., 1922; ag. solr.-gen., Mar., 1923 to Oct., 1924; mem. legis. coun., May, 1923 to Oct., 1924; ag. atty.-gen. and mem. exec. coun., Apr., 1924; Swahili (L.S.), Sept., 1923; res. mag., Jamaica, Mar., 1925; ag. judge, Kingston ct., Oct., 1925 to Jan., 1926 and from Mar., 1926 to Jan., 1927; ag. puisne judge Dec., 1926 and from Jan., 1927; mem., standing comtee. of res. mag., June, 1926; mem., coman. of enquiry into admstr. of prisons, Sept., 1926.

LAW, Y. P., B.A. (Cantab.).—B. 1888; asst. mast., educn. dept., Hong Kong, 1915; ag. inspr. of vernac. schls., 1915 and 1917; mem. bd. of examrs., 1919-22; inspr., vernac. schls., 1920 and 1921; hon. sec., bd. of educn., in addn., 1920-22: inspr., vernac. schls., New Territories, 1922; reapptd. mem., bd. of exams., 1924; ag. sen. inspr., vernac. schls. for urban dists. in addn., 1925.

LAWLEY, HON. SIR ARTHUR, G.C.S.I. (1911); G.C.I.E. (1906); K.C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1860; sec. to adminstr., S. Rhodesia, May, 1896; dep. adminstr., Matabeleland, Nov., 1896; adminstr., Mashonaland, July, 1899; gov. of W. Aust., Jan., 1901; lt.-gov., Transvaal, 15th Aug., 1902; ag. gov. of Transvaal and O.R.C. and high comsnr., 1903; chmn. of excoun., pres. of leg. coun., mem. I.C.C.; gov. of Madras, 1906.

LAWRENCE, MAJOR A. S.—Major, reserve of officers; major, Middlesex Hussars; served with I.Y. in S. Africa, 1900-1902 (Queen's medal with 3 clasps, King's medal with 2 clasps, ment. in desp.); served with 6th Batt., K.A.R., Somaliland, 1905-1911 (African General Serv. medal with clasp "Somaliland 1908-1910," ment. in desp.); asst. res., N. Nigeria, 1st Feb., 1911; dist. comsnr., Somaliland, Sept., 1914; sec. to govt., 1926.

LAWRENCE, MAJOR ERNEST HARRY THORNE, O.B.E. (1919).—Capt., reserve of offrs., 4th Batt. Connaught Rangers; served in S. Africa with 20th Batt. I.Y., 1900-1901; 2nd lieut., 5th Batt. Connaught Rangers, Oct., 1901; seconded to remount dept. and served in United States and S. Africa, 1902 (Queen's medal and four clasps); passed promotion to capt., 1903; schl. of musketry, Hythe (distinguished), 1904; A.S.C. transport course, Aldershot, 1904; army physical training, Aldershott, 1905; instr. of musketry, 5th Connaught Rangers, 1905; instr. of signalling, Aldershott, 1905; army veterinary course, Aldershott, 1906; passed for maj., 1906; mil. schl. of instr., Chelsea, 1908 and 1907 (special); dist. supt. of pol., Uganda, June, 1907; R.I.C. course, 1909; ag. comsnr. of pol. and prisons, Uganda, Mar. to Sept., 1910, Sept., 1911 to Jan., 1912, and from Mar.-Sept., 1914; asst. comsnr. of pol. and prisons, 1st Apr., 1911; offr. in ch. defence measures, Uganda, on outbreak of War with Germany with temp. rank of major; provost marshal, Uganda, Aug., 1914; commdt., Uganda Vol. Res., Aug., 1914; commdt., Uganda Pol. Service Batt., Dec., 1914; served in G.E. Africa until 15th Jan., 1917 (ment. in desp.); major, res. of offrs., Jan., 1917; ag. comsnr. of pol. and prisons, Uganda, Sept., 1917 to Oct., 1918, and from Apr.-July, 1921; mem., Uganda Development Coms., 1921; comsnr. of pol. and prisons, 2nd May, 1922.

LAWSON, HON. H. S. W.—B. 1875; M.L.A. of Castlemaine, Victoria, 1899; has sat continuously in State Parlt. since 1899; comsnr. of crown lands and survey, Dec., 1913 to Nov., 1915; afterwards atty.-gen., solr.-gen. and min. of public instrn., premier and atty.-gen. 1918; premier and min. f agr., 1920-24.
LAWSON, R. A.—Asst. traffic man., Uganda ly., Apr., 1919.

LEA, LESLIE.—Ed. privately and Univ. Coll. schl., Gray's Inn, 1922; clk. acct., fencing dept., S. Africa Prot., Sept., 1908; conf. clk. to comsnr. or lands, 1909; conveyancing clk., land dept., 1910; ag. conveyancer, 1913; asst. regsr., crown lands, 1916; musketry instr., K.A.R., 1916 (read. hanks G.O.C.L. of C.); registr. of titles, 1919; ep. recorder of titles, 1919; ag. prin. regsr., crown lands, 1920; dep. regsr.-gen., titles and ch. controlling rev. authority, 1920-21; law offr., legal adviser, etc., St. Helena, Aug., 1923; col. audr., mem., bl. of comsrs., crown property, Sept., 1923; offr.-in-charge, secretariat, 1925; comsnr. for preparation of new edn. of laws, 1925.

LEA, WALTER ALBERT, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1883; ed. at Carpenters' Company's Coll. and West Ham Tech. Inst.; employed by Messrs. Perry & Co., Ltd., Contractors, Bow, 1900 to 1914; dist. engnr., Br. Guiana, 3rd Dec., 1914; seconded to sea defence work, Feb., 1916.

LEACH, FRANK, M.C., B.A. (Oxon).—B. 1889; on mily. serv., July, 1917; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., May, 1921; attd. to the secretariat, June, 1921; office asst. to asst. govt. agt., Hambantota, Nov., 1922; ag. asst. sttlmt. offr., Oct., 1923.

LEACH, JOHN VINCENT, M.B.E. (1924).—Ed. Stonyhurst Coll., England; admitted solr. sup. ct., Jamaica, 1881; res. mag., 1888; has frequently been specially apptd. to conduct important inquiries and to report thereon.
LEADBETTER, SIDNEY.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 4th May,

LEAVITT, CLYDE.—B. 1878; ed., High Schl., Bellaire, Mich., Univ. of Mich. (B.A., 1901; M.Sc. For., 1904); ent. U.S. forest serv. as student asst., 1903; apptd. forest asst., 1904, with successive appts. as ch. of boundaries, asst. ch., office of organization, ch., office of organization, dist. forester, and asst. forester; ch. forester, coman. of conservn. and bl. of rly. comsrs., Canada, 1912; ch. fire inspr., bd. of rly. comsrs., 1921; mem., soc. of American Foresters; successively, pres. and sec., Can. Soc. of Forest Engrs.

LECHMERE-GUPPY, GARETH EVERARD.— B. 1874; entd. civ. ser., Trinidad, 20th May, 1895; seconded for special serv. to water authority and sewerage bd., 1904-1905; ag. inspr. of irrigts. in 1909, 1910, 1912 to 1913; statistical clk. and librn., col. secretariat, Trinidad, 15th July, 1915; sec. income tax comtce., ests. comtce., tenders comtce., and civ. serv. comtce.; J.P. for the col. and Tobago; dep. sub-intendant crown lands, Dec., 1920; sub.-intend., crown lands, Jan.,

LECHMERE-GUPPY, P. (Junr.).—Supernumerary clk., audit off., Trinidad, 1889; 4th clk., sub-treasy., San Fernando, 1st Jan., 1892; landing waiter, customs, 1st May, 1894; clk., col. treasy., 1st Mar., 1898; acted as warden, Tacarigua, in 1902, 1904 and 1905; sub-recvr., postmaster, collr. of cust. and harbr. mast., Tobago, 16th Oct., 1917; collected fresh-water fishes of Trinidad for British Museum.
LECLEZIO, SIR HENRY L., K.C.M.G. (1915); C.M.G. (1897).—Elected mem. of coun. of govt. for dist. of Moka, Mauritius.

LEDEBORR, CLAUDE MARIUS.—Served in S. African Constab., 1901-7; Bech. Prot. police, from 1907; sub-inspr., Bech. Prot. police, 1st Apr., 1914; asst. res. mag.; passed Cape civ. serv. lower law exam. and 2nd grade Sechuana exam.; ag. res. mag., 1921; res. mag., 1921; passed Cape civ. serv. higher law exam., 1923.

LEE, FRANK G.—B. 1903; ed. Brentwood Schl. and Downing Coll., Cambridge; 1st cls. English tripso, 1923; 1st cls. historical tripso, part II, 1924; foundation schl., 1924; apptd. to C.O. after compet. exam., 8th Nov., 1926.

LEE, H. N., O.B.E. (Mily.).—Ed. Portmouth Grammar schl. and King's Coll., London, 1906-11; articles, Inst. of Chartered Acnts., London; col. audit dept., 1913; asst. audr., E.A.F., Jan., 1914; E.A. Expedy. Force, 1914-19 (twice ment. in desp.); senr. asst. audr., Kenya, 1921; ag. audr., 1926.
LEE, Hon. Sir W. H., K.C.M.G. (1922), Kt. Bach. (1920).—M.H.A., Tasmania; premier, chief sec., and min. for educn., 1916-22.

LEES, Henry Berry, M.C., M.I.C.E., M.I.M. and Cy. E., F.S.I.—B. 1880; dist. engrn., P.W.D., Ceylon, 1905; ag. prov. engrn., N. Prov., 1908; extra asst. dir. of pub. wks., 1909; 2nd asst. dir. pub. wks., 1912; ag. asst. dir. of pub. wks., 1913 and 1920; on active serv. with R.E.'s in France, Belgium, Italy, and with the army of occupation on the Rhine, 1915-19; major, R.E., M.C., Legion d'Honneur, Croix de Chevalier, twice ment. in desps.; comanr. of wks. and mem., legis. coun., Fiji, 1920; comanr. of water supply and drainage, 1920; chmn., cent. road bd., 1920; dir., pub. wks. and mem., advisory coun., Palestine, 1923; chmn., cent. housing comsn., 1923; apptd. adviser to H.M.G. under concessions protocol to treaty of Lausanne in connection with certain pre-war Turkish concessions, 1925; chmn., road bd., 1926.

LEESE, Charles William.—Ast. dist. comsnr., S. Nigeria, Sept., 1906; transf. to treasry. dept., 1907; acted as supervisor of customs, 1908, and as junior asst. sec. in 1911; served as finan. offr., Cameroons Expedy. Force, 1916; dep. treas., 1921; ag. treas., May to Dec., 1921 and June to Dec., 1923; treas., G. Coast, 1924; ag. col. sec., Sept.-Nov., 1925.

LEESE, Capt. Ernest Benjamin.—3rd Batt., E. Yorks Regt., res. of offrs.; served in I.Y. in S. African War, 1900-1901 (Queen's medal with 3 clasps); subaltern in G. Coast Regt., 22nd July, 1905; capt., 9th Jan., 1908; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to govr. of G. Coast, Nov., 1910, to Feb., 1911; trav. comsnr., Gambia, 6th Nov., 1911; served with Cameroons Expedy. Force, 1915-16.

LEE-WARNER, W. H.—B. 1880; ed. at Rugby and Univ. Coll., Oxford (scholar), 1st cls. class. mods., 1901; 2nd cls. lit. hum., 1903; B.A., 1903; barrister-at-law, 1912; cadet, F.M.S. civ., ser., 1903; passed cadet, 1905; ag. asst. dist. offr., Batu Gajah, Jan., 1907; ag. 2nd asst. sec. to res. gen., and priv. sec. to res. gen., May, 1909, to Feb., 1910; bonus exam. in Tamil, 1910; ag. asst. res., Brunei, 1910-11; temporary 2nd cls. clk., C.O., Feb.-Oct., 1912; resumed in Brunei, 1913; ag. sec. to high comsnr., Malay States, and priv. sec. to O.A.G., S. Sttlmts., July-Sept., 1914; ag. priv. sec. to gov., Sept., 1914; A.D.C. to gov., Oct., 1914; also ag. sec. to high comsnr., Malay States, Jan., 1916; asst. to res., Malacca, Aug.-Nov., 1916; seconded under F.O. vice-consul, Sabang, Jan.-Mar., 1917; ag. vice-consul, Batavia, Apr. to July, 1917; seconded for mil. serv., intell. dept., W.O., Sept., 1917; temp. hony. 2nd lieut., Feb., 1918; seconded under F.O., Mar., 1918; temp. hony. capt., sp. list, June, 1918; supernmy. offr., cls. III., Jan., 1919; agent, food contrlr., Penang, Aug., 1919; sec., food contrlr., Jan., 1920; supernmy. offr., cls. II., Nov., 1920; attd. for sp. duty, col. secretariat, Singapore, 10th Jan., 1922.

LEFEUVRE, Philip Llewellin.—B. 1875; ed. at Victoria Coll., Jersey, and Brighton Coll., Sussex; clk., G.P.O., Cape Town, Feb., 1895; clk., atty.-gen.'s off., Feb., 1896; clk. to req. mag., Glen Grey, Feb., 1896; clk. to res. mag., East London, Sept., 1898; clk., sol.-gen.'s off., Grahamstown, Apr., 1899; clk., atty.-gen.'s off. (treason branch), Cape Town, Sept., 1900; B.A., Cape Univ., 1900; ch. clk. to atty.-gen., O.R.C., June, 1901; sec. to law dept., Oct., 1904; ag. clk. to legis. coun., May-July, 1906; relieving mag., 1912; mag., Thaba' Nchu, May, 1917; mag., Ladybrand, Feb., 1925; joint editor of law-book, O.R.C., 1900-1906.

LEFROY, Hon. Sir Henry B., K.C.M.G. (1919), C.M.G. (1903).—Elected M.L.A., W. Australia, 1892; re-elected, 1894 and 1897; min. of educn., May, 1897; min. of mines, May, 1898; ag. gen. for W. Australia in London, 1901; resigned, 1904; min. for lands and agric., July, 1916; premier, 1917; resigned, Apr., 1919.

LEGAT, Charles Edward.—B.Sc.—joined forest dept., Cape Colony, May, 1898; ast. to supt., George, Apr., 1899; supt., Concordia plantation, Jan., 1901; dist. forest offr., Hanover, Aug., 1901; transf. to Transvaal govt as ag. forestry ast., Apr., 1902; conservator of forests, Transvaal, Oct., 1904; 1st grade conservator, Transvaal, Apr., 1912; chief conservator of forests, Union of S. Africa, 31st May, 1913.

LEGGE, Charles Arthur Lionkl.—B. 1879; clerical ast., B. Guiana, May, 1897; 6th cls. offr., cust., June, 1899; 5th cls. clk., pol. dept., Dec., 1900; lieut.-B. G. Artillery, 1901; attached to No. 32 Coy. R.G.A., Portsmouth, July, 1904; cert. in depression range finding, July, 1904; Hythe musk. cert., Aug., 1904; sub-inspr. of pol. and excise, Grenada, Oct., 1904; promoted capt., B. Guiana Artillery, and seconded, Dec., 1904; mem. local health authority, St. Andrew's parish, Grenada, Jan., 1906; ag. ch. of pol., Grenada, from June, 1907.

LEGGE, Cyril Henry Ethelwood.—B. 1883; joined B. Guiana civ. serv., 22nd June, 1901; clk. in surg. gens. office, cent. bd. of health, customs and atty.-gens. office; commissary, 24th May, 1906; ag. stip. mag., 29th Mar., 1914 to last Dec., 1916; stip. mag. 2nd grade, 1st Dec., 1916; stip. mag., 1st grade, 29th Mar., 1921; passed final bar exams., 1912; called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 28th July, 1922; passed govt. Hindi exam.

LEIGH-CLARE, Harry James Leigh.—B. 1891; on mily. serv., Jan., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Feb., 1920; attd. to Colombo Kachcheri, Mar., 1920; attd. to office of food contrlr., Apr., 1920; ag. office ast. to govt. ast., S. Prov., Mar., 1921; office ast. to govt. ast., Cent. Prov., Apr., and Sept., 1922; ag. ast. govt. ast., Kandy, May, 1922; ditto to govt. ast. Cent. Prov., Apr., 1922; ag. ast. govt. ast., Kandy, Mar. to Sept., 1922; addnl. landing survr., cust., Colombo, Oct., 1924; 2nd landing survr., Nov., 1924.

LEMBERGER, Maurice.—B. 1900; apptd. after compet. exam., ast. clk., 23rd Apr., 1918; on mily. serv., 26th Aug., 1918 to 27th Feb., 1920; apptd. cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; trans. to P.O. stores dept., Mar., 1920; trans. to C.O., 1st Sept., 1923.

LE MESSURIER, Henry Wm., C.M.G. (1916).—B. 1848; J.P., Newfoundland, 1879; M.H.A., 1885 to 1890; 3rd landing waiter and ast. tide survr. of cust., Newfild., 1894 to 1897; ast. collr. and sec. to bd. of rev., 1897; dep. min. of cust. with title of ast. collr., 1898; superintended reorganisation of cust. dept., and planned and inaugurated cust. service in connection with Reid Newfild. Co.'s steamer and rly. system, 1898; corrpdnt., C.I.B., B. of T., 1905; served as commissariat clk. under ast. commissaries prior to removal of the Imperial troops.

LEMIEUX, Hon. Rodolph, K.C., LL.D.—B. 1866; ed. Nicolet Coll. and Laval Univ., R.C.L. (1891), LL.D. (1896); called to the bar, 1891; prof. of law, Laval Univ., 1896-1906; Q.C., Quebec, 1898; K.C. by Dom. Govt., 1904; elec. to H. of C., 1896; re-elec. 1900, 1904, 1908, 1911, 1917 and 1921; solr.-gen. of Canada, 1904; postm.-gen. and min. of labour, 1906; Knight of Legion of Honour, France, 1906; envoy of Canadian govt. to Japan in connection with Japanese immigr., 1907; deleg. of Canadian govt. to the opening of first parlmt. of Union of S. Africa, 1910; min. of marine and fisheries, 1911; resigned portfolio on defeat of Laurier govt., Oct., 1911; speaker of House of Commons, 1922.

LENNOX, Hon. Haughton I. S.—B. 1850; ed. pub. and grm. schls., Barrie, Ontario; called to the bar, 1877; K.C., 1908; practised at the bar for many years at Barrie; elec. to H. of C., Canada, 1900; re-elec., 1904, 1908 and 1911; judge of high ct., Ontario, 1912.

LENTON, Henry John.—Cape teleg. serv., 1st Sept., 1898; clk., staff branch, 21st Jan., 1902; 1st cls. clk., ditto, 1st Apr., 1912; senr. clk., ch. clk's office, 1st Apr., 1918; prin. clk. ditto, 18th Dec., 1919; 2nd grade, ch. clk., 1st Apr., 1921; asst. sec., 1st Nov., 1923; ag. P.M.G., May to Oct., 1924 and from 3rd July, 1925; P.M.G. and chmn., civ. air bd., 1st Apr., 1926.

LEONARD, Hugh Goodwin Russell, M.A. (Edin.)—B. 1880; cadet, F.M.S., 1903; passed cadet, 1905; ag. ast. dist. treas., Kuala Lumpur, 1906; ag. dist. treas., Raub, Apr., 1906; ag. 2nd ast. sec. to res.-gen. and priv. sec. to res.-gen., Aug., 1906; ag. ast. supt. of imigr., Nov., 1906; ag. harbmr., Port Swettenham, in addn., Aug.-Oct., 1907; bonus exam. in Malay, 1908; ag. emigr. agt. in India, Mar., 1909-Mar., 1912; ag. dep. contr. of lab., Feb., 1913; bonus exam. in Telegu, 1913; ag. 2nd mag., Kuala Lumpur, Apr., 1915; ag. dep. contr. of lab., Sept., 1915-Aug., 1919 and June, 1920; offg. ast. treas., F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, Nov., 1921; chmn., European unemployment comtee., in addn., Apr., 1922; on sp. duty, Aug., 1924; ag. treas., F.M.S., Nov., 1924; astd., fedl. sect. for sp. duty, Sept., 1925; contr. rubber, S.S. and F.M.S., Nov., 1925; ag. finan. commr. and audr.-gen., Johore, Jan., 1926.

LESHEL, Allan Wallack.—Ag. clk., Estcourt magistracy, Natal, 1889; 3rd cls. clk., Newcastle, Jan., 1890; clk. of ct. and Zulu interp. (2nd cls.), Alexandra, May, 1891; 1st cls. clk., Jan., 1898; transf'd. to Newcastle, Jan., 1899; mag., Ndandwe, July, 1900; Krantzkop, Apr., 1904; Richmond, Oct., 1906; mag. for col. of Natal, Oct., 1906; ast. under-sec., Dec., 1907; ag. principal under-sec., Apr. to Nov., 1909; ag. under-sec. for justice, Union of S. Africa, Jan., 1911; chief clk., dept. of justice, 1st Apr., 1912; mag., Dundee, Nov., 1915; mag., Durban, May, 1920; judge, native high ct., 1st Oct., 1920.

LESHEL, Major George Arthur James, R.E.—B. 1867; ed. at Dulwich and King William's Colls.; entd. royal mil. acad., 1885; 2nd lieut., R.E., Mar., 1887; two years' practical training at Chatham; one year's training for submarine mining at Chatham and Portsmouth; employed in India on submarine defences at Rangoon and Karachi, 1891-1894; special employment at Aden, 1895; mil. works services in Baluchistan, 1896-1899; survey offr., Tirah campaign, 1897 (ment. in desps., medal and two clasps); brevet majority, May, 1908; regimental majority, Oct., 1905; special service, Chitral campaign, 1900-1902; with corps of 3rd rangers and miners, Kirkee, 1903-1908; acted as mil. sec. to G.O.C., Southern army, India, 1907; services lent to Gold Coast Colony for construction of trunk road in Ashanti, 1st Nov., 1908.
LETHEM, G. J.—B. 1886; ed. at Mill Hill, Sohl., Middlesex, and Edin. and Granoble Univs.; M.A. and LL.B. (with distinction) Edin.; mem. of Lincoln's Inn; ast. res., N. Nigeria, 22nd Mar., 1911.

LEWIS, Alfred Dale, B.A. (Cape), M.A. (Cantab.), M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1881; P.W.D., India, 1906-07; circle engr., irrig. dept., Cape and Union of S. Africa, 1907-15; on mily. serv. as major, R.E., in France, Egypt, Mesopotamia, 1915-19; ag. dir. of irrig., 1920; dir. of irrigation, 1921; author of "Irrigation in America."

LEWIS, Charles Henry.—B. 1890; linotype operator, govt. printing press, Zanzibar, 24th Nov., 1921; ag. govt. printer, 29th Oct., 1922 to 16th May, 1923; ast. govt. printer, Nov., 1923 and from 24th Jan. to 11th June, 1925.

LEWIS, Frederick James.—Tempy. draughtsman, survr.-gen.'s office, Natal, Mar., 1892; draughtsman, July, 1896; draughtsman and computer, Sept., 1898; ag. examnr. of diagrams, Jan., 1908; examnr. of diagrams, Jan., 1909; professional ast., survr.-gen.'s office, Natal Prov., Apr., 1912; survr.-gen., Natal Prov., 1st Oct., 1915.

LEWIS, John Williamson, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1887; ed. King's schl., Canterbury; Crystal Palace schl. of practical engng.; jr. ast. engr., constrn. dept., F.M.S. rly., 1909; ast. engr., F.M.S. rly., 1912; dist. engr., F.M.S. rly., 1913; senr. dist. engr., F.M.S. rly., 1923.

LEWIS, L. H.—B. 1886; clk., acct.'s office, G.P.O., O.R.C., 1903; clk., P.O. savings bank, Transvaal, 1904; clk., sec.'s office, P.O., Union of S. Africa, 1914; seconded to admstr., S.W. Africa Prot., 1915-17; 1st grade clk., treasy., S. Africa, 1917; senr. clk., 1919; prin. clk., 1921; sec., prov. finances comen., 1922-23; sec., educn. admstr., comen., 1923.

LEWIS, Hon. Sir Neil Elliott, K.C.M.G. (1902), C.M.G. (1901), B.A.—B. 1858; atty.-gen. in Dobson-Hervy ministry, Tasmania, 1892-4; premier and atty.-gen. in Lewis ministry, 1900; joined 1st Commonwealth ministry as min. without portfolio, Jan., 1901; again premier of Tasmania, 1909-1912; treasr., and min. for mines, Apl., 1916.

LEWIS, Sir Walter Llewellyn, K.T. Bach. (1904), M.A.—B. 1849; gold medallist, Queen's Univ. (Ireland), 1869 and 1871; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., Jan., 1876; South Wales and Chester circuits; stip. justice, Trinidad, Jan., 1886; chmn. of coman. on agricultural contracts; chmn. of directors, public service widows' and orphans' fund, 1890-1900; puisne judge, 1893; ch. justice, British Honduras, 1900; coronation medal, 1902; ret., 1906.

LEY, Major Cuthbert Hillyar, O.B.E. late R.E., F.R.Met.Soc.—B. 1872; coman. in R. Engrs., 1892-1907; ret. from army, 1907; army serv. in Jamaica, 1896-99; S. African war, 1899-1900 (Tugela Heights and relief of Ladysmith); A.D.C. to inspr.-gen. of fortifications and engrs., 1900-03; geodetic survey of S. Africa, 1903-05; topographical secn., gen. staff, 1906-07; survey of Fiji, 1908; ordnance survey of Gt. Britain, 1912; served during war at Southampton in depts. of fortifications, miny. of munitions, and directorate of arty.; dir., Palestine survey, Dec., 1920.

LEYH, Stanley George Hoare, Pay-Lieut. R.N.R., O.B.E. (Mily.)—B. 1893; ed. East Ham Tech. Coll., A.S.C. (T.F.), 1908-12; Royal Navy, 1913-20; staff of comdr-in-chief, Grand Fleet, 1914-17; admty. war staff (allied naval coun.),
LIDDARD, M.—B.1875; ed. at Heath House Sohl., Kent, and St. Charles Coll., London; served under Royal Niger Co., 1895-1898; 3rd cls. res., N. Nigeria, 20th Jan., 1910.

LIESCHING, PERCIVALE.—B. 1895; ed. Bedford Schl., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford (scholar); lit. hum. (D.) 1920; pvt., 1st batt. H.A.C., 1914; 2nd lieut., Rifle Brig., Feb., 1915; lieut., June, 1915; capt., Tank Corps, Oct., 1918; served in France, 1914, 1917-18; E. Africa, 1916; ment. in despr., 1918; app'td. under reconstruction scheme, asst. prin., C.O., 26th July, 1920; joint sec., col. survey comte., 1920; sec., comte., on priv. enterprise in trop. Africa, 1923; pvt., sec. to perm. sec. for dominion affrs., 6th Aug., 1925.
LIGHT, JOSEPH.—B. 1898; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1921; att., Galle Kachcheri, Jan., 1922; ditto, Kegalla Kachcheri, Feb., 1923; office asst. to asst. govt. agt., Kegalla, Aug., 1923.
LILLEY, CYRIL CHARLES.—B. 1889; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and Sandhurst; capt., Worcestershire Regt.; asst. dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, 25th Aug., 1920.
LINCOLNSHIRE, 1ST MARQUESS OF (cr. 1912).

CHARLES ROBERT WYNN-CARRINGTON, EARL CARRINGTON (cr. 1895), VISCOUNT WENDOVER (U.K.), BARON CARRINGTON (Great Britain, 1797, and Ireland, 1796), P.C. (1881), K.G. (1906), G.C.M.G. (1885).—B. 1843; ed. at Eton and Trin. Coll., Camb., B.A. 1863; late capt. Roy. Horse Guards, lieut.-col. 3rd batt. Oxford Light Infantry; capt. Queen's Body Guard, 1881 to 1885; gov. of N. S. Wales, 1885-90; lord chamberlain, 1892-95; pres., bd. of agric., 1905; lord privy seal, 1911; joint hered. lord great chamberlain of England.

LINDSAY, JAMES.—Ed. Edin. Univ.; M.B., Ch.B., 1906; late res. physician, Grampian sanatorium for consumption; late clinical asst., Royal Victoria Dispensary for Consumption and diseases of the chest, Edin.; joined W.A.M.S., Apl. 1910.
LINDSAY, K. G.—Asst. dist. comsnr., Kenya, June, 1921.
LINDSELL, HAROLD OFFLEY.—B. 1884; ed. at Bradford Coll. (exhibitioner) and All Souls Coll., Oxford, B.A. (Litt. Hum.), 1907; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 10th Apr., 1909.

LINDSELL, ROGER EDWARD.—B. 1885; Ed. at Uppingham schl. (scholar) and King's Coll.; Camb. (scholar); class. tripods, part I, cls. I, div. 2, cadet, Hong Kong, 1909; passed cadet, 1911; ag. 2nd asst. registr.-gen., 1911; J.P., 1912; passed law exam., 1913; ag. 1st asst. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1913; ag. 2nd ditto, 1913; 3rd ditto, but continued to act as 2nd ditto, 1914; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1915; ag. chief asst. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1915 to 1916; ag. asst. P.G., 1916; ag. ch. asst. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1916; ag. asst. supt., imports and exports dept., 1918; ag. dep. registr., sup. ct., 1918; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1919, 1921, 1922 and 1923; ag. 1st pol. mag., 1922, 1924, 1925 and 1926; ag. P.M.G., 1923.

LINEHAN, WILLIAM.—B. 1892; ed. Christian Brother's Coll. and Univ. Coll., Cork (class. schol. and exhib.), 1910-13; B.A. (1st cls. class., 1913); post grad. scholarship, 1913-14; M.A. hona., class., 1916; cadet, F.M.S., 1916; 2nd asst. dist. offr., Kuala Kubu, Jan.-April, 1919; asst. dist. offr., Ipoh, Apr., 1919; asst. dist. offr., Krian, 1920-21; asst. dist. offr., Lower Perak, May, 1922; ag. dist. offr., Pekan, Aug., 1923; offr., cls. IV., Dec., 1923; ag. dist. offr., Kuantan, Aug., 1924.

LINNELL, CAPT. THOMAS FORESTER, R.K. (res. of offrs.), A.M.I.C.E.—Ed. Denstone Coll., 1899-1904; articled pupil, civ. engrs., 1905-09; lieut., Royal Engineers, S.R. of O., 1910; temp. asst. engr., P.W.D., Br. E. Africa, 1911; trans'd. to Uganda rly., Apr., 1914; war. serv. on Uganda rly. and in German E. Africa, 1915-18; "1914-15" Star, Victory and Gen Serv medals; dist. engr., Kenya and Uganda rlys., June, 1924.

LITT, WILLIAM GEORGE.—B. 1877; ed. Shrewsbury schl.; barrister-at-law (Inner Temple); served in Impl. yeomanry in S. African War, 1900-01; Queen's Med., 4 clasps; coman. in The King's Shropshire Light Infy. (vol. and territorial battns.), 1901-20; territorial decorn.; substantive major, June, 1916; A.P.M., Hongkong, 1915-16; B.E.F., France, 1917; administrative commdt., Aleppo, E.E.F., 1919; Gen. Service, Territorial, War and Victory med.; ret. with hon. rank of major; judl. offr., O.E.T.A. (South), Dec., 1919; pres., dist. offr., Haifa, 1920.

LIVERPOOL, EARL OF, RT. HON. ARTHUR POLJAMBE, P.C. (1917); G.C.B.; G.C.M.G. (1914); G.B.E. (1918); K.C.M.G. (1912), M.V.O. (1900); Viscount Hawkesbury of Kirkham; Baron Hawkesbury of Hasselbech; B. 1870; ed. Eton and Sandhurst; 2nd lieut., Rifle Brigade, 1891; lieut., 1893; captain, 1897; staff captain, Dublin district, July, 1900 to Dec., 1901; served in S. Africa, 1901-2; major, 1907; state steward and chamberlain to Earl of Aberdeen, K.T., lord lieutenant of Ireland, 1906 to 1908; gov. of New Zealand, 9th Sept., 1912; assumed govt., 19th Dec., 1912; gov.-gen. of New Zealand, 1917-20.

LLEWELLYN, JOHN LIONEL BRETHERTON LLYWELYN, M.A. Oxon. (hons. hist.), F.R.G.S.—B. 1889; asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., 22nd Apr., 1914; ag. dist. comsnr., N. Frontier, Sept., 1915-June, 1920; A.G.S. med., "Jubaland, 1917-18"; ag. dist. comsnr., Jubaland, Nov., 1922-May, 1924.

LOYD, ALFRED NORMAN MOSTYN.—Clk., asst., gov.'s office, Natal, Feb., 1886; cler. ast., postal dept., 1886; clk., European emigrn. office, Natal, Feb., 1889; 3rd grade clk., deeds office, Mar., 1891; 3rd grade clk., sub-audit office, Durban Mar., 1893; 2nd grade clk., deeds office, Aug., 1899; 1st grade clk., ditto, July, 1903; senr. clk., ditto, July, 1906; ast. registr., deeds, Oct., 1908; registr., deeds, Natal, Apr., 1922.
LOYD, EDGAR BRINLEY.—B. 1894; ed. Aberavon; clk., land dept., E.A.P., 1919; ag. land ast., 1920; ag. registr., crown lands, documents and titles, 1921.
LOYD, H. S. D.—Asst. store-keeper, Uganda rly., 1913.

LOYD, J. D.—Ed. at Blundell's schl., Tiverton, and Sidney Sussex Coll., Camb.; class. tripods, cls. II., div. I.; Blundell exhibiter., and major scholar of Std. Suss. Coll.; B.A., 1906; cadet, Hong Kong, 1907; offr. for exam. of male assisted emigrts., 1908, to 1909; passed cadet, 1910; ag. 1st ast. registr.-gen., 1910; J.P., 1911; ast. postmaster-gen., 1912; 2nd ast. registr.-gen., 1913; ag. dep. registr., acctnt. and appraiser, sup. ct., 1913; ast. P.M.G., 1914; ag. dep. registr. and acctnt., sup. ct., 1914; attached to imports and exports dept., 1916; ag. supt., ditto, 1916; seconded for mil. serv., 1917; coman. in Chinese Labour Corps, 1918; offl. recvr. and regis. of trade marks and patents, 1919; custodian of enemy property, and contrlr. of local clearing office, 1920; admstr. of Austrian and Bulgarian property in addn., 1920; census offr. in addn., 1921; custodian of enemy property, etc., and dep. regis., sup. ct., 1922-23; supt. impts. and expts., 1923; commended by S. of S. for repta. on opium smuggling, 1924.

LLOYD, RUPERT CORDEN.—B. 1870; clk., contr. and audr.-gen.'s dept., 1889; ag. civ. comanr. and regis. of deeds, Kimberley, 1898; ch. examr. of accts., law dept., 1900; acctnt., sup. ct., Cape Town, 1901; civ. comanr., res. mag. and dep. admstr., martial law, Barkly East, 1902; civ. comanr. and res. mag., Barkly West, 1906; comanr., Robben Island, 1909; civ. comanr. and mag., Mafeking, 1913; Somerset East, 1915; inspgr. mag. and inspr. of prisons, 1916; civ. comanr. and mag., Oudtshoorn, 1919; ch. mag., Cape Town, 1923; pub. serv. comanr., 1926.

LLOYD, T. I. K.—B. 1896; ed. Rossall Schol. and Caesius Coll., Cambridge (schol., 1914); cadet, Royal Mil. Acad., 1915; 2nd lieut., R.E., 1916; served in Egypt and Palestine, ment. in deaps., 1917; apptd. under re-construction scheme, astt. prin., miny. of health, July, 1920; temp. attached to C.O. as astt. prin., Dec., 1921.

LLOYD, WILLIAM BUTLER.—B. 1880; ed. Shrewsbury, and Christ Church, Oxford; M.A.; called to the bar, 1905; dist. and stip. mag., Mauritius, 1913; mag., Rodriguez, 1916; mag. for occupied territories of German East Africa, 1919; mag., Tanganyika Territory, 21st Nov., 1919; pol. mag., S. Leone, 6th June, 1922; dep. judge, sup. ct., 3rd July to 1st Aug., 1922.

LLOYD, HON. SIR WM. FREDERICK, K.C.M.G. (1919).—B. 1864; ed. Owen's Coll., Manchester and Durham Coll. of Science, Newcastle-on-Tyne; bachelor of law, London Univ., 1894; D.C.L., Victoria Univ., Toronto, 1901; vice-principal, Bishop Field Coll., St. John's, Newfild., 1891; editor, "Evening Telegram" 1904; M.H.A., Trinity dist., 1904; leader of the opposition, H. of A., 1916; with Sir Edward (now Lord) Morris formed a National Govt., becoming attorney-general, 1917; ag. prime minister, 1917; prime minister, 1918.

LLOYD-BLOOD, CAPT. L.I.N., M.C., B.A., Barrister-at-law.—Dep. regis., sup. ct., Kenya, Oct., 1920; regis., July, 1921; astt. atty.-gen., Nyasaland, 16th Aug., 1924; ag. atty.-gen., May to Dec., 1925 and Apr., 1926.

LOBB, REGINALD POPHAM, C.M.G. (1914), B.A., F.R.G.S.—Ed. at Clifton and Christ's Coll., Camb.; mod. and medieval languages tripes, 1895; priv. sec. to Sir F. Lugard, high comanr. of N. Nigeria, Oct., 1900; transfld. to polit. dept. as astt. res., Nupe Prov., Oct., 1901; 3rd cls. res., Zaria, May, 1902; ag. res., Zaria, Jan., 1903; 2nd cls. res., Muri Prov., Oct., 1903; Kano-Sokoto campaign (medal and clasp), 1903, and minor operations; 1903-5; ag. 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 1st Jan., 1906; mem. of African trop. ser. comitee, 1907-8; col. sec. and regis.-gen., Bermuda, 8th Aug., 1908; chmn., gen. bd. of health, bd. of pub. wks., and bd. of piloting comanrs.; recvd. thanks of S. of S. for memo. on tuberculosis, 1912; admstr. and col. sec., St. Vincent, 8th June, 1915; ag. admstr., St. Lucia, 23rd Mar., 1917, to 16th Dec., 1918; dormant comanr. to administer govt. of Windward Is., dated 21st May, 1917; recvd. thanks of U.S. Govt., 1918, for services to U.S. Navy Dept.; senr. del. from Windward Is. at inter-col. educn., confee., Trinidad, 1921; chmn., Windward Is. med. comtee., 1922; col. sec., B. Guiana, 23rd Nov., 1922; ag. gov., B. Guiana, Dec., 1922 to Apr., 1923 and May to Oct., 1925; ag. gov.'s dep. for over four months; chmn., currency comanrs. and harb. bd.; chmn., rly. comanr., 1925; col. sec., Cyprus, Jan., 1926; admtd. govt., 26th July to 29th Nov., 1926.

LOCK, GEORGE ROBERT.—B. 1876; ed. Weymouth Coll. and Sidney-Sussex Coll., Camb., 1895 (schol.); senior optime, maths. tripes, 1898; lst cls. mech. sciences tripes, 1899; ent. C.A. as tech. astt., 1905; dep. head, wks. dept., 1913.

LOCKHART, CHARLES RAMSDALE.—B. 1892; mil. serv., Aug., 1914 to Mar., 1919 (Gallipoli, Egypt, and with K.A.R. in E. Africa); twice ment. in deaps.; astt. treas., Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1919; ag. senr. astt. treas., May, 1921; senr. astt. treas., 1st Apr., 1923.

LOCKHART, SIR JAMES HALDANE STEWART, K.C.M.G. (1908); C.M.G. (1898); F.R.G.S., M.R.A.S.; LL.D. (Hong Kong Univ.).—B. 1858; Edin. Univ.; Greek medallist, etc.; apptd., after compet. exam., to a Hong Kong cadetship, 1878; attached to the C.O. for one year, 1879; passed cadet, 1882; supt. opium revenue, Mar., 1883; astt. col. sec. and astt. auditor-gen., Aug., 1883; ag. registr. gen., 1884 and 1885; registr.-gen., 1887, and M.L.C., 1889; mem. exec. coun., 1891; chairmn. bd. of examiners in Chinese, 1891; ditto govern. body Queen's Coll., dep. registr., sup. ct. in prize, 27th Nov., 1914; 1894; ag. col. sec., Apr., 1894; col. sec. and registr.-gen., Mar., 1895; special comanr. to report on the extension of the hndry. of H. Kong, 1898; representative of Gt. Br. to delimitate hndrys., for both of which recd. thanks of S. of S., 1899; rector of coll. of medicine for Chinese, 1895-1902; comanr. of Weihaiei, 20th Jan., 1902; assumed govt., 3rd May, 1902; ret., 1920.

LOCKHART, KENNETH SPENCER.—B. 1890; copyist, admstr.'s office, Dominica, 1908; 2nd clk., registr.'s office, 1910; 2nd rev. offr. and harbmr., Nevis, 1913; ag. acctnt., treasy., 1917; is also sec. to quarantine bd., and clk. to cent. bd. of health; govt. trade agt. to enquire into and report upon conditions affecting W.I. labourers at Santo Domingo, Nov., 1918; rev. offr., St. Kitts, May, 1920; ag. audit. clk., Sept., 1920; 2nd clk., treas. dept., 23rd July, 1921; capt., St. Kitts-Nevis Defence Force; ag. 1st clk., treasy., May-Sept., 1922; audit clk., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1st June, 1922; income tax comanr., 1924.
