Hugh Edward.—B. 1869; ed. at Jarvis St. Collegiate Inst., Univ. of Toronto;(B.A., 1891; LL.B., 1892), and Osgoode Hall; mem. of the firm of Fasken, Cowan, Chadwick and Rose; called to the bar, Ontario, 1894; K.C., 1908; judge of the sup. ct., Ontario, 4th Dec., 1916; examr. in law for some years for University of Toronto and the Law Society of Upper Canada.

ROSEBY, THOMAS ERNEST.—B. 1888; ed. at Sydney Univ.; M.A.; honouram in French and German; senr. clk., Royal Mint, Sydney, 1885-1896; called to the bar, New S. Wales, 1896; also to high ct. of Australia; called to the bar, Mid. Tem., 1912; crown proscr., N.S. Wales assizes, 1902-3; Brit. judge of joint ct. and judicial comsnr. for the New Hebrides, Nov., 1908; thanked by S. of S. for memoranda on land suits, 1910; co-author of the joint ct. civ., criminal and land registration procedure codes; puisne judge, sup. ct., Mauritius, Feb., 1917; chmn. of comte. to advise on treatment of former enemy aliens, 1919; chmn., govt. scholarship grants comte., and chmn., rly. enquiry comn., 1923.

ROSS, ALEXANDER HOWARD.—B. 1880; ed. Epsom Coll. and on H.M.S. "Worcester"; supervisor of customs, Gold Coast, 1905; ag. inspr., P.S., 1908 and 1909; ag. dist. comsnr. and travelling comsnr., 1908 and 1909 in Ashanti and Northern Territories; ast. dist. comsnr. Ashanti, 1910; ag. prov. comsnr., 1912 and 1913; ag. dist. comsnr., 1913; dist. comsnr., 1914; ag. prov. comsnr., Mar. to Oct., 1916; passed in criminal law, Tropical African Service Course; dist. comsnr., 1917-18; ag. prov. comsnr., Mar. to Sept., 1919; ag. sec., native affrs., May to June, 1919; prov. comsnr., Sierra Leone, May, 1920.

ROSS, CHARLES RICHARD.—Clt., P.O. savings bank, Cape, 1894; agric. dept., 1894; clk. and acctnt., office of conservator of forests, Midland Conservancy, Cape, 1895; dist. forest offr., George, Cape, 1897; Kokstad, Transkei, 1901; ast. conservator of forests, Transkeian conservancy, 1907; transf. to King William's Town, 1910; conservator of forests, Eastern conservancy, Cape, 1st Apr., 1912; ditto, Western conservancy, Oct., 1916.

ROSS, EDWARD ANDREW.—B. 1895; ed. Leigh Gram. Schl. and Manchester Univ.; on active serv., 1914-19; awarded M.C.; relinquished comsnr., 1921 with rank of capt.; cadet, F.M.S., 1920; ag. ast. contr. of lab., May, 1921; superv. offr., cls. V., May, 1923; ag. dep. contr., lab., Kuala Lumpur, Dec., 1923; ag. ast. dist. offr., lands, Kinta, Mar., 1924; ast. cont., lab., Penang, Mar., 1925; ag. dep. contr., lab. (B), Penang, Jan., 1926; ag. ast. emigr. comsnr., Malaya, Negapatam, May, 1926.

ROSS, ARCHIBALD PARK, M.D., Ch.M. (Edin.), D.P.H. (Edin.); diploma, trop. diseases, Liverpool.—B. 1879; S. African War, Lovat's Scouts, 1900-02; Anglo-Portuguese bdry. comsnr., Zambesia, 1904-5; native rebellion, Natal, 1906; trop. diseases investig., 1906-10; govt. pathologist, Natal, 1911; ast. health offr., Union of S. Africa, statnd. in Natal, 1918; major, S. African med. corps, in ch. sleeping sickness bureau, Durban, and sanitation offr., Natal, 1914-19.

ROSS, J. G. H.—Ast. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., Sept., 1919; ag. junr. ast. sec., Kenya, 10th Nov., 1919 to 26th Oct., 1919; ag. clk. of exec. and leg. couns., 12th Dec., 1919 to 14th Mar., 1920; editor, offl. Gazette, 6th Feb., 1920 to 31st Mar., 1920.

ROSS, JAMES PAULL.—B. 1881; ed. George Watson's Coll. and Edin. Univ.; M.A.; writer to the Signet; capt., 5th Batt. The Royal Scots (Q.E.R.); declined to retain rank of capt. as permission to rejoin for war serv. refused; ast. dist. comsnr., G. Coast, 13th Aug., 1913; ag. ast. col sec., Feb., 1918; dist. comsnr., 9th Apr., 1919; ag. pol. mag., Kumasi, 12th Apr., 1919 to 5th Feb., 1920; ag. ast. col. sec., 29th July to 31st Dec., 1920; ch. census offr. in addnr., 1st ast. col. sec., 1st Jan., 1921; ag. ch. regis. and sheriff, 15th July, 1921 to 22nd Oct., 1921; senr. ast. col. sec., 15th Nov., 1921; pres., Accra town coun., 1921, 1923, 1924, 1925 and 1926; commr. for munic. affrs., 1st Apr., 1926.
ROSS, JAMES SANDFORD.—B. 1884; ed. Merchant Taylor's Schl.; mil. serv., 1914-19; N.W. Frontier campaign, 1915; Salonica Field Force, 1916-19; ast. treas., Kenya, 1919.

ROSS, OSWALD F.—Ed. Malvern Coll. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; capt., N. Staffs. Regt., European War; served Dardanelles expdn., 1915; Mesopotamia, 1916; passed A.T.S. course, 1920; ast. dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, 1920.

ROTH, WALTER E.—B.A. (Oxon), late Demy of Magd. Coll.; M.R.C.S. (Eng.); L.R.C.P. (Lond.); hon. mem. anthrop. societies of Berlin and Florence, and of anthorp. inst., Lond.; late ch. protector of aborigines, Queensland; and Royal comsnr. to inquire into condition of natives of W. Aust.; stip. mag., dep. prot. of Indians, and govt. med. offr., Pomeroon River, B. Guiana, 8th Dec., 1906; stip. mag., Demerara River, 1915; author of "Elements of School Hygiene," 1886, "Theatre Hygiene," 1888, "Ethnological Studies among the N.W. Central Aborigines, Queensland," 1897, "N. Queensland Ethnography Bulletins," "Superstitions, Magic and Medicine," 1903, "Burial Ceremonies and Disposal of the Dead," 1907, "Animism and Folk-lore of the Guiana Indians," 1915, "Arts, Crafts and Customs of the Guiana Indians," 1923, translator of Richard Schomburgk's "Travels in Br. Guiana," 1923.

ROTINI, J. F. AMOSU.—Ed. Baptist elementary schl. and academy, Lagos; marine clk., Calabar, Oct., 1895; ast. regis. of ct., Calabar, June, 1896; dep. regis. of ct., Jan., 1899; trav. supervisor of native cts., S. Nigeria Prot., Sept., 1901, to Apr., 1907; ditto, E. Prov., S. Nigeria, Apr., 1907; has recd. thanks of govt. for services in connection with reorganization of native courts, S. Nigeria, 1902-3.

ROUILLARD, JEAN EDMOND.—Ed. Roy. Coll., Maur., and King Henry VIII.'s schl., Coventry; called to bar, Mid. Tem., 1891; licent. laws, Paris, 1891; ag. dist. mag., Plaines Wilhelms, Maur., Oct., 1939; ag. Crown pros., Feb., 1900; Crown pros., leg. advr., pol. mag., etc., Sevohelie, 4th Sept., 1900; mag., Rodrigues, July, 1902; dist. and stip. mag., Mauritius, 1913.

ROUSE, ROBERT STUART, C.E., A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1888; ed. Truro Coll. and Christ's Hosp.; survr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, Jan., 1912; engnr. drainage wks. office, Dec., 1913; transf. to gen. wks. office, P.W.D., Apr., 1915; ag. exec. engnr., G.W.O., Sept., 1915-16 and from Feb., 1921 to Oct., 1922; offr. in charge, G.W.O., May, 1924.

ROUTH, REGINALD LAMY.—B. 1856; ed. St. Mary's Hall, Southsea, and Cadet school; frigate, H.M.S. "Conway"; extra master's certif., 1893; master of govt. vessels, Lagos, Apr., 1897; ast. marine supt., Lagos, Dec., 1902; ag. marine supt., Dec., 1903, to June, 1904; comdr., H.M.C.S.Y. "Ivy," S. Nigeria, June, 1906; sen. marine offr., E. Prov., S. Nigeria, May, 1907.

ROWAN, ALBERTUS NICOLAAS.—Examr. deeds office, Transvaal, May, 1902; ch. examr., Ditto, Oct., 1905; ast. regis., deeds, Transvaal, Dec., 1918; regis., deeds, mining rights, companies, patents, trade marks, designs

ROWATT, HUGH HOWARD.—B. 1861; ed., Ottawa Coll., Inst., and St. Catherine's Affiliated Coll.; ent. civ. serv., Can., 1887, as correspondent; ch. clk., mining lands br., Mar., 1907; contrlr., mining lands, 1912; supt., 1920.

ROWE, MAJOR R. H., D.S.O., M.C.—B. 1883; ed. Wellington Coll. and Royal Mily. Acad., Woolwich; Royal Army., 1902-25; Bermuda and Jamaica, 1902-6; served with native infantry, Nigeria, 1907-10; Northern Hinterland expd., 1907-8; Niger Cross River expd., 1908-9; operations against Abini, 1909, and against Munich, 1910; dep. dir., surveys, Nigeria, 1911; Nigeria-Dahomey bdrtry. comm., 1912; Great war (Cameroons, France and Russia), 1914-18; survr.-gen., Gold Coast, 1920; comsnr., lands, in addn., 1924; survr.-gen., Sierra Leone, in addn., 1925; Gold Coast rep. with staff of H.R.H. the Prince of Wales on his visit to W. Africa, 1925; chmn., comtees, on pub. wks. acct., rly. constrn., munic. finance and mily. defence; mem., leg. coun., 1926; comsnr., lands, Nigeria, 27th Oct., 1926.

ROWELL, THOMAS HUBERT.—Clk., loco. dept., I.M.R., Pretoria, Oct., 1900; acctg. brch., ch. loco. supt.'s office, Jan., 1903; acctg. off., ch. mech. engnr., dept., July, 1908; inspr., audit dept., S.A.R. and Harbrs., Oct., 1910; inspr., ast. contr. and audir. gen.'s dept., Union of S. Africa, May, 1911; inspr., (rly. and hbrs.) control and audit dept., 1st July, 1916; senr. inspr., 1st Oct., 1922; ag. ch. inspr., rly. and harbrs., 24th Apr. to 11th Sept., 1924.

ROWLEY, FORTESCUE WILLIAM.—B. 1874; ed. Canterbury Univ. Coll.; entd. N.Z. dept. of labr., 1893; dep. chief inspr. of factories, 1907; chief clk. and dep. chief inspr., 1911; sec. of labr., chief inspr. of factories, req'd. of industrial unions; and supt. of workers' dwellings, 1914.

RUDDICK, JOHN ARCHIBALD, LL.D.—B. 1862, dairy expert; ast. to dairy comsnr., Canada, 1891; prin. dairy schl., Kingston, Ont., 1896; dairy comsnr., New Zealand, 1898; ast. to comsnr. of agr., Can., 1900; dairy comsnr., Can., 1905; dairy and cold storage comsnr., Can., 1906; hon. LL.D. (Queen's Univ.), 1924.

RUGGLES, NEPEAN CLARKE, K.C., LL.B.—Barrister of sup. ct., Nova Scotia; Bachelor of Laws at Dalhousie Coll., Halifax, Canada, with honrs. 1891; called to the bar, Nova Scotia, with highest honours, 1891; K.C., Nova Scotia, Jan., 1911, served with the Canadian forces during S. African War; now on the reserve of officers. Canadian militia; public prosecutor at Barberton, Transvaal, 1902; ast. res. mag., 1903 to 1907; mag., dist. F., Dominica, Oct., 1909 to Dec., 1915; ag. mag., Roseau and regiar., sup. ct. in addition on various occasions; took census of Dominica, 1911; mem., leg. coun., Dominica; mag., Grenada, 1915 to 1920; chmn., various comms. while in Grenada; atty.-gen., St. Vincent, Aug., 1920 to Apr., 1921; ag. ch. just for short period; 1st cls. stip. mag., B. Guiana, Apr., 1921.

RUNDLE, GEN. SIR HENRY MACLEOD LESLIE, R.A.—B. 1856; G.C.B. (1911); G.C.M.G. (1914); G.C.V.O. (1911); K.C.B., 1898; K.C.M.G., 1901; D.S.O., 1897; ed. Roy. Mil. Acad., Woolwich; entd. R.A., 1876; maj.-gen., 1896; served in Zulu war, 1879; Boer war, 1881; Egyptian war, 1882; Nile expdn., 1884-5; Soudan Frontier field force, 1885-7; Soudan Frontier, 1889; Dongola expdtn. force, 1896; adjt.-gen., Egyptian army; Khartoum expdn., 1898; comdg. S.E. Dist., 1898-9; dep. adjt.-gen. to the forces, 1899-1900; in comd. of div., Aldershot, 1900; lieut.-gen. on staff in comd. of 8th Div., S. African field force, 1900-1902; lieut.-gen. of Nthn. Comd., 1905-7; col.-comdt., R.A., 1907; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Malta, 1909-1915.
RUSCOE, H. G.—Asst. supt., pol., Kenya, Sept., 1923.

RUSHMER, JAMES.—B. 1876; entered C.O., Oct., 1893; apptd., after exam., library attendant, 1st July, 1896; promoted, after exam., under order-in-council of 1910, clk. of the 2nd div., 18th Feb., 1911; ast. sec. to colonial blue-books comte., 1917-1918; seconded for serv. at oversea settlement office, Apr., 1920.

RUSHTON, CHARLES BERKELY.—Clk., comsnr. of rlys. dept., W. Aust., Apr., 1896; ch. clk., July, 1896; ag. sec. to comsnr. of rlys., 23rd Aug., 1901; sec. to ditto, 18th Dec., 1901; sec., W. Australian London Agency, 3rd Nov., 1919.

RUSHTON, HERBERT HARVEY.—B. 1876; acct., electric light dept., Gibraltar, 1897; ast. sec. and ast. acct., munic. authority, 1902; ch. acct. and ast. sec., 1903; sec. and finan. offr., 1908 (also man., pub. electricity and water supplies, mem., comte., on exchange and currency); ast. ch. acct., Uganda Rly., E. Africa Prot., 1915; lieut., E.A. Forces, 1915; capt., 1916; ag. dep. ch. acct., Uganda Rly., 1916 to June, 1919 (also govt. rep. Nairobi munic. coun., 1918, mem., gen. manager's advisory comte., 1919); ag. ch. acct. to Uganda Rly., June to Dec., 1919; dep. treas., Kenya Colony, 1920 and 1921; ag. treas., Aug., 1920 to Apr., 1921; mem., exec. and leg. couns. (also chmn., currency and other comtees.) col. treas., Fiji, 1922 (with charge of group of depts., cust., post and marine, transport, posts, telegraphs and telephones, gov't. stores, and returned soldiers' dairy sttlmt.); mem., exec. and leg. couns.; comsnr. of currency, mem., educn., mining and other bds.; chmn., many comtees.

RUSSELL, MAJOR-GEN. SIR ANDREW HAMILTON.—K.C.B. (1918); K.C.M.G. (1915).—B. 1868; held comen. as lieut. for 5 yrs. in Imperial Army; vol. offr., New Zealand, 1900; hon. A.D.C. to H.E. the Gov. of N.Z., 1911; brig.-gen., 1914; temp. major-gen., 1915; major-gen. in command of N.Z. Div., 1916; foreign decoration, Order of Danilo (Montenegro), 1917.

RUSSELL, HON. BENJAMIN, K.C., M.A., D.C.L.—Ed. at Halifax gram. schl. and Mount Allison Univ.; called to the bar, 1872; for some years law adviser to legis. coun., Nova Scotia; elec. to H. of C., Canada, 1896; judge of supreme ct., Nova Scotia, 1904.

RUSSELL, THOMAS BROWNLEE.—B. 1873; ed. Pembroke Coll., Oxford, M.A.; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1897; office asst. to govt. agt., E.Prov., Mar., 1899; pol. mag., Balapitiya, Nov., 1899; dist. judge, Taungalla, Aug., 1900; office asst. to govt. agt., N.C. Prov., Nov., 1901; Uva Prov., Nov., 1902; dist. judge, Batticaloa, July, 1903; office asst. to govt. agent, N. Prov., Nov., 1903; pol. mag., Jaffra, Jan., 1904; ag. dist. judge, Jaffra, Mar., 1904; pol. mag., Kandy, July, 1905; dist. judge, Kalutara, Dec., 1910; ag. dist. judge, Kurunegala, June, 1915; dist. judge, Batticaloa, Aug., 1915; acted on two occasions in 1915 and 1916 as addtl. agt. gov't., E. Prov. in addition to his own duties; comsnr. of requests, Colombo, Mar., 1918; dist. judge, Galle, Nov., 1920; govt. agt., S. Prov., Feb., 1924.

RUSSELL, WILLIAM ALISON, K.C.—B. 1875; ed. Rugby and Trin. Coll. Camb.; B.A., LL.B., honours, 1897; barrister-at-law, Inner Temp., 1900; equity bar, 1900-6; apptd. Crown advoc., pub. proscr., admstr. gen., prin. regis. of docmts., regis. of companies, Uganda Procr., May, 1906; Crown advoc. and pub. proscr., Jan., 1909; ag. chief sec. to govt., Aug., 1909, to Mar., 1910; Crown advoc. and pub. proscr., Mar. to May, 1910; ag. chief sec. to govt., June, 1910 to May, 1911; Crown advoc. and pub. proscr., June, 1911 to June, 1912; King's advoc., Cyprus, July, 1912; mem. of exec. and legis. couns., 1912; joint commr. under Reprint of Statutes Law, 1913; edition publ., Feb., 1914; K.C., Cyprus, 1924; ch. just., Tanganyika Territory, 1924; published, "Legislative and Other Forms," (1924).

RUTHERFORD, GEORGE JAMES, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lond.—Ed. at Epsom Coll. and Middlesex Hosp.; asst. col. surg., G. Coast, 1897; on spec. serv. in Lagos during the Sarki expdn., 1897-98; sen. med. offr., G. Coast, 1907; sen. med. officer, S. Nigeria, 1907; prov. med. offr., Ashanti, 1910; asst. prin. civ. med. offr. and inspr. gen. of hospitals, Ceylon, 1911; Ceylon deleg. at "All India" sanitary confcn., 1911-12; ag. prin. civ. med. offr., Ceylon, Oct. to Dec., 1912, Feb. to Dec., 1913, and from June to Aug., 1915; prin. civ. med. offr. and inspr. gen. of hosps., Aug., 1915; M.L.C.; sec., col. advisory med. and sanv. comte, at C.O., 1924.

RUTHERFORD, CAPT. PETER WILLIAM, M.C. (1916), F.R.G.S.—B. 1894; ed. Brighton Coll.; 2nd lieut., W. Yorks Regt., 1914; lieut., 1916; seconded, Flying Corps, 1916; capt., 1917; retd. from army, 1919; asst. dist. commr., G. Coast, 20th Sept., 1921.

RUXTON, MAJOR UPTON FITZHERBERT, C.M.G.—B. 1873; asst. res., Nigeria, 6th Feb., 1901; 2nd cls. res., 1st Oct., 1902; 1st cls. res., 1st Oct., 1908; seconded to Admty., 1st Apr., 1916 to 27th May, 1919; seconded to F.O. for polit. duties, Constantinople, 28th May, 1919; returned to Nigeria, 14th Aug., 1921; lieut.-gov., S. Provs., Nigeria, 17th Oct., 1925.

RYAN, HUGH KATE, M.A., LL.M., etc. (Cantab).—Barrister-at-Law, Inner Temple (1905); mem., Western Circuit; special pleader; author of several legal works; mag., B.E. Africa, 1913; joined Inns of Court O.T.C., 1914; depot adjutant; selected by mily. authorities for secret work, 1917; seconded for serv., Royal Engineers, 1918; joined directorate, I.W. and D., War Office; ment. in desps. and awarded O.B.E. (mily. divn.) asst. atty.-gen., Jamaica, 1918; ag. atty.-gen., 1922; D.B.C., Cyprus, 1923.

RYCROFT, MAJ.-GEN. SIR WILLIAM HENRY, K.C.B. (1919), K.C.M.G. (1918), C.M.G. (1915), C.B. (1910).—B. 1861; ed. Eton and Sandhurst; joined 71st H.L.I., 1879; served in Nile expdn. (including desert march to Metembeh), 1884-85 (medal and clasp and bronze star); Egyptian Army, 1886-87; action of Sarras (desps., 4th cls. Medjidie); promoted into 7th Dragoon Guards, 1888; staff coll., 1891-92; D.A.A.G., York, 1895-96; promoted into 11th Hussars, 1896; commanded 11th Hussars, 1904-8; staff, S. Africa, 1911-12; A.Q.M.G., Southern Command, 1913-14; service, N.W. Frontier of India, 1897-98 (medal and clasp); D.A.A.G., Cairo, 1898-99; served in S. Africa on staff, 1899-1900 (desps., medal and 2 clasps); Somaliland, 1902-3 (desps., medal with clasp); European War, 1914-18 (desps., 7 times, prom. maj.-gen., Knight of Grace, St. John of Jerusalem, Legion of Honour, Croix de Commandeur, Orders of White Eagle of Serbia, Redeemer of Greece, Star of Roumania, Greek Order of Merit 2nd cls.); retd. pay, 1921; govt., N. Borneo, 1922.
SADLER, C.—Asst. gen. man., govt. rly., Trinidad, 1st Dec., 1914; traffic man., Nigerian govt. rly., Aug., 1922; gen. man., Trinidad govt. rly., 16th Jan., 1926.

SAINTSBURY, JOHN SEYMOUR.—Called to the bar, Lincoln's Inn, 1906; 1st cls. in evidence, procedure and criminal law, bar exams., 1904; certif. of honour from council of legal educ. at Bar Final, 1906, and a prize of 50/., 1883; in P.O., 1884; 7th clsk., P.O., June, 1886; 4th clsk., audit off., Sept., 1890; offr. of cust., Dec., 1890; inland rev. offr., Oct., 1905; ag. pol. mag., dist. "E," July to Nov., 1906; ag. pol. mag., dist. "A," June to Nov., 1911, and from Apr., 1912 to June, 1913; ag. judge, Bridgetown petty debt ct., 30th June to 31st Oct., 1913; pol. mag. and judge of petty debt ct., dist. F., Sept., 1915; pol. mag. dist. "E," 1919; judge, Bridgetown petty debt ct., 1921; judge, asst. ct. of appeal, 1925; regnr., 1925.
SALISBURY, THOMAN RONALD FPOULKS.—B. 1882; served in Fiji, and under the condominium govt., New Hebrides; govt. agt., New Hebrides, 18th Mar., 1922.
SALMON, T. G. A.—Asst. dist. commr., E.A.P., Dec., 1936.
SAMBIDGE, GERALD CHURCHILL.—B. 1886; ed. King Edward's Schl., Birmingham; served Nigeria Regt., 27th Mar., 1915; instr. in arts and crafts, N. Provs., Nigeria, 1st Dec., 1920.

SAMUEL, RT. HON. SIR HERBERT LOUIS, P.C. (1908), G.B.E. (1920), M.A.—B. 1870; ed. University Coll. Schl. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; M.P., Cleveland Divn., N. Riding, Yorks., 1902-18; parly. under sec., home dept., 1905-9; chancellor of the Duchy of Lancaster (with a seat in the cabinet), 1909-10 and 1915-16; P.M.G., 1910-14 and 1915-16; pres. of the L.G.B., 1914-15; sec. of state for home affairs, 1916; chmn. of the select comte, of house of commons on national expenditure, 1917-18; British sp. commr. to Belgium, 1919; grand offr. of the Order of Leopold of Belgium, 1919; high commr., Palestine, 1920-25.

SAMUEL, SILVERIC I., M.B.E. (1924).—Ed. at St. Gregory's Rom. Cath. grm. schl., Lagos; mesar and copyt., educ. off., Oct., 1897; clk. to inspr. of schl., Mar., 1899; sec. to comte for exhibtn. of schl. wks., 1902 and 1903; clk. and interpreter to trav. comsnr., Ilesha, interior dept., Feb., 1904; 3rd cls. clk., govr.'s off., Aug., 1904; conf. clk. to the gov. of Nigeria.

SAMUELS, WILLIAM FREDERICK.—B. 1876; ed. Chesterfield Sch. Sch., Birr., King's Co., Ireland, Corrig Sch., Kingstown and Trinity Coll., Dublin; med. offr. and mag., Anguilla, B.W. Indies, Apr., 1909; med. supt., cent. mental hosp., F.M.S., Dec., 1910; publications, "General paralyses of the Insane in the F.M.S." (Jnl. of Mental Science, Apr., 1916); "A case of Pellagra in the Cent. Asylum, Tanjong Rambutan, F.M.S." (Jnl. of Mental Science, Oct., 1917); "Malaria and Insanity" (Transactions of the Far Eastern Assn. of Trop. Med., 1923); "A History of the Central Mental Hosp., Tanjong Rambutan" (Jnl. of Mental Science, Jan., 1925).
SANDERSON, G. M.—M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P.; med. offr., Nyassaland, 1910; temp. major, Nyassaland Field Force, Aug., 1914; sanitation offr., Nov., 1921.

SANDERSON, JAMES.—Clk. to ch. just., Barbados, 1867; clk. to gen. bd. of health, Barbados, Apr., 1871, also clk. to quarantine bd. in 1880; selr., atty., and proctor, Barbados, in 1873; was deleg. for Barbados to quarantine confe. at Demerara in 1888; govt. dir. on bd. of Barbados rly. co. in 1891; comsnr. of probates, 1904; J.P., 1904.

SANDFORD, GEORGE RITCHIE, O.B.E. (1924), Barrister-at-Law, Inner Temple, 1924.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E. Africa, Prot., Feb., 1915; priv. sec. to gov., 1918; junr. asst. sec. and clk. of couns., Sept., 1922; ag. senr. asst. sec., Mar. to Sept., 1923; resig., 1925; clk. to leg. coun., Jan., 1926; edlr., official gazette, Mar., 1926.
SANDS, WILLIAM H.—B. 1869 pub. schl. teacher, Bahamas, 1889; Out Island comsnr. (4th div.) and teacher, 1917.

SANDYS, MELVILL KEVERNE TRELAWNY, M.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1890; on mily. serv., Aug., 1914; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1919; attd., Ratnapura Kach., Nov., 1919; office asst. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Mar., 1922; ag. asst. govt. agt., Puttalam, Oct., to Dec., 1922; ag. asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, July, 1923; dist. judge, Anuradhapura, July, 1925; pol. mag., Kurunegala, Oct., 1926.

SANGER-DAVIES, ARTHUR ELPHINSTONE.—B. 1885; ed. Dover Coll., Coopers Hill and Oxford (Diploma of Forestry, 1907); asst. conservator, forests, grade II., F.M.S. forest dept., 1907; grade I., 1912; dep. conservator, forests, 1919; ag. conserv., forests, S.S. and F.M.S., June, 1923; dep. conserv., forests, Oct., 1923.

SANSON, CHARLES HENRY.—B. 1886; ed. Plymouth and Munnamead Coll.; Barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1923; pol. probationer, 1905; ag. asst. commr. of pol., 1908; ag. regis. of criminals, Selangor, 1909; regis. of criminals, F.M.S. and S.S., 1910; asst. commr. of pol., Ipoh, 1911; adjt., F.M.S. pol., 1913-20; commr. of pol., Johore, 1921-24; ag. commr. of pol., F.M.S., 1924.

SARAVANAMUTTU, P.—B. 1894; B.A., Lond.; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., local divn., Aug., 1919; attached to Colombo, Kach., Aug., 1919; ag. office asst. to govt. agt., Kalutara, Feb., 1920; attd. to Matale Kach., June, 1920; addnl. dist. judge, Badulla, Mar., 1921; pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, Sept., 1921; ditto, Kurunegala, June, 1922; addnl. asst. govt. agt., Colombo, Mar., 1923; addnl. asst. dir., educ., Dec., 1925.
SARGRANT, CAPT. R. G., A.I.N.A., F.R.G.S.—Port. offr., Kenya, Jan., 1917; ag. port capt., 1926.
SARGENT, JOHN DENYS, B.A. (Oxon.), Dipl., Forestry, Oxford.—B. 1885; asst. conserv., forests, Ceylon, Dec., 1909; dep. conserv., grade II, Apr., 1917; grade I, May, 1919; conserv., Sept., 1921.

SARWAR, HAFIZ GHULAM.—Cadet, S.S., Sept., 1897; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, Jan. to June, 1900; and from Sept., 1900, to Nov., 1901; passed final exam., Chinese, July, 1900; 3rd mag., Sing., 29th Apr., 1902; ag. sheriff, deputy-regir., and asst. official assignee, Penang, May, 1902; dep. regir., sup. ct., Penang, Dec., 1903; asst. regir., sup. ct., Malacca, May, 1907; dep. regir., sup. ct., Penang, Apr., 1908; ag. regir., July, 1909; ag. dist. judge, May, 1915; ag. censor, Dec., 1915 to Jan., 1916; ag. offr. assignee, Penang, in addition, May, 1916; regir. and sheriff, sup. ct., Penang, also asst. regir. of cos., Penang, Jan., 1917; offr., cls. II., Jan., 1919.

SAUNDERS, CHARLES JAMES, B.A.—B. 1868; ed. Merchant Taylors and St. John's Coll., Oxon; cadet, S.S., 1891; passed final exam. in Chin., Feb., 1895; ag. 2nd asst. prot. of Chin., Feb., 1895; ag. 3rd mag., Sing., May, 1895; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, Dec., 1896; ag. dist. offr., Penang, Mar., 1897; dist. offr., Malacca, May, 1897; ag. asst. prot. of Chin., Sing., Aug., 1897; ag. asst. prot. of Chin., and supt. of Ind. imgrn., Penang, and munic. comsnr. for Penang, 1898; ag. asst. prot. of Chin., Sing., Aug., 1898, to Feb., 1899; also ag. 3rd mag., Sing., Sept., 1898; 2nd asst. prot. of Chin., Penang, Aug., 1898; ag. 2nd mag., Sing., May, 1900; ag. offr. assignee, S. S., Sept. to Dec., 1900, Jan., 1901, to Feb., 1905, and Feb., 1906; sheriff, dep. regir., supreme court, and asst. official assignee, Penang, May, 1902; munic. comsnr. for Sing., Oct., 1900, and Sept., 1903; also ag. prot. of Chin., Sing., Apr., 1904; also asst. supt. of Indian imgrts.; asst. prot. of Chin., Penang, May, 1904; ag. sec. for Chin. affairs, S.S. and F.M.S., Apr., 1907; ag. comsnr., ct. of requests, Singapore, Jan., 1908; ag. dist. judge, Apr., 1908; sec. for Chinese affairs, S.S. and F.M.S., Feb., 1910; ag. offr. assignee, Mar., 1914; regir. of cos. and offr. assignee, Jan., 1915; pres., rent assessment bd., Sept., 1917.

SAUNDERS, SIR CHARLES JAMES RENAULT, K.C.M.G., 1906. C.M.G., 1895.—B. 1857; held various apptsms. in Natal civ. ser., 1876-87, when he ratified treaty with Queen of Amatongaland (desps.); res. mag., Eshowe, and J.P. for Zululand, 1888; defined bndry. between Amatongaland and Zululand, 1889 (desps.); variously employed under Zululand Govt., 1889-95 (desps.), when he was apptd. spec. comsnr. for Trans-Pongoland territory, carried out final act of annexation of that territory and declared British prot. for Amatongaland; acted as res. comsnr. and chief mag. of Zululand, 1896; apptd. chief mag. and civ. comsnr. for prov. of Zululand on its incorporation with Natal, 1897; medal, S. African War, 1899-1902; col. representative on Zululand Lands Delimitation Coms., 1902, and comsnr. for native affairs, 1905; ret., 1909.

SAUNDERS, JOHN C.—B. 1862; ed. pub. schls. and High Schl., Halifax, N.S.; Acadia Univ., Wolfville, N.S.; ent. pub. serv., 23rd Mar., 1882; transferred to finance dept., Ottawa, 1st July, 1887; asst. dep. min. of finance, 1st Jan., 1917; dep. min. of finance, 1st Apr., 1920.

SAVAGE, GEORGE.—B. 1887; supt. of registration, G.P.O., Singapore, Oct., 1914; supt. of mails, ditto, 1st Jan., 1915; ag. supt. of mails, Penang, Jan., 1915; ag. senr. supt. of mails, Feb., 1917; supt. of mails, Penang, but continued ag. senr. supt. of mails, July, 1917; supt., grade II, Jan., 1919; ag. supt., money order branch and savings dept., May, 1919 and Nov., 1920; supt., grade I, but cont. to act as above, Jan., 1920; supt., posts and telegraphs, Malacca, Sept., 1920; ag. supt., money order branch and savings bank dept., 27th Nov., 1920; ag. ast. P.M.G., Singapore, 1st Apr., 1921; ag. acctnt., G.P.O., Singapore, 26th July, 1921 to 1st Nov., 1921; ag. ast. P.M.G., 25th July, 1922.

SAVARY, JOHN LEWIS HENRY WILLIAM.—B. 1884; col. schol., Trinidad, 1901; Gray's Inn, 1902; prizeman in evidence, procedure and criminal law exam., 1904; barrister-at-law, Nov., 1905; practised at bar, Trinidad, 1906-24; retainer from Trinidad govt. as junr. coun. to atty.-gen., Jan., 1916-Dec., 1923; ag. solr.-gen., June to Oct., 1921 and Nov., 1922-Nov., 1923; reglar. and marshal and reglar.-gen. and prin. reglar., West Indian ct. of appeal, Jan., 1925; ag. puisne judge, May-June, 1925, Oct., 1925-Apr., 1926 and from June, 1926.

SAVI, VICTOR GEORGE.—Pol. probationer, S. Stltmts., Nov., 1904; ast. supt. of pol., Jan., 1908; passed final exam. in Punjabi, Feb., 1910; ag. 2nd supt. of pol., Aug., 1910 and Feb., 1914; ag. supt. of pol., Dec. to Feb., 1914; ag. supt. of pol., Malacca, Sept., 1916; ast. supt. of pol., Penang, Oct., 1917; ag. dir., crim. intelligence, Jan., 1919; dir., crim. intelligence, Jan., 1920; ag. supt., pol. and ch. pol. offr., Singapore, May, 1921; dep. comsnr. pol., Perak, Mar., 1922; ag. dep. pub. pros., Perak, in addn., July, 1925.

SAWARD, CAPT. SIDNEY CARLTON, M.C. (1916), M.M. (1916).—B. 1889; fellow of surveyors' inst.; served with 509 (1-1) Lond. Field Coy., R.E., 6th divn.; home serv., 6th Aug. to 11th Dec., 1914; France and Germany, 12th Dec., 1914 to June, 1919; Ireland, June to Aug., 1919; commdg. cadre, 12th Field Co., R.E.; commdg., 8th Sept., 1915; lieut., 1st June, 1916; capt., 22nd Oct., 1918; ag. major, 4th Dec., 1918 to 29th Jan., 1919; ment. in despa., 16th Mar., 1919; survr., Gold Coast, 5th Nov., 1919; field supervisor, 1st Apr., 1924; ag. dep. survr.-gen., 14th Feb. to 4th Aug., 1925, 21st Apr. to 31st Aug., 1926 and from 27th Oct., 1926.

SAWREY-COOKSON, SYDNEY SPENCER, B.A.—B. 1876; ed. Uppingham and New Coll., Oxford; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1903; judicial comsnr., Brit. N. Borneo, 1910-20; judge, sup. ct., Gambia, 1920; puisne judge, Gold Coast, 11th June, 1925.

SAXTON, SIDNEY WARING.—Ed. Toulouse (France), and Dean Close Schol., Cheltenham; paymr. cadet, R.N., 14th July, 1905; paymr.-lieut.-comdr., 19th Mar., 1918; 1st cls. French interpreter; lent to C.O. and apptd. ast. dist. comsnr., G. Coast, 15th Sept., 1914, but did not take up apptmt., having resumed duty in H.M. Navy for the period of the war; assumed duties as junr. ast. col. sec., G. Coast, 16th Apr., 1919; ag. ast. col. sec., 16th May, 1919; ag. dist. comsnr., Accra, and ag. trav. comsnr. for Accra-Akwapim and Birrim dists., 30th Oct., 1919; invalided, H.M. Navy, 1920; passed Tropical African service course (all subjects, 2nd part).

SAYERS, GEOFFREY ROBERT, B.A.—B. 1887; ed. at Highgate schl. (founderion) and Queen's Coll., Oxford; open exhibiter in classics and Fitzgerald exhibition; 2nd cls. class. mods.; 3rd cls. Lit. Hum. (B.A., 1910); cadet, Hong Kong, 24th Oct., 1910; passed in Cantonese, Nov., 1912; ag. ast. dist. offr. (South), 1912 to 1913; ag. ast. supt. of police, 1913 to 1914; ag. dep. offr. recvr., 1914 to 1915; ag. chief ast. sec. for Chinese affairs 1914 and 1915, in addition to other duties; priv. sec. to gov., in addition to other duties, 1915; attached to crown solr.'s off., 1915; passed final exam. in Hakka, July, 1915; ag. chief ast. sec. for Chinese affairs, 1915; on milly. serv., 1915-19; dep. reglar. and appraiser, Hong Kong, 1920; head of sany. dept., 1920; passed in Mandarin, May, 1921; ast. to treasy., 1924; ast. treas. and comsnr. of estate duties, Apr., 1925.

SAYERS, ELDER FRANK.—B. 1889; ed. King's Coll. Schol., Wimbledon, St. John's Coll., Cambridge, and Grenoble Univ., B.A., LL.B. (Cantab.); barrister-at-law (Inner Temple), Apr., 1913; passed lower and higher stand. exams. in Temne, and lower stand. exam. in Koranko; war service, E. Africa, 1918; ast. dist. comsnr., Sierra Leone, 1st Jan., 1915; dist. comsnr., 21st Aug., 1922.

SAYERS, FREDERICK RICHARDSON.—M.D., D.P.H., Dublin Univ.; ed. Rathmines Schol., Dublin, The Meat Hosp., Dublin, and Trim Coll., Dublin; res. med. offr., Monkstown Hosp., Dublin, 1909-12; med. offr., S. Settmts., 2nd Mar., 1912; med. offr., gen. hosp., Penang, 27th Apr., 1912; med. offr., dist. hosp., Penang, 2nd June, 1912; port health offr. and health offr., rural areas, Penang, cor., Penang, and med. offr. in charge of leper asylum, Pulau Jerejak, Penang, 3rd Sept., 1912; ag. ast. port health offr., Singapore, 10th Sept., 1912; resumed duties in Penang, 21st Oct., 1912; ast. censor, Penang, in addition, Sept., 1915, to Apr., 1916; ag. sen. health offr., S. Settmts., in addition, 1st Mar., 1917; ag. ast. health offr., rural areas, Singapore, 20th Mar., 1919; ag. chief health offr., S. Settmts., in addition, 4th Apl., 1919; lecturer in hygiene, King Edward VII. medical schl., Singapore, 10th June, 1919; ag. ch. health offr., Singapore, Oct., 1920 and in 1923 and 1925; senr. health offr., Penang, 28th Feb., 1921; promoted cls. B., 1st Jan., 1925; mem., Br. delegation to internat. sany. conf., Paris, 8th May to 21st June, 1926.

SAYERS, GERALD FLINT.—B. 1893; ed. Westminster and Christ Church, Oxford; B.A. (hons.) 1914; ast. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Feb., 1915; ag. senr. ast. sec., Nov., 1919 to July, 1920; ast. sec., Tanganyika territory, 21st July, 1920; ag. senr. ast. sec., for various periods 1920-24; elk. to exec. coun., Sept., 1923 to Jan., 1924; ast. to C.O., Aug., 1924.
SCADDAN, HON. JOHN, C.M.G. (1924), M.L.A.—B. 1876; leader of Parly. Lab. party in W. Aust. since Aug., 1910; elected for Ivanhoe, June, 1904; premier of W. Australia, 1911-1916.

SCAMMELL, ERNEST HENRY.—B. 1873; ed. privately; sec. various business concerns, 1891-1907; visited Australia, engaged in business and journalism in Canada, 1907-1913; organizing sec. Canadian Peace Centenary Association, under the auspices of the govt. of Canada, 1913; ast. cable censor, dept. of militia and defence, Canada, 1915; sec., mil. hosps. comsnr., Canada, 1915; ast. dep. min. and sec., dept. of soldiers' civil re-estabmt., Canada, 1918; negotiated reciprocal agreements between Canadian, British and United States Govts., regarding treatment and training of Naval and Military Forces resident in these countries, 1919; Fellow of the Chartered Institute of Secretaries (London); Fellow of the Royal Colonial Institute (London); mem. of the Exec. of the Canadian Peace Centenary Assoc.

SCHMIDT, CARL FRIEDRICH, B.A.—Civ. serv. law clk., law dept., Cape, May, 1893; control... and audit office, July, 1893; asst. examnr. of accts., Jan., 1894; examnr., May, 1898; ch. examnr., July, 1907; inspr. of accts., Jan., 1909; prin. clk., Apr., 1912; sec., educn. dept., Bloemfontein, 16th Aug., 1915; ag. dir. of educn., O.F.S., July, 1918; dir. of educn., O.F.S., June, 1920; sec. for interior, 13th Sept., 1926.
SCHNEIDER, GUALTERUS STEWART, K.C.—B. 1864; ag. puisne just., Ceylon, Jan., 1921; comsrr. of Navy, Nov., 1921; puisne just., Dec., 1921; ag. ch. just., 20th Sept., 1926.

SCHOCH, AUGUST ARNOLD.—B. 1883; ed. Berne, Switzerland; graduated, Leiden, LL.D. and called to bar, Middle Temple, 1910; ent. Union pub. serv., 1911; priv. sec. to min. of justice, 1913-16; professional asst. to atty.-gen. (Transvaal), 1916-24; solr.-gen., Cape Eastern Dists., Aug., 1924; law adviser and Dutch draughtsman, Sept., 1925.

SCHOFIELD, WALTER.—B. 1888; ed. Univ. of Liverpool, M.A.; non-coll. student, Oxford, 1908-1911, M.A.; cadet, Hong Kong, 17th Nov., 1911; attached to secretariat for Chinese affairs, 1914; asst. cable censor, in addition to other duties, 1914 to 1915; passed cadet, 12th Feb., 1915; ag. 3rd asst. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1915; ag. 2nd ditto, 1915; passed law exam., 1916; seconded to H. Kong and Shanghai Bank, 1918; ag. 2nd asst. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1918 to 1919, ag. dep. regiar. and appraiser, in addn. to other duties, 1919; ag. chief asst. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1919; ag. 2nd asst. to ditto, 1919, 1921, 1922 and 1923; ag. ch. asst., Aug., 1920 to Nov., 1921; dist. offr., South in addn., 1922; dist. offr., South, 1923.

SCHOLEFIELD, GUY HARDY, D.Sc. (Lond.), O.B.E. (1919).—Ed. Tokomairiro High Schl. and Univ. of N.Z. (Bowen and Macmillan Brown prizeman) and Univ. of London; lib. librarian, gen. assembly lib. and contrlr., dominion archives, N.Z., 1926.

SCHRADER, LOUIS WILLIAM CONRAD.—B. 1873; ed. Highgate School and Lincoln Coll., Oxford (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1896; off. asst. to govt. agt., Prov. of Uva, April, 1897; pol. mag. Galagadara, July, 1899; off. asst. to govt. agt., S. Prov., Nov., 1899; N. C. Prov., Mar., 1901; asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Nov., 1901; dist. judge, Ratnapura, Jan., 1903; asst. coll. of cuat., Trincomalee, Dec., 1904; dist. judge, Tangalla, Feb., 1905; asst. govt. agt., Hambantota, April, 1907; dist. judge, Badulla, Oct., 1909; ag. dist. judge, Galle, Aug., 1912; dist. judge, Matara, May, 1913; comsrr. of requests, Colombo, Oct., 1913; ag. dist. judge, Galle, Feb., 1914; dist. judge, Kalutara, June, 1914; ditto, Negombo, Oct., 1915; ditto, Galle, Dec., 1915; ag. govt. agt., E. Prov., Sept., 1918; ditto, Prov. of Uva, June to Sept., 1919; govt. agt., N. Prov., Nov., 1921; ditto, E. Prov., Oct., 1922; regiar. gen., Mar., 1923; govt. agt., N. Prov., July, 1923; dist. judge, Galle, Aug., 1924; govt. agt., S. Prov., 15th Mar., 1926.

SCHWARZ, ERNEST HUBERT LEWIS, A.R.C.S. Hons., London.—B. 1873; ed. Westminster and Royal Coll. of Science; ed., "Scientific African," 1895; joined Cape govt. survey, 1896; professor, Rhodes University Coll., Grahamstown, 1905; author of "South African Geology," "South African Geography," "Causal Geology," "The Kalahari, or Thirstland Redemption."

SCICLUNA, HANNIBAL P., L.P.—B. 1880; ed. St. Ignatius' Coll., Malta; matric., Malta Univ., 1897; ent. Malta civ. serv., 1902; legal procurator, 1905; sec. and regiar., Malta Univ., 1913-20; rep. of govt. emigr. dept. in devastated regions, France, 1920; attd., ministry of justice, 1921; esquire, Order of St. John of Jerusalem (Rome), 1910; Officier d'Academie (France), 1921; mem. of the Societe Academique d'Histoire Internationale de Paris, 1923; libr., Malta public library, July, 1923.

SCIORTINO, J. C.—B. 1875; ed. at Lyceum Univ., Malta; in service of Eastern and S. African telegraph companies, 1891-1903; rev. offr., N. Nigeria, 26th Sept., 1903; ass't. res., 1904; 3rd cl. res., 29th Apr., 1907; 2nd cl. res., 15th June, 1910; senr. res., 12th June, 1921.

SCOTLAND, DOUGLAS WILLIAM, F.S.I., N.D.A., N.D.D., H.A.A.C. Dip.—Ed. at Musoroe Schi., India and Alleynes Schi., Stone, Staffs.; Agric. Coll., Newport, Salop, 1904-1907; Salop County Scholar, 1906-1907; Profsnl. Assoc. of Survrs. Inst., 1909; agric. instr., Tyrone (Board of Agric. Ireland), 1907-08; lectr. at Harper-Adams agric. coll., 1908-11; asst., agric. dept., Sierra Leone, 1911-1915; ag. dir. of agric. on several occasions; dir. of agric., Sierra Leone, 3rd Dec., 1915; visited W. Indies and Br. Guiana on behalf of Sierra Leone govt., Nov., 1913 to Feb., 1914; del. to oil palm confce. at C.O., Sept., 1919; ag. comsrr., lands and forests, 20th Feb., 1924 to 31st Jan., 1925; M.L.C., Nov., 1924 to 31st Jan., 1925; del. to 2nd Imp. Entomological confce., 9th to 18th June, 1925.
SCOTLAND, ERICA ELEANOR.—Ent. C.O. as typist, July, 1913; shorthand writer, Aug., 1918; cler. offr., 9th Mar., 1922.

SCOTT, DUNCAN CAMPBELL, F.R.S.C.—B. 1862; apptd. to Indian comsrr.'s off., Canada, 1879; jun. 2nd cl. clk., dept. of Indian affairs, 1880; promoted to sen. 2nd cl., 1883; 1st cl., 1889; ch. clkship, 1893; ch. acctnt. and asp. of Indian educn., 1908; deputy supt.-gen. of Indian affairs, 1913; hon. sec., Royal Soc. of Canada.

SCOTT, HENRY HAROLD, M.D., London Univ.; M.R.C.P., London; F.R.S., Edin.; D.P.H.; M.R.C.S., Eng.; L.R.C.P., London; Fell. of Royal Soc. of medicine; Fell. of Royal Inst. of preventive medicine; Fell. of Soc. of tropical medicine and hygiene.—Ed. at St. Bart's and St. Thomas's hosps.; Univ. Colls. London and Copenhagen; prosector, 1894; asst. demonstrator in Materia Medica, 1895; asst. house physician, 1897; house physician, 1898, all at St. Thomas's hosp.; rec. med. offr., Teignmouth hosp., 1898; serv. in S. African War (Queen's medal with 5 clasps); med. offr. in charge bacteriological and X-Ray depts., mil. hosp., Maritzburg, 1903; M.O. in charge Fort Napier hosp. for women and children, 1904; rec. med. offr., Kingsbury and Neasden isolation hosp., 1910; bacteriologist to Willesden Dist. Coun., 1910; govt. bacteriologist and fermentation chemist, Jamaica, 1910; is also pathologist to Kingston gen. hosp.; mem. of med. coun., board of examiners, quarantine board, and cent. board of health, Jamaica; during 1915-1917, seconded for war service; bacteriologist at Command Laboratory, Aldershot, 1915; in charg No. XIII. mobile laboratory, 1916; pathologist and med. specialist, (Cambridge hosp.), Aldershot, 1916.

SCOTT, HERBERT SEPTIMUS.—B. 1873; ed. at Eton and Hertford Coll., Oxford; M.A.; inspr. of schools, Transvaal, 1902; examnr., 1908; sec. to educnt. dept., Transvaal, 1911; dir. of educn., Transvaal, 1924.

SCOTT, JOHN, C.M.G. (1923).—B. 1878; ed. Bath Coll., and King's Coll., Cambridge (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1901; priv. sec. to H.E. the offr. admng. the govt., Nov., 1903; off. asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Jan., 1904; asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Jan., 1905; Mannar, Apr., 1905; pol. mag., Hatton-Nuwara Eliya, Apr., 1907; asst. govt. agt., Mannar, Feb., 1910; ditto, Hambantota, May, 1911; dist. judge, Negombo, Nov., 1911; dist. judge, Chilaw and Puttalam, Mar., 1913; asst. govt. agt., Chilaw and Puttalam, Sept., 1913; addtl. asst. col. sec., Aug., 1914; attached to censor's off., Apr., 1915; censor, Sept., 1915; prin. asst. col. sec., Apr., 1917; dep. ch. sec. to govt., Nigeria, 23rd Feb., 1921; ch. sec., Tanganyika Territory, 30th Jan., 1924.
SCOTT, MARGARET.—Ed. Sherbourne Schlr. for Girls; V.A.D. nurse, Brit. R. Cross, 1915-19; housekeeper's dipl., Edin. Sch. of Domestic Econ., 1920; matron, Achimota, 1925.

SCOTT, RALPH.—B. 1874; B.A. Lond. Univ.; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1895; ag. collr. of land rev., Singapore, Mar., 1897; passed final exam. in Malay, Sept., 1897; ag. dist. offr., Malacca, Sept., 1897; confirmed, Aug., 1898; apptd. to cls. IV., Jan., 1903; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, Apr., 1903; attached to land off., Sing., on spec. duty, July, 1905; ag. collr. of land rev., Singapore, Jan., 1906; ag. 2nd mag., Singapore, Mar., 1906; ag. commr. of ct. of requests, Singapore, July, 1906, to Feb., 1907; dist. offr., Dindings, Aug., 1906; collr. of land rev., Penang, Feb., 1907; ag. commr., ct. of requests, Singapore, Dec., 1907; ag. 2nd mag., Penang, Jan., 1908; ag. collr. of land rev., Malacca, Oct., 1908; in charge of stintm., Feb. to May, 1909, and from Feb. to July, 1910; assumed duties as collr. of land rev., Penang, July, 1910; ag. senr. dist. offr., Prov. Wellesley, Feb., 1911; promoted to cls. ii., Dec., 1913; ag. dist. judge and 1st mag., Penang, Apr., 1915; ag. temp. 2nd mag., Penang, Dec., 1915; censor, Penang, Jan., 1916; ag. dist. judge and 1st mag., Jan., 1916; ag. dist. judge (civil), May, 1916; ag. res. and pres., munic. commrs., Malacca, Mar., 1920; dist. judge and 1st mag., Penang, Jan., 1921.

SCOTT, R. R., M.C., M.B., B.S. (Durham), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Lond.).—R.A.M.C. (S.R.), Aug., 1914; ment. in despr., 1919; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1919; senr. med. offr. of health, Apr., 1924.

SCOTT, THOMAS.—Clt., aud. office, Transvaal, 18th Feb., 1901; inspr., 1st Jan., 1904; prin. clk., revenue div., treasry., 1st Oct., 1904; rev. inspr., 1st Mar., 1905; prin. clk., audit office, 1st Dec., 1908; chief inspr. of expenditure audit, control and audit office, Union of S. Africa, 1st Apr., 1912; ag. asst. contr. and audr.-gen., Feb. to Aug., 1921 and May to Oct., 1922.
SCOTT, WILLIAM DUNCAN.—B. 1861; commr. for Canada to the Paris Exhibn., 1900; supt. of immigr., 1903-1919; asst. dep. min. of immigr. and colonization, 1919.
SCOTT, WM. ERNEST.—Ed. at Bradford and Giggleswick gram. schls., England; dep. min. of agric. and supt. of farmers' institutions, B. Columbia, 1909.
SCRAGG, JOHN, B.A.—B. 1902; ed. Manchester Gram. Schl. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; schol., 2nd cls., philos., politics and economics, 1925; asst. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.

SCRIVENER, RUPERT FREDERICK.—B. 1890; assoc., R. Schl. of Mines, dipl., Imp. Coll. Science, assoc., I.M.M., assoc., Inst. of Mining and Metallurgy; survey work, England and Alaska, 1911-14; comend. serv., 1914-20; capt., 1918; served in Gallipoli, Egypt, Mesopotamia, India and Palestine; commanded, 29th Rly. Coy., Sappers and Miners, L.A.; asst. ch. engr., Palestine rly., 1920; engr., ways and wks., Palestine rly., 1924.

SCRIVENOR, JOHN BROOKE.—B. 1876; ed. King's Schl., Canterbury, and Hertford Coll., Oxford; served on geol. surv. of the U.K. in 1902-03; geologist, F.M.S., 14th Aug., 1903; served in the Army in England and France from 8th Dec., 1916 to end of war as 2nd lieut., R.E. signal service; on sp. duty for state of Kelantan, May, 1924.

SEAGOE, ERNEST GOLDFINCH.—B. 1886; clk. to res. commr., New Hebrides, 1902; inspr. of native labourers, Br. residency, New Hebrides, 1909; ag. asst. to res. commr., 1911-12 and 1915-16; joint ag. native advocate, Oct., 1915 to Dec., 1922; ag. asst. to res., Nov., 1919 to Feb., 1920 and Sept. to Dec., 1921; commdt., constab., Aug., 1922; dep. commr., W. Pacific (New Hebrides), Nov., 1922; in ch. of acts., May to Aug., 1923; ag. asst. to res. commr., May, 1923 to Aug., 1924; dist. agt. and lab. inspr., Apr., 1923.

SEALY, GORDON COURLAND MILLER.—B. 1863; clk. to the atty.-gen., Tobago, Jan., 1882; clk. in col. sec.'s dept., 1883; acted as confid. clk. to the admtr. and clk. of couns., 1883 to 1886; 3rd clk., surg.-gen.'s dept., Br. Guiana, 1887; 2nd clk., 1887; has acted as ch. clk.; 4th class clk., treas., dept., 1st June, 1901; 3rd class clk., ditto, 14th May, 1906.

SEALY, JAMES ERNEST CAMERON.—B. 1871; 2nd clk., audit and registry dept., Tobago, Feb., 1888; clk. to commrs. and financial bd., Nov., 1890, and Oct., 1893; sec. to bd. of educn., Sept., 1892; ch. clk. treasry., June, 1893; postmr. of Tobago, Dec., 1895; clk. to sub-recvr., Tobago, Jan., 1899; clk. to warden, Tobago, Sept., 1899; ch. clk. and cashier, treasry., Tobago, Feb., 1904.
SEATON, G. L.—B. 1860; apptd., after exam., 3rd cl. mess., C.O., 17th May, 1899; 2nd cl., 18th Feb., 1900; King's mess., 10th June, 1904.

SEDGWICK, RICHARD ROMNEY.—B. 1894; ed. Westminster and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; schol., Trinity Coll., 1912; 1st cls. hist. tripos, pt. I., 1914 and pt. II., 1915; Gladstone prize, 1914; elected fellow of Trinity Coll., 1919; M.A., 1919; apptd. under reconstruction scheme sec. prin., Min. of Labour, 1919; asst. prin., C.O., 1922.

SEDWICK, N. A.—B. 1890; ed. at Univ. Coll. Schl., London, and St. Peter's Coll., Camb.: B.A., London and Camb.; cadet, S. Stitlms; civ. serv., Dec., 1913; dep. to supt., govt. monopolies dept., Jan., 1914; in charge of govt. opium factory, May to Aug., 1914; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, Jan., 1915; ag. asst. supt., govt. monopolies dept., 1916; passed cadet (Malay), Mar., 1917; attached to col. sec.'s office, Apl., 1917; ag. dist. offr., Jasir, Malacca, Sept., 1917; collr. of war tax, Singapore, July, 1918; supernmy. offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1920; ag. asst. treas. and commr. of stamps, Penang, Dec., 1920.

SEEL, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1895; ed. Macclesfield Grammar Schl. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford (schol.); on mily. serv. with Cheshire Regt. from 20th Nov., 1914 to 23rd Jan., 1919; served in Gallipoli, Egypt and Palestine; higher divn. clk. under reconstruction scheme, and apptd. to air miny., 28th July, 1919; seconded for serv. in C.O., as asst. prin., 9th Jan., 1922; sec., managing comtees, Imp. Bureau of Entomology.

SEELY, MAJOR-GEN. RT. HON. JOHN EDWARD BERNARD, P.C. (1909), C.B. (1916), C.M.G. (1918), D.S.O. (1900).—Ed. at Harrow and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1897; lieut.-col. commdg. Hampshire Carabineers; served with Imperial Yeomanry in S. Africa, 1900-1901 (deeps., Queen's medal, five clasps, D.S.O.); M.P. for Isle of Wight, 1900-06; M.P. for Abercromby div., Liverpool, 1906-1910; M.P., Ilkeston div., Derbyshire, 1910; recd. gold medal (French govt.) for saving life at sea, 1891; partly, under-sec. of state for the colonies, 1905; chmn. of comte., to inquire into organisation of crownagents office, 1908; under-sec. of state for war, 1911; sec. of state for war, 1912-14; joint partly, sec. to ministry of munitions, 1918; under-sec. of state for air, Jan., 1919.

SKHEULT, JOSEPH EMANUEL.—B. 1881; entd. civ. serv., Trinidad, 12th Mar., 1902; ch. clk., dept. of agric., 1st Jan., 1916; ast. censor, 1914; censor, 18th Apr., 1918; ag. man., agril. bank, 1st Nov., 1925.

SEHEULT, LEO GABRIEL, M.I.C.E., A.C.G.I.—B. 1886; ast. dist. offr., pub. works, Trinidad, 1st Mar., 1911; 2nd grade dist. offr., 1st Sept., 1916; 1st grade dist. offr., 1st June, 1917; gen. ast. to ast. D.P.W., 1st Jan., 1925.

SEIGNORET, CLARENCE AUGUSTUS.—B. 1867; supernumerary treas., Dominica, 1890; sec. to special yawms comm., Mar., 1891, to Sept., 1892; ch. clk., registar.'s office and clk. pub. market, 1892; ast. sec. to Sir R. Hamilton during his inquiry in Dominica, 1893; gov't. offr., treasy., 1895; sec., bd. of health and quarantine, 1895; ag. clk., admstr.'s office and exec. comm., 1895; ag. treas., Dominica, July, 1901; harbrmr., 1906; survr. of shipping, 1907; ag. treas., Sept., 1910, to Sept., 1911, and from Apr. to June, 1918.
SEIGNORET, G. B.—Clk. to postmr., Dominica, 1889; gov't. offr., treasy., 1893; ch. clk., registar.'s off., 1895; ag. postmr., 1896-7; clk. gov't. offr., Roseau, 1897; gov't. offr., treasy., 1901.

SELANDER, JOHN BINAR, M.I.C.E.—B. 1873; rly. surveys and constrn., Cape Colony, 1896-1908; Nigerian rly., 1908-1920; exec. engr., P.W.D., N. Nigeria; served in Boer War and European War; temp. major, R.E.; ment. in deeps.; dir., pub. wks., Tanganyika Territory, 1920.

SELBORNE, 2nd EARL OF (creat. 1882), VISCOOUNT WOLMER, BARON SELBORNE (U.K.), K.G. (1909). P.C. (1900), G.C.M.G. (1905). THE RT. HON. WM. WALDEGRAVE PALMER.—B. 1859; ed. at Winchester and at Univ. Coll., Oxford; B.A. (1st cls. hist.), 1882; ast. priv. sec. to Mr. Childers, Chanc. of Excheq., 1882-85; was mem. of parlmt. for E. Hants, 1885-92, and for W. Edinburgh City, 1892-95; succeeded his father, 1895; Under S. of S. for Cols., June, 1895; chmn. Pacific cable comte., 1896; mem. of Pacific cable comte., 1899; first lord of the admiralty, Nov., 12th, 1900; high commr. for S. Africa, and gov. and comdr.-in-chief of Transvaal and O.R.C., 1905 to 1910; ceased to be gov. of O.R.C. on grant of resp. govt., June, 1907; pres. of bd. of agric. and fisheries, 1915-16.

SELBY, PHILIP HAMILTON.—B. 1870; B.S.A. Pol., 1890-91; collr., N. E. Rhodesia, 1st July, 1898; civ. commr. and mag., 1900; judge of administrator's ct., N.W.R., 1905; mag., N.R., 17th Aug., 1911; ag. sec. for mines on various occasions, 1921 to 1924; ag. sec. for agr. and mines, 4th Apr., 1924; mem., leg. coun.; I.P.
SELDEN, A. A.—Ast. dist. commr., E.A.P., Dec., 1913; 1st grade admstrve. offr., Kenya, 1926.

SELLS, H. C., B.A. (Oxon.).—Cadet, S. Sttlmts., Oct., 1897; ag. priv. sec. to gov., Nov., 1898, to Nov., 1899; ag. 4th mag., Singapore, Jan., 1900; passed fin. exam. in Malay, Apr., 1900; ag. dist. offr., prov. Well., May, 1901; supt., money order branch and govt. savings bank, Sing., May 1902; head of Malay coll., Malacca, July, 1904; ag. dist. offr., Nibong Tebal, Apr., 1905; ag. ast. postmr.-gen., Penang, Nov., 1906; confirmed, May, 1907; ag. postmr.-gen., Apr. to Dec., 1909; offr. of Cls. III., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1912; ag. collr. of land rev. and reg. of deeds and reg. of Muhammedan marriages, Penang, 27th Mar., 1912; ast. censor in addtn. to other duties, 1st Jan., 1915; chmn., santiy. bd., Kinta, 7th Aug., 1919; offt. regist. of titles, Perak, ag. supt., convict estab., and inspr. of prisons, F.M.S., in addn., 5th Jan., 1920; ag. P.M.G., cls. I.A. S. Sttlmts., 1st Oct., 1920; ag. collr., land rev., and regist. of deeds and bills of sale, Singapore, in addn., cls. I.B., 14th Feb., 1921; ag. collr.-gen., income tax and collr.-gen., war tax, S. Sttlmts., cls. I.B., 1st Apr., 1921; ag. P.M.G., S. Sttlmts., cls. I.A., 1st Nov., 1921; offr., cls. I.A., June, 1925; sec., postal affrs., S.S. and F.M.S., June, 1925.

SELLS, MARTIN PERRONET, O.B.E., A.M.I.M.E., M.I.Loco. E.—B. 1887; ed. Trent Coll. and Univ. Coll., London; junr. ast. loco. supt., Lancs and Yorks Rly.; temp. major R.E., 1917; supt., light rlys., France, 1917-1919 (twice ment. in deeps.); dist. loco. supt., Tanganyika Terry, rly., 1919; ag. ch. mech. engrnr., Tanganyika Terry, rly., 1920; ch. mech. engrnr., Gold Coast rly., 1922; ch. mech. engrnr., Nigerian Rly., 1926.

SEMPER, D. H.—Clk. to atty.-gen., Leeward Islands, 1893; dep. coroner for dist. B., Oct., 1898; ag. Federal treas., Oct., 1902; fed. treas., Mar., 1903; acted as clk., leg. coun., Antigua, Dec., 1902; ditto, ex. coun., Montserrat, Mar., 1903; ag. ch. regist., Leeward Is., and regist. and prov. marshal, admstr. of estates, offl. recvr. in bank., regist. of titles, regist. of friendly societies, and regist.-gen. of births, deaths and marriages, Antigua, Feb. to July, 1905; apptd. regist. and prov. marshal, admstr. of estates, offl. recvr. in bank., and regist. of titles, St. Kitts-Nevis, July, 1905; J.P., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1905; ag. mag., dist. C., Feb., 1911; M.L.C., St. Kitts-Nevis, May, 1911; escheator-gen., 1911; offr. in charge of prison discipline, St. Kitts-Nevis, 1911; addtl. mag., Dist. C., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1916; mag. and coroner, dist. D., 30th Nov., 1921.
SEMPILL, CHARLES IGNATIUS, M.M.—B. 1825; Australian Forces, 1914-19; ast. supt., pol., Kenya, Nov., 1920.

SEMPLE, J. M., B.A., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O., T.C.D., L.M. (Rotunda).—Sandhurst, 1915; lieut., R. Irish Rifles, 1915-18; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Mar., 1922; med. offr., Zanzibar, Oct., 1924; ag. S.S.O., July, 1925 to Oct., 1926.
SENARATNE, CORNELIUS.—B. 1870; cls. V., Ceylon civ. serv., Jan., 1921; extra office ast. to gvt. agt., S. Prov., Feb., 1921; office ast. to ast. gvt. agt., Matale, Feb., 1922.

SENEVIRATNE, DIONYSIUS BARTHOLOMEW, M.M.—B. 1895; on mily. serv., Nov., 1915; cadet, Ceylon, Oct., 1920; attd. to B'calo Kachcheri, Oct., 1920; ditto, Kurunegala Kach., Nov., 1921; ag. pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, June, 1922; office ast., Batticaloa kach., Dec., 1923; ditto, Ham-bantota kach., June, 1926.

SENEVIRATNE, LEOPOLD JAMES DE SILVA, LL.B., Barrister-at-law, Lincoln's Inn.—B. 1899; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1923; attd., Matara kach., Dec., 1923; ditto, Jaffna kach., July, 1924; ditto, Kegalla kach., Apr., 1925; pol. mag., Puttalam, Mar., 1926.

SENNETT, Cedric William Arthur.—B. 1890; ed. Plymouth Coll., Edinburgh Univ. and non-collegiate, Oxford (B.A., 1913); cadet, S. Sttlmts., 1914; ag. 4th mag., Singapore, 1915; asst. censor, 1916; asst. regis., impts. and expts., 1917; asst. supt., govt. monopolies, 1920; ag. priv. sec. to ch. sec., F.M.S., 1920; asst. dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, 1920; ag. dist. offr., Raub, 1921; supernumy. offr., cls. IV., 28th Nov., 1921.
SERGEANT, John.—Ed. at Athelhampton schl., Birkdale; chief acnt., pub. wks. dept., E. Africa Prot., 30th May, 1905.

SERRET, Evarista Joseph Eugene.—B. 1864; ed. Royal Coll., Mauritius; English schol., 1884; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1887; crown pros., Mauritius, 1892; addnl. substitute procureur and advoc. gen., 1901; ag. ch. just., Seychelles, 1911-12; substitute procureur and advoc. gen., Mauritius, 1913; ag. proc. and advoc. gen., 1913-14; K.C., 1919; puisne judge, sup. ct., 1922.

SERTSIOS, B. D.—B. 1876; ed., Athens; Ph.D., University of Athens; B.A., Greek College, Serres; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1906; judge, dist. ct., Cyprua, 1909; asst. king's advoc., June, 1924; title changed to asst. atty.-gen., May, 1925.

SETH, G. G., B.A., Barrister-at-Law (Certif. of Hon., 1910), K.C.—Calcutta; cadet, S.S., Nov., 1901; ag. sheriff, Singapore, Jan., 1903; passed final exam. in Malay, Jan., 1903; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, 5th Jan., 1904; ag. dep. regis., sup. ct., Singapore, July, 1904; ag. dist. offr., N. Tebal, June, 1906; ag. dep. regis., sup. ct., Penang, Sept., 1906; asst. to atty.-gen., Apr., 1907; asst. to atty.-gen., 15th Dec., 1910; barrister-at-law, Gray's Inn (cert. of hon.), 1910; offr. of Cls. IV., F.M.S., 6th Dec., 1912; collr., war tax, Singapore, 26th Mar., 1917; supernumerary offr. of Cls. III., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1918; offr. asst. to atty.-gen., Singapore, Nov., 1918; ag. dep. pub. prosecutor in addition, May, 1919; has held numerous acting appts.: solr.-gen., S. Sttlmts., Mar., 1920; ag. puisne judge, sup. ct., civil and criminal assizes, Malacca, Apr., June and Oct., 1920; ag. atty.-gen. and offl. mem., select comttee. of leg. coun. on the constitution of the leg. coun. of the S. Sttlmts., 28th Oct., 1920 to 14th May, 1921; rep. of cols. and prots. at Empire patent confce., London, June, 1922.

SETON, Claud Ramsay Wilmot.—B. 1888; ed. Framlingham Coll., Suffolk, Laleham, Margate and Univ. Coll., London; admitted solr., sup. ct., England, 1910; on mily. serv., 1914-19; M.C., twice ment. in desps.; pres., dist. ct., Jaffa, Palestine, 1919.

SEVERN, Sir Claud, K.B.E. (1923), C.M.G. (1917), M.A. (Cantab.), LL.D., Hong Kong (1920).—B. 1869; temp. clk. in librarian's dept., Foreign Office, Mar., 1891, to June, 1892; priv. sec. to gov., S. Sttlmts., Feb., 1894; junr. offr., Selangor, Dec., 1895; passed in Malay and law, 1898; asst. dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Selangor, June, 1897; asst. dist. offr., Serendah, May, 1899; ag. mag., Kuala Lumpur, 1900; asst. sec. to res.-gen., F.M.S., Aug., 1903; ag. fed. sec., F.M.S., Apr., to Nov., 1905; ag. dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Dec., 1906, to Apr., 1907; ag. sec. to high commr., F.M.S. and priv. sec. to gov., S. Sttlmts., May, 1907; sec. to high commr., Malay States, Feb., 1911; col. sec., Hong Kong, Feb., 1912; admstd. govt. 16th Mar. to 3rd July, 1912, 21st Aug. to 24th Dec., 1913, 6th Nov. to 8th Dec., 1916, 10th Nov. to 16th Dec., 1917, 12th Sept., 1918 to 29th Sept., 1919, 8th Apr. to 11th May, 1921 and from 15th June to Oct., 1922; ret., 1926.

SEVIGNY, Hon. Albert, K.C.—B. 1881; ed. Nicolet and Valleyfield (Quebec) Colls. and Laval Univ., B.A., L.L.L.; advoc., city of Quebec; unsuccessful candidate, legis. assem., Quebec, bye-elec., 1907; elec. to H. of C., Canada, g.e. 1911; dep. speaker, 1915; speaker, 1916; min. of inland rev., 1917; defeated at g.e., 1917; mem. of the P.C. for Canada; judge, superior ct., Quebec, 3rd Mar., 1921.

SEYMOUR, Alfred Wallace.—B. 1881; ed. Warwick Schol. and St. Andrew's Univ.; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1906; offr. asst. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Mar., 1906; N.C. Prov., Apl., 1907; W. Prov., Nov., 1906; asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Aug., 1910; ditto, Trincomalee, June, 1911; asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Dec., 1912; ditto, Nuwara Eliya, Jan., 1913; ditto, Matale, Sept., 1915; on mil. duty, June, 1916; dist. judge, Chilaw, Aug., 1919; asst. govt. agt. Kegalla, Mar., 1920; govt. agt., N.C. Prov., May, 1921; regis. gen., Dec., 1924; col. sec., Fiji, Dec., 1926.

SEYMOUR-HALL, Charles Cyril William, F.R.S.A., F.R.G.S., F.R. Met. Soc., F.R.C.I., F.Z.S.—B. 1897; prob. flight offr., R.N.A.S., 1917; flight sub-lieut., R.N., 1917; lieut., R.A.F., 1918; ment. in desp., 1919; capt., H.M.A.Z., 31, 1919; demob., 1919; asst. dist. commr., Zanzibar, 1921; ag. 2nd asst. sec. and priv. sec. to ag. Br. res., Zanzibar, 1923-4.

SHARP, George Frederic.—B. 1888; ed. Univ. Coll. Schol. and King's Coll., London; comprtr. and acct.-gen.'s dept., G.P.O., 1907-12; acct., postal dept., S. Nigeria, 1912-14; acct., posts and tels. dept., N. and S. Provs. of Nigeria, 1914-19; ch. acct., posts and tels., Nigeria, 1920; on mily. serv., London Rifle Brig., 1908-12; Nigerian Land Contgt., 1914-18.

SHARPE, Sir Alfred, K.C.M.G. (1903), C.B. (1897).—B. 1853; H.M.'s vice-consul, Nyasaland, 14th Feb., 1891; ditto, for territories under British influence North of the Zambesi, 18th Feb., 1893; consul for same territories, 1st Feb., 1894; ag. commr. and consul-gen. at Zomba, 7th May, 1894, to 3rd May, 1895; given rank of deputy commr., 1st Apl., 1896; and again acted as commr. and consul-gen., 16th Apl., 1896, to 14th July, 1897; commr. and consul-gen., for territories under Brit. infl. N. of Zambesi, 15th July, 1897; recd. Jubilee medal, 1897; commr., commdr.-in-chief, and consul-gen., Br. Cent. Africa Prot., 1st Jan., 1902; recd. Coronation medal, 1902; gov. of Nyasaland (late B.C.A. Prot.), 6th Sept., 1907; ret., 1910; represented E. African Prot. at the Coronation of H.M. King George V., 1911.
SHARPE, H. B.—Asst. dist. commr., Kenya Colony, Apl., 1919; ag. dist. commr., 1926.

SHARPE, Richard.—B. 1882; ed. Sexey's Schol., Somt. (Somt. county jnr. and inter. scholarships and hon. mention senr. scholarship); ent. Impl. cust. serv. by open compet. exam., and appld. to London, 1903; London Univ. cert. polit. economy with sp. distinc. in exchange, finance and taxation, 1907; seconded to govt. of Liberia as sub-inspr., cust., 1908-10; ch. inspr., cust. and finan. adviser, 1911-12; Br. receiver of Liberian cust., 1911-19; offr. recvr., liquidating German property in Liberia, 1917-18; knight commdr., Order of African Redemption, 1915; treas., Dominica, Feb., 1920; mem. of exec. and legis. couns., collr. of cust. and excise, regist. of shipping, chmn., quarantine bd., Impl. trade corres.; dep. contr., cust., Gold Coast, 3rd Jan., 1923; ag. contr., cust. on various occasions, 1923-25; contr., cust., 24th Sept., 1926.

SHAW, GEORGE ERNEST, O.B.E. (1919).—B. 1877; ed. Trin. Coll., Dublin; scholar and senr. moderator in classics; vice-chancellor's prizeman in English prose; B.A.; LL.B.; cadet, F.M.S. civ. ser., 1900; collr. of land rev., Krian, 1901; mag., 1st cls., Perak, 1903; offr. of Cls. V., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1906; collr. of land rev., Lower Perak, 1908; adviser, land office, Kedah, and supt. of mines, 1911; offr. of Cls. IV., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1912; supernumerary offr. of Cls. III., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1918; lent to War Office, directorate of prisoners of war, Aug., 1916; adviser, land off., Kedah, 19th July, 1919; ag. comanr., lands and mines, Johore, May, 1923; ag. sec., agr., S.S. and F.M.S., Feb., 1924; on sp. duty, Burma, Oct., 1925; ag. sec., agr., S.S. and F.M.S., Nov., 1925; ag. Br. adviser, Kelantan, Dec., 1925.
SHAW, JOHN HENRY DE BURGH, B.L.—B. 1887; ed. Clifton Schol., Dalkey, Dublin and Corrig Schol., Kingstown; ast. dist. comanr., Sierra Leone, 25th May, 1921.

SHAW, SIR WALTER SIDNEY, KT. BACH.(1921).—B. 1863; ed. Brighton Coll. and Trin. Coll., Camb.; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1888; pol. mag. and ag. chief just., St. Vincent, 1906; mem. exec. and legis. couns.; confirmed as chief just., 1907; ag. chief just., Grenada, Feb., 1908; to June, 1909; revised and consolidated laws of St. Vincent, 1912; admstd. govt. of St. Vincent, June, 1911 to Jan., 1912, and for various shorter periods; chief just., B. Honduras, 1912; puisne justice, Ceylon, Aug., 1914; ag. chief just., June to Oct., 1916; ch. just., S. Stlmlts., 23rd May, 1921; ret., 1926.

SHEEDY, CAPT. FREDERICK JOHN, M.B.E., B.Sa., M.R.C.V.S.—Ed. Clongowes, Queen's Coll., Cork; late capt., E.A.P. forces; ent. army, A.V.C. 1911; vety. offr., E.A.P., July, 1914; served throughout Great War, 5th Aug., 1914 to 22nd Sept., 1919 (E. Africa Mounted Rifles and E.A.V.C.); adjutant, E.A.V.C., 9th Jan., 1916; M.B.E. (mil.), 1918, "1914-15" Star, Brit. War med., Victory med.; dep. ch. vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 31st Oct., 1919; ag. ch. vety. offr., May, 1920 to Mar., 1921 and Apr., 1923 to 1924.

SHEEDY, ANDREW JOSEPH.—B. 1879; joined S. Stlmlts. pol., Jan., 1902; ag. 3rd cls. inspr., Singapore, Aug., 1904; 3rd cls. inspr., Penang, Mar., 1906; 2nd cls. inspr., Penang, Aug., 1907; 1st cls. inspr., Penang, July, 1908; ag. ch. inspr., Singapore, Aug., 1909; ag. ch. detect. inspr., Singapore, Apr., 1910; ch. detect. inspr., Singapore, July, 1910; ag. ast. supl. pol., Singapore, Feb., 1911; ast. supl. pol., Singapore, Feb., 1911; ag. supl. pol., Singapore, Oct. and Dec., 1920; senr. ast. supl. pol., Singapore, Feb., 1925; dep. comanr., pol., F.M.S., July, 1925.

SHEEHAN, JOHN JOSEPH.—B. 1896; ed. Christian schol., Dublin Univ. and Univ. Coll., Dublin.—Pte., Artists Rifles, Dec., 1916; 2nd lieut., R.F.A., Sept., 1917; demob., May, 1919; cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1920; ag. 2nd ast. adviser, Muar, Mar., 1921; ag. P.M.G., Johore, Oct., 1921; passed cadet, Nov., 1921; ag. collr., land rev., Johore Bharu, Jan., 1922; sec., rubber restriction, Johore, Oct., 1922; supernmry. offr., Cls. V., Mar., 1923; dep. contr., rubber expts., Johore, in addn., Aug., 1923; recr., Alsagoff concession, Kukub, in addn., June, 1924; stdk., dist. office, Kinta, Mar., 1925; ag. dist. offr., Ipoh, Apr., 1926.

SHELDON, ROBERT PAUL.—B. 1880; ed. Harrow and in Germany; ag. vice-consul, Ghent, 1905; ag. vice-consul, Havre, 1906-7; ag. consul, Bordeaux, 1907; ast. collr., Zanzibar, 27th Dec., 1907; sec. to first minister, 1909-10; collr. (now dist. comanr.), Pemba, 16th Oct., 1912; ag. mag., 30th Aug., 1915; ag. ch. sec., 5th Mar. to 17th Nov., 1919; senr. comanr., Zanzibar, 20th Sept., 1923; 4th cls., Order of the Aliyeh and 3rd cls., Order of the Brilliant Star of Zanzibar.

SHELLEY, MALCOLM BOND.—B. 1879; ed. Dulwich Coll. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1901; cadet, F.M.S. civ. serv., 1902; ag. ast. supt., immigrts., 1906; ag. 2nd ast. sec. to gen. mag., 1907; ag. mag., Kuala Lumpur, 1910; ag. dist. offr., Batang Padang, 1911; comanr. in M.S. Vol. Rifles, 1911; ag. ast. regiar., sup. ct., Ipoh, 1912; ag. dist. offr., Larut, 1914; ag. inspr. of prisons, F.M.S., 1914; capt., M.S.V.R., 1915; ag. dep. pub. pros., Perak, 1916; ag. mag., Ipoh, 1920; off. assignee and pub. trustee, 1922; ag. dep. treas., Singapore, 1924; ag. treas., S.S., 1924; ag. dep. treas., Singapore, 1926.

SHELSWELL, GEOFFREY HENRY.—B. 1897; ed. Whitgift Schol.; served with R.F.A. in India, Mesopotamia and Persia; ast. pol. offr., Mesopotamia, 1918-20; personal ast. to Sir Arnold Wilson, ag. civ. comanr., 1918; Iraq rebellion, 1920; A.D.C., Zanzibar, 1921; priv. sec. to res. and 2nd ast. sec., secretariat, in 1921 to 1924; clk. of coun., 1924; ag. 1st ast. sec., 10th Sept. to 29th Dec., 1924.
SHELTON, C. F., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.D. (Lond.), B.S.—Civil surgeon, Serbian red cross, 1913; surgeon lieut., 1914-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Sept., 1921.

SHEPPARD, WALTER SYDNEY, B.A., M.B., B. Ch. (Cantab.).—B. 1871; civil surg. in charge of H.M.'s troops, N.E. dist., Eng., May, 1898, to May, 1899; spec. plague med. offr., India, May, 1899, to Nov., 1900; house surg., gen. hosp., Singapore, 19th Nov., 1900; super. col. surg., S. Stlmlts., 1st Mar., 1901; spec. mission to Brunei, Aug., 1904; col. surg., Prov. Wellesley, Aug., 1906; senior med. offr., Singapore, July, 1911.
SHERGOLD, F. V.—B. 1900; apptd. after compet. exam., ast. clk., C.O., Apr., 1918; on mil'y. serv., 1918 to 1920; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920.

SHERIDAN, JOSEPH.—Ed. at Castleknock Coll. and Trin. Coll., Dublin; called to the bar, Ireland, 1907, went Connaught circuit; judcl. clk., Nyasaland Prot., July, 1908; ag. atty.-gen., Nov., 1909, to Jan., 1910; ag. judge of high ct., Dec., 1909, to Jan., 1910; ag. atty.-gen., July, 1911, to Mar., 1912; apptd. ast. to atty.-gen., Apr., 1912; ag. atty.-gen., Aug., 1912; town mag., E.A.P., 1913; ag. puisne judge, July, 1919; 3rd puisne judge, Kenya, Apr., 1920.

SHERWOOD, MONTAGUE EARLE, M.R.E.—B. 1884; cadet, S. Stlmlts., 21st Oct., 1907; attached to col. sec.'s office, 1st Jan., 1908; attached to land office, Singapore, 23rd Jan., 1908; dep. collr. of land rev. and dep. regiar. of deeds, Singapore, 31st Jan., 1908; ag. dist. offr., Labuan, 29th Jan. to 16th Nov., 1909; attached to dist. office, Alor Gajah, 28th Dec., 1910; passed final exam. in Malay, Jan., 1911; passed cadet, 25th Jan., 1911; attached to land office, Malacca, 1st Mar., 1911; ag. dist. offr., Jasin, 7th Apr., 1911; offr. of Cls. V., 13th May, 1911; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, 15th Oct., 1913; ag. dist. offr., Alor Gajah, 18th Feb., 1914; ag. ast. to res., Malacca, 1st Sept., 1915; ag. harbr.-mast., Malacca, in addition to other duties, 26th Jan., 1916; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to gov., 1st Aug., 1916; offr. of Cls. III., 16th Nov., 1919; ag. priv. sec. to H.E. the gov., Feb., 1920; ag. sec. to high comanr., March, 1921.
SHEILD, CHARLES GEOFFREY.—B. 1896; on mily. service (France) 1914-18; on staff, 1918-19; priv. secy. to gov., and elk. to couns., Seychelles, 1920.
SHIELDS, W. P.—Labour inspection offr., E. Africa Prot., Apl., 1919.
SHIPWAY, WALTER ROBERT, M.B.E. (1924).—B. 1879; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd div. elk., and assigned to C.O., Dec., 1898; minor staff elk., 1911; regar. of col. laws, 1st Apr., 1922.

SHIRCORE, JOHN OWEN, C.M.G. (1926); M.B., Ch.B.—Med. offr., Nyasaland, 1908; transf. to Uganda, 1909; re-transf. as med. offr., Nyasaland, 1910; med. offr., E.A.F., 1912; dep. prin. med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 15th Oct., 1919; ag. P.M.O., Mar.-Dec., 1924; P.M.O., 22nd Dec., 1924.

SHORTT, ADAM, C.M.G. (1911); M.A., F.R.S.C.—B. 1859; ed. at Walkerton High Schl. and Queen's Univ., Canada (B.A., 1883, M.A., 1885) and Glasgow and Edin. Unive.; asst. prof. of philosophy, Queen's Univ., 1885; lect. and prof. of polit. science, 1889-1908; civ. ser. comsnr., Canada, 1908-1917; chmn. of historical manuscript publication board, Oct., 1917; writer on history, banking and economics; del. to internat. congress of history of America, Rio de Janeiro, 1922; mem., preparatory coman. for econ. confee., L. of N., Geneva, 1926.

SHREWSBURY, HERBERT SUTCLIFFE, F.I.C., F.C.S.—B. 1878; ed. Newchurch Grammar Schl., Nottingham Univ.; asst. govt. analyst, Trinidad, 1910; prin. asst. govt. analyst, 1912; govt. analyst, 1920; author of various papers on orig. and analytical processes, and on agrl. and forensic chemistry.

SHUCKBURGH, SIR JOHN EVELYN, K.C.M.G. (1922), C.B. (1918).—B. 1877; ed. Eton and King's Coll., Cambridge; B.A., 1899; M.A., 1906; apptd. jnr. elk., store dept., I. O., Oct., 1900; transf. to judi. and pub. dept., Mar., 1901; joint editor, "India Office List," Oct., 1901; priv. sec. to permant. under-sec. of state, July, 1902; senr. elk., polit. dept., Mar., 1906; ast. sec. polit. dept., Oct., 1912; sec. polit. dept., Mar., 1917; ag. ast. under sec. of state, C. O., 1st Mar., 1921; ast. under sec. of state, 1st Apr., 1924.

SIBBALD, SAMUEL KAY, A.M.I.C.E., M.T.P.I.—B. 1878; ed. Dumfries Acad. and West Scot.and Tech. Coll.; asst. engrn., P.W.D., F.M.S., 1901; ast. to govt. town planner, F.M.S., 1921; ag. govt. town planner, F.M.S., 1925.

SIDEBOTHAM, JOHN BIDDULPH, B.A. (1914), M.A. (1920).—B. 1891; ed. King's Schl., Canterbury, and Gonville and Caius Coll., Cambridge (Stanhope exhibr., open class exhibr., 1911, schol., 1912); lat cls., theolog. tripes, pt. I., 1914; 2nd lieut., Home Counties R.E. (T.F.), 1914; lieut., 13th June, 1916; serv. in France, 1914-15; wounded, Apr., 1915; demob., 1919; ent. inland rev., Somerset House, 7th Feb., 1920; transf. to C.O. as ast. prin. under reconstruction scheme, Dec., 1922.

SIDLEY, WILLIAM HENRY FRANCIS, B.A. (Trinity Coll., Dublin).—Cadet, R.I.C., Aug., 1908; 3rd cls. dist. inspr., Oct., 1908; 2nd cls., Feb., 1910; 1st cls., Aug., 1919; cou'ty inspr., Nov., 1920; served in R.I.C. until disbanded; ent. of that force, July, 1922; depot inspr. (1st cls.), Jamaica constab., Dec., 1922; ag. dep. inspr. gen., Aug., 1923; dep. inspr. gen., Dec., 1923; ag. inspr.-gen., 1st June to 14th Oct., 1925.

SIFTON, HON. SIR CLIFFORD, K.C.M.G. (1915), K.C.—B. 1861, Co. Middlesex, Ont.; ed. London, Ont., High Schl. and Victoria Univ. (Prince of Wales Medallist, 1890); called to the bar of Manitoba, 1882; elec. to legis. assem., Man., 1888; atty.-genl. and min. of educn., 1891-96; codified laws relating to civil procedure in Man.; had charge of negotiations with federal govt. respecting the Manitoba Schl. Law; Q.C. (Dominion), 1896; called to federal cabinet as min. of the interior, 1896; elec. to H. of C. by accl. for Brandon; British agt. before the Alaska Bndry. Tribunal, 1903; re-elec. to H. of C. 1900 and 1904; resig. from the cabinet, 1905; one of Canadian representatives at internat. confere. at Washington on preservation of natural resources, 1908; re-elec. to H. of C., 1908; chmn. Canadian coman. of conservation 1909-1918; did not seek re-elec. at g.e., 1911.

SIKES, HOWARD LECKY, B.A., B.E. (hons. and extbr.), R.U.I., M.I.C.E., F.G.S.—B. 1881; ed. Bootham Schl., York; asst. engrn., P.W.D., E. Africa Prot., 1907; res. engrn. Mombassa water wks., 1911; engrn. in ch., Magadi water wks., constr., 1913; res. engrn., Magadi rly., 1915; lieut., E.A. Pioneers, 1915; capt., 1916; govt. hydraulic engrn., P.W.D., Kenya, Jan., 1919; dep. dir., pub. wks., Kenya, 1921; D.P.W. and offl. mem., leg. coun., 1923.
SILVESTER, J. M.—Asst. dist. comanr., E.A.P., Dec., 1913; ag. dist. comanr., Kenya, 1926.
SIM, HON. SIR WM. ALEXANDER, Kt. Bach. (1924).—B. 1868; judge of sup. ct. of New Zealand, 16th Jan., 1911.

SIMMONS, JAMES WILLIAM.—B. 1877; ed. at Malvern and Oxford; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1900; in charge of treasy., Seremban, 29th May, 1901; ag. ast. dist. offr., Tampin, 1st Aug., 1901; ag. sec. sany. bd., Seremban, 1st Apr., 1902; ag. inspr. of schl. in addn., 1904; 1st ast. collr. of land rev., Seremban, 1st Jan., 1905; ag. ast. collr. of land rev., Kuala Lumpur, 13th Nov., 1907; ag. ast. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, 6th May, 1909; comanr. of out., Johore, 21st Dec., 1910; comanr. of lands and mines, Johore, 9th Oct., 1914 to 4th June, 1920; sec. to comm. of enquiry, Trengganu, 5th Sept., 1918; 1st mag., Kuala Lumpur, 10th Mar., 1921; attd. to col. secretariat, Singapore, 9th June, 1921; 1st ast. sec. A., S. Stltims., 9th June, 1921; ag. finan. comanr., Johore, 6th Dec., 1921; ag. collr. land rev., S. Stltims., 8th May, 1922; dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Sept., 1922; ag. dist. offr., Kinta, July, 1923; ag. chmn., sany. bd., Kuala Lumpur, Nov., 1924; ag. Br. advisor, Trengganu, May, 1925.

SIMMONS, HON. WM. CHAS.—B. 1865; ed. at Collingwood and Owen Sound High Schls.; B.A., Toronto Univ., 1895; called to the bar, N.W.T., 1900; elec. to legis. assem. of Alberta for Lethbridge dist., 1906; resig., 1908; puisne judge of sup. ct., Alberta, 12th Oct., 1910.
SIMPSON, JOHN.—Imp. tel. serv., Jan., 1922; transf., post office, Cape of Good Hope, Nov., 1893; surrv., posts and tels., Mar., 1920; pub. serv. inspr. Aug., 1921.

SIMPSON, SAMUEL, C.M.G. (1926).—B. 1876; ed. at Owen's Coll., and Edin. Univ.; R.Sc.; Stevens scholar; sec. to the Union; sen. pres. of students' representative coun.; Highland and agric. soc'y.'s prizeman; life mem. and silver medallist of roy. agric. soc'y.; holder of nat. diploma in agric.; sen. lecturer in agric. to govt. agric. coll., Ghizeh, Egypt; cotton expert to B.C.A. Prot., Mar., 1906; dir. of agric., Uganda, 1912; mem., leg. and exec. couns.; comanr. for Uganda at Br. Emp. Exhibn.; author of "Report on Cotton Growing Industry in B.C.A. Prot."; reported on the agric. resources of Angola, Trinidad, Tobago, B. Guiana and Surinam.
SIMS, JAMES HUGH.—B. 1878; sub-inspr., Basutoland Mounted Pol., 1905; inspr., 1913; asst. commr., 1918.

SINCLAIR, HON. JOHN EWEN.—B. 1879; ed. local common schls.; mem. of swine comm. which visited Europe in 1909; unsucc. cand., legis. ass., P.E.I., 1908; el. to H.C., g.e., 1917; re-el., 1921; min. without portfolio in King admstn., 29th Dec., 1921.
SINCLAIR, HON. SIR J. R. KT. BACH (1918).—B. 1851; ed. Dunedin High Schl., N.Z.; barrister; M.L.C., N.Z., 1907-14 and since 1918; rep. N.Z. on Dominion Royal Comm., 1912.

SINCLAIR, ST. CLAIR OVERBECK, M.A., F.I.C., D.Sc.—Ck., atty.-gen.'s office, 1893; clk., col. sec.'s office, 1894; analyst, 1897; transf., dept. of agr., 1907; transf., dept. of int., 1911; govt. analyst, Cape Town, 1918; transf., dept. of agr., 1st Apr., 1923; chief of divn. of chemistry, 3rd Mar., 1926.

SINCLAIR, WILLIAM FRANCIS.—B. 1879; ed. Oxenford House, Jersey; served in S. African War as lieut., 1st Kitchener's Fighting Scouts; Queen's Med., 5 bars; served in Gt. War as lieut., 5th D.G.'s Imp. Camel Corps and sp. list, in Flanders, Egypt and Palestine, 1914-20; comdt., grade II, Palestine pol., July, 1920; comdt., grade I, Nov., 1920; dist. supt., Palestine pol., Apr., 1926.

SINGAPORE, LORD BISHOP OF RIGHT REV. CHARLES JAMES FERGUSON-DAVIE, B.A., M.A., D.D.—Formerly curate of St. Paul, Preston, 1896-99; dom. chap. to Bishop of Lahore, 1899-1902; S.P.G. mission at Rewari, 1902-1907; S.P.G. mission at Rawal Pindi, 1907-09; consecrated Lord Bishop of Singapore, 24th Aug., 1909.

SIRCOM, HAROLD SEBASTIAN.—B. 1878; cadet, F.M.S., 1902; passed cadet, May, 1904; ag. asst. sec. to res., Selangor, Aug., 1904; ag. asst. dist. offr., Ulu Langat, May, 1905; ag. dist. offr., Temerloh, May, 1907; ag. dist. offr., Pekan, Nov., 1907; cl. V, Jan., 1910; ag. dist. offr., Pekan, Apr., 1910; seconded, commr. of cust., Johore, 1913; cl. IV, Nov., 1913; ag. dist. offr., Butterworth, Dec., 1917; cl. III, Jan., 1918; seconded, asst. adviser, Muar, Johore, June, 1918; cl. II, Nov., 1919; ag. commr. of lands and mines, Johore, June, 1920; asst. adviser, Muar, Apr., 1921; mag., Kuala Lumpur, Oct., 1922; dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, Jan., 1923; chmn., sany. bd., Apr., 1923; sec. to res., Selangor, May, 1923; ag. Br. res., Selangor in addition, Oct., 1923; contr., rubber, S.S. and F.M.S., May, 1925.

SISNETT, SIR HERBERT KORTRIGHT McDONNELL, KT. BACH (1927).—Ed. at Harrison Coll., Barbados; called to the bar, Inner Temp., May, 1896; practised at bar, Barbados, June, 1897, to Feb., 1898; ag. junior pol. mag., Bridgetown, Barbados, Sept. to Nov., 1897; practised at bar, Jamaica, Mar., 1898, to Apr., 1903, during which period acted as res. mag. for St. Ann's, June to Dec., 1901; as priv. sec. to Sir A. Hemming, May to June, 1902, and priv. sec. to Mr. Olivier, the ag. gov't., June to Nov., 1902; clk. of cts., St. James, Jamaica, Apr., 1903; ag. res. mag., St. James and Trelawny, Sept., 1903, as res. mag., St. Elizabeth, Mar., 1904, as res. mag., Manchester, Apr. to June, 1904, as res. mag., St. James and Trelawny, Sept., 1904, for two and a half months in 1905, and from June to July, 1906; regiar.-gen., Br. Hond., and dist. commr. of Belize, Apr., 1907; ag. atty.-gen. and ag. ch. just., Br. Hond., on several occasions; J.P. for Br. Hond., 1907; stip. mag., B. Guiana, 8th Mar., 1913; held inquiry into Rose Hall coolie riots, 1913, and was highly commended by S. of S.; ch. just., B. Hond., 1923.

SITTAMPALAM, CATHIVAVELU, B.A. (Cantab.).—Barrister-at-law (Middle Temple).—B. 1898; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Feb., 1923; addl. to Kalutara kach., Mar., 1923; ditto, Kandy kach., Mar., 1925; office ast., Puttalam kach., Aug., 1926.

SKEEN, DONALD TRENCH, M.B., Ch.B.—B. 1883; ed. Aberdeen Grammar Schol. and Aberdeen Univ.; med. offr., F.M.S., grade III, May, 1912; med. offr., grade II, Dec., 1912; war service, 1915-19; med. offr., Batu Gajah, Sept., 1919; senr. med. offr., Negri Sembilan, May, 1921; ag. senr. med. offr., Perak., Feb., 1924; senr. med. offr., Negri Sembilan, Oct., 1924.

SKELTON, OSCAR DOUGLAS, M.A., Ph.D.—B. 1878; ed. Orangeville and Cornwall High Schls., Queen's Univ. and Univ. of Chicago; counsellor, dept. of external affrs., 15th July, 1924; under-sec. of state for external affrs., 30th Mar., 1925.

SKENE, CHARLES EDWARD.—B. 1889; ed. St. Paul's Schol. and Wren's; ast. dist. commr., Gold Coast, Nov., 1912; seconded for serv. in col. sec.'s office, 1912-13; dist. commr., G. Coast, 27th Nov., 1912; col. sec.'s office, 1912-15; priv. sec. to ag. gov., 1914; ag. dist. commr., Ashanti, 1916-18; dist. commr., 6th Oct., 1918.
SKERETT, HON. SIR CHARLES PERRIN, K.C.M.G. (1927), K.C.—B. 1863; ed. privately; called to bar, 1884; created K.C., 1907; ch. just., N.Z., 1926; for many years pres., N.Z. law soc'y.

SKINNER, ARTHUR DONALD WILLIAM.—B. 1890; ed. Bedford Schol.; 4th clk., col. sec.'s office, Antigua, Aug., 1914; sub-inspr. of schools, St. Kitts-Nevis, Apl., 1915; o/c cadet corps, St. Kitts-Nevis, 1917; ag. inspr. of schls., Leeward Is., Apr., 1918 to Feb. 1920; clk. to commr., clk. of couns. and educnl. dist. offr., Montserrat, Feb.-Oct., 1920; 1st clk., col. sec.'s office, clk. of couns., Antigua and supt., govt. printing office, Leeward Is., Nov., 1920; ag. astt. col. sec. and ag. clk. of couns., Leeward Is. on various occasions, 1921-24.

SKINNER, LESLIE ERNEST.—Gloucestershire R.F.A., 3rd Apr., 1908 to 9th July, 1910; B.S.A. Pol., 22nd Aug., 1910 to 21st Aug., 1913; E.A.P. Police, 17th Sept., 1913; astt. supt., 1st Apr., 1915; staff offr. to inspr.-gen. of pol. and prisons, Kenya Colony and Uganda Prot., 1st May, 1920 to 28th Feb., 1922; qualified in senior captain's course, Schl. of Musketry, Hythe, Sept., 1921; supt. pol., Nyasaland, 11th Nov., 1922; ag. ch. commr., pol. and ch. inspr., prisons, 17th May, 1923 to 31st Jan., 1924; astt. commdt., pol., Zanzibar, Nov., 1924.
