SKINNER, ROBERT BURNELL.—B. 1893; ed. Bedford Modern Schol.; held various minor appts., Leeward Is., 1910-20; ch. clk. to admr., and clk., couns., St. Kitts-Nevis, Nov., 1920; ch. clk. to admr. and clk., couns., Dominica, Nov., 1924; sec., loan bd., May, 1926.

SKINNER, WILLIAM ALEXANDER GEORGE.—B. 1865; joined N.Z. printing and stationery dept., 1879; offr.-in-charge, Lyttelton prison printing office, 1889; overseer, govt. printing office, 1906; supt., 1909; govt. printer, 1922.

SKIRVING, JOHN MACKENZIE.—B. 1874; ed. at George Watson's Coll., Edin.; served nine years in 91st Higths.; 1st cls. certif. of educn., Group I., 1894; certif. of mil. eng., 1896; on Boer prisoners of war staff as warrant off., S. Africa and St. Helena, Nov., 1899, to Oct., 1902 (Queen's medal and clasp); inspr. of pol., gaoler and mag.'s clk., St. Helena, Mar., 1903; served in various off. capacities in St. Helena, Antigua, Montserrat and Dominica, 1906-14; ex-officio J.P. for Leeward Islands; ag. mag., Dist. F., May-Aug., 1913; capt. (2nd in command), Dominica defence force, 4th Aug., 1914; comdg. local forces, Dominica, 13th Sept., 1914; in command of 3rd (Leeward Is.) contingent for B.W.I.R., at Barbados, June-July, 1917; also paying offr. and offr.-in-charge records and dispersal, B.W.I. Regt., Dominica, from 1st Nov., 1917 to 29th Oct., 1920; inspr. pol., offr. instr. to local forces and in charge of prison, fire brig. and weights and measures depts., Montserrat, Oct., 1920; ag. in charge of P.W.D., and mem. exec. and leg. couns., Apr., 1923; re-transfd. to Antigua as ch. keeper of prisons, sup't., St. John's Training Schol. and supt., pauper cemetery, Leeward Is., 1st Nov., 1924.
SLACK, ERIC, M.B., L.R.C.P. (Lond.), certif., L.S.T.M.—B. 1872; med. offr., Basutoland leper asylum, 1919; war serv., R.A.M.C., 1915-17.

SLACK, KENNETH HUGH.—B. 1885; ag. clk., mag., dist. "C," St. Kitts, in 1905, 1906 and 1907; clk. and inspr. wks., P.W.D., Jan., 1909; rev. offr., Dieppe Bay, Apr., 1910; gov't. offr. and harbrmr., Sandy Point, July, 1914; 3rd rev. offr., Apr., 1915; ag. 1st rev. offr. and harbrmr., 1916; 2nd rev. offr., Jan., 1917; ag. 1st rev. offr. and harbrmr., 1919: dep. harbrmr., Apr., 1920; ag. cashier, 1920; ag. 1st rev. offr. and harbrmr., 1922; lieut., St. Kitts-Nevis Defence Force.
SLADE-HAWKINS, W.—Asst. dist. commnr., E. Africa Prot., Apl., 1919.

SLADEN, ARTHUR F., C.M.G. (1911); C.V.O. (1916).—B. 1866; ed. Haileybury Coll. and Royal Naval Coll., Greenwich; went to Canada, 1887; apptd. to gov.-gen.'s office, 1891; now priv. sec. to H.E. Lord Byng of Vimy, gov.-gen.; acted in similar capacity to five previous gov'sen.

SLADER, C. H. YORKE.—Ent. Jamaica Govt. serv., 1883; 1st cls. clk., sup. ct., 1894; ag. regiar., 1895-7; lieut., Jamaica militia, 1895; offr. with Jubilee contingent (medal), 1897; capt., 1899; passed exam. equal to solrs. final, 1899; clk. of cts., Clarendon, 1900; ag. commnr. and judge of grand court, Cayman Islands, 1906; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1907; ag. judge, sup. ct., Turks Island, 1909; J.P. and stip. mag., Kingston, Oct., 1909; res. mag., Portland, Jan., 1910; res. mag., Clarendon, 1912; ditto, St. Ann, 1914; major, sea. regt., 1914; res. mag., St. Elizabeth, 1922; ditto, St. Catherine, 1926; compiled 3rd edit. "Index to Laws of Jamaica," 1911; ed. 5th edit., "Sub-Officer's Guide," 1924.

SLATER, SIR ALEXANDER RANSFORD, K.C.M.G. (1924); C.M.G. (1916); C.B.E. (1918).—B. 1874; ed. at King Ed. schl., Birmingham, and Emm. Coll., Camb. (scholar); B.A. 1897 (30th wrangler); cadet, Ceylon, Nov., 1898; extra offr. asst. gov't. agt., W. P., Aug., 1899; 2nd asst., P.M.G., Mar., 1900; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Dec., 1901; on spec. duty at camp for prisoners of war Diratalawa, July, 1902; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec., Jan., 1903; clk. legis. coun., May, 1904; dist. judge, Badulla, Apl., 1906; additional asst. col. sec. and clk., legis. couns., Apl., 1907; on special deptn. to Straits and F.M.S., Jan., 1908; 2nd asst. col. sec., Feb., 1908; ag. prin. asst. col. sec., Dec., 1909; dep. collr. of cust., Colombo, Sept., 1910; joint commnr. to report on salaries of pub. serv., Nov., 1911; prin. asst. col. sec., Sept., 1912, and clk. to exec. coun.; col. sec., G. Coast 18th Aug., 1914; on special duty in Togoland, 1st to 28th Sept., 1915; ag. gov't., G. Coast on several occasions; gov. and commr.-in-chief, Sierra Leone, 4th Apr., 1922.

SLEEP, CAPT. ARTHUR.—B. 1894; cadet, F.M.S., Feb., 1920; asst. supt., lands, Kelantan, Mar., 1923; attd. dist. office, Lower Perak, Dec., 1924; asst. dist. offr., Bruas, Mar., 1925; offr., cls. IV, Mar., 1926.

SLOAN, HON. WILLIAM.—B. 1866; took up residence in British Columbia in 1888, residing at Vancouver and Nanaimo; unsuccessful cand. for H. of C., 1900; elec. to H. of C., 1904, for Comox-Atlin; re-elec., 1908, but resigned in favour of Hon. William Templeman, min. of int. rev., who was defeated in Victoria; elec. to legis. ass., B.C., 1916; re-elec., g.e., 1920; min. of mines in Brewster cabinet, 1916.

SLOLEY, SIR HERBERT CECIL, K.C.M.G. (1911); C.M.G. (1906).—Served in C.M.R. in campaigns against Griquas and Moirosi (medal with clasp, 1877-8-9); capt. in native contingent, Basuto war, 1880-1; mentioned in despatches; sub-inspr., Cape police, 1883; ditto, Basutoland, 1884; inspr., 1886; asst. commnr., Basutoland, 1889; ag. res. commnr., July to Dec., 1896; gov't. sec., 1896; ag. res. commnr., Dec., 1900; res. commnr., Sept., 1901; ret., 1926.

SMALL, ALEXANDER SYM.—B. 1887; ed. Glasgow Univ.; cadet, F.M.S., Jan., 1911; ag. asst. sec. to res., Selangor, Aug., 1911; ag. dist. offr., Kuala Langat, Apr., 1912; ag. sec. to res., Nogri Sembilan, Jan., 1913; ag. asst. dist. offr., Krian, Jan., 1916; ag. dist. offr., Raub, May, 1916; ag. ast. adviser, Endau, Johore, Sept., 1918; ag. ast. adviser, Muar, Johore, June, 1920; ag. ast. dir., educn., S.S. and F.M.S., Apr., 1922; ag. ast. treas., F.M.S. and state treas., Selangor, Nov., 1923.

SMALLEY, JAMES THORNTON, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.).—B. 1882; late clinical ast., ast. house surg., out-pat. offr. and house surg., Guy's hosp.; clinical ast., Evelina hosp., London; R.M.O., Colony hosp., Suva, Fiji; D.M.O., Ra.; D.M.O. and M.O.H., and port med. offr., Levuka; stip. mag., Levuka, 1910-1913; med. offr., Kowloon and New Territories, Hong Kong, med. offr., Kowloon-Canton rly., and med. offr. in charge, pub. mortuary, Kowloon, 1913; ag. ast. M.O.H., 1916; ditto, in addition to other duties, 1916; lecturer in Pharmacology and Therapeutics, and examiner in Physiology, Hong Kong Univ.; J.P., 1919; visiting med. offr., Kwong Wah hospital; med. offr. in charge, Tai Chi Kok branch prison; surg. lieut., Hong Kong Volunteer Corps; med. offr., govt. civil hosp., 1923; med. offr., lunatic asylum, 1923; ag. port med. offr., 1924; med. offr., Kowloon and New Territories, med. offr. in charge pub. mortuary, Kowloon and Kowloon pub. hosp., 1925.
SMART, L. M.—Ast. traffic manager, Uganda Rlyv., Apl., 1919.
SMARTT, FITZPATRICK FORBES PERCY.—Ed. Denstone Coll.; probationer, col. audit dept., July, 1921; ast. audr., Br. Honduras, Oct., 1921.

SMARTT, HON. SIR THOS. WM., P.C. (1911), K.C.M.G. (1911)—L.R.C.S., Ireland, 1878; L.K.Q.C.P.I., 1880; M.L.A., Cape Colony, since 1894; col. sec., 1898; comnrr. of pub. wks., 1900-02, and 1904-1908; ag. prime minister during periods of 1904, 1905 and 1906; attended Impl. confee., 1907; mem. of S. African Nat. Conventn. in connection with S. Africa Bill, 1909; holds Cape Colony general service medal, 1879, with clasp, "Transkei," having served as surg. with P.A.O.C.V.A.; also holds medal and clasp for Kimberley siege, 1899-1900; M.L.A. for Fort Beaufort; min. for agr., 1921-24.

SMIT, HABOLD PRINGLE.—B. 1883; joined Cape civ. serv., Jan., 1901; office of dir. of mil. intell., May-Aug., 1902; Transvaal law dept., 1st Sept., 1902; oh. clk. to atty-gen., Transvaal, July, 1908; addl. mag., Benoni, Sept., 1914; war stores comnrr., 1915; mag., Bloemhof dist., Dec., 1915; ch. clk., dept. of just., Dec., 1917; pub. serv. inspr., Feb., 1922; sec. for S.W. Africa, Apr., 1923; dep. admstr., 19th May-31st Oct., 1924.

SMIT, JACOBUS STEPHANUS.—B. 1878; D.T.D. (Dekoratie Trouwe Dienat) Anglo-Boer War; barrister-at-law, Middle Temple, 1905; pvt. sec. to Gen. Smuts as col. sec., Transvaal, 1907; to Gen. Botha as premier, Transvaal, 1907 and to Hon. J. de Villiers as atty.-gen., Transvaal, 1908; asst. mag., Transvaal, 1909; asst. mag., Union civ. serv., 1910; resig., 1913; el. mem. for Klerk-dorp, Union of S. Africa legis. assem., 1920, 1921 and 1924; high comnrr. for Union of S. Africa, London, 1925.

SMITH, ALLAN F., I.S.O. (1906).—Clk. in P.O., Bermuda, 1879; olk., col. sec.'s office, 1886; asst. col. sec., 1896; col. postmtr., 1896; ag. col. sec., July and Aug., 1899, and from May, 1900 to July, 1901; recvr.-gen., 1908; awarded Royal Humane Socv.'s vellum cert. for saving life, 1914.

SMITH, LIEUT. ARTHUR BERTRAM, F.R.G.S.—B. 1889; ed. R. Masonic Schl., Bushey; sub-lieut., R.N.R., Oct., 1914; ag. lieut., Jan., 1915; served in 8th submarine flotilla, 1914-15; Baltic submarine flotilla, 1915-17; Russian Order of St. Anne, 3rd cls. (with swords), 1915; Russian Order of St. Vladimir, 4th cls. (with swords), 1916; commanded submarine C 14 (N. Sea), Mar. to Nov., 1918, and various surrendered German submarines, Nov., 1918 to Mar., 1919; port offr., Dar-es-Salaam, Mar., 1919; harmr. and supt. of lighthouses, Trinidad, 27th Feb., 1926.

SMITH, B. O.—Cler. asst. post. dept., Br. Guiana, Aug., 1898; sec., leave and pensions comnrr., 1920; sec., dept. salaries comtce., 1921; gradually promoted to 1st cls. clk. and sec. currency comnrrs., July, 1923; transf. to col. secretariat, Apr., 1925; asst. sup., Oct., 1925.

SMITH, CECIL FURNES.—B. 1890; ed. Birkenhead schl. and St. John's coll., Camb.; B.A., 1912; LL.B., 1913; 2nd lieut., gen. res. of offrs.; asst. dist. comnrr., G. Coast, 29th Apr., 1914; dist. comnrr., 1921; called to bar, Inner Temple, 1922; dist. crown coun., Mar., 1925.
SMITH, CHARLES BERNARD, B.A.—B. 1887; ed. Pembroke Coll., Oxford; jnr. supt., educn. dept., N. Nigeria, 9th July, 1913; attached Nigeria Regt., 1917-18; asst. dir., educn., S. Provs., Nigeria, 1926.

SMITH, CHRISTOPHER PATRICK, B.A.—B.1894; ed. St. Mary's Coll., Dublin and National Univ. of Ireland; Inns of Court O.T.C., Oct., 1915; cadet, R. Mily. Coll., Sandhurst, 1916; 2nd lieut., R. Irish Regt., attd. to Machine Gun Corps, 1917; demob., 1919; dep. supt., merc. marine branch, Board of Trade, 1920; cadet, F.M.S., Apr., 1921; addt. to Chinese prot., Perak, May, 1921; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Selangor and Pahang, Feb., 1924; passed cadet, Feb., 1924; ag. prot. of Chinese, Negri Semilan, May, 1924, ditto, Taiping, Dec., 1924; offr., cls. IV, Sept., 1925; addt., cust. dept., F.M.S., Mar., 1926.
SMITH, E. A.—B. 1896; ent. E.I.O., Dec., 1911; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., Dec., 1913; on mily. serv., Dec., 1915 to Jan., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920.
SMITH, EMILE HAMEL, L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S., L.F.P. and S., Glas.—B. 1879; ent. med. serv., Trinidad, 16th Sept., 1909; dist. med. offr., 21st Dec., 1912.

SMITH, ERNEST GARDINER, M.A., LL.B.—Advoc.; univ. prizeman in constitl. law and hist.; hons. certifs. in civ. law, pub. internat. law and med. jurisprudence; asst. dist. comnrr., S. Nigeria, 29th Aug., 1908; asst. pol. mag., 23rd Jan., 1911; 2nd grade mag., Nigeria, 1st Jan., 1914; 1st grade mag., 29th Jan., 1914; crown proser., 10th Sept., 1914; ag. legal adviser and mem., leg. coun. for various periods, 1914-19; crown counsel, 1st Jan., 1920; ag. solr.-gen., Aug., 1920 to Apr., 1921, and Aug., 1921 to Mar., 1922; circuit judge, Ashanti and N. Territories, G. Coast, 9th Aug., 1922; ag. puisne judge, G. Coast, 1st Apr. to 4th July, 1924; puisne judge, G. Coast, 12th June, 1925.

SMITH, FRANCIS JAGOE.—M.A. (Oxon); ed. Merchant Taylors schl.; scholar, St. John's Coll., Oxford, 1892; 2nd cls. class. moda., 1894; 2nd math. moda., 1894; 2nd lit. hum., 1896; B.A., 1896; M.A., 1902; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Oct., 1896; attached to col. sec.'s off., 1896; office asst. to govt. agt., and deputy fiscal, Cent. Prov., Apr., 1998; comnrr. of requests and pol. mag., Balapitiya, Aug., 1900; ditto, Galle, June, 1901; land surveyr., customs, Colombo, Dec., 1901; 2nd asst. col. sec., Sept., 1906; dep. collr. of cust., Colombo, Feb., 1908; dist. judge, Galle, Dec., 1911; govt. agt., Prov. of Uva, July, 1912; on special duty, visiting post offices in Ceylon, Straits and F.M.S., May, 1913; ag. dist. judge, Galle, Aug., 1913; postmtr.-gen., Oct., 1913; dir. of food production, Nov., 1920; P.M.G., Apr., 1921; ag. col. treas., July to Sept., 1923; govt. agt., N. Prov., July, 1924.

SMITH, F. M. URLING.—B. 1876; ed. at St. John's, Leatherhead, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; schoolmaster in England, Scotland and Wales from 1900 to 1906; served in ed. dept., Egypt, 1906 to 1910; supt. of Nassarawa schools, N. Nigeria, 28th Sept., 1910; senr. supt., 1st Jan., 1913; dir. of educn., Northern Provs., 24th Aug., 1919.

SMITH, SIR GEORGE, K.C.M.G. (1914), C.M.G. (1911).—B. 1883; entd. war office, 10th Apr., 1878; clk., ch. sec.'s office, Cyprus, 24th Nov., 1879; ch. clk., ditto, 1881; asst. to ch. sec., 15th Dec., 1883; ag. dir. of survey, 10th Aug., 1886, to 14th Feb., 1887; clk., legis. coun., 1st Mar., 1886, to 24th Nov., 1891; ag. ch. sec., and mem. of exec. comn., 15th May, to 10th Oct., 1888, and on other occasions; comnrr., Papho, 25th Nov., 1891; registr. gen. and off. mem. of legis. coun., 7th June, 1895; has served also as mem. of comtce. of management, Cyprus museum, 1896; sec., Col. and Ind. Exhib. comtce., 1896; sec. to Queen's Jubilee Memorial comtce., 1897; mem. of land registn. comnrr., 1891; pres. mun. comnrr., Papho, 1892; mem. of ecclesiastical corp. comnrr., 1895; mem. of mun. comnrr., Nicosia, 1895; mem. of gen. hosp. bd., Nicosia, 1896; mem. of bd. of agric., 1896; ag. British delegate of Evcaf, under conven. with Turkey, 20th Mar., to 25th Nov., 1903; ag. prin. forest offr., 1903, and 1907; ag. dir. of agric., 1904 and 1908; British delegate of Evcaf, 20th July, 1905; ag. rear-gen., and ch. collr. of cust., 1906, 1906, 1907 and 1908; col. sec., Mauritius, 10th June, 1910; administered govt., Apr. to Nov., 1911; gov., Nyasaland, 17th May, 1913; assumed govt., 23rd Sept., 1913; ret., Dec., 1923.

SMITH, CAPT. GEORGE ECHLIN.—B. 1871; ed. Rathmines Sch. and Trin. Coll., Dublin; joined 5th batt. R. Irish Regt., 1891; capt., 1895; hon. capt. in army, 1900; asst. inspr., Hauss force (G. Coast batt.), Jan., 1897; sub-inspr., Br. Guiana pol., Feb., 1898; dist. inspr., Oct., 1900; A.D.C. to Sir J. A. Swettenham, 1902-04; A.D.C. to Sir F. Hodgson, 1904-06; asst. dist. supt., pol., E. Africa Prot., Oct., 1906; supt., pol., Apr., 1914; comdt. pol. and gov. of prisons, Zanzibar, May, 1926-25; pte. sec. and A.D.C. to Sir D. Cameron, gov., Tanganyika Territory, 1925; 1914-15 Star, Gen. Serv. and Victory med.; King's pol. med., 1924.

SMITH, GERALD STANLEY WELLS.—B. 1873; gr. duate, Ontario business coll., Canada; superv. clk., regist. s'office, Grenada, Jan., 1890; asst. clk., post office, Mar., 1890; 4th clk., treasy., June, 1890; asst. clk., col. sec.'s office, Oct., 1890; 2nd clk., treasy., Nov., 1891; audit clk., Nov., 1897; thanked by gov. for report on condition of paupers, 1905; has held numerous acting appointments, including ag. aud. of Windward Islands, 4th June to 31st Dec., 1906; 1st Jan. to 13th Mar., 1907; and from 18th Mar., 1908, to 24th Nov., 1909; ag. comnr. of Carriacou and St. Vincent, Southern Grenadines, 24th Jan. to 31st Dec., 1912; ag. auditor, Windward Is., for six months in 1915; colonial postmaster, Grenada, 9th Nov., 1915; colonial postmaster, B. Honduras, 13th Apr., 1919; treasy., B. Hond., 1st Apr., 1923; mem., exec. and leg. couns.; mem., bd. educn.; pres., quarantine bd.; chmn., poor relief comtee.; dir., pub. offr., quarantine fund; chmn., income tax comtee.

SMITH, HERBERT DENSHAM.—B. 1882; entd. Impl. civ. serv. and apptd. to act. gen'l. dept., Adm'y., 1901; asst. acct., P.W.D., Nigeria, 1910; acct., P.W.D., Sierra Leone, 1914; transfd. to treasy., Sierra Leone, as ast. treas., Jan., 1916; ag. senr. ast. treas. for various periods in 1917, 1918 and 1919; ag. ch. acct., rly., May to Aug., 1922; ag. treas. and currency offr., 1919 and 1923; reor. gen. and currency offr., Gambia, Jan., 1925; mem., exec. and leg. couns.

SMITH, HUGH MIDDLETON BRICE, M.A.—B. 1884; ed. at Pecklington Schl. and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 16th Jan., 1909; 2nd cls. dist. offr., 17th Oct., 1917; ag. mag. in 1921 and 1922.

SMITH, HERBERT FRANCIS, O.B.E. (1920).—B. 1873; ed. at Malvern coll. (class. scholar); entd. Crown Agents' office, Feb., 1893; ast. cashier, 1900; head of gen. stores dept., August, 1904; sec., Dec., 1920 to Nov., 1921; mem., fair wages advisory comtee., 1921; sec., E. African Currency Bd., 1922; head of stores dept., 1922; head of shipping dept., 1924.
SMITH, JAMES.—B. 1882; stock inspr., Basutoland, 1911; sub. inspr., Basutoland mounted pol., 1917; inspr., 1926.

SMITH, JAMES ALFRED, M.B.E. (1918).—B. 1871; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to the C.O., Apr., 1891; promoted to higher grade, 1902; ast. sec. to col. regna. comtee., 1907-8; staff clk., 1st grade, 1915; staff offr., 1st Apr., 1922.

SMITH, JAMES DAVID MAXWELL, M.A., Aberdeen.—B. 1896; cadet, F.M.S., July, 1921; ast. collr., land rev., Lower Perak, Oct., 1920; offr. in charge, treasy., Telok Anson, Oct., 1920; ag. 2nd ast. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Aug., 1921; ag. ast. dist. offr., Gopeng and Kampar, Nov., 1921; ag. ast. dist. offr., Sitiawan, Dec., 1922; ag. dist. offr., Temerloh, Nov., 1923; ast. sec. to res., Perak, July, 1925; ag. sec., res., Perak, July, 1926; offr., cl. IV, Sept., 1926.

SMITH, JAMES LANDRETH.—B. 1870; draftsman, P.W.D., St. Lucia, 13th July, 1891; draftsman, survey offr., Sept., 1893; warden and supt. of water-wks. under St. George's town bd., Apr., 1896, to Aug., 1901; chief overseer of roads and works, Grenada, 19th Aug., 1901; ast. supt. of wks., 1st Apr., 1902; comnr. of crown lands, 28th June, 1904; survr. of crown lands, 1904; ag. supt. of wks., 14th June, 1906, to 16th Jan., 1907; supervisor, govt. cotton ginnery, 1st Oct., 1912; ag. supt. pub. wks., St. Vincent, 4th Oct., 1913; col. engnr., Oct., 1918 to Aug., 1920; supr. pub. wks., St. Kitts-Nevis, Sept., 1920.

SMITH, JOHN.—B. 1883; ed. New Vety. Coll., Edinburgh (M.R.C.V.S.), and Liverpool Univ. (D.V.H.); vett. offr., N. Rhodesia, June, 1913; resigned, Apr., 1916; active serv., 1916-19; capt.; reappt., Oct., 1921; ag. ch. vety. offr., Nov., 1921; May, 1922; ch. vety. offr., May, 1922; mem., leg. counc.; ag. sec. for agr., Apr., 1925; J.P.

SMITH, JOHN NOEL.—B. 1886; ed. at Summer Fields, Oxford, Radley Coll. and Hertford Coll., Oxford; 2nd lieut., 3rd (Res.) Batt., Oxford and Bucks. Light Infantry, 1909; seconded for serv. as ast. res., N. Nigeria, 30th Nov., 1910; attd., Nigeria Regt., Cameroons, 24th Mar., 1915; station mag., N. Provs., 22nd Mar., 1919; dist. offr., 17th Jan., 1921; ag. senr. res., 7th Sept., 1923.
SMITH, HARRY ALSTON.—B. 1886; stock inspr., Basutoland, 1913; sub. inspr. Basutoland mounted pol., 1919; war serv., 1914-18.

SMITH, LESLIE STEPHEN.—B. 1898; entd. regyrs. of friendly societies, 16th Jan., 1914; apptd. after compet. exam., ast. clk., 27th Apr., 1916; on mily. serv., Jan., 1917 to Feb., 1919; trans. to savings bank dept., G.P.O., May, 1919; trans. to C.O., June, 1923.

SMITH, LINDSAY LEA.—B. 1870; ag. clk. to comnr., Turks Is., 1894, 1896, 1900, and 1903; confirmed 1st July, 1903; ag. acctnt. and clk. (collr. of customs, postmr., cashier and reg. of shipping) comnr.'s office, June to Sept., 1904, and May to Aug., 1906; acctnt. and clk. (collr. of cust., postmr., cashier and reg. of shipping), Jan., 1907; gen. man., savings bank, 1st July, 1907; ag. ast. comnr., Dec., 1910 to Jan., 1911, and from Sept., 1912 to Apr., 1913.

SMITH, NORMAN LOCKHART.—B. 1887; ed. at Sedburgh and Queen's Coll., Oxford (Hastings scholar); B.A. 1910; cadet, Hong Kong, 20th Oct., 1910; ag. ast. dist. offr., (north), 1912; ag. 2nd A.R.G., 1913; J.P. 1913; ag. ast. col. sec. and elk. of couns., 1913; ag. 1st ast. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1913; ag. 3rd ast. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1914; ag. ast. postmr-gen., 1914; seconded for serv. as British postmaster at Shanghai, 1914; ag. postmr-gen., 1915; ag. ast. P.M.G., 1916; seconded for military service, 1916-19; ag. deputy regyrs. and appraiser, 1919; ag. supr., Victoria Gaol, 1919; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1919, 1920 and 1923; recd. Chinese order of Wen-hu, 5th cls., 1920; qualified in Pakistane, 31st Mar., 1920; ag. supt., impts. and expts., 1921; head of sany. dept., 1924.

SMITH, P. C., M.I.Munic.E. (Lond.)—B. 1879; ed. at Wyggeston boys' schl., Eng.; pupil with Messrs. Keites and Fosbrooks, archs. and surrks., Leicester, for five years, and remained for two years as ass't.; joined staff of Messrs. Johnson and Langley, contra., Lond.; ass't. to Mr. C. F. Wike, M.I.C.E., city engnr., Sheffield, 1902; town engnr., Rainy River, Ont., Can., 1910; town engnr., Souris, Manitoba, 1911; dist. engnr., Berbice, Br. Guiana, Aug., 1915.
SMITH, PETEER HAMBLIN, B.A. (Oxon.)—B. 1900; ed. Denstone coll. and Lincoln coll., Oxford; ass't. dist. comsnr., Sierra Leone, 5th Sept., 1923.

SMITH, REGINALD HOPKINSON.—B. 1898; ed. Chatham House, Ramsgate; served in the Royal Naval Divn., Oct. to Dec., 1914; H.A.C., Oct., 1915 to Oct., 1917; Indian Army R. of O., Nov., 1917 to Aug., 1919; Aden Field Force, July, 1918 to July, 1919; dist. pol. offr., Somaliland, July, 1919.
SMITH, REGINALD MONTAGU BOSWORTH.—Sub.-inspr., Basutoland mtd. pol., 1896; inspr., 1905; ass't. comsnr., 1909; govt. sec., 1920; ag. dep. res. comsnr. for various periods in 1921, 1923 and 1924.
SMITH, SIDNEY W.—B. 1902; apptd., after compet. exam., ass't. clk., C.O., Oct., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920.

SMITH, STEPHEN HENRY, F.R.Aus.H.S.—B.1865; ed. Grafton Superior Pub. Sch.; inspr., continuation schls., N.S. Wales, 13th June, 1912 to 29th Feb., 1920; ass't. under sec., educon., dept., N.S.W., June, 1920; dir., educon., N.S.W., 1923.

SMITH, SYDNEY RICHARD, O.B.E. (Mil.), 1919.—B. 1875; ass't. engnr., P.W.D., Selangor, Apr., 1900; dist. engnr., Ulu Selangor, Jan., 1901; ag. ex. engnr., grade III, Sept., 1902; ag. ex. engnr., grade II, May, 1904; ex. engnr., grade III, Perak, Dec., 1909; ex. engnr., grade II, Jan., 1913; adjt., M.S.V.R., Apr., 1915; ment. in despa., July, 1918; sen. ex. engnr., P.W.D., Jan., 1919; ag. state engnr., Selangor, May, 1921; awarded Col. Aux. Forces Long Serv. med., June, 1922; ag. state engnr., Pahang, Jan., 1923; state engnr., Negri Sembilan, Aug., 1923; ag. asst. dir., pub. wks., F.M.S., Aug., 1923; ag. dir., pub. wks., F.M.S., Feb., 1925; ag. state engnr., Selangor, Apr., 1925.

SMITH, WALTER JOHN.—B. 1884; ass't. supt. wks., P.W.D., S. Stltmsts., Jan., 1908; ass't. engnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Sept., 1909; ex. engnr., Kedah, Apr., 1917; ex. engnr., grade III, July, 1908; ag. state engnr., Kedah, Aug., 1920 to Apr., 1921; sen. ex. engnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Aug., 1923.

SMITH, WILLIAM HUMPHREY.—B. 1879; ed. Uppingham and Jesus Coll., Camb., Rustatschol., 1897; Abbott univ. schol., 1899; B.A., 1st cls.; class. tripos, pt. I, 1901; 2nd cls. pt. II, history, 1902; clk., col. audit branch, exchequer and audit dept., 1st Dec., 1903; ass't. audr., Nyasaland, 1st Oct., 1904; audr., N. Nigeria, 1st Feb., 1910; examr., exchequer and audit dept., 17th Aug., 1910; audr., Seychelles, 23rd Mar., 1911; 1st divn. clk. (2nd cls.), col. audit dept., 1st Oct., 1912; on mil. serv., 5th Aug., 1914; capt., K.O.Y.L.I., 29th Jan., 1915; wounded, 8th Mar., 1917; relinquished coman. owing to wounds, and returned to col. audit dept., 29th Sept., 1918; audr., Uganda, 29th Dec., 1923.

SMITH, WILLIAM RAMSAY.—D.Sc., M.D., M.S., Edin.; chmn. cent. bd. of health, coroner, vaccination offr., and inspr. of anatomy, S. Australia, 1899-1903; chmn., cent. bd. of health, and coroner, 1903; author of several medical and scientific works.

SMITH-DORRIEN, GEN. SIR HORACE LOCKWOOD, G.C.B. (1913), G.C.M.G. (1915), D.S.O. (1886); grand off., legion of honour; A.D.C. gen. to the King, 1910-14.—B. 1858; served in Zulu war, 1879; Egyptian war, 1882; Soudan campaign, 1885; Soudan field force, 1885-6, 4th cls. Medjidie; 4th cls. Osmanlieh, Chitral expedit., 1895; N.W. Frontier, India, 1897-98; Tirah exped. force; Nile expedit., 1898; S. African war, 1900-01; Kuropean war, 1914-15; adjt.-gen. in India, 1901-3; commdg. Quetta div. of army in India, 1903-7; commdg.-in-chief, Aldershot, 1907-12; commdg.-in-chief southern commd., 1912-14; commdg. 2nd army corps and 2nd British exped. force in France, 1914-15; commdg. E. African exped. force, 1915; gov., Gibraltar, 9th July, 1918; assumed govt., 7th Sept., 1918, ret., 1923.

SMITH-REWS, GEOFFREY WHISTLER BINGHAM.—B. 1878; ed. at Tonbridge Schl. and R. Mily. Coll., Sandhurst; R. Warwickshire Regt., 1898; 28th Madras Infy., 1900; 4th Punjab Infy., Punjab Field Force, 1901; China War, 1900; med., Fiji civ. serv., 1908; dist. mag., Ellice Is., Dec., 1908; dist. offr., Gilbert and Ellice Is., July, 1912; dep. comsnr. for W. Pacific (Ellice Is.), Jan., 1914; dist. offr., Tarawa, May, 1915; dep. comsnr. for W. Pacific (Gilbert Is.), May, 1915 and Sept., 1922; ag. agt. and consul, Tonga, Sept., 1916; ag. admstr., Nauru, Nov., 1917 to June, 1921; 1st dist. offr., Gilbert and Ellice Is., Col., July, 1920; dep. regis. of marriages, Nov., 1922; ag. res. comsnr., New Hebrides, June, 1924; dep. comsnr., for W. Pacific, (New Hebrides), June, 1924; res. comsnr., New Hebrides, July, 1924.
SMITHERS, ROBERT FRANCOIS.—Clk., atty. gens.-office, Apr., 1902; senr. clk., Jan., 1910; pub.-serv. inspr., Sept., 1921.

SMUTS, GEN. THE RIGHT HON. J. C., P.O. (1917), C.H., K.C.—B. 1870; ed. at Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; B.A., Cape Univ., 1891; Ebbden scholar at Christ's Coll., Camb.; double first in law tripods, 1894; barrister, Cape Col.; state-attorney, S. African Republic, 1898; served with Boer forces during S. African war, and was in comm. of Boer forces in Cape Col. during the latter part of the campaign; mem. of head comtes. of Het Volk; col. sec., Transvaal, 1907; mem. of Imp. Conf. on Naval and Military Defence, 1909; min. of finance and defence, Union of S. Africa, 1912; min. of defence, 1915; in command of troops in E. Africa, 1916; mem. of British war cabinet; represented S. Africa at peace confce. at Paris, 1919; prime min. and min. of native affrs., Union of S. Africa, 1919-24; M.L.A. for Standerton, 1924.

SMYLY, SIR PHILIP CRAMPTON, KT. BACH, (1903).—B. 1866; ed. at Trin. Coll., Dub., B.A., LL.B.; called to the bar, King's Inns, Dub., 1888; LL.D., 1891; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, 1902; Queen's advoo., S. Leone, 1895; atty.-gen., 1896; ch.just., S. Leone, 1901; ch. just., Gold Coast, 1911.

SMYTH, HERBERT WARINGTON, C.M.G. (1919).—B. 1887; ed. at Westmstr. and Trin. Coll., Camb., M.A., L.L.M., F.G.S., F.R.G.S., M.I.M.M., barr.-at-law; advoc. of sup. ct., Transvaal; unpaid asst. to mineral adviser to office of woods, 1891; sec., dept. of mines, Siam, 1891; director-gen. of mines and geological survey, Siam, 1895; sec., Siamese Legation, 1897 (order of White Elephant, 3rd cls.); Murchison award of R.G.S. for travels in Siam, 1898; sec. to mines dept., Transvaal, 1901; M.L.C., Transvaal, 1906; mem. exec. coun. while ag. comsrr. of mines, 1906; sec. for mines, 1907; ag. sec. for mines, Union of S. Africa, and comsrr. of mines, Natal, 1910; sec., mines and industries, 1917; ch. inspr. of factories, 1918; author of "Five Years in Siam," "Mast and Sail in Europe and Asia," papers on Indo-China, etc., etc.

SNELGAR, HERBERT JOHN.—B. 1890; ed. Christ's Hosp. and King's Coll., London; clk., bd. of educn., 1906; clk., C.A., 1910; clk., land dept., E.A.P., 1912; ag. office supvr., 1917 and 1919; land astt., 1919; sec., land tenure coman., 1920; warden of mines, 1924; regisr. of titles, etc., 1922; astt. land offr., 1922.

SOLOMON, MICHAEL CLAUDE.—Copyist, med. dept., Jamaica, Oct., 1885; 3rd cls. clk., 1888; 2nd cls. clk., 1891; 1st cls. clk. and sec., cent. bd. of health, 1908; ch. clk., 1920; sec. to coman. on reorganization of med. serv., 1920.

SOLOMON, HON. SIR WILLIAM HENRY, K.C.M.G. (1913), KT. BACH. (1907), M.A.—B. 1852; ed. at S. African Coll., Cape Town and Cambridge Univ.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1876; raised to the bench, Kimberley, 1887; afterwards transferred to Eastern Districts Court in same capacity; puisne judge sup. ct., Transvaal, Mar., 1902; ordinary judge of appellate div. of sup. ct. of South Africa, May, 1910; ch. just., 1st Mar., 1927.

SOMASUNTHARAM, KANDIAH.—B. 1897; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Dec., 1921; addt., Kalutara Kach., Dec., 1921; ditto, Kurunegala Kach., Jan., 1922; ditto, Colombo Kach., Dec., 1922; office astt. to rubber contr., Mar., 1923; office astt., Galle kach., Aug., 1926; 5th astt. col. sec., Nov., 1926.

SOMERVILLE, JAMES, O.B.E.—Ck., traffic dept., Cape, 20th Mar., 1897; clk., C.T. M. office, Cape, 1st Oct., 1898; ch. clk., admstr.'s office, Transvaal, 1st Dec., 1900; sec., refugees aid dept., 1st Nov., 1901; sec., Transvaal immgrn. office, 1st Aug., 1903; ch. clk., lands dept., 1st May, 1904; under-sec. for lands, Union of Africa, 1st June, 1917; sec. for lands, 1st Apr., 1921.

SOUTHBOUROUGH, 1ST BARON (creat. 1917), RT. HON. SIR FRANCIS JOHN STEPHENS HOPWOOD, P.C. (1912), G.C.B. (1916), G.C.M.G. (1908), K.C.B. (1901), K.C.M.G. (1906), C.B. (1895), C.M.G. (1893).—B. 1860; ed. at Louth by Canon W. H. Hopwood; admitted a solr., 1882; astt. law clk., B. of T., 1885-1888; astt. solr., 1888-1892; priv. sec. to pres. of B. of T., 1892; sec., rly. dept., 1892-1901; perm. sec., 1901; perm. under-sec. of S. for the colonies, 1907-1910; employed on different occasions upon offl. missions to U.S.A., Canada, and Newfoundland; Brit. deleg. to internat. rly. congress in London, 1896; and in Paris, 1900; hon. sec. to coman. of select comte. of H. of C. on Jameson raid, 1897; mem. of London traffic coman., 1903; visited S. Africa as mem. of Transvaal and O.R.C. constitutions coman., 1906; mem. of comans. on canals and waterways, 1906; and on ocean freights and shipping "rings," 1906; accompanied H.R.H. the Prince of Wales on his visit to Quebec, 1908; mem. of Royal coman. on electoral reform, 1909; regisr. of the Order of St. Michael and St. George, 1907-1909; sec. of the Order, 1909-1911; accompanied H.R.H. the Duke of Connaught on his visit to S. Africa in connection with the opening of the first parlmt. of the Union of S. Africa, 1910; vice-chmn. of the Development coman., 1910; specially apptd. to act as under sec. of state for the colonies during the period of the Imperial Conf., 1911; additional civil lord of the Admiralty, Jan., 1912; sec. to the Irish Convention, 1917.

SOUTHORN, WILFRID THOMAS.—B. 1879; ed. Warwick schl. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1903; off. astt. to govt. agt., N. Prov., Oct. 1904; N.C. Prov., May, 1906; dist. judge, Tangalla, Apr., 1907; landing survr. customs, Colombo, July, 1909; astt. govt. agt., Matale, Nov., 1911; priv. sec. to gov., Aug., 1914; dep. collr. of customs and landing survr., Colombo, Sept., 1915; 2nd astt. col. sec., Dec., 1919; prin. astt. col. sec., July, 1920; prin. collr., cust. and food contr., Dec., 1923; mem., exec. coun., 1925; col. sec., Hong Kong, May, 1926.

SPEARMAN, BARUCH, M.A., M.B., B.C. (Camb.), D.T.M. and H., certif., London Schl. of Trop. Medicine (with distinshion).—B. 1877; med. offr., Uganda Prot., Sept., 1912; capt., Uganda med. serv., Sept., 1916; med. offr. of hth., Zanzibar, Nov., 1920; recd. thanks of ag. gov., Uganda, for services in connection with outbreak of plague at Jinja, Nov., 1914; ment. in desps., 1919; M.O.H., Zanzibar, Sept., 1920; senr. med. offr. of health, Zanzibar, 1st Jan., 1922; ag. D.M.S.S., 30th June, 1922 to 13th Feb., 1923 and 8th July, 1925 to 24th Mar., 1926.

SPEED, SIR EDWIN ARNEY, KT. BACH. (1911).—M.A., LL.B.; B. 1869; ed. at Rugby, and Trin. Coll., Camb.; major scholar and senior exhibtnr., Rugby schl.; scholar and prizeman of Trin. Coll., Camb.; 1st cls. class. tripso., 1890; 2nd cls. law tripso., Part I., 1891; called to the bar, Inner Temple, June, 1893; dist. commnr., G. Coast, 1899; atty.-gen., Lagos, 1900; edited a revised edition of Lagos laws, 1901; has acted on numerous occasions as ch. just. and also as col. sec., from Jan., 1905, to Mar., 1906; atty.-gen., S. Nigeria, May, 1906; edited revised edtn. of laws of S. Nigeria, 1907; ch. just., N. Nigeria, 1908; retired.

SPENCE, JOHN, B.A., LL.B. (Syd.).—Ent. pub. wks. dept., N'S. Wales, 1897; admitted to bar, 1909; ch. acct., P.W.D., 1918; senr. inspr., pub. serv. bd., 1919-22; in charge, govt. printing office re-organisation, 1922-23; under sec., treasury and dir. of finance, 1923.

SPENCER, CYRIL EDWARD.—B. 1873; enmd. col. ser., Cyprus, 1st Mar., 1891; in secretariat from Apr., 1892, to May, 1898; also clk. to legia. coun., Mar., 1894, to May, 1898; priv. sec. to high comanr. on several occasions, 1898-1900; inspr., mil. pol., 1st May, 1898; ag. dist. comdt., July, 1900, to Jan., 1904; apptd. in comd. of diva. of pol., Jan., 1904; also gov. of pris. and astt. to dist. comanr., and dep. coroner; ag. dist. comanr., Apr. to Dec., 1905; passed in med. Greek, higher standard, 1904; and in Turkish, lower standard, 1906; transf'd to E. East Africa as astt. dist. comanr., Nov., 1906; ag. sup't. inland rev., Apr. to Oct., 1907; ag. dist. comanr., Mombasa, from July, 1907; dist. comanr., K.A.F., Apr., 1912; senr. asst. sec., secretariat, Jan., 1919; elk. of councils, Jan., 1919; ag. asst. chief sec., Jan., 1919; comsnr. of prisons, Oct., 1923.

SPILLER, JOHN WYATT, M.Inst.C.E.—B. 1878; ed. Wellington Schl. (Som.); served in engng. dept., G.W.R., 1896 to 1910; joined engng. dept., Crown Agents, 1910; services lent to Miny. of Munitions, 1916; tech. adviser to dir., Munitions Overseas Transport, 1917; returned to C.A., 1919; dep. ch. engnr., 1919; visited Ceylon, F.M.S. and S. Stltmts., 1920; ch. engnr. (designs), 1922; visited Br. Guiana and Trinidad, 1922; acted for one year as gen. man., F.M.S. rly., 1926.

SPIO-GARBRHAH, JOSIAH.—B. 1878; 2nd asst. mast., tech. schl., Accra, Gold Coast, 1st Jan., 1899; 1st ast. mast., 19th Nov., 1902; 3rd grade teacher, 1st Jan., 1907; prin. teacher, 3rd Nov., 1912; ast. headmast., 1st Apr., 1920; headmast., 1st Apr., 1922; insp., schls., 1st Apr., 1923.

SPOONER, MAJOR JOHN CHARLES GARTH, M.C.—B. 1883; ed. Marlborough Coll.; jr. ast. engnr., F.M.S. rly., 1903-06; ast. engnr., F.M.S. rly., 1906-12; dist. engnr., grade III, 1912-14; dist. engnr., grade II, 1914; on active serv., 1915-19; dist. engnr., grade I, Ipoh, Jan., 1920; sen. dist. engnr., Ipoh, Jan., 1921; ast. engnr. constrn., F.M.S. rly., Sept., 1925.

SPROULE, PERCY JULIAN, B.A., Camb.—B. 1873; barrister-at-law (Mid.Tem.); cadet, S.S., Nov., 1896; ag. dep. registr., sup. ct., Penang, Mar., 1897; passed final in Malay, Mar., 1899; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, June, 1899; dep. registr., sup. ct., Penang, Sept., 1899; ag. ast. registr. sup. ct., Penang, May, 1903; ag. 2nd mag., Sing., Sept., 1904; ag. dep. pub. pros., May, 1906; ag. sol.-gen., Penang, May-Aug., 1906; dep. pub. prosecutor, Sing., Aug., 1906; ag. solr.-gen., Penang, Mar., 1908; ag. registr., supreme ct., Penang, Dec., 1908; ag. solr.-gen., July, 1909; solr.-gen., Apr., 1911; ag. judel. comsnr., F.M.S., Jan., 1912; solr.-gen., May and Oct., 1912; ag. temp. judge, sup. ct., July-Sept., 1912, and Nov., 1912; puisne judge, Dec., 1913; pres., toddy coman., Selangor, Oct., 1916; ag. ch. judl. comsnr., F.M.S., Mar., 1920; ag. ch. just., S. Stltmts., Oct., 1920; ag. senr. puisne judge, Feb.-Apr., 1921; ag. ch. just., S.S., 16th June, 1922; ag. ch. judl. comsnr., F.M.S., June, 1924.

SRESHTA, MAURICK SALVADOR.—B. 1873; B.A., Madras; Barrister-at-law; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1896; off. astt. to gov't. agt., E. Prov., Jan., 1898; Sabaragamuwa, Dec., 1898; addl. pol. mag., Tangalla, Sept., 1900; pol. mag., Panadure, Dec., 1900; Avissawella, Dec., 1901; Galle, Sept., 1905; comsnr. of requests, Colombo, Nov., 1907; dist. judge, Jaffna, Jan., 1911; ditto, Negombo, Nov., 1915; ditto, Karunegala, Apr., 1920; ag. dist. judge, Kandy, May, 1921; ag. dist. judge, Colombo, Aug., 1921; dist. judge, Jaffna, Oct., 1921; regiar. gen., Apr., 1922; P.M.G., Feb., 1923.

STACE, WALTER TERENCE.—B.A., Trin. coll., Dublin; B. 1886; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., 29th Oct., 1910; attached to the Galle Kachcheri, 1st Dec., 1910; office astt. to gov't. agt., Galle, March, 1912; pol. mag., Chilaw, May, 1913; pol. mag., Gampola, Feb., 1914; ag. pol. mag., Kandy, May, 1915; priv. sec. to gov't. and extra astt. col. sec., Sept., 1915; ast. censor, Dec., 1916; censor, May, 1918; dist. judge, Negombo, Apr., 1920; astt. attlit. offr., Jan., 1922; ag. stltm. offr., Mar., 1926.

STALLWOOD, HERBERT ATHILL, L.R.I.B.A., M.R.San.Inst., F. Inst. of Arbitrators.—B. 1873; ch. draftsman, S. Stltmts., 12th Sept., 1906; architectural astt., 13th May, 1913; ag. astt. engnr., 11th Aug., 1916; hon. sec., priority of indents comtee., 1st Jan., 1918; ag. exec. engnr., 11th June, 1919; govt. architect, 22nd Mar., 1920.

STANFORD, COL. THE HON. SIR WALTER ERNEST MORTIMER, K.B.E. (1919), C.B. (1901), C.M.G. (1892), O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1850; mag.'s elk., Cape Col., 1863; mag., 1876; capt. of levies in Gcaleka war, 1877-8 (medal and clasp); mem. native laws and customs coman., 1880-2; comdt. in war of 1880-1 (med. and clasp); on special service to Pondoland, 1884; ch. mag. Griqualand E., 1885; negotiated treaty with Pondos, 1886; sec. nat. affairs dept., 1898; comd. forces in E. Griqualand, Boer War, 1899-1902 (desps., 2 meda. and 2 clasps); ditto and ch. mag., July; 1904; mem. of S. African native affairs coman., Sept., 1903, to Jan. 1905; holds rank of colonel in Cape colonial forces; ret., May, 1907; M.L.A. for Tembland, 1908; mem. of S. African National Convention in connection with South Africa Bill, 1909; mem. of senate, Union of S. Africa; dir., war recruiting, Union of S. Africa and comsnr. for returned soldiers, 1918-19.
STANLEY, HON. SIR ARTHUR LYULPH, K.C.M.G. (1914).—B. 1875; ed. Oxford Univ. (B.A.); called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1902; M.P. for Eddisbury div. of Cheshire, 1906-10; gov. of Victoria, 1914-20.

STANLEY, SIR HERBERT JAMES, K.C.M.G. (1924), C.M.G. (1913).—B. 1872; ed. at Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford (B.A.); priv. sec. to H.M. min. resident at Dresden and Coburg, and British vice-consul at Dresden, 1897-1902; astt. priv. sec. to first Lord of the Admiralty, 1906-1908; priv. sec. to Lord Pres. of the Coun., 1908-1910; priv. sec. to Viscount Gladstone, gov. gen. of Union of S. Africa, 1910; sec. to gov.-gen., 1913; resident comsnr., Southern and Northern Rhodesia, 1st Apr., 1915; Imperial sec. and acctnt. to high comsnr. for S. Africa, 1918; first gov. and commodr.-in-chief, N. Rhodesia, 1st Apr., 1924.

STANLEY, W. B., C.M.G. (1923), M.B.E. (1918), Barrister-at-Law, Gray's Inn, 1921.—Served with 1st Border regt. occupation of Crete, 1898; S. Africa, 1899-1900 (medal and 4 clasps); 2nd lieut., W. India regt., 1900; lieut., 1901; ag. adjut., 3rd W. India regt., Gambia expdn., 1901, (medal and clasp); ag. trav. comsnr., Gambia May and June, 1901; trav. comsnr., Gambia Aug., 1901; A.D.C. and priv. sec. to ag. gov., Gambia, Aug. to Oct., 1901; capt., 1902; passed course of survey, schl. of mil. engineering, Chatham, 1905; resig. coman., 1906; capt., 3rd Yorks regt., 1908; qualified in native language; dist. comsnr., S. Leone, 1910; prov. comsnr., May, 1920; ag. col. sec., S. Leone, Nov., 1918 to May, 1919, Oct., 1919 to Jan., 1920 and from 19th May, 1922 to 10th Oct., 1923; gov.'s dep. on several occasions.

STANTON, AMBROSE THOMAS, M.D., Toronto; M.R.C.P., Lond.; M.R.C.S., Eng.; M.C.P. and S., Ont.; D.T.M. and H., Camb.; D.P.H.—B. 1875; senr. house surg., regiar. and demonstr., Lond. Schl. of Trop. Med., 1905-07; bacteriologist, Inst. for Med. Research, F.M.S., 1908; on ap. duty in Siam, Indo-China and Netherlands E. Indies, 1915-16; dir., govt. labs., F.M.S., 1920; ch. med. adviser to S. of S., 1926; Cragge's research prize, L.S.T.M., 1911; Bisset Hawkins med., Royal Coll. of Physicians, London, 1926; author of "Etiology of Beriberi," "Malayan Culicoides," "Meloidosis," etc.
STAPLES, JAMES WEBSTER.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. clk., G.P.O., Oct., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; transfd. to C.O., Mar., 1923.
STAPLEY, FRANK R.—B. 1895; mily. serv., Sept., 1914 to Dec., 1919; temp. clk., C.O., Apr., 1920; apptd. cler. offr., 4th July, 1922.

STARK, WALTER JOHN KIRKPATRICK.—B. 1887; ed. at Robert Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen, and at Aberdeen Univ. (M.A.), 1908; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1910; ag. asst. dist. offr., Klang, Mar. to Apr., 1911; ag. asst. dist. offr., Ulu Langat, May to July, 1911; ag. asst. dist. offr., Klang, May, 1912; passed cadet, Aug., 1912; ag. 2nd asst. supt. of imgrts., Penang, Sept., 1912; ag. asst. contr. of lab., Dec., 1912; ag. dist. offr., Jelebu, Mar., 1913; offr., cls. V, Dec., 1913; asst. contr. of lab., Seremban, Apr., 1914; ag. mag., Seremban, in addn., Feb. to Mar., 1915; asst. contr. of lab., Klang, Sept., 1915; ag. asst. dist. offr., Klang, in addn., Dec., 1915 to Jan., 1916; ag. dep. contr. of lab., Sept., 1917 and June, 1918; dep. contr. of lab., Klang (offr., cls. IV.), 1st Jan., 1919; emigrn. agt., S.S. and F.M.S., Cocoonada, Sept., 1920; ag. emigrn. agt., S.S. and F.M.S., Madras, 9th to 24th Oct., 1920; supt. emigrn. depot, Negapatam, 31st Mar., 1922; mag., Ipoh, 28th July, 1922; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1922; 1st mag., Kuala Lumpur, Mar., 1924.
STARNES, CORINTH.—B. 1864; served in N.W. rebellion, 1886, as ag. adjutant, 65th Montreal; Inspr., R.N.W.M. Pol., 1st Mar., 1886; adjutant, 1891-97; supt., 1909; asst. comsnr., 1st Dec., 1919.

STATEN, OSCAR WILLIAM—Asst. res. mag., Heidelberg, Transvaal, 1902; ag. res. mag. and res. mag., Barberston, 1902-11; res. mag., Heidelberg, 1911-16; insprg. mag., 1916-21; ch. pub. serv. inspr., 1921; sec., pub. wks., Jan., 1925.
STATHAM, HON. SIR CHARLES ERNEST, KT. BACH. (1926).—Mem., N.Z. parl., since 1915; speaker since 1923.

STEAD, KINGSLY WILLANS.—B. 1883; ed. at King Edward's, Birmingham; apptd., after open compet. exam., asst. in impl. cust., Cardiff, Mar., 1903; Harwich, Oct., 1906; asst. collr. of cust., Larnaca, Cyprus, dep. harbrmr. and tide surveyor, Apr., 1908; collr. of cust. and excise, Apr., 1910; passed prelim. exam. in modern Greek, June, 1910; registr. of trade marks, June, 1911; passed lower standard exam. in modern Greek, June, 1912; asst. cable censor, Aug., 1914-Apr., 1919; cable censor, May-July, 1919; pub. cust. of enemy property, 1st Jan., 1919 to 31st Mar., 1920; refugee comsnr., Larnaca, May, 1919 to Apr., 1920; registr. of patents, Aug., 1920; ch. collr. of cust. and excise, 1st Oct., 1921; inspr. of fisheries, 1921; Médaille de la Reconnaissance Française, 3rd cls., 1922; ag. comsnr., Larnaca for various periods in 1922, 1923 and 1924; registr. of companies, July, 1922; ch. supt., Port of Larnaca, June, 1922; registr. of companies, July, 1922; registr. of Cyprus ships, Jan., 1923; registr. of Br. ships, Jan., 1923; dir., cust. and trade, Palestine, 7th July, 1924; ag. registr.-gen., 16th Aug. to 19th Dee., 1924.

STRAD, ROBERT J. C.—B. 1880; asst. gen. publicity agt., colonization dept., C.P.R., 1913; gen. publicity agt., colonization dept., C.P.R., 1917; dir. publicity, dept. of imgrts. and colonization, Ottawa, 1919; has written several books.

STEDMAN, HUGH JOHN HARRY, M.I.C.E.—Ed. at Weymouth Coll. and trained at Royal Naval Depot, Portland; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., E. Africa Prot., 1905; ag. exec. engrnr., Kisumu, Mombasa, 1907; in charge, Thika rly. survey and constrn., 1910-11; ag. exec. engrnr., Nairobi and Naivasha, 1912-16; joined to 3rd batt., K.A.R., 1915 ("1914-15" Star, Br. War and Victory meda.); exec. engrnr., P.W.D., 1917; D.P.W., Zanzibar, 1925.
STEED, L. F.—B. 1897; on mily. serv., Sept., 1914 to Mar., 1919; temp. clk., O.S.O., Feb., 1920, apptd. after compet. exam., cler., offr., O.S.O., July, 1922.

STEEL, HENRY WILLIAM.—B. 1875; ed. Grenada Gram. Schl. and Dulwich Coll.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 26th Apr., 1899; admitted Grenada bar, 7th July, 1899; mag., dist. F., Dominica, 16th Jan., 1916; ag. mag. and registr.-gen., dist. E., Dominica, for various periods, 1917-20; ag. crown atty. and mem. of exec. and legis. coun., St. Christopher and Nevis, Oct. to Dec., 1918; ag. crown atty. and mem. of the exec. and legis. coun., Dominica, Feb.-Mar., 1917, and July-Oct., 1920.

STEBEL-MAITLAND, SIR A. H. D. R., BART. (crest, 1917).—B. 1876; ed. Rugby (scholar and exhibitioner); Balliol Coll., Oxford (classical scholar); Eldon scholar, 1899; 1st cls. class. mods., 1897; 1st cls. class. schls., 1899; 1st cls. final law schls., 1900; sec., junr. treasur. and pres., Oxford Univ. Soc'y; special comsnr. to royal comsn. on Poor Laws, 1906-7; M.P. (C.) East Birmingham, since 1910; chrmn. of Unionist party organisation, 1911; partly. under-sec. of state for the colonies, 31st May, 1915; ditto, 11th Dec., 1916; additional under-sec., F.O., and additional party-sec., B. of T., in charge of the work of the dept. of overseas trade, 1917-1919; min. of lab., 7th Nov., 1924.

STEPHENS, MAJOR FRANCIS TRANT, O.B.E., M.C.—Served in S. African war, 1900-02; joined B.S.A. pol., Rhodesia, 1903; ocmdsm., 1907; served with Rhodesian column in S.W. Africa and E. Africa, 1914-16; seconded to 1st K.A.R. for service in E. Africa, 1916-20; 4 times ment. in despr.; ch. ocmdsm. pol., and ch. inspr. of prisons, Nyasaland Prot., July, 1920.

STEPHENS, JOHN EDWARD ROBERT.—B. 1869; ed. St. Olave's Schl., York and Royal Univ. of Ireland; called to the bar, Middle Temp., 1894; mag., H.B.M. ct., Zanzibar, 4th Jan., 1912, and mag. of the ct. of H.H. the Sultan of Zanzibar, 2nd Feb., 1912; ag. asst. judge, 30th Aug., 1915 to 20th Apr., 1916 and from 26th Feb. to 5th Aug., 1918; 3rd puisne judge, Kenya, Feb., 1925; editor-in-ch. of "The Manual of Naval Law and Court-martial procedure" (4th edn.); asst editor of 11th edtn. of "Addison on Contracts"; author of works on "Demurrage," "Freight," "Charter parties," etc.; contributor to the Encyclopaedia of the Laws of England, Journal of Comparative Legislation, etc.

STEPHENSON, SIR ALBERT EDWARD, K.C.M.G. (1921); C.M.G. (1914).—B. 1864; clk. E. and A. dept., Feb., 1884; local auditor, Lagos, Aug., 1888, also ag. local auditor, G. Coast, Dec., 1888, to Aug., 1889; returned to E. and A. dept., and apptd. to col. audit branch, Jan., 1891; clk. in charge of accts., May, 1893; asst. supt., Aug., 1897; apptd. sec. clk. on amalgamation of col. audit branch with E. and A. dept., 1st July, 1906; dir. of col. audit, C.O., 1910; mem. of financial investigation comittee, Malta, 1912; major (ret.), 4th V.B. East Surrey Regt.; V.D. (1910).

STEPHENSON, Lieut.-Col. Arthur C.M.G., (1918), D.S.O. (1917), M.C. (1916).—B. 1881; served S.A. War (Queen's med., 3 clasps); S. Rhodesia civ. serv., 1902-04; N.R. civ. serv., 1904-12; served in Great War, 1915-19; (desp.); lieut.-col., Reg. Army R. of O. (R. Scots), 1918; commdt., N.R. Volunteer Forces, Aug., 1919; ch. intell. offr., N.R., June, 1920; ag. commdt., N.R.P., Oct., 1924; commdt., April, 1925; J.P.

STEPHENSON, John Everard.—B. 1893; ed. at Winchester Coll. and Oxford; passed intermediate exam. in Laws, London Univ., 1911; scholar, New Coll., Oxford, 1912; 1st cls., class mods., 1914; called to the bar, 1923; on military service, 28th Aug., 1914, to 28th Feb., 1919; B.A. (war), 1919; tempy. 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 12th Sept., 1919; apptd. under re-construction scheme, 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 12th Jan., 1920; asst. prin., 1st Apr., 1920; asst. priv. sec. to S. of S., 18th June, 1920; ag. prin., 1st Mar., 1923.

STEVENS, George Alexander, B.A.—B. 1901; ed. Bancroft's Sch., Woodford, Essex and Queen's Coll., Oxford; foundation schol. in mod. hist., 1919; 2nd cls. hon., mod. hist.; B.A., 1923; asst. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1925.

STEVENS, William Oswald.—B. 1891; B.A., Oxon; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1914; attached to Jaffna Kachcheri, Dec., 1914; addl. pol. mag., Jaffna, in addition to his own duties, May, 1915; attached to Batticaloa Kachcheri, Oct., 1915; ag. office asst. to govt. agt., S., Prov., Nov., 1916; pol. mag., Matale, Aug., 1918; ditto, Avisawella, Jan., 1923; ditto, Kandy, Mar., 1923; ditto, Colombo, Feb., 1925.

STEVENS, Cecil Young, M.I.C.E., A.M.I.Mech.E., F.B.Met.Soc.—B. 1886; ed. Queen's Coll., Belfast; lieut., R.E., 1910; asst. engr., P.W.D., E. Africa, Jan., 1913; served with B.E.F., France, Aug., 1914 to Sept., 1916; Indian Ex. Force and Aden Field Force, 1917-19; "Mons Star," ment in desps.; exec. engr., German E. Africa, July, 1919; ag. dep. D.P.W. from Oct., 1923.

STEVENSON, Sir Malcolm, K.C.M.G. (1923), C.M.G. (1920).—B. 1878; ed. Trin. coll., Dublin (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1901; off. asst. to govt. agt., Ratnapura, Mar., 1902; Badulla, May, 1904; addl. asst. to govt. agt., N. Prov., and addl. dist. judge, &c., Mannar in connection with the Pearl Fishery, Feb., 1906; asst. govt. agt., Kegalla, Apr., 1906; Mannar, Aug., 1908; asst. land attlit. offr., Feb., 1910; 2nd asst. col. sec., May, 1911; attached to col. sec.'s office for special duties, Oct., 1911; temporarily employed in C.O., Dec., 1912; priv. sec. to gov., Ceylon, Oct., 1913; asst. govt. agt., Matale, Aug., 1914; prin. asst. col. sec. and clk. to exec. coun., Sept., 1915; ch. sec. to govt., Cyprus, 14th May, 1917; admtd. govt., Nov., 1918 to 24th Aug., 1920; high comsnr., Cyprus, 25th Aug., 1920; title changed to gov., May, 1926.

STEWARD, Thomas Alfred Frank.—B. 1880; ed. privately; served in S. African war with Army Ser. Corps.; ch. clk. in staff office for payment of mil. receipts, O.R.C., 1902-1903; regis. of war claims, Kronstad and Heilbron districts, O.R.C., 1903-1904; clk., Swaziland adminstn., July, 1905; principal clk., Apr., 1910; also regis. and master of the special ct. of Swaziland and sheriff of Swaziland, Apr., 1907; passed Cape Univ. civ. ser. lower law exam., 1906; ag. asst. comsnr., Peak dist., 1916-18; asst. comsnr., Ubombo dist., 1919.
STEWART, Augustus George, M.D., F.R.C.S. (Edin.).—Med. supt., Durban hosp., 1922; dir., govt. cottage hosps., Natal, 1922.

STEWART, Charles A.—Elec. to Alberta legis., 1909-21; min. of munici. affrs., 1913; min. of pub. wks., 1913; premier of Alberta and min. of rlya. and telephones, 1917; ag. min. of immigr. and colonization, 1921-23; min. of int. and mines and supt.-gen. of Indian affrs. in Dominion govt., 1921 and 1926.

STEWART, Douglas Roy.—B. 1886; ed. Gordon's coll., Aberdeen; clk., native dept., Fiji, Apr., 1905; cadet, July, 1906; ag. oh. clk., native dept., Jan.-Dec., 1906; ag. 1st clk., col. sec.'s office, June, 1909; passed cadet, Dec., 1909; stip. mag. of colony and ag. stip. mag., Nadroga and Colo West, and gov'r. s comsnr., Colo West, May, 1910; ag. asst. col. sec. on several occasions, 1910-1915; 1st cls. clk., col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1911; ag. clk. to exec. and legis. couns., Aug., 1911 and July, 1913; priv. sec. to admstr., June, 1912; 2nd asst. col. sec., Jan., 1912; mem., bd. of examms., Fijian language, Dec., 1913; asst. native comsnr. and gov'r. s comsnr., Rewa Prov., May, 1914; ag. asst. col. sec., Aug., 1915; asst. sec. for native affairs, Jan., 1916; ag. asst. col. sec., Nov., 1916; 2nd lieut., Fiji defence force, 21st July, 1917; hon. A.D.C. to ag. gov., June, 1918; 1st lieut., Fiji defence force, Aug., 1918; ag. dist. comsnr., Rewa and prov. comsnr., Rewa and Naitasari, 1918; asst. col. sec., 1917; ag. sec. for native affairs, mem. of leg. coun., bd. of health, etc., 1920; prin. asst. col. sec. and editor, native newspaper "Na Mata," May, 1921; ag. col. sec. and mem., exec. and legis. couns., May, 1920 and Apr., 1921; ag. col. sec., etc., 2nd Sept., 1922 and 18th Jan., 1924 to Apr., 1926; gov.'s dep., June and Sept., 1924, Jan., May and Aug., 1925; dep. comsnr., W. Pacific H.C. and ag. asst. high comsnr. and gov't. dep., Sept.-Oct., 1925; gov't. dep., Jan., 1926; col. sec., Barbados, Aug., 1926; mem. exec. and leg. couns.

STEWART, Capt. John Livingstone, M.C., B.Sc., M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1894; enlisted Scottish Horse, 1914; 2nd lieut., Argyll and Sutherland Highlanders; served with B.E.F., France, wounded, M.C., Br. War and Victory med.; ment. in desps.; vety. offr., Gold Coast, 27th Dec., 1923.
STEWART, Neil, M.M.—B. 1893; Royal Highlanders (Black Watch), 1915-19 (M.M.); asst. supt., pol., Kenya, Sept., 1919.
STEWART, Hon. Percy Gerald.—B. 1885; el. mem., H. of R., Commonwealth of Australia for Wimmers, Dec., 1919; min. of pub. works and rlya. C. of A., 1923-24.

STEWART, Maj.-Gen. Sir Robert Macgregor, K.C.B. (1902).—B. 1842; served in Hazara campaign, 1888; Afghan war, 1878-9; Soudan, 1885; A.D.C. to Queen Victoria, 1887-97; late commdr., R.A., south dist., Portsmouth; gov. of Bermuda, 1904-07.

STEWART, Robt. Peter.—Exhbtrnr., Queen's Coll., B. Guiana; ed. at St. John's Coll., Camb.; ag. ast. mast., Queen's Coll., B. Guiana, 1892-93 and 1893-94; clk., immigr. dept., 1894; seconded to govt. secretariat, 1897-98, and 1900-02; passed govt. exam. in Hindustani, July, 1903; immigr. agt., Dec., 1906; ag. sen. immigrtn. agt., 1908, 1909, and 1913; senr. immigrtn. agt., Feb., 1914; mem. bd. of examms. in Hindi, etc., 1914; ag. immigrtn. agt.-gen., June, 1916.

STEWART, Hon. William Downie, LL.B.—B. 1878; ed. Otago High Schl. and Otago Univ., N.Z.; ent. N.Z. parkt., 1914; served in France with N.Z. Rifle Brigade; min. of internal affrs., 1921 to 1923; min. of cust., 1921; min. of industries and commerce, 1923-26; atty.-gen., Jan.-May, 1926; min. of finance and min., stamp duties, 1926.
STEYN, J. R.—B. 1876; prin. C.N.O. schl., Lastron, O.F.S., 1904-5; vice-prin., 1905-20; prin., 1920-22; inspr. of schls., O.F.S., 1st Apr., 1922.

STIEBEL, HERBERT CECIL, O.B.E. (1922).—B. 1876; Boer War, 1899-1902 (King's and Queen's meds.); Transvaal civ. serv., as sub-native comsnr., 1902; prot. of natives, 1907-16; E. Africa campaign, 1916; polit. dept., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1916; dist. polit. offr., 1917; 1st grade administrative offr., 1st Apr., 1920.

STIGAND, ALLMAR GORDON.—Ed. Rugby; 2nd clk. to res. comsnr., for Bech. Prot. at Mafeking, 1898; 1st clk. and regtar. to res. comsnr., 1899; served in town guard during siege of Mafeking, Oct., 1899 to May, 1900; clk. of ct. and clk. to asst. comsnr., Gaberones, 1902; J.P., Bech. Prot., 1903; ag. asst. comsnr., Jan. to Feb., 1904; 4th Dec., 1905 to 28th Feb., 1906, and 11th May, 1906 to 29th Aug., 1906; asst. res. mag. for Southern dist., Bechuanaland Prot., 15th Jan., 1907; passed Cape civ. ser. lower law exam., 1909; res. mag., N'gamiland, 1910; hon. inspr., Bech. Prot. pol., 1914; awarded "1914-15" star for services as milly. intell. offr., German S.W. Africa Campaign; lieut. R.W. Kent Regt., 1916; served B.E.F., France; attd.; intell. I.E.F., Italy, Oct., 1917; ment. in desps.; temp. capt., Jan., 1919; mil. control offr., Italy, to Nov., 1919; capt., R.W. Kent Regt. R. of O., Mar., 1923; awarded Murchison grant by R.G.S., May, 1923 for surveys and map of N'gamiland; res. mag., Kweneng dist., Jan., 1924.
STIRLING, SIR JOHN LANCELOT, K.C.M.G. (1909); KT. BACH. (1902), O.B.E. (1918), B.A., LL.B.—B. 1849; mem. legis. coun., S. Aust., 1891; chief sec., 1899; pres., legis. coun., since 1901.
STIRLING, JOHN WIGHTMAN.—B. 1885; med. offr., Basutoland, 1912.
STOBART, HENRY.—B. 1902; ed. St. Andrews, Eastbourne, Shrewsbury Schl. and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; B.A. in chem., I. agr. I and economics; supt., educn., Tanganyika Territory, 1924.

STOCKDALE, FRANK ARTHUR, C.B.E. (1925), M.A. (Cantab.), F.L.S.—B. 1883; ed. Wisbech and Magdalene Coll. Camb.; Holmes exhibnr., Mag. Coll., Camb., 1901; B.A. (1st cls. Nat. Sc. Trip.) 1904; M.A. 1911; mycologist and lecturer in agric. science, Impl. dept. of agric. for the West Indies, May, 1905; on special service to Trinidad in connection with diseases of coconuts, 1906; scient. sec. to West Indian agricultural conference, 1907 and 1908; asst. dir., dept. of science and agric., and gort. botanist, B. Guiana, Aug., 1908; dep. chmn. of bd. of agric., B. Guiana, 1909; mem. of agric. sch. comsnr., 1909; mem. of banana comsnr., 1910; on special service to Dutch Guiana in connection with banana industry, 1910; sec. of tobacco comte., 1911; comsnr. for Br. Guiana at Int. rubber exhibn., London, 1911; dir. of agric., Mauritius, 1912; vice-president, bd. of agric., 1913; mem. of coun. of govt., 1913; regtar., co-operative credit societies, July to Oct., 1913; mem. of bd. of directors, Mauritius Institute, 1914; mem. of irrigation comte., 1914; visited Rodrigues, 1914; regtar., co-operative credit societies, dir. of agric., Ceylon, 1916; del. to Imp. Entomological Conf., London, 1920; author of several rept. and articles relating to trop. agr.; M.L.C.

STOCKS, ARTHUR HUDSON.—B. 1889; ed. Heath Gram. Schl., Halifax and Oriel Coll., Oxford; B.A. (Oxon.) war service, E. Africs., 1917-18; passed progress exam. in Mende; asst. dist. comsnr., Sierra Leone, 29th Apr., 1914; priv. secy. to ag. gov., 1916-17; dist. comsnr., 21st Aug., 1922.

STONE, ARNOLD ALFRED PRICE DUNBAR, F.S.I., A.M.I.C.E., M.I.M. & Cy. E., A.M.I. Mech.E.—B. 1877; eng. draughtsman, Sierra Leone, 1912; ag. asst. dir., pub. wks., Nov., 1916 to June, 1919; ag. col. eng., Gambia, Jan., 1920; dist. eng., Dec., 1920; ag. D.P.W., May to Oct., 1922 and May to Oct., 1924; asst. D.P.W., 1923; D.P.W., Cyprus, 1925; mem., leg. coun., 1925.
STONE, ROBERT GEORGE.—Asst. paymaster, 1st King's African rifles, E. Africa Prot., 1st June, 1906; asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., Apr., 1911; dist. comsnr., Apr., 1914.

STONE, THOMAS.—B. 1876; sub-dist. commandant, S. African constab., 1901-8; King's S. African medal with five clasps; King's police medal, 1906; asst. comsnr. of police, N. Nigeria, 1st Oct., 1908; inspr., prisons, S. Provs., 18th Oct., 1922; ag. dir., prisons, 25th May, 1923.

STONEHAVEN, 1ST BARON (cr. 1925), Rt. Hon. Sir John Lawrence Baird, Bart., P.C. (1922), G.C.M.G. (1925), C.M.G. 1904, D.S.O. (1916), J.P., D.L.—B. 1874; ed. Eton and Christ Church, Oxford; ent. diplomatic service, Vienna, 1896; Cairo, 1898; Abyssinia, 1899; pvt. sec. to Sir U. Garstin, under-sec., pub. wks., Egypt, 1900-02; ag. agt. and cons. gen., Abyssinia, 1902; polit. offr., Br. E. Africa and Abyssinia frontier survey, 1902-3; polit. offr., Abyssinian army in Somaliland, 1903-4 (med. and clasp, desps., C.M.G.); 2nd sec., Paris, 1904-6; Buenos Aires, 1906-8; served as intelligence offr., European war, 1914-16 (desps., D.S.O.); M.P., Rugby, 1910-22; Ayr Burghs, 1922-25; partly mem., Air Bd., 1916-18; partly under sec., R.A.F., 1918; partly under sec., H.O., 1919-22; min. of transport, 1922-24; 1st comsnr. of wks., 1924; gov. gen. and commdr.-in-ch., Commonwealth of Australia, 10th Aug., 1925.

STONOR, OSWALD FRANCIS GERARD, C.M.G. (1925).—B. 1872; ed. Oscott and Stonyhurst; junr. offr., Selangor civ. ser., 17th Nov., 1890; ag. priv. sec. to gov., S. Stltlnb., Nov., 1890, to Jan., 1891; held various posts in Selangor and Perak; asst. dist. offr., Ulu Langat, 6th May, 1896; mag. Kuala Lumpur, 26th May, 1899; ag. dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, 5th July, 1900; ag. treas., Selangor, 23rd Sept., 1901; ag. registr. of titles, 14th Mar., 1902; dist. offr., Ulu Langat, 10th Mar., 1904; ag. registr. of titles, 1st Jan., 1907; dist. offr., Batang Padang, 25th Mar., 1909; ag. sec. to res., Selangor, 25th Apr., 1912; sec. to res., Selangor, 1st Jan., 1914; ag. British res., Selangor (in addition), from 8th June, 1919; under sec., F.M.S., 24th July, 1919; ag. comsnr. of trade and cust., F.M.S., 7th Mar., 1921; ag. Br. res., Perak, 25th Apr., 1921; ag. Br. res., Selangor, 3rd Nov., 1921; Br. res., Selangor, 13th Dec., 1921; ag. ch. sec., govt. F.M.S. in addn., 21st Dec., 1921 to 10th Jan., 1922; ditto, Dec., 1924 to Jan., 1925; ag. Br. res., Perak, Dec., 1925; Br. res., Perak, Feb., 1926.

STORM, WILLIAM THORBURN.—B. 1881; Imp. post office, 1896-97; asst., Cape of Good Hope P.O., June, 1901; survr.'s staff, N.diat., Dec., 1901; engnr. in ch.'s staff, Nov., 1902; postmtr., Feb., 1904; postmtr., N.W. Rhodesia, 1909-10; prov. postmtr., S. Nigeria, Aug., 1912; survr., Nigeria, Jan., 1914; divnl. survr., 1916; senr. survr., Jan., 1920; ag. dep., P.M.G., various occasions between Jan., 1918 and July 1920; P.M.G., Tanganyika Territory, 5th Oct., 1920; mem., legis. coun., July, 1926.

STORRES, SIR RONALD, KT. BACH. (1924), C.M.G. (1916), C.B.E. (Mil.) (1919).—B. 1881; ed. Temple Grove, Charterhouse and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; classical scholarship, Charterhouse and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; 1st cls. class, tripod, 1903; ent. min. of finance, Egypt, 1904; occupied posts in secretariat, finance, dept. of mines (sec.), cust. admin. and audit dept., Egypt, 1909; served with Sir E. Gorst, Lord Kitchener, Sir H. MacMahon and Sir R. Wingate; asst. polit. offr. to Anglo-French polit. mission, E.E.F., 1917; liaison offr. for the mission in Baghdad and Mesopotamia (desp.); secretariat of war cabinet, autumn, 1917; mem., Comité, Conservation des Monuments Arabes, Cairo, also comtee., Palestine exploration fund; mily. gov. of Jerusalem, 1917-20; civ. gov., Jerusalem, Jaffa, Judaea and Southern Palestine; founder, Pro-Jerusalem Soc'y., Jerusalem schl. of music, Jerusalem chamber of commerce; chmn., Palestine local govt. comsm., chmn., Palestine bd. of higher studies, vice-pres., Palestine Oriental Soc'y.; Knight of Grace of the Order of St. John of Jerusalem; comdr. of the Crown of Italy and St. Saviour of Greece; apptd. gov. and comdr.-in-ch., Cyprus, 16th Sept., 1926; assumed duty, 30th Nov., 1926.

STOUT, THE RT. HON. SIR ROBERT, P.C. (1921), K.C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1844; admitted a barrister of sup. ct., N. Zealand; entered the prov. coun. of Otago, N.Z., 1872; prov. solr., 1873; elected to the gen. assem., 1876; atty.-gen., Mar., 1878, to June, 1879; min. of lands, etc., for imigr., 1878; pres. of Dunedin Freethought Soc.; prime min. and atty.-gen., and min. for educn., 1884-87; fellow and ex-chancellor of N. Z. Univ.; chief justice of N.Z., 1899-1926; mem. of coun. of Victoria Coll.; M.L.C., 1926.

STOUTE, CYRIL EUSTACE.—B. 1882; clk., dept. of agr., Barbados, Nov., 1898; after various promotions, apptd. 1st grade clk., and oh. clk., C.S.O., and clk., leg. coun., 9th Feb., 1925; ag. col. sec., 14th July to 24th Aug., 1926.

STRACHAN, JOHN.—B. 1877; ch. engr., lake development scheme, Colombo, Ceylon, Aug., 1911; ast. D.P.W., F.M.S., Dec., 1920; ag. D.P.W., F.M.S., Jan. to Sept., 1921; ast. D.P.W., F.M.S., Oct., 1921; ag. D.P.W., F.M.S., Jan., 1923; D.P.W., Ceylon, Aug., 1923; gen. man., rly., F.M.S., 1926.
STRACHAN, PETRR DONALD, M.D.—B. 1872; med. offr., Bechuanaland Prot., 1921-23; supt., Basutoland leper asylum, 1923.

STRACHEY, SIR CHARLES, K.C.M.G. (1926), C.B. (1920).—B. 1862; apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cl. jun. clk., F.O., 2nd Apr., 1885; 1st cl. jun. clk., 1st May, 1885; sec. Uganda rly. comtee., 10th Sept., 1895; 1st cl. clk., C.O., 26th Nov., 1898; prin. clk., 1st Jan., 1907; delegate on Anglo-German Boundary Conf. (Yola-Chad), Mar., 1906; ditto, Anglo-French (Niger-Chad), May, 1906; ditto, Anglo-German (Yola to sea), Oct., 1909; mem., W. African lands comtee., 1912; plenipotentiary at Brussels Internat. Liquor Conf., 1912; travelled in Nigeria, 1914; mem., Br. delegation, Paris Peace Conf., Jan. to Sept., 1919; Br. rep. on internat. comtees, for preparation of conventions on (1) African liquor traffic, (2) arms traffic, (3) revision of Berlin and Brussels Acts, signed at St. Germain, 10th Sept., 1919; ast. sec., 1st Apr., 1920; ast. under sec. of state, 12th Dec., 1924.

STRADBROKE, (3rd EARL OF) (cr. 1821). GEORGE EDWARD JOHN MOWBRAY ROUS, BARON ROUS (cr. 1796), VISCOUNT DUNWICH (cr. 1821), C.B. (1904), C.V.O., A.D.C., D.L., J.P.—B. 1862; ed. Harrow and Cambridge; col., 1st Norfolk Royal Garrison Arty. Volunteers, 1888-1908; col., 3rd (howitzer) brig., E. Anglian divn., R.F.A., 1908-17; col., 272nd brig., R.F.A., Jan. to Nov., 1917; gov., Victoria, 1920-26.
STRAKER, MAJOR G. H., M.C., B.A. (Oxon).—Ast. dist. comsmr., Kenya, Oct., 1921.

STRATFORD, JAMES.—B. 1869; ed. St. Aidan's Coll., Grahamstown, Cape Colony and Exeter Coll., Oxford; M.A., B.C.L., Oxon; mem., Inner Temple; called to the bar, Eng'and, 1898; Transvaal, 1902; practised, Johannesburg; K.C., 1912; judge, sup. ct., Transvaal prov. divn., 1st Mar., 1921.

STRATHAIRN, DONALD BEITH.—B. 1884; ed. Edinburgh Univ.; passed law agent's exam.; ast. dist. comsmr., Gold Coast, 1912; dist. comsmr., 1918; senr. ast. col. sec., 1921; olk. of couns., 1922; ag. ch. ast. col. sec., for various periods, 1922-25; ag. sec. for native affrs., 1924.

STRATHAIRN, GEORGE CECIL, M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.). D.P.H., certif. from London S.T.M.—Med. offr., Orange River Colony Burgher Camps, 1901-03; med. offr., E. Africa and Uganda Prot., 1903; senr. med. offr., Uganda, 1914; war serv. with rank of major, 1914-18; ch. med. offr., Fiji, 1920; mem., leg. couns., Fiji, 1920-22; senr. sany. med. offr., Jamaica, 1922.
STREET, PHILIP WHISTLER.—B. 1863; puisne judge, N.S. Wales; judge in bankruptcy and probate jurisdictions, Feb., 1907; ch. just., 1925.

STRICKLAND, SIR GERALD, COUNT DELLA CATENA, G.C.M.G. (1913), K.C.M.G. (1897), C.M.G. (1889).—B. 1861; ed. Oscott Coll., Birmingham; lieut. C.U.R.V., and elected mem. coun. of govt., Malta, 1886; B.A. and LL.B., Trin. Coll., Camb., honours law tripod; attended col. conf., 1887, on behalf of Malta; called to the bar, Inn Temp., and pres. cholera comm., Malta, 1887; thanked by govt. for obtaining from Leo XIII. recognition of Imp. veto of appts. to See of Malta; unoffic. mem., ex. coun., and ag. ast. sec. to govt., 1888; vice-pres., bd. of health, pres., comtee. of privileges, ch. sec. to govt., and major, Royal Malta militia, which he established, 1889; re-organized Malta rly., 1891; planned breakwater, 1894; pres., coun. of Malta univ., 1900; chmn., mil. and civ. drainage bd.; mem. comtee. on Malta naval reserves; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Leeward Is., 1902; estab. central factories for sugar and cotton in Antigua, 1903; gov., Tasmania, 23rd July, 1904; read scientific papers as pres. of Royal Society, 1904; gov., W. Australia, 6th Apr., 1909; mem. of W. Aust. Inst. of engrs.; govr., New South Wales, 25th Nov., 1912; assumed govt., 14th Mar., 1913; ex-officio gov. of Norfolk Is., 1913-14; codified the laws of Norfolk Is., and negotiated the transfer of the dependency to the Commonwealth; hon. col., 86th W. Australia Infantry Regt.; mem. of Inst. of local govt. engrs., N.S. Wales; held dormant comsn. to administer C. of A. in absence of gov.-gen.; retired, 1917.
STRINGER, HON. THOMAS WALTER.—B. 1855; ed. high schl., Christchurch, New Zealand; barrister and solr.; K.C., 1908; judge of the sup. ct., New Zealand, 1914.

STRONG, ARTHUR NESBITT.—B. 1890; M.A., Edin.; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., 13th Dec., 1913; office aast. to govt. agt., Cent. Prov., Jan., 1914; addtl. pol. mag., Kandy, in addition to his own duties, Jan., 1914; ag. office aast. to govt. agt., Sabaragamuwa, Nov., 1914; addtl. pol. mag., Ratnapura, in addition to his own duties, Dec., 1914; addtl. comsrr. of requests, Ratnapura, in addition to his own duties, May, 1915; on military duty, 1917; pol. mag., Kurunegala, Nov., 1919; landing survr., Colombo cust., Nov., 1920; dep. food contr. in addn., July, 1921; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, Feb., 1924; ast. govt. agt., Matare, Apr., 1925.
STRONG, ERNEST EDWARD.—B. 1879; stock inspr., Basutoland, 1912; sub.-inspr., Basutoland mounted pol., 1915.

STRONGE, HERBERT CECIL.—B. 1875; ed., Falmouth Sch., and Trin Coll., Dublin; prizes classics and English literature; B.A.; called to the bar, King's Inn, Dublin, 1900; joined N.E. circuit, 1901; stip. mag., Bahamas, 1911; ag. atty.-gen., 2nd July to 4th Aug., 1914, 3rd Sept. to 3rd Nov., 1914, and from May to Sept., 1915; also acted as chief just. in Jan., 1915; chief just. Tonga, 1917; recd. thanks of Tongan gov't. for services in 1918 epidemic; seconded for sp. duty as pres. of comsrr., in Fiji, 1921; ch. just., Leeward Is., 1925.

STUART, HON. CHARLES ALLAN, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1884; ed. High schl., Strathroy, Ontario, and Toronto Univ.; called to the bar, Ontario, 1896; called to the bar, N.W.T., 1898; elect. to first prov. legis., Alberta, 1905; judge of supreme ct., N.W.T., 1906; judge of supreme ct., Alberta, 1907; chancellor of univ. of Alberta, 1908.

STUART, REV. CYRIL EDGAR, M.A.—B. 1892; ed. Dulwich Coll., Repton and S. John's Coll. and Ridley Hall, Cambridge; B.A., 1914; M.A., 1920; mily. serv., 1914-19, Salonica, 1916-19; deacon, 1920; priest, 1921; curate, St. Mary's, Hemdy Rise, 1920; chaplain and lect., Ridley Hall, Cambridge, 1920-24; chaplain and librarian, Achimota, Gold Coast, 1926.

STUART, WALTER LESLIE.—3rd associate to judges, sup. ct., and clk. of arraigns, S. Australia, 1898; called to the bar, S. Australia, 1900; 2nd associate and clk. of arraigns, Nov., 1905; judges' assoc., chief clk. of sup. ct., and clk. of arraigns, Feb., 1908; also a solr. to lands titles office, 1911; master of sup. ct., regis. of probates, regis. in admiralty, regis. of companies, trades unions and industrial and provident societies, and dist. regis. of high ct. of Australia, Jan., 1913, after acting in these offices from 1911.

STUBBS, Sir REGINALD EDWARD, K.C.M.G. (1919), C.M.G. (1914).—B. 1876; ed. at Radley and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford (exhibitioner); 1st cl. classical mods., 1897; 1st cl. lit. hum., 1899; B.A., 1899; M.A. (1920); apptd., after compet. exam., 2nd cl. clk., C.O., Jan., 1900; ag. 1st cls. clk., May, 1907; 1st cls. clk., Dec., 1910; visited S. Sttlmts., F.M.S. and Hong Kong to inquire into question of revision of civ. ser. salaries, 1910-11; mem. of West African lands comtee., 1912; col. sec., Ceylon, Jan., 1913; offr. administering the govt., Jan. to Oct., 1913; Dec., 1915 to Apr., 1916, and Mar. to Sept., 1918; gov't., Hong-Kong, 16th June, 1919; assumed govt., 30th Sept., 1919; Order of the Rising Sun of Japan, 1st cls., 1921; hon. LL.D., Hong Kong, 1926; hon. fellow, Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford, 1926; capt.-gen. and gov. in ch., Jamaica, 1926; joint editor of C.O. List, 1910-1912; edited "Lucas' Historical Geography of the British Colonies," vol. i., 2nd edition, 1906.
STUDDEN, REGINALD ROBERT.—A.I.A.; pub. actuary, S. Australia, 1914; under treas., 1923.

STUPART, SIR ROBERT FREDERIC, KT. B.A.H. (1914).—B. 1857; ed., priv. schl.; Upper Can. Coll.; ent. Can. meteorological serv., Nov., 1872; for some years was sen. inspr. and probability offr., Toronto observ.; in charge of ch. station, Hudson's Straits, 1884-86; supt. and dir., meteorological serv. of Dom. and of magnetic observ., 28th Dec., 1894; fellow, Royal Soc. Can., 1901; pres., Royal Astron. Soc., Can., 1902-03; pres., Royal Can. Inst., 1906-07; mem., internat. meteorological comtee., 1907 and 1919; pres., St. George's Soc., Toronto, 1919-20.
STURGESS, GEORGE WILLIAM.—B. 1871; M.R.C.V.S.; govt. vet. surgeon, Ceylon, Mar., 1896.

STURROCK, ALFRED JOHN.—B. 1879; ed. at Geo. Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ., M.A. 1902 (hons. class.); cadet, F.M.S., 1902; ag. 2nd asst. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, 1st May, 1903; passed cadet, 26th Oct., 1904; ag. asst. dist. offr., Batang Padang, 17th Feb., 1906; ag. dist. offr., Temerloh, 9th Nov., 1907; ag. asst. dist. offr., Gopeng, 14th Mar., 1910; seconded for service in Kelantan, 23rd Apr., 1910; judge, high ct., and ag. supt. of oust., 13th May, 1910; dist. offr., Batu Mengekabang, 28th Dec., 1910; dist. offr., Ulu Kelantan, 1st Jan., 1913; supernmy., mag., Ipoh, 5th Mar., 1915; dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, 19th May, 1915; asst. dist. offr., Batu Gejah, 28th Oct., 1915; supernmy. offr., cls. II., 14th Nov., 1919; dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, 1st July, 1921; chmn., sany. bd., Kinta, 6th July, 1922 and Dec., 1924; ag. fed. examr. in Malay, Apr., 1922; chmn., rubber restric., comtee., supermy., mag., in Malay, Nov., 1922; offg. chmn., sany. bd., Kinta, Dec., 1924; fed. examr. in Malay, Nov., 1925.

STURROCK, GEORGE, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1880; ed. Daniel Stewart's Coll. and Herist Watt Coll., Edin.; asst. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Aug., 1907; ex. engrnr., grade III., Jan., 1913; ex. engrnr., Kuala Pilah Jan., 1914; ditto, Jelebu, in addn., 1915; ditto, Klang, Aug., 1920; sen. ex. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Aug., 1923; ag. state engrnr., Negri Sembilan, July, 1923.

STURROCK, JOHN CHRISTIAN RAMSAY, M.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1875; ed. Charterhouse and Balliol Coll., Oxford; tutor to H.H. the Kabaka of Buganda, Mar., 1905; dist. comsrr., Uganda Prot., Aug., 1914; prov. comsrr., June, 1921; ag. ch. sec., June, 1924; res. comsrr., Basutoland, 1926.

STUTCHBURY, SAMUEL.—B. 1882; supt. of mails, G.P.O., Singapore, Nov., 1909, and served in different depts. of the P.O.; ag. senr. supt. of mails, Singapore, Feb., 1913; supt. of mails, P.O., Penang, Jan., 1916; senr. supt. of mails, Juily, 1918; ag. supt., money order branch and savings bank, Jan., 1918; supt., grade I., Jan., 1919; ag. acnt., G.P.O., May, 1919; supt., money order branch and savings bank, Singapore, but contd. to act as acnt., Singapore, 1st Jan., 1920; ag. asst. P.M.G., 13th May, 1920; acnt., G.P.O., but contd. to act as ast. P.M.G., 12th Sept., 1920; ag. P.M.G., 1st Apr., 1921; ag. ast. P.M.G., 26th July, 1921; resumed duties as acnt., Singapore, 1st Nov., 1921.

SUDSBURY, EDWIN RONALD, M.A. (Oxon.).—B. 1879; on mily. serv., Jan., 1916; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Aug., 1921; attd. to Kurunegala Kach., Sept., 1921; attd., Nuwara Eliya kach., Nov., 1921; extra office asst. to govt. agt., C. Prov., Jan., 1925; pty. sec. and A.D.C. to O.A.G., Apr., 1926; ag. archl. comsrr., Jan., 1926.

SUFFERN, T. H., M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O. (R.U.I.), Cert. of London, S.T.M.—Apptd. to W.A.M.S., S. Nigeria, Jan., 1909; served in Cameroons E.F., Oct., 1914 to Feb., 1915; meet. in desps., May, 1916; tempy. capt. (general list), Oct., 1914 to July, 1915; senr. med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Aug., 1920.

SULLIVAN, CHARLES ERIC EUGENE, B.A. (Oxford).—Asst. dist. commnr., Uganda Port., 7th Dec., 1909; temp. capt., intell. dept., E. African Forces, 1916; ag. ist. asst. sec., Uganda, Feb., 1918; ag. asst. chief sec., July, 1918; ag. regis. gen. of births, marriages and deaths, Sep., 1918; dist. comsnr., Jan., 1919; senr. asst. sec. and clk. to exec. and leg. couns., Apr., 1925; ag. prov. comsnr., 1926; ag. dep. ch. sec., Aug., 1926.
SULLIVAN-TAILOUR, EDWARD ROBERT.—B. 1886; supt., pol., Kenya, June, 1923.

SUMMERS, WALTER LLOYD.—B. 1870; entd. crown lands off., S. Aust., July, 1887; clerk, agric. off., May, 1892; chief clk., July, 1902; ag. sec. to min. of agric., Feb., 1910; sec. to ditto, July, 1911; also sec. to min. of industry and sec. to min. of marine, 1915.
SUMNER, JAMES A. W.—B. 1903; ent. L.P.S. (Inland Rev.), May, 1919; apptd. after compet. exam. cler. off., London telephone serv., Apr., 1921; transfd., C.O., Oct., 1923.
SUMNER, WILSON GEORGE HAROLD.—B. 1887; ed. at Chigwell Schl.; Colonial Fellow of Surveyors' Inst., 1916; asst. dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, 18th Aug., 1914.

SURRIDGE, BREWSTER JOSEPH.—B. 1894; ed. Felsted Coll., and Downing Coll., Cambridge; B.A.; served, N. Lance regt., 1914-17; asst. sec. ch. secs. office, Cyprus, 16th Mar., 1918; passed exam. in mod. Greek, Nov., 1918; ag. ch. asst. sec., Jan. to Apr., 1919; local comdt., police, Apr., 1919; ag. comsnr., Larnaca, June, 1919; ag. A.D.C., July, 1920; ag. asst. sec., Oct., 1920; passed exam. in Turkish, Aug., 1922; ag. comsnr., Aug., 1922; ag. asst. sec., Sept., 1922; ag. comsnr., Dec., 1922 to Oct., 1923; comsnr., Jan., 1924.

SURVEYER, HON. EDOUARD FABRE.—B. 1875; ed., Ste. Marie Coll., McGill and Laval Univ.; called to bar, 1896; vice-pres., Jun. Bar Assoc., 1902-03; pres., 1903-04; mem. of coun. of Quebec bar, 1904-05; lectr. law, McGill Univ. since 1906; judge, superior ct., Quebec, 1st Jan., 1920; edr. of practice repts., Queb., since 1898; also of pocket eds. of civ. code and code of civ. procedure (the latter in both languages); pres., Can. Club, Montreal, 1909-10; vice-pres., l'alliance Francaise, Montreal.
SUTCLIFFE, MAJOR A. W., D.S.O., M.C., B.Sc. (Durham).—Asst. dist. comsnr., Kenya, May, 1920.

SUTHERLAND, ALEXANDER CHARLES, M.A., M.C.E. (Melbourne Univ.), A.M.I.C.E. (Lond.).—B. 1869; asst. mine survr., govt. mining engr.'s office, Transvaal, 1902; mine survr., Transvaal, 1903; mine survr., Union of S. Africa, 1910.
SUTHERLAND-GREAME, ALEXANDER WILLIAM, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1882; dep. loco. supt., F.M.S. Riya., 1915-24; loco. supt., F.M.S. Riya., 1924.

SWABEY, COMMODORE GEORGE THOMAS CARLISLE PARKER, D.S.O. (1916), R.N., H.M.S. "Dunedin."—B. 1881; ed. H.M.S. "Britannia," Dartmouth; flag comdr. to Adml. Sir Lewis Bayly, 1913-14; served ashore with army in Aegean, 1915-16; comdr., H.M.S. "Lord Nelson," 1916-18; opera. divn., admv. naval staff, 1918-19; in comd. "H.M.S. 'Revenge,'" 1919-21; dep. dir. naval ordnance, admv., 1921-23; capt., R.N. coll., Greenwich, 1924-26; commodore comdng. N.Z. divn. and naval adviser to N.Z. govt., 1926; chevalier, Legion of Honour, 1916.

SWAYNE, COL. SIR ERIC JOHN EAGLES, K.C.M.G. (1910), C.B. (mil.) (1904); grand offr. of order of the Crown of Italy (1906).—B. 1863; ed. at naval coll., Gosport, and abroad; R.M.C., Sandhurst, 1882; entd. Welsh regt., 1883; Bengal staff corps, 1884; adjut., 1888; capt., 1894; local lieut.-col., 1900; brevet lieut.-col., 1901; brevet-col., 1903; temp. brig.-gen., 1903-1906; retired from army, 1906; served with Burma expedn., 1885-86 (medal with clasp); intell. offr., Hukong Valley reconnaissance; in command of advanced base exploration, Turong River and Sing Pho country, Nov., 1895, to Mch., 1896 (McGregor medal); exploration to Somaliland, Feb. to July, 1891 (thanked by resolution of Bombay govt., and appreciation by govt. of India); Gildessa affair on Abyssinian frontier, 1892 (commendation by resolution of Bombay govt.); engaged on delimitation of internat. frontier between French and British Somaliland, 1892; served for five years on intell. staff, India, as staff-capt. and D.A.Q.M.G.; apptd. 2nd-in-command, Uganda Rifles, on active serv. in Jubaland, E. Africa (medal with clasp); on active serv. in Uganda, 1899; selected to command Somaliland police force, 1900; raised and organised levies, and commanded expeditionary force in Somaliland, Nov., 1900; defeated and routed Mullah (ment. in desps., medal with clasp, brevet lt.-col., thanked by govt.); organised 6th Batt. Uganda Rifles, second expdn., 1901; again defeated Mullah (C.B., ment. in desps.); apptd. comand., consul-gen. and comdr.-in-chief, Somaliland Prot., and judge of its high court, Apr., 1902; on special duty in Egypt, 1904; arranged evacuation of Somaliland, and organised control of tribes; British deleg. to conference with Italian deleg. at Lyons in connection with agreement with Italy respecting Somaliland; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, British Honduras, 1906-13; spec. duty to Canada, with reference to Indian labour (thanked by govt. of India) 1908; spec. duty to Mexico, 1909.

SWEET-ESCOTT, SIR E.B., K.C.M.G. (1904), C.M.G. (1895).—B. 1857; ed. Somerseshire Coll., Bath, and at Balliol Coll., Oxon; exhibitioner, Worcea.
