Coll., Oxon, June, 1876; 2nd class classical moda., June, 1878; 3rd class in mod. hist. finals, June, 1880; B.A., July, 1880; M.A., 1911; classical prof. at the Roy. Coll., Mauritius, June, 1881; précis writer, col. sec.'s office, Feb., and 2nd asst. col. sec., Apr., 1886; ag. col. sec., 1889; ag. col. sec., Br. Honduras, Mar., 1893; admstd. the govt. of that col., Apr. to Nov., 1893; col. sec., Br. Honduras, 1894; admstd. govt., Apr. to Nov., 1895, again in 1897; ag. 1st class clk., C.O., Jan., 1898; admstr. of the Seychelles Is., Aug., 1899; assumed govt., 29th Nov., 1899; gov. and comdr.-in-chief, Oct., 1903; gov., Br. Honduras, 15th Apr., 1904; gov., Leeward Is., 30th Jan., 1906; recd. coronation medal, 1911; gov., Fiji, and high comsnr., W. Pacific, 11th March, 1912; assumed govt., 25th July, 1912; ret., 1918; rep. of Fiji at Br. Empire Exhibn., 1924.

SWETTENHAM, SIR FRANK ATHELSTANE, G.C.M.G. (1909), K.C.M.G. (1897), C.H. (1917), C.M.G. (1886).—B. 1860; cadet, S. Sttlmts., July, 1870; passed final exam. in Malay, July, 1872; asst. collr. of land rev. for Penang and Prov. Wellesley, Aug., 1872; collr. of land rev., July, 1873; J.P. and mag. and comsnr., ct. of requests, Penang, May, 1874; sent on special missions to Perak in Jan., June, and July, 1874; sent to reside with the Sultan of Selangor, Aug., 1874; asst. res., Selangor, Dec., 1874; gazetted to act temporarily as res. of Selangor, 22nd Oct., 1875; took charge of the residency in Perak on the murder of the res., Nov., 1875; dep. comsrr., Perak, Nov., 1875; mentioned in despatches; asst. col. sec. for native states, Mar., 1876; asst. col. sec., July, 1881; Br. res., Selangor, Sept., 1882; comsrr. for S. S. and Malay States, Calcutta Exhibn., 1883-84; ag. Br. res., Perak, Mar., 1884, to Jan., 1886; exec. comsrr. for S. S. and Malay States, Col. and Ind. Exhibn., 1886; Br. res., Perak, 1889; res.-gen., Malay States, Jan., 1896; admst.govt. S. S., Feb., 1901; gov., 1901; ret. 1904; chmn. of royalcomen. on Mauritius, 1909

SWETTENHAM, Sir J. Alexander K.C.M.G. (1898).—B. 1846; ed. Clare Coll., Camb.; scholarship, May, 1867; writer, Ceylon service, 1868; ag. pol. mag., Harrispattu, Feb., 1870; pol. mag., Kayta, Sept., 1870, continuing to act at Harrispattu; ag. asst. govt. agt., Galle, Dec., 1871; ag. landing and tide survr., Galle, June, 1872; asst collr. of customs, Jaffna, Oct., 1872, to continue to act at Galle; ag. dist. judge, Matara, Apr., 1873; 2nd asst. col. sec., and clk. of the legis. coun., 1876; rec.-gen., Cyprus, 1883; audr.-gen., Ceylon, 1891; col. sec., S. S., 1895; admst. govt., June to Aug., 1895, from Mar. to Dec., 1898, and from Dec., 1899, to Feb., 1901; gov., Br. Guiana, 1901; gov., Jamaica, 14th July, 1904; resign., 1907.

SWETTENHAM, Lieut.-Col. James Parry, M.S.V.R.—B. 1876; ed. Shrewsbury and Bedford Grammar Schls.; Boer War, 49th Imperial Yeomanry (Montgomeryshire) 1899-1900 (Queen's med. with 3 bars); asst. engr., Gold Coast (Kumasi) Rly., 1901; asst. engr., P.W.D., Selangor, F.M.S., 1902; exec. engr., grade II, Pahang, F.M.S., 1907; 2nd lieut., M.S.V.R., 1911; capt., M.S.V.R., 1912; hon. A.D.C. to gov., S.S. and high comsrr., F.M.S., 1912-19; exec. engr., grade I, F.M.S., 1914; seconded for milly. serv. in the Great War; asst. to chf. engr., 2nd Army Cent. Force; temp. capt., R.E., 1915; O.C., 201st Field Co., R.E.; temp. major, R.E.; invalided from R.E., 1916; "1914-15 Star," War and Victory meds.; commdt., M.S.V.R.; 1919; state engr., Negri Sembilan, 1919; asst. dir. of pub. wks., F.M.S., 1923; ag. D.P.W., F.M.S., 1923; ag. gen. man., rly., F.M.S., Feb., 1925; on sp. duty, pub. wks. dep., F.M.S., Dec., 1925.

SWETTENHAM, Roger Francis Ross.—B. 1883; ed. privately, Bedford and Aston Rowant; stintl. offr., Negri Sembilan, June, 1906; ag. asst. dist. offr., Coast, Negri Sembilan, July, 1908; ag. chmn. and sec., san. bd., Seremban, Negri Sembilan, Apr., 1909; ag. sec., san. bd., Kuala Lumpur, Selangor, Apr., 1910; supt. chandu monopoly, Selangor and Negri Sembilan, Nov., 1910; supervisor of cust., Kuala Lumpur in addn., Apr., 1911; seconded for milly. serv. (R.G.A. Cadet Schl.) Mar., 1918; 2nd lieut., R.G.A., Nov., 1918; supt., chandu monopoly, F.M.S., Jan., 1921; sec., standing advisory comtece. on use of opium in Malaya, June, 1924; dep. chmn. and sec., ditto, Nov., 1924; dep. comsrr., trade and cust. (chandu), F.M.S., May, 1925.

SWIFT, J. Arthur B.Sc., B.E., M.I.S.E.—B. 1878; ed. Univ. of Tasmania; asst. engr., P.W.D., Negri Sembilan, Apr., 1904; ag. ex. engr., grade II, asst. to D.P.W., F.M.S., June, 1911; ex. engr., grade III, Selangor, Apr., 1913; ex. engr., grade II, F.M.S., June, 1914; sen. ex. engr., P.W.D., F.M.S., Sept., 1920; ag. state engr., Selangor, Apr., 1923; ag. state engr., Pahang, Apr., 1924; ag. state engr., Negri Sembilan, Oct., 1926.

SWINBOURNE, Major Charles Augusta, O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1884; clk., audit dept., N.S.W. govt.rlys., May, 1909; lieut., Australian Rifle Regt., Sept., 1906; passed with honours in topography and range-finding, Sydney, 1906; clk., pay and qtrmr., with rank of sub-insp., Fiji constab., 6th Oct., 1909; capt., Commonwealth mil. forces, 1st Jan., 1910; comdg. offr., Fiji cadet corps, 13th Feb., 1911; mem. of bd. of survey, Suva, 13th Feb., 1911; supervising offr., Fiji rifle assoc., 16th Apr., 1911; qualified rifle course, Hythe, May, 1914; qualified (distinguished) machine gun course, Hythe, June, 1914; attached 1st Loyal North Lancs. Regt., Mar. June, 1914; accompanied Fiji 1st contingent to Europe as comdg. offr., 1st Jan., 1915; J.P., Suva, 13th Sept., 1916; comdt. of Fiji Defence Forces with rank of major, 30th Sept., 1916; A.D.C. to gov., 6th Oct., 1916; mentioned by Army Council for services in connection with defence of Fiji; on active service, July, 1917; tempy. capt., Loyal North Lancs. Regt., 30th Aug., 1917; regmtl. musketry offr., 3rd Batt., L.N.L.Reg., 25th Jan., 1918.

SWINDELL, Rev. Frank Guthrie, M.A. (Oxon.).—Chap., Selangor, May, 1902; col. chap., Malacca, June, 1906; ag. col. chap., Singapore, Apr., 1907, to Jan., 1908; ag. col. chap., Penang, 20th Dec., 1912; col. chap., Singapore, 1st Nov., 1914; registr., diocese of Singapore, 13th Feb., 1915; archdeacon of Singapore, 2nd Feb., 1916; commissary, diocese of Singapore, 29th Sept., 1918; resigned registr. of diocese, 3rd Feb., 1920; dep. registr., July, 1921.

SYDENHAM OF COMBE, Lord (1st Baron, 1913, U.K.).—Sir George Sydenham Clarke, R.E., G.C.M.G. (1906), G.C.S.L. (1911), G.C.I.E. (1907), K.C.M.G. (1893), C.M.G. (1887).—B. 1848; entered R.E., 1868; capt., 1880; major, 1887; served in Egypt, 1882 (medal and star); in Suakin expedn., 1885, mentioned in despatches (clasp); sec. col. defence comtece., 1880-92; supt. of carriage factory, Woolwich, 1893; gov. Victoria, 1901-1904; apptd. as one of a comtece. to advise as to the creation of a Board for the administrative business of the War Office, Nov., 1903; sec. to Imperial Defence comtece., 1904; gov. of Bombay, 1907-1913.

SYDNEY, Lord Archbishop of Mont Rev. John Charles Wright, B.A., M.A., D.D.—Formerly curate of Kilworth-Beauchamp, 1886-1888; Bradford, 1888-1893; lect., 1889-1893; vicar of Ulverston, 1893-1896; St. George's, Leeds, 1896-1904; can. res. of Manchester Cathedral, 1904-1909; rector of St. George's, Hulme, Manchester, 1904-1909; chaplain to Bp. of Manchester, 1904-1909; archdeacon of Manchester, 1909; consecrated Lord Archbishop of Sydney, 24th Aug., 1909; Primate of Australia, 1910; author of "Thoughts on Modern Church Life and Work."

SYERS, Cecil George Lewis.—B. 1903; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Oxford; classical schol., Balliol Coll., 1921-25; 1st cls. classical mods., 1923; 1st cls. lit. hum., 1925; apptd. after compet. exam., asst. prin., Dominions Office, 21st Oct., 1925.

SYKES, George Roynvald.—Cadet, S. Stltms., Nov., 1913; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, July, 1914; attached censor's office, Singapore; passed final exam. in Chinese, Dec., 1916; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Apr., 1917; asst. regir. imports and exports (war trade dept.) Singapore, June, 1917; ag. asst. prot. of Chinese (in addition) Mar., 1918; ceased to perform duties in war trade dept., ag. asst. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Sept., 1918; ag. 3rd mag., Singapore, in addn., Dec., 1919; ag. 2nd asst. prot. of Chinese, Singapore, Jan., 1920; supernum. offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1920; ag. asst. supt., gov't, monopolies and asst. prot. of Chinese, Malacca, June, 1921.

SYMES, Lieut.-Col. George Stewart, C.M.G., D.S.O.—B. 1882; ed. Malvern Coll. and Sandhurst; ent. Army, 1900; serv. S. African War (Queen's med., 2 clasps); Aden, 1903-04 (despa., D.S.O.); attd., Egyptian Army, 1906 and Sudan govt., 1907; Blue Nile expdn., 1908 (meds.); promoted major, 1915 and brevet lt.-col., 1917; A.D.C. to Sirdar and gov-gen. of the Sudan, 1906-07; asst. dir. of intelligence, 1908-12; pte. sec. and sec., gov-gen.'s coun. (Sudan), 1913-16; European War (3 times ment. in despa., medals, brevet lt.-col.); attd., staff of high comsnr., Egypt, 1917-19; attd., miny. of int., Egypt, 1919-20; dist. gov't., Palestine, Sept., 1920; ch. sec., 1925.

SYMON, Sir Josiah Henry, K.C.M.G. (1901), K.C.—B. 1846; mem. of H. of Assem., S. Australia, 1881-1887; atty.-gen., 1881; Q.C. 1881; representative to Aus. fed. conven., 1897-8; and chmn. of its judiciary comte.; pres. of fed. league of S. Aust.; pres. of Commonwealth league; senr. for S. Aust., Commonwealth parl., 1901-1913; atty.-gen., C. of A., 1904-5; has ret. from politics.
SYMONETTE, W. J.—B. 1878; pub. schl. teacher, 18th Apr., 1898; 4th divn. comsnr., 11th June, 1921; ag. ch. clk.; recvr. gen. in treas.'dept., 6th Nov., 1922; confirmed in appt., 4th Feb., 1924.

SYMONS, Claude Trevine.—B. 1879; B.A., Oxon., F.R.G.S.; vice-principal, govt. training coll., Ceylon, Aug., 1908; asst. govt. analyst, June, 1912; ag. govt. analyst, in addn., Dee., 1916; govt. analyst, Sept., 1919; ag. regis., med. coll. in addn., May to Dec., 1922.

TACHE, Joseph de Labouquerre.—B. 1858; ed. St. Hyacinthe Sem. and Laval Univ.; notary public, 1881; priv. sec. to Hon. J. A. Chapleau, premier of Quebec, 1880-82; priv. sec. to lieut.-gov. Angers, 1887-92; proprietor and editor of "Le Courrier," St. Hyacinthe, 1902-14; mem. of prov. coun. of agric., 1890-91; unsuccessful candidate for H. of C., bye-elec. and g.e., 1904; apptd. King's printer of Canada, Mar., 1914; jt. librn. of parl., June, 1920.

TAGART, Edward Samuel Bourn.—B. 1877; ed. Bath Coll. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge, (B.A.); barrister-at-law; asst. collr., N.E. Rhodesia, 11th Jan., 1901; asst. native comsnr., 1st Apr., 1901; native comsnr., 1st Sept., 1902; ag. mag., May, 1903; asst. mag., 15th May, 1905; has acted as mag. on various occasions between Aug., 1905 and Mar., 1913; asst. legal adviser, 1st Apr., 1913 to 16th June, 1915; mag., 18th June, 1915; ag. judge, high ct., 31st Mar to 23rd June, 1922; sec. for native affrs., 10th Apr., 1924; mem., exec. and leg. couns.; ag. ch. sec., 1st June to 22nd Nov., 1925; J.P.

TAINSH, Lieut.-Col. Joseph Ramsay, C.B.E. (1924), V.D.—Offr., loco. dept., Indian rly., 1895-1917; 2nd lieut., Volunteer Force, India, 1898; major, 1915; major, Indian Defence Force, 1916; capt. (temp. major), I.A.R.O., 2nd Apr., 1917; ch. mech. engr., Basra Light Rly., 3rd June, 1917; ag. asst. dir., works, Iraq, 4th July, 1917 to 13th Aug., 1917; asst. dir. of works, in charge of Basra Light Rly., 27th Sept., 1917; trans. to Rly. branch, R.E., 4th Oct., 1917; lieut.-col., 15th June, 1918; loco. supt., Basra divn. of rly., Jan., 1919; dep. dir. rly., Iraq, May, 1919; dep. loco. supt. combined system of rlys., Baghdad, June, 1919; demob. with rank of lieut.-col., 3rd Sept., 1919; loco. supt., combined system, Nov., 1919; ag. dir. rly., 11th Dec., 1921.

TAIT, Hugh Nimmo.—B. 1888; ed. at Clifton and St. John's Coll., Camb.; 1st cls. Math. Tripos, Parts I. and II.; 1st cls. Nat. Science Tripos, Part I.; apptd. after compet. exam., clk., chief sec.'s office, Ireland, Oct., 1911 2nd cls. clk., C.O., Mar., 1912; ag. 1st class clk., 20th July, 1917; on mil. service from 27th Sept. to 9th Dec., 1918; sec. to Rhodesian Comsn., 1919-20; prin., 1st Apr., 1920.

TAITT, Alfred.—Supernumerary clk., Trinidad, Mar., 1889; extra clk., savings bank dept., 1st Sept., 1890; 3rd clk., royal gaol off., 1st Jan., 1891; 3rd clk., educn. off., Apr., 1891; 3rd asst. clk. of the peace, Port-of-Spain, 1st June, 1891; 4th clk., col. sec.'s off., 12th July, 1892; 3rd clk. col. sec.'s off., 16th Aug., 1897; confld. and 2nd clk., col. sec.'s off., Feb., 1898; ag. ch. clk., col. sec.'s off., and clk. to leg. coun., Nov., 1898; June, 1897, to Nov., 1898; and Nov., 1900; ag. asst. col. sec., July to Oct., 1906; sec. to the col. coun.; priv. sec. to Govr. Sir F. Napier Broome; sec. to the municipal inquiry comsn., 1896; clerical asst. to coman. apptd. to inquire into admn. of just. in Trinidad; sec. to industrial educn. coman.; priv. sec. to Sir H. E. H. Jerningham; asst. priv. sec. to Sir A. Moloney; priv. sec. to Mr. Hugh Clifford; priv. sec. to Sir H. M. Jackson; priv. sec. to Sir G. T. Carter; priv. sec. to Mr. S. W. Knaggs; ag. asst. col. sec., Mar. to May, 1908; ag. chf. clk., May to Dec., 1908; ag. asst. col. sec., June to Oct., 1909; warden of Couva and Chaguanas, 1st Aug., 1918; warden, Caroni, Jan., 1919; warden, County of St. George, 1st Nov., 1925.

TAITT, Horace Virgat.—B. 1887; ed. at Harrison Coll., Barbados; Island scholar, 1906; 3rd cls. class B.A., 1909; M.A., 1917; educn. offr., educn. dept., B. Guiana, 1914; ag. 2nd asst. inspr. of schools, Oct., 1914, to Apr., 1915; June-Sept., 1915; July-Oct., 1916; ag. 3rd cls. clk., educn. off., Jan.-Apr., 1917; ag. classical master, Queen's Coll., Apr.-July, 1917.

TALBOT, Maj.-Gen. Hon. Sir Reginald Arthur James.—K.C.B. (1902); C.B. (1885); b. 1841; ed. Harrow; formerly A.D.C. to Queen Victoria; M.P. for Stafford, 1869-74; serv. in Zulu War, 1879; Egyptian Campaign, 1882; Nile expdn., 1884-85; lt.-col. com. 1st Life Guards, 1886-88; mil. attache, Paris, 1889-95; oomg. cavly. brig., Aldershot, 1896-99; comd. army of occupation, Egypt, 1899-1903; gov. of Victoria, Feb., 1904, to July, 1908.

TALMA, Edwy L.—B.A. Christ's Coll., Camb., 14th Wrangler, math. tripos, 1895; cadet, S. S., Nov., 1896; ag. asst. prot. immigts., Penang, Aug., 1897, to Mar., 1898; and from Oct., 1898; confirmed Jan., 1901; passed final exam. in Tamil, Feb., 1889; ag. asst. P.M.G., Penang, Apr., 1902; ag. 3rd mag., Penang, May, 1903; ag. 2nd ditto, Nov., 1903; ag. asst. supt. of Indian immigts., in addition to other duties, Nov., 1903; dep. regist., sup. ct., Singapore, July, 1904; also asst. supt. of Indian immigts., Singapore, June, 1907; ag. regist., sup. ct., Singapore, Oct. to Dec., 1908; promoted to Cla. III., Aug., 1911; passed final exam., coun. of legal ed. (cert. of honour, Inner Temple), Trinity, 1912; ag. 2nd mag., July, 1912; ag. dist. judge and 1st mag., May, 1913; ag. regist., sup. ct., Mar., 1914; ag. temp., dist. judge, Feb., 1915; ag. dist. judge, and 1st mag., Apr., 1915; ag. dist. judge, Dec., 1915; ag. 2nd mag., Feb., 1916; offr., cl. II., Mar., 1916; ag. collr., land rev., Mar., 1916; on sp. duty, Somerset House, May-Aug., 1917; temp. attached, war tax office, Penang, Oct., 1917; collr., war tax, Nov., 1917; collr., gen. war tax, Feb., 1918; ag. treas., May, 1920; collr., gen., income tax, Singapore, 20th Nov., 1920; dep. treas. 'A,' 31st Jan., 1922.

TANNER, COL. (Temp. Brig.-Gen.) WILLIAM ERNEST COLLINS, C.B. (1919), C.M.G. (1916), D.S.O. (1918).—B. 1875; lieut., Natal Carbineers, 1899; ent. Natal civ. serv., 1899; served S.A. War, 1899-1902 (Queen's med., 4 clasps, King's med., 2 clasps); lieut., dist. adjt., Natal Militia perm't. staff, 1903; capt., 1903; dist. offr., ditto, 1904; sec., Natal defence comte., 1905; served Natal native rebellion, 1906; med. with clasp; staff offr., hdqrs., Natal Militia, 1907; major, ditto, 1910; grad., Staff Coll., Camberley, 1910; major, Perm't. Forco (staff), Union Defence Forces, 1912; temp. lt.-col., 1914; brev. lt.-col., Perm't. Force (staff), 1915; int. col. comdg. 2nd S.A. Inf'y., Union Imp. Serv. Cont. (Overseas), 1915; brig. comdr. (temp. brig.-gen.), 8th Inf. Bde., 1917; bde.-comdr., S.A. Inf. Bde., 1918; ret. with rank of brig.-gen. in Imp. Army, 1919; served in Great War, 1914-18, G.S.W. Africa, 1914-15, Egypt, 1916, and France, 1916-18; wounded, Delville Wood; ment. in despatches six times; "1914-15" Star, Br. War Med., Victory Med., Croix d'Offr., Ordre de Leopold and Croix de Guerre (Belgium), Croix de Chevalier, Legion d'Honneur (France); dist. staff offr., No. 1 mily. dist., and O.C. base, Capetown, 1919; ag. adjt.-gen., defence hdqrs., 1919; substantive lt.-col. (staff offr., 1st grade), Perm't. Force (staff), 1919; adjt.-gen. and temp. col., Nov., 1919; ag. comdt., S.A. Mil. Coll. and O.C., troops, Roberts Heights, 1920; O.C., Cape Peninsula garrison, 1921; substantive col., 1921; temp. brig.-gen., 1921; dist. staff offr., No. 1 mily. dist. (Cape), in addn. to other duties, 1921; posted S.A. Staff Corps, 1923; adjt.-gen., defence hdqrs., 1925.

TAPLIN, CAPT. B. B. M.—B. 1883; asst. dist. commr., G. Coast, 31st July, 1909; staff offr., H.Q.C.L.C., 1917; Capt., Chinese labour batt., 1917; staff capt., Weihaiwei, 1917-18; capt., Chinese labour batt., 1918-19; dist. commr., Gold Coast, 23rd Feb., 1911; resumed duty, 1919.
TATE, FRANK, C.M.G. (1919), I.S.O. (1903).—B. 1863; dir. of educ., Victoria, Apr., 1902.

TATHAM, HON. FREDERIC SPENCE, D.S.O., V.D., K.C., Col., S. African Defence Force; hon. major, British Army.—B. 1865; ed. Bishop's Coll., Natal; deputy speaker, Natal parlmt., and chmn. ways and means, 1897-1907; called to the bar, 1886; served Rasuto war, 1881; Boer war, 1899-1902; European war, 1915-1918 (five times ment. in despatches, D.S.O.); judge of supreme ct. of S. Africa (Natal Prov. Divn.), 1918.

TAUPHY, RAJA SAID, M.B.E. (1926), THE RAJA KECHIL TENGAH OF PERAK, F.M.S.—B. 1879; ed. St. Croix Sch., Richmond and Raffles Inst., Singapore; attmnt. offr., 1900; ag. asst. dist. offr., Ulu Langat, 1909; ag. asst. dist. offr., Ulu Langat, 1910; padi inspr., Krian, 1911; Malay mag., Lenggong, 1914; ag. asst. dist. offr., Krian, 1916; offr., sl. V, M.C.S., 1916; ag. dist. offr., Krian, 1919; offr., sl. IV, 1920; apptd. the Raja Kechil Tengah of Perak, 1921; in charge, Malay artisans, B.E.E. and offr. guide, Malaya Pavilion, Wembley, 1924; ag. chmn., sany. bd., Larut and Matang, 1924; offr., sl. III, 1925; dist. offr., Krian, 1926; chmn., sany. bd., Larut and Matang, 1926.

TAYLOR, EDWARD ARCHIBALD THORNTON, B.A.—B. 1885; ed. Dover and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge (schol.); B.A., 1907 (2nd cls. class. tripes); asst. dist. commr., Gold Coast, Mar., 1913; dist. commr., Oct., 1918; senr. asst. col. sec., 1921; ag. ch. asst. col. sec. and ag. clk. of couns. for various periods, 1922-26; ag. clk. for native affrs., 1925; sec., bd. of examrs., native languages, 1st Apr., 1926.

TAYLOR, EDWIN.—B. 1881; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to C.O., Jan., 1901; served with I.Y. in S. African war, 1901-02 (medal and four clasps); re-apptd. to 2nd div., and assigned to B. of T., 1902; clk., P.W.D., B.C. Africa Prot., July, 1906; treasy. asst., Uganda, Jan., 1909; asst. treas., civil adminstr., German East Africa (occupied territory), Jan., 1917; treas., Seychelles, May, 1919; dep. treas., Uganda, Apr., 1923; ag. treas., May 1923 to 1924; senr. asst. treas., Sierra Leone, Apr., 1925; ag. treas., Apr.-Aug., 1925.

TAYLOR, EPHRAIM GODMAN.—B. 1877; ed. Wesleyan (Boys) High Schl., Freetown; passed Coll. of Preceptors exam., 2nd cls., 1893; civ. serv. exam., 1895; tempy. outdoor offr., cust., 20th Nov., 1894; 2nd cls. offr., 6th Mar., 1896; compet. exam. in acctng., May, 1902; 2nd cls., acctng. branch, treasy., 19th May, 1902; 1st clk., 1st Dec., 1903; 2nd grade clk., 1st Jan., 1913; 1st grade, 12th Oct., 1913; ch. clk., 1st Jan., 1915; ag. senr. asst. col. treas., May to Sep., 1919; staff supt., treasy., 1st Jan., 1921; African asst. treas., 1st Jan., 1923.

TAYLOR, EVAN NUTTALL.—B. 1898; ent. R.M.A., 1916; 2nd lieut., R.F.A., 1918, France; R. of O., 1920; cadet, F.M.S., 1921; attd., dist. office, Raub, June, 1921; attd., legal adviser's office, Kuala Lumpur, Sept., 1921; addnl. mag., Kuala Lumpur, Feb., 1922; attd., land office, Kuala Lumpur, Mar., 1922; supervisor, census, Perak, Apr., 1922; ag. asst. dist. offr., Tanjong Malim, Dec., 1922; passed cadet, Jan., 1923; offr. cls. V., May, 1924; ag. dist. offr., Batang Padang, May, 1924; asst. dist. offr., Tanjong Malim, July, 1924; asst. dist. offr., Bombai, Dec., 1925.
TAYLOR, FRANCIS BRYANT OLUDELE.—Ed. at C.M.S. Collegiate schl., Lagos; 3rd cls. certif., Lond. Coll. of Preceptors, 1903; 2nd cls. clk., audit dept., Lagos, Feb., 1907.

TAYLOR, FRANK WILLIAM, M.A.—B. 1887; ed. at Newbury Grammar School and Oxford. Non-Coll.; 3rd cls. hous. mod. hist., 1910; bd. of educ. certif. exam.; certif. in geogr., 1911; asst. mast., Magdalen Coll. School, Brackley, 1911-14; jun. supt. of educ., N.P. Nigeria, 17th June, 1914; seconded polit. dept., 1st June to 30th Nov., 1919; regraded supt., educ., 1920; late capt., 4th Batt. Oxf. and Bucks. Lt. Infy. T.F.; served in the Northern Cameroons, Garua and Banyo, 1915-16, and attached to the 1st Training Centre, W.A.S.B., 1917-18; publications, "A Fulani Grammar" (1921), "Two Fulani Readers" (1922), "A Practical Hausa Grammar" (1923), "A Fulani-Hausa Phrase Book" (1926), "Empire Reading Charts" (1926), "A Fulani-Hausa Vocabulary" (1927), "Fulani Hausa Readings in the Native Scripts" (1927), "Genesis in Fulani" (1927), contributions to the African Soc., the Philological Soc., and "West Africa."

TAYLOR, HON. GEORGE EDWARD.—B. 1878; ed. London and Toronto; called to the bar, Ont., 1902; practised in London and Moosejaw, Sask.; K.C., 1st Jan., 1913; pres., Sask. Bar Assoc., 1917; just., ct. of King's Bench, Saak., 1st Mar., 1918.

TAYLOR, GEORGE WILSON.—B. 1866; ed. in Toronto; apptd. to excise branch, inland revenue dept., 1883; engaged in spec. organization work, Quebec, 1898-1909; asst. sec., inland rev. dept., 1909; sec., ditto, 1913; asst. deputy min. and sec., 1914; asst. comsrr. of customs and asst. deputy minister of inland rev., Oct., 1919.
TAYLOR, GODFREY JOHN CAMERON.—B. 1902; ed. St. Andrew's Sch., Eastbourne, Rugby Sch., and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; B.A. (hons.), 1923; A.D.C., Zanzibar, 1926.
TAYLOR, GREVILLE POWER.—B. 1899; ed. Elstow Sch., Bedford, and H.M.S. "Worcester"; asst. dist. comsrr., Sierra Leone, 4th Mar., 1925.

TAYLOR, J. A., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), L.M. (Dub.), D.T.M. (L'pool).—Med. offr., Uganda, 8th Oct., 1908; temp. capt., U.M.S., Oct., 1914 to Nov., 1917; asst. med. supt., cent. asylum, F.M.S., 1920; S.M.O., Uganda, 3rd Oct., 1920; ag. D.P.M.O., Uganda, 22nd Mar., 1921 to 1st July, 1921 and 10th July, 1922 to 4th Mar., 1923; D.M.S.S., Zanzibar, 11th Mar., 1923.

TAYLOR, JOHN, O.B.E. (1918).—B. 1863; entd. Royal Navy, 1883; served in Soudan war, 1884-5 (medal and Khedive's bronze star); impl. prison serv., Lond., Dec., 1887, to June, 1897; ch. warder, Belize priz., R. Hond., July, 1897; keeper of prisons, 1898; offl. recr. in bank, and offl. adminstr., 23rd Oct., 1908 to 22nd May, 1909; ag. dist. comsrr. on numerous occasions, 1906 to 1914; dist. comsrr., Toledo dist., 18th May, 1914; J.P. for the colony, 1908.

TAYLOR, ROBERT STANLEY, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), M.A., M.D., B.C., D.P.H. (Cantab.), Cert., S.T.M.—B. 1875; ed. Downing Coll., Cambridge; exhibr., Charing Cross Hosp., London; D.M.O., Cue, West Australia; M.O.H., Cue and M.O.H., Day Dawn, West Australia; M.O., Uganda, 5th Sept., 1914; active serv., E.A. campaign and temp. capt., U.M.S., Oct., 1914 to Nov., 1916; S.M.O., Somaliland, 24th May, 1922; P.M.O., Somaliland, July, 1925.

TAYLOR, ROBERT WALTER, O.B.E. (1919).—B. 1883; ed. Emmanuel Schol. and King's Coll., London; clk. in crown agt's. office, 1903-1906; head acctnt., Somaliland, 1906; ag. mil. paymstr., Aug., 1907, to Jan., 1908; Somaliland expdn., 1908-1910 (G.A.S. medal and clasp); asst. treas., Uganda, 1910; asst. treas., Somaliland, 6th Mar., 1914; treas., Somaliland, 11th July, 1915; dep. treas., Tanganyika Territory, 13th Feb., 1920; ag. treas., Dec., 1921 to May, 1922; treas., Tanganyika Territory, 18th May, 1922.
TAYLOR, S. F., Labour inspection offr., E. Africa Prot., Apr., 1919.

TAYLOR, SIR W. T., K.O.M.G. (1905), C.M.G. (1896).—B. 1848; collr. of customs and excise, Larnaca, 1879; ch. collr. of customs, Cyprus, 1882; ag. recvr.-gen., Nov., 1883, to Feb., 1884; ag. comsrr., Larnaca, in addition to his own duties, 1883 and 1885; ag. recvr.-gen. in addition to duties as ch. collr. of cust., Jan. to Nov., 1887; recvr.-gen. and ch. collr. of cust. and excise, 1891; audr.-gen., Ceylon, May, 1895; ag. col. sec., Ceylon, Oct., 1896, to Feb., 1896, Mar. to Dec., 1896, Mar. to Nov., 1899, and Apr., 1900, to Nov., 1901; col. sec., S. Settnata, June, 1901; admntd. gov't. of S. S., Oct., 1903, to Apr., 1904, and Feb. to June, 1906; ag. recr.-gen., F.M.S., Sept., 1904; confirmed, Jan., 1905; ret., 1910; in charge of F.M.S. development agency, London, Oct., 1910; mem., W. African lands comtee., 1912.
TELFER, ELIZABETH WILSON.—B. 1895; headmistress, govt. girls' schl., Accra, Gold Coast, 1st Oct., 1924.

TEMPANY, HAROLD AUGUSTIN, D.Sc. (Lond.), F.I.C., F.C.S.—B. 1881; ed. privately and Univ. Coll., London; asst. gov't. chemist, Leeward Is.; 1903; ag. gov't. chemist and supt. of agric., July to Dec., 1906, and Jan. to Nov., 1909, gov't. chemist and supt. of agric., Nov., 1909; J.P., Antigua, 1910; offl. mem. legis. coun., Antigua, Mar. to July, 1912; represented Leeward Is. at 3rd Internat. Congress of Trop. Agric., June, 1914, and at rubber and tropical products exhibtn., June-July, 1914; chmn. of trustees, Antigua pub. library, 1916; mem., Antigua Defence Reserve, 1905 to 1916; dir. of agric., Mauritius, 1917; nominated M.L.C., Mauritius, May, 1917; mem., bd. of dirs., Mauritius Inst., Jan., 1918; reported on S. African sugar industry, 1917; organised gov't. tobacco factory, 1920; sugar experiment station, 1921; agr. coll., 1923-24; dairy, 1923; prin., Mauritius Agr. Coll. in addn. to substantive appt., 1924; reported on local sugar industry, 1926; organising vice-pres., Mauritius agr. and indus. exhibtn., 1926; chmn., various comtees; author of numerous papers and repts. on agrl. chem., cane sugar production, agrl. educn. and trop. agr.

TENISON, EDWARD HERON RYAN.—B. 1898; on mly. serv., Apr., 1917; cadet, Ceylon, Nov., 1921; attd., Kandy Kach., Dec., 1921; office asst., Anuradhapura kach., Feb., 1924; ditto, Badulla kach., June, 1925; ditto, Batticaloa kach., Nov., 1926.

TENNANT, MATTHEW PETTIGREW, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1881; ed. Montrose Academy; ch. asst. engrnr., Jamaica gov't. rly., 1905; ag. loco. supt., ditto, July, 1909 to Dec., 1910; ag. ch. engrnr., Mar.-Nov., 1913; senr. inspgr. engrnr., P.W.D., Jamaica, Apr., 1914; ag. asst. dir., P.W.D., Dee., 1918 to Sept., 1921; ag. dir. of pub. wks., July-Oct., 1920; exec. engrnr., Uganda, 28th July, 1924.

TENNENT, JAMES HALL.—B. 1880; ed. Geo. Watson's Coll., Edin., and Edin. Univ.; M.B., Ch.B., 1911; sen. house surg., Edinburgh Royal Infirmary; house physician, Cumberland Infirmary, Carlisle; house surg., and asst. health offr., Penang and Singapore, S. Settnata, 1912-1914; seconded for service in Kedah, 1914; resumed duty in S. Settnata, 1918; ag. surg., native hosps., Singapore, and lectr. in clinical and operative surg. to the King Edward VII. med. schl.; ag. senr. surg., Penang, 1919.

TENNYSON, Rt. HON. LORD, 2ND BARON (U.K., creat. 1884), G.C.M.G. (1903), K.C.M.G. (1899). HALLAM TENNYSON, succeeded 1892 (on the death of his father), Alfred Lord Tennyson, poet laureate, 1850-92.—B. 1852; ed. Marl. Coll., Trin. Coll., Camb., and Inner Temple; J.P. for Hants; mem. of Marl. Coll. exec. coun.; prepared his father's memoir, published in 1897; gov't. S. Australia, 1899; gov't. gen., Commonwealth of Australia, 1902-1904; chmn. of comtee. for stint. of ex-service men within the Empire, 1917.

TETTBY, VICTOR ADOLPHUS, B.A. (Durham).—B. 1891; 2nd cls. teacher, Acura Training Coll., Gold Coast, 22nd Jan., 1915; ag. housemast., 10th Mar., 1919 to 8th Oct., 1919; ditto, 11th Jan., 1920; 1st cls. teacher and asst. headmast., 1st Apr., 1923; inspr., schls., 9th July, 1925.

THAINE, ROBERT NIEMANN.—B. 1875; ed. Eastbourne Coll. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge, B.A.; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1898; offl. asst. to gov't. agt. S. Prov., Apr., 1899; Cent. Prov., Aug., 1900; comsnr. of requests and pol. mag., Balapitiya, June, 1901; off. astt. to govt. agt., Ratnapura, Dec., 1901; astt. govt. agt., Kalutara, Mar., 1902; dist. judge, Badulla, May, 1902; pol. mag., Kandy, May, 1904; landing survr. cust., Colombo, Sept., 1904; astt. govt. agt., Trincomalee, June, 1906; Puttalam, Aug., 1906; dist. judge, Jaffna, July, 1908; comsnr. of requests, Colombo, Jan., 1911; astt. govt. agent, Mannar, Sept., 1911; ag. govt. agt., Province of Sabaragamawwa, Feb., 1913; pol. mag., Colombo, Nov., 1915; comsnr. of requests, Colombo, Feb., 1917; censor, 18th Apr., 1917; dep. collr. of cust., Colombo, May, 1918; ag. prin. collr. of cust., Apr., 1919; govt. agt., Prov. of Uva, June, 1920; ag. prin. collr. of cust. and food contr., Nov., 1921; govt. agt., W. Prov., Dec., 1923; ag. col. sec., 18th Oct., 1923.
THATCHER, J.—Ed. at Bristol Gram. schl.; examr. of loan acts, audit dept., O.R.C., 1st Jan., 1904; examr. of acts, ditto, 1st July, 1904; provincial auditor, O.F.S.Prov., 16th Apr., 1912.

THEODORE, HON. E. G.—B. 1884; M.L.A. for Woothakata, Queensland, Oct., 1909; M.L.A. for Chillagoe since 1912; dep. leader of the labour party, 1913; treas. and sec. for pub. wks. in Ryan miny., June, 1915; premier of Queensland, 1919-25.
THEUNISSEN, E. D.—Ast. supt. pol., Kenya, Nov., 1922.
THIBOU, CLEMENT GARRY.—Foreman of wks., P.W.D., Antigua, Mar., 1915; astt. town clk., St. Kitts, Apr., 1916; ag. supt. of pub. wks., Aug., 1916.
THOM, M. ST. C.—B. 1887; S. African constab., 1905-10; astt. supt. of police, E. Africa Prov., 1911; supt. pol., Kenya, Jan., 1921.
THOMAS, A. MUSGRAVE Barrister-at-Law.—Crown counsel, Kenya, Oct., 1920; pres., dist. ct., Cyprus, Nov., 1924; ag. judge, sup. ct., May and June, 1925 and Apr., 1926.

THOMAS, CHARLES CLIFFORD.—B. 1890; ed. Blundells; 2nd lieut., The Duke of Cornwall's Light Infy., 1909; India, 1914-15; Aden Field Force, Jan., 1915; Egyptian Expedy. Force, Jan., 1916-19; adjt., 1/4th D.C.L.I., 1917-19; ment. in despatches; Territorial Decoration; adjt., 4th D.C.L.I. (T.F.), 1919-24; pte. sec. to gov. of Seychelles and clk. to couns., 1925.

THOMAS, SAMUEL JOYCE.—Ed. King's Coll., London; Inglis schl. and early English Text Soc'y. prizeman; assoc. (with distinc.) King's Coll., 1897; B.A., Lond., 1897; called to bar, Middle Temple, 1898; enlisted, 1915; commissioned, R.A.S.C. (M.T.), and attd. to R.G.A. (145th Siege Battery), France, Flanders, Italy; invalided, 1919; ch. just., St. Vincent, Oct., 1919; ag. col. sec. and admstr., St. Vincent, Feb. to Dec., 1920, and on three subsequent occasions; 2nd prison judge, Trinidad, Mar., 1923.

THOMAS, Rt. Hon. James Henry, P.C. (1917), M.P., J.P., LL.D., Cambridge.—B. 1875; served as cleaner, fireman and driver, G.W.R.; has served in turn as organiser, astt. sec., gen. sec. and pres., Nat. Union of Ryemen; gov. of Dulwich College; mem., Balfour mission to America, 1917; M.P. for Derby since 1910; J.P., Surrey, 1920; S. of S. for Cola., 23rd Jan. to 7th Nov., 1924; author of "When Labour Rules" and other works.

THOMAS, THOMAS SHENTON WHITELEGGE, O.B.E. (1919).—Ed. at Leatherhead and Queen's Coll., Camb. (scholar); B.A., Hons. 1901; astt. dist. comsrr., E.A.P., Aug., 1909; 3rd astt. sec., Apr., 1911; sen. astt. sec., Apr., 1912; ag. astt. ch. sec., Aug.-Oct., 1913, Aug., 1916-Mar., 1917, and Apr., 1917-Jan., 1919; clk. to exec. and legis. couns., 1917-19; mem. E. Africa civ. serv. coman., 1918; astt. ch. sec., Uganda, Jan., 1919; ag. ch. sec., June, 1919 to Apr., 1920; chmn., Uganda development coman., 1919; gov. dep., Jan.-Feb., 1920; prin. astt. sec., Nigerian secretariat, Mar., 1921; ag. dep. ch. sec., Apr.-Dec., 1921; dep. ch. sec., 30th Jan., 1924; ag. ch. sec., 8th May to 11th Nov., 1925 and from 19th Aug. to 10th Nov., 1926; gov.'s dep., 20th to 30th May, 15th June to 13th July, 17th Aug. to 16th Sept., 1925 and from 31st Oct. to 10th Nov., 1926.
THOMPSON, AUBREY DENZIL FORSYTH.—Ed. Weenen County Coll., Natal and New Coll., Oxford (B.A.); lieut., R.F.A., 1917-19; cadet, Uganda Prot., 19th June, 1921; A.D.C., 18th July, 1923.
THOMPSON, C. B.—Ast. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Nov., 1911; 1st grade admstive offr., Kenya, Jan., 1924.

THOMPSON, HENRY BURFORD.—B. 1880; cashier, savings bank, Antigua, Apr., 1905; acctnt., ditto, Apr., 1906; ag. 1st treasy. offr., Montserrat, Aug.-Nov., 1906; ag. 1st clk., P.O., Antigua Dec., 1906; ag. 3rd clk., C.S.O., Mar., 1907 to Mar., 1908; 3rd outdoor offr., treasy., Jan., 1908; ag. 3rd clk., C.S.O., Jan., 1911 to Mar., 1912, and from Apr. to Sept., 1912; student, Middle Temple, 1912; cashier, treasy., May, 1914; ag. 1st clk., registr.'s office, May, 1915; comsnr. to admstr. oaths, Antigua, July, 1916.

THOMPSON, HERBERT W.—B. 1899; entd. C.O., 4th Oct., 1915; on mil. ser. from 13th Aug., 1917 to 9th Apr., 1919; apptd., after compet. exam., astt. clk., C.O., 18th June, 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; promoted to 2nd div. clk., 24th Feb., 1920; cler. offr., higher grade, 10th Oct., 1925.
THOMPSON, JOHN BAIRD, M.N.Z. Soc. C.E.—B. 1868; ed. secondary and private schls.; joined lands and survey dept., N.Z., 1891; undersec. for lands, 1922.
THOMPSON, J. H.—M.B. Bac. Surg.; offr., E.A.P., Aug., 1913.

THOMPSON, JOHN HARRISON.—B. 1882; matric., London Univ., 1912; diploma, English language and lit., London Univ., 1921; apptd. after compet. exam., astt. clk., commercial intelligence branch, B. of T., 23rd June, 1912; library attendant, C.O., 6th Apr., 1914; after further compet. exam., apptd. as clk. of the 2nd div. and assigned to C.O., 24th Aug., 1914; cler. offr., higher grade, 1st Apr., 1922; astt. librarian, 10th Oct., 1925.

THOMPSTONE, CAPT. ERIC WESTBURY, M.C.—B. 1897; ed. Shrewsbury Schl.; 2nd lieut., King's Shropshire Light Infy., 1915; served in France and Italy, 1916-19; wounded; silver med. for valour (Italy); adminis. offr., N.P., Nigeria, July, 1919.

THOMSON, FRANK DAVID, B.A., C.M.G., (1920).—B. 1877; ed. High Schl., Napier and Canterbury and Victoria Colls., N.Z.; cadet educn. dept., 1894; priv. sec. to various ministers since 1901; sec. to prime min., since 1912; clk. of exec. coun. and sec. of cabinet since 1919; perm. head, prime minister's dept., 1926; mem. of secretariat, Br. Empire delegation, Peace Confce., Paris, 1919; mem., Imp. Confce. secretariat, 1917, 1918, 1921, 1923 and 1926.
THOMSON, SIR GRAEME, K.C.B.—B. 1875; ed. Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; higher div. clk., Admiralty, 1900; astt. dir. of trans., 1914;

THOMSON, HENRY WAGSTAFFE, B.A. (Oxon.)—B. 1874; ed. Winchester and Trin. Coll., Oxford; cadet, Selangor, F.M.S., Nov., 1896; passed final exam. in Malay, Oct., 1897; passed cadet, Dec., 1898; ag. ast. dist. offr., Kuala Kubu, Feb., 1898; ag. ast. dist. offr., Serendah, May, 1898; 2nd ast. dist. offr., Kuala Kubu, Dec., 1898; ag. ast. collr. of land rev., Kuala Lumpur, May, 1899; ag. ast. dist. offr., Serendah, Apr., 1900; dist. offr., Kuantan, June, 1900; seconded for service in Kelantan, May, 1903; resigned F.M.S. serv., and in the serv. of the Kelantan govt., May, 1906 to July, 1909; offr., cls. III., F.M.S., performing duties of ast. adviser, Kelantan, July, 1909; ag. dist. offr., Klang, Jan., 1910; collr. of land rev., Kuala Lumpur, and reg. of titles, Selangor, Feb., 1914; ag. reg. of titles, Perak, Oct., 1914; ag. supt., convict estab., Taiping, in addn., Feb., 1915; supt., convict estab., Taiping, and inspr. of prisons, F.M.S., Jan., 1917; ag. reg. of titles, Perak, in addn., May, 1918; chmn., sanry. bd., Kinta, Dec., 1918; ag. Br. adviser, Kelantan, Feb., 1919; confirmed in appt., Oct., 1919; Br. res., Pahang, July, 1921; ag. Br. res., Selangor, Dec., 1925; Br. res., Selangor, Feb., 1926.

THOMSON, JAMES MOFFAT.—B. 1874; ed. at George Watson's Coll.; joined N.E. Rhodesia adminstr., 13th June, 1903; transf. to N.W.R., 1st Oct., 1905; ag. collr., 1st Apr., 1906; native commsnr., 1st Apr., 1907; ag. ast. mag., 3rd Sept., 1907 to 22nd Oct., 1911; ast. mag., N.R., 23rd Oct., 1911; has acted as mag. on various occasions between Sept., 1917 and Oct., 1923; ag. sec. for mines, 14th Aug. to 9th Dec., 1922; mag., 18th Oct., 1923; J.P.

THOMSON, JOHN HASTINGS.—B. 1873; ed. Wallace Hall Academy, Dumfries-shire, Scotland; apptd. ast. dist. offr., Somaliland, May, 1901; ag. consul, Berbera, Sept., 1901, to Aug., 1903; ag. dist. offr., Bulhar, Jan., 1904-5; polit. offr. with Gen. Swayne in Abyssinia, Mar. to Apr., 1905; polit. offr. with troops on Abyssinian border, May to July, 1905; ag. sub-commsnr., Zeyla, 2nd June, 1905; Somaliland medal with clasp, 1902-4; dist. offr., Makhir Coast, Aug., 1906; ag. sub-commsnr., Zeyla, 5th June, 1907; dist. commsnr., E.A.P., Apr., 1914; med. offr., Uganda, Dec., 1922; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Oct., 1924.

THORNE, WM. HOBART HOUGHTON.—B. 1875; ed. at St. Paul's Schl., King's Coll., Camb., and Paris Univ.; called to the bar, Inner Temple, Nov., 1900; Licencié en Droit de la Faculté de Paris, Nov., 1903; British representative on Internat. Claims Coms., at Casa Blanca, 7th May, 1908; employed at F.O. in investigation of Anglo-American pecuniary claims, 15th Sept., 1910, to 11th July, 1911; pres. of dist. ct., Larnaca, Cyprus, 29th July, 1911; passed exam. in mod. Greek, Nov., 1912, and in Ottoman Turkish, June, 1916; judge of the mixed tribunal, Alexandria, 18th Sept., 1916; resig. 5th Mar., 1917; British commsnr. on the mixed indemnities coms., Athens, 6th Mar., 1917; re-apptd. a judge of the mixed tribunal, Alexandria, 1st Feb., 1919; is comdr. of the Order of the Saviour.
THORNE, RICHARD ALFRED.—B. 1902; apptd. after compet. exam., ast. clk., sec.'s office, G.P.O., 24th Oct., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; transf. to C.O., 15th Oct., 1920.

THORNTON, Lieut.-Col. (Temp. Col.), Sir EDWARD NEWBURY, K.B.E. (1919), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. Lond., D.P.H. (Camb.), S.A.M.C., Eq. of the Order of St. John of Jerusalem (1924).—B. 1878; ed. Cheltenham Coll. and London Hosp.; ast. med. offr., Imp. Yeomanry Hospitals, Anglo-Boer war, 1900-01; plague serv., Punjab, 1902-03; ent. civ. serv., Cape Colony, 1903; additional M.O.H., Cape Colony, 1906; med. inspr. of hosp. and charitable insts., and ch. local govt. inspr., Cape, 1910; major, med. br., sec. I, Union Res. of Offrs. in German S.W. Africa, 1915; (ment. in desps.); Lieut.-Col., S.A.M.C. and O.C.S. African mil. hosp., Richmond, Surrey; mem., comtee. of management, and surg. to Queen Mary's aux. convol. hosp. for limbless soldiers, Roehampton, and mem., comtee. management, Queen Mary's aux. convol. hosp. for facial injuries, Sidcup; chmn., exec. comtee. on vocational training of soldiers in hosp., Lond. dist.; represented S. Africa, Inter Allied Conf. on disablement problems arising out of the war, 1916-20; ast. health offr. for the Union, dir. of med. services, Union Defence Forces, and chmn., cent. housing bd. for the Union, 1920.

THORNTON, GEORGE.—M.D. (Edin.); M.R.C.P. (Lond.); D.P.H. (Oxford); house physcm., Edin. Royal Infirmary, 1890; house surg., Metropolitan hosp., Lond.; house surg., children's hosp., Hull; ast. med. offr., Tooting Fever hosp., 1893-99; civil surg. to H.M. forces in S. Africa; med. supt., Pretoria hosp., 1900-1908; med. supt., gen. hosp., Colombo, Ceylon, Nov., 1908; ag. prin. civ. med. offr., Sept.-Dec., 1919; ast. civ. med. offr., Jan., 1920; ag. prin. civ. med. offr., May, 1922; ag. reg. civ., Ceylon Med. Coll., Jan. to Oct., 1923; ag. prin. civ. med. offr., Oct., 1924 to May, 1925; dep. dir., med. and many. services, Oct., 1925.

THORNTON, Sir Henry Worth, K.B.E. (1919), M.I.C.E.—B. 1871; ed. St. Paul's Schl., Concord, New Hampshire, U.S.A., Univ. of Pennsylvania; ent. serv., Penna Rly., (lines west of Pittsburgh) in engnr. dept., 1894; gen. supt., Long Is. Rly., 1911-14; gen. man., G.E. Rly. (Eng.), 1914; dep. dir., inland waterways and docks, 1917; ast. dir. gen., movements and rlys. with temp. rank of col., 1917; dep. dir. gen., movements and rlys. with rank of brig.-gen., 1918; inspr.-gen., transportation, with temp. rank of major-gen., 1919; Cross of Offr. Order of Leopold, 1919; French Legion of Honour, 1920; American D.S.M., 1919; chmn., bd. of dirs. and pres., Canadian Nat. Rlys., 1922.

THORNTON, Hugh Cholmondeley, C.M.G. (1920), C.V.O. (1920).—B. 1881; ed. Kelly Coll., Tavistock, and at Christ Church, Oxford; priv. sec. to Lord Milner, as mem. of the War Cabinet without portfolio, Dec., 1916 to Apr., 1918; as sec. of state for war, 1918-19; as sec. of state for the colonies, 14th Jan., 1919; apptd. crown agt. for the colonies, 1st Oct., 1920.

THOROGOOD, William Jesse, M.C.—B. 1869; ed. Latymer Upper Schl. and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; enlisted, K.R.R.C., Sept., 1915; 2nd lieut., Tank Corps, Jan., 1917; M.C., Sept., 1918; cadet, F.M.S., Apr., 1921; attd. to Chinese prot., Ipoh; ag. ast. dist. offr., Kuala Selangor, Mar., 1922; ast. contr. of lab., Klang, May, 1923; passed cadet, Sept., 1923; offr., cls. V., Apr., 1924; ast. contr. of lab., Negri Sembilan and Malacca, Aug., 1924; attd., land office, Batu Gajah, Jan., 1926; offr. ast. dist. offr., Gopeng and Kampor, Feb., 1926.

THRELFALL, WM. HERBERT.—B. 1875; apptd., after open compet. exam., to controller's off., London postal service, Apr., 1898; supt. of registn., G.P.O., Singapore, Oct., 1905; ag. sup. of mails, G.P.O., Singapore, Nov., 1905; supt. of regim., ditto, Sept., 1906; supt. of mails, Penang, Sept., 1907; supt., money order branch and savings bank, Singapore, Jan., 1912; ag. ast. postmnr.-gen., Penang, July, 1914; acctnt., G.P.O., Singapore, Jan., 1915; ag. ast. P.M.G., Oct., 1918; ag. P.M.G., May, 1919; ag. ast. P.M.G., Singapore, May, 1919; ag. ast. P.M.G., Penang, July, 1919; ast. P.M.G., Penang, July, 1920; ast. P.M.G., Singapore, but continued to act as above, Sept., 1920.

TIBBITS, ARTHUR CHARLES KENT.—B. 1877; employed col. sec.'s off.; treas. and aud. off., Antigua, Apr. to Dec., 1894; clk. sav. bank, Jan., 1895; 1st clk., post off., Jan., 1898; ag. postmnr. in 1899, 1900, 1901, 1903, 1904, 1906, 1908 and 1907; postmnr. of Antigua, and federal postmnr. of Leeward Is., 1st Apr., 1907; apptd. a library trustee and a J.P., Antigua, 1910; income and trade tax assessor in 1908, 1913, 1914 and 1915; chmn., bd. of guardians in 1911, 1913 and 1915; dir., P.O.G. fund, May, 1912; ag. ast. censor and ch. censor during war; dep. chmn., city comsnr., 4th Dec., 1916; ag. offr. in ch. Antigua—Montserrat flashlight serv., Aug., 1918 to Feb., 1919; ag. comsnr., Virgin Is., Aug., 1918 to Feb., 1919; warden and mag., Nevis, 1st Oct., 1925; mem., exec. and leg. couns., St. Kitts-Nevis.

TIBBITS, ELWOOD D'ARCY.—B. 1880; ag. 4th clk., col. sec.'s off., Antigua, Jan., 1899, to Nov., 1900 (almost continuously); jun. audit clk., 1st Dec., 1900; 4th clk., col. sec.'s off., 8th July, 1901; ag. clk. to adminstr. and clk. to exec. coun., Dominica, 2nd Aug., 1905, to 5th Apr., 1906; 3rd clk., col. sec.'s off., Antigua, 23rd Sept., 1906; 2nd clk., clk. to exec. and legis. couns., and supt. of govt. printing off., Antigua, 11th Oct., 1911; ag. priv. sec. to gov., in 1903, 1904, 1911 and 1912; ag. ast. col. sec., in 1907, 1908, 1911 and 1912; ag. ch. clk. on abolition of off. of ast. col. sec., 26th June to 1st Oct., 1912; clk., C.S.O., and clk. to exec. and legis. couns., Leeward Is., 2nd Oct., 1912; ast. col. sec., 1st Oct., 1920; ag. col. sec., in 1922, 1923, 1924 and 1925; admstr. govt. of Antigua on several occasions during that period; comsnr. for St. John, Nov., 1925; ag. comsnr., Montserrat, Jan., 1926.

TICHBORNE, C. R. H., L.A.H. (Dub.)—Surgeon, S. African War, 1901-02; lieut., R.A.M.C., 1902-05; capt., R.A.M.C. (S.R.), 1905-11; O.C., 2nd D.D.Co., R.A.M.C., Mil., 1908-07; capt., R.A.M.C., Aug., 1914 to June, 1918; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, June, 1918.

TIGHE, MACARTAN H., B.A., B.L.—(Called to Irish Bar, 1910); lieut., R. Dublin Fusiliers, France, 1915; capt., 1917, ment. in desps.; demob., 1920; crown pros., Tanganyika Territory, 1920; ag. solr. gen., Jan. to July, 1923, Nov., 1923 to Sept., 1924 and June to Dec., 1925.

TILLEY, ALBERT.—B. 1878; Impl. serv., 1895-02; post off., exchequer and audit depts.; transferred to Transvaal serv., Mar., 1902; clk., audit off., Transvaal, 1902; senr. clk., 1909; ditto, audit off., Union of S. Africa, 1910; prin. clk., 1912; prov. audr. and local govt. inspr., Transvaal Prov., May, 1915.

TIMMS, CHARLES GORDON, L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S.Ed., Certif., L.S.T.M.—B. 1886; ed. R. Coll., Surgeons, Edin.; served with R.A.M.C., Oct., 1914 to Mar., 1919; M.O., Uganda, 26th Jan., 1922; transf'd, Somaliland, 1st Dec., 1922.

TINDALL, BENJAMIN ARTHUR.—B. 1879; ed. Victoria Coll., Cape Prov.; B.A., LL.B.; called to bar, 1903; practised, Pretoria; K.C., 1919; presided, sp. ct., Swaziland, 1914; judge, sup. ct., Transvaal prov. divn., 1st Dec., 1922.
TISDALL, E. G. S.—Ast. dist. commr., E. Africa Prot., Oct., 1919.

TISDALL, HERBERT GEORGE, B.Sc., A.R.C.Sc., A.M. Inst. C.E., A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1879; ed. Bedford Schl. and Imp. Coll. of Science (Whitworth schl.), 1904; eng. inspr., C.A., 1907; ast. engrg., engrg. contracts dept., 1913; dep. head, engrg. contracts dept., 1926.
TITCHENER, HENRY IAN HERRICK.—B. 1900; entd. C.O., Feb., 1916; apptd., after compet. exam., ast. clk., C.O., 24th Apr., 1918; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920.
TODD, RONALD RUSKIN.—B. 1902; ed. County High Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Camb. (schol.); B.A. (hons.), 1924; cadet, Hong Kong, 1924.
TOLMIE, ALEXANDER WILLIAM.—B. 1884; ed. Kincaidine high schl., Toronto Univ.; apptd. to Canadian govt. exhibtn. commr., 1908; Canadian govt. exhibtn. commr., Aug., 1919.

TOLMIE, RODERICK FINLAYSON.—B. 1858; ed. coll. schl., Victoria, British Columbia; clk. in bank of B. Columbia (Cariboo agency) 1874, entd. B.C. govt. serv., 1877; gen. sec. B.C. mine owners assoc., 1899; re-entd. govt. serv. as dep. min. of mines, 1902.

TOLMIE, HON. SIMON FRASER.—B. 1867; ed. Victoria High Schl., Ontario, and Vet. Coll., Toronto; farmer and breeder of live stock; pres., Dominion Holstein Breeders, B. Columbia Vet. Assoc., B. Columbia Holstein Breeders; chf. inspr. of health of animals branch, B. Columbia; B. C. representative on live stock comm.; elec. to Canadian H. of c. g. e., 1917 and 1921; min. of agric. in fed. govt., Aug., 1919; resign. portfolio on defeat of admtn., in g.e., Dec., 1921.
TOMKINSON, C.—Ast. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., June, 1915; seconded as lieut., Carrier Corps.

TOMLINSON, GEORGE JOHN FREDERICK, M.A. (Oxon), Barrister-at-law (Inner Temple).—Served in educn. dept., Transvaal, Feb., 1903, to Oct., 1904; ast. res., N. Nigeria, July, 1907; seconded to G. Coast as dir. of educn., Dec., 1909, to Dec., 1910; 3rd cls. res., N. Nigeria, 1st Apr., 1911; 1st cls. dist. offr., Nigeria, May, 1918; mem., salaries revision comm., 1919-21; ast. sec. native affrs., 1st Jan., 1921; ag. sec. native affrs., Feb. to Dec., 1921, Mar. to Oct., 1923 and from May to Nov., 1925.

TOMLINSON, SIR THOMAS SYMONDS, Kt. B.A. (1926).—B.A. Trin. Coll., Camb.; B. 1877; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1901; town mag., Zanzibar, 5th May, 1907; ast. judge of H.B.M. ct. for Zanzibar, 2nd Jan., 1911; ag. judge, 23rd Feb. to 4th Nov., 1916; judge (now ch. just.), 11th Oct., 1919; seconded as legal adviser of the civil admtn. of the occupied terry. of Ger. E. Africa, Feb., 1918; ag. atty-gen., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1920 to July, 1921; 2nd cls., Order of the Brilliant Star, Zanzibar, 1923.

TONGE, Lieut. Comdr. C. G., R.N.—B. 1878; ed. Fettes Coll., Edinburgh; naval transport offr., Aug. to Dec., 1914; lent for serv. in gunboats on Lake Nyasa, Jan., 1915; senr. naval offr. Lake Nyasa, and 1st offr., marine transport dept., June, 1919; Royal Naval Res. Offr.'s Decoration.
TONGUE, ERIC DAUNCKY, B.A., LL.B.—Ed. Taunton Schl. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge (schol. and exhibr.); asst. dist. comsnr., Uganda, 8th October, 1913; dist. comanr., 15th Aug., 1923.

TOPP, WM.—B. 1884; Gambia govt. serv., 1901-07; S. Nigeria govt., 1907-08; N. Nigeria govt., Oct., 1908 to Sept., 1910; transf'd. to the Gambia, Sept., 1910; lat. clk., col. sec.'s office, Nov., 1911; ch. clk., June, 1913; sp. cls. apptmt., Jan., 1920; ast. col. sec., Jan., 1924; offr.-in-charge, secretariat, July-Aug., 1925; J.P. and comsnr., ct. of requests, July, 1926.

TOPPIN, CHARLES SAMUEL.—B. 1866; ed. at Diocesan schl., Waterford, Ireland; served as book keeper and acctnt. with W. Australian land co. (Great Southern rly.), 1887 to 1897; on taking over of line entd. govt. ser. as sub-acctnt., rly. dept., 1st Mar., 1897; ast. acctnt., 1st July, 1900; sg. ch. acctnt., 1st July, 1903; aud.-gen. for State of W. Aust., Feb., 1904; also dep. aud.-gen. for Commonwealth, Feb., 1904, to Jan., 1906.
TORY, JAMES CRANWICK.—B. 1862; ed. common schl., Gysburo Acad.; Wesleyan Theological Coll., Montreal; Nova Scotia legislature, 1911; re-el., 1916; lieut.-gov., Nova Scotia, 1st Oct., 1925.

TOTHILL, FRANCIS JAMES.—Extra astt. to postmtr.-gen., Ceylon, 28th Oct., 1898; astt. inspr. of post offices, Jan., 1899; astt. sec., cent. irrigtn. bd., 16th Jan., 1900; sec., ditto, and office astt. to dir. of irrigtn., 16th May, 1900; astt. acctnt., gen. treasy., Jan., 1907; ag. financial astt. and acctnt., general treasy., Apr., 1911 to Mar., 1912, and Nov., 1915 to Mar., 1916; ag. paymr., Ceylon volr. force, Aug., 1914; ag. staff offr. in addition to his own duties, Apr., 1915; ag. financial astt. and acctnt., gen. treasy., Nov., 1915; 1st astt. acctnt., Apr., 1916; ag. finan. astt. and acctnt., Apr. to Nov., 1921.

TOUSSAINT, JAMES REGINALD.—B. 1879; ag. off. astt., Matara Kach, Ceylon, Jan., 1917; sec., Buddhist Temporalities ord. comsn., and extra off. astt. to dir. of educn., Nov., 1918; ag. off. astt. to col. auditor, Apr., 1919; papt'd. to cls. V. of civ. serv., local divn., July, 1919; ag. astt. col. audr., Mar., 1920; ag. sec. to food contr., July, 1921; addnl. astt. comsnr. of stamps, Sept., 1921; promoted cls. IV, Mar., 1926.

TOWNER, H. V., Assoc. Mem. Inst. Civ. Engrs.—Astt. supt. of wks., S. Stltmts., Mar., 1901; supt. of wks. and surveys, Malacca, Oct., 1904; ag. supt. of wks. and surveys, Singapore, Apr. to Sept., 1902, and from Nov., 1904, to Mar., 1908; exec. engrnr., Dec., 1909; ag. dep. col. engrnr., Penang, Jan., 1910; exec. engrnr., Singapore, Mar., 1915; on sp. duty in Malacca on road reconstruction, Oct., 1916; ag. dep. col. engrnr., grade I., Malacca, Dec., 1919; dep. col. engrnr., and survr.-gen., Malacca, Feb., 1919; exec. engrnr., grade I., Malacca, Dec., 1919; dep. col. engrnr., S. Stltmts., Feb., 1921; ag. col. engrnr., Mar., 1921.
TRAFFORD, H. H.—Astt. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Aug., 1913; 1st grade admstve. offr., Kenya, 1926.

TRAFFORD, JOSEPH LOUIS.—B. 1888; ed. Beaumont Coll.; astt. dist. comsnr., G. Coast, 18th Aug., 1914; ag. dist. comanr., June, 1915, and from July, 1916 to July, 1917; astt. dist. comsnr., 1914; ag. dist. comanr. for various periods, 1915-20; ag. astt. col. sec., June to Aug., 1920; dist. comanr., 1st Jan., 1921; astt. col. sec., 11th Oct., 1922.

TRATMAN, DAVID WM.—Ed. at Clifton Coll., schlr. of Univ. Coll., Oxford, 1900; B.A., 1904; cadet, Hong Kong, Nov., 1904; passed cadet, Jan., 1907; ag. astt. land offr. in New Territories, and regsr. of land st., 1907; mem. of land ct., 1907; ag. pol. mag. in New Territories, 1907; ag. astt. dist. offr., 1909; 2nd astt. regsr.-gen., 1909; ag. supt. of imports and exports, 1910; 1st astt. regsr.-gen., 1911; ag. dist. offr., N.T., 1911; ag. head of sanitary dept., 1912; astt. dist. offr. N.T. (South), 1912; ch. astt. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1914; ag. dist. offr., N.T., 1915; ag. head of sanitary dept., 1916; head of sanitary dept., 1917; ag. supt. of imports and exports, 1917; local priority authority, 1918; ag. col. treas., 1920 and 1922; dist. offr., N. Territory, 1920; ag. col. treas., 1924; ag. sec., Chinese affrs., 1925.

TRAVERS, MICHAEL F.—B. 1897; ed. St. Mary's and O'Connell's Schls., Dublin; boy clk., Irish land comsnr., Dublin, 8th May, 1913; astt. clk., board of educn., 22nd Apr., 1915; war serv., R. Irish Regt., 19th Apr., 1916 to 6th Dec., 1919; appdld. cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1921; transf'd. to C.O., 7th Apr., 1926.

TRAVERSI, ANTONIO THOMAS, F.I.A. (London), F.C.A.S. (New York).—B. 1871; govt. insurance dept., N.Z. 1891-1907; actuary, friendly societies dept., 1907-17; actuary and sec., national provident fund, 1910-17; astt. actuary, govt. insurance dept., 1917-22; actuary, 1922; comsnr., 1923.

TREE, FRANCIS THOMAS, B.A., Dublin.—B. 1887; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1910; ag. astt. dist. offr., Kuala Pilah, Jan., 1911; astt. dist. offr., Ipoh, June, 1911; 2nd lieut., R.G.A., sp. res., Nov., 1915; lieut., Sept., 1916; attd. R.E. for research in poison gas, Mar., 1918; dist. offr., Ulu Langat, Sept., 1919; ag. dist. offr., Kuantan, May, 1920; ag. astt. adviser, Batu Pahat, Jahore, Oct., 1922; offr., cls. III., Nov., 1922; ag. astt. advr., Muar, May, 1924.

TREMLETT, FREDERICK THOMAS GEORGE, M.B.E. (1818).—B. 1881; ed. Denstone Coll., Staffs.; 2nd lieut., Jamaica Militia, 1902; lieut., 1903; attached Lancs. Fusiliers, 1903; sub.-inspr. constab., Jamaica, 1903; ag. inspr., ditto, 1908-11; studied finger-print system, Scotland Yard, 1908; J.P., 1909; inspr. of constab., Jamaica, 1912; supt., Spanish Town fire brigade, 1911-12; passed mil. schl. of instrn., Chelsea, 1912; transf'd. to Mauritius pol., 1912; dep. inspr.-gen. of pol., Mauritius, 1916; capt. and adjt., Mauritius volr. force, 1916; capt. in army, 1917; raised and comm. Mauritius labour batt., 1917; comm. Mauritius volr. force, Mar. to Dec., 1919; ag. inspr.-gen. of pol., Mauritius, May, 1917, and Mar. to Dec., 1919; major, 1920; ag. inspr.-gen., supt., prisons and C.O. Mauritius Vol. Force, Feb. to Sept., 1922; astt. comsnr. pol. and prisons, Uganda, Nov., 1922.

TREMLETT, CAPT. JOHN DANIEL SCHWEDE, M.C., M.R.C.V.S.—B. 1886; ed. Harrow Schl. and R. Vety. Coll., London; war serv., Gallipoli and France, 1915-18; M.C., 1917; vety. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1920; senr. vety. offr., Apr., 1923.
TRENCH, ARTHUR DONALD LE POER, O.A.C. (dip.)—Senr. coffee offr., agrl. dept., Kenya, Oct., 1916.

TREVOR, TUDOR GRUFFYDD.—B. 1865; dep. inspr., mines, Transvaal, Aug., 1901; ag. inspr., mines, Apr., 1911; inspr., mines, Apr., 1912; sec. for mines and wks., N. Rhod., 1st Apr., 1925; mem., leg. couns.

TREWIN, CAPT. ALGERNON BURNET.—B. 1884; ed. the Royal Naval Schl. and Sandhurst; ent. Army (Essex Regt.), Jan., 1905; instr. of signalling, 1908; seconded, K.A.R., Uganda, 1908; ooy. comdr., 1911; seconded, Zanzibar armed constab., 1912; ent. Uganda admstrn., July, 1914; adjt., Uganda vols., Aug., 1914; comrn., gen. res. of offra., 1919; dist. comnr., Uganda, Aug., 1923.

TROTTER, CRICIL DOUGLAS.—B. 1886; ed. Ardingly Coll.; joined B.S.A. Pol., Jan., 1906; transf. to native affairs dept., Sept., 1907; placed on fixed estabmt., 1908; passed native language and Cape civ. serv. law exam., 1910; asst. native comnr., Apl., 1911; J.P. for S. Rhodesia, May, 1911; asst. mag., Belingwe, Dec., 1911 to Mar., 1912; ch. clk. to native affairs dept., native comnr., and asst. mag., Sebungwe, May, 1912 to Nov., 1913; asst. dist. comnr., G. Coast, 18th Aug., 1914.

TROWBRIDGE, EDMUND.—B. 1881; ed. pub. model and high schls., Toronto, Ontario; asst. clk., exec. coun., 1906; sec., Alberta coal comnr., 1907; sec., Alberta pork comnr., 1908; dep. prov. sec. and regir. of companies, prov. of Alberta, 1910.

TROWELL, WM. JOHN.—B. 1864; M.I.N.A.; engrnr. and shipwright survr. and examnr. of engnas., Liverpool, Feb., 1898; inspr. under bd. of agric., Dec., 1898; inspr. of marine surveys, S. Sttlmts., 16th Jan., 1903; on special ser. in England in connection with expropriation of Tanjong Pagar Dock Co., Ltd., July to Nov., 1905; on duty at C.O. and Bd. of Trade on draft of merchant shipping ordn., 1910, Apr., 1908, to Jan., 1909; war serv. recog. by S. of S., Apr., 1918; on sp. serv. in England in connection with the designing of marine craft, 19th Sept., 1920; resumed duties, 21st June, 1921.

TRUTER, SIR THEODORUS GUSTAFT, K.B.E. (1924), C.M.G. (1918).—B. 1873; ed. Boys Pub. Schl., George; Normal Coll., Cape town; Diocesan Coll., Rondebosch; entd. Cape civ. ser., 1st Dec., 1892; chief examnr. of accts., 1895; lieut., Robert's Horse, 1899-1902 (medal and four clasps); asst. mag., Pretoria, 1st Apr., 1901; mem., spec. criminal ct.; mag., Ermelo, 1st Dec., 1904; mag., Standerton, 1st Apl., 1908; prov. sec., Transvaal, 1st July, 1910; comnr., S. African pol., 15th Oct., 1910; J.P., Union of S. Africa.

TUBOKU-METZGER, ALBERT EMERIC, B.A.—Ed. in C.M.S. Gram. schl. and Fourah Bay Coll., Sierra Leone; gained Fourah Bay Coll. foundation scholarship, Jan., 1877; grad. in Arts, June, 1880; first ols. hon. in theology, Durham Univ., Dec., 1881; jun. cls. tutor, Fourah Bay Coll., 1881; entd. S. Leone civ. serv. as extra clk., secretariat and treasy., 1st Mar., 1885; govrs' office, 1886; col.-treasy., 1887; served as finan. clk. in the Yonni expdn. under Sir Samuel Rowe, 1887; ch. clk., regir.-gen.'s dept., 1890; regir. of births and deaths, Freetown dist., 1890; police clk. and clk. of ct. of requests, Freetown, Apr., 1895; ag. regir.-gen., 1904 to 1905, and 1907; asst. dist. comnr., 1st May, 1908; apptd. Crown prosecutor in the Sept. sessions of the sup. ct. at Sherbro, Oct., 1908; is a J.P. for the col.; asst. dist. comnr., N. Sherbro dist., 1914; Karene dist., 1916; mem., lunatic asylum visiting comte., 1924; el. rural mem., leg. coun., Oct., 1924.

TUCKER, KEITH RAVENSCROFT, F.I.A.A., certified acctnt.—B. 1890; articled pupil to Messrs. Yarrow & Co., Glasgow, 1906-8; entd. engineering dept., office of crown agts. for the colonies, Mar., 1908; transf. to col. audit dept., cent. office, June, 1913; asst. auditor, G. Coast, Sept., 1913; asst. aud., Nyasaland Prot., June, 1916; despatched Pretoria, on mily. audit duties, Oct., 1918 to Apr., 1919; served with Togoland Field Force, Aug.-Sept., 1919 (ment. in desps.); lieut., G. Coast Volks., Apl., 1915; hon. temp. capt., Nyasaland Field Force, 1919; asst. auditor, Nyasaland Prot., June, 1916; ag. auditor, Apl., 1919 to Mar., 1920; ag. dep. treas., June, 1921; dep. treas., Dec., 1921; ag. treas., Apr., 1923; treas., 14th Jan., 1924.

TUCKER, LESLIE.—Ed. at Liverpool Collegiate Instit. and Univ. Coll., Liverpool; prelim. and inter. B.A.; 1st cls. scholarship; 1st cls. both years certif. exam.; parchment certif., 1897; head master, govt. boys' schl., St. Helena, 1904; head master, senior schl., St. Helena, 1905; hon. sec. and treas., govt. lace schl., 1908; supervisor of govt. schls., 1910; hon. sec., bd. of educn., 1911; govt. rep. on Hussey Charity bd. of educn., 1911; J.P. and coroner and memb. bd. of health, 1912; lieut., St. Helena Rifles, Sept., 1914 to Nov., 1916; lieut., R.A.S.C., Apr., 1917 to Nov., 1919; inspr. schls., Leeward Is., Feb., 1920; chmn., gen. purposes comte., Antigua bd. of educn.; chmn., St. Kitts bd. of educn., 1922.

TUDOR, SIR DANIEL THOMAS, KT. BACH. (1917), K.C.—B. 1886; called to the bar, Gray's Inn, Nov., 1890; eq. dr.; also practised on West Circ.; atty.-gen. of Grenada and St. Vincent, July, 1903; K.C. of both colonies, May, 1908; acted as chief just., also as col. sec. of Grenada on several occasions; admnstr. govt. of St. Vincent, June, 1907, to Jan., 1908; and govt. of Grenada, May and June, 1908, and Mar. to May, 1910; comnr. to consolidate and revise the Laws of Grenada, 1911; ag. legal ass't., C.O., May to Oct., 1911; chief justico, Bahamas, Nov., 1911; pres. of discharged soldiers comm. of inquiry, Jan., 1918; ch. just., Gibraltar, 1922; ret. 11th Oct., 1926.

TUPPER, HON. SIR CHARLES HIBBERT, K.C.M.G. (1893).—B. 1855; son of late Sir C. Tupper, mem. of N.S. bar; grad. Harvard law schl.; mem., house of commons, Canada, 1882-1900; min. of marine and fisheries, 1888-1896; deleg. to Washington in connection with Behring Sea difficulty, 1890; Br. agt. for the arbitration with the U.S. on the Behring Sea question, 1892-3; min. of just., 1895; resig. 1896; mem. of priv. coun. for Canada.

TURBETT, IVAN JOLIFFE TUPFELL.—B. 1890; ed. Trinity Coll., Dublin; barrister-at-law, King's Inn, Dublin, 1913; cadet, Fiji, 1914; ag. dist. comnr., 1915; ag. crown solr., 1916; ag. ch. pol. mag., 1917; pol. mag., Gambia, 1918; ag. legal adviser, 1919-22 and in 1924; ag. judge, sup. ct. on various occasions; solr.-gen., Sierra Leone, 1926.

TURGEON, WILLIAM FERDINAND ALPHONSE, K.C., B.A.—B. 1877; ed. at New York city and Laval univ., Montreal; barrister; atty.-gen. of Sask., 23rd Sept., 1907; elec. to Sask. legi., 1907; re-elec., 1908, 1912 and 1917; atty.-gen. and prov. sec., 1915; judge, ct. of appeal, Sask., 14th Mar., 1921.

TURNBULL, AUBREY MARRIOTT DALWAY.—Ed. Bath Coll.; clk., treasy., B.C. Africm, Jan., 1903; editor, B. C. A. Gazette, Nov., 1903, to Sept., 1906; 3rd cls. res., May, 1906; ag. regir. of high ct., offi. recvr. and liquidator, May to Dec., 1908; ag. res. Manje dist., July, 1909, to Mar., 1910, and May to Nov., 1912; dist. mag., July, 1906; ag. supt. of native affairs and comm. intell. offr., May, 1910, to Mar., 1911; attached to deputy govt. office for special duty, Sept., 1911, to Apl., 1912; ag. sec. to the administration, Nyasaland Prot., Dec., 1913; asst. ch. sec., Mar., 1914; mem. of native rising comm., 1915; dist. pol. offr. in occupied territory in German E. Africa, July, 1916; admin. offr., 1st grade, Tanganyika Territory, 1st Apr., 1920; hon. major, Nyasaland Field Force.

TURNER, ERNEST EDGAR.—B. 1880; clk., post office, Sheffield, 1894-1900; served in S. African war and in S. African constab. (medal and 6 clasps); dep't srgt.-major, R.N.W. mounted police, Canada, 1908-1916; coronation medal, 1911; lieut., Bahamas police and defence force, 1916; capt., 1917; major and offr. com. local forces, comdt. of police, provost marshal, and inspr. of prisons, June, 1919.
TURNER, CAPT. H. W.—Port offr., E. Africa Prot., May, 1915.

TURNER, JAMES POGSON.—B. 1871; clk., pub. library, St. Kitts, 1890; clk., regis.'s off., St. Kitts, 1897; comsnr. to administer oaths, St. Kitts, 1899; clk. to mag., dist. "C," St. Kitts, May to Oct., 1905; 1st clk., regis.'s off., Antigua, 3rd May, 1906; comsnr. to admist. oaths, Antigua, May, 1906; clk. to trustees, sup. ct. library, June, 1906; clk. to regis. of jlt. stock companies, Nov., 1906; ag. regis. and prov. marshal, Dominica, Oct., 1910, to Feb., 1912; ditto, Antigua, and ch. regis., Leeward Is., 17th Feb. to 31st March, 1912, 26th Apl. to 20th Nov., 1914, and from 4th May to 5th Nov., 1915; dep. coroner, dists. "A" and "B" Antigua, Apl., 1916; dir. of P.O.G. fund, Jan., 1917; regis. and prov. marshal, Antigua, and chief regis., 1st Sept., 1917.

TURNER, LEWIS JAMES BARNETSON.—B. 1886; ed. Edinburgh Univ. (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1908; off. asst. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Dec., 1909; pol. mag., Gampola, Nov., 1911; office asst. to govt. agt., S. Prov., May, 1913; asst. comsnr. of excise, Nov., 1913; asst. comsnr., S. Div., Jan., 1914; ditto, N.W. Div., Aug., 1916; asst. govt. agt., Kandy, in addition to his own duties, Oct., 1917; ag. asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Sept., 1918; asst. comsnr. of excise, N.W. divn., and asst. govt. agt., Kandy, Aug., 1919; ag. asst. govt. agt., Nuwara Eliya, Oct., 1919; supt. of census, July, 1920; dir. of statistics in addn., Sept., 1920.

TURNER, WILLIAM.—Ed. at Abdn. and Edin. Univs.; M.A. (Abdn.), 1876; M.B. Edin., 1879; M.D., 1881; res. phys., roy. hosp. for sick child., Edin., 1879; non-res. phys., roy. infirmary., Edin., 1890; res. accoucheur, Glasgow maternity hosp., 1881; mem. roy. med. soc., Edin., fell obstetrical soc., Edin.; asst. surg. civil hosp., Gibraltar, 1882; also surg. civ. prison, med. offr. lunatic asyl., and dist. med. offr., 1882; pub. vaccinator, pd. surg., 1888; surg. to col. hosp., Oct., 1889.

TURTON, NEVILLE HARRY.—B. 1889; ed. at Merton Coll., Oxford; B.A. (hone.), 1911; M.A., 1915; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple; asst. auditor, S. Leone, 1912; ag. auditor on four occasions; volunteer, R.G.A., Sierra Leone; asst. to the atty.-gen., Uganda, 1921; 1st cls. mag. and judge, Kampala in addn.; atty.-gen., St. Vincent, June, 1924; chmn., income tax comsnrs., 1924; mem., educ. bd., 1925; ag. col. sec., Aug., 1925 and July, 1926; ag. ch. just., Oct., 1925.
TUTK, RICHARD CLIFFORD.—B. 1874; ed. Royal Schl. of St. Peter, York; ent. I.C.S., 1898; served in Gt. War; barrister-at-law (Middle Temple), 1923; pres., land ct., Jerusalem, 1925.

TWELLS, JOHN.—Ed. at Jesus Coll., Camb. (scholars); B.A., 1907; examr., E. and A. Dept., July, 1909; asst. auditor, S. Leone, Nov., 1909; asst. auditor, E. Africa Prot., July, 1912; senr. asst. mast., educn. dept., Kenya, 1919 to June, 1926.
TWINING, T. C.—Asst. dist. comsnr., Kenya, Sept., 1921.

TWOSE, LAWRENCE RENDLE.—B. 1891; clk. to mag., dist. A, 1915; sec. to fire brig. commn., 1916; lieut., H.M. forces, 1916-19; ag. A.D.C. to gov., Leeward Is., on occasion of visit of Prince of Wales, 1920; clk. to comsnr., clk., couns., dep. coroner, educn. dist. offr., Montserrat, 1920; J.P., chin., prison justices, 1924; mag., coroner, regisr.-gen., sup. ct., 1924; M.E.C. and M.L.C., 1925.
TYLER, JAMES ERNEST.—Govt. printer, S. Sttlmts., 1st July, 1905.

TYLER-SMITH, H. A., A.M.I.C.E. A.M.I.M.E.—B. 1887; B.Sc. (Eng.) with 1st cls. hons., civil engng., Liverpool Univ., 1909; 2nd lieut., R.E. (sp. res.) 1910; asst. engrmr., P.W.D., Kenya, 1911; exec. engrmr., 1917; capt., R.E. (S.R.), 1917; D.P.W., Gambia, Dec., 1920.
TYNDALL, EUSTACE JOHN.—B. 1895; ed. Beaumont Coll., Windsor, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; asst. dist. comsnr., Sierra Leone, 9th July, 1924.

TYRRELL, FRANCIS GRAEME.—B. 1876; ed. Pembroke coll., Oxford (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1900; ag. priv. sec. to H.E. the governor, Aug., 1901; priv. sec. to H.E. the offr. admstg. the govt., Apr., 1902; off. astt. to govt. agt., Cent. prov., Jan., 1903-Dec., 1904; ag. astt. govt. agt., Nuwara Eliya, Oct., 1904; seconded supt. of pol., N.W. prov., Aug., 1905, cent. prov., Mar., 1907; dist. judge, Badulla, Dec., 1908; astt. govt. agt., Matale, Feb., 1909; dep. collr. of customs, Nov., 1911; on mil. duty, Jan., 1915; ag. govt. agt., N.C. Prov., Aug., 1919; govt. agt., N.W. Prov., May, 1921; prin. collr., cust., Apr., 1926; mem., exec. and leg. couns.

TYSER, SIR C. R., K.T. BACH (1909).—Ed. at Rugby and Trin. Coll., Camb., M.A. (1872); called to bar, In. Tem., 1873; astt. ag. judic. comsnr. and leg. advsr., Cyprus, 1880-1; atty.-gen., Leewarda, 1886-9; pres. dist. ct., Kyrena, Cyprus, 1895; puisne judge, Cyprus, Jan., 1902; ch. just., 1906; ret., 1919; author of "Marine Insurance Losses," 1894; wrote translation of Omar Hilmi Effendi's Evkaf Law, 1899; and of the Mejelle, 1901.

UNIACKE, CAPT. ANDREW GORE, D.S.O.—Served with N.W. mtd. pol., Canada (including Yukon expedn.), 1893-1898; served in S. African war, with Canadian forces and M.I., 1899-1902 (2 medals, 8 clasps, D.S.O.); dist. supt., N. Nigeria police, 1903; served with expdn. against Okpotos, 1903-4 (A.G.S. medal and clasp); served with Nigeria Regt., 1916-18; inspr.-gen., N. Provs., Nigeria pol., 1919.

UNWIN, ARTHUR HAROLD, D.Oec.—Forestry branch, dept. of interior, Canada, 1903-4; astt. conservator and senr. conservator of forests, Nigeria, 1904-17; office of woods, timber supply dept., England, 1917-19; prin. forest offr., Cyprus, 1921.

URQUHART, CHARLES FREDERIC ROBERT HILDYARD.—B. 1867; entd. Crown agt.'s off., Jan., 1885; supt. of stock transfer offr., 1895; regisr. of inscribed stocks and head of stock dept., 1905; head of finance dept., 1st Jan., 1920.
USHER, CAPT. C. G., M.C.—Ast. dist. comsnr., Kenya, May, 1920.

USHER, WALTER WHITBREAD.—B. 1886; ed. at Marist Bros. Coll., Uitenhage; clk., Swaziland, 1910; regisr. and master, sp. ct. of Swaziland, and Sheriff, 1916; dep. asst. commnr., Mbabane, 1919; served in Anglo-Boer War, 1901, S.W. campaign, 1914-15 and Flanders, 1916-18.

VELLAOCOTT, PHILIP NORTHCOTT, M.B., Lond. (hons. in for. and obst. med.), 1895; B.S., 1896; M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. (Lond.), 1896; F.R.C.S. (Eng.), 1901.—House surg., res. obstetric and clin. asst., Guy's hosp.; asst. curator of museum, St. George's hosp.; civ. surg., S. African field force, 1901 to 1902; capt., S.A. Constab., 6th June, 1902; transf'd to O.R.C. govt. serv. as res. med. offr., National hosp., Bloemfontein. 1st Dec., 1905; fellow of R. Inst. of pub. health; deleg. from O.R.C. to 2nd Internat. Congress on School Hygiene.
VENABLES, OSWALD ERIC.—Cadet, S. Stltmts., 29th Nov., 1914; on active ser., June, 1917; ag. dist. offr., Jasim, 30th Sept., 1919; ag. dist. offr., Dingdingo, 27th Feb., 1922.
VENIOT, PETER JOHN.—Mem. New Brunswick legislature, 1894-1900; re-elec., 1917 and min. of pub. wks.; premier, New Brunswick, 1923-26; postm.-gen. of Canada, 1926.

VENN, HARRY NORMAN.—West Australian service, 1895-1900; S. African war (2 medals, 5 clasps); Imperial army pay dept. in Australia, 1902-03; ch. clk. and acctnt., dist. hosps. dept., Transvaal, Aug., 1903; pub. health dept., Transvaal, 1904; acctnt., col. sec.'s dept., 1905; acctnt., dept. of interior, May, 1910; ag. under-sec. for interior, Union of S. Africa, 1916; under-sec. for the Interior, 1st Dec., 1919.

VENNING, G.—B. 1879; apptd., after compet. exam., asst. clk., and assigned to local govt. bd., May, 1899; after further compet. exam., apptd. clk. of the 2nd div. of the civ. ser., and assigned to W.O., 8th Jan., 1900; transf'd to C.O., 16th Apr., 1903; conf. clk., 1st Apr., 1919.

VENNING, JOHN HENRY.—B. 1876; ed. Worthing Coll.; Matabele Rebellion, 1896; apptd. to dist. admstn., N.W. Rhodesia, June, 1907; asst. native comsnr., Apr., 1908; native comsnr., Apr., 1908; asst. mag., N.R., Apr., 1913; ag. res. mag., Barotse dist., Mar., 1915-Mar., 1916; has acted as mag. on various occasions between Apr. 1919 and Aug. 1924; mag., Aug., 1924; J.P.

VEREKER, STANDISH HENRY PRENDERGAST.—B. 1878; served in I.Y. in S. African War, 1900-01 (ment. in desps., awarded medal "For distinguished conduct in the field"); served under F.O. as British vice-consul, and frequently as acting consul, Cherbourg, Jan., 1902 to Sept., 1908; assisted partly comtees on vagrancy, Nov., 1906, to Mar., 1906; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 1st Sept., 1906.
VERNEY, FRANK ARTHUR, O.B.E. (1925).—B. 1874; vet. offr., Natal, 1901-08; prin. vet. offr., Basutoland, 1908.

VERNON, ROLAND VENABLES, C.B. (1924).—B. 1877; ed. at Clifton and Oxford; schol. of Balliol, 1895-9; 1st cls. moda., 1897; prox. acc. Hertford schlr.; Craven schlr., 1897; Jenkyns exhib., 1899; 1st cls. lit., hum., 1899; apptd. after compet. exam., clk. in sec.'s dept., inland rev., Nov., 1899; 2nd cls. clk., C.O., 13th Aug., 1900; sec. to Trinidad riot. coman., 1903; mem., managing comtees of E.I.O.; asst. priv. sec. to Lord Elgin, Jan., 1908; priv. sec. to Col. Seely, 16th Apr., 1908; read. S. African Union medal, 1910; ag. 1st cls. clk., 1st Jan., 1911; priv. sec. to Lord Denman, gov.-gen. of Australia, 1911-12; 1st cls. clk., 9th Nov., 1912; visited and reported on British Solomon Islands, Nov.-Dec., 1911; represented H.M.G. on Anglo-French New Hebrides confce., May-Aug., 1914; transf'd to treas., Oct., 1914; lent to Miny. of Munitions, June, 1915; asst. gen. sec., 1916; visited France and Belgium on sp. serv., Feb., 1917; asst. sec., Irish Convention, June, 1917 to Mar., 1918; ret'd. to treas., May, 1918; dep. acct.-gen., B. of E., Apr., 1920; lent to C.O., Middle East dept. as asst. sec., finan. and economic, Apr., 1921; visited Egypt, Palestine and Trans-Jordania on sp. serv., Sept., 1921 to Jan., 1922; sp. serv. at Lausanne for Turkish Peace Treaty, Nov. to Dec., 1922 and June, 1923; transf'd to permanent staff of C.O. as asst. sec., Jan., 1924; seconded for service as finan. adviser to govt. of Iraq, 1925.

VERRY, CECIL TYRELL.—B. 1886; ed. malvern; clk., treas., Nyassaland, 1909; transf'd, dist. admstn., 1900; res., 3rd grade, 1910; ag. A.D.C. o gov., 1915-18; temp. lieut., K.A.R., 1916; res., 1918; admstn. offr., 1st grade, 31st July, 1923.
VERSCHAFFELT, PAUL DESIRÉ NESTOR, L.L.B., F.I.A.N.Z.—B. 1887; grad. in law, Victoria Coll., N.Z.; ent. N.Z. pub. serv., 1904; asst. pub. serv. comsnr., 1921; pub. serv. comsnr., 1923.

VICTORIA, RIGHT REV. GERARD HEATH LANDER, Lord Bishop of Victoria, Hong Kong.—B. 1861; ed. at Trin. Coll. and Ridley Hall, Camb., B.A., 1884; M.A., 1888; D.D., 1907; consec. at Lambeth Parish Church, 29th June, 1907; curate of St. Bride's, Liverpool, 1884 to 1888; vicar of St. Benedict's, Liverpool, 1888 to 1896; of Litherland, 1896 to 1905; of St. Cyprian, Liverpool, 1905 to 1907; chrmn., Litherland schl. bd., 1898 to 1903; chaplain to Liverpool dist. lay readers, 1892 to 1905.
VIDAL, M. R. R.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Dec., 1912; 1st grade admstv. offr., Kenya, Jan., 1924.

VILJOEN, WILLIAM JACOBUS, M.A., L.N.C., Phil. D.—B. 1869; ed. at Normal Coll., Cape-town, Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, and S. African Coll., Cape-town; apptd. to staff, Normal Training Coll., Cape-town, 1889; after graduation studied at Univ. of Amsterdam, Leyden, Strasbourg, and in 1894 took degree of Phil. D. (in modern languages and comparative philology); prof., mod. languages and hist., Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch, 1896-1903; prof. of Dutch (lang. and lit.) ditto, now Univ. of Stellenbosch, 1903-10; elec. mem. of coun. of C. of G.H. Univ., 1897; mem., Maatschappij der Nederlandse, Leiden, 1900; rep. Cape Univ. at quincentenary of Leipzig Univ., 1909; dir., educn., O.F.S., 1910-18; mem. and chmn., of Grey Univ. Coll. coun., Bloemfontein, 1911-18; rep. Union of S. Africa at Impl. Educn. Confce., in London, 1911 and 1923; first vice-chan., Univ. of S. Africa, 1918; supt. gen. of educn., Cape Prov., 1918; mem., coun., Huguenot Univ. Coll., Wellington, 1919; hon. D.C.L., Oxford, 1923; author of several standard wks. on Dutch language and simplified orthography.
VIMPANY, HAROLD RAVENSCROFT.—B. 1870; clk., Basutoland, 1907; treas. clk., 1913; clk. to asst. comsnr., 1915; dir., inducs. schl., 1916.
VINCENT, GEORGE ALVES, M.B., Ch.B.—B. 1872; asst. med. supt., lunatic asylum, Trinidad, 16th Mar., 1901; supt., ditto, 1st Apr., 1909.
VINCENT, WILLIAM HENRY, L.M.S.S.A., Lond.—B. 1886; ed. Colet Court, St. Paul's schl., St. Mary's Hosp., and London Univ.; dist. med. offr., Fiji, 1915.

VISCHER, HANNES, C.B.E.—B. 1876; ed. Switzerland and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 1903; res., 1909; dir. of educn., 1908-18; lieut., Gen. List, 1916; capt., 1916; major, 1916; Étoile Noire, 1917; Legion d'Honneur, 1918; Crown of Italy, 1918; Crown of Belgium, 1918; C.B.E., 1918; demob., 1919 with rank of major in G.R.O.; mem. and sec., S. of S.'s advisory comte, on native educn. in Trop. Africa, Nov., 1923.

VISSER, DANIEL HENDRIK, J.P.—B. 1877; entered Cape pub. serv., 23rd Mar., 1894; asst. mag. and ag. civil comsnr., 1902-05; clk.-asst., Cape House of Assm., 8th Apr., 1907; clk.-asst., House of Assm., Union of S. Africa, Nov., 1910; clk., House of Assm., 1st Oct., 1920.

VLIELAND, CHARLES ARCHIBALD.—B. 1890; ed. Exeter Schl. and Balliol Coll., Oxford (schol.); 1st cls. hon., nat. sci., 1911; cadet, F.M.S., 1914; asst. sec. to res., Selangor, 1917; 3rd asst. sec., fedl. secretariat, 1919; asst. dist. offr., 1929-21; sec. to res., Negri Sembilan, 1922; pvt. sec. to O.A.G., June, 1925; addl. col. secs. office, Singapore, Nov., 1925; ag. 1st asst. sec. (A) Singapore, Dec., 1925.

VOLLET, EMILE EDOUARD CHARLES, M.D. (Paris), L.M.S.S.A. (Lond.).—B. 1869; med. offr., Basutoland, 1918; war. serv., médicin aide major, 2nd cls., 1914; médecin aide major, 1st cls., 1916; Chevalier de la Legion d'Honneur, 1917; Croix de Guerre, 1917.

VOLNEY, JOSEPH HENRY LOUIS.—B. 1875; entd. pub. ser., St. Lucia, Feb., 1892, as supernum. clk., post off.; ag. 4th clk., treasy., Apr. and May, 1893; ag. rev. offr., June to Nov., 1893; clk., immigr. off., Feb., 1894; 4th clk., treasy., Apr., 1894; ag. 3rd clk., in 1895, 1896, and 1897; 3rd clk., treasy., Jan., 1898; ag. 2nd clk. and treasy. acctnt., May to Oct., 1898; ag. 2nd clk. and cashier, treasy., Aug., 1900, to Apr., 1901; 2nd clk., regis.'s off., Apr., 1901; ag. ch. clk., Aug. to Oct., 1901; ag. audit clk., Aug. to Nov., 1902; transf'd. on temporary duty to pub. wks. dept.; ch. clk., regis.'s dept., Apr., 1903; ag. regist. on several occasions, 1908-1915; ag. mag., dist. III, Mar. to May; asst. mag., dist. I, Sept. to Dec., 1913, and from Aug., 1914; ag. marshal of the Royal court (Admiralty jurisdiction) from Sept., 1914.

VOULES, ARTHUR BLENNERHASSETT.—Ed. Dulwich Coll. and Sidney Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1892; called to the bar, Inner Temple, 1912; entered F.M.S. civ. ser., 1892; solr.-gen., S. Sttlmnts., 1913; ag. atty.-gen., S. Sttlmnts., 1919; ag. judicial comsnr., F.M.S., 1919; ag. legal adviser, F.M.S., in addn., Mar., 1920; legal adviser (offr. cls. I.A.), June, 1920; ag. jud. comsnr., F.M.S., in addn., June, 1920; ag. ch. sec. to gov't., Oct., 1920; ag. Br. res., N. Sembilan, Mar.-Apr., 1921; ag. atty.-gen., S. Sttlmnts., 10th Mar., 1922.
WADDEL, WILLIAM.—B. 1863; ed. Southland Boys' High Schl., N.Z.; entd. N.Z. state advances dept., 1895; asst. supt., 1904; dep. supt., 1907; supt., 1922.

WADDINGTON, E. J., O.B.E. (1919).—Asst. dist. comsnr., E.A.P., Dec., 1913; ag. prov. comsnr., Jubaland, Nov., 1919; senr. asst. sec. secretariat, Kenya, Feb. to Sept., 1923; clk. of couns., Sept., 1923; asst. sec., govs.' confce., 1926.
WADE, A. DE V.—Ed. Lancing Coll., exhibtn. and Keble Coll., Oxford; scholar; M.A.; asst. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Mar., 1912; ag. dep. ch. native comsnr., Kenya, 1926.

WADE, CECIL HENRY, B.A. (hons.), 1909.—Ed. Lancing Coll. (exhibtn.) and Keble Coll., Oxford; 3rd grade res., Nyasaland, 5th Aug., 1911; ag. 1st asst. sec. (native affrs.), July, 1920; 1st asst. sec., June, 1921; examr. in Chinyanja language; ag. ch. sec., May, 1926.

WADIA, SIAYAX HIRJI.—B. 1890; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., May, 1915; attached to Jahia Kachcheri, June, 1915; addl. pol. mag., Jaffna, in addition to his own duties, Oct., 1915; ag. office asst. to gov't. agt., N. Prov., Oct., 1916; financial asst. to chmn., municipal comn., Colombo, Jan., 1918; office asst. to gov't. agt. Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Mar., 1920; pol. mag., Avissawella, June, 1920; ag. dist. judge, Anuradhapura, July, 1921; asst. contr., rev., Apr., 1922; addl. asst. contr., rev., Aug., 1928; asst. comsnr., stamps, Jan., 1928.

WAIAPU, BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. WILLIAM WALMSLEY SENDGICK, M.A., D.D.—B. 1858; ed. Maidstone Gram. Schl. and Christ Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1881; served in marine batt. with ships at Sukim, 1884-5 (Egyptian medal with Sukim clasp and Khedive's bronze star); vicar of Waikari, New Zealand, 1901-3; of Akaros, 1903-4; of St. Luke's, Christchurch, 1904-13; Bishop of Waiapu, 1914.

WAINWRIGHT, WILLIAM FREDERICK, O.B.E.—B. 1881; Indian pol., 1899; accompanied Sir Charles Yate on polit. mission to Mekran, S. Persia; transf'd. to Bengal pol., 1901; on sp. famine operns., 1908-08; serv. in Gt. War, Egypt and Palestine, 1916-20; major, Territorial Res. (Yeomanry); dep. dist. comdt. of pol., Palestine, 1920; dist. supt., pol., Palestine, 1926.
