WAIT, WALTER ERNEST.—B. 1878; ed. Fettes and Edinburgh Univ. (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Oct., 1902; addl. asst. to gov't. agt., N. Prov., and addl. comsnr. of requests and pol. mag., Mannar and Puttalam during pearl fishery, Feb., 1905; asst. land stltmt. offr., May, 1906; dist. judge, Batticaloa, Dec., 1910; asst. land settm. offr., Feb., 1911; on mil. duty, Aug., 1914; pol. mag., Colombo, May, 1915; asst. gov't. agt., Puttalam and Chilaw, Sept., 1916; deputy-colr. of customs, Colombo, Apr., 1919; settlement offr., June, 1920; ag. contr., rev., Apr., 1922; contr., Indian immgrt. lab. in Ceylon, Apr., 1923; ag. contr., rev., Dec., 1925; mem., leg. coun.

WALCOTT, HENRY BARCLAY.—C.M.G. (1913), C.B.E. (1926); B. 1866; excise and customs offr., St. Lucia, 1884; treasy. acctnt., 1890; ag. ch. clk., treasy., 1891; ag. ch. clk. and acctnt., St. Vincent, 1892; ag. treasy. acctnt., Grenada, 1892; oh. clk., audit office, Dec., 1893; ag. postmr., 1896-6; ag. ch. of pol. and excise, 1897; 2nd audit clk., col. sec.'s off., Br. Honduras, 1897; auditor, Feb., 1898; ch. asst. treas., Gold Coast, Jan., 1900; ag. treas. and mem. ex. and legis. couns., dir., pub. offr.'s guarantee fund, mem. bd. of educn., July, 1900, to Jan., 1901, and May to Oct., 1902; ag. comsnr. of police and supt. of prisons, 1901-1902; pres. Accra town coun., 1901-1902; treas. and collr. of cust., regist. of shipping, pres., quarantine bd., chmn. poor relief comte., mem. of bd. of educn., mem. ex. and leg. couns., Br. Hond., Mar., 1903; J.P., Apr., 1903; ag. col. sec., 22nd July to 6th Aug., 1905, 10th Nov., 1905, to 12th Aug., 1906, and from 8th May, 1908; collr. of cust. and detaining offr., M.S. Act, Trinidad, 1909; colony's del. at confce. on Canadian reciprocity, Ottawa, 1912 and 1920; ag. col. sec., Oct., 1918, Mar.-Nov., 1919, July-Aug., 1920 and from Jan., 1921 to Jan., 1922; treas., Trinidad, Jan., 1924; ag.-gov., July to Aug., 1924; ag. col. sec. from Aug., 1924; chmn., agrl. bank, 1926.
WALKER, A.—Laboratory supt., E. Africa Prot., Oct., 1918.
WALKER, ARTHUR FRANCIS GREGORY, M.C.—B. 1884; asst. supt., prisons, Ceylon, Jan., 1910; inspr. gen., prisons, Feb., 1922.

WALKER, MAJOR GEORGE HENRY—D.S.O. (1901); b. 1875; ed. at pub. schls., Nelson and Taranaki; capt., New Zealand militia; served in S. African war as capt., 4th N.Z. Rough Riders and Colonial Light Horse (ment. in desps., King's, Queen's medals and 6 clasps); dist. supt. of pol., Northern Nigeria constab., 1907; asst. comsnr. of pol., S. Nigeria, July, 1908; comsnr. of pol., Southern Provinces, Nigeria, Jan., 1915; awarded the King's police medal, 1911; on mily. serv., Cameroons and E. Africa, 1915-19; dep. inspr. gen., pol., 1st Jan., 1921; ag. inspr. gen. in 1922 and 1923.
WALKER, JAMES.—Ch. veterinary research offr., E. Africa Prot., June, 1918.

WALKER, JOHN C.—4th batt. Yorkshire regt.; Coorga Nagpore rifles; served with Yorks. I.Y. in S. Africa (two medals with 5 clasps); asst. polit. offr., Somaliland, Sept., 1905; mentioned in despatches, 1906; asst. req., N. Nigeria, 1910.
WALKER, REGINALD PLUMER, A.M.I.T.—B. 1891; travelling audr., F.M.S., Rlys., 1914-18; asst. acct., 1918-25; dep. acot., Jan., 1926; ag. ch. acct., F.M.S. Rlys., Feb., 1926.

WALKER, WILLIAM HENRY, C.M.G. (1917); I.S.O. (1912); B.A.—B. 1864, apptd. 3rd class clk., govt. genl.'s office, Canada, 1887; promoted to 2nd cls., 1892; 1st cls., 1898; ch. clk., 1908; chf. clk., dept. of external affairs, 1909; asst. under-sec., for external affairs, 1912.

WALKER-LEIGH, ARTHUR HENRY CHAMBERLAIN.—B. 1871; ed. Cheltenham, Jersey and privately; major, late R. Munster Fusiliers and 7th Service Batt., Northamptonshire Regt.; Gold Coast constab., May, 1898; served in The Greys, Fra-Fra and Sapari expds., 1898-99 (ment. in desps.); served in S. African Light Horse, S. African War, 1900; Queen's Med., 4 clasps; Talani Exped., 1902; capt., 2nd Batt. Gold Coast Regt., W.A.F.F., 1901-02; Cameroons, 1915; severely wounded, "14-15" Star, Gen. Serv. and Victory Meds.; asst. dist. comsnr., N. Territories, Gold Coast, June, 1907; prov. comsnr., Aug., 1918; dep. ch. comsnr., Jan., 1921; ch. comsnr., May, 1924.

WALL, JAMES HORNE DARRELL.—B. 1871; elk. and storekeeper, Holberton Instit., Antigua, Nov., 1891; shorthd. reporter to W. Ind. Roy. Comsn., 1897; aud. def. force accts.; ag. ch. elk. to aud.-gen., Leeward and fedrl. accts., June, 1899; also elk. to trade and income tax comsnrs., Sept., 1899; ch. elk. to aud.-gen. and fedrl. accts., May, 1900; ag. treas. of Antigua and fedrl. treas., May-Oct., 1909, and Aug., 1910; ag. inspr. of schls., Leeward Is., Apr., '11, to Aug., 1912; also ag. treas. of Antigua, and federal treas., Mar.-Apr. and Aug.-Sept., 1912; ag. auditor-gen., Leeward Is., May to Aug. and Oct.-Nov., 1914; auditor-gen., Tonga, Mar., 1915.
WALLACE, C. R., L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S., L.M. (Ireland).—Capt., R.A.M.C. (T.F.), 1915-18; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, June, 1918.

WALLACE, CAPT. DAVID EUAN, M.C., M.P.—B. 1892; ed. Harrow and Sandhurst; joined 2nd Life Guards, 1911; adjt., 1915-18; now capt., res. of offrs.; served in France, 1914-18; wounded; three times ment. in desps.; on staff of mily. attache, Washington, 1919; A.D.C. to gov. gen., Canada, 1920; M.P. for Rugby, 1922-23; priv. sec. to Mr. Amery as 1st Lord of the Admiralty, 1922-23; M.P. for Hornsey, 1924; parly. priv. sec. to Mr. Amery as S. of S. for the Colonies, 1924.
WALLACE, SIR LAWRENCE AUBREY, K.B.E. (1918); C.M.G. 1910.—Acting admstr., N.E. Rhodesia, 15th May, 1907; ag. admstr., N.W. Rhodesia, Jan., 1909; admstr., Northern Rhodesia, 1911; ret., 1921.

WALLACE, WILLIAM ARTHUR JAMES.—B. 1876; ed. privately and at Brisbane Grammar Schl.; field surv. cadet, 1896-99; surv. gen.'s office, Queensland, 1899-1904; surv., F.M.S., 1904-08; asst. supt., survs., F.M.S., 1909-1916 and 1917-1919; ag. supt. of survs., F.M.S., 1916-17; supt. of survs., F.M.S., Mar., 1919.

WALLACE, WILLIAM THOMAS EWART.—Ed. privately and at St. George's Cathedral Coll., Cape Town; Cape govt. postal tel. serv.; transf., B.S.A. Co's serv., 1889; lieut., Mashonaland Defence Force (Matabele War), 1893; transf., B.S.A. Co's admstr. serv., 1894; resig. after Jameson raid, 1906; lieut. and adjt. B Squadron, Lord Loch's Horse, intelligence offr., 4th and 8th Mounted Infy. Brig., A.P.M. 6th Mounted Infy. (De Lisles), capt., 4th Batt. "The King's" (Liverpool Regt. (S. Africa), 1899-1902; supt., R.E. tel. constrn. party, S. Nigeria, 1903-05; seconded for serv. under C.O. as asst. supt., tels., Uganda and E.A.P., 21st Dec., 1905; asst. P.M.G. and asst. tel. engnr. for the Uganda Prot., 1st Jan., 1912; transf., dep. P.M.G., Gold Coast, 12th Mar., 1913; ag. P.M.G. on various occasions; major (2nd in commd.), 13th (Service) Batt., "The King's" (Liverpool) Regt., 1914; censor, Accra, 1915-16; sp. mil. serv., Palime, Togoland, Feb., 1916; served, headqrs. supply staff, min. of munitions, Aug., 1916 to Jan., 1917; ag. engnr.-in-ch., postal tels., Gold Coast, 1st Nov., 1923 to 10th May, 1924.

WALSH, DUDLEY J. C.—B. 1895; ed. Merchant Taylors Schl.; served with H.A.C. in France, 1914-15; 2nd lieut., Middlesex Regt. 1916; W. African Regt., Sierra Leone, Nigeria, 1916-17; transf., 2nd K.A.R. and served in E. Africa 1918-21; Somaliland campaign, 1920; dist. pol. offr., Somaliland, Feb., 1922.

WALSH, GROFFREY.—B. 1884; Imp. cust. serv., Apr., 1906; collr. of cust., Mombasa, May, 1913; seconded to occupied territory of German E. Africa to organise cust. serv., with rank of capt., 1916-17; in charge of cust. dept. of occupied area, German E. Africa, Jan. to July, 1917; dep. comsnr., cust., Kenya and Uganda, Jan., 1920; comsnr., cust., Kenya and Uganda and mem., leg. coun., Kenya, Apr., 1923.

WALTER, A., F.R.A.S.—Joined magnetic and meteorological dept. royal observatory, Greenwich, 1892; asst. director, Royal Alfred Observatory, Mauritius, Apr., 1897; statistical adviser to Mauritius Royal coman., 1909; census comanr., Mauritius, 1911 and 1921; dir., Royal Alfred Observatory, May, 1911 to 1921; ag. ast. col. sec., Mauritius, Nov. to Dec., 1918; food, trade and export contr., Mar., 1920 to Jan., 1922; ag. prot. of imgrts. and poor law comsnr., Jan., 1922; mem. of coun. of govt., 1922; headed deputation to India for the re-opening of emigr. to Mauritius, 21st Dec., 1922; statistician, Kenya, 1926; statistician to govs' confere., 1926; author of "Sugar Industry of Mauritius"; Insurance of crops against cyclones; editor, Mauritius almanac.

WALTER, ROBERT, C.M.G. (1918), B.A.—B. 1873; ed. at Marlborough, and Worc. Coll., Oxford; 1st cls. honours class. mods.; 3rd cls. honours lit. hum.; cadet F.M.S., Nov., 1896; passed in Chinese (Cant.), Apr., 1900; ag. asst. comsnr. of pol., Perak, 1901; passed F.M.S. law exam., 1901; seconded to Weihaiwei, Dec., 1901; sec. to govt. and mag., Weihaiwei, Apr., 1902; seconded for ser. under Transvaal govt. as emigrn. agt., May, 1904; passed in Pekingese, May, 1904; returned to Weihaiwei as sec. to govt. and mag., Jan., 1906; ag. commr., Weihaiwei, June-Oct., 1909, and Sept.-Oct., 1913; passed bar final exam., 1913; col. sec., B. Honduras, Feb., 1914; admstd. govt., Mar., 1917 to Apr., 1918, and from Aug., 1918 to Mar., 1919; war service recognised by S. of S., Nov., 1918; admstr., Dominica, May, 1920; admstr., St. Vincent, Apr., 1923.

WALTERS, JOHN RADLEY.—B.A. Cantab.—B. 1888; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., 17th Nov., 1911; attached to Kandy Kachcheri, 15th Dec., 1911; attached to Galle Kachcheri, May, 1912; ag. pol. mag., Negombo, Nov., 1912; office asst., Galle Kachcheri, Nov., 1913; pol. mag., Gampola, May, 1915; ag. dist. judge, Badulla, July, 1917; pol. mag., Karunagala, Dec., 1920; asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Sept., 1921; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, July, 1923; asst. govt. agt., Kegalla, Feb., 1924; dist. judge, Matara, Oct., 1926.

WALTON, B. S.—B.A. (Oxon.); cadet, S. Sttlms. civ. serv., 29th Nov., 1913; ag. asst. dist. judge and 4th mag., Singapore, 23rd Mar., 1914; asst. censor, 23rd Nov., 1914; 2nd lieut., Singapore V.R., 28th Jan., 1916; ag. asst. supt. govt. monopolies, Singapore, 2nd Nov., 1916; supernumerary offr., cls. V., 25th Apr., 1917; 2nd lieut., R.G.A. (S.R.), 17th Dec., 1917; ag. asst. supt. govt. monopolies, Penang, 5th Aug., 1919; supernumy offr., cls. IV., 29th Nov., 1920.

WALTON, HON. SIR EDGAR HARRIS, K.C.M.G. (1911).—M.L.A., Cape, for Port Elizabeth, since 1898, and in Union parlmt. since 1910; treas. gen., Feb., 1904, to Feb., 1908; mem. of S. African National Convention in connection with S. Africa Bill, 1909; is proprietor and editor of Eastern Province Herald; high commr. for Union of S. Africa, London, 1921-25.

WALTON, SIR GEORGE O'DONNELL, Kt. Bach. (1925).—B. 1871; matriculated London Univ., 1891; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1893; practised at the bar, Barbados, 1894 to 1902; acted on several occasions as pol. mag. and judge of asst. at. of appeal, Barbados; apptd. pol. mag., dist. "F," Barbados, 9th Dec., 1902; lieut. of M.I., Barbados volunteers; ag. chief just. St. Lucia, 18th May to 25th Oct., 1908, and from 22nd March to 26th Oct., 1911; mag. and cor., dist. "C," St. Kitts, 11th Nov., 1911; ag. 2nd puisane judge, Leeward Is., 14th Nov., 1911 to 14th March, 1912, 11th Aug., 1913 to 26th Feb., 1914, and from 15th Dec., 1914 to 29th May, 1915; atty-gen., St. Lucia, 29th May, 1915; ag. adminstr. on several occasions in 1915, from 5th Sept. to 5th Oct., 1916, and from 18th July to 4th Sept., 1917; ag. chief just., from 7th Oct., 1916 to 17th Mar., 1919; atty-gen., B. Honduras, 18th Mar., 1919; ag. chief justice, 5th May to 5th Nov., 1919; ch. just., Grenada, 20th July, 1921; 1st puisane judge, Trinidad, 9th Apr., 1926.
WARD, C. E.—Asst. dist. commr., E.A.P., 7th Aug., 1909; dist. commr., Jan., 1918.

WARD, ESPIRE FRANCIS, M.D., B.Ch., B.A.O.—Ed. Epsom Coll. and Queen's Univ., Belfast; med. offr., W.A.M.S., Sierra Leone, 1912; trans., Gambia, 1918; trans., G. Coast, 1920; on special service for C.O., Oct., 1915 to Feb., 1916; temp. lieut. gen. list, Sept., 1914 to Mar., 1916; attached Cameroons Expedy. Force, 1914-15; priv. secy. to ag. gov., Gambia, 1918; author of "Beri-Beri, Etiology, Symptoms, and Treatment."

WARD, HOWARD HYLAND.—B. 1884; ed. pub. schls. and cent. business coll., Stratford, Ont.; ch. clk., operating dept., bd. of rly. comrs. for Canada, 1911; ch. clk. and asst., 1913; dep. min. of labour, 1923.

WARD, JOHN CORBET.—I.Y., S. Africa, 1900-02 (Queen's medal and 4 clasps, King's medal and 2 clasps); Transvaal civ. ser., 1902-04; dep. regisr., E. Africa Prot., 1906; regisr. of documents, 1906; staff offr., defence force, Kenya, Apr., 1920.

WARD, RIGHT HON. SIR JOSEPH GEORGE, Bart. (1911), P.C. (1907), K.C.M.G. (1901)—Mem., H. of R., N.Z., 1887-97 and 1899-1919 and since 1925; prime min., 1908-12; held the offices of col. sec., postmr.-gen. min. of telegraphs, rly.s., cust., marine, finance, defence, lands, agric. industries and commerce and public health at various times between 1893-1912; min. of finance, postmr.-gen. and min. of tels. in national ministry, 1915-19; rep. New Zealand at Impl. Confec., 1907 and 1911; mem. of Imp. Confec. on Naval and Mil. Defence, 1909; rep. New Zealand at the Coronation of H.M. King George V., 1911.

WARD, LEONARD KEITH, B.A., B.E.—Ed. Sydney Gram. Schl. and Brisbane Gram. Schl.; entd. univ. of Sydney, 1897, as Queensland govt. exhibitioner, and graduated in arts, 1900, and in engineering, 1903; in service of Broken Hill Proprietary Mining Co., till Oct., 1903; lecturer in geology, mineralogy, petrology and mining geology, Kalgoorlie schl. of mines, W. Australia, 1903-7; asst. govt. geologist and inspr. of mines, Tasmania, 1907-11; govt. geologist, S. Australia, Jan., 1912; also dir. of dept. of mines.
WARD, ROBERT PERCY.—B. 1868; ed. Wanganui Coll. Schl., N.Z.; New Zealand dept. of just., 1894; held various offices prior to 1921, in which year apptd. departl. inspr.; under-sec. of just., 1925.

WARD, WILFRED ARTHUR.—B. 1892; ed. privately and at Christ's Hosp.; served in France with Civil Service Rifles, 15th London Regt., as lance-corp.; 2nd lieut., 3rd Lancy. Fusillers, May, 1915; seconded to Machine Gun Corps, and served with them in France, Salonica and Palestine; M.C. at taking of Jerusalem and promoted capt.; cadet, F.M.S., Mar., 1920; govt. rice agt., Batang Padang, Sept., 1920; ag. 2nd asst. dist. offr., Tapah, Mar., 1921; ag. asst. dist. offr., Batang Padang, May, 1921; passed cadet, Jan., 1923; asst. dist. offr., Krian, Mar., 1922; offr., cls. V., Nov., 1919; ag. dist. offr., Ulu Kelantan, Apr., 1923; offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1923; ag. supt., lands, Kota Bharu, Feb., 1925; dist. offr., Port Dickson, Apr., 1925.

WARD, WILLIAM ERNEST FRANK, B.A., B.Litt.—B. 1900; ed. Mercer's Schl., London, Dulwich Coll. and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; open hist. schol., Lincoln Coll., 1918; 2nd cls. homs., B.A., mod. hist., 1922; B.Litt., 1923; Goldsmith's exhib., mod. hist., 1921; Ridley Hall, Cambridge; mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1924.

WARD, W. F.—Assoc. Roy. Schl. of Mines, Lond. (1876).—Analyst, etc., to the govt. of Tasmania, 1882; asst. to Dr. T. Percey and Prof. W. C. Roberts-Austen, etc., in chemical and metallurgical laboratories of Roy. Schl. of Mines, Royal Mint, and Normal Schl. of Science, S. Kensington (1876-80); special apptnt. to Potosi Gold Mining Co., Venezuela (1881); in charge of chemical testing of smoke-consuming appliances at exhibition, S. Kensington (1881).

WARDE, HARRY MONTAGU JOHN.—B. 1873; ed. at Epsom Coll., entd. Crown Agents' office, Sept., 1892; asst. head of miscellaneous dept., Oct., 1900; chief cashier, July, 1914; head of gen. stores dept., Dec., 1920 to Nov., 1921; head of pay and checking dept., 14th Nov., 1921; head of gen. stores dept., 1924.
WARDLE, D. S.—Temp. asst. treas., Kenya, June, 1920; acct., med. dept., Apr., 1921; asst. treas., July, 1923.
WARNE, OSWALD HORNBY.—B. 1891; ed. Rossal Sch., and Keble Coll., Oxford; lieut., South Staffordshire Regt.; asst. dist. comsnr., Ashanti, G. Coast, 29th Apr., 1914.

WARNER, BASIL HALE B.A.—B. 1868; ed. St. Edward's Schl., and Keble Coll., Oxford, served with C.M.R., 1891-95; schoolmaster, 1896-1900; served in S. African war, with Neshitt's Horse, 1900-2; clk. to Swaziland admin., July, 1902; ch. clk., June, 1903; dep. asst. comsnr., Swaziland, Dec., 1909; asst. comsnr., Umbombo dist., Apr., 1911; asst. comsnr., Mbabane dist., 1919.

WARNER, BROADRICK ASHTON.—B. 1888; ed. Winchester Coll.; asst. dist. comsnr., Uganda Prot., 1912; 1st grade administrative offr., 1923; late capt., 5th batt., City of London Regt. (T.F.); capt., Territorial Force Reserve.

WARREN, EDGAR HEBBERT.—Served with 28th I.Y., S. African war, 1902 (medal with 2 clasps); S. African constab. (civil), 1903-1908; lieut., Transvaal H.A., 1907; clk. in customs, Nyassaland Prot., 1908; offr. of customs, 1909; also immigr. offr., 1913; principal immigrn. offr., May, 1914; asst. contr., cust., Apr., 1920; ag. contr., cust., July, 1922.

WARREN, ERNEST, D.Sc. (Lond.)—Demonstrator of zoology at Univ. Coll., Lond., 1894; life mem. of convocation of the Univ. of Lond., 1895; asst. lecturer and museum curator at Univ. Coll., Lond., 1899; elected fellow of Univ. Coll., 1898; asst. prof. of zoology at Univ. Coll., 1900; mem. of the faculty of science, Univ. of Lond., 1902; dir. of Natal govt. museum, 1st Feb., 1903.
WARREN, FREDK. DRUMMOND.—B. 1881; stock inspr., Basutoland, 1914; sub.-inspr., Basutoland mounted pol., 1922; war serv., 1915-19.

WARREN, PHILIP DAVID, C.M.G. (1908); Assoc.Inst.C.E., F.R.G.S., F.Met.Soc.—B. 1851; asst. survr., Ceylon, 8th Feb., 1878; dist. survr., 1st Oct., 1879; office asst. to survr.-gen., 16th June, 1885; asst. survr.-gen., 26th Mar., 1897; survr.-gen., 23rd June, 1904; M.L.C., Ceylon, 1907-8; ret. 1910.

WARRINGTON, J. C.—B. 1887; educ. Marlborough and Trin. Coll. Camb.; B.A., 1908; served in France with R.E. (Signals) 1914 to 1918; twice mat. in desps.; French Croix de Guerre; asst. dist. comsnr., G. Coast, 1919; dist. comsnr., 1st Apr., 1924.

WATERMEYER, ERNEST FREDERICK.—B. 1880; ed. Graaff Reinet Coll., Stellenbosch Boys Schl., Bath Coll. and Caius Coll., Cambridge; maths. schol., Caius Coll., 1899; maths. tripos, 1902; law tripos, 1903; called to bar, Inner Temple, Apr., 1904; pres., income tax sp. ct., 1919; ag. puisne judge, Cape Prov., divn., sup. ct., 1920; puisne judge, C.P. divn., sup. ct., 1922.

WATERS, ALFRED JOHN, M.B.E. (1920), B.A. (Oxford and Cambridge).—B. 1875; entered Cape civ. serv., Apr., 1898; held various appts. in magis. branch; acted as priv. sec. to atty.-gen., and as ch. clk. to atty.-gen., solr.-gen. and crown prosecutor; asst. mag., Cape Town; asst. mag., Wynberg, 1907-12; trans. to native affrs. dept., 1915; seconded as crown pros. for S.W. Africa Prot. in mily. occupation, Oct., 1915, to Nov., 1919; prin. clk., native affrs. dept., Oct., 1917; mem., S.W. Africa concessions comsn., Dec., 1919; ch. clk., S.W. Africa Prot. admn., Oct., 1920; ag. sec. for the Prot., Aug. to Nov., 1919, and 1920, 1921 and 1922; ch. clk., dept. of just., Mar., 1923; joint translator of Imp. German criminal code into English; editor "Martial Law in S.W. Africa," "The Laws of S.W. Africa 1915-22."

WATERS, BERNARD DAVID.—B. 1879; telegraphist, Cape Colony, May, 1902 to Aug., 1905; postal asst., N. Rhodesia, Aug., 1905; ch. asst., Apr., 1912; sec., post office, May, 1913; ag. P.M.G., Jan., 1926 to May, 1926; dep. P.M.G., Nov., 1926.

WATKINS, OSCAR FERRIS, C.B.E. (1918), D.S.O. (1916).—Ed., Marlborough and All Souls, Oxford, B.A., M.A., 1910; served with Oxfordshire Lt. Infy. (43rd) during S. African War, Dec., 1899 to 1901 (Queen's Med. and 4 clasps); S. African constab., Dec., 1902 to Apr., 1904; col. sec.'s office, Transvaal, May, 1904 to Oct., 1907; sec. to Pretoria sewage comsn. and govt. laboratories coman.; asst. dist. comsnr., Br. E. Africa, 16th Jan., 1908; dist. comsnr., 1st Apr., 1914; seconded for Coast Land arbitrn., Sept., 1913 to Aug., 1914; seconded as intell. offr. to organise carrier transport D.A.D.T., E. African Forces, 1915; dir., mil. labour, 1916; ag. ch. native comsnr., Aug., 1920 to Aug., 1921; offl. mem., leg. coun., Aug., 1920 to Feb., 1922; mem., land tenure comsn., 1920; mem., central bd. of health, 1921; mem., native punishments comsn., 1921; chmn., labour bureau comsn., 1921.

WATSON, ANDREW D.—B. 1882; ed. common schls., Baddeck Acad., Dalhousie Univ., B.A., 1905; ent. civ. serv., Canada, dept. of insurance, Feb., 1906; actuary, 1914; assoc., Inst.of Actuaries, Lond., 1909; Assoc. Actuarial Soc. of America, 1913; Fellow, 1919; Contrib. to the jnl. of the Inst. of Actuaries, and the transactions of the Actuarial Soc.

WATSON, DONALD FRANCIS, O.B.E., (1926).—B. 1883; 2nd clk., admstr.'s office, Seychelles, 1899; dispensar and clk., med. dept., 1900; clk. to local bd. of health, quarantine and cemetery comteee, 1900; man., govt. pharmacy, 1901; ag. supt., lunatic asylum, May to Oct., 1920; ag. treas., collr. of customs, chmn., marine bd., and chmn., profiteering comteee., July, 1920 to Apr., 1921; contr., local clearing office, June, 1921; treas., postmr., collr. of cust., comsnr. of income tax, man. of savings bank, registr. of shipping, Apr., 1923.

WATSON, ERNEST CHARLES.—B. 1874; ed. at Shrewsbury; called to the bar, Middle Temp., 1897; 3rd cls. res., N. Nigeria, July, 1902; ag. atty.-gen., Sept. to Nov., 1904; atty.-gen., Nov., 1905; ag. ch. just., Mar. to Oct., 1906, May to Aug., 1907, Feb. to Apr., 1908, Mar. to Sept., 1909; puisne judge, Gold Coast, Jan., 1912; ag. ch. just., Sept. to Dec., 1915 and Mar. to June, 1917; senr. puisne judge and ag. ch. just., May, 1920; puisne judge, S. Stltnta. and judicia comsnr., F.M.S., June, 1920.
WATSON, ERNEST SIBBLES, B.Sc. Tech.—B. 1902; ed. Cent. High Schl., Manchester and Coll. of Technology, Manch.; B.Sc., Tech., 1923; asst. mast., Achimota, Gold Coast, 1926.

WATSON, HERBERT GORDON, I.S.O. (1922).—Clerk in governor of Zululand's office, 1895; acted as sec. for Zululand at different periods in 1896; taken over by the Natal govt. on the annexation of Zululand to Natal and apptd. clerk in the governor's office on 30th Dec., 1896; transferred to the Cape Civil service and apptd. clerk, governor's office 6th March, 1901; chief clerk, 1st July, 1904; performed duties of clerk of the exec. coun. from 16th Apr., 1904, to 22nd Apr., 1907; ag. priv. sec. to the offr. adminstrg. the govt., Major-General E. S. Brook, C.B., from 13th July, 1904, to 13th Feb., 1905; clerk of exec. coun., 22nd Apr., 1907; priv. sec. to Maj.-gen. Sir Henry Sobell, K.C.V.O., 15th Dec., 1909, to 30th May, 1910; asst. clerk of the exec. coun. of the Union of S. Africa, 31st May, 1910; sec. to prime min. and clerk of exec. coun., 1st Feb., 1916; clerk of the exec. coun., Union of S. Africa, 1922; sec. to prime min. and clerk, exec. coun., 1924.

WATSON, J., M.A., B.Sc.—B. 1883; ed. George Heriot's Schol. and Edinburgh Univ., M.A., 1902, B.Sc., 1904; European mast., Raffles Institution, Singapore, 1906; science mast., 1910; inspr. of schls., Pahang, F.M.S., 1915; headmast., Anderson schl., Ipoh, 1920; inspr., of schls., Penang, 1921; ch. inspr. of English schls., S.S. and F.M.S., 1923.
WATSON, P. C. M.—Treasur. asst., E.A.P., May, 1914; senr. asst. treas., Kenya, Jan., 1922; ag. dep. treas., Nov., 1922 to Mar., 1924.

WATSON, HON. ROBERT.—B. 1868; M.E.C. and M.H.A., Newfoundland; ed. at St. John's and Rugby; elected for Trinity, 1897, 1902, 1908 and 1909; mem. exec. coun.; col. sec., and partly head of depts. of post office, telegraphs, educ., rly., pub. health and pub. charities, 1909; mem. exec. comttee., Diocesan Synod, 1910; ag. premier, 1910; attended Imp. Conf. and the Coronation, 1911; cashier, govt. savings bank, 1913; hon. priv. sec. to gov., 1916; asst. priv. sec. to gov., 1918; hon. corres. sec., Royal Col. Inst.

WATSON, WILLIAM LAW, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1883; ed. Grammar Schol. and Robert Gordon's Coll. (Abdn.); loco. dept., G.N.S.R., 1899; loco. dept., G.W.R., 1905; engnr. design dept., C.A., 1912; min. of munitions, 1915; dep. head, engnr. design dept., C.A., 1919.

WATT, ALEXANDER STUART.—B. 1884; ed. Edin. Acad., Edin. Instit., Blair Lodge and Edin. Univ.; writer to the signet, 1909; capt., 5th Batt. Royal Scots; asst. dist. commr., G. Coast, 29th Apr., 1914; ag. dist. commr., Dec., 1915; attached G. Coast Regt., 1915; with E. Africa Exped. Force, July, 1916.

WATT, SIR THOMAS, K.C.M.G. (1912); C.M.G. (1906).—B. 1857; ed. at priv. schls. and Glasgow Univ.; admtd. a sol. to Scottish cts.; practised in Natal; served as subaltern in Imperial L.I., 1900; apptd. capt. and offr. commdg. Newcastle Town Guard, and ment. in desps.; M.L.A., Natal, 1901-1910; formerly min. of just. and educ., Natal; min. of posts and telegraphs and pub. wks., Union of S. Africa, 1912; min. of pub. wks. and interior, 1916; min. of pub. health, 1919; min. of posts and telegraphs and of public wks., 1921-24.

WATT, Rt. Hon. Wm. Alexander.—P.C. (1920); M.H.A.; treasur. of Victoria Jan., 1909; premier and treas., 1912, and again in Dec., 1913; resig. premiership to enter H. of R., C. of A., to which he was elected in Sep., 1914; min. for works and rlys., 1917; ditto, from 10th Jan., 1918; treasur., 27th Mar., 1918; min. for trade and cust., 13th Dec., 1918; acting prime min., 1919; resig. office, 1920; speaker, H. of R., 1923.

WATTS, BERTRAM T.—B. 1883; ed. Wellingborough Schol. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge, B.A., F.R.G.S.; inspr., Egyptian govt. surver., 1905-06; inspr., Egyptian govt. survey, 1912; on sp. surveys in the Sudan for irrign. wks.; dir. sp. irrign. surveys, Mesopotamia, for Turkish govt., 1908-11; asst. dir. surveys, Cyprus, 1912-20; ag. regiar. gen., 1913, 1918, 1919 and 1920; ag. Br. del. of Evcaf., 1919 and 1923; regiar. gen., Cyprus, 1920; passed in mod. Gk. and Turkish.

WATTS, CLIVE.—2nd lieut., Northumberland Fusiliers, Sept., 1915; lieut., July, 1917; capt., June, 1918; ment. in desps., and wounded, European War; acctnt., treasur., Cyprus, 1920; acctnt. to pub. custodian and Cyprus contr. in addition, Apr., 1921 to May, 1923; passed exam. in Greek, 1921; ag. asst. treas., June to Nov., 1924 and May to Oct., 1925.

WATTS, SIR FRANCIS, K.C.M.G. (1917), C.M.G. (1904), D.Sc., F.C.S., F.I.C.—B. 1859; analytical chemist, Antigua, Jan., 1889; mem. legis. couns., 1897; analytical and agricultural chemist, Jamaica, May, 1898; Leeward Is., May, 1899 (in connection with the Imperial dept. of agriculture for the W. Indies); office subsequently made that of govt. chemist and supt. of agric. for the Leeward Is.; mem. exec. couns., Leeward Is., Oct., 1899; mem. exec. couns., Antigua, Dec., 1903; Imperial commr. of agric. in W. Indies, 1909; prin., W. Indian Agril. Coll., 1921; prin., emeritus, 1924; author of numerous reports and papers on W. Indian agriculture, etc.

WEATHERHEAD, ARTHUR EVELYN.—B. 1880; served in S. African constab., 1900-1907; S. African war, Queen's medal with 5 clasps; dist. supt., Uganda police, 1908; asst. dist. commr., Uganda Prot., 4th Apr., 1910; ag. dist. commr., 1914; dist. commr., 1919; ag. prov. commr., 1924; lab. commr., Uganda, 1st Jan., 1926.
WEAVING, L. A.—Asst. dist. commr., Kenya, June, 1921.
WEBB, CECIL RICHARD, O.B.E. (1926), M.C.—Asst. traffic manager, Uganda rly., 1914; traffic man., Tanganyika Territory rly., 1st Oct., 1919; gen. man., govt. rlys., Sierra Leone, 20th June, 1923.

WEBB, AMBROSE HENRY.—B. 1882; ed. Clifton Coll., Bath Coll. and Trinity Coll., Oxford, 1899 (class. school); 1st Victoria prizeman and John Brooke schol., King's Inn, Dublin, 1908; called to bar, 1909; K.C., 1920; pres., dist. ct., Samaria, 1921.

WEBBER, ARTHUR FREDERICK CLARENCE.—B. 1873; ed. Merton Coll., Oxon., B.A., 1896; called to bar, Inn. Tem., Feb., 1896; ag. stip. mag., 1898 and March to Oct., 1900, Br. Guiana; stip. mag., Essequibo, Oct., 1900; ditto, Whim Corentyne, July, 1902; ditto, Berbloe judicial dist., 1904; ag. solr.-gen., Nov., 1906, to Jan., 1907, and in July, 1909; puisane judge, S. Nigeria, Sept., 1909.

WEBBER, OSCAR WM.—B. 1871; clk. ast., prisons office, B. Guiana, 1887; 5th cls. clk., P.O., 1889; transfed to secretariat, 1896; 4th cls. clk., 1896; 3rd ditto, 1899; 2nd ditto, 1901; 1st cls. clk., 1910; prin. clk., 1917; 2nd ast. col. sec., 1920; ag. commr., Montserrat, Oct., 1919 to May, 1920; sec. to bd. of agric., 1901-1910; mem. of bd. of agric., 1910; priv. sec. to gov., Feb.-Aug., 1911, and from Sept., 1914 to Aug., 1915; ag. asst. col. sec., May, 1920 to Oct., 1921 and from June to Dec., 1924; asst. P.M.G., Jan., 1925; ag. P.M.G., Jan. to Sept., 1926.

WEBSTER, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1889; asst. polit. offr., German E. Africa provisional admstrn., 20th Oct., 1916; adminstr. offr., 1st grade, Tanganyika Territory, 1st Feb., 1922; ag. senr. comsnr., Mwanza dist., Feb. to Nov., 1923; ag. senr. comsnr., Dar-es-Salaam dist., Jan. to Dec., 1925; dep. prov. comsnr., 1st Jan., 1926; ag. prov. comsnr., E. Prov. 2nd Mar., 1926; offl. mem., leg. coun., Dec., 1926.
WEBSTER, G. W., M.B.E. (1913).—Ed. at Rossall; served with Yeomanry in S. Africa, 1900-1901; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 16th Nov., 1901; 2nd cls. res., 23rd Aug., 1909; 1st cls. res., 1918.
WEBSTER, H. G.—Asst. treas., Kenya, July, 1920; asst. audtr., Kenya, Apr., 1923.

WEDDUMBURN, MAXWELL MACLAGAN.—B. 1883; ed. George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ. (M.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Dec., 1906; pol. mag., Point Pedro and Chavakachcheri, Apr., 1908; Matara, Aug., 1909; Kurunegala, June, 1909; asst. land settm. offr., Jan., 1911; ag. addtl. asst. col. sec. in addition to own duties, July, 1912; ag. asst. col. sec., July, 1912; asst. land settm. offr. and special offr. under "Waste Lands Ordoe," Aug., 1912; asst. censor, Aug., 1914; asst. land settm. offr. and special offr. under "Waste Lands Ordoe," Oct., 1914; asst. censor, Nov., 1914; asst. govt. agt., Nuwara Eliya, Sept., 1915; asst. settm. offr., July, 1920; ag. settm. offr., Mar. to Nov., 1921 and from Apr., 1922 to June, 1924; rubber contr., Mar., 1925; govt. agt., N.C. Prov., Sept., 1926.

WEERAKOON, WILLIAM AUGUSTUS.—B. 1878; cadet, local div., Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1899; off. asst. to govt. agt., Ratnapura, Sept., 1900; Batticaloa, Nov., 1901; Kurunegala, May, 1902; Jaffna, Mar., 1904; Ratnapura, May, 1904; pol. mag., Matara, Jan., 1908; Point Pedro, Aug., 1908; addtl. pol. mag., Kurunegala, N.W. Prov., Jan., 1911; pol. mag., Panadure, Nov., 1913; pol. mag., Negombo, June, 1916; pol. mag., Jaffna, Aug., 1915; office asst. to govt. agt., Prov. of Sabaragamuwa, Dec., 1916; pol. mag., Puttalam, Sept., 1918; office asst. to govt. agt., W. Prov., July, 1920.

WEIGALL, LIEUT.-COL. SIR WILLIAM ERNEST GEORGE ARTHURBALD, K.C.M.G. (1920).—B. 1874; ed. Wellington Coll., Royal Agric. Coll. (gold medallist); major (ret.), 3rd Batt. Northants Regt.; served in S. Africa, 1902; M.P. (U.), Hornostile Div., Lincs., 1911-1920; capt. Lincolnshire Yeomanry, and lieut.-col. and inspr. Q.M.G. services, Eastern Comd., 1914-17; mem. of national salvage council; dep. chmn., surplus govt. property disposal bd.; mem. of select comtee. on national expenditure; mem., national agric. coun.; pres. agric. organisation soc.; dep. chmn., federation of county agric. comtees.; J.P., Kent and Lincs.; gov., S. Australia, 27th Mar., 1920; assumed govt., 9th June, 1920; resign., 1922.

WEISBECKER, ARNOLD.—C.I.K., census dept., Cape Prov., 17th Apr., 1891; P.W.D., 29th Feb., 1892; admstr.'s office (Cape Prov.), 1st July, 1910; ch. clk., 1st Apr., 1911; ch. local govt. inspr. and ch. clk., 1st Apr., 1919; prov. seoy. and ch. local govt. inspr., 1st Sept., 1922.

WEISBERG, HYMAN, B.A. Cantab.—B. 1890; 1st cls. math. tripos.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1914; passed final exam. in Malay, June, 1917; ag. 2nd asst. dist. offr., Batang Padang, June, 1917; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kinta, May, 1919; asst. dist. offr., Klang, Nov., 1919; dist. offr., Jelebu, May, 1920; dist. offr., Kuala Langat, May, 1922; mag., Seremban, July, 1922; ag. state treas., Perak; Dec., 1922; ag. dist. offr., Upper Perak, July, 1924; ag. state treas., Perak, Aug., 1924; temp. ag. chmn., sany. bd., Larut and Matang, in addn., May, 1926.
WELBY, H. R.—Asst. dist. comsnr., E. Africa Prot., Nov., 1911; dist. comsnr., Kenya, Jan., 1920.
WELCH, H. H. V.—M.R.C.S., L.C.R.P., med. offr., E.A.P., Oct., 1913; res. surg., European Hosp., Nairobi, Kenya, Aug., 1920.
WELCH, THOMAS BURGES, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., M.R.B.S., D.T.M. & H.—B. 1881; med. surp., Chacachacare leper settlement, Trinidad, 23rd Sept., 1925.

WELLINGTON, BISHOP OF, RIGHT REV. THOMAS HENRY SPOTT, M.A., D.D.—Ed. Trin. Coll., Dublin; Hebrew prize, 1877; B.A., 1878; (Respondent) 1887; Hebrew Testamur, 1879; Divinity Testimonial (1st cls.) and Church Formularies Prize, 1879; M.A., 1882; B.D. and D.D. (jure dignitatis), 1911; deacon, 1879; priest, 1880; curate of Holy Trinity, Kingston-on-Hill, 1789-1832; St. John the Evangelist, Waterloo Road, 1832-1886; vicar of St. Barnabas, Auckland, 1886-1891; examining chaplain to Bp. of Auckland, 1888-1891; vicar of St. Paul's, Wellington, 1892-1911; examining chaplain to Bp. of Wellington, 1892-1911; consecrated Bp. of Wellington, 6th June, 1911.

WELLINGTON, ARTHUR ROBARTES, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. and H. and D.P.H. (Camb.).—B. 1877; med. offr., Sarawak, Sept., 1905; med. offr., port health office and med. offr., quarantine station, Singapore, 10th Sept., 1908; health offr., Selangor, Negri Sembilan and Pahang, 13th May, 1909, health offr., F.M.S., 1st Jan., 1911; senr. health offr., F.M.S., 5th Mar., 1919; malaria res. offr. in addn., May, 1926; ag. prin. med. offr., F.M.S., Apr., 1926.

WELMAN, CHARLES WELLESLEY.—B. 1878; ed. Christ's Hosp.; open scholarship, Pemb. Coll., Oxon., 1897; 1st cls. hon. class. mods., 1899; 2nd cls. hon. lit. hum., 1901; M.A. Oxon., 1907; bar final exam., 1909; called to the bar, Middle Temple, Jan., 1911; enlisted in 130th co. I.Y. and served in S. Africa (Queen's medal, 2 clasps), 1902; educ. dept., Transvaal, Nov., 1902, to 1906; sec., Transvaal Rep., Govt. Assoc. and Transvaal National Assoc., 1906-1907; in office of agt.-gen. for the Transvaal in London, 1907-1909; asst. dist. comsnr., Gold Coast, 1909; asst. col. sec., 1913; dist. comsnr., 1917; senr. asst. col. sec., 1917; ag. comsnr., Eastern Prov., Jan. to Apr., 1918; dep. pol. mag., Acora, Aug. to Sep., 1918; ag. chief asst. col. sec., May to Dec., 1919; ag. comsnr., Central Prov., from Dec., 1919; dep. sec. for native affairs, 1920.

WELSH, ALEXANDER SIMPSON.—B. 1881; Cape civ. serv., 1889; B.A. (Cape), 1904; LL.B. (Cape), 1907; professional assist. to crown prosecutor, Johannesburg, 1914; prof. asst. to atty.-gen., Cape, 1918; crown prosecutor, Johannesburg, 1921; law adviser, 1926.

WELSH, WILLIAM THOMSON.—B. 1873; clk. to mag., Cape Town, Nov., 1892; Bedford, Dec., 1892; Kimberley, Jan., 1894; Klipdam, April, 1895; Barkly West, July, 1895; Beaconsfield, Jan., 1896; Prince Albert, Mar., 1896; Jansenville, May, 1896; asst. regitr., high ct., Kimberley, July, 1896; clk. to mag., Victoria East, Aug., 1900; asst. rec. mag., Paarl, Aug., 1901; King Williams Town, Aug., 1904; Middledrift, Aug., 1907; res. mag., Libode, July, 1908; Mganduli, May, 1919; Kokstad, Feb., 1916; ast. chief mag., Transkeian Territories, and res. mag., Umtata, Apr., 1919; ch. mag., Transkeian Territories, Sept., 1920.
WERTH, ALBERTUS JOHANNES.—B. 1888; ed. Victoria Coll., Stellenbosch; teacher at Grey Coll. Schl., Bloemfontein, 1911-19; M.P. for Kroonstad, 1921-26; admstr., S.W. Africa, Apr., 1926.
WESSELS, W. G. W.—B. 1878; ed. Kroonstad and Stellenbosch; organising inspr. of schls., O.F.S., 1912; inspr. of schls., 1914.

WEST, CAPT. J. H.—Ed. Middleton Coll. and Dublin Univ.; 2nd lieut., R.G.A. (S.R.), 1915; lieut., Aug., 1917; capt., Apr., 1918; France, 1917 to 1919; demob., May, 1919; French Croix de Guerre (with star); ast. dist. comanr., G. Coast, Sept., 1919.

WESTBROOK, MAJOR TOM, M.C.—Ed. Askes and Ratcliffe; served in S. African campaign, 1900-02; stock inspr., S. Africa, 1902-07; 2nd lieut., Hants T. Carabiniers, lieut., Hants Regt., major, Tank Corps; awarded M.C., 1917; ch. recruiting offr., W. Africa Chamber of Mines, Gold Coast, 1920-21; inspr., live stock, vety. dept., Gold Coast, 4th Jan., 1922.

WHEAR, JOHN FREDERICK.—B. 1867; ed. at public schools and at Prince of Wales Coll., P.E. Island; admitted as a barrister and attorney-at-law, 1890; served for four years as a city councillor in Charlottetown; elec. to the legis. assembly in 1900; as a mem. of the exec. coun. was associated with the late premier A. Peters and former lieut.-governor Rogers as delegates from the P.E.I. govt. for the settlement of provincial claims against the federal govt.; apptd. postmaster of Charlottetown, 1904; post office inspr., 1st Apr., 1911.

WHEATLEY, MAJOR H. H., O.B.E., M.C.—Served with Royal Engineers in France, 1914-19 (twice ment. in despa.); ast. to ch. engr. and sec. to govt., public wks. dept., Iraq, 1920; ast. adviser to min. of communications and wks., 1st Jan., 1921; adviser to min. of communications and wks., 9th Apr., 1922.

WHRBELL, CHARLES JOHN.—B. 1887; joined Royal North West Mounted Pol., Canada, Dec., 1912; proceeded overseas with 1st Canadian contingent, Sept., 1914; joined Irish Guards, Oct., 1914; transf. to 1st Guards Brig. Machine Gun Coy., Sept., 1915; served throughout Great War with Guards Divn., 1914-19; "1914-15" Star, Victory and Gen. Serv. Meds., Meritorious Serv. Med., Croix-de-Guerre (Belgian), ment. in desps., Apr., 1917; rejoined R.N.W. M. Pol., Canada, May, 1919; ast. comdt., pol., Bahamas, 21st Apr., 1921; ast. comdt., June, 1921 to Apr., 1923 and from May to Dec., 1924; comdt. of pol. inspr. of prisons, provost marshal and ch. of fire dept., 20th Aug., 1926.

WHEELER, LEONARD RICHMOND, B.A., B.Sc. (Lond.).—B. 1888; science master, Letchworth Schl., 1909-12; Gram. Schl., Antigua, 1912-14; entered col. serv. as science master, Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad, Dec., 1914; coman. in 1st batt., B.W.I.R., from 1st Trinidad contingent, Sept., 1915; pilot, R.A.F., 1917; served in France and Salonica, 1916-19; returned to Trinidad, Jan., 1920; comanr. for Boy Scouts, Trinidad, 1920; transf. to S.S. and F.M.S. as European mast., Nov., 1921; science and maths. mast., Malay Coll., Kuala Kangsar, Dec., 1921; vice-prin., Sultan Idris Training Coll., Tanjong Malim, Aug., 1924; ast. inspr., schla., Peak, July, 1926; inspr., schla., Malacca, Sept., 1926; author of "Flora of Antigua," "Scouting in the Tropics," etc.
WHEELER, CAPT. RICHARD CHAMBERLAIN, M.B.E., M.R.C.V.S.—Veterinary offr., Kenya, July, 1913; senr. veterinary offr., Apr., 1920.

WHEELWRIGHT, CHARLES APTORPE, C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1873; mag.'s clk., Eshowe, Feb., 1890; clk. and interpr., Lower Umfolozi, Aug., 1892; office of res. comanr., and chf. mag., Mar., 1894; ag. ast. mag., Umlazi, Jan., 1896; ag. mag., Ingwavuma, Dec., 1897; mag., Mahlabatini, Apr., 1898; nat. comanr., Zoutpanberg, June, 1902; mag., July, 1907; Pieterburg, Aug., 1912; chf. nat. comanr., Natal, Mar., 1916.

WHISKARD, GEOFFREY GRANVILLE, C.B. (1923).—B. 1886; ed. St. Paul's Schl. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; 1st cls. mods., 1907; 1st cls. lit., hum., 1909; ent. H.O., 1911; ast. priv. sec. and subsequently priv. pvt. sec. to successive secs. of state, 1915-20; ast. sec., ch. sec.'s office, Dublin Castle, 1920-22; ast. sec., C.O., 1st Apr., 1924.
WHITCOMBE, SIDNEY F.—B. 1899; entered O.O., July, 1915; apptd., after compet. exam., ast. clk., C.O., 1917; on military service, Jan., 1918, to Apr., 1920; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920.

WHITE, ALEXANDER HAROLD, O.B.E., (1924).—B. 1889; ast. collr. (now ast. dist. comanr.), Zanzibar, 27th Dec., 1911; ag. 2nd ast. sec. and clk. of coun., 14th June, 1915; ag. 1st ast. sec., 7th Oct. to 28th Dec., 1915; ag. mag., His Highness' ct. for Zanzibar, 1918 (Brilliant Star, 4th cls., July, 1919); ag. dist. comanr., 21st Feb. to 20th July, 1919; ast. sec., German E. Africa, provisional admin., July, 1919; dist. political offr., Tanganyika Territory, 1st Apr., 1920; ag. mag., Dar-es-Salaam, Apr. to July, 1922; seconded for sp. serv. as Br. comanr., Anglo-Belgian bdy. comanr. (Ruanda-Urundi), Aug., 1922 to Aug., 1925.

WHITE, ARTHUR V.—B. 1871; ed. Woodstock Pub. and High Schs., and Univ. of Toronto (M.E.); engr. with several prominent firms; lect. in median. drawing and machine design, Toronto Tech. Schl.; later with dept. of pub. wks., Can.; consulting engrnr., Coman. of Conservation, Can., until its abolition, 1921; consulting engrnr., representing Can. to the internat. joint coman., Lake of the Woods investigation under the boundary waters treaty, 1911; consulting engrnr. to Hydro-electric Coman., Ontario, 1922; has written extensively on water power, elec. development and fuel supply.

WHITE, JAMES, F.R.G.S., Grad. R.M.C., Kingston, Canada.—B. 1863; entd. civ. serv., Canada, 1884; geographer and ch. draughtsman, geog. surv., 1894; ch. geographer, dep. of Interior, 1899; sec., Comanr.of Conservation, 1909, and sec. and ohmn. until its abolition, 1921; engaged on sp. work in connection with Labrador boundary dispute.

WHITE, WALLIS HARRY BRINSLEY.—Ed. at Bruton and Linc. Coll., Oxford; ast. master, St. Andrew's Coll., Grahamstown and Graaff Reinet Coll.; 1st class teacher's certif., Cape Colony; served during S. African War in Brabant Horse (Queen's medal and four clasps); inspr. of schla., O.R.C., 6th Jan., 1901; ch. ditto, 1st Feb., 1904; ag. dir. of educn., Aug., 1905, to Feb., 1906, and Apr. to July, 1907.

WHITE, W. ARTHUR.—Apptd. after open compet. exam. for home civil serv. to P.O., Jan., 1890; 2nd div. olk., acctnt.-gen.'s dept., July, 1892; olk., controller's office, Mar., 1898; supt. of registrtn., G.P.O., Singapore, May, 1903; ag. supt. of mails, Oct., 1903; acctnt., postal dept., F.M.S., 1906; ag. dir., posts and telegraphs, F.M.S., 19th May, 1910 to 27th Aug., 1911, and 13th May, 1915 to 2nd Jan., 1916; sen. astt. auditor, external audit, S. Stiltns., and F.M.S., 1st July, 1917; seconded as collr. of war tax, Singapore, July to Nov., 1917; dir., external audit, S. Stiltns., and F.M.S., 12th May, 1919; ag. audr.-gen., S. Stiltns., in addition, 29th May, 1920 to 10th Nov., 1921; audr.-gen., 14th Aug., 1923.

WHITE, Rt. Hon. Sir William Thomas, P.C. (1920); K.C.M.G. (1916), B.A.—B. 1866; ed. High Schls., Oakville and Brampton, Ont., Univ. of Toronto (B.A.), graduate of the Ontario law schl.; app'td. manager National Trust Co., Ltd., Toronto, 1899; subsequently general manager and vice-pres. of the same institution; mem. of the Privy Council for Canada and min. of finance in Mr. Borden's cabinet, Oct., 1911; ditto in National govt., Oct., 1917; re-elec. at g.e., Dec., 1917, for Leeds-Brockwood, resig. portfolio, July, 1919.
WHITE, W. V. C.—B. 1897; on mily. serv., 8th Sept., 1914 to 14th Dec., 1915; discharged through wounds, 17th Feb., 1916; ent. Miny. of Food, 18th Mar., 1918; appt. cler. offr., O.O., 24th Apr., 1922.

WHITEHEAD, Charles Basil.—Ast. supt. of pol., S. Stiltns., Sept., 1900; ag. second supt. of pol., Singapore, June to Sept., 1908, and from Sept., 1909, to Feb., 1910; astt. supt. of pol., Singapore, Feb., 1910, and again, Dec., 1910; ag. supt. of pol., Malacca, and ag. harbrm. in addition, June, 1910; ag. supt. of pol., Singapore, Dec., 1910; ag. supt. in charge, Prov. Wellesley, Oct., 1911; astt. supt. of pol., grade II, Jan., 1912; ag. supt. of pol., Penang, June, 1915; astt. supt. of pol., Prov. Wellesley, Sept., 1917.

WHITEHEAD, Frank Edred, O.B.E. (1926), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.) certif. Lond. Schl. of Trop. Med.—B. 1880; ed. St. Bartholomew's Hosp., London; late hon. surg. County Hosp., Huntingdon; med. offr., Sierra Leone, May, 1913; Cameroons Exped. Force, Aug., 1914 to Feb., 1916; med. offr., Somaliland, June, 1916; senr. med. offr., Apr., 1919; senr. med. offr., Nyasaland, July, 1922; ag. prin. med. offr., Dec., 1922; dir., Med. and san'y. services.
WHITEHEAD, Louis Harold.—B. 1871; ch. olk. to atty.-gen., Trinidad, 1st Apr., 1906.

WHITEHORN, Roger Herbert, B.A. Cantab.—B. 1887; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., 17th Nov., 1911; attached to col. sec.'s office, 13th Dec., 1911; attached to Colombo Kachcheri, Aug., 1912; ag. pol. mag., Gampola, Jan., 1913; office astt., Ratnapura Kach., Feb., 1914; pol. mag., Galle, May, 1914; 4th astt. col. sec., Sept., 1914; on military duty as astt. censor, Nov., 1914; ag. astt. govt. agt., Colombo, Apr., 1916; 4th astt. col. sec., Aug., 1916; ag. third astt. col. sec., Oct., 1918; priv. sec. to gov. in addition to his own duties, Nov., 1916; on military duty, 1918; extra astt. govt. agt., Colombo, Jan., 1920; ag. astt. govt. agt., Mannar, Feb., 1920; ag. priv. sec. to offr. admstg. govt., 5th-23rd Apr., 1920; pol. mag., Kandy, Sept., 1922; ag. astt. govt. agt., Kegalla, Mar., 1923; astt. govt. agt., Colombo, Feb., 1924; ditto, Puttalam and Chilaw, Dec., 1924.

WHITEHOUSE, Bertram Reginald.—B. 1891; ed. at King Edward's, Birmingham, and St. John's Coll., Camb.; B.A. 1913; cadet, S. Stiltns. civ. ser., Feb., 1918, and lent to Johore; attached to censor's office, Singapore, till Apr., 1918; granted permission to join H.M. Forces, Apr., 1918; attached to land office, Johore, Sep., 1919; ag. supt. of Indian immigr., Nov., 1919; ag. collr. of land rev., Johore Bahru, June, 1920; offr., cls. IV., 9th Jan., 1922.
WHITEHOUSE, Lieut.-Commdr. G. T., D.S.C., R.N.R.—Pilot, Kenya, Feb., 1921.
WHITTING, John Beery.—B. 1869; entd. public serv., S. Australia, 1873; sec., state children's dept., 1886; sec. and regis. of stock in ag.-gen.'s dept., London, 1908; ag. agent-gen., 1914-15.

WHITLEY, Michael Henry.—B. 1872; ed. at Blundell's Schl. and King's Coll., London; jun. offr., Selangor, 1896; dep. pub. pros., F.M.S., 1906; legal adviser, Johore, 1910; called to the Bar, Inner Temple, 1913; ag. legal adviser, F.M.S., 1916; puisme judge, S.S. and jud. comm., F.M.S., 1918; ag. senr. puisme judge, S.S., 1921; ag. puisme judge, Penang, 1st Jan., 1922; ag. senr. puisme judge, Penang, June, 1922; atty.-gen., S.S., 1925.

WHITTALL, Lieut.-Col. Percival Frederick.—B. 1877; ed. Felsted; joined army, 1896; lieut., Lincolnshire Regt., 1902; served in S. African war, 1899-1900 (Queen's medal and three clasps); lieut., G. Coast Regt., 1907; rec'd thanks of S. of S. for services during outbreak of yellow fever at Secondee, 1910; astt. dist. commnr. N. Territories, G. Coast, 1912; released for service in European War, 1915-1919 (D.S.O. and one bar, French Croix de Guerre, 1914-15 Star); dist. commnr., N. Territories, G. Coast, 1918.
WHITTING, Charles Edward Jewel.—B. 1900; ed. Haileybury and St. John's Coll., Oxford; supt., eduon. dept., N. Provs., Nigeria, 26th July, 1922.

WHYBRO, Charles, B.A., LL.B.—B. 1902; ed. Berkhamsted Schl., London Univ., Schl. of Economics, and Christ's Coll., Cambridge, 1920-23; economic tripes, pt. I, 3rd cls., 1922; law tripes, pt. II, 3rd cls., 1923; supt., eduon., Tanganyika Territory, 1926.

WICKRAMASINGHA, Sena Pandukabhaya, B.A., LL.B. (Cantab.)—B. 1899; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1922; astt. to Matara kach., Dec., 1922; ditto, Batticaloa kach., Apr., 1923; extra office astt., Anuradhapura kach., Jan., 1925; office astt., ditto, June, 1925; pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, Feb., 1926.

WICKREMESINGHE, Cyril Leonard.—B. 1890; cadet, local div., Ceylon civ. ser., Apr., 1912; attached to Badulla Kachcheri, Apr., 1912; Matara Kachcheri, Oct., 1912; office astt. to govt. agt., Uva, July, 1913; ag. pol. mag., Dandagamuwa, Nov., 1913; addl. pol. mag., Colombo, Negombo and Avisawella, June, 1916; pol. mag., Kalutara, Mar., 1917; pol. mag., Jaffna, Mar., 1920; ag. addnl. pol. mag., Colombo, Negombo, and Avisawella, Mar., 1921; addnl. astt. govt. agt., Colombo, May, 1921; astt. govt. agt., Mannar, Mar., 1923; dist. judge, Nuwara Eliya, Nov., 1923; astt. govt. agt., Puttalam, Oct., 1926.

WICKWAR, Arthur Joseph.—B. 1871; ed. at St. Cuthbert's gram. schl., Newcastle-on-Tyne; astt. survr., Ceylon, 1st Sept., 1889; dist. survr., 13th Mar., 1897; supt. of topographical surveys, 23rd June, 1904; supt. of application surveys, 15th Oct., 1910; supt. of surveys, waste land ordce., 23rd Mar., 1913; astt. survr.-gen., 1st Oct., 1914; deputy survr.-gen., 30th Nov., 1915; ag. survr.-gen., Jan.-Oct., 1920 and from Sept. to Dec., 1923; survr.-gen., Dec., 1923.

WIDDUP, CHARLES PONSONBY.—Clerical astt., treasy., B. Guiana, 1902; 6th cls. clk., lands and mines dept., 1903; ag. sub-inspr. of police, 1903; sub.-inspr. of police, 1904; qualified as musketry instr., Hythe, 1905; dist. inspr. of police, 1906; passed Hindustani exam., 1909; county inspr. of police, 1914; dep. inspr. gen., B. Guiana, 16th Dec., 1920.

WIGGINS, CLARE AVELING, C.M.G. (1923), M.R.C.S.(Eng.), L.R.C.P.(Lond.), Fellow, Entom. Soc.—Med. offr., E. Africa Prot., 4th Apr., 1901; med. offr., Uganda Prot., 4th Apr., 1908; dep. P.M.O., 1st Apr., 1911; ag. P.M.O., 18th May to 29th Nov., 1910, and 19th May to 26th Dec., 1913; D.A.D.M.S. (Mob.), 24th Oct., 1915, with rank of major; O.C. Uganda Native Med. Corps, and O.C. Uganda Bearer Corps, 17th Nov., 1915; ast. censor, 2nd Feb., 1916; off. censor, 12th Mar., 1916; P.M.O., Uganda.
WIGHTMAN, J. W. E.—Ast. dist. commr., Kenya, Apr., 1914; ag. res. commr., 1926.

WIGHTON, ALEXANDER RITCHIE, Mem., Transvaal Socy. of Acctnts.—Joined Impl. military rly., S. Africa, June, 1902; audit inspr., ch. acctnt.'s dept., 23rd June, 1903; acctnt., engrnr.'s dept., 1st Feb., 1904; ch. clk., ditto, 1st July, 1908; inspr., dept. of audr., S. African rly. and harb., 1st Apr., 1910; ch. inspr., ast. controller and audr.-gen.'s dept., Union of S. Africa, 1st Apr., 1912; ch. inspr. (rly. and harb.), cont. and audit office, 1st July, 1916.

WIGHTWICK, CLAUDE.—B. 1881; ed. at Tunbridge schl. and Pembroke Coll., Camb.; 3rd cls. class. tripod; astt. res., N. Nigeria, 18th Aug., 1906; 3rd cls. res., 1st Jan., 1913; title changed to 2nd cls. dist. offr., 1st Jan., 1914; 1st cls. ditto, 22nd Mar., 1919; res. 16th Oct., 1921; ag. senr. res., 10th Jan., 1923.

WIGLEY, WILFRID MURRAY.—Ed. Derby schl.; held various clerical appttns. under govt., St. Kitts-Nevis, Feb., 1895, to Apr., 1898; called to the bar, Mid. Temp., Jan., 1901; practised at bar of Leeward Is. for some years; ag. admstr., St. Kitts, puisne judge, Leeward Is. and atty.-gen., on various occasions; crown atty., St. Kitts-Nevis, 1918.

WILCOCKS, CARL THEODORUS MULLER.—B. 1861; ed. Godbolt's Commi. Acad., Graaff Reinet; represented Fauresmith including Jagersfontein and Koffyfontein in first Union parl.; M.L.A. for Winburg (O.F.S.), 1915-24; mem., rly. and harbrs. bd., 1924; pub. debt, commr., 1925.

WILKIE, DAVID.—Ed. at Christ's Hosp., London; S. African campaign, 1900-1; pay and acctng. branch, S.A.C., 1902; clk. to lieut.-gov., O.R.C., 1902; ag. priv. sec. to lieut.-gov., O.R.C., 1906; ch. clk., gov.'s off., 1907; clk. of exec. coun., 1909; gov.-gen.'s off., Union of S. Africa, 1910; sec. to tender bd., 1911; ag. contr. of supplies, 1916; govt. buyer, 1920.

WILKINSON, ALAN FREDERICK LAWDER (Lieut., R.N.V.R.)—B. 1892; ed. Royal Naval Colls., Osborne and Dartmouth, and Trin. Coll., Cambridge; war service (Navy), 27th Aug., 1914, to 7th June, 1919; gunnery offr. from July, 1918; ast. dist. commr., G. Coast, 9th July, 1919; ag. dist. commr., 10th Sept. to 8th Nov., 1919.

WILKINSON, ERNEST EDWARD, M.B.E. (1922).—B. 1874; apptd., after compet. exam., olk., 2nd div., civ. ser., and assigned to the bd. of agriculture, May, 1894; transf. to C.O., June, 1896; minor staff offr., ch. clk.'s dept., 1902; clk. to finance and concessions comm., 1916; clk. for legal instruments, Sept., 1921; staff offr., 1st April, 1922; librarian, 26th Feb., 1923.

WILKINSON, H.S.—Chk., highcomsr.'s office, S. Africa, 1899; clk., polit. sec.'s office, Transval, 1900; acctnt. to sec., Transvaal adminstr., 1901; clk., col. sec.'s office, 1902; controller of stores, educn. dept., 1902; acctnt. health dept., 1904; sec., civ. serv. bd., 1906; distributor of stamps, treasy., 1906; distributor of stamps, dept. of finance, Union of S. Africa, 1910; prin. clk., treasy., 1916; ch. clk., treasy., 1925.

WILKINSON, REGINALD WARREN HALE K.C. (1924, Gold Coast Col.).—B. 1883; ed. France, Germany, Leamington Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford, B.A. (hons.), 1904; M.A., 1911; barrister-at-law, Inner Temple, 1907; ast. dist. commr., Sierra Leone, 1909, admtd. to Sierra Leone bar, 1909; frequently acted as crown prosecutor, 1909-1911; ag. pol. mag., coroner and regir.-gen. in 1911 and 1913; J.P., Sierra Leone, 1911; ast. col. sec., 1912; acted as master of sup. ct. and as sol.-gen., S. Leone, 1913; senior crown counsel, Gold Coast, 1914; ag. solr.-gen., G. Coast, 1914; attached to legal adviser's dept., C.O., Apr.-May, 1915; solr.-gen., G. Coast, 28th Apr., 1915; ag. atty.-gen. and mem. exec. and legis. coun., Sept., 1915 to Mar., 1916; ag. puisne judge, G. Coast, Apl.-June, 1916; ag. atty.-gen., G. Coast, Nov., 1917 to Oct., 1918, Apr. to Oct., 1919, and from Oct., 1919 to Apr., 1920; puisne judge, G. Coast, 29th Mar., 1920; atty.-gen., 7th Apr., 1921; admtd. govt. of G. Coast, July-Dec., 1921.

WILKINSON, WALTER ALLERN.—B. 1872; ast. engrnr., P.W.D., F.M.S., 1902; ex. engrnr., grade III, 1906; ditto, grade II, 1908; ditto, grade I, 1914; lieut., R.E., 1915; capt., 1916; temp. major, 1916; served in France, 1916; state engrnr., Selangor, 1920.

WILLAN, HAROLD CURWEN, M.C., B.A., OXON.—B. 1896; ed. Kendal Grammar Schol. and Jesus Coll., Oxford; open exhib., Jesus Coll., Oxford; lieut., R.G.A.; M.C., 7th June, 1917; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1920; ag. 2nd ast. dist. offr., Tapah, May, 1921; ag. ast. dist. offr., Batang Padang, Jan., 1923; passed cadet, Jan., 1923; offr., cls. V., Oct., 1923; dist. offr., Jelebu, Dec., 1923; ast. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Aug., 1925; ag. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Oct., 1925; offr. ast. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Nov., 1925.
WILLAN, REGINALD, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P.—Ed. Oakham Schol. and Guy's Hospital; med. offr. to Baro-Kano rly., Nigeria, 1909-1911; joined West African med. staff, 10th Jan., 1912.
WILDON, MARGARET ISABEL, M.B.E. (1925).—B. 1877; matron, Basutoiland leper asylum, 1913.

WILLETT, THOMAS GRAHAM.—B. 1883; ed. Merchant Taylors and St. John's Coll., Oxford (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1906; ast. astt. to govt. agt., N.W. Prov., Aug., 1908; pol. mag., Chilaw, Dec., 1909; addl. pol. mag., Galle, Matara, Tangalla, Hambantota and Balapitiya, July, 1910; pol. mag., Jaffna, Jan., 1911; pol. mag., Kurunegala, Jan., 1913; ag. dist. judge, Kegalla, Aug., 1913; ast. land settm. offr., and special offr. under waste land order, June, 1914; spec. commr. in connection with the rice, June, 1915; ast. land settm. offr., Sept., 1915; on military duty, 1918; ast. govt. agt., Kandy, Feb., 1920; pol. mag., Point Pedro, May, 1923; asst. govt. agt., Kurunegala, Jan., 1923; ditto, Colombo, Dec., 1924.
WILLIAMS, A. D. J. B.—Med. offr., E.A.P., Aug., 1912; senr. med. offr., Kenya, Sept., 1923; ag. dep. dir., med. services, 1926.

WILLIAMS, CHARLES EDWARD, O.B.E. (1918), M.I.Mech.E.—B., 1873; ed. Llandovery Coll.; asst. wks. man., Vulcan loco. works, Lancs.; ch. inspr., N.Z. and W. Australian rly., 1898; entd. Crown Agent's office, 1900; asst. man., Robt. Stephenson's wks., Darlington, 1901; dep. ch. inspgr. engr., Crown Acts, 1904; staff capt. W.O., 1915; section dir., Miny. of Munitions, 1916; major, R.E., D.A.D., W.O., 1918; tech. head of engnr. inspection dept., C.A., 1919; visited Nigeria, 1920; Palestine and Cyprus, 1921; ch. inspgr. engr., 1922; mem. of coun., Inst. of Loco. Engrs.
WILLIAMS, D. L. GWINNE.—B. 1886; solr., sup. ct., Eng., 1914; served European War, 1915-18; ot. martial offr., 1918; staff capt. (legal), 1919; conveyancer and regiar. of titles, Uganda, 1923.

WILLIAMS, EDWARD BRIDGEWATER.—B. 1886; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1908; asst. dist. offr., Brusas, Mar., 1910; ag. asst. dist. offr., Pekan, July, 1911; ag. dist. offr., Dindinga, S.S., June, 1912; ag. asst. dist. offr., Larut, July, 1914; 2nd asst. dist. offr., Larut, Dec., 1914; asst. dist. offr., Kuantan, Aug., 1917; asst. dist. offr., Lower Perak, Sept., 1918; mag., Seremban, Jan., 1920; dist. offr., Tampin, Mar., 1920; dist. offr., Coast, Nov., 1920; ag. dist. offr., Kuala Pilah, Nov., 1921; collr., land rev., Seremban, Mar., 1922; ag. Br. res., Negri Sembilan, in addn., Aug., 1926; offr., ols. III, Nov., 1926; ag. collr., land rev., Seremban, Nov., 1925.

WILLIAMS, EDWARD LLOYD.—B. 1875; ed. at Brecon and Victoria Univ. (Univ. Coll., Liverpool); indentured for three years to the water engrnr. of Liverpool; student of the insttn. of civ. engrnr.; contractor's engrnr. on rly. construction, G.W.R.; res. engrnr. on waterworks construction, N. Wales county asylum; contractor's sub-agt. and engrnr. on rly. construction, G.W.R., served with 29th I.Y. (Denbighshire Hussars), South Africa, 1900-1; asst. engrnr. on rly. survey, Gold Coast and Ashanti, 1901-2; asst. sur. veyor of mines, G. Coast, 1902-3; ch. surveyor, 1904-5; contractor's agt., waterworks construction for Liverpool corporation, 1905-6; exec. engrnr., P.W.D., Northern Nigeria, 9th June, 1906; ag. dep. D.P.W., in 1908, 1910, 1911 and 1912.

WILLIAMS, ERIC TREGEAR.—B. 1890; ed. Blundell's schl. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; cadet, S. Stlitma. civ. ser., 28th Nov., 1913; attached to col. secs. office, 26th Dec., 1913; ag. supt., govt. printing office, 12th Mar., 1914; ag. dist. offr., Jasir, 1st Sept., 1915; dist. offr., Alor Gajah, 19th Oct., 1917; supernmy. offr., cls. IV, S. Stlitma., Nov., 1920; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec. "B," June, 1921; ag. 2nd asst. col. sec. "A," 5th Dec., 1921.
WILLIAMS, ERNEST HILLAS, B.A., Trinity Coll., Dublin.—B. 1899; cadet, Hong Kong, 1923.

WILLIAMS, FRANK LESLIE.—B. 1899; ed. Weymouth Coll. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge, (B.A.); 2nd lieut., R.G.A., 1918; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1921; ag. asst. sec. to res., Selangor, June, 1924; attd. to office of sec. for Chinese affrs., July, 1924; ag. asst. prot., Chinese, Perak, Nov., 1924; attd. for duty Chinese prot., Ipoh, July, 1925.
WILLIAMS, FRANK STANLEY.—B. 1894; ed. St. Paul's Schl., London; col. audt. dept., Feb., 1920; asst. audr., Tanganyika Territory, Apr., 1920; senr. asst. audr., July, 1924.
WILLIAMS, G. A., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.C.R.P. (Lond.)—Tempy. commission, R.A.M.C., 1915-19; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, May, 1921.

WILLIAMS, GILBERT NEVILLE, D.S.O., V.D.—Cape civ. serv., surv. office, 1892; dept. of agr., 1893; prin. clk., 1906; ch. clk., 1909; ch. clk., Union dept. of agr., 1912; ag. under-sec., 1919; confirmed, 1920; served, Bechuana land Rebellion, 1897 (med. and clasp); S. A. War, 1899-1900 (med. and three clasps); served with Union troops, S.W. Africa, 1914-15; A.A. and Q.M.G., 3rd divn., E. African Expedy. Force, 1917-19 (D.S.O., 3 times ment. in desps.); sec. for agr., 1st Aug., 1926.
WILLIAMS, HON. KENNETH STUART.—B. 1870; ed. Heretaunga Schl. and Christ's Coll., N.Z.; mem., N.Z. parl. since 1920; min. of pub. wks., 1926.

WILLIAMS, MYLES.—Clk.tores.comsnr., Bech. Prot., 1902; ch. clk. and regiar., 3rd Feb., 1906; ag. govt. sec., Nov., 1908, to May, 1909, and from Apr. to Oct., 1911; acctnt., Bech. Prot., Apr., 1912; passed Cape civ. ser. lower law exam., 1909; passed 2nd grade Sechuana exam., 1912; ast. res. mag., Apr., 1913; ag. res. mag., 3rd July, 1916; ag. ast. comsnr., N. Prot., 1915; ag. ast. comsnr., S. Prot., 1916; res. mag., 1920; finan. sec. master and regiar. of deeds, 1920.

WILLIAMS, O. G. R.—B. 1886; ed. Lansing Coll and Oxford; exhibitioner, Hertford Coll., 1906; 1st cls. mods., 1907; 1st cls. Lit. Hum., 1909; Aubrey Moore studentship, 1910; apptd., after compet. exam., clk. in sec.'s off., In. Rev., Oct., 1910; 2nd cls. clk., C.O., Mar., 1911; priv. sec. to Mr. (now Sir Arthur) Steel-Maitland, 4th June, 1916; priv. sec. to Mr. Hewins, 26th Sept., 1917; priv. sec. to Col. Amery, 14th Jan., 1919; 1st cls. clk., 8th Mar., 1919; prin., 1st Apr., 1920; ag. ast. sec., Feb., 1926.
WILLIAMS, PATRICK EDWARD WILLOWS.—B. 1904; ed. Shrewsbury Schl. and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; B.A.; supt., educon., Tanganyika Territory, 1926.

WILLIAMS, P. MILES.—B. 1890; ed. Pontypridd County Schl. and Ravensburg High Schl., Würtemburg, Germany; R.F.C., 1915-17; disabled; clk., Miny. of Labour, Bristol, Feb., 1918 to May, 1922; apptd. after compet. exam., cler. offr., C.O., 29th May, 1922; seconded to Empire Marketing Bd., 1926.

WILLIAMS, PHILIP STANHOPE.—B. 1889; ed. at Harrow (head of the schl. 1907-08) and at Caius Coll., Camb. (schol. and Sayer schol.), 1st cls. classical tripes, 1911; cadet, F.M.S., Dec., 1912; ag. 2nd asst. dist. offr., Tapah, Dec., 1913; ag. 2nd asst. dist. offr., Kuala Kangsar, Feb., 1915; passed cadet, June, 1915; offr. cls. V, Jan., 1916; ast. sec. to res., Perak, Feb., 1917; offr., cls. IV, Dec., 1919; ag. 2nd asst. dist. offr., Larut, June, 1920; ag. temp. as dist. offr., Batang Padang, July-Aug., 1920; ast. dist. offr., Larut, June, 1921; ag. dist. offr., Batang Padang, Dec., 1921; 2nd mag. Penang, Mar., 1925; ag. Br. advr., Perlis, May, 1925.

WILLIAMS, SIR RALPH CHAMPNEYS, K.C.M.G. (1907), C.M.G. (1901).—B. 1848; ed. at Rossall; explored parts of Patagonia and Terra-del Fuego, 1873-4; and Cent. Africas, 1883-4; head of civil intell. dept., Bechuana land expedit., 1884; author of "The British Lion in Bechuanaland"; Br. consul. offr., S. African republic, 1887; Br. agt. there (with letter of credence), 1888; col. treas., and mem. of sanitary comm., Gibraltar, 1890; also contract offr., 1891; and also capt. of the port, 1896; reed. certif. and med. from the Italian govt. for services in connection with the wreck of the "Utopia," Mar. 17, 1891; col. sec., Barbados, 1897; res. commnr., Bechuanaland Protec., 1901; govr., Windward Is., 8th Sept., 1906; govr., Newfoundland, 6th May, 1909; assumed govr., 6th Sept., 1909; ret.; 1913 volunteered for service in Brit. E. Africa, 1914; appt. pres. of 2nd line of defence, 8th Aug., 1914; invalided out of the force, 13th Apr., 1915; and read. off. thanks of the govr. and comdr-in-chief and of the G.O.C., published in "Command Orders."

WILLIAMS, R. GREGSON.—Ed. at Rossall Sch., Caius Coll., Camb.; B.A. 3rd cls. hona., Science Tripos; Royal Sch. of Mines, A.S.R.M., last cls. diploma; post-graduate course, Mysore goldfields, India; asst. res., N. Nigeria, 22nd Mech., 1911; ag. inspr. of mines, June, 1911; ag. chief survr., Sept., 1911; contr., mines, Tanganyika Terr., 1926.
WILLIAMS, THOMAS ALFRED.—B. 1875; accntg. clk., Basutoland, 1905; acctnt., 1913; finan. sec., 1916.
WILLIAMSON, E.—B. 1884; clk., G.P.O., Transvaal, Mar., 1903; treasy., Transvaal, July, 1904; senr. clk., Jan., 1919; prin. clk., Apr., 1921; sec., cabinet comte., on expenditure, 1922.

WILLINGDON, 1ST VISCOUNT (cr. 1924); 1st Baron of Ratton (cr. 1910); Freeman Freeman-Thomas, G.B.E. (1917); G.C.S.I. (1918); G.C.I.E. (1913).—B. 1866; A.D.C. to Lord Brassey when gov. of Victoria, 1895; M.P., Hastings, 1900-06; Bodmin divn. of Cornwall, 1906-10; jun. lord of treas., 1905-12; J.P.; gov. of Bombay, 1913-19; ditto, Madras, 1919-24; major, Sussex Imperial Yeomanry; lord-in-waiting to His Majesty; govr.-gen. of the Dominion of Canada, 1926.

WILLIS, HARRY GRAHAM.—B. 1875; ed. Marlborough Coll.; apptd. to N.E. Rhodesia, Oct., 1900; asst. native commnr., Jan., 1901; civ. commnr., Sept., 1903; native commnr., Nov., 1904; ret. owing to ill-health, Oct., 1906; rejoined as clk., mag.'s ct., Livingstone, N.W.R., Dec., 1907; native commnr., Oct., 1908; ag. asst. mag., N.R., Sept., 1912-Mar., 1913; asst. mag., Apr., 1913; ag. res. mag., Barotse dist., Sept., 1913-Mar., 1914; ag. mag., Nov., 1923-May, 1924; mag., 31st May, 1924; J.P.

WILLIS, MAURICE A.—B. 1900; ent. M.O.D., G.P.O., May, 1915; apptd., after compet. exam., asst. clk., Apr., 1918; on mil. serv., May, 1918 to Feb., 1919; cler. offr., 1st Jan., 1920; transf., P.O. stores dept., Mar., 1920; transf., C.O., May, 1924.

WILLIS, NORMAN EDWARD OMMANNEY.—B. 1872; ed. at Eton; entd. crown agent's office, 1st Jan., 1891; asst. cashier, 4th Jan., 1899; asst. acctnt., 1st July, 1902; acctnt. and dep. chief clk., 9th Jan., 1913; head of gen. dept. and ch. clk., 15th Nov., 1920.

WILLMOTT, ARTHUR CHARLES.—B. 1880; ed. Lancing Coll. and Aspatria Agric. Coll., Cumberland; asst. survr., Uganda Prot., Mar., 1907; dist. engnr., P.W.D., May, 1908; ag. asst., D.P.W., Apr.-Nov., 1913; engnr.-in-charge, Kampala Port Bell rwy. constr., Dec., 1913.
WILMOT, FREDERICK CHARLES, M.D., D.P.H., L.R.C.P.—Asst. health offr., dept. of pub. health, Union of S. Africa, 1913.

WILSON, BASIL MAYOR, M.B., Ch.B. (1901), D.P.H. (1911), M.D. (1911).—B. 1878; ed. Malvern Coll. (Faber Exhib.), and Univ. of Manchester; govt. med. offr., Fiji, 1904; dist. med. offr., Levuka, 1907; dist. med. offr., Ba., 1915; temp. lieut., R.A.M.C., Feb., 1918-19; served with B.E.F. in France; res. med. offr., col. hosp., Suva, M.O.H. and bacteriologist, Fiji, 1922; prin. med. offr., chmn., cent. bd. of health, mem., leg. coun., Br. Hond., 1923; suptg. med. offr., chmn., cent. bd. of health, chmn., quarantine bd., Jamaica, 1925.

WILSON, CAPT. CHARLES.—B. 1889; ed. Foyle Coll., Derry, and Trinity Coll., Dublin (sis., jun. and sen. ex., schol., sen. med., B.A.); cert. B., Dublin Univ. O.T.C.; cadet, F.M.S., 17th Jan., 1913; attd. Chinese prot. and contr. of lab. office, Kuala Lumpur, Aug., 1913 to May, 1914; asst. contr. of lab., Penang, 18th Dec., 1914; passed cadet, 9th Jan., 1915; on mily. serv., 2nd lieut., 6th K.S.L.I., 22nd July, 1915; Franco, 13th Feb., 1916; offr., cls. V., 7th May, 1916; lieut., 7th July, 1916; capt., 13th Feb., 1919; demob., 16th Mar., 1919; ment. in deep., dep. contr., lab., Seremban, 14th June, 1919; offr., cls. IV., 11th Jan., 1920; ag. sec. to res., N. Sembilan, is addn., during June, 1920; dist. offr., coast, in addn., 1st Sept., to 4th Nov., 1920; ag. dist. offr., Ipoh, 4th Dec., 1920; govt. rice agt., Kinta, in addn., 6th Dec., 1920; 2nd lieut. and adj., Malay Vol. Inf'y., Perak, 12th Apr., 1921; ag. ch. asst. dist. offr., Kinta, 1st July, 1922; emigrn. agt. in India, 1st Aug., 1922; ag. stamp and probate offr., Singapore, May, 1924; offr., cls. III., Jan., 1925; ag. dep. treas. (B), Singapore, in addn., Mar., 1926; ag. emigrn. commnr. for Malaya, Madras, Feb., 1926.
WILSON, C. J.—Medical effr., E.A.P., May, 1911; dep. dir., med. serv., Kenya, Sept., 1923; ag. dir., med. and sanry. servs., 1926.

WILSON, CHARLES LIVINGSTONE.—B. 1874; ed. Queen's Royal Coll., Trinidad; entd. civ. serv., Grenada, as 2nd clk., G.P.O., Aug., 1892; ag. ch. clk., 1893; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s office, Jan., 1894; ex. off. sec., gen. bd. of health and bd. of educn., 1897-1903; ag. ch. clk. to gov. of Windward Is., 1907-8; ch. clk., govt. office, St. Vincent, clk. of couns., man. of printing sub-dept., and sec., hurricane loan bd., 1907-11; ch. clk., col. sec.'s office, clk.of couns. and man., printing sub. dept., 1911-19; col. postmstr., Grenada, 1919; a dir. of the pub. offrs. guarantee fund, 1919; sec. of reception comte., visit of H.R.H. the Prince of Wales to Grenada, Sept., 1920.

WILSON, COLIN E. J.—Boarding offr. and clk., Br. Solomon Is., 25th June, 1913; ag. dist. offr., Guadalcanal, 18th Jan., 1915 and 2nd Apr., 1923; ag. dist. offr., Shortlands, 15th Nov., 1916; ag. collr., cust., 7th May, 1918; ag. treas. and collr., cust., 20th Apr., 1919 and 6th Dec., 1924; acctnt. and 1st clk., treas. and cust., 11th Oct., 1920; visiting dist. offr., 26th Mar., 1923; ag. dist. offr., E. Solomons, 2nd Apr., 1923; dep. commnr. for W. Pacific, 27th July, 1923; dist. offr., Santa Cruz, 10th Dec., 1923; ag. govt. sec., 6th Mar., 1925.
WILSON, ERNEST PIERRE FRANCOIS.—B. 1876; ed. Amersham Gram. Schl.; clk. acctnt-gen.'s dept., Admiralty, 1914-1922; cler. offr., C.O., 2nd Oct., 1922.
WILSON, FRED.—B. 1896; ed. Robert Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen and Aberdeen Univ., M.B. Ch.B., 1921; surg., sub-lieut., R.N.V.R., 1915-19; asst. med. supt., Cent. Mental Hosp., F.M.S., Sept., 1924.
WILSON, GEORGE C.B. (1899).—Sub-comsnr., Uganda Prot., 30th Aug., 1894; dep. comsnr., 1st Apr., 1902.

WILSON, G. GORDON.—Cadet, S.S., Nov., 1899; ag. 4th mag., Sing., Apr., 1902; passed final exam. in Chinese, Aug., 1902; ag. supt., money order branch and savings bank, Singapore, May, 1903; ag. astt. postm.-gen., Penang, 2nd Oct., 1903; supt., money order branch and sav. bank, Sing., but cont. to act in postm.; astt. postm.-gen., Singapore, 15th Feb., 1908; ag. astt. postm.-gen., Penang, Apr., 1909; ag. astt. supt., govt. monopolies, Penang, Jan., 1911; astt. postm.-gen., Penang, 1st Jan., 1912, but still ag. astt. supt., govt. monopolies, Penang; ag. supt., govt. monopolies, Singapore, Mar., 1912 to Mar., 1913; ag. postm.-gen., S. Stilts., Mar., 1914; promoted to Class III., May, 1915; ag. supt., govt. monopolies, Feb., 1916; offr., cls. II., Jan., 1919.

WILSON, GEORGE FREDERICK.—B. 1886; ed. King Edward's Schl., Bath; apprentice, pub. wks. dept., Cyprus, 1903; clk., ch. sec's. off., 1904; ag. astt. sec., ch. sec's. off., Jan. to July, 1919 and June to Oct., 1920; collr., cust., Sept., 1919; ag. ch. clk., ch. sec's. off., Sept. to Dec., 1919; ag. comsnr., Aug., 1923; ch. collr., cust. and excise, July, 1924; regtr., Br. ships, regtr., Cyprus ships, inspr., fisheries; ag. regtr., trade marks, ag. regtr., companies, ag. regtr., patents, July, 1924; mem. leg. coun., 1925.
WILSON, GEORGE HAROLD SUMNER.—B. 1887; ed. Chigwell Schl., Essex; astt. dist. comsnr., G. Coast, 18th Aug., 1914; dist. comsnr., 1st Jan., 1921.
WILSON, G. R. C., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), Cert. of London S.T.M.—Tempy, capt., R.A.M.C., May, 1915 to Feb., 1919; med. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Feb., 1919.

WILSON, SIR HENRY FRANCIS, K.C.M.G. (1908), K.B.E. (1919); C.M.G. (1902)—B. 1859; ed. Rugby and Trin. Coll., Camb.; foundation scholar, 1878; sen. Bell's Univ. scholar, 1879; Chancellor's medal for English verse, 1880; 6th in 1st class classical tripos, and distinguished in exam. for Chancellor's class, med., 1882; fellow of Trin. Coll., 1884; M.A., 1885; called to the bar (Lincoln's Inn), 1888; sec. to Trinidad judicial inquiry coman., 1892; sent to Malta to collect evidence for the Protestant communities in connection with the Marriages Case, 1893; priv. sec. to the Rt. Hon. J. Chamberlain, M.P., S. of S. for colo., June, 1895; legal astt. in the C.O., 1897; went to S. Africa as legal astt. on high comsnr.'s staff, Feb., 1900; sec. to administration of Orange River Colony, 1901; col. sec., June, 1902; acted as lieut.-gov., Apr. to Aug., 1903, Dec., 1903, to Aug., 1904, and from Aug. to Dec., 1906; mem. of I.C.C., 1903-1907 (represented O.R.C. on rly. comtee.); retired; was sec. to the Royal Colonial Institute.

WILSON, SIR JAMES GLENNY, KT. BACH. (1915).—B. 1849; ed. Sir James Murray's Schl., Hawick, Scotland, Bruce Castle, Tottenham, Edinburgh Inst., and Edinburgh Univ.; M.H.R., N.Z., 1881-97; pres., N.Z. Farmers' Union; pres., N.Z. bd. of agr. since 1914.

WILSON, SIR JEREMIAH, KT. BACH. (1920), C.M.G. (1918).—Entd. Impl. telegraph ser., 22nd May, 1876; transf'd. to Cape telegraph ser., 1880; prin. clk., foreign mails branch, 1893; chief clk., G.P.O., 1898; asst. sec., 1905; sec., 1st Feb., 1908; under sec., under Union admin., 31st May, 1910; postm.-gen., Union of S. Africa, 19th Oct., 1910; ret., Dec., 1920.

WILSON, HON. JOHN CARNEGIE DOVE, K.C., M.A., LL.B.—B. 1885; ed. privately and at Aberdeen and Edinburgh Unive., advocate of the Scottish bar; second puisne judge of sup. ct., Natal, Oct., 1904; judge pres. of the sup. ct. of S. Africa (Natal Prov. Div.), Jan., 1911.
WILSON, JOHN WOLSELEY.—B. 1876; entd. civ. ser., Trinidad, 1st Nov., 1899; sup-inspr. of constab., 24th Jan., 1905; inspr., ditto, 1907; now serving as tempy. major in army.

WILSON, CAPT. LOGIE WEIR.—B. 1894; ed. Giggleswick Schol., St. Andrew's Coll., Dublin, Royal Coll. of Science, Dublin, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; B.A. (Dublin); R.A.S.C., Oct., 1914; Royal Irish Rifles, 1917; astt. dist. comsnr., Sierra Leone, 23rd July, 1919; ag. astt. col. sec., Aug., 1919 to Mar., 1920; ag. dist. comsnr., Pujehun and Bonthe dists., 1921; dist. comsnr., 1st Aug., 1926.
WILSON, HON. REGINALD VICTOR.—Elected senator, Commonwealth of Australia, 13th Dec., 1919; hon. min. in Bruce ministry, 1923; min. of markets and immigr., 1926.

WILSON, RONALD ERIC.—B. 1898; H.A.C., 1915; France, Jan.-Sept., 1916; R.M.C., Sandhurst, 1916-17; gazetted to Reg. Forces, (Middlesex Regt.), Sept., 1917; 1st Bn. Middlesex Regt. (France), Jan., 1918; transfd. to Indian Army, Sept., 1918 (59th Scinde Rifles, F.F.); N.W. Frontier (Waziristan); attd. 82nd Punjabis, 1919; resigned coman. on appt. as cadet, F.M.S., Sept., 1921; ag. astt. collr., land rev., Kuala Lumpur, Apr., 1922; ag. supt., cust., Krian, Oct., 1922; ag. 2nd astt. dist. offr., Larut, May, 1923; ag. astt. sec. to res., Perak, Feb., 1924; offr., cls. V., Sept., 1924; dist. offr., Jelebu, 1925; 4th astt. sec. to govt., F.M.S., Aug., 1926.

WILSON, BRIG.-GEN. SIR SAMUEL HERBERT, K.C.M.G. (1923), K.B.E. (1921), C.B. (1918), C.M.G. (1914).—B. 1873; entd. Army, R.E., 1893; capt., 1904; major, 1913; bt. lieut.-col., 1916; bt. col., 1917; served in S. Africa, 1899-1900 (Queen's medal, two clasps); European War, 1914-18; Chevalier Legion d'Honneur, 1915; Commandeur de la Couronne (Belgian), 1918; Officier Legion d'Honneur, 1918; French croix de guerre avec palme; Belgian croix de guerre; sec. to overseas defence comtee., 1911-14; gen. staff offr., 2nd grade, 1914; gen. staff offr., 1st grade, 1915; brig.-gen. gen. staff, 1916; sec. to overseas defence comtee., 19th Nov., 1918; gov. of Trinid., 7th Nov., 1921; gov., Jamaica, 1st Sept., 1924; assumed govt., 29th Sept., 1924; perm. under sec. of state for the colo., 5th Aug., 1925.

WILTSHIRE, EDGAR.—B. 1893; articled pupil to county survr. and architect, Middlesex, 1911-14; served with H.M. Forces, 1914-19; astt. engnr., P.W.D., Zanzibar, 1920; A.M.I.C.E., Oct., 1924; ag. dist. engnr., 13th July, 1925 to 11th Jan., 1926 and from 15th Feb., 1926.

WINGATE, ANDREW FREDERICK.—B. 1830; ed. Bundell's Schl.; on Indian rlys., 1912-14; Guayaquil and Quito rly., 1914-19; traffic offr., Sierra Leone govt. rly., 1919-20; astt. traffic supt., Tanganyika Territory cent. rly., 1920.
WINSER, LEIGH, M.V.O. (1920).—Priv. sec. to govs. of S. Australia since May, 1915.
WINSLOW, H. F., B.A. (Cantab.), M. Inst. T.—Entd. service of Shanghai-Nanking Rlwy., 1903; dep. traffic man., 1908; man., Kowloon-Canton rly. (British section), 1911.

WINSTEEDT, RICHARD OLAF, C.M.G. (1926), M.A., Oxon., D. Litt., Oxon.—B. 1878; ed. at Magdalen College School and New Coll., Oxford; 2nd cls. mods.; 2nd cls. Lit. Hum., M.A.; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1902; ag. inspr. of schla., Perak; passed in Malay, 1904; in law, Oct., 1904; ag. 2nd asst. dist. offr., Batang Padang, June, 1904; ag. asst. dist. offr., Batang Padang, Feb., 1906; ag. 2nd asst. dist. offr., Tapah, Oct., 1909; ag. asst. dist. offr., Matang, and insp. of fisheries, Jan., 1908; class V., Jan., 1910; ag. asst. dist. offr., Gopeng, Apr., 1910; ag. dist. offr., Kuala Pilah, Sept., 1912; sec. to comte. for Malay studies in addition, Sept., 1913; class IV., confirmed dist. offr., Kuala Pilah, Dec., 1913; federal examiner in Malay in addition, Dec., 1913; promoted to class III., asst. dir. of education, S.S. and F.M.S., June, 1917; ag. chmn., sanitary bd., Kinta, June to Aug., 1919, in addition; cls. II., Nov., 1919; ag. dir. educn., S. Stiltns., and F.M.S., Feb., 1920; fed. examr. in Malay, S. Stiltns., and F.M.S., June, 1920; ag. chmn., comte. for Malay studies, F.M.S., 1920; seconded to act as prin. of Raffles' Coll., Singapore, June, 1921; ag. dir., educn., S.S. and F.M.S. in addn., May, 1923; offg. as sec. to high commr. in addn.; Fed. examr. in Malay in addition, Mar., 1923; dir. educn., S.S. and F.M.S., cls. IA, July, 1924; author of an "English-Malay Dictionary" (3 vols.), a "Malay Grammar" (Clarendon Press), "Colloquial Malay," a "Malay Reader" (Clarendon Press), "Malayan Memories," "Shaman, Saws and Sufi," a study of Malay magic (London), editor and part author of "Malaya" (London, 1922), and author of various pamphlets published by the F.M.S. govt. on Malay subjects; editor of many Malay folk-tales and (with R. J. Wilkinson, C.M.G.) of Pantun Melaya.
WISE, A. R.—Asst. dist. commr. (cadet), Kenya, Sept., 1923.

WISE, KENBRICK STANTON.—B. 1881; ed. Dover, Cambridge, and London; M.B., B.S., B.S.C. Lond. Univ.; M.R.C.S., England; L.R.C.P., Lond.; D.P.H.; govt. bacteriologist, Br. Guiana, 1906; govt. med. offr. of health, 1912; surg.-gen. and reglar.-gen., 1914; surg.-gen., Trinidad, 4th Aug., 1919.

WISERMAN, R. A.—B. 1886; ed. Monmouth Gram. Schl. and Oxford; math. demy, Magdalen Coll., 1905-1909; 2nd cl. math. mods., 1906; 2nd cls. hist., 1908; 2nd cls. jurisprudence, 1909 apptd. after compet. exam., clk., sec.'s dept., Admiralty, Mar., 1910; 2nd cls. clk., C.O., Mch., 1911; sec. to overseas prize disposal comte., Nov., 1914; temporarily served at ministry of shipping, Jan., 1917, to Mar., 1919; sec. to international shipping comte., Feb., 1917; sec. to inter-allied chartering comte., May, 1917; priv. sec. to Col. Amery, 1st Apr., 1919; 1st cls. clk., 1st Jan., 1920; prin., 1st Apr., 1920.

WITHYCOMBE, ROBERT, M.B.E. (1918), A.M.I.E.E.—B. 1880; dir., rlyw. and elec. dept., Zanzibar, 12th Dec., 1911; dir., wireless telegraph, 1st Jan., 1914; 3rd cls., Order of the Brilliant Star, Zanzibar.

WITTENNOOM, HON. SIR E. H., K.C.M.G. (1900).—B. 1854; elected M.L.C., W. Australia, 1883; resig. in Dec.; re-elected 1884; resig. 1886; elected, M.L.C., July, 1894; min. for mines, Dec., 1894; agent-gen., July, 1898; resigned, 1901; M.L.C.

WOODEHOUSE, Lieut.-Gen. Sir Josceline Henneage, G.C.B. (1913), K.C.B. (1908), C.B. (1889), C.M.G. (1890).—B. 1852; ed. at R.M.A., Woolwich; commr. in R.A., 1872; served in Zulu war, 1879; Afghan war, 1880; Soudan, 1883-1894; gov. of Eng. frontier, 1887-1894; commd. "I" battery, R.H.A., 1894-1898; with Malakand field force (severely wounded), 1898; in action with Egyptian forces at Arim; infantry div. at Toaki; Presidency dist., 1888-1899; Secunderabad, 1900-1; Lahore, 1902; Rawal Pindi, 1903; ag. in comd. of W. Comd., 1905; Zulu war (medal and clasps); Soudan war (medal, 3 clasps); N.W. Frontier (medal and clasp); 2nd cls. Medjidie; 2nd cls. Osmannieh; Egyptian bronze star; Order of Palm, French Govt.; gov. of Bermuda, 1907-1908; late G.O.C. Northern Army, India.

WOODEHOUSE, P. P. J., C.I.E. (1919).—B. 1877; ed. Eliz. Coll., Guernsey; first clk., regen.'s dept., Hong Kong, 1897-1900; passed in Chinese, 1899; census offr., 1900; asst. supt. of police, 1901; attached Punjab police, 1901-2; passed in Urdu, 1902; J.P., 1902; census offr., 1906; pol. mag., N.T. (Islands), Sept., 1906; passed in Punjabi, 1908; mem. of bd. of examiners, 1907; ag. dep. supt. of pol. and fire brig., 1907, 1908 to 1909, 1911 to 1912; census offr., 1911; dep. supt. of police and fire brigade, 1912; aide-de-camp to gov., 1918 to 1919; ag. capt. supt. of pol., on various occasions, 1918-25.

WODRMAN, GUY STANLEY.—B. 1886; ed. Rossall schl. and Jesus Coll., Camb. (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., Nov., 1909; office asst. to govt. agt., Sabaragamuwa, Oct., 1910; pol. mag., Matale, Feb., 1912; asst. commr. of excise attached to the office of the excise commr., Apr., 1913; asst. commr. of excise, Cent.
