Div., Jan., 1914; asst. govt. agt., Kandy, Apr., 1915; ag. asst. govt. agt., Matale, Aug., 1915; attached to chief censor's office, Sept., 1915; asst. govt. agt., Trincomalee, Apr., 1916; ditto, Colombo, Oct., 1918; ditto, Nuwara Eliya, Dec., 1919; ditto, Kegalla, Dec., 1921; ditto, Matara, Mar., 1923; dep. collr., cust., Apr., 1925; dep. commr., excise, Nov., 1926.

WOLFE, E. D. C. Tonbridge schl. (1890-94); exhib. in mod. langa., Caius Coll., Camb. (1894); exhib. from Tonbridge schl. (1894); 3rd class mod. langa., tripos. (1896); 1st class Pt. I, and 2nd class Pt. II, Law Special (1897); B.A. (1897); M.A. (1925); cadet, F.M.S., 1898; sent to Canton to study Cantonese (1899); transf. to Hong Kong, 1901; passed cadet, Sept., 1901; sec., P.W.D. enquiry comm., 1901; reglar., land ct., 1901; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1902; ag. asst. reglar.-gen., 1902; reglar., land ct., 1903; ag. pol. mag., N.T., and asst. supt. of pol., 1903-5; seconded to Transvaal govt. serv., 1905; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1907; ag. inspr. of schla., 1907; ag. reglar.-gen., 1907; ag. asst. reglar.-gen., 1907; ag. inspr. of schla., 1907 to 1909; head of sanitary dept., 1909; postmnr.-gen., 1913; ag. col. treas., 1914-16; 1st pol. mag., 1917; ag. col. treas., and custodian of enemy property, 1917 and 1918; ag. capt. supt., pol., 1918; capt. supt. of pol., 1920; ag. col. sec., 1921; attended internat. pol. confce., New York, 1925.

WOLFE, HENRY, M.Sc. (Agric.) Cornell Univ. U.S.A.—B. 1887; tech. asst., dept. of agr., S. Africa, 1916; dep. dir., agr., Tanganyika Territory, 1920; ag. dir., agr., Feb. to Dec., 1921 and Mar. to Nov., 1924; E. Africa campaign 1916-17.

WOLFERTSTAN, LITTLETON EDWARD PIPER.—Ed. Eton and Clare Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1888; cadet, S.S., Oct., 1889; passed final exam in Malay, July, 1891; ag. priv. sec. to Sir C. Smith Jan. to Apr., 1890; ag. dist. offr., Bukit Mertajam, Feb. to Mar., and June to Nov., 1892; ag. dist. offr., S.W. dist., Penang, Mar., 1893; 3rd mag., Penang, Apr., 1893; ag. dist. offr., prov. Wellesley, June, 1897; ag. 2nd mag., Singapore Aug., 1897; sheriff and dep. registr., sup. ct., Singapore, 1898, but still ag. 2nd mag., Singapore; ag. dist. offr., Dindings, Feb., 1900; ag. collr. ld. rev., Penang, May, 1900; ag. sec. to H. Cr. for Fed. Malay States, May, 1901; ag. sen. dist. offr. Prov. Wellesley, May, 1902; collector of land rev. and regis. of deeds, Penang, Aug., 1902; offl. assignee, S.S., Sept., 1903; sen. dist. offr., Prov. Wellesley, Feb., 1907; ag. 1st mag., Penang, Apr., 1907; ag. inspr. of prisons, S.S., Apr., 1909; ag. dist. judge and 1st mag., Singapore, Oct., 1909; ag. dist. judge (civil), Singapore, Feb., 1910; ag. dist. judge and 1st mag., Singapore, Apr., 1910; res., Malacca, July, 1910, and Dec., 1915; ag. British adviser, Kedah, June, 1914 to Apr., 1915; offr., cls. Ia., Jan., 1919; title of appt. altered to res. coun., Malacca, mem. of exec. coun. and offl. mem. of leg. coun., Jan., 1921.

WOLFF, ERNEST CHARLIS HOLFORD.—F.M.S. civ. ser.; b. 1875; ed. at Merchiston and Trin. Coll., Oxford; B.A., 1897; cadet, Pahang, F.M.S., Nov., 1897; passed final exam. in Malay, May, 1899; ag. treas., supt. of posts and tels., and collr. of stamp duties, Pahang, Jan., 1898; ag. asst. dist. offr., Pekan, May, 1899; ag. sec. to res., Negri Sembilan, May, 1901; sec. to ditto, Oct., 1901; ag. asst. sec. to res.-gen., F.M.S., May, 1906; dist. offr., Kuantan, Sept., 1909, but still ag. asst. sec. to res.-gen.; ag. chmn. and sec., sanitary bd., Kinta South, Perak, Jan., 1911; ag. dist. offr., Upper Perak, Apr., 1911; officiating dist. offr., Larut, Jan., 1912; officiating supt. of convict estabt. and inspr. of prisons, F.M.S., Feb., 1915; ag. asst. col. sec. and clk. of couns., S. Stltmts., Apr., 1915, to Sept., 1919; offr., cls. Ib., Jan., 1919; prin. asst. col. sec., Jan., 1920; ag. col. sec., S. Stltmts., 12th to 27th Feb., 1921; ag. dir., educon., S. Stltmts. and F.M.S., June, 1921; dir. educon., S. Stltmts. and F.M.S., Dec., 1921; ag. Br. adviser, Kedah, Dec., 1923; Br. res., Negri Sembilan, July, 1924.

WOLFE, PHINAK ERNEST.—B. 1885; ed. Glasgow Academy; clk., land dept., E.A.P., 1909; ag. office supt., 1917; land asst., 1919; regis. of titles, documents and coast land titles, 1920; liquidator of enemy property, July-Nov., 1921.

WOLSELEY-BOURNE, CAPT. JAMES FREDERICK, M.C.—B. 1878; 2nd lieut. Royal Berks. Regt.; lieut. S. African Constab.; served in S. African war, 1899-1902 (Queen's and King's medals); capt., K.A.R.; served in E. Africa, 1914-1918 ("1914-15" Star, War and Victory medals); commanded a column in operations against Aulihan (Somali) tribe, Jubaland, 1917-18 (M.C., African Gen. Serv. medal); supt. of police, Kenya, 1919; ag. asst. commsr., pol., 1925.

WOOD, A. E.—Ed. at Liverpool Coll.; scholar of Univ. Coll., Oxford, 1903; 1st mods.; 2nd lit. hum.; cadet, Hong Kong, 1907; offr. for exam. of male assisted emigrants, 1908; ag. asst. registr.-gen., 1908 to 1909; passed cadet, 1st Apr., 1910; ag. asst. registr.-gen., Apr.-May, 1910; ag. asst. dist. offr., N.T., 1910 to 1911; 2nd asst. registr.-gen., 1911; ag. asst. dist. offr., South dist., New Territories, 1911 to 1912; passed in Hakka, 14th June, 1912; title of asst. registr.-gen. altered to 2nd asst. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1913; ag. 1st asst. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1913; ag. chief asst. to sec. for Chinese affairs, 1914; ag. dist. offr., 1915; ag. chief asst. to S.C.A., 1916, and 1918 to 1919; passed in Pekingese, June, 1916; sent to Japan to study Japanese, 1916; passed in Japanese, May, 1918; ag. dist. offr., Taipo, 1919 to 1920; ag. ch. asst. to sec. for Chinese affrs., 1921; ag. dist. offr., Taipo in addn., 1922; ag. sec. for Chinese affrs., 1923 and 1924; ag. dir., educon., Dec., 1925.

WOOD, BERNARD READER.—B. 1890; ed. Tonbridge Schl., Sid. Suss. Coll., Camb.; M.A., dipl. in forestry; asst. conservator, Indian forest serv., 1914; dep. conservator, 1918; conservator, of forests, Br. Guiana, 1925.

WOOD, FRANCIS ERNEST, M.B., B.C. (Cantab.), 1902, B.A. (nat. sci. tripos) 1898—B. 1877; ed. Downing Coll., Cambridge and St. Bartholomew Hosp., London; London Schl. Trop. Med., 1905; med. offr., Malay States Guides, 1905-20; on active serv. with Aden Field Force, 1915-19; ment. in despa., Apr., 1919; major (local), 1920; sen. med. offr., Perak, 1921; ag. prin. med. offr., F.M.S., Apr., 1922-Mar., 1923.

WOOD, JOHN R.—Ed. City of London schl. and Camb.; foundation scholar of Jesus Coll., 1896; 1st cl. class. tripos, 1899; B.A., 1899; M.A., 1913; called to the bar, 1919; cadet, Hong Kong, 1899; registr. of land ct., 1900; ag. pol. mag. in New Territories, 1902; J.P., 1902; passed cadet, 1902; mem. land ct., 1904-3; asst. land offr. in N. T., Jan., 1905; ag. asst. reg-gen., 1905; mem. of squatters' bd., 1906; ag. 2nd pol. mag., 1908; dep. regis. and appraiser, sup. ct., 1908; 2nd pol. mag., 1909; ag. dir. of educn., 1911 to 1912; ag. 1st pol. mag., on several occasions; 1st pol. mag., 1920; ag. puisne judge, 1920-22 and in 1925; chmn., Sui An piracy coun., 1922; puisne judge, 1926.

WOOD, LESLIE WILLIAM.—B. 1890; ed. Merchant Taylors' schl. and Queens' coll., Camb.; 1st cl. math. tripos, pt. 1; 2nd cl. math. tripos, pt. 2; B.A., 1912; asst. dist. commsr., Gold Coast, 18th Aug., 1914; asst. col. sec., 1st Jan., 1921; ag. elk. of couns., Feb., 1922.
WOOD, CAPT. REGINALD CECIL MERVYN.—B. 1889; E. African Expedy. Force, 1915-17; seconded, K.A.R., 1918; K.A.R. staff, 1919; adjt., 3rd K.A.R., 1919-22; coy. commdr., 1923; asst. treas., Kenya, 1923.

WOOD, WILLIAM HAROLD.—B. 1896; ed. Abingdon schl.; served with Royal Berkshire Regt., 1915-20; France, 1916-19; Egypt, 1919-20; lieut., July, 1917; ag. capt., Apr., 1918; ment. in despa., 1918; cadet, F.M.S., 15th Apr., 1921; attd., dist. office, Port Dickson, Oct., 1921; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kuala Selangor, May, 1923; ag. asst. dist. offr., Kuantan, Mar., 1924; ag. supt., lands, Kelantan, Apr., 1925.
WOODFORD, HON. W. W.—Elected M.H.A., Harbor Main, Newfoundland, 1889; chrmn., bd. of works, 1897; min. of public works, 1898, 1909, and 1918; M.E.C., 1918.

WOODHOUSE, GEORGE WILLIAM.—B. 1867; ed. St. John's Coll., Camb.; LL.M., Camb.; math. trip.; M.A. of the Middle Temple; Campbell-Foster prizeman, 1910; called to the bar, Middle Temple, 1916; cadet, local div., Ceylon civ. ser., Sept., 1892; asst. collr. of cust., Jaffna, Dec., 1892; Trincomalee, Dec., 1895; dist. judge, Tangalla, Oct., 1897, and in Mar., 1898; addtl. dist. judge, etc., Matara, Oct., 1898; addtl. pol. mag., Matara, Mar., 1899; pol. mag., Galle, Dec., 1901; dist. judge, Negombo, Feb., 1905; Batticaloa, Dec., 1905; dist. judge, Matara, June, 1912; dist. judge, Kurunegala, May, 1913; addtl. dist. judge and addtl. pol. mag., Kandy, May, 1915; dist. judge, Kurunegala, Sept., 1916; ditto, Jaffna, Apr., 1920; ag. addnl. dist. judge, Colombo, Jan., 1922; dist. judge, Jaffna, Mar., 1922.

WOODHOUSE, JOHN LESLIE.—B. 1892; ed. Merchant Taylors Sch.; asst. transport offr., with rank of lieut., Uganda Transport Corps, 27th Nov., 1914; ment. in desps. for service in German E. Africa, 22nd Nov., 1916; asst. dist. commr., Uganda, 30th Dec., 1916; 2nd grade adminstr. offr., Tanganyika Territory, 17th June, 1920.

WOODS, PERCY SINCLAIR COMYN.—B. 1879; ed. St. Michael's Sch., Lyme Regis, and Durham Univ.; clk., col. audit branch, exchequer and audit dept., Somerset House, 1st Jan., 1903; asst. auditor, Lagos and S. Nigeria, 29th Apr., 1905; dep. ch. acctnt., G. Coast rly., 30th Jan., 1909; ag. ch. acctnt., 21st Mar. to 26th Sept., 1909; promoted rly. acctnt. on abolition of office of ch. acctnt., 31st Jan., 1910; col. treas., Feb., 1922.

WOODS, WILFRED WENTWORTH, C.M.G. (1928)—B. 1878; B.A. Oxon.; 2nd cls. final hon. schl., mod. hist., 1901; clk., col. audit branch, E. and A. dept., 2nd Sept., 1901; asst. auditor, G. Coast, 22nd Feb., 1902; local auditor, N. Nigeria, 30th Oct., 1904; asst. col. auditor, Ceylon, 8th Feb., 1908; ag. col. auditor on several occasions; col. auditor, Aug., 1915; temp. employed at the War Office, Dec., 1917 to 1919; ag. col. treas., May to Nov., 1920; ag. P.M.G., Nov., 1920 to Apr., 1921; col. treas., Feb., 1922; ag. col. sec., 30th Sept. to 17th Oct., 1926.

WOOLLEY, CHARLES CAMPBELL.—B. 1893; on milly. serv., Aug., 1914; cadet, Ceylon civ. serv., May, 1921; attd., Kandy Cash, June, 1921; ag. office asst. to gov't.; agt. N.C. Prov., Sept., 1922; 4th astt. col. sec., Feb., 1924; 3rd astt. col. sec., Mar., 1925; temp. attd. C.O., 1925 to June, 1926; addnl. astt. col. sec., Sept., 1926; 3rd astt. col. sec., Oct., 1926.

WOOLLEY, HOWARD MARK.—B. 1879; apptd., after open compet. exam., to G.P.O., London, and served in different depts.; astt. postmtr.-gen., N. Nigeria, 22nd Apr., 1908; deputy P.M.G., 1st Apr., 1908; P.M.G., 31st Oct., 1910.

WORKMAN, CHARLES RUFUS MARSHALL.—B. 1874; ed. Christ Ch., Oxford, M.A.; called to bar, Inner Temple; pol. offr., Ocean Is., 1909; dep. commr., Western Pacific, 1911; admstr., Nauru, 1914; ag. res. commr., Gilbert and Ellice Is., May, 1916 to Feb., 1917; ag. res. commr., Solomon Is., June, 1917; asst. commr., Apr., 1919; judl. commr., Feb., 1920; col. sec., Gambia, Sept., 1921; ag. gov. on various occasions, 1921-25.

WORLEY, NEWNHAM ARTHUR.—B. 1892; ed. Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge, B.A., 1913; cadet, F.M.S., Nov., 1914; astt. contr. of lab., Kampar, July, 1917; astt. dist. offr., Ipoh, May, 1920; astt. prot. of Chinese, Negri Sembilan and ag. dep. contr. of lab., Seremban, Aug., 1920; astt. prot. of Chinese, Perak, Dec., 1921; astt. dir. of educn. (Chinese), F.M.S., June, 1922; ag. astt. dir., educn. (Chinese), S.S. and F.M.S., Jan., 1924.

WORLDENGE, JOHN LEONARD.—B. 1896; ed. St. Paul's Sch.; active service in France, Sept., 1914 to Sept., 1918; ment. in desps.; Ireland, 1919; astt. audr., Kenya, Apr., 1920; astt. audr. in charge, Zanzibar, May 1920 to Feb., 1921; ag. senr. astt. audr., Kenya, Mar., 1923; senr. astt. audr., Nov., 1925.

WORSLEY, RALPH MARCUS MEABURN, B.A. Oxon.—B. 1887; cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., 12th Nov., 1910; attached to Colombo Kachcheri, Dec., 1910; attached to col. sec.'s office, Aug., 1911; extra office asst. to gov't. agt., W. Prov., Oct., 1911; astt. land settm't offr., Jan., 1912; office astt. to gov't. agt., Central Prov., July, 1914; astt. censor, Dec., 1914; on military duty, Feb., 1915; ag. astt. gov't. agt., Mullaitivu, Nov., 1917; astt. gov't. agt., Trincomalee, Sept., 1921; ag. gov't. agt., Prov. of Uva, Aug. to Oct., 1922; astt. stimt. offr., Jan., 1924; astt. gov't. agt., Hambantota, May, 1924.

WORTHINGTON, ARTHUR FURLEY.—B. 1874; ed. at Tonbridge and Sidney Sussex Coll., Camb.; B.A., 1896; M.A., 1910; cadet, Perak, Nov., 1897; passed cadet, Feb., 1900; ag. astt. dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, June, 1900; astt. dist. offr., Kuala Pilah, Feb., 1902; offr., cls. VI., Jan., 1903; ag. ch. astt. dist. offr., Kinta, June, 1905; offr., cls. V., Sept., 1904; offr., cls. IV., Nov., 1908; offr. dist. offr., Kuantan, Aug., 1910; offr., cls. III., Jan., 1912; dist. offr., Kuala Lipis, July, 1917, Lower Perak, Feb., 1919; offr., cls. II., Jan., 1919; ag. Br. adviser, Kelantan, Mar., 1920; offr. dist. offr., Ulu Selangor, Jan. to June, 1921; Br. adviser, Kelantan, Sept., 1922; ag. dist. offr., Larut, Mar.-Aug., 1922; appt. of Br. adviser, Kelantan raised to "staff," July, 1924; on sp. duty, England, Apr., 1924; Br. adviser, Kelantan, May, 1925; ag. Br. res., Pahang, Dec., 1925; Br. res., Pahang, Feb., 1926.

WORTLEY, EDWARD JOCELYN, O.B.E. (1926), M.B.E.—B. 1884; ed. Jamaica Coll.; course in agric. and agric. chem. at Jamaica, govt. lab. and sp. course at the Ontario agric. coll.; fellow of the chem. socy., 1906; jnr. astt. chem., Jamaica, 1902; astt. chem., 1905; lect. in agric. sci., 1906; headmstr., govt. farm schl. and stock farm, 1909; comnrs. for Jamaica, Canadian Nat. Exhibn., Sept., 1910, and Sept., 1912; dir. of agr., Bermuda, 1913-20; food comnrs., 1917; hon. sec., internat. comtes. on potato industry, 1919; visited Canary Is. on behalf of Bermuda govt., 1920; dir. of agr., Nyassaland, Dec., 1920; author of "Companion to Blackie's Tropical Readers," "Poultry Diseases," and "Foods and Food Products of Jamaica."
WRATISLAW, JOHN CYRIL.—B. 1894; ed. Oundle Schl.; astt. dist. commr., Sierra Leone, 14th Jan., 1924.

WRIGHT, ANDREW BARKWORTH, M.A., M.C. (1917), Bar (1918).—B. 1895; ed. Haileybury and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; served in European War with Suffolk Regt., 1914-19; major, 1918; astt. sec., ch. sec's. office, Cyprus, 3rd June, 1922; astt. priv. sec. to O.A.G., June to Oct., 1923; astt. priv. sec. to high commr., Apr. to June and Sept. to Oct., 1924; priv. sec. and A.D.C. to O.A.G., June to Sept., 1924; ag. ch. astt. sec., reg'r. of co-op. societies and reg'r. of co-op. credit societies, Apr. to Oct. 1924; passed exam. in mod. Greek, Aug., 1924; ditto, Turkish, Apr., 1925.

WRIGHT, ARTHUR EDGAR.—B. 1880; Fellow of the Surveyors' Institution; ed. at Tiffin's Endowed Schl., Kingston-on-Thames; astt. engrnr., P.W.D., Hong Kong, 5th May, 1903; exec. engrnr., 1911; ag. assessor in addition, 1915; exec. engrnr., 1st grade, 1917; seconded to Weihaieu on special serv., 1918 and 1920; astt. astt. D.P.W., 1923.

WRIGHT, CHARLES HAROLD.—M.A., F.I.C. F.C.S.; B. 1880; scholar, Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; B.A. (Nat. Sc. Tripos), 1902; M.A., 1916; astt. analyst and science master, Trinidad, Apr., 1908; ag. govt. analyst and prof. of chemistry Trinidad, on two occasions; agric. chemist, Fiji, Mar., 1914; ag. dist. commr., Kadavu, July-Aug., 1918; govt. chem., Fiji, Jan., 1920; senr. agr. chem., Nigeria, June, 1923.

WRIGHT, H. PELLEW.—Served in S. Africa war in I.Y. and S.A.C., 1899-1902 (Queen's medal and 3 clasps, King's medal and 2 clasps); staff offr. to O.C., Riet River div., O.R.C.; dist. commr., Edenburg and other districts, O.R.C., 1903-1906; asst. dist. commr., Uganda Prot., 15th Oct., 1908; dist. commr., June, 1914; recd. Royal Humane Society's medal for saving life.

WRIGHT, HERBERT ARTHUR.—B. 1882; instr., govt. technical schl., Accra, G. Coast, 10th Apr., 1909; principal, ditto, 28th Sept., 1911; attached to Togoland field force, 16th Aug. to 8th Sept., 1914; in joint charge of detention camp for German prisoners of war, 8th Dec., 1914 to 13th Jan., 1915; asst. censor, 2nd Oct., 1914 to 2nd Feb., 1915, and 2nd Oct., 1915 to 26th Feb., 1916; prov. inspr. of schls., 25th July, 1917; ag. senr. inspr. of schls., 29th July to 19th Sept., 1917; asst. dir., educn., 1st Apr., 1926.
WRIGHT, HON. ROBERT ALEXANDER.—Mem., N.Z. parlmt., 1908-11 and since 1914; min. of educn., 1926.

WRIGHT, VICTOR WILLIAM, A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1891; served with R. Naval Divn. in Gallipoli and France; wounded, 1914-16; lieut., R.E. and gen. staff, Macedonia, Caucasus, Trans-Caspia, Constantinople, 1916-20; mast., tech. schl., educn. dept., Gold Coast, 1921.
WRIGHT, WM.—B. 1855; clk. to pub. trustee, S. Aust., 1885; ch. clk. to pub. trustee, and ch. clk., succession duties dept., 1891; pub. trustee and curator of convicts' estates, 1903.

WRIGHT-NOOTH, CAPT. RODNEY GEORGE, M.C. (1916), A.M.I.C.E.—B. 1884; ed. St. Paul's Schl., stud., engnr. faculty, King's Coll., London, 1904-08; 2nd lieut., R.E., 1909; engnr. on survey and constrn. of Magadi rly. and water wks., 1911-14; R.E., 1914-22; served, Kenya, German E. Africa and Jubaland; severely wounded, twice ment. in desps., M.C.; div. offr., R.E. Londonderry and Enniskillen Divs., Ireland, Apr., 1920 to Apr., 1922; capt., Reg. Army, R. of O., 1922; supt. of pub. wks., Antigua, 4th Aug., 1922; mem., leg. coun., Antigua, 1922; asst., D.W.P., B. Guiana, 1926.
WROUGHTON, JOHN HENRY.—B. 1877; med. offr., Basutoland, 1908.

WUPPERMAN, RUDOLF CARL, M.B., Ch.B., Edin.—B. 1883; entered med. serv., Trinidad, 18th Oct., 1909; asst. res. surg., colonial hosp., Port of Spain, 27th Oct., 1914; res. surg., col. hosp., 8th May, 1923.

WYATT, ALEXANDER HENRY LOUIS.—Ed. Wellington; served in Impl. Yeomanry, S. African War, 1900-01; Queen's Med., 4 clasps; W.O., 1901-04; asst. res., Nyasaland, 1904-14; seconded for serv. with 1st K.A.R., 1914-17; ment. in desps. and awarded M.C.; served in Nyasaland rising, 1915; A.G.S.M. and clasp; dist. polit. offr., Tanganyika Territory, Dec., 1917; sen. commr., Nov., 1922; prov. commr., Mar., 1926.

WYATT, ROBT.—B. 1870; Lond. Metro. pol., 1891 to 1896; B. Hond. pol., June, 1895; asst. supt., Feb., 1902; ag. supt., May, 1902, to May, 1903; ag. dist. commr., Orange Walk, July to Aug., 1903; ag. dist. commr., Coroal, 26th Apr. to 11th July, 1906; ditto, Caya, Feb., 1906; ag. supt. pol., 14th July, 1906; J.P. for Colony; Hythe extra certif., 1899; supt. of pol., 1st Apr., 1906; certif. of proficiency for field offrs. with spec. distinction, schl. of instr., Chelsea barracks, 1907; certif. for management of English convict and local prisons, 1907; attached to London fire brigade for training, 1907.

WYNNE-JONES, EDWARD IRVINE.—B. 1896; ed. Oxford and Univ. of Wales; cadet, Hong Kong, July, 1920; ag. dist. offr., South, June, 1922; addl. col. secretariat, Nov., 1922; passed final exam., Feb., 1923; dist. offr., North, May, 1923.

YATES, GEO. W.—B. 1872; ed. London pub. and high schls.; ent. civ. serv. of Ont. as priv. sec. to various mins. from Feb., 1899 to Oct., 1911; accompanied Hon. F. Cochrane, Ont. min. of lands, forests and mines, to Ottawa on latter accepting portfolio of min. of rlys. and canals in Borden govt.; cont. priv. sec., min. of rlys. and canals until formation of union govt., 1917, when became ch. priv. sec. to Sir Robt. Borden; asst. dep. min. of rlys. and canals, 1920.

YATES, JOSEPH MERVYN ST. JOHN, B.A.—B. 1878; ed. Winchester Coll. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; called to the bar, Inner Temple, May, 1903; northern circuit, 1903-15; H.M. Forces, 1915-19; reglar-gen. Bahamas, Feb., 1920; ag. stip. magis., Mar., 1920; ag. atty.-gen., Nov., 1920; ag. ch. just., Feb.-Mar., 1921; ag. stip. and circuit mag., Mar., 1921 to Sept., 1922; seconded for serv. as ag. asst. to atty.-gen., Jamaica, Sept., 1922; res. mag., Jamaica, 1923; ag. atty.-gen., Apr., 1923 to Jan., 1924; ag. judge, Kingston ct., Nov., 1924 to Oct., 1925; ag. puisne judge, Oct., 1925; circuit judge, Ashanti and N. Territories, Gold Coast, 1926.

YOUILL, JOSEPH.—Seconded from Impl. serv. to B. E. Africa and Uganda Prot., as 1st grade dist. survr., 1st Jan., 1913 to 17th Dec., 1915; seconded to G. Coast, 31st May, 1916; offr.-in-charge, post and telegraph dept., Togoland, 22nd May, 1918 to 23rd Jan., 1919, and from 12th Aug., 1919.
YOUNG, ALFRED ERNEST, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), L.D.S. (Eng.).—B. 1891; med. offr., Basutoland, 1924.

YOUNG, SIR ALFRED CARNEY, KT. BACH. (1923), K.C., B.A.—Ed. Magdalen Coll., Oxon; called to the bar, In. Tem., May, 1889; 2nd clk., col. sec.'s office, Br. Honduras, Oct., 1890; ag. dist. mag., Toledo dist., Aug., 1891; priv. sec. to Sir A. Maloney, and clk. of councils, 1892; priv. sec. to admstr. of the govt., 1896; ag. reglar.-gen., 1896-7; dist. commr., Orange Walk, 1898; reglar.-gen., June, 1898; ag. supt. of police, May to Nov., 1899; J.P. for col.; crown prosecutor, legal adviser, and police mag., Seychelles, 23rd Sept., 1903; atty.-gen., B.C. Africa Prot., 17th Mar., 1906; stip. mag., Trinidad, 1908; ch. just., Seychelles, Aug., 1909; admstr. govt., Apr. to Nov., 1911, and from 16th to 27th Dec., 1912; atty.-gen., Fiji, May, 1914; ag. chief just., Jan., 1915 and from Mar., 1919 to May, 1920; ch. just., Leeward Is., Feb., 1921; ch. just., Fiji, 1923.

YOUNG, CAPT. SIR ARTHUR HENDERSON; G.C.M.G. (1916); K.C.M.G. (1908); K.B.E. (1918), C.M.G. (1897).—B. 1854; late 27th Inniskillings; ed. at Edin. Acad., Rugby, and Sandhurst; local comdt. mil. pol., Kyrenia, Aug., 1878; asst. commr., Paphos, Nov., 1878; commr., Paphos, Sept., 1879; ditto, Famagusta, Jan., 1882; dir. of survey and prin. forest offr., 1892; mem. legis. coun., 1892; ch. sec., 1894; admstr. the govt., July to Dec., 1895, Jan. to Apr., 1898; sent on special mission to St. Vincent in connctn. with relief work, Nov., 1902; col. sec., S. Sttlmts., June, 1906; admtd. govt., Feb. to May, 1909; ch. sec., F.M.S., 1910; gov., S. Stiltns, 8th Aug., 1911; assumed govt., 2nd Sept., 1911; also high commr. for Malay States and for Brunei and Brit. Agt. for N. Borneo and Sarawak; ret. 25th Nov., 1919.

YOUNG, HON. SIR FREDERICK WILLIAM, K.T. BACH, (1918), LL.B.—M.H.A., S. Australia, 1909; commr. of crown lands and immigr., 1912-14; agt.-gen. for S. Australia, 1915-18; mem. of comte., for sttlmt. of ex-service men within the Empire, 1917; M.P. (Co. U.), Swindon, 1918.

YOUNG, HENRY ALFRED, K.C.—Ed. at Derby Schol. and Edin. Univ.; called to the bar (Scotland), July, 1899; asst. crown advocate, E. Africa Prot., 22nd June, 1906; asst. atty.-gen., 1913; res. mag., 1917; stip. mag., B. Guiana, Aug., 1921.

YOUNG, MAJOR HUBERT WINTHROP, C.M.G. (1923), D.S.O. (1918).—B. 1885; ed. Eton and R. Mily. Acad., Woolwich; ocmdn., R. Garrison Arty., 1904; Indian Army, 1908; capt., 1913; major, 1919; asst. censor, Army headqrs., Simla, July, 1914 to Feb., 1915; N.W. Frontier, Feb.-Dec., 1915 (1914-15 Star); asst. polit. offr., Mesopotamia, Dec., 1915 to Aug., 1917; dep. dir., local resources, Mesopotamia, Aug., 1917 to Mar., 1918 (ment. in desps.); gen staff offr., 2nd grade, Hejas operations, Mar. to Oct., 1918 (ment. in desps., D.S.O., 3rd class Order of Nahda); pres., local resources bd., Damascus, Oct., 1918; F.O., Feb., 1919 to Mar., 1921; asst. sec., C.O., Mar., 1921; col. sec., Gibraltar, 1926.

YOUNG, JAMES.—B. 1869; ed. Ayr Acad.; served in Bechuanaland Border Pol., 1890-93; served in Bechuanaland Field Force, Dec., 1896 to Oct., 1897; clk., office of commissary of ordnance C.C., Nov., 1897; Bechuanaland Rebellion, 1897, (med. and clasp); Anglo-Boer War, 1899-1900, (Queen's med. and 2 clasps); served in Bechuanaland Rifles, 1899-1900; asst. mily. adminstr., W. Transvaal, Dec., 1900; clk., mag.'s office, Zeerust, June, 1901; A.R.M., Standerton, Sept., 1902; detached A.R.M., Ventersdorp, Sept., 1903; relieving mag., Transvaal, Nov., 1903; senr. A.R.M. (detached) Krugersdorp, May, 1904; ag. 2nd div. mag., Johannesburg, Sept., 1905; ag. 3rd ditto, Sept., 1906; A.R.M. (detached), Boksburg, Dec., 1906; A.R.M. (detached), Krugersdorp, Apr., 1907; R.M., Germiston, July, 1909; inspgr. mag., Transvaal, Griqualand West and Bechuanaland, Nov., 1918; civ. mag., Johannesburg, Nov., 1916; mag., Pietersburg, Nov., 1922; mag., Johannesburg, Feb., 1926.
YOUNG, HON. JAMES ALEXANDER.—Mem. N.Z. parl. since 1911; min. of health, 1926.

YOUNG, JAMES ANDERSON, M.C., M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), Certif., L.S.T.M.—B. 1889; ed. St. Andrews and Edinburgh Univ.; late house surg., Derbyshire R. Infirmary; served with R.A.M.C. (T.F.), 1915-19; granted hon. rank of major on resignation; med. offr., Somaliland, Sept., 1919; asst. bacteriologist, Nigeria, 1927.

YOUNG, JOHN CHARLES.—B. 1852; ed. Quebec Normal Schol. and Ottawa Univ.; ent. civil serv., Can., Feb., 1860; clk. of routine and proceedings of the senate and clk. of stationery, 12th May, 1890; dep. clk. of the sen. and first clk. asst., 1914.

YOUNG, MARK ATCHISON.—B. 1886; ed. Eton and King's Coll., Camb. (B.A.); cadet, Ceylon civ. ser., Nov., 1909; off. asst. to gov't. agt., N. Prov., Aug., 1910; ditto, Kandy, Nov., 1911; ag. asst. govt. agt., Mullaitivu, Jan., 1913; 4th asst. col. sec., Nov., 1913; ag. 3rd ditto, Feb., 1914; 2nd ditto and clk. of leg. coun., Sep., 1914; 3rd asst. sec. col., Oct., 1914; on mil. duty, 1915-1919; 3rd asst. col. sec., Jan., 1920; 2nd asst. col. sec., July, 1920; asst. govt. agt., Hambantota, Mar., 1922; prin. asst. col. sec., Mar., 1923.

YOUNG, MAJOR-GEN. ROBERT, C.B. (1919), C.M.G. (1916), D.S.O. (1915).—Volunteer serv. in N.Z.; left N.Z. with main body, N.Z. Expd. Force with rank of major, 1914; temp. lieut.-col., May, 1915; lieut.-col., Nov., 1915; in command, 1st batt., Canterbury Regt., Nov., 1915 to Aug., 1917; brig.-gen. (temp.), Aug., 1917; in command, 1st N.Z. infy. brig., Dec., 1917 to Feb., 1918; in command, 2nd N.Z. infy. brig., Feb., 1918 to Apr., 1919; comdr., "A" group, N.Z. Expd. Force, Sling Camp, Apr. to July, 1919; col., N.Z. staff corps, Oct., 1919; O.C., Canterbury dist., Nov., 1919 to Jan., 1921; O.C., Southern Command, Feb., 1921 to Dec., 1925; col.-comdt., 1921; major-gen. and comdt., N.Z. mily. forces, Dec., 1925; chevalier of Legion of Honour; six times ment. in desps.

YOUNG, W. A., M.A., B.M., B.Ch. (Oxon.), B.Sc. (War.), Lond., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.), cert., Lond. Schl. of Trop. Med. (with distinction, 2nd place), late exhibr., Jesus Coll., Oxford.—War serv., France, 1916-18; clin. asst., children and ear depts., St. Thomas's hosp., 1922; R.M.O., Kaling hosp., 1922-23; East Africa med. serv. (Zanzibar), Sept., 1923.

YOUNG, SIR WILLIAM DOUGLAS, K.B.E. (1919); C.M.G. (1907).—Ed. at Charterhouse; clk. in govt. sec.'s office, Br. Guiana, Jan., 1877; ch. clk., 1889; ag. priv. sec. on several occasions, 1877-1893, to gov'ts. of Br. Guiana, Trinidad, and G. Coast; ag. asst. sec. sec., Br. Guiana, 1892-3-4-5; ag. govt. sec., Dec., 1892; ag. govt. agr. N.W. dist., 1895; asst. col. sec., Mauritius, 1896; ag. col. sec., 1896-7-8, 1900-01; admtd. govt., Mauritius, Aug., 1897; commr., Turks and Caicos Islands, 1901; admtr., Dominica, 19th Apr., 1906; ag. govt., Leeward Is., 1909; admtr., St. Lucia, 1913; ag. govt., Windward Is., May to Dec., 1914; govt., Falkland Is., 28th Dec., 1914; assumed govt., 16th May, 1915; ret., 1920.

ZAMMIT, HON. PROFESSOR THEMISTOCLES, C.M.G. (1911), M.D. (hon.) D.Litt. (Oxon.).—B. 1864; ed. Malta Lyceum; matric., Malta Univ., 1882; grad. in medicine, 1889; govt. analyst, 1891-1920; curator, Valletta Museum, 1903; prof. of chemistry, Malta Univ., 1905-20; mem. Mediterranean fever comm., 1904-07; rector, Malta Univ., July, 1920; mem., exec. and leg. coun., 1920-21; hon. fellow, Royal Anthropological Inst., 1919; local sec. (Malta) of Soc'y. of Antiquaries, 1921; medallist (Mary Kingsley), Liverpool Schol of Trop. Med.; delegate of Malta govt. at Imp. Educn. Conf., London, June to July, 1923.
